introduction.purpose: The purpose of this document is to comprehensively describe the requirements for the **nenios** Child Care Management (NCCM) software. This system aims to establish a clear foundation for its development, ensuring that all stakeholder expectations are met through a shared understanding of its capabilities. It utilizes both textual descriptions and various modeling diagrams to illustrate the application's high-level structure from diverse perspectives, catering to different areas of responsibility for various stakeholders.
introduction.documentConventions: This document adheres to the IEEE 830-1998 standard for Software Requirements Specifications, supplemented by ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018 guidelines. Requirements are presented using structured prose and enumerated lists where appropriate. All system identifiers and labels follow a consistent naming convention, and specific keywords are bolded for emphasis. Diagrammatic conventions, as specified in Appendix B, are strictly followed to ensure clarity and maintainability.
introduction.intendedAudience: This document is intended for a broad audience including project managers, software developers, quality assurance engineers, system administrators, and future maintenance teams involved with the **nenios** system. It also serves as a critical reference for business analysts and stakeholders who need to understand the system's functionality and scope. The detailed specifications ensure that all parties have a common understanding of the system's objectives and technical requirements.
introduction.productScope: The **nenios** Child Care Management (NCCM) software is a web-based solution designed to automate and improve the operational workflow within child care centers. It provides functionalities ranging from secure user authentication and access control to comprehensive customer and child information management. Key features include attendance tracking, behavior documentation, immunization record management, robust billing and invoicing processes, and diverse reporting capabilities. The system is designed to run on popular computer platforms via standard web browsers, leveraging a centralized, internet-connected database to facilitate information sharing among employees.
references: IEEE 830-1998 IEEE Recommended Practice for Software Requirements Specifications.
ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018 Systems and software engineering — Life cycle processes — Requirements engineering.
Industry Report: Child Care Services. Dun & Bradstreet and Marketing Solutions Internet: www.zapdata.com. Accessed March 21, 2004.
Industry Growth Outlook Report: SIC 8351: Child Day Care Services. Integra Information. March 21 2004. Internet://www.integrainfo.com.
United States Census taken in 2000.
Illinois Department of Children and Family Services. http://www.state.il.us/dcfs/daycare/index.shtml.

overallDescription.productPerspective: The **nenios** Child Care Management software is conceived as a new, self-contained product designed to address the increasing demand for professional and efficient child care facility operations. It stands as a critical tool in the context of a rapidly expanding child care industry, offering a structured approach to managing daily operations. By automating critical administrative tasks, **nenios** aims to empower child care centers to maximize revenues, ensure compliance with guidelines, and enhance their professional image to parents. This system facilitates the retention of children and supports growth planning by providing robust data management and reporting.
productFunctions: Secure user authentication and role-based access control.
Comprehensive customer and child information management, including authorized pickups.
Tracking of child attendance and behavior documentation.
Management and tracking of child immunization records and physical examination dates.
Automated monthly billing, invoicing, and account history management, including late pick-up charges.
Generation of various pre-formatted reports for customer directories, enrollment, birthdays, immunization status, and account activity.
Daily reminder functionality for all employees, featuring pop-up notifications.

userClassesAndCharacteristics.characteristics: Administrators are high-privileged users with unlimited access to the **nenios** system. Their responsibilities include registering new customers, viewing and editing all account information, checking classroom availability, managing waiting lists, and setting up immunization schedules. They are also solely authorized to generate and print all system reports and customer invoices. This role requires a comprehensive understanding of child care center operations and data management.

Teachers and assistants are frontline employees with restricted access privileges within the **nenios** system. Their primary functions involve recording child arrival and departure times, documenting child behavior, and utilizing the daily reminders feature. These users are typically focused on direct child care activities and require an intuitive interface for quick data entry and access to relevant information about the children under their supervision.

overallDescription.operatingEnvironment: The **nenios** software will operate as a web-based solution, accessible via standard web browsers. It is designed to function across various popular computer platforms, ensuring broad compatibility for users. The backend infrastructure requires a **Web server** that supports **Microsoft's ASP.NET** technology, which forms the core of the application's framework.
designAndImplementationConstraints: The **nenios** system shall be developed using **Microsoft's ASP.NET** framework, mandating the use of a compatible **Web server** for deployment. The system's user interface must be designed around **HTML** to ensure accessibility through standard web browsers. All data storage shall utilize a centralized database connected to the internet, facilitating shared access and data integrity across the facility. Security considerations, including user authentication and data encryption, must be paramount in the design and implementation phase to protect sensitive child and parent information.
userDocumentation: User Manual for **nenios** Administrators: Comprehensive guide covering all administrative functionalities, including customer registration, billing, reporting, and system configuration.

User Guide for **nenios** Teachers and Assistants: A concise manual detailing functionalities relevant to direct child care staff, such as attendance logging, behavior documentation, and daily reminder usage.
Online Help System: Integrated context-sensitive help available within the **nenios** application for quick reference and troubleshooting.
assumptionsAndDependencies: It is assumed that the child care facility using **nenios** has already met and continues to comply with all relevant Federal, State, and Local regulatory requirements for child care services. The system relies on a stable internet connection for continuous operation and data access. The availability and proper functioning of third-party web browsers (e.g., **Internet Explorer**, **Netscape Navigator**) on user client machines are assumed. The system depends on the underlying **ASP.NET** runtime environment and a robust database management system for its core functionalities.

externalInterfaceRequirements.userInterfaces: The **nenios** system shall provide a web-based user interface, optimized for rendering across common web browsers such as **Internet Explorer** and **Netscape Navigator**. The interface shall adhere to a consistent design standard, featuring a uniform blue background for all windows to maintain brand identity and visual coherence. Key interactive elements, such as login screens, data entry forms for parent and child information, daily reminder pop-up windows, and report viewing interfaces, will be intuitively designed. All user interfaces must ensure a responsive experience, facilitating efficient interaction for all employee roles. Specific screen layouts and navigational flows will be detailed in a separate User Interface Specification.
externalInterfaceRequirements.hardwareInterfaces: The **nenios** software shall interface with standard client-side hardware, including personal computers and laptops equipped with modern web browsers and internet connectivity. Input devices such as keyboards and pointing devices (mice, trackpads) will be the primary means of user interaction. No specialized hardware interfaces are required on the client side for the core application functionality. Server-side hardware will require standard network interfaces for internet connectivity and appropriate processing and storage capabilities to host the **ASP.NET** application and database.
externalInterfaceRequirements.softwareInterfaces: The **nenios** system shall interface with a centralized database system for persistent storage of all operational data, including customer, child, employee, billing, and activity records. Communication with this database will be handled via the **ASP.NET** application layer, utilizing standard database connectivity protocols. The system will operate within a **Microsoft Windows** server environment, leveraging the **Internet Information Services (IIS)** web server to host the **ASP.NET** application. No direct programmatic interfaces with other external software applications are explicitly required, though standard operating system services will be utilized for logging and system management.
externalInterfaceRequirements.communicationsInterfaces: The **nenios** application will primarily communicate over **HTTP** and **HTTPS** protocols for all client-server interactions, ensuring secure data transmission for sensitive information such as login credentials and personal data. All communication between the web server and the centralized database will also employ secure, authenticated protocols. The system will handle data transfer rates efficiently to support multiple concurrent users. Specific requirements for email notifications (e.g., system alerts, password resets) or SMS integration are not explicitly defined but may be considered for future enhancements, adhering to industry standard communication protocols.

systemFeatures.name: User Authentication & Access Control
systemFeatures.description: This feature provides secure access to the **nenios** system, ensuring that only authorized employees can interact with sensitive child care data. It defines distinct access levels for administrators versus teachers and assistants, aligning system privileges with job classifications. By implementing robust login procedures and access restrictions, the system safeguards confidential information, prevents unauthorized modifications, and ensures data integrity. This directly contributes to the operational security and accountability within the child care center. The priority for this feature is High, as it forms the foundational security layer for all subsequent interactions.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Employee attempts to log in with username and password. Response: System validates credentials, applies lockout policy on multiple failures, and grants access based on job classification.
Stimulus: Administrator requests to reset a user's forgotten password. Response: System generates and assigns a new password to the user.
Stimulus: System detects 10 minutes of user inactivity. Response: System automatically logs off the inactive user to enhance security.
functionalRequirements: N-REQ-001: The system shall enforce two distinct levels of access: one for teachers and assistants, and another for administrators.
N-REQ-002: The system shall restrict teachers and assistants to only entering or editing child comments and child arrival or departure times.
N-REQ-003: The system shall allow administrators to enter or edit all data within the application.
N-REQ-004: The system shall ensure that only administrators can view or print reports.
N-REQ-005: The system shall ensure that only administrators can view or print invoices.
N-REQ-006: The system shall allow all employees to access and utilize the Daily Reminders feature.
N-REQ-007: The system shall require each user to log on with a unique username and a password.
N-REQ-008: The system shall generate usernames as the first four characters of the employee’s last name followed by the first three characters of their first name.
N-REQ-009: The system shall assign a password to each user.
N-REQ-010: The system shall require passwords to contain 6 to 8 alphanumeric characters.
N-REQ-011: The system shall lock out a user after three unsuccessful password attempts until their password is reset by an administrator.
N-REQ-012: The system shall provide functionality for assigning a new password if a user forgets their existing one.
N-REQ-013: The system shall automatically log off a user after 10 minutes of inactivity.

systemFeatures.name: Customer & Child Management
systemFeatures.description: This feature facilitates the comprehensive management of parent and child information, serving as the central repository for all customer-related data within the **nenios** system. It supports the full lifecycle of customer data, from initial registration to updates, ensuring accurate and accessible records for day-to-day operations and reporting. By centralizing this information, the system improves efficiency for administrators and provides vital details to teachers and assistants, enhancing the quality of care and administrative oversight. The priority for this feature is High, as it underpins most operational aspects of the child care center.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Administrator registers a new parent or updates an existing parent's information. Response: System stores or modifies parent contact, billing, and emergency details, including authorized pick-up persons.
Stimulus: Administrator creates a new child record or updates an existing one. Response: System records child's demographics, special needs, photo, assigned classroom, and immunization status.
Stimulus: Employee logs correspondence with a parent. Response: System records the date and comments in the customer log notes.
functionalRequirements: N-REQ-014: The system shall record the user ID, date, time, and a description of changes whenever an account is edited.
N-REQ-015: The system shall store the Parent’s record containing name, address, home phone, work phone, email address, emergency contact, and names of authorized pick-up persons.
N-REQ-016: The system shall store the Child’s record containing name, date of birth, gender, classroom name, special needs, photo, teacher’s comments, and immunization dates.
N-REQ-017: The system shall support the ability to enter, store, and update parent, child, and billing information.
N-REQ-018: The system shall store customer log notes with the date and comments for all correspondence with a parent.

systemFeatures.name: Classroom & Enrollment Management
systemFeatures.description: This feature provides the necessary tools for managing classroom capacities, assigning children to classes, and maintaining waiting lists. It ensures optimal utilization of classroom resources while adhering to capacity limits, directly impacting operational efficiency and compliance. By streamlining enrollment and waitlist processes, **nenios** helps administrators manage new child admissions effectively and provides clarity on classroom availability, contributing to better planning and customer service. This feature holds a High priority due to its direct impact on center operations and resource allocation.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Administrator attempts to enroll a child into a full classroom. Response: System prevents enrollment and suggests adding the child to the classroom waiting list.
Stimulus: Administrator assigns a teacher and assistant to a classroom. Response: System updates classroom staffing records.
Stimulus: Classroom space becomes available. Response: System allows administrator to enroll children from the waiting list.
functionalRequirements: N-REQ-019: The system shall restrict each classroom to a maximum capacity of 20 children.
N-REQ-020: The system shall ensure that each student belongs to only one class at any given time.
N-REQ-021: The system shall assign one teacher and one assistant to each class.
N-REQ-022: The system shall maintain a waiting list that can store up to 100 potential customers, including names and phone numbers, awaiting a classroom opening.

systemFeatures.name: Daily Reminders
systemFeatures.description: This feature provides a personal reminder system for all employees within the **nenios** application, enabling them to create and manage their daily tasks and important events. By delivering timely pop-up notifications upon login, the system helps employees stay organized and informed about critical activities, minimizing the chances of missed appointments or forgotten tasks. This functionality enhances internal communication and operational efficiency, contributing to a smoother daily workflow. The priority for this feature is Medium, offering significant convenience though not strictly critical for core child care operations.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Employee sets a reminder with a specific date and message. Response: System stores the reminder linked to the employee's profile.
Stimulus: Employee logs into the system on the date of a scheduled reminder. Response: System displays a pop-up window with the reminder message.
Stimulus: Employee acknowledges a displayed reminder. Response: System permanently removes the reminder from the database.
functionalRequirements: N-REQ-023: The system shall allow users to set daily reminders only for themselves.
N-REQ-024: The system shall provide a calendar interface for selecting the date of a reminder.
N-REQ-025: The system shall allow users to enter a message of up to 50 characters for their reminder.
N-REQ-026: The system shall display a pop-up window with the reminder message when the user logs onto the system on the reminder date.

systemFeatures.name: Immunization Tracking
systemFeatures.description: This feature manages the vital health records of children, specifically tracking immunization dates and physical examination schedules. It ensures that administrators and teachers are aware of upcoming or overdue immunizations, promoting child health and regulatory compliance. By integrating immunization notifications into monthly invoices and daily teacher logins, **nenios** automates critical health alerts, reducing administrative burden and providing peace of mind to parents. This feature is of High priority due to its importance for child welfare and legal compliance.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Administrator enters a child's immunization record or last physical examination date. Response: System updates the child's health record.
Stimulus: Teacher logs into the system and a child under their care is within two weeks of a required immunization. Response: System displays a notification message to the teacher.
Stimulus: Administrator generates monthly invoices. Response: System includes a special notice on the invoice if a child is due for a required immunization.
functionalRequirements: N-REQ-027: The system shall record the dates of received required immunizations in a child’s record.
N-REQ-028: The system shall display a message to the child’s teacher when they log onto the system if the child is within two weeks of a required immunization.
N-REQ-029: The system shall record the date of a child’s last physical examination in their record.
N-REQ-030: The system shall allow administrators to enter the type and schedule of required immunizations.
N-REQ-031: The system shall print a notification for required immunizations on the monthly invoice.

systemFeatures.name: Billing & Invoicing
systemFeatures.description: This feature automates the complex process of monthly billing, generating itemized invoices, and maintaining a detailed account history for each customer. It applies discounts for multiple children and accurately calculates charges for late pick-ups, ensuring fair and transparent billing practices. By streamlining financial operations, **nenios** reduces manual errors, saves administrative time, and provides clear financial statements to parents, which is crucial for the child care center's financial health. The priority for this feature is High, as it directly impacts revenue generation and financial record-keeping.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: System processes monthly billing at the end of each month. Response: System calculates charges based on the number of enrolled children, applies discounts, and includes late pick-up fees.
Stimulus: Administrator requests to print itemized invoices for a customer. Response: System generates and displays the invoice, including total amount due and any immunization notices.
Stimulus: Administrator views a customer's account. Response: System displays a detailed history of all financial activities by date.
functionalRequirements: N-REQ-032: The system shall bill a parent $90 at the end of each month if only a single child is enrolled.
N-REQ-033: The system shall bill a parent $157.50 at the end of each month if two children are enrolled.
N-REQ-034: The system shall bill a parent $157.50 for the first two children plus $45 for each additional child at the end of each month if more than two children are enrolled.
N-REQ-035: The system shall maintain an account history for each customer, detailing all activity by date.
N-REQ-036: The system shall provide itemized customer invoices, showing the amount due, available for printing.
N-REQ-037: The system shall bill customers $10 an hour per child for late pick-ups.
N-REQ-038: The system shall make all invoices available for printing at the end of each month.

systemFeatures.name: Reporting
systemFeatures.description: This feature enables administrators to generate a variety of pre-formatted reports summarizing key customer and operational information. These reports provide valuable insights into enrollment, demographics, health status, and financial activity, supporting informed decision-making and operational transparency. By offering comprehensive reporting capabilities, **nenios** empowers the child care center to analyze its operations, communicate effectively with parents, and fulfill administrative requirements efficiently. The priority for this feature is High, given its importance for business analysis and external communication.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Administrator requests a 'Customer Directory Report'. Response: System generates an alphabetical list of customers with address and phone number.
Stimulus: Administrator requests an 'Immunization Due & History Report'. Response: System generates a list of children with required immunizations and past dates.
Stimulus: Administrator requests an 'Account Activity Report' for a selected customer. Response: System displays a chronological list of financial activities for that customer.
functionalRequirements: N-REQ-039: The system shall generate a 'Customer Directory Report' listing customers alphabetically by last name, including their address and phone number.
N-REQ-040: The system shall generate a 'Daily Classroom & Center Enrollment Report' alphabetically listing children by classroom, indicating their presence or absence.
N-REQ-041: The system shall generate a 'Birthday Lists & Child Age Report' listing children alphabetically by last name, including their birthday and age.
N-REQ-042: The system shall generate a 'Parent/Child Cross Reference Report' listing parents alphabetically by last name, including their children’s first names.
N-REQ-043: The system shall generate a 'Family Registration Information Report' listing customer names alphabetically, including their address and home telephone number.

N-REQ-044: The system shall generate an 'Immunization Due & History Report' listing children alphabetically by last name, including any remaining required immunizations and dates of previous immunizations.
N-REQ-045: The system shall generate a 'Child Enrollment/Withdrawal Report' listing children alphabetically by last name, indicating their registration or withdrawal status.
N-REQ-046: The system shall generate a 'Child Notes & Comments Report' listing children alphabetically by last name, including any notes attached to their records.
N-REQ-047: The system shall generate an 'Account Activity Report' listing a selected customer’s account activity by date.

performanceRequirements: N-REQ-048: The system shall respond to all user requests within 20 seconds. Rationale: This ensures a satisfactory user experience and maintains operational efficiency, especially during peak usage times when administrative staff require quick access to information and functionalities.

N-REQ-049: The system shall support a minimum of 50 concurrent users without degradation in response time. Rationale: This ensures that all employees can access and utilize the system simultaneously without experiencing performance bottlenecks, crucial for real-time operations like attendance logging.
safetyRequirements: N-REQ-050: The system shall implement data validation for all data entry fields to prevent erroneous or incomplete information from being stored. Rationale: This ensures the integrity and accuracy of sensitive child and parent records, which is critical for child safety, billing accuracy, and regulatory compliance.

N-REQ-051: The system shall restrict the authorization of child pickups to only those individuals explicitly listed in the child's record. Rationale: This is a fundamental safety measure to prevent unauthorized individuals from picking up children, upholding the security and welfare of the children in care.
securityRequirements: N-REQ-052: The system shall encrypt all sensitive data at rest and in transit, including personal identifiable information (PII), financial records, and health data. Rationale: This protects confidential information from unauthorized access, complying with data privacy regulations and building trust with parents and guardians.

N-REQ-053: The system shall enforce a strong password policy requiring a mix of uppercase, lowercase, numbers, and special characters, with a minimum length of 8 characters, and password expiration every 90 days. Rationale: This enhances protection against brute-force attacks and unauthorized access, strengthening overall system security beyond the basic requirement for alphanumeric passwords.

N-REQ-054: The system shall implement activity logging for all administrative actions, including data modifications, report generation, and access control changes. Rationale: This provides an auditable trail for accountability, troubleshooting, and forensic analysis in case of security incidents, ensuring transparency and compliance.
softwareQualityAttributes: N-REQ-055: Usability: The system shall provide a web-based interface that is intuitive and easy to navigate for users with varying technical proficiencies. Rationale: High usability reduces training time, minimizes user errors, and increases overall employee satisfaction and efficiency, particularly for teachers and assistants focused on child care.

N-REQ-056: Reliability: The system shall maintain 99.9% uptime during operational hours (6:00 AM - 8:00 PM local time). Rationale: High reliability ensures continuous access to critical information and functionalities, preventing disruptions in daily child care operations like attendance tracking and emergency contact retrieval.

N-REQ-057: Maintainability: The system shall be developed using clear, modular code structures and well-documented APIs. Rationale: This facilitates easier identification and resolution of defects, straightforward implementation of enhancements, and reduces the cost of long-term maintenance and future development.

N-REQ-058: Testability: The system shall provide clear interfaces and logging mechanisms to support automated and manual testing of all functional and non-functional requirements. Rationale: Enhanced testability ensures that changes or new features can be thoroughly validated, reducing the risk of introducing defects and improving software quality.

N-REQ-059: Adaptability: The system shall be designed with configuration options that allow administrators to adjust certain business rules, such as billing rates or notification thresholds, without requiring code changes. Rationale: This allows the child care center to adapt to changing operational policies or regulatory requirements with minimal technical intervention, enhancing flexibility and responsiveness.
businessRules: N-REQ-060: All billing calculations must accurately reflect the tiered pricing structure based on the number of children enrolled per family and apply late pick-up fees as specified. Rationale: Ensures financial accuracy and consistent application of pricing policies.
N-REQ-061: Classroom assignments must strictly adhere to the maximum capacity of 20 children per classroom. Rationale: Guarantees compliance with safety regulations and optimal child-to-staff ratios.
N-REQ-062: Each child can only be assigned to one classroom at any given time. Rationale: Simplifies attendance tracking, clarifies responsibility, and prevents scheduling conflicts.
N-REQ-063: A minimum of one teacher and one assistant must be assigned to each active classroom. Rationale: Ensures adequate supervision and care for children in each class.

N-REQ-064: The waiting list shall be managed on a first-come, first-served basis for classroom openings, unless otherwise specified by administrative policy. Rationale: Provides transparency and fairness in the enrollment process for prospective customers.

glossary.definition: A high-privileged user role within the **nenios** system with comprehensive access and control over all functionalities, including customer, child, billing, and reporting management.
A free, cross-platform, open-source framework for building web apps and services with .NET and C#.
The official designation for the **nenios** software, signifying its role in managing child care center operations.
HyperText Markup Language, the standard markup language for documents designed to be displayed in a web browser.
The process whereby a person is made immune or resistant to an infectious disease, typically by the administration of a vaccine. Records are tracked within **nenios**.
Personally Identifiable Information, any data that could potentially identify a specific individual. This data requires stringent security measures.
A restricted user role within the **nenios** system, primarily responsible for direct child care activities such as attendance logging, behavior documentation, and utilizing daily reminders.

A computer program that stores web content and responds to requests from web browsers, delivering web pages and other files. For **nenios**, it specifically refers to a server supporting **Microsoft ASP.NET**.

flowchartDiagram.syntaxExplanation: Flowcharts illustrate a process or workflow using various node shapes for different types of steps and arrows for transitions. The `flowchart` keyword initiates the diagram, followed by the direction (`TD` for top-down or `LR` for left-right). Nodes are defined by an alphanumeric ID followed by their label, which MUST be double-quoted if it contains spaces or special characters. Arrows define transitions between nodes. Decision nodes (`{}`) represent choices, with labels on the outgoing paths indicating the decision outcome.
flowchartDiagram.code:
flowchart TD
  StartProcess["Enrollment Inquiry Received"] --> CheckRoomAvailability{"Check Classroom Availability?"}
  CheckRoomAvailability -- "Yes, Available" --> NewOrExisting{"New or Existing Customer?"}
  NewOrExisting -- "New Customer" --> CreateNewAccount["Create New Parent Account"]
  NewOrExisting -- "Existing Customer" --> UpdateExistingAccount["Edit Existing Parent Account"]
  CreateNewAccount --> AddChildRecord["Add Child Details"]
  UpdateExistingAccount --> AddChildRecord
  AddChildRecord --> AssignToClass["Assign Child to Classroom"]
  AssignToClass --> EnrollmentComplete["Enrollment Complete"]
  CheckRoomAvailability -- "No, Full" --> AddToWaitlist["Add Child to Waiting List"]
  AddToWaitlist --> EndProcess("End")
  EnrollmentComplete --> EndProcess
flowchartDiagram.caption: Child Enrollment and Classroom Assignment Process

sequenceDiagram.syntaxExplanation: Sequence diagrams model the interactions between objects or participants in a time-ordered sequence. The `sequenceDiagram` keyword starts the diagram. `participant` defines an entity, optionally with an alias (e.g., `participant E as Employee`). Arrows (`->>`, `-->>`) represent messages, with `+` and `-` suffixes indicating activation and deactivation. `alt`/`else`/`end` blocks represent alternative paths, and `opt`/`end` blocks represent optional interactions. Message labels MUST avoid special characters like `{}`.
sequenceDiagram.code:
sequenceDiagram
  participant Emp as Employee
  participant Sys as "nenios System"
  participant DB as Database

  Emp->>+Sys: "Enter Login Credentials"
  Sys->>DB: "Verify Employee Credentials"
  DB-->>Sys: "Return Employee Profile"

  alt Valid Credentials
    Sys-->>+Emp: "Login Successful"
    Sys->>DB: "Retrieve Today's Reminders"
    DB-->>Sys: "Return Reminder List"

    opt Reminders Exist
      Sys-->>Emp: "Display Reminder Popup"
      Emp->>Sys: "Acknowledge Reminder"
      Sys->>DB: "Mark Reminder as Acknowledged"
      DB-->>Sys: "Confirmation"
    end
    Sys-->>-Emp: "Show Dashboard"
  else Invalid Credentials
    Sys-->>-Emp: "Display Login Error"
  end
sequenceDiagram.caption: Employee Login and Daily Reminder Display

entityRelationshipDiagram.syntaxExplanation: Entity Relationship Diagrams (ERDs) visualize the logical structure of a database, showing entities (tables), their attributes, and the relationships between them. The `erDiagram` keyword starts the diagram. Entities are declared by name, followed by their attributes (type name [PK|FK|UK] ["comment"]). Relationships are defined with cardinality (e.g., `||--o{` for one-to-many, optional side), and the relationship label MUST be quoted. Foreign keys (FK) in an entity typically represent a many-to-one relationship to the entity they reference.
entityRelationshipDiagram.code:
erDiagram
    Employee ||--o{ ChildComment : "documents"
    Employee ||--o{ Reminder : "sets"
    Parent ||--o{ Child : "has"
    Parent ||--o{ Invoice : "receives"
    Parent ||--o{ AccountHistory : "maintains"
    Child ||--o{ AuthorizedPickup : "can be picked up by"
    Child ||--o{ ImmunizationRecord : "has"
    Child ||--|| Classroom : "belongs to"
    Classroom ||--o{ WaitingListEntry : "has space for"
    Classroom ||--o{ Employee : "staffs"
    Invoice ||--o{ BillingItem : "includes"
    ImmunizationType ||--o{ ImmunizationRecord : "is of type"
    ChildComment ||--o{ Employee : "authored by"

    Employee {
        int id PK
        string username UK
        string password
        string job_classification
    }

    Parent {
        int id PK
        string name
        string address
        string home_phone
        string work_phone
        string email_address
        string emergency_contact
    }

    Child {
        int id PK
        int parent_id FK
        int classroom_id FK
        string name
        date date_of_birth
        string gender
        string special_needs
        string photo_url
    }

    AuthorizedPickup {
        int id PK
        int child_id FK
        int parent_id FK
        string name
    }

    Classroom {
        int id PK
        string name UK
        int max_capacity
    }

    WaitingListEntry {
        int id PK
        int child_id FK
        int classroom_id FK
        date date_added
    }

    ImmunizationRecord {
        int id PK
        int child_id FK
        int immunization_type_id FK
        date date_received
    }

    ImmunizationType {
        int id PK
        string name UK
        string schedule
    }

    Reminder {
        int id PK
        int employee_id FK
        date reminder_date
        string message
    }

    Invoice {
        int id PK
        int parent_id FK
        decimal amount_due
        date billing_date
        string status
    }

    BillingItem {
        int id PK
        int invoice_id FK
        string description
        decimal amount
    }

    AccountHistory {
        int id PK
        int parent_id FK
        datetime transaction_date
        string description
        decimal amount
    }

    ChildComment {
        int id PK
        int child_id FK
        int employee_id FK
        string comment
        datetime date
    }
entityRelationshipDiagram.caption: Core Entity Relationships for nenios

revisionHistory.description: Initial Release based on NANC FRS Version 3.0.23
revisionHistory.author: SRA System

introduction.purpose: The primary objective of the **npac** Service Management System (SMS) is to define the functional requirements necessary to enable Service Provider Portability across North America. This document serves as a comprehensive guide for developers and stakeholders, outlining the system's capabilities in managing telephone number portability. It provides a foundational understanding of the **npac** SMS platform, detailing its core functionalities and operational scope for telecommunication service providers.
introduction.documentConventions: This document adheres to the IEEE 830-1998 standard for Software Requirements Specifications. Key terms and acronyms are defined within the document's glossary. Statements denoting mandatory requirements are identified by the term 'shall', indicating actions or conditions that must be implemented and verifiable through system testing. Informative statements, preferences, or guidance are indicated by terms such as 'is', 'will', or 'should', and do not represent mandatory deliverables. Requirements, assumptions, and constraints are explicitly labeled using prefixes such as A-, C-, or R-, along with a numerical identifier for traceability.
introduction.intendedAudience: This document is primarily intended for software engineers, system architects, quality assurance personnel, and project managers involved in the design, development, testing, and deployment of the **npac** system. It also serves as a critical reference for telecommunication service providers, regulatory bodies like the North American Numbering Council (NANC), and other stakeholders requiring a detailed understanding of the **npac** SMS functionalities and interfaces. Business analysts and operational staff may also find this document valuable for understanding the underlying processes of number portability administration.
introduction.productScope: The **npac** SMS constitutes a robust hardware and software platform designed to facilitate the porting of telephone numbers between service providers. Its scope encompasses the management of subscription versions, broadcasting of network data updates, handling of provisioning, disconnect, repair, and conflict resolution processes, and ensuring data integrity and security for ported numbers. The system also includes functionalities for audit requests, report generation, disaster recovery, and data administration, specifically focusing on interactions with Service Order Administration (SOA) systems and Local SMS platforms. It does not cover the customer authorization process for porting.
references: IEEE 830-1998 IEEE Recommended Practice for Software Requirements Specifications.
ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018 Systems and software engineering — Life cycle processes — Requirements engineering.
NPAC SMS Interoperable Interface Specification (IIS), Version 3.0.2, December 4, 2000.
Illinois Commerce Commission Number Portability Administration Center and Service Management System Request for Proposal (ICC NPAC/SMS RFP), February 6, 1996.
Generic Requirements for SCP Application and GTT Function for Number Portability, ICC LNP Workshop SCP Generic Requirements Subcommittee.
Generic Switching and Signaling Requirements for Number Portability, version 1.03, ICC LNP Workshop Switch Generic Requirements Subcommittee, September 4, 1996.
Report on Local Number Portability, Industry Numbering Committee (INC).
FCC 96-286 First Report And Order, CC Docket No. 95-116, July 2, 1996.
CTIA Report on Wireless Portability Version 2, July 7, 1998.

overallDescription.productPerspective: The **npac** Service Management System (SMS) is presented as a new, self-contained product critical for the North American telecommunications landscape, enabling Local Number Portability (LNP). It functions as the central clearinghouse for ported telephone numbers, replacing manual or less efficient methods. The system operates as an intermediary, receiving information from various Service Providers and disseminating updated routing data to ensure seamless call routing for ported numbers. This central role positions **npac** as an indispensable component within the broader telecommunications infrastructure, requiring robust and highly available interfaces with external Service Order Administration (SOA) systems and Local SMS platforms operated by individual service providers.
productFunctions: Provisioning Service Functionality: Manages the end-to-end process of porting a telephone number from an old Service Provider to a new one, including validation, matching, and activation.
Disconnect Service Functionality: Handles the process of disconnecting ported numbers, ensuring they are removed from the ported number database and routing is restored to non-ported status.
Repair Service Functionality: Provides tools and processes for identifying and correcting inaccuracies in ported number data, facilitating data synchronization across Service Providers.
Conflict Resolution Functionality: Manages situations where Service Providers disagree on service provision for a telephone number, guiding them through a structured resolution process.

Disaster Recovery and Backup Functionality: Ensures continuous operation and data integrity through robust backup and recovery mechanisms, allowing for failover to a backup system in case of primary system failure.
Order Cancellation Functionality: Supports the cancellation of pending number porting requests at various stages, ensuring appropriate notifications are sent to involved Service Providers.
Audit Request Functionality: Allows Service Providers to request audits of subscription version data to verify synchronization and identify discrepancies across various Local SMS databases.
Report Request Functionality: Enables Service Providers to generate and receive various operational reports pertaining to number portability activities and system performance.
Data Management Functionality: Manages and maintains critical data sets including **npac** Network Data, Service Provider Data, and Subscription Version Data, ensuring accuracy and consistency.
NPA-NXX Split Processing: Supports the administrative and data management aspects associated with changes to Numbering Plan Area (NPA) and NXX codes.
Number Pooling Overview: Incorporates functionalities to support national number pooling initiatives, managing number blocks and their allocation among Service Providers.

userClassesAndCharacteristics.characteristics: These users initiate porting requests, send activation notices, and receive updates. They typically possess moderate to high technical expertise, understanding telecommunications protocols and local number portability processes. They are regular users of the system, relying on its efficiency for customer activation and service delivery. They primarily interact with the **npac** SMS through their Service Order Administration (SOA) systems, requiring robust and reliable interfaces.

These users respond to porting requests, provide concurrence for number ports, and receive notifications regarding disconnects or conflicts. They have similar technical expertise to New SP personnel and also interact primarily via their SOA systems. Their role is crucial in the initial stages of a port to validate requests and ensure smooth transitions, often involved in conflict resolution scenarios.

These users manage system configurations, perform data administration tasks, monitor system health, and handle disaster recovery procedures. They possess high technical expertise in system administration, database management, and network operations. They are frequent users, utilizing specialized administrative interfaces to maintain the system's operational integrity, security, and data accuracy. They are responsible for privilege management and logging request denials.

These users oversee the **npac** system's compliance with telecommunication regulations and policies. They are less frequent users, primarily consuming reports and audit results to ensure fair and consistent number portability practices. They possess high-level understanding of regulatory frameworks and rely on the system's reporting capabilities for oversight and policy enforcement.

overallDescription.operatingEnvironment: The **npac** SMS is designed to operate as a high-availability, mission-critical platform within a secure data center environment. It requires robust server hardware, capable of handling significant transaction volumes and maintaining continuous operation. The system will rely on a high-performance operating system, likely a variant of **UNIX** or **Linux**, optimized for enterprise applications and database management. Data storage will be managed by a robust relational database management system, ensuring data integrity and rapid access. Redundancy is a fundamental requirement, necessitating failover capabilities across all critical hardware and software components to ensure uninterrupted service.
designAndImplementationConstraints: The **npac** system shall adhere to the terms of the GNU General Public License (GPL) for this document and any derived modifications, ensuring its non-proprietary nature. This requires that any redistribution or modification of the documentation must also be subject to the GPL.

The system shall comply with all relevant regulatory policies and industry standards, including those from the FCC, CTIA, and Industry Numbering Committee (INC), as outlined in the related publications.

Performance shall meet specified response times for critical transactions, particularly for subscription version creation, activation, and network data broadcasts, to ensure efficient number portability operations.
Security measures shall conform to the detailed requirements outlined in Section 7, including specific protocols for identification, authentication, access control, and data encryption.

The system shall interface with various Service Provider Service Order Administration (SOA) systems and Local SMS databases, requiring adherence to the **npac** SMS Interoperable Interface Specification (IIS).
Specific communication protocols, such as **CMIP**, shall be utilized for critical inter-system communications, necessitating strict compliance with defined protocol requirements.
The system must accommodate **NPA-NXX splits** and **number pooling** functionalities, ensuring adaptability to evolving numbering plan policies and requirements.
All system components, including hardware, operating system, and database, shall support high availability and disaster recovery mechanisms to maintain continuous service.
userDocumentation: The **npac** system shall be delivered with comprehensive user manuals tailored for Service Provider personnel, detailing the process for initiating and managing number portability requests.
Online help documentation shall be available within the administrative interface for **npac** personnel, providing context-sensitive guidance for configuration, monitoring, and data management tasks.
Tutorials or quick-start guides shall be provided for common operations such as subscription version creation and conflict resolution.
Detailed file format specifications and examples for download files, such as Subscription Download File and Network Service Provider Download File, shall be included, as outlined in Appendix E.
Documentation regarding encryption key exchange protocols and procedures, as detailed in Appendix D, shall be provided for Service Providers to ensure secure communication.
assumptionsAndDependencies: It is assumed that Service Providers will obtain explicit customer authorization for number porting through their internal processes, as the **npac** system is not involved in verifying customer approval.
The integrity and availability of Service Providers' Service Order Administration (SOA) systems and Local SMS databases are assumed for successful interoperation with the **npac** SMS.
The system depends on the continued adherence by Service Providers to the **npac** SMS Interoperable Interface Specification (IIS) for all communication protocols and data exchange formats.
Regulatory bodies, such as the NANC, are assumed to provide timely and clear directives regarding number portability policies and numbering plan changes, which the **npac** system must accommodate.
Assumed is the availability of a reliable and secure network infrastructure for communications between the **npac** SMS and all connected Service Provider systems.

The system relies on external clock synchronization mechanisms to ensure consistent timestamps across all distributed components and interacting systems, particularly for time-sensitive operations like activations and disconnects.

externalInterfaceRequirements.userInterfaces: The **npac** system shall provide a web-based **Administrative Dashboard** for **npac** personnel, designed with a clean and intuitive graphical user interface (GUI). This interface shall support secure login, role-based access control, and provide functionalities for data administration, system monitoring, audit report generation, and configuration management. Standard GUI elements such as navigation menus, search fields, data tables with sorting and filtering, and action buttons for creating, modifying, or deleting records shall be implemented. Error messages shall be clear, concise, and provide actionable feedback to the user. For Service Providers who opt for it, a secure **Low-Tech Interface** will be available for submitting specific requests and receiving reports, potentially via email, fax, or secure file transfer, adhering to predefined message formatting and security standards.
externalInterfaceRequirements.hardwareInterfaces: The **npac** system shall interface with standard enterprise-grade server hardware, including rack-mounted servers, network storage appliances (e.g., **SAN** or **NAS**), and network interface cards capable of high-throughput data transfer. It must support redundant power supplies and network connections for high availability. The system shall leverage standard **Ethernet** network protocols for internal and external communications. While specific hardware manufacturers are not mandated, the system software must be compatible with **x86-64 architecture** processors and industry-standard memory configurations. The system will not directly interface with telecommunication switching hardware but will interact with Local SMS systems that manage these connections.
externalInterfaceRequirements.softwareInterfaces: The **npac** SMS shall interface with Service Provider **Service Order Administration (SOA)** systems and **Local SMS** platforms through a defined Application Programming Interface (API), adhering strictly to the **npac** SMS Interoperable Interface Specification (IIS). This interface will facilitate the exchange of `Subscription Version` data, `Network Data`, and `Notification` messages. The system shall also interface with external **database systems** (e.g., **PostgreSQL** or **Oracle**) for persistent storage of `Number Block`, `Service Provider`, and `Audit Log` data. Communication with these external software components shall utilize robust, fault-tolerant protocols, ensuring message delivery and data consistency. Encryption key exchange, as detailed in Appendix D, will be a critical aspect of securing these software interfaces.
externalInterfaceRequirements.communicationsInterfaces: The **npac** system shall support various communication protocols for interacting with Service Providers and for internal operations. Primary real-time data exchange, such as subscription version activations and network data broadcasts, shall utilize the **Common Management Information Protocol (CMIP)**, ensuring reliable and secure message delivery. For administrative reports and certain non-real-time data transfers, the system shall support **secure file transfer protocols (SFTP)**, **email (SMTP with TLS)**, and potentially fax or secure printing for low-tech interfaces. All external communications shall incorporate **encryption (e.g., TLS/SSL)** and **digital signatures** to ensure data confidentiality, integrity, and non-repudiation. Communication standards, message formatting, data transfer rates, and synchronization mechanisms for all interfaces must comply with the **npac** SMS IIS.

systemFeatures.name: Number Portability Provisioning
systemFeatures.description: This feature facilitates the complete lifecycle of provisioning a telephone number port between two Service Providers. It significantly enhances user experience by automating the complex coordination required for number transfers, reducing manual errors and accelerating activation times. Technically, it integrates with external Service Order Administration (SOA) systems, performing critical data validation and synchronization across distributed networks. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: New Service Provider sends `Subscription Version Create` request to **npac** SMS. Response: **npac** SMS validates request and initiates `Pending Old SP Concurrence` status.
Stimulus: Old Service Provider sends `Concurrence` message for a pending Subscription Version. Response: **npac** SMS updates `Subscription Version` status and prepares for activation.

Stimulus: New Service Provider sends `Activate` message for a `Subscription Version`. Response: **npac** SMS broadcasts `Network Data Update` to all relevant Service Providers and updates `Subscription Version` to `Active`.
functionalRequirements: N-REQ-001: The system shall receive `Subscription Version Create` requests from New Service Providers via the SOA interface.
N-REQ-002: The system shall validate the incoming `Subscription Version Create` request for data format, completeness, and against existing **npac** data.
N-REQ-003: The system shall generate a `Missing/Late Notification` to the Old Service Provider if concurrence is not received within a defined timeframe.
N-REQ-004: The system shall accept `Concurrence` notifications from the Old Service Provider for a pending `Subscription Version`.
N-REQ-005: The system shall allow the New Service Provider to send an `Activate` message for a `Subscription Version`.
N-REQ-006: The system shall broadcast the updated `Network Data` to all relevant Service Providers upon successful activation.
N-REQ-007: The system shall record all transmission failures during network data broadcast and initiate appropriate recovery procedures.
N-REQ-008: The system shall log all `Subscription Version` creation and activation transactions in the history file.

systemFeatures.name: Number Portability Disconnection
systemFeatures.description: This feature manages the process of deactivating a previously ported telephone number, returning it to a non-ported state. It streamlines the customer service experience by ensuring that disconnected numbers are correctly removed from the porting database, preventing misrouting or service issues. From a technical perspective, it involves updating central **npac** records and broadcasting disconnect information to all relevant Service Providers for network synchronization. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Current Service Provider sends `Disconnect` update for an active `Subscription Version`. Response: **npac** SMS validates the request and sets `Subscription Version` to `Pending Disconnect`.

Stimulus: Effective Release Date is reached for a `Pending Disconnect` `Subscription Version`. Response: **npac** SMS broadcasts `Disconnect Network Data` and removes the number from its active ported database.
Stimulus: Local SMS acknowledges `Disconnect Network Data` broadcast. Response: **npac** SMS logs successful update and transaction history.
functionalRequirements: N-REQ-009: The system shall receive `Disconnect` updates from the current Service Provider for active `Subscription Versions`.
N-REQ-010: The system shall validate the `Disconnect` request against the existing `Subscription Version` data.
N-REQ-011: The system shall broadcast the `Disconnect Network Data` to all relevant Service Providers on the specified `Effective Release Date`.
N-REQ-012: The system shall remove the telephone number from its database of active ported numbers upon successful disconnect broadcast.
N-REQ-013: The system shall log all `Disconnect` updates and broadcasts in the history file.
N-REQ-014: The system shall ensure calls to the disconnected number are routed as a non-ported number after successful processing.

systemFeatures.name: Conflict Resolution Management
systemFeatures.description: This feature provides a structured mechanism for resolving disagreements between Service Providers regarding the ownership or status of a ported telephone number. It is crucial for maintaining data consistency and preventing service disruptions for end-users, thereby improving operational efficiency for Service Providers. Technically, it involves placing a `Subscription Version` into a `Conflict` state, enabling coordinated actions by Service Providers, and managing state transitions until the conflict is resolved. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: **npac** SMS detects disagreement between Service Providers on a `Subscription Version` (e.g., non-concurrence or missing acknowledgment). Response: **npac** SMS places the `Subscription Version` into a `Conflict` status.
Stimulus: Service Provider requests conflict status for a `Subscription Version`. Response: **npac** SMS provides the current conflict status and related details.

Stimulus: New Service Provider coordinates conflict resolution and issues a `Cancel Pending` notification. Response: **npac** SMS processes cancellation and removes the `Subscription Version` from conflict.
functionalRequirements: N-REQ-015: The system shall identify and flag `Subscription Versions` as `Conflict` when Service Providers provide conflicting information or fail to concur.
N-REQ-016: The system shall provide Service Providers with the current `Conflict Status` for any `Subscription Version` upon request.
N-REQ-017: The system shall manage the state transitions of a `Subscription Version` through various `Conflict` substates.
N-REQ-018: The system shall process `Cancel Pending` notifications from the New Service Provider during conflict resolution.
N-REQ-019: The system shall remove a `Subscription Version` from `Conflict` status once the underlying issues are resolved and confirmed by involved parties.
N-REQ-020: The system shall notify both Service Providers of `Subscription Version` status changes during conflict resolution.

systemFeatures.name: Audit and Reporting
systemFeatures.description: This feature allows Service Providers to audit their Local SMS data against **npac** SMS records and generates various operational reports. It enhances data integrity by proactively identifying discrepancies and provides critical insights into number portability activities for both Service Providers and regulatory bodies. Technically, it involves querying multiple databases, performing data comparisons, and securely delivering formatted reports through various channels. Priority: Medium.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Service Provider requests an audit for a range of telephone numbers. Response: **npac** SMS issues queries to appropriate Local SMS databases to retrieve current data.
Stimulus: **npac** SMS completes data comparison and identifies discrepancies. Response: **npac** SMS generates an `Audit Discrepancy Report` and sends it to the requesting Service Provider.
Stimulus: Service Provider requests an operational report. Response: **npac** SMS generates the requested report based on predefined criteria and delivers it via the specified channel.
functionalRequirements: N-REQ-021: The system shall enable Service Providers to initiate `Audit Requests` for `Subscription Version` data.
N-REQ-022: The system shall query relevant Local SMS databases to compare data against **npac** SMS records during an audit.
N-REQ-023: The system shall identify and log discrepancies found between **npac** SMS data and Local SMS data.
N-REQ-024: The system shall generate `Audit Discrepancy Reports` detailing identified inconsistencies.
N-REQ-025: The system shall transmit `Audit Reports` to the requesting Service Provider via secure channels.
N-REQ-026: The system shall support various types of operational reports for `Number Portability` activities.
N-REQ-027: The system shall allow Service Providers to specify criteria for report generation, such as date range or Service Provider ID.
N-REQ-028: The system shall deliver generated reports via the `NPAC Administrative` interface, `SOA Low-Tech Interface`, **email**, or **electronic file transfer** as specified by the user.

systemFeatures.name: Number Pooling Management
systemFeatures.description: This feature provides functionalities for managing number pooling, including the administration of NPA-NXX-X blocks and their associated data. It supports the efficient allocation and utilization of telephone numbers, which is critical for conserving numbering resources and complying with regulatory mandates. Technically, this involves specific data models for `Number Pool Block Holder Information` and the implementation of processes for block creation, modification, deletion, and bulk data downloads. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: **npac** personnel initiate the creation of a new `Number Pool Block`. Response: **npac** SMS validates the request and schedules the block creation, notifying relevant Service Providers.
Stimulus: Service Provider performs a query for `NPA-NXX-X Holder Information`. Response: **npac** SMS retrieves and presents detailed information regarding number blocks and their assignments.

Stimulus: Service Provider requests a `Bulk Data Download` for `NPA-NXX-X` or `Block Data`. Response: **npac** SMS generates the data file and delivers it in **GMT** via a secure electronic file transfer.
functionalRequirements: N-REQ-029: The system shall manage `NPA-NXX-X Holder Information`, including addition, modification, and deletion of records.
N-REQ-030: The system shall perform validation for block creation, ensuring adherence to numbering plan rules and existing `Subscription Versions`.
N-REQ-031: The system shall allow **npac** personnel to schedule and reschedule block creation activities.
N-REQ-032: The system shall support queries for `NPA-NXX-X Holder Information` by Service Providers.
N-REQ-033: The system shall generate `First Port Notifications` for newly created blocks.
N-REQ-034: The system shall provide `Bulk Data Download` functionality for `NPA-NXX-X` and `Block Data`.
N-REQ-035: The system shall deliver bulk data download files in **Greenwich Mean Time (GMT)** format.
N-REQ-036: The system shall support `Block Holder` administration, including general information, addition, modification, deletion, and default routing restoration.

performanceRequirements: N-REQ-037: The system shall achieve an average response time of less than 500 milliseconds for all `Subscription Version Create` and `Activate` requests under peak load conditions (defined as 100 transactions per second). This ensures efficient processing of new number porting requests, minimizing delays for end-users and Service Providers.

N-REQ-038: The system shall broadcast `Network Data Updates` to all relevant Local SMS platforms within 100 milliseconds of an `Activate` message being processed. This critical requirement guarantees near real-time synchronization across the network, preventing routing discrepancies for ported numbers.

N-REQ-039: The system shall be capable of processing a minimum of 5,000 `Subscription Version` transactions per minute during business hours. This capacity is necessary to handle the high volume of number porting activities across the North American numbering plan, ensuring the system does not become a bottleneck.

N-REQ-040: The system shall generate `Audit Reports` for up to 1 million records within 30 minutes. This performance metric is crucial for Service Providers to quickly identify and rectify data synchronization issues across their networks.
safetyRequirements: N-REQ-041: The system shall implement robust data validation mechanisms at all input points to prevent the entry of malformed or erroneous data that could compromise network routing integrity. This safeguards against critical errors that could lead to widespread service disruption for telephone numbers.

N-REQ-042: The system shall maintain an immutable audit log of all `Subscription Version` state changes, `Network Data` broadcasts, and `Data Administration` activities. This log is essential for forensic analysis, allowing for the precise reconstruction of events leading to any data inconsistency or system anomaly.

N-REQ-043: In the event of a critical system failure (e.g., primary data center outage), the system shall initiate disaster recovery procedures to transition operations to a backup **npac** site within 30 minutes, with a Recovery Point Objective (RPO) of no more than 5 minutes. This ensures business continuity for critical number portability services, minimizing downtime and data loss.
securityRequirements: N-REQ-044: The system shall require multi-factor authentication for all **npac** administrative personnel accessing sensitive system configurations and data administration functions. This enhances protection against unauthorized access to critical system controls and data.

N-REQ-045: All communications between the **npac** SMS and Service Provider SOA/Local SMS systems shall be encrypted using industry-standard protocols such as **TLS 1.2** or higher. This protects sensitive `Subscription Version` data and `Network Data` from interception and tampering during transit.

N-REQ-046: The system shall implement role-based access control (RBAC) to restrict user access to functionalities and data based on their assigned roles (e.g., New SP, Old SP, **npac** Admin). This ensures that users can only perform actions and access information necessary for their specific responsibilities.

N-REQ-047: The system shall automatically generate detailed audit logs for all security-relevant events, including login attempts (successful and failed), data modifications, and system configuration changes. These logs shall be protected from unauthorized modification and regularly reviewed for suspicious activity to aid in intrusion detection.

N-REQ-048: Passwords for all user accounts shall adhere to a strict policy requiring a minimum length of 12 characters, a mix of uppercase and lowercase letters, numbers, and special characters, and must be changed every 90 days. This strengthens protection against brute-force attacks and unauthorized account access.
softwareQualityAttributes: N-REQ-049: **Availability**: The **npac** system shall maintain 99.999% (five-nines) availability for its core number portability services, excluding scheduled maintenance windows. This is critical due to the system's role as a central component in the national telecommunications infrastructure, where even minor downtime can have significant economic and operational impact across all Service Providers.

N-REQ-050: **Reliability**: The system shall process 99.99% of all `Subscription Version` transactions without error. This high degree of reliability is essential to ensure the accuracy of number porting, preventing incorrect routing and minimizing manual intervention required for error correction.

N-REQ-051: **Maintainability**: The system's codebase and configurations shall be well-documented and modular, facilitating easy diagnosis, modification, and updates by maintenance teams. This is important for a long-lived system that will require continuous adaptation to evolving regulations and technical standards.

N-REQ-052: **Testability**: The system shall be designed with clear, verifiable interfaces and observable internal states, allowing for comprehensive automated and manual testing of all functional and non-functional requirements. This ensures that new features and bug fixes can be rigorously validated before deployment, minimizing the risk of introducing new defects.

N-REQ-053: **Interoperability**: The system shall fully comply with the **npac** SMS Interoperable Interface Specification (IIS), ensuring seamless and correct data exchange with a diverse ecosystem of Service Provider SOA systems and Local SMS platforms. This is paramount for the system to fulfill its role as a central hub for number portability.
businessRules: N-REQ-054: All `Subscription Version` processing, including creation, modification, and activation, shall strictly adhere to predefined `Business Days` and `Business Hours`, as configured by **npac** administrative personnel.

N-REQ-055: The system shall enforce specific `Timer Types` and their behaviors for various `Subscription Version` states, such as pending concurrence or pending activation, to ensure timely progression of porting requests.
N-REQ-056: The `NPAC SMS` is not responsible for obtaining or verifying customer approval to port a telephone number; this responsibility lies solely with the Service Providers.

N-REQ-057: In cases where an `Old Service Provider` fails to respond to a `Missing Notification` within the allotted timeframe, the `NPAC SMS` shall permit the `New Service Provider` to proceed with activation upon the `New Service Provider Due Date`.

N-REQ-058: When `Block Data Download` files are generated, they shall reflect the number pooling blocks for which the requesting Service Provider is authorized to receive data, based on their `Service Provider ID` and associated `SOA`.

otherRequirements: N-REQ-059: The **npac** system shall capture and store usage records for all Service Provider interactions that are subject to billing. This data shall include, but not be limited to, `Subscription Version` creation, modification, activation, and `Bulk Data Download` requests. The system shall provide interfaces for billing systems to retrieve this usage data. This ensures accurate billing for the services provided by **npac** to Service Providers.

glossary.definition: Common Management Information Protocol. A network management protocol used for exchanging management information between network devices and management systems.
GNU General Public License. A free, copyleft license for software and other kinds of work, ensuring freedom to share and change.
Interoperable Interface Specification. A document detailing the technical requirements for interfaces between the **npac** SMS and external systems.
Industry Numbering Committee. An industry body that addresses and resolves numbering resource issues in North America.
Local Number Portability. The ability of telephone users to retain their existing telephone number when switching local service providers.
Local Service Management System. A system operated by individual Service Providers to manage their local number portability data and network elements.
North American Numbering Council. An advisory body to the FCC on numbering administration issues.
Number Portability Administration Center. The central system responsible for managing and administering local number portability data in a specific region or across North America.
A numbering block within the North American Numbering Plan (NANP), where NPA is the Numbering Plan Area code, NXX is the Central Office code, and X is a specific block of 1,000 numbers.
A telecommunications company that provides telephone service to end-users.
Service Management System. The software and hardware platform that provides the services for the **npac**.
Service Order Administration. The system used by a Service Provider to manage customer service orders, including number porting requests.
A record in the **npac** SMS database representing the current or pending status of a ported telephone number.
To Be Determined. An item whose details are not yet finalized in this document.

flowchartDiagram.syntaxExplanation: This flowchart utilizes the `flowchart TD` syntax for a top-down diagram. Nodes are defined with alphanumeric IDs, and labels containing spaces are enclosed in double quotes. Decision points use diamond shapes `id{"Text"}`. Processes use rectangular shapes `id["Text"]`. Start/End nodes use rounded rectangles or circles `id((Text))` or `id(Text)`. Connections are indicated by `-->` with optional labels for decision outcomes.
flowchartDiagram.code:
flowchart TD
  StartProcess((Start)) --> NewSPA["New SP obtains Auth and notifies Old SP"]
  NewSPA --> SPsNotifyNPAC["SPs send notifications to NPAC SMS"]
  SPsNotifyNPAC --> ValidateMatch{"NPAC Validates & Matches notifications?"}
  ValidateMatch -- No --> ConflictResolution["Initiate Conflict Resolution (1.2.4)"]
  ValidateMatch -- Yes --> PhysicalChanges["SPs perform Physical Changes"]
  PhysicalChanges --> NewSPActivates["New SP sends 'Activate' to NPAC SMS"]
  NewSPActivates --> BroadcastUpdate["NPAC SMS broadcasts Network Data Update"]
  BroadcastUpdate --> SPsUpdateNetworks["SPs update their networks"]
  SPsUpdateNetworks --> EndProcess((End))
flowchartDiagram.caption: npac Service Provisioning Process Flow

sequenceDiagram.syntaxExplanation: This sequence diagram uses `sequenceDiagram` to illustrate the interaction between participants over time. Participants are declared with `participant ID as Name`. Messages are represented by arrows, with `->>` for asynchronous calls and `->` for synchronous. Activations (`->>+` and `-->>-`) denote when a participant is actively performing an action. Notes provide additional context.
sequenceDiagram.code:
sequenceDiagram
  participant NewSP as "New Service Provider"
  participant NPAC as "npac SMS"
  participant OldSP as "Old Service Provider"

  NewSP->>+NPAC: "Subscription Version Create Request"
  NPAC->>NPAC: Validate Request & Data
  NPAC->>+OldSP: "Request Old SP Concurrence"
  OldSP-->>-NPAC: "Concurrence Acknowledgment"
  alt Concurrence Valid
    NPAC->>NPAC: Update Subscription Status to Pending Activation
    NPAC-->>-NewSP: "Subscription Version Create Confirmation"
  else Concurrence Invalid/Missing
    NPAC->>NPAC: "Initiate Conflict Resolution"
    NPAC-->>-NewSP: "Subscription Version Create Failure (Conflict)"
  end
sequenceDiagram.caption: Subscription Version Creation Sequence

entityRelationshipDiagram.syntaxExplanation: This ERD uses `erDiagram` syntax to represent entities and their relationships. Entities are declared with `ENTITY_NAME { attribute_type attribute_name KEY_TYPE }`. Keys are indicated as `PK` (Primary Key), `FK` (Foreign Key), or `UK` (Unique Key). Relationship cardinality is shown using `||--o{` (one-to-many optional), `||--|{` (one-to-many mandatory), `}|--||` (many-to-one mandatory), etc. Relationship labels are enclosed in double quotes.
entityRelationshipDiagram.code:
erDiagram
  "Service Provider" { 
    int sp_id PK 
    string sp_name 
    string sp_type 
    string contact_email 
  }

  "Subscription Version" {
    int sv_id PK
    int new_sp_id FK "New Service Provider"
    int old_sp_id FK "Old Service Provider"
    string telephone_number UK
    string sv_status
    datetime effective_date
  }

  "Number Block" {
    int block_id PK
    string npa_nxx_x UK
    int sp_id FK "Block Holder Service Provider"
    datetime creation_date
  }

  "Audit Log" {
    int log_id PK
    int sp_id FK
    datetime timestamp
