introduction.purpose: The primary purpose of this document is to comprehensively detail the functional and non-functional requirements for the **microcare** Voucher Management System (VMUS). It serves as a definitive guide for the development team, outlining the system's intended functionalities, user interactions, and technical specifications. This SRS aims to ensure a shared understanding among all stakeholders, facilitating the design, development, and testing phases of the VMUS project.
introduction.documentConventions: This Software Requirements Specification (SRS) adheres to the IEEE 830-1998 standard for documenting software requirements. All requirements are systematically identified with unique alphanumeric codes, such as M-REQ-XXX, to enable clear traceability throughout the system lifecycle. Technical terms, acronyms, and abbreviations are defined within the glossary, and any aspects requiring further clarification are marked as 'TBD' and cataloged in an appendix.
introduction.intendedAudience: This document is intended for a diverse audience, including **microcare**'s development team, quality assurance personnel, project managers, and the client organization, Marie Stopes International Uganda (MSIU) Admin team. Developers will utilize this SRS for system design and implementation, while the testing team will derive test cases to validate system functionality. Project management and client stakeholders will use it to verify that the delivered system aligns with business objectives and program requirements.
introduction.productScope: The **microcare** Voucher Management System is a new, self-contained product designed to automate and enhance the operations of the Voucher Management Unit (VMU) within the Output-based Aid (OBA) program. Its core functionality includes the creation, distribution, sales, claim processing, and reporting for health service vouchers. The system aims to minimize manual processes, ensure data quality, mitigate fraud risks, and provide timely insights for program management, with an initial capacity to manage twenty thousand vouchers and built-in scalability for future expansion.
references: IEEE 830-1998 IEEE Recommended Practice for Software Requirements Specifications.
ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018 Systems and software engineering — Life cycle processes — Requirements engineering.
Final report on Programme Design Study, Dated 10-Sepetember-2005 Prepared by MSIU.
Functional Flow of VMUS Prepared by Microcare on 30th November 2005.

overallDescription.productPerspective: The **microcare** Voucher Management System represents a new, self-contained software solution specifically developed to automate and streamline the operations of the Voucher Management Unit (VMU) within Marie Stopes International Uganda (MSIU)'s Output-based Aid (OBA) program. This system replaces predominantly manual processes for voucher creation, distribution, sales tracking, and claims processing. By digitalizing these workflows, **microcare** aims to significantly enhance efficiency, reduce human error, and provide robust mechanisms for fraud detection and program monitoring.
productFunctions: Voucher Creation / Preparation
Marketing / Sales
Claim Entry / Processing
Voucher Sales Return
Client Feedback
Reports (Standard and Customized)
Security and User Privileges

userClassesAndCharacteristics.characteristics: These users are responsible for the initial creation and preparation of vouchers, including generating unique identifiers and managing validity periods. They require moderate computer literacy and a thorough understanding of voucher management protocols and security policies. VMU Staff members possess elevated access privileges to critical system functions.

Members of the MSIU Sales Team are involved in the distribution of vouchers to external distributors and recording sales transactions. They interact with the system primarily for data entry related to sales activities and require basic computer skills. Their focus is on efficient and accurate recording of distribution transactions.

Distributors are external entities responsible for selling vouchers directly to clients. While they do not directly interact with the **microcare** system, their master information and sales records are managed within it. They provide critical data inputs for sales tracking and financial reconciliation.

VSP (Voucher Service Provider) staff, such as medical doctors and qualified nurses, provide healthcare services to clients redeeming vouchers. They are responsible for accurately completing claim forms and ensuring the collection of voucher slips and biometric data. Their interaction is primarily with physical forms that are later entered into the system by MSIU Field Office staff.

These personnel are responsible for entering VSP-submitted claims into the **microcare** system, performing both manual and system-based validations. They require good data entry skills, attention to detail, and a clear understanding of claim processing rules and fraud detection procedures. They also manage client feedback entry.

The MSIU Admin Team provides high-level oversight of the OBA program and the **microcare** system. Their responsibilities include arranging VSP payments, analyzing performance reports, and making strategic decisions based on system data. They require strong analytical skills and a comprehensive understanding of program objectives, utilizing the system primarily for reporting and high-level management.

These individuals are the end-users of the healthcare services, purchasing and redeeming vouchers for STD treatment. They have no direct interaction with the **microcare** system itself, but their treatment details, voucher usage, and feedback are integral data points captured by the system. Their primary interaction is with Distributors and VSP staff.

overallDescription.operatingEnvironment: The **microcare** Voucher Management System is designed to operate within a specific technical environment. The backend infrastructure will be built upon the **Oracle 9i** platform, leveraging its robust database management capabilities for data storage and retrieval. This ensures high data integrity and efficient handling of large volumes of transaction data, particularly critical for an extensive voucher program.
designAndImplementationConstraints: The system shall be developed using **Oracle 9i** for the database, **Visual Basic (VB)** for the frontend application, and **Crystal Reports 9** for report generation. This mandates adherence to the architectural patterns and best practices associated with these technologies.

The system shall incorporate high intrusion controls and granular access level settings to ensure robust security. User privileges will be defined at organizational, module, and screen levels to prevent unauthorized data access or modification.

The database schema shall be designed with sequential links and utilize surrogate keys for efficient storage management and to minimize unused blocked space, ensuring scalability for future program expansion.

All user interfaces shall prioritize user-friendliness, incorporating maximum master table structures and dropdown selection menus to minimize data entry errors and reduce reliance on keyboard input for faster transaction processing.
Vouchers, once created, shall not be editable or deletable; however, a provision for withholding specific voucher numbers for administrative reasons will be implemented.

The system shall interface directly with barcode readers for efficient voucher number verification and claim form entry, and with thumb-print readers for biometric verification during claim submissions.
The system shall incorporate a validity date check for vouchers, with provisions for authorized users to amend or alter these dates at a system level.
userDocumentation: The **microcare** system will be delivered with a comprehensive, step-by-step user manual designed for users with minimum computer skills. This manual will provide clear instructions for all operational procedures, guiding users through the various modules and functionalities to ensure efficient and effective system usage. Additionally, contextual help and field-level descriptions may be integrated into the user interface to enhance usability.
assumptionsAndDependencies: The **microcare** project assumes that the Output-based Aid (OBA) program requirements, including the voucher scheme principles and service quality standards, will remain stable throughout the pilot phase and subsequent operations.
The system is dependent on the stable and continuous operation of the **Oracle 9i** database and the availability of **Visual Basic (VB)** runtime environments on client workstations.

It is assumed that external hardware, specifically barcode readers and thumb-print readers, will be available and correctly configured to interface with the **microcare** system for data capture and verification.

Decisions regarding the activation or deactivation of Voucher Service Providers (VSPs) due to fraud or other reasons will be made by the MSIU Management and communicated to the system for status updates.

The initial pilot phase for the program will involve a target of twenty thousand vouchers, with the understanding that the system architecture must support future scalability beyond this initial volume without requiring major re-engineering.

externalInterfaceRequirements.userInterfaces: The **microcare** system's user interface will be developed using **Visual Basic (VB)**, presenting a desktop application environment. The design emphasizes user-friendliness, incorporating structured screen layouts with master table references to guide data entry and minimize errors. Key design elements include extensive use of dropdown selection menus for common fields, reducing manual typing and accelerating data input processes. Navigation will be intuitive, providing quick access to related master screens (e.g., for adding new distributors or salesmen) without interrupting current transaction flows. Standard GUI elements, such as consistent button layouts for 'New', 'Edit', 'Delete', 'Save', and 'Cancel' operations, will be uniformly applied across all screens. Data validation will be actively integrated into the input forms to provide immediate feedback to users, further enhancing data quality and operational efficiency.
externalInterfaceRequirements.hardwareInterfaces: The **microcare** system will integrate with two primary hardware devices to enhance operational efficiency and security. Firstly, it will interface with **barcode readers** to facilitate rapid and accurate data entry for voucher numbers, verification processes, and claim form details. This integration minimizes manual input errors and speeds up transaction processing. Secondly, the system will interface with **thumb-print readers** to capture and verify biometric data during the claim submission process. This enhances the security and authenticity of claims, providing an additional layer of verification for client and partner voucher usage.
externalInterfaceRequirements.softwareInterfaces: The **microcare** Voucher Management System primarily interfaces with the **Oracle 9i** Relational Database Management System for all data persistence and retrieval operations. This connection will manage the storage of all transactional data, master data, and configuration settings. For reporting functionalities, the system will integrate with **Crystal Reports 9**, which will be used to generate all standard and customized reports. This integration allows for robust data visualization and analysis capabilities, transforming raw database information into actionable insights for MSIU management. Communication between the **Visual Basic** frontend and **Oracle 9i** will utilize standard database connectivity protocols, ensuring secure and efficient data exchange.
externalInterfaceRequirements.communicationsInterfaces: The **microcare** system will primarily operate within a local area network (LAN) environment, relying on standard network protocols for communication between client workstations running the **Visual Basic** application and the central **Oracle 9i** database server. Secure and reliable **TCP/IP** communication will underpin all data transfers, ensuring the integrity and confidentiality of sensitive voucher and claim information. While direct internet-facing communication such as email or external web services are not explicitly detailed for the 2005 context, internal network communication will adhere to established security practices. No explicit external communication protocols like HTTP or FTP are specified for direct system interaction; instead, data transfer is confined to the secure internal network for the core functionalities.

systemFeatures.name: Voucher Creation and Management
systemFeatures.description: This feature enables the authorized VMU Staff to securely generate, print, and manage unique voucher numbers for the Output-based Aid program. It provides critical functionality for initial program setup and ongoing voucher stock control. Technically, this involves algorithmic generation of secure, unique identifiers, integration with barcode printing, and strict controls over voucher status, preventing unauthorized alteration and supporting administrative holds. This directly impacts the integrity and traceability of the entire voucher lifecycle. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Authorized VMU Staff initiates the 'Create Voucher Batch' function. Response: The system prompts for batch parameters (e.g., quantity, project code).

Stimulus: VMU Staff enters required batch details and confirms. Response: The system generates a block of unique voucher numbers with security codes, updates the voucher inventory, and prepares them for barcode printing.
Stimulus: VMU Staff requests to view or amend a voucher's validity date. Response: The system displays the voucher details and allows authorized modification of the validity period.
functionalRequirements: The system shall generate unique alphanumeric voucher numbers, including project codes, group batch codes, batch numbers, and security codes.
The system shall print generated voucher numbers in **barcode** format for physical attachment to vouchers.
The system shall allow authorized VMU Staff to define and amend the validity dates for voucher batches.
The system shall associate a Maximum Retail Price (MRP) with each voucher.
The system shall prevent the editing or deletion of a voucher once it has been created.
The system shall provide a mechanism for authorized VMU Staff to withhold (deactivate) specific voucher numbers for administrative reasons.
The system shall enforce a minimum quantity of vouchers that can be created in a single batch, as defined by management.

systemFeatures.name: Marketing and Sales Management
systemFeatures.description: This feature manages the master data for Distributors and MSIU Sales Team members, facilitating the recording and tracking of voucher distribution transactions from MSIU to distributors. It ensures accurate inventory management and provides a historical record of sales, which is vital for performance analysis and financial reconciliation. Technically, it involves maintaining interconnected master tables and transaction records, ensuring data integrity through unique identifier checks and automatic calculations for invoice amounts. This streamlines the sales process and supports robust reporting. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: MSIU Sales Staff selects a Distributor and Salesman, then enters the quantity of vouchers to be distributed. Response: The system automatically allocates specific voucher numbers from stock, calculates the invoice amount, and generates a unique distribution invoice.

Stimulus: MSIU Sales Staff attempts to create a new Distributor with an existing code. Response: The system displays a warning message and prompts for a unique code or confirmation to proceed with a duplicate name.

Stimulus: Authorized user requests a sales history report for a specific Distributor. Response: The system retrieves and displays a summary of purchases, sales, returns, and current balance for that Distributor.
functionalRequirements: The system shall maintain a master record for each Distributor, including unique codes, names, business types, addresses, contact information, and status (active/deactivate).
The system shall enforce mandatory fields for Distributor master information, including Distributor Code, Name, Type of business, Proprietor Name, and Address components.
The system shall prevent the creation of duplicate Distributor Codes and provide a warning for duplicate Distributor names.
The system shall maintain a master record for each MSIU Salesman, including unique codes, names, age, gender, address, and associated Sales Team.
The system shall record distribution transactions detailing voucher sales from MSIU to Distributors, including Distributor, Salesman, date, number of vouchers sold, and invoice amount.
The system shall automatically generate a unique Distribution Invoice Number for each transaction.
The system shall automatically allocate specific voucher numbers from available stock based on the quantity sold in a distribution transaction.
The system shall calculate the invoice amount automatically based on the quantity of vouchers sold and the current wholesale price defined in system settings.
The system shall provide quick navigation to Distributor and Salesman master screens from the distribution transaction screen to allow on-the-fly creation of new entries.
The system shall provide a screen to view the sales history of a particular Distributor, including batch numbers, purchase dates, quantities purchased, sold, returned, and balance.

systemFeatures.name: Claim Entry and Processing
systemFeatures.description: This module is critical for capturing and validating client treatment claims submitted by Voucher Service Providers (VSPs) to the MSIU Field Office. It ensures the integrity of the OBA program by meticulously checking claim details, including voucher validity, clinical information, and associated costs, against defined rules to prevent fraud. Technically, it integrates master data from VSPs and VSP staff, performs extensive data validations, and manages the lifecycle of claims from submission to approval or quarantine, directly impacting financial settlements and fraud mitigation. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: MSIU Field Office Staff selects a VSP and initiates a new claim entry. Response: The system prompts for mandatory claim details, including voucher number, patient information, diagnosis, and treatment specifics.

Stimulus: MSIU Field Office Staff attempts to save a claim with missing mandatory information or an invalid voucher number. Response: The system flags the claim as invalid, prevents immediate approval, and directs it to a quarantine area for review and verification.

Stimulus: An authorized user requests to view the claim status for a specific VSP. Response: The system displays a report detailing submitted claims, their status (e.g., approved, quarantined), dates, amounts, and remarks.
functionalRequirements: The system shall maintain a master record for each VSP, including unique codes, names, physical and communication addresses, facility details, contact information, and status (active/in-active).
The system shall automatically generate unique VSP codes with an 'SP' suffix.

The system shall allow authorized users to define and update payment terms for each VSP, including payment mode, bank details, payment type (Bi-Monthly/Monthly), valid dates, and consultation/lab fees.
The system shall automatically change the status of a VSP to 'In-active' if more than two fraud entries are detected during the claim process.
The system shall maintain a master record for VSP Staff, including staff codes, names, types, and qualifications, linked to their respective VSP.
The system shall automatically generate VSP Staff codes (e.g., HP0001SC0001).
The system shall record client treatment claims, capturing details such as VSP, voucher number, patient visit type, diagnosis, lab tests, drugs, and associated costs.
The system shall validate all mandatory fields during claim entry, including voucher number, patient profile selection, and medicine costs.
The system shall interface with a **thumb-print reader** to verify client thumb-prints on claim forms during the claim validation process.
The system shall place claims with missing or invalid mandatory information into a 'quarantine area' for further review and verification by the MSIU Field Office.
The system shall allow quarantined claims, once verified by the VSP, to be re-entered and processed in a subsequent month's batch.
The system shall generate bi-monthly or monthly financial and medical reports based on approved claims for the MSIU Admin team to arrange VSP payments.

systemFeatures.name: Voucher Sales Return Management
systemFeatures.description: This feature provides functionality to manage the return of unsold or damaged vouchers from Distributors back to MSIU. It ensures accurate inventory adjustments and facilitates financial reconciliation for returned stock, maintaining precise control over the physical and financial lifecycle of vouchers. Technically, this involves updating voucher statuses, adjusting distributor accounts, and logging return transactions to maintain an auditable trail, which is crucial for fraud prevention and financial accuracy. Priority: Medium.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: MSIU Field Office Staff processes a voucher return request from a Distributor. Response: The system validates the returned voucher numbers, updates their status to 'returned' or 'withheld', and records the transaction.
Stimulus: System processes a return for a batch of vouchers. Response: The system updates the available voucher stock count and adjusts the financial liability or credit for the Distributor.
functionalRequirements: The system shall allow MSIU Field Office staff to record the return of unsold vouchers from Distributors.
The system shall update the status of individual voucher numbers from 'sold' to 'returned' or 'withheld' upon processing a return.
The system shall adjust the available voucher stock quantity in the inventory system to reflect returns.
The system shall update the Distributor's sales history and financial records to account for returned vouchers.
The system shall generate a return receipt or acknowledgment for the Distributor.

systemFeatures.name: Client Feedback Management
systemFeatures.description: This feature facilitates the capture and management of client feedback, enabling the MSIU Admin team to monitor program effectiveness and client satisfaction. By systematically collecting and storing feedback, the system provides valuable qualitative data for program evaluation and improvement. Technically, it involves simple data entry forms for feedback details and a structured storage mechanism to link feedback to specific claims or VSPs, enhancing the overall monitoring system of the OBA program. Priority: Low.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: MSIU Admin team collects client feedback and submits the data to the MSIU Field Office. Response: MSIU Field Office Staff enters the feedback details into the system.
Stimulus: MSIU Field Office Staff enters client feedback, linking it to a specific claim or VSP. Response: The system stores the feedback record and makes it available for reporting and analysis.
functionalRequirements: The system shall provide a dedicated interface for MSIU Field Office staff to enter client feedback data.
The system shall allow linking client feedback records to specific claims or Voucher Service Providers (VSPs).
The system shall store client feedback details, including date of feedback and textual comments.
The system shall make stored client feedback data accessible for reporting and analysis by the MSIU Admin team.

systemFeatures.name: Reporting (Standard and Customized)
systemFeatures.description: This feature provides comprehensive reporting capabilities, utilizing **Crystal Reports 9** to generate both standard and customized reports across all modules. It is crucial for program monitoring, financial reconciliation, fraud detection, and strategic decision-making by the MSIU Admin team. Technically, this involves querying the **Oracle 9i** database to extract and aggregate data from various entities, then presenting it in a user-friendly format via the reporting tool, allowing stakeholders to gain deep insights into program performance and identify areas for improvement. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Authorized user selects a report type (e.g., 'VSP Claim Status') and specifies parameters (e.g., VSP name, date range). Response: The system queries the database via **Crystal Reports 9**, generates the requested report, and displays it on screen or offers print options.
Stimulus: MSIU Admin team reviews the 'MSIU Sales Team Performance' report. Response: The system displays data on salesman performance, voucher distribution quantities, and associated revenue.
functionalRequirements: The system shall generate standard reports for Voucher Creation/Preparation, including batch details and voucher status.
The system shall generate standard reports for Marketing/Sales, including Distributor sales history and MSIU Sales Team performance.
The system shall generate standard reports for Claim Entry/Processing, providing details on submitted, approved, quarantined, and rejected claims, and VSP claim statuses.
The system shall generate financial reports for VSP payments, detailing consultation fees, lab fees, and cumulative payment totals.
The system shall generate reports on client feedback data to assess program effectiveness.
The system shall provide functionality for authorized users to define and generate customized reports based on various data parameters.
The system shall integrate with **Crystal Reports 9** for all report generation and display functionalities.
The system shall enforce user login permissions for accessing and printing specific reports.

systemFeatures.name: Security and User Privilege Management
systemFeatures.description: This feature provides robust security controls, including user authentication, role-based access control, and granular privilege settings, to protect sensitive program data and prevent unauthorized access or system manipulation. It is foundational for maintaining data integrity and complying with regulatory policies, especially in a system handling financial transactions and medical information. Technically, this involves a layered security model with user credential management, role assignments to modules and screens, and audit logging to track all critical user actions. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: A user attempts to log in with incorrect credentials. Response: The system denies access and logs the failed login attempt.

Stimulus: An authorized administrator attempts to assign specific edit permissions for the 'VSP Master Information' screen to a new user role. Response: The system updates the role's permissions, ensuring the new user can only perform allowed actions.
Stimulus: A user attempts to access a module for which they lack privileges. Response: The system displays an 'Access Denied' message and prevents entry to the restricted module.
functionalRequirements: The system shall implement a secure user authentication mechanism requiring unique usernames and passwords.
The system shall enforce role-based access control (RBAC) to restrict user access to modules, sub-modules, and screens based on their assigned roles.
The system shall provide granular control over user privileges, allowing administrators to define specific permissions for viewing, adding, editing, and deleting records.
The system shall allow only authorized personnel to create, modify, or delete VSP master information and payment terms.
The system shall implement intrusion controls to protect against unauthorized system access and data breaches.
The system shall log all security-sensitive actions, including login attempts, data modifications, and privilege changes, for auditing purposes.

performanceRequirements: The system shall provide a response time of less than 3 seconds for all data entry and master data retrieval operations, including searches and dropdown selections, to support faster transaction data entry by users. This is critical for maintaining operational efficiency and minimizing user frustration during high-volume data input.

The system shall generate all standard reports within 10 seconds for datasets up to 10,000 records. For complex customized reports or larger datasets, the system shall provide notification mechanisms if expected generation time exceeds 30 seconds. This ensures that management and operational staff receive timely insights for decision-making and payment processing.
safetyRequirements: The system shall implement transactional integrity for all data modifications, ensuring that database operations are atomic, consistent, isolated, and durable (ACID compliant). This safeguards against data corruption or loss in the event of system failures, which is paramount for the financial and medical records managed by **microcare**.

The system shall maintain audit trails for all critical data modifications and deletions, recording who, what, when, and where changes occurred. This provides a mechanism for forensic analysis and accountability, essential for mitigating risks associated with claim fraud and data manipulation.
securityRequirements: The system shall enforce strong password policies, requiring a minimum length of 8 characters, inclusion of uppercase, lowercase, numbers, and special characters, with mandatory password changes every 90 days. This protects user accounts from brute-force attacks and unauthorized access, ensuring the confidentiality of sensitive patient and financial data.

The system shall implement role-based access control (RBAC) to restrict functionalities and data visibility based on a user's assigned role and organizational level, extending to granular control at the module, sub-module, and screen levels. This ensures that users can only access the information and functions necessary for their assigned duties, mitigating internal security threats.

The system shall log all successful and failed login attempts, as well as critical system events such as data changes, deletions, and access to sensitive reports. These logs shall be securely stored and regularly reviewed to detect and respond to potential security breaches or policy violations, providing an auditable trail for compliance.
softwareQualityAttributes: **Usability (M-REQ-NFR-001)**: The system shall be user-friendly with an intuitive graphical user interface (GUI) and logical navigation, requiring minimal training for users with basic computer skills. This is achieved through extensive use of master tables, dropdown menus, and consistent screen layouts, minimizing data entry errors and accelerating transaction processing, as explicitly requested to ensure easy adoption by a diverse user base.

**Reliability (M-REQ-NFR-002)**: The system shall operate with a 99.5% uptime during scheduled operational hours (8 AM - 5 PM local time, weekdays). This includes robustness against unexpected errors and graceful handling of invalid inputs, minimizing system crashes and data inconsistencies, which is crucial for continuous voucher distribution and claim processing without disruption.

**Maintainability (M-REQ-NFR-003)**: The system's architecture shall be modular, with clearly defined interfaces between components (e.g., frontend, database, reporting engine), facilitating ease of future modifications, bug fixes, and enhancements. The use of **Oracle 9i**, **Visual Basic**, and **Crystal Reports 9** provides a known technological stack, supporting easier maintenance by developers familiar with these platforms.

**Scalability (M-REQ-NFR-004)**: The system's design, particularly the **Oracle 9i** database structure with surrogate keys and efficient storage management, shall support a minimum of a 5x increase in transaction volume and data storage beyond the pilot phase's 20,000 vouchers without significant performance degradation or architectural overhaul. This allows for future expansion of the OBA program without requiring complete system re-engineering.

**Testability (M-REQ-NFR-005)**: The system shall be designed with clear, verifiable functional requirements and accessible data layers to facilitate comprehensive testing of all modules. This enables the testing team to develop robust test cases and thoroughly validate system functionality, ensuring the quality and correctness of the software as highlighted in the purpose.

**Correctness (M-REQ-NFR-006)**: The system shall perform all calculations, data validations, and rule enforcements (e.g., voucher allocation, invoice calculation, claim status determination) with 100% accuracy according to specified business rules. This is paramount for preventing financial discrepancies and ensuring the integrity of the OBA program, especially concerning fraud detection in claims.
businessRules: Vouchers shall be distributed in pairs, with one portion for the client and one for their partner.
Only one voucher shall be sold to a single person at a time to minimize potential fraud.
Distributors are required to maintain a detailed distribution list, documenting voucher numbers, dates of sale, and customer information.
Clients may redeem their vouchers at any approved Voucher Service Provider (VSP) of their choice.
STD treatments provided under the voucher scheme must adhere to the National Treatment Algorithms for STD in Uganda (NTA), adapted for the OBA scheme (TA-OBA).

Each voucher entitles the client to a first consultation with basic lab testing and treatment, a first follow-up visit, and potentially a second follow-up visit if symptoms persist, after which referral to a hospital may occur.
VSPs are required to document each voucher case using a standardized patient treatment form.
VSPs shall submit collected vouchers, patient treatment documentation, and a summary claims form to the MSIU field office for reimbursement on a monthly basis.
Conspicuous claims identified during processing will be investigated and cleared by the VMU.
The VMU shall operate a monitoring system, including follow-up with patients, to ensure the proper operation of the voucher scheme.

The address fields for Distributors and VSPs (District, Sub-District, County, Sub-County, Village/Town, Road/Street) shall be managed through master tables to ensure data consistency and allow for updates.
A VSP shall be automatically inactivated by the system if more than two fraud entries are detected during the claim process; reactivation is at the discretion of MSIU Management.

glossary.definition: Voucher Management Unit System; the software product being specified.
Marie Stopes International Uganda; the client organization implementing the OBA program.
Output-based Aid; a funding mechanism that finances agreed outputs with pre-defined quality.
Voucher Service Provider; an approved healthcare provider (medical doctors, nurses, hospitals) authorized to accept vouchers.
National Treatment Algorithms for STD in Uganda; national guidelines for treating sexually transmitted diseases.
Treatment Algorithms for STD treatment in the OBA scheme; adapted NTA guidelines for the specific voucher program.
Programme Design Study; a foundational document outlining the design of the OBA program.
Behavioural Change Campaign; marketing and informational initiatives to promote voucher services.
Maximum Retail Price; the highest price at which a voucher can be sold.
To Be Determined; an item whose details are not yet finalized and require further definition.

flowchartDiagram.syntaxExplanation: Flowcharts are visual representations of processes, algorithms, or workflows, using standardized symbols to depict steps and decisions. In Mermaid, a flowchart begins with 'flowchart' followed by a direction ('TD' for top-down, 'LR' for left-right). Nodes are defined by an alphanumeric ID, and labels with spaces or special characters must be enclosed in double-quotes (e.g., NodeID["Label Text"]). Connections between nodes are indicated by '-->', and decision points use '{ }' or '{ "Text" }'. Critical syntax rules include using only alphanumeric characters for Node IDs (avoiding reserved words like 'end', 'subgraph', 'class') and strictly quoting all labels that contain spaces or symbols.
flowchartDiagram.code:
flowchart TD
    VMUStart["VMU Creates & Prepares Vouchers"] --> Distribute["Distributor Sells Vouchers to Clients"]
    Distribute --> DistSubmit["Distributor Submits Sales Data to MSIU"]
    DistSubmit --> ClientUsesVoucher{"Client/Partner Uses Voucher?"}
    ClientUsesVoucher -->|Yes| VSPProvides["VSP Provides Treatment & Collects Voucher"]
    VSPProvides --> VSPSubmit["VSP Submits Claim Forms to MSIU FO"]
    VSPSubmit --> MSIUFOValidate{"MSIU FO Validates Claims?"}
    MSIUFOValidate -->|Valid| ProcessClaim["Process & Approve Claim"]
    ProcessClaim --> GenerateReport["Generate Financial/Medical Reports"]
    GenerateReport --> ArrangePayment["MSIU Admin Arranges Payment"]
    ArrangePayment --> MSIUAdminFB["MSIU Admin Gathers Client Feedback"]
    MSIUAdminFB --> FOEnterFB["FO Enters Client Feedback into System"]
    FOEnterFB --> EndProcess([End Process])

    MSIUFOValidate -->|Invalid/Suspect| RejectQuarantine["Reject & Quarantine Claim"]
    RejectQuarantine --> SendBack["Send Back to VSP for Verification"]
    SendBack --> VSPReturns["VSP Returns Verified Claim (Next Batch)"]
    VSPReturns --> ProcessClaim
flowchartDiagram.caption: Microcare voucher management system process flow.

sequenceDiagram.syntaxExplanation: Sequence Diagrams illustrate the temporal order of interactions between system participants (actors or components). They begin with 'sequenceDiagram', followed by participant declarations (e.g., 'participant Alice as "Alice User"'). Messages are depicted with various arrow types (e.g., '->>' for asynchronous calls, '->' for synchronous). Activations can be shown with '+/-' suffixes on arrows (e.g., '->>+' and '-->>-') to indicate a participant becoming active and then inactive, ensuring a balanced representation of their lifecycle. Critical syntax includes using unquoted aliases for participant IDs (e.g., 'participant U as User') and avoiding special characters like '{ }' in message labels. Activations should not be nested within 'alt', 'opt', or 'loop' blocks.
sequenceDiagram.code:
sequenceDiagram
    participant VSP
    participant Client
    participant MSIUFieldOffice as "MSIU Field Office"
    participant microcareSystem as microcare
    participant Database as "microcare Database"

    VSP->>Client: Provides Treatment (Visits)
    Client->>VSP: Presents Voucher & Biometrics
    VSP->>MSIUFieldOffice: Submits Claim Form & Vouchers
    MSIUFieldOffice->>+microcareSystem: EnterClaimData(ClaimDetails)
    microcareSystem->>Database: Query VoucherValidity(VoucherID)
    Database-->>microcareSystem: Return VoucherStatus
    microcareSystem->>Database: Query VSP/StaffDetails(VSPID, StaffID)
    Database-->>microcareSystem: Return VSP/StaffInfo
    microcareSystem->>microcareSystem: ValidateClaimRules(ClaimDetails, VoucherStatus, VSPInfo)
    alt Claim is Valid & Approved
        microcareSystem->>Database: SaveApprovedClaim(ClaimDetails)
        Database-->>microcareSystem: ClaimSavedConfirmation
        microcareSystem-->>-MSIUFieldOffice: ClaimApproved(ClaimID)
        MSIUFieldOffice->>MSIUFieldOffice: Prepare for Payment Reporting
    else Claim is Invalid or Suspect
        microcareSystem->>Database: LogQuarantinedClaim(ClaimDetails)
        Database-->>microcareSystem: ClaimQuarantinedConfirmation
        microcareSystem-->>-MSIUFieldOffice: ClaimQuarantined(ClaimID)
        MSIUFieldOffice->>VSP: RequestClaimVerification(ClaimID)
        VSP->>MSIUFieldOffice: ResubmitsVerifiedClaim(ClaimID) (Next Batch)
    end
sequenceDiagram.caption: Claim entry and processing sequence.

entityRelationshipDiagram.syntaxExplanation: Entity Relationship Diagrams (ERD) model the logical structure of a database, depicting entities, their attributes, and relationships. It starts with 'erDiagram'. Entities are defined with 'EntityName { attributeType attributeName KeyType "Comment" }'. Allowed key types are 'PK' (Primary Key), 'FK' (Foreign Key), and 'UK' (Unique Key), with multiple keys comma-separated if necessary. Attribute names must be alphanumeric without spaces, while entity names with spaces must be quoted. Relationships are defined as 'ENTITY1 Cardinality1--Cardinality2 ENTITY2 : "Label"'. Cardinalities (e.g., '||--o{') must strictly adhere to convention, where '}|--||' denotes a many-to-one relationship when an FK implies it, and labels must always be double-quoted. Hidden many-to-many relationships (without a junction table) are prohibited.
entityRelationshipDiagram.code:
erDiagram
    VOUCHER_TEMPLATE {
        int id PK
        string project_code UK
        string template_name
        date validity_period_start
        date validity_period_end
        decimal mrp
    }

    VOUCHER_INSTANCE {
        int id PK
        int template_id FK
        string unique_voucher_number UK "Unique Physical ID"
        string security_code
        string status "Available, Sold, Used, Returned"
        date date_created
        int distribution_transaction_id FK
    }

    DISTRIBUTOR {
        int id PK
        string code UK "Unique Distributor Code"
        string name
        string type_of_business
        string contact_no
        string status
    }

    MSIU_SALESMAN {
        int id PK
        string code UK "Unique Salesman Code"
        string name
        string contact_no
    }

    DISTRIBUTION_TRANSACTION {
        int id PK
        int distributor_id FK
        int salesman_id FK
        date transaction_date
        string invoice_no UK
        decimal total_amount
        string payment_mode
    }

    VSP {
        int id PK
        string code UK "Unique VSP Code"
        string name
        string physical_address
        string status
    }

    VSP_STAFF {
        int id PK
        int vsp_id FK
        string staff_code UK "Unique Staff Code"
        string name
        string type
    }

    CLAIM {
        int id PK
        int vsp_id FK
        int voucher_instance_id FK "Voucher Used for Claim"
        int vsp_staff_id FK
        string claim_no UK "Unique Claim Number"
        date submission_date
        decimal claimed_amount
        string status "Approved, Quarantined, Rejected"
    }

    CLIENT_FEEDBACK {
        int id PK
        int claim_id FK
        date feedback_date
        string feedback_details
    }

    PAYMENT_TERM {
        int id PK
        int vsp_id FK
        string payment_type
        date valid_from
        decimal first_visit_fee
        decimal lab_fee
    }

    VOUCHER_TEMPLATE ||--o{ VOUCHER_INSTANCE : "is based on"
    DISTRIBUTOR ||--o{ DISTRIBUTION_TRANSACTION : "participates in"
    MSIU_SALESMAN ||--o{ DISTRIBUTION_TRANSACTION : "conducts"
    DISTRIBUTION_TRANSACTION ||--o{ VOUCHER_INSTANCE : "distributes"
    VSP ||--o{ VSP_STAFF : "employs"
    VSP ||--o{ CLAIM : "submits"
    VSP ||--o{ PAYMENT_TERM : "defines"
    CLAIM ||--|| VOUCHER_INSTANCE : "uses"
    CLAIM ||--o{ CLIENT_FEEDBACK : "relates to"

introduction.purpose: The purpose of this Software Requirements Specification (SRS) is to precisely define the core requirements for the **znix** research and development initiative. **znix** endeavors to advance existing information management paradigms by introducing an object-based, application-independent framework for the retrieval and manipulation of information. This document aims to guide development, facilitate testing, and ensure a common understanding among all project stakeholders.
introduction.documentConventions: This document adheres to the guidelines set forth by IEEE 830-1998, 'Recommended Practice for Software Requirements Specifications,' ensuring a standardized and comprehensive approach to requirement definition. Key terms such as 'MUST,' 'SHALL,' and 'SHOULD' are interpreted strictly according to RFC 2119 to denote varying degrees of requirement criticality. All references to the project name, **znix**, are maintained with precise capitalization and spelling as defined by the project governance.
introduction.intendedAudience: This SRS is primarily intended for system architects, software developers, and quality assurance engineers involved in the implementation and testing of **znix**. Additionally, it serves as a foundational reference for project managers and other stakeholders seeking a detailed understanding of the system's intended functionality and design constraints. The document aims to provide clarity on all specified requirements, promoting accurate development and validation.
introduction.productScope: The scope of this SRS encompasses the high-level design and core functionalities of the **znix** Framework, focusing on its ability to manage diverse user information in an object-based hierarchy. It defines the mechanisms for information capture, storage, organization, and interaction via a public API and user interface. The document specifically addresses requirements for integration with client processes and third-party applications, outlining a self-contained information management system.
references: IEEE 830-1998 IEEE Recommended Practice for Software Requirements Specifications.
ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018 Systems and software engineering — Life cycle processes — Requirements engineering.

overallDescription.productPerspective: **znix** is conceived as a novel approach to personal information management, moving beyond traditional application-centric data organization. It proposes a shift where users interact with information based on its intrinsic meaning and hierarchical relationships, rather than being confined by the structure of individual software applications. This system aims to offer superior integration with the underlying operating system, contrasting with standalone tools that provide limited third-party application support. The framework operates through a collection of system services designed to facilitate user interactions and expose lower-level functionalities via a public API.
productFunctions: Provide a centralized archive for user information.
Offer a flexible hierarchical model for managing archived information, supporting various relationship types.
Enable users to perform CRUD operations and backup on information without disrupting the hierarchy.
Automate data capturing and categorization to assist in building information hierarchies.
Expose a simple Application Programming Interface (API) for developing **znix**-aware client applications.
Facilitate interaction with '**znix**-unaware' applications through a proxy mechanism.

userClassesAndCharacteristics.characteristics: These users are expected to have basic IT skills and familiarity with **Windows 2000/XP** operating system interfaces. They will primarily interact with the **znix** Framework through a Graphical User Interface (GUI) and require an intuitive experience with minimal or no formal training. The system design should cater to their need for straightforward information management and navigation.

This group comprises users who require efficient organization and retrieval of diverse business and research-related information. They are likely to possess moderate IT proficiency and value the hierarchical organization and ease of access to their data. The system must support their need to quickly capture, relate, and retrieve information across different document types.

Researchers will utilize **znix** for archiving and managing large volumes of web documents, textual documents, and other research artifacts. They may have varying levels of technical expertise but will benefit significantly from the system's ability to create and manage complex relationships between information entities. The system should facilitate their research workflows by providing robust categorization and retrieval capabilities.

**znix** also caters to developers who wish to integrate their applications with the **znix** Framework. These users possess advanced technical skills and will interact directly with the public API. They require comprehensive documentation and a stable API to build 'smart' applications that leverage **znix**'s object-based information management capabilities, extending its utility.

overallDescription.operatingEnvironment: The **znix** Framework is designed to operate on conventional personal computing hardware, specifically requiring an **Intel Pentium III** or compatible 1-GHz processor with a minimum of 256 megabytes of RAM. This resource-conscious design contrasts with more demanding systems, enabling broader deployment. The software is targeted for deployment on **Microsoft Windows** operating systems, specifically **Windows 2000 Professional** and **Windows XP**, ensuring compatibility with widely used user environments.
designAndImplementationConstraints: The system is subject to stringent hardware constraints, requiring compatibility with an **Intel Pentium III** or compatible 1-GHz processor and a minimum of 256 megabytes of RAM. This limitation ensures the framework remains lightweight and accessible to a broad user base with older hardware configurations. Development efforts must optimize resource utilization to adhere to these memory and processor specifications.

All coding conventions utilized during the implementation phase of **znix** MUST be formally documented prior to the commencement of coding activities. Strict adherence to these established conventions throughout development is mandatory to ensure code consistency, readability, and maintainability. This practice facilitates collaborative development and long-term support for the system.

The **znix** Framework is intended to operate in a single-user mode exclusively, without integrated networking capabilities, limiting its current scope to individual information management. This design constraint simplifies initial development and security considerations. Future extensions to multi-user or networked environments will require re-evaluation of architecture and resource allocation.
userDocumentation: An online help system shall be provided with **znix**, designed to be as non-technical as possible, aligning with the system's goal of intuitive usability for casual users. This documentation will focus on guiding users through the core functionalities without overwhelming them with exhaustive technical details. It should enable users with basic operating system knowledge to instinctively interact with the system.

A comprehensive Developer’s Guide MAY be compiled to facilitate the creation of third-party **znix**-aware applications. This guide will detail the public API, data structures, and integration patterns necessary for external developers to extend the framework's capabilities. It will serve as an essential resource for the developer community, fostering an ecosystem of compatible applications.
assumptionsAndDependencies: It is assumed that the interfaces designed for the specified third-party software (e.g., **Outlook Express**, **Internet Explorer**) will maintain compatibility with their subsequent releases. This assumption implies that future updates to these external applications will not introduce breaking changes that would require significant re-engineering of the **znix** Framework's integration components. Any deviations from this compatibility may impact system functionality and require unforeseen development efforts.

The security offered by the listed third-party products (e.g., operating systems, email clients) forms the fundamental basis of the **znix** Framework's security posture. It is assumed that these underlying applications are inherently safe from significant vulnerabilities. The **znix** Framework will not attempt to re-implement security features already provided by these trusted components, relying instead on their established mechanisms. Therefore, the overall security of **znix** is dependent on the integrity of its integrated software environment.

The decision to support a specific set of **Microsoft Windows**-based applications is predicated on their widespread popularity among the intended user base. This selection assumes that catering to these applications will be sufficient to address the primary information management needs of home users, managerial staff, and researchers. Should user demographics or preferred application suites shift, this dependency may need to be re-evaluated to ensure market relevance.

externalInterfaceRequirements.userInterfaces: The **znix** Framework shall provide a Graphical User Interface (GUI) that enables users to browse and manage their information in a hierarchical manner. This interface must be visually and functionally similar to **Windows Explorer**, ensuring a familiar and intuitive user experience. The design shall prioritize ease of navigation and interaction for users with basic **Windows 2000/XP** operating system knowledge, minimizing the need for extensive training. The GUI must also include capabilities for users to define and visualize custom relationships between disparate Entities, enabling personalized information organization.
externalInterfaceRequirements.hardwareInterfaces: The **znix** system is designed to operate without requiring any specialized hardware interfaces. It leverages standard hardware components and peripherals typically found in personal computers, such as processors, memory, and storage devices. The framework relies on the operating system's native drivers and interfaces to interact with generic hardware, thus avoiding the need for custom hardware integration development. This approach simplifies deployment and reduces hardware-related compatibility issues.
externalInterfaceRequirements.softwareInterfaces: The **znix** Framework is designed to interface with several specific third-party applications to facilitate information capture and management. These include **Microsoft Outlook Express 6.0**, **Microsoft Outlook 2003 11.5**, and **Microsoft Address Book 6.0** for email and personnel information. For WWW documents, interfaces with **Microsoft Internet Explorer 6.0**, **Opera 7.5**, and **Firefox 1.0** are required. Textual document integration will be supported through **Microsoft Word 2003 11.5**. The framework will also operate on **Windows 2000 Professional** and **Windows XP** operating systems, requiring seamless interaction with their respective APIs and file systems. A public API will be exposed to allow **znix**-aware client processes to interact with the framework's core functionalities.
externalInterfaceRequirements.communicationsInterfaces: At its current stage of development, the **znix** Framework does not necessitate any direct communications interfaces for its core operation. The system is designed for single-user, local installation without inherent networking capabilities for data synchronization or remote access. Information capture from email (POP3, SMTP) and web browsing will leverage the communication functionalities of integrated third-party applications, with **znix** primarily intercepting and processing local data streams rather than managing network protocols itself. Future enhancements may introduce communication requirements, but these are not within the scope of this initial specification.

systemFeatures.name: Centralized Information Storage and Capture
systemFeatures.description: This feature provides a unified, object-based repository for all user information, moving away from disparate application-specific data silos. It enhances user productivity by centralizing data, making it universally accessible and manageable regardless of its origin. Technically, it establishes a robust data model capable of ingesting diverse data types from integrated third-party applications, categorizing them as first-class Entities or dependent Associates, and persisting them in a structured store. Priority: High, as it forms the foundational data layer for all other **znix** functionalities.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User creates or modifies a contact in **Microsoft Address Book**. Response: **znix** Framework intercepts the action, creates or updates an Entity under the 'People' category in the central store.

Stimulus: User sends an email via **Outlook Express** or **Outlook 2003**. Response: **znix** Framework intercepts the outbound email, creates an Associate for the message body, and links it to recipient Entities (creating new ones if necessary).

Stimulus: User receives an email from a POP3 server via **Outlook Express** or **Outlook 2003**. Response: **znix** Framework fetches the email, creates an Associate for the message body, and links it to the sender Entity (creating a new one if necessary).

Stimulus: User specifies the start of a research project via the **znix** GUI, then visits web sites using **Internet Explorer**, **Opera**, or **Firefox**. Response: **znix** Framework creates a new Entity for the project, and subsequently visited web pages are captured as Associates linked to this project Entity.

Stimulus: User invokes a special **znix** GUI after editing a document in **Microsoft Word 2003**. Response: The GUI allows the user to define relationships between the current document and existing Entities, saving the document to reflect these new hierarchical links.
functionalRequirements: Z-REQ-001: The system shall be capable of capturing information from **Microsoft Outlook Express 6.0**, **Microsoft Outlook 2003 11.5**, **Microsoft Address Book 6.0**, **Microsoft Internet Explorer 6.0**, **Opera 7.5**, **Firefox 1.0**, and **Microsoft Word 2003 11.5**.
Z-REQ-002: The system shall store captured information in a centralized data store, maintaining data integrity and consistency.
Z-REQ-003: The system shall categorize captured information as either a primary Entity or a dependent Associate based on its nature and context.
Z-REQ-004: The system shall create an Entity under the 'People' category when a new contact is added via the **Address Book**.
Z-REQ-005: The system shall allow users to modify, add, delete, or group 'People' Entities through a virtual folder interface.
Z-REQ-006: The system shall intercept outbound emails before dispatch to the SMTP server.
Z-REQ-007: The system shall create an Associate for the message body of each intercepted outbound email.
Z-REQ-008: The system shall identify recipients of outbound emails and create 'People' Entities if they do not already exist.
Z-REQ-009: The system shall link the email Associate to each identified recipient Entity for outbound emails.
Z-REQ-010: The system shall fetch inbound emails from the POP3 server.
Z-REQ-011: The system shall create an Associate for the message body of each inbound email.
Z-REQ-012: The system shall identify the sender of inbound emails and create a 'People' Entity if it does not already exist.
Z-REQ-013: The system shall link the email Associate to the sender Entity for inbound emails.
Z-REQ-014: The system shall allow users to specify the commencement of a web research project, triggering the archiving of subsequent web visits.
Z-REQ-015: The system shall spawn a new Entity in the Framework upon user specification of a research project name.
Z-REQ-016: The system shall create an Associate for each visited WWW document during a research project and link it to the project Entity.
Z-REQ-017: The system shall provide a special GUI for users to define relationships between textual documents (e.g., from **Word 2003**) and existing Entities.
Z-REQ-018: The system shall automatically save edited documents to disk in a manner that reflects the created relationships to other Entities.
Z-REQ-019: The system shall allow users to retrieve documents by navigating through established relationship paths.

systemFeatures.name: Hierarchical Information Browsing
systemFeatures.description: This feature empowers users to intuitively explore their archived information through a dynamic, user-defined hierarchical view. It provides significant user value by making complex data relationships understandable and navigable, mirroring familiar operating system interfaces. Architecturally, it requires a robust querying and rendering engine to present the object-based information store in a fluid, tree-like structure. The system must support the dynamic creation and display of various relationship types as defined by the user during information capture and linking. Priority: High, as it is the primary means of user interaction with the centralized information.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User launches the **znix** browser. Response: The system displays the central information store in a hierarchical view, similar to **Windows Explorer**.

Stimulus: User navigates through folders or relationships in the **znix** browser. Response: The system dynamically updates the display to show the selected information hierarchy, revealing contained Entities and Associates.

Stimulus: User activates the document linking GUI within the **znix** browser. Response: The system presents an interface allowing the user to draw arrows to and from the current item to other Entities, creating new relationships.
functionalRequirements: Z-REQ-020: The system shall enable users to view their stored information in a customizable hierarchical manner.
Z-REQ-021: The system shall include a browser capable of displaying the information hierarchy within a user interface similar to **Windows Explorer**.
Z-REQ-022: The system may optionally integrate its browsing capabilities directly into the **Windows Explorer** shell.
Z-REQ-023: The system shall ensure that the browser can display relationships based on inheritance, association, or any other user-defined criteria.
Z-REQ-024: The system shall integrate the document linking GUI (as described in Z-REQ-017) directly within the information browser for comprehensive relationship management.

systemFeatures.name: Information Archiving and Retrieval
systemFeatures.description: This feature allows users to archive portions of their information store to removable media while ensuring that the system retains awareness of the archived data. This provides user value by enabling efficient management of storage space and facilitating disaster recovery without losing track of information. From a technical standpoint, it involves mechanisms for data serialization to external devices and maintaining metadata 'residue' within the primary store. This residue must be robust enough to inform the user about archived content and guide retrieval. Priority: Medium, as it addresses data lifecycle management and storage optimization.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User selects a portion of the information store (e.g., all data related to a 'Person' Entity) for archiving to a CD-ROM or USB Pen Drive. Response: The system serializes the selected data to the specified removable storage media and leaves a persistent residue in the central data store indicating the archived status.

Stimulus: User attempts to access an Entity that has been archived. Response: The system does not declare the Entity as not found; instead, it informs the user that the Entity has been archived and optionally prompts the user to remount the relevant storage device for retrieval.
functionalRequirements: Z-REQ-025: The system shall allow users to archive arbitrary portions of their information store to removable storage media, specifically CD-ROMs and USB Pen Drives.
Z-REQ-026: The system shall leave a permanent residue in the central data store to indicate that a portion of information has been archived.
Z-REQ-027: The system shall inform the user when an Entity they are attempting to access has been archived.
Z-REQ-028: The system may optionally prompt the user to remount the relevant storage device when attempting to access archived information.

systemFeatures.name: Public Application Programming Interface (API)
systemFeatures.description: This feature provides a public API that exposes core **znix** Framework functionalities to external client processes, enabling third-party developers to build 'smart' applications. It creates significant value by fostering an ecosystem of interoperable applications, extending the utility and reach of **znix**. Technically, the API must encapsulate complex data manipulation logic into a clear, consistent, and well-documented interface, similar to established file system APIs, providing CRUD operations for Entities and relationships. Priority: High, as it is critical for extensibility and integration with the broader software landscape.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: A **znix**-aware client process calls the API to create a new Entity. Response: The API processes the request and creates the Entity within the **znix** data store.
Stimulus: A client process queries the API for Entities based on specific criteria. Response: The API executes the query and returns a list of matching Entities.
Stimulus: A client process uses the API to list all Entities within a specific hierarchical path. Response: The API returns a structured listing similar to a file system directory output.
functionalRequirements: Z-REQ-029: The **znix** Framework API shall expose functionality for creating Entities and Entity groups.
Z-REQ-030: The **znix** Framework API shall expose functionality for deleting Entities and Entity groups.
Z-REQ-031: The **znix** Framework API shall expose functionality for modifying Entities and Entity groups.
Z-REQ-032: The **znix** Framework API shall expose functionality for creating relationships between Entities.
Z-REQ-033: The **znix** Framework API shall expose functionality for deleting relationships between Entities.
Z-REQ-034: The **znix** Framework API shall expose functionality for modifying relationships between Entities.
Z-REQ-035: The **znix** Framework API shall provide an interface for listing and accessing Entities that is similar to a standard file system API (e.g., **System.IO** APIs of the **.NET Platform**).
Z-REQ-036: The **znix** Framework API shall support the execution of queries for the retrieval of Entities using various criteria.

performanceRequirements: Z-REQ-037: The **znix** Framework shall operate solely in a single-user mode, precluding any integrated networking capabilities at this stage of development. This constraint simplifies resource management and prevents performance bottlenecks associated with concurrent access. The system is optimized for individual usage, ensuring responsiveness without the overhead of network communication.

Z-REQ-038: The **znix** Framework Shell shall not exceed a maximum of 40 megabytes of RAM utilization during its operation. This strict memory footprint requirement is critical for ensuring the system runs comfortably on machines with limited resources, such as those with 256 megabytes of RAM. Adherence to this constraint guarantees efficient resource allocation and overall system stability, preventing performance degradation due to excessive memory consumption.
safetyRequirements: Z-REQ-039: The **znix** Framework shall implement robust data integrity checks to prevent unintended corruption of information by the user during various operations. This includes validation mechanisms for data input and transactional safeguards during storage and modification processes. The system aims to protect the user's information from accidental errors, ensuring consistency and reliability within the central data store. This is crucial for maintaining user trust and data accuracy.

Z-REQ-040: The **znix** Framework shall employ mechanisms to protect its information store from potential data loss or corruption in the event of unexpected system shutdowns or power failures. This may involve journaling, periodic backups, or other resilience strategies to ensure that the user's archived data remains recoverable. The system prioritizes data safety, providing a reliable repository for critical user information.
securityRequirements: Z-REQ-041: The **znix** Framework shall implement measures to guard against malicious scripts originating from remote websites that attempt to access or manipulate its internal information store. This protection is vital for maintaining the confidentiality and integrity of user data, preventing unauthorized external access. The framework will integrate with the operating system's security features to provide a robust defense against common web-based threats.

Z-REQ-042: The **znix** Framework shall incorporate internal safeguards to prevent unintended corruption of information by the user itself. This involves user interface design principles that minimize opportunities for accidental data deletion or modification, as well as internal validation rules. The system prioritizes data consistency and user-friendly error prevention, ensuring that user actions, even unintentional ones, do not compromise the integrity of their stored information.

Z-REQ-043: The **znix** Framework's security model shall leverage the established security mechanisms of the underlying operating system (**Windows 2000 Professional**, **Windows XP**) and integrated third-party applications. This approach assumes the inherent security of these platforms and focuses on preventing unauthorized access to the **znix** data store through its exposed interfaces. All interactions will conform to standard operating system security protocols.
softwareQualityAttributes: Z-REQ-044: The **znix** Framework's Graphical User Interfaces (GUIs) shall be designed for high usability, ensuring that users with a basic understanding of **Windows 2000/XP** operating systems can instinctively interact with them. This necessitates mimicking popular **Windows** application conventions to minimize typical training time required. The system aims for an intuitive user experience, allowing casual users to manage information with little or no prior instruction.

Z-REQ-045: The **znix** Framework shall exhibit a high degree of reliability, with a Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF) exceeding three months. This metric ensures that the system provides consistent and uninterrupted service to its users, minimizing unexpected downtime or data access issues. Rigorous testing and robust error handling mechanisms will be implemented to achieve this reliability target, contributing to user trust and system stability.

Z-REQ-046: The **znix** Framework shall be highly maintainable, requiring all coding conventions used during implementation to be documented and strictly adhered to. This practice ensures code consistency, readability, and ease of understanding for future maintenance and enhancement efforts. Proper documentation and standardized coding practices will significantly reduce the effort and time required for bug fixes and feature development.

Z-REQ-047: The **znix** Framework shall adhere to Open Source Standard Practices, ensuring its availability in both source and binary forms, free of charge. This attribute promotes transparency, community contribution, and adaptability by allowing developers to inspect, modify, and extend the system. Hosting on a popular Open Source distribution website will facilitate community engagement and support the project's long-term evolution.

Z-REQ-048: The **znix** Framework shall possess a degree of testability, meaning that its components and functionalities are designed in a modular fashion to facilitate independent verification and validation. This attribute ensures that the system can be thoroughly tested against its specified requirements, contributing to its overall quality and reliability. Testability will be considered during the architectural design and implementation phases.
businessRules: Z-REQ-049: The **znix** Framework itself shall be licensed under the GNU Library General Public License (LGPL), ensuring its free use, modification, and distribution while protecting the open-source nature of derived works. This licensing choice encourages broad adoption and community involvement, aligning with the project's open-source ethos. All redistributed versions must comply with the terms of the LGPL.

Z-REQ-050: The **znix** browser and any other tools distributed alongside the Framework shall be licensed under the GNU Public License (GPL). This licensing ensures that any modifications or derivative works of these client-side components also remain open source, promoting a fully transparent and collaborative development environment. Users and developers must adhere to GPL terms when using or redistributing these tools.

Z-REQ-051: All components associated with **znix** shall conform to the **Windows 2000 Logo Requirements**, ensuring compatibility and quality standards established by **Microsoft**. This compliance is critical for market acceptance and seamless integration within the **Windows** operating environment. Adherence to these guidelines guarantees a professional and reliable user experience.

Z-REQ-052: The system shall conform to the ISO 10646 (Unicode UTF-8) standard for character set encoding, ensuring global language support and proper rendering of diverse textual information. Optionally, the system may also conform to ISO 10646-1 (Unicode UTF-16) standards, providing flexibility for different encoding requirements. This broad character support is crucial for international users and varied information content.

otherRequirements: Z-REQ-053: The distribution website for **znix** shall include provisions for users to submit comments on the product and to compile a wish-list of desired features. This functionality is crucial for gathering user feedback and driving future development iterations. It promotes an iterative, user-centric development approach, ensuring the system evolves in response to community needs and suggestions.

glossary.definition: A research and development effort aiming to refine traditional information management systems, not an acronym. It suggests the aspiration for a final evolution for POSIX systems.

A collection of system services (daemons) that facilitate user interactions with information and expose lower-level functionality through a public API. Often used interchangeably with Project **znix** and Framework Shell.

A software application that interacts with the **znix** Framework, either by utilizing the public API or by directly accessing information presented by the Framework. Client applications need not be 'ZNIX-aware'.
A collection of Framework components enabling 'ZNIX-unaware' applications to utilize the Framework's capabilities without direct API integration.
The basic, first-class block that builds up **znix**'s information hierarchy. An Entity's identity is unique and non-dependent on any other Entity.

Similar to an Entity, but its identity is dependent on at least one Entity. The Framework's interest in an Associate depends solely on its relationship(s) to first-class citizens (Entities), leading to its removal if all related Entities lose interest.
Application Programming Interface, a set of defined methods for external software components to interact with **znix** Framework functionalities.
Graphical User Interface, the visual part of the **znix** system that users interact with directly, designed to be intuitive and familiar.
Mean Time Between Failure, a measure of system reliability indicating the average time a system performs without failing.
Software Requirements Specification, a document that describes the requirements for a software system.

flowchartDiagram.syntaxExplanation: This flowchart illustrates the general process of information capture and processing within the **znix** Framework. Nodes represent processes or decisions, and arrows indicate the flow of control. Decision nodes are diamond-shaped, while processes are rectangular. The diagram demonstrates how client application actions lead to Framework intervention, distinguishing between direct API calls and proxy mechanism usage. It then shows the Framework's internal logic for classifying information as an Entity or Associate before storage. All node labels are double-quoted if they contain spaces or special characters, and node IDs are strictly alphanumeric.
flowchartDiagram.code:
flowchart TD
    A1["Client Application Action"] --> A2{"ZNIX-Aware?"}
    A2 --> |Yes| A3["Call ZNIX API Directly"]
    A2 --> |No| A4["ZNIX Proxy Mechanism Intervenes"]
    A3 --> A5["ZNIX Framework Process"]
    A4 --> A5
    A5 --> A6{"Determine Information Type?"}
    A6 --> |Is Entity| A7["Create/Update Entity"]
    A6 --> |Is Associate| A8["Create/Update Associate"]
    A8 --> A9["Link Associate to Entity(s)"]
    A7 --> A10["Store Information"]
    A9 --> A10
    A10 --> A11["Confirm and Complete"]
    A11 --> EndProcess(["End"])
flowchartDiagram.caption: **znix** Information Capture Flow

sequenceDiagram.syntaxExplanation: This sequence diagram details the interaction sequence for an outbound email capture by the **znix** Framework. It identifies key participants: a User, the Client Email Application, the **znix** Framework, and the Data Store. Arrows represent messages, indicating communication flow and type (synchronous or asynchronous). Activations are used to denote the period a participant is performing an action, and they are carefully balanced. The `alt` block demonstrates conditional logic based on whether a recipient Entity already exists in the system. Message labels avoid special characters and provide clear descriptions of interactions.
sequenceDiagram.code:
sequenceDiagram
    participant User
    participant ClientEmailApp as "Client Email App"
    participant ZNIXFramework as "ZNIX Framework"
    participant DataStore as "Data Store"

    User->>ClientEmailApp: "Compose and Send Email"
    activate ClientEmailApp
    ClientEmailApp->>ZNIXFramework: "Intercept Outbound Email (Message Body)"
    activate ZNIXFramework
    ZNIXFramework->>ZNIXFramework: "Parse Email Content and Recipients"
    ZNIXFramework->>DataStore: "Query for Recipient Entities"
    activate DataStore
    DataStore-->>ZNIXFramework: "Return Existing Entities"
    deactivate DataStore

    alt New Recipient Entity Required
        ZNIXFramework->>DataStore: "Create Recipient Entity"
        activate DataStore
        DataStore-->>ZNIXFramework: "Confirmation"
        deactivate DataStore
    end

    ZNIXFramework->>DataStore: "Create Email Associate and Link to Recipient Entities"
    activate DataStore
    DataStore-->>ZNIXFramework: "Confirmation of Storage"
    deactivate DataStore
    deactivate ZNIXFramework
    ClientEmailApp->>SMTP: "Deliver Email to SMTP Server"
    deactivate ClientEmailApp
sequenceDiagram.caption: Outbound Email Information Capture Sequence

entityRelationshipDiagram.syntaxExplanation: This Entity Relationship Diagram (ERD) models the core data structure of the **znix** Framework, focusing on Entities, Associates, and their relationships with Categories and Users. Entities represent first-class information objects, while Associates are dependent information blocks.
