Relationship cardinalities define how many instances of one entity can relate to another, with labels clarifying the nature of the relationship. Primary Keys (PK) and Foreign Keys (FK) are explicitly defined for each entity to ensure data integrity and establish links between tables. Attribute names are alphanumeric, and relationship labels are strictly quoted.
entityRelationshipDiagram.caption: **znix** Core Information Model ERD

introduction.purpose: This Software Requirements Specification (SRS) details the functional and non-functional requirements for the System Administration Module of an Integrated Library System (ILS). The primary goal is to outline capabilities suitable for large, urban, multiple-branch, centralized library systems, drawing insights from the King County Library System. This module is designed to replace, enhance, and extend existing administrative functionalities of commercially available ILSes, introducing new features vital for modern library management.
introduction.documentConventions: This SRS adheres to IEEE 830-1998 standards for software requirements specifications, ensuring clarity, consistency, and completeness. Requirements are identified with a stable prefix 'L-REQ-' and sequentially numbered. Where relevant, requirements may reference process flowcharts that illustrate current System Administration approaches at King County Library System, serving as contextual information rather than prescriptive constraints for new software development. All terms and acronyms are defined in Appendix A: Glossary for universal understanding.
introduction.intendedAudience: The document targets a dual audience: library managers and staff who may contribute further requirements or commentary, and software project managers and developers responsible for implementation. It strives for high readability for non-technical readers while providing sufficient technical specificity for developers. The underlying assumption is a collaborative and iterative development environment where end-users actively participate in reviewing prototypes and refining both the user interface and software functionality. Readers are expected to possess a general understanding of core library services and processes.
introduction.productScope: The **library** System Administration Module is focused on managing every operational aspect of the Integrated Library System. This includes configuring the ILS for branches, patrons, collections, and circulation, alongside monitoring and troubleshooting server, database, and application performance. It also encompasses managing services, ports, APIs, user and group accounts, software installations, upgrades, and comprehensive data backups. This specification strictly focuses on the administrative functionalities, building upon existing ILS data structures and features, with other modules like Acquisitions, Serials Management, Circulation, and Cataloging being covered in separate documentation. The specification primarily addresses functional characteristics, with user interface and data structure details earmarked for iterative, prototype-oriented development.
references: IEEE 830-1998 IEEE Recommended Practice for Software Requirements Specifications.
ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018 Systems and software engineering — Life cycle processes — Requirements engineering.

overallDescription.productPerspective: The **library** System Administration Module is conceived as a crucial component within a larger, enterprise-level **Integrated Library System (ILS)**. It is not a standalone product but rather an integral part that enhances and extends the existing administrative capabilities of the overarching ILS. This module will supersede current commercial ILS functionalities, introducing advanced features essential for managing a complex, multi-branch **library** environment. It interacts seamlessly with other established ILS modules such as Acquisitions, Serials Management, Circulation, and Cataloging, providing centralized control over their operational parameters.
productFunctions: Configuration management of the **library** ILS for branches, patrons, collections, and circulation transactions.
Monitoring, troubleshooting, and controlling server, database, application, service, port, and API performance.
Management of user and group accounts, including their associated privileges and access levels.
Handling server and client software installation, upgrades, and updates across the system.
Executing comprehensive data backup procedures for databases, configuration files, and critical log files.
Enforcement and customization of business rules governing circulation, record visibility, and data integrity.
Providing detailed query and reporting tools for system performance, resource usage, and operational insights.

userClassesAndCharacteristics.characteristics: A Patron is a customer of the King County Library System, with or without a library card, whether on-site or remote. They utilize print, media, or electronic resources. While not direct administrators, their interactions (e.g., placing holds, accessing records via **OPAC**) are subject to administrative rules and monitored by the system.

Staff includes managers, librarians, library technicians, library assistants, and library pages. These individuals are actively involved in designing and providing **library** services. Their system access and permissions are defined by their roles and privileges, impacting how they interact with administrative functionalities, particularly in circulation and cataloging.

System Administrators are staff members primarily responsible for managing servers, databases, applications, services, ports, and **APIs** related to the **ILS**. They possess high-level technical expertise and require full access and control over system configurations, performance monitoring, security settings, and data recovery procedures. This group defines and manages user privileges and system-wide settings.

Managers include management staff who oversee various **library** processes. They require access to dashboards and reports for monitoring operational performance, such as circulation statistics and system health indicators. Their focus is on high-level oversight and decision-making, leveraging system data to optimize service delivery and resource allocation without necessarily engaging in low-level technical administration.

Library Managers, specifically Cluster and Site Managers, provide critical input to the design and implementation of **library** services. They need to understand the impact of system configurations on daily operations and may require access to customize certain business rules or view specific reports relevant to their respective branches or clusters. Their technical involvement is typically higher than general staff but lower than System Administrators.

Library Directors are members of the Library Executive Team who plan and direct overall **library** services and priorities. They require high-level aggregated reports and dashboards to assess system-wide performance, strategic resource utilization, and compliance. Their interaction with the administrative module is typically at a summary level, focusing on strategic oversight and long-term planning rather than day-to-day operations.

overallDescription.operatingEnvironment: The **library** system administration module is designed to support the demanding needs of a large, multiple-branch **library** system, specifically accommodating environments with up to 50 physical locations. It is expected to handle a substantial transaction volume, including approximately 20 million circulations and the purchasing and processing of over 500,000 new items annually. A critical requirement is the system's ability to process complex searches and generate comprehensive reports during peak operational hours without causing any disruption to other concurrent system functions, ensuring continuous service availability. This necessitates an efficient and robust underlying infrastructure.
designAndImplementationConstraints: The **library** System Administration Module shall utilize a fully relational database as its back-end, ensuring data integrity and flexible querying capabilities consistent with modern database management practices.

The user interfaces generated by the System Administration Module shall produce standards-compliant **HTML**, promoting cross-browser compatibility and adherence to web accessibility guidelines for a broad user base.

A dedicated development and training environment must be provided, equipped with the functionality to seamlessly migrate tested configurations to the production environment. This ensures a controlled release process and minimizes risks associated with changes to live systems.

User rights and privileges within the system shall be meticulously controlled through the implementation of security groups and role-based access control mechanisms. This granular approach will govern access for individual staff, workgroups, and arbitrary staff groupings, safeguarding sensitive data and functions.
userDocumentation: The software developer shall furnish complete data specifications for all critical record types, including authority, bibliographic, order, item, and hold/request records. These specifications are essential for records maintained or accessed by the **library** System Administration Module, ensuring clarity and consistency in data models.

The software developer shall provide a thorough, high-level description of major system processes. This includes detailed explanations of bibliographic record import and export procedures, validation against internal and external authority sources, and the generation of standard reports, offering comprehensive operational guidance.

The **library** system shall incorporate an online, hierarchical, and comprehensively cross-linked help system accessible via **HTML**. This help system will describe and illustrate all system functions, serving as a primary resource for user assistance and troubleshooting.
assumptionsAndDependencies: The **library** System Administration Module is assumed to operate as an integrated component of an overarching enterprise-level **library** Automation System, rather than a standalone application. Processes related to system administration are presumed to be consolidated at a central location, providing input and services to multiple distributed library branches.

The **library** System Administration Module is highly dependent on the data structures and core functionalities provided by the enterprise-level **library** Automation System, including its Acquisitions and Cataloging modules. It interfaces with various vendor websites through published **APIs** or automated transfers of standard-format data files, such as **USMARC21** and **EDIFACT**. Additionally, the module interacts with the public-facing **Online Public Access Catalog (OPAC)**, exchanging information relevant to patron and item statuses.

externalInterfaceRequirements.userInterfaces: The **library** system administration module will provide a graphical user interface accessible through standard **web browsers** and a dedicated **Windows-compatible client**. Web accessibility mandates support for **Microsoft Internet Explorer** (v6.0 and later) and **Mozilla Firefox** (v2.0 and later), ensuring broad compatibility. The interface must fully support screen-reading and screen-magnification software, adhering to accessibility guidelines for an inclusive user experience.
externalInterfaceRequirements.hardwareInterfaces: The **library** system shall interface with server hardware running **Linux** or **Solaris** operating systems, accommodating the core application and database. It will also manage connectivity to various client workstations, overseeing their software installations and updates. Furthermore, the system is required to support a range of printing devices, including receipt printers, standard document printers, label printers, and specialized forms printers, ensuring compatibility with all **Windows**-based printer drivers.
externalInterfaceRequirements.softwareInterfaces: The **library** system will primarily interface with a **SQL-based relational database** back-end, enabling direct **SQL** querying and acting as an **ODBC** source. It is tightly coupled with other **Integrated Library System (ILS)** modules, specifically Acquisitions, Cataloging, Circulation, and the **OPAC**, for data exchange related to patrons, items, and transactions. Integration with external vendor systems will occur via published **APIs** and standard data exchange formats like **USMARC21** and **EDIFACT**. Support for third-party backup solutions, such as **EMC NetWorker**, is also required to ensure robust data recovery capabilities. Communication between software components will be managed through defined system interfaces and ports.
externalInterfaceRequirements.communicationsInterfaces: The **library** system will utilize **SMTP** for all outgoing email communications, facilitating reliable delivery of notifications and alerts with configurable settings for masquerading and bounce management. Secure data transfer with external entities will be ensured through support for **SFTP** (both active and passive modes), **SSL**, and **SSH** protocols, with vendor-specific configurability. Network protocols will also govern real-time data processing, ensuring timely updates for operational data like pull lists and shelf-reading information. All communications involving sensitive patron data will be encrypted to maintain confidentiality and integrity, complying with security best practices.

systemFeatures.name: SQL-based Database Management
systemFeatures.description: The **library** system will leverage a fully relational, **SQL-based database** to ensure robust data management and flexible data access. This design facilitates direct **SQL** querying capabilities against all database tables, allowing advanced analytics and custom reporting for administrative staff. Furthermore, the database will be accessible as an **ODBC** source, enhancing interoperability with various external tools and applications. This approach provides transparency and full accessibility to all stored data, supporting comprehensive data management. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Administrator executes a custom SQL query. Response: The system shall return query results from the specified tables.
Stimulus: External application attempts to connect via ODBC. Response: The system shall provide database access as an ODBC source, subject to authentication.
functionalRequirements: The system shall operate on a fully relational, SQL-based database system.
The system shall allow running SQL queries against any table in the database.
The system shall provide access to the database as an ODBC source.
The system shall ensure all data tables and data storage are fully accessible.

systemFeatures.name: Real-time Processing
systemFeatures.description: The **library** system is designed to provide real-time data processing capabilities across critical functions. This ensures that operational data, such as pull lists for items to be retrieved or shelf-reading statuses, are immediately updated and accurate at the point of viewing or printing. Real-time processing enhances operational efficiency and ensures that staff and patrons have access to the most current information, supporting dynamic library workflows. This capability is vital for maintaining an efficient and responsive library environment. Priority: Medium.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Staff views a pull list. Response: The system shall display the pull list with up-to-date item statuses.
Stimulus: Staff performs a shelf-reading operation. Response: The system shall update item locations and statuses in real-time.
functionalRequirements: The system shall provide real-time processing for operational data.
The system shall ensure pull lists are up-to-date at the time of viewing or printing.
The system shall support live shelf-reading and weeding processes.

systemFeatures.name: Concurrent Record Access and Sharing
systemFeatures.description: The **library** system will support simultaneous access and updates to patron and item records by multiple staff members and patrons. This concurrent access is critical for various touchpoints, including staff check-in/check-out terminals, self check-out stations, **SIP2/NCIP2** protocols, **APIs**, and the **OPAC**. Access to fields will be governed by assigned privileges, allowing staff to view all fields while patrons access only selected information. The system will manage concurrent changes by warning users when a record has been modified since it was last displayed, ensuring data integrity. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Multiple staff members attempt to update the same patron record. Response: The system shall allow concurrent access and warn users of recent changes to the record.
Stimulus: Patron accesses their account via OPAC. Response: The system shall display only the fields authorized for patron access.
functionalRequirements: The system shall allow multiple staff members and patrons to simultaneously access and update patron and item records.
The system shall support record access and updates from staff check-in/check-out terminals, self check-out stations, SIP2/NCIP2 protocols, APIs, and the OPAC.
The system shall display all patron and item fields to staff based on assigned privileges.
The system shall allow patrons to access only selected fields based on assigned privileges.
The system shall warn users when a record has been changed since it was last displayed.

systemFeatures.name: Record Lock Administration
systemFeatures.description: The **library** system will provide robust record lock management capabilities to prevent data conflicts during concurrent access. For any patron or item record, staff will be able to identify its current usage status, including the specific location, user, and the date and time it was placed under lock. This feature is essential for administrators to diagnose and resolve issues related to blocked record access, ensuring smooth **library** operations and maintaining data consistency. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Administrator queries the usage status of a specific item record. Response: The system shall display the record's location, user, and timestamp of its last use or lock.
Stimulus: Staff tries to access a locked patron record. Response: The system shall indicate that the record is in use and by whom.
functionalRequirements: The system shall identify where any patron or item record is currently in use.
The system shall display the location, user, date, and time a record was placed under lock.

systemFeatures.name: User Account and Privilege Management
systemFeatures.description: The **library** system will support both individual and shared staff login accounts, enabling flexible and secure access control. Access to various modules within the system will be granted through the assignment of 'roles' or 'privileges,' allowing granular control over each account's capabilities. Individual logins will facilitate user-level preferences and maintain a comprehensive audit trail of actions performed by specific staff members, enhancing accountability and system transparency. This approach ensures security while accommodating diverse operational needs. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Staff logs in with an individual account. Response: The system shall apply user-level preferences and log their activity for audit purposes.
Stimulus: Administrator assigns a new role to a staff account. Response: The system shall update the account's access privileges to specific modules immediately.
functionalRequirements: The system shall support individual staff login accounts.
The system shall support shared staff login accounts.
The system shall grant access to modules based on assigned 'roles' or 'privileges'.
The system shall allow user-level preferences for individual login accounts.
The system shall maintain an audit trail for individual login accounts.

systemFeatures.name: System Documentation Access
systemFeatures.description: The **library** system will provide comprehensive, library-specific documentation tailored to its particular version in use. This documentation will adhere to standard formats for technical documentation, ensuring clarity and consistency. It will be web-based, indexed, organized by function, and easily searchable, facilitating quick access for staff to find necessary information. This resource is vital for training, troubleshooting, and maintaining system knowledge. Priority: Medium.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Staff searches for documentation on a specific function. Response: The system shall return relevant, version-specific documentation accessible via a web browser.
Stimulus: Administrator needs to understand a system component. Response: The system shall provide web-based, indexed, and searchable documentation.
functionalRequirements: The system shall provide library-specific system documentation.
The system documentation shall follow standard formats for technical documentation.
The system documentation shall be specific to the particular version of the software in use.
The system documentation shall be web-based, indexed, organized by function, and easily searchable.

systemFeatures.name: System Upgrade Guidelines Provision
systemFeatures.description: The **library** system will provide clear and comprehensive written guidelines for all system upgrades and updates. These guidelines will detail the procedures for updating both servers and clients, ensuring a smooth and predictable upgrade process. Each upgrade package will include a list of new, changed, and removed features, enabling administrators to understand the impact of the update and plan accordingly. This structured approach to upgrades minimizes disruption and ensures system stability. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Administrator initiates a system upgrade. Response: The system shall provide written guidelines for updating servers and clients.
Stimulus: Administrator reviews an upgrade package. Response: The system shall display a list of new, changed, and removed features.
functionalRequirements: The system shall include written guidelines for updating servers and clients during upgrades and updates.
The system shall provide a list of new, changed, and removed features with each upgrade.

systemFeatures.name: Configuration File Access
systemFeatures.description: The **library** system will provide comprehensive access to all configuration files, empowering system administrators with full control over system settings. This accessibility is crucial for advanced troubleshooting, fine-tuning system behavior, and implementing custom configurations not available through graphical interfaces. Providing direct access ensures maximum flexibility and administrative oversight, allowing expert users to manage the system precisely. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Administrator requires direct access to a specific configuration file. Response: The system shall provide access to the requested configuration file.
Stimulus: Administrator modifies a configuration file. Response: The system shall apply the changes as per system specifications.
functionalRequirements: The system shall provide access to all configuration files.

systemFeatures.name: Log File Access and Management
systemFeatures.description: The **library** system will offer full access to all log files, enabling thorough monitoring and diagnostics without interrupting system operations. Administrators will be able to review logs in real-time while the system continues to function normally. The system will also provide capabilities to enable, disable, and set specific retention thresholds for log files, allowing for efficient storage management and compliance with data retention policies. This feature is fundamental for troubleshooting, auditing, and performance analysis. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Administrator needs to diagnose a system issue. Response: The system shall provide full access to all log files for review.
Stimulus: Administrator configures log file retention. Response: The system shall apply the specified retention threshold for log files.
functionalRequirements: The system shall provide full access to all log files.
The system shall allow log files to be reviewed without stopping the system.
The system shall allow logs to be enabled, disabled, and set to a specific retention threshold.

systemFeatures.name: Root Shell Access
systemFeatures.description: The **library** system will provide access to the root shell, enabling authorized system administrators to perform advanced system-level operations and maintenance tasks. This access is crucial for deep-level diagnostics, patching, and custom script execution that may not be available through standard administrative interfaces. This feature, while requiring strict access control, grants ultimate flexibility for experienced administrators to manage the underlying operating system environment. Priority: Low.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Authorized administrator requests root shell access. Response: The system shall provide secure access to the root shell.
Stimulus: Administrator executes a system-level command via root shell. Response: The system shall execute the command with root privileges.
functionalRequirements: The system shall provide access to the root shell for authorized administrators.

systemFeatures.name: Custom Dashboard Configuration
systemFeatures.description: The **library** system will support the creation of custom dashboards, allowing administrators to tailor their view of critical system information. These dashboards can display both current and historical data related to system performance, record creation and modification, and circulation transactions. Administrators will possess the capability to design these dashboards and grant specific access permissions to selected users and groups, ensuring relevant information reaches the appropriate personnel for effective decision-making. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Administrator creates a new dashboard. Response: The system shall provide tools for selecting and arranging data widgets on the dashboard.
Stimulus: Administrator assigns dashboard access to a user group. Response: The system shall enable the specified user group to view the custom dashboard.
functionalRequirements: The system shall support the creation of custom dashboards.
The system shall display current and historical data about system performance, record creation and modification, and circulation transactions on dashboards.
The system shall allow administrators to create dashboards.
The system shall allow administrators to give access to selected users and groups for dashboards.

systemFeatures.name: System Resource Monitoring
systemFeatures.description: The **library** system will provide comprehensive support for **SNMP** and facilitate robust monitoring of system resources, including disk space, **CPU** load, memory load, system processes, interfaces, and ports. Administrators can configure alert thresholds for these metrics, ensuring timely notification of potential issues. Alerts can be delivered via administrative dashboards, email messages, and text messages to an unlimited number of recipients, using any or all specified alert methods, enabling proactive problem resolution. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: System resource usage exceeds a configured threshold. Response: The system shall trigger an alert and send notifications via dashboard, email, and/or text message.
Stimulus: Administrator configures a new alert threshold. Response: The system shall apply the new threshold for monitoring the specified resource.
functionalRequirements: The system shall provide full support for SNMP.
The system shall support monitoring of system resources including disk space, CPU load, memory load, system processes, system interfaces, and ports.
The system shall allow alert thresholds to be configurable.
The system shall send alerts via administrative dashboards, email messages, and text messages.
The system shall send alerts to an unlimited number of recipients via any or all alert methods.

systemFeatures.name: Advanced Record Lock Administration
systemFeatures.description: The **library** system will provide advanced administrative capabilities for managing record locks. This includes the ability to set thresholds on the duration records can remain locked, preventing indefinite resource contention. Furthermore, for all record types, the system will provide a comprehensive list of records currently in sustained use or a locked condition. From the same administrative console, authorized staff will have the ability to unlock one or more of these records, facilitating resolution of access conflicts and ensuring continuous operation. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Administrator sets a new record lock duration threshold. Response: The system shall apply the specified threshold to all applicable record types.
Stimulus: Administrator requests a list of all currently locked records. Response: The system shall display a list of records in sustained use or locked condition.
Stimulus: Administrator selects one or more locked records to unlock. Response: The system shall immediately release the locks on the selected records.
functionalRequirements: The system shall allow setting thresholds on the length of time records are locked.
The system shall provide a list of records in sustained use or locked condition for all record types.
The system shall provide the ability to unlock one or more records from the administrative console.

systemFeatures.name: System Performance Dashboard Display
systemFeatures.description: The **library** system will feature a dedicated dashboard for comprehensive performance monitoring and management. This dashboard will provide detailed identification of active processes, including their process ID, owner username, **IP address** (if applicable), **CPU utilization**, memory utilization, and run time. It will specifically highlight runaway processes, enabling quick identification and intervention. System status will be represented through clear visual indicators, such as green and red lights, offering an at-a-glance overview of system health. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Administrator accesses the system performance dashboard. Response: The system shall display real-time performance metrics and process details.
Stimulus: Administrator observes a runaway process indicator. Response: The system shall highlight the runaway process for immediate attention.
functionalRequirements: The system shall provide a dashboard of performance monitoring and management tools.
The system shall identify processes with process ID, owner username, IP address (if applicable), CPU utilization, memory utilization, and run time.
The system shall identify runaway processes.
The system shall represent system status by visual indicators (e.g., green and red lights).

systemFeatures.name: Server Management Console
systemFeatures.description: The **library** system will provide a dedicated server management console for controlling fundamental server and software operations. This console will include utilities for performing software shutdown, software startup, server shutdown, and server restart. These functionalities empower authorized administrators to manage the operational state of the server and its hosted applications directly from a centralized interface, ensuring controlled system availability and maintenance. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Administrator initiates a software shutdown from the console. Response: The system shall execute the software shutdown utility.
Stimulus: Administrator performs a server restart from the console. Response: The system shall execute the server restart utility.
functionalRequirements: The system shall provide a server management console.
The server management console shall include a software shutdown utility.
The server management console shall include a software startup utility.
The server management console shall include a server shutdown utility.
The server management console shall include a server restart utility.

systemFeatures.name: Application Status Dashboard
systemFeatures.description: The **library** system will feature an administrative dashboard dedicated to displaying critical application-level information. This dashboard will provide an overview of the last full and incremental backups, the last planned and unplanned system reboots, and the most recent software upgrade details, including the current software version. It will also show transactions waiting to be processed, the current size of log files, the count of records by type (item, bibliographic, patron, etc.), and database utilization metrics such as size and active processes, offering a comprehensive application health snapshot. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Administrator views the application dashboard. Response: The system shall display the last backup status, software version, and transaction queue.
Stimulus: Administrator monitors database utilization. Response: The system shall show current database size and running processes.
functionalRequirements: The system shall provide an administrative dashboard displaying the last full and incremental backup.
The system shall display the last planned and unplanned system reboot.
The system shall display the last software upgrade and current software version.
The system shall display transactions waiting to be processed.
The system shall display the size of log-files.
The system shall display the current count of records by record type (item, bibliographic, patron etc).
The system shall display database utilization (size, processes running).

systemFeatures.name: Client Management Console
systemFeatures.description: The **library** system will provide a dedicated client management console to oversee all workstations running client software. This console will display essential information such as the workstation name and **IP address** for each connected client. Furthermore, it will offer utilities for managing and terminating client sessions, enabling administrators to troubleshoot connectivity issues, enforce policies, or recover resources. This centralized management capability streamlines the administration of distributed client applications. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Administrator accesses the client management console. Response: The system shall display a list of workstations running client software with their names and IP addresses.
Stimulus: Administrator selects a client session to terminate. Response: The system shall execute the utility to kill the selected client session.
functionalRequirements: The system shall provide a management console displaying workstations running client software.
The management console shall display workstation name and IP address.
The management console shall include utilities for managing and killing client sessions.

systemFeatures.name: Circulation Performance Dashboard
systemFeatures.description: The **library** system will include a dedicated circulation dashboard to display key performance indicators related to library item movement. This dashboard will feature metrics such as check-outs per hour, check-ins per hour, holds placed per hour, and holds paged per day. These indicators can be configured to show data specific to a single branch or aggregated system-wide, providing valuable insights into operational efficiency and patron demand. This tool assists managers in monitoring and optimizing circulation workflows. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Manager views the circulation dashboard for a specific branch. Response: The system shall display circulation key performance indicators limited to that branch.
Stimulus: Manager views system-wide circulation data. Response: The system shall display aggregated circulation key performance indicators.
functionalRequirements: The system shall provide a circulation dashboard showing key performance indicators.
The circulation dashboard shall display check-outs per hour, check-ins per hour, holds placed per hour, and holds paged per day.
The indicators can be limited to a single branch or set to system-wide.

systemFeatures.name: Centralized Configuration File Console
systemFeatures.description: The **library** system will provide a single, centralized console for accessing all configuration files. This console will offer granular control over read and write permissions, allowing administrators to assign specific access rights to individual configuration files for different users and groups. This ensures that only authorized personnel can modify critical system settings, enhancing security and preventing unauthorized changes, while maintaining flexibility for delegated administrative tasks. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Administrator accesses the configuration file console. Response: The system shall display a list of all configuration files.
Stimulus: Administrator assigns write permission to a user group for a specific file. Response: The system shall update the access control list for that configuration file.
functionalRequirements: The system shall provide a single console with access to all configuration files.
The system shall allow read and write permission to individual configuration files to be assigned to users and groups.

systemFeatures.name: Log File Viewing Dashboard
systemFeatures.description: The **library** system will offer a dedicated dashboard for locating and viewing log files. This centralized interface streamlines the process of accessing diagnostic and activity logs, making it easier for administrators to monitor system events, troubleshoot issues, and audit system behavior. The dashboard will provide a user-friendly way to navigate through various log sources and retrieve relevant information efficiently. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Administrator accesses the log-file dashboard. Response: The system shall display a list of available log files.
Stimulus: Administrator selects a specific log file to view. Response: The system shall display the content of the selected log file.
functionalRequirements: The system shall provide a dashboard for locating and viewing log files.

systemFeatures.name: Unified Job Scheduling Console
systemFeatures.description: The **library** system will provide a unified interface for reviewing and controlling all scheduled tasks. This console will manage a diverse range of jobs, including staff-scheduled tasks, automated reports, scheduled imports and exports, and software updates. By centralizing control, administrators can efficiently monitor the status of ongoing and pending operations, modify existing schedules, or intervene if necessary, ensuring proper execution of all automated processes. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Administrator accesses the job scheduling console. Response: The system shall display a list of all scheduled tasks, reports, imports, and exports.
Stimulus: Administrator modifies the schedule of an automated report. Response: The system shall update the execution time for the specified report.
functionalRequirements: The system shall provide a single interface for reviewing and controlling scheduled tasks.
The scheduled tasks shall include staff-scheduled tasks, automated reports, scheduled imports and exports, and software updates.

systemFeatures.name: Email Configuration Management
systemFeatures.description: The **library** system will provide the ability to access and edit email configuration settings, streamlining the management of system-generated communications. This feature will include a quick menu offering common settings such as masquerading, log retention, and bounce management, simplifying complex configurations. Administrators can fine-tune how the system sends notifications, alerts, and other email-based communications, ensuring proper delivery and management of outgoing messages. Priority: Medium.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Administrator accesses email configuration settings. Response: The system shall display editable email settings and a quick menu of common options.
Stimulus: Administrator changes the email masquerading setting. Response: The system shall apply the new masquerading configuration for outgoing emails.
functionalRequirements: The system shall provide ability to access and edit email configuration.
The email configuration interface shall include a quick menu of common settings such as masquerading, log retention, and bounce management.

systemFeatures.name: SMTP Support for Email Transport
systemFeatures.description: The **library** system will inherently support **SMTP** as its protocol for email transport, ensuring reliable and standardized communication for all system-generated emails. This foundational support allows for seamless integration with existing email infrastructure and services. By using **SMTP**, the system ensures that notifications, alerts, and other essential communications are delivered effectively and conform to industry standards. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: System needs to send an alert email. Response: The system shall use SMTP for email transport.
Stimulus: Administrator configures an email recipient. Response: The system shall utilize SMTP to deliver test emails to verify configuration.
functionalRequirements: The system shall support SMTP for email transport.

systemFeatures.name: Business Rules Enforcement
systemFeatures.description: The **library** system will enforce restrictions based on predefined business rules to maintain data integrity and operational consistency. Examples include preventing the deletion of item records that are currently in a checked-out status or prohibiting the deletion of bibliographic records with existing holds. This feature ensures that critical **library** processes and data states are respected, preventing accidental or unauthorized actions that could compromise the integrity of the library's collection and patron services. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Staff attempts to delete a checked-out item record. Response: The system shall prevent the deletion and display an error message based on business rules.
Stimulus: Staff attempts to delete a bibliographic record with active holds. Response: The system shall prevent the deletion due to the enforced business rule.
functionalRequirements: The system shall support restrictions based on business rules.
The system shall prevent the deletion of item records that are in checked-out status.
The system shall prevent the deletion of bibliographic records with existing holds.

systemFeatures.name: Record Suppression Rules Management
systemFeatures.description: The **library** system will provide customizable 'Rules of Suppression' to control the visibility of authority, bibliographic, order, and item records. These rules will determine whether specific records are viewable by patrons and staff in both staff and **OPAC** interfaces. Visibility can be configured for specific workgroups, all staff and patrons at particular locations, or globally for all staff and patrons. This granular control over record visibility ensures that sensitive information is protected and that users only see relevant data according to defined policies. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Patron searches for a suppressed bibliographic record in OPAC. Response: The system shall not display the record based on suppression rules.
Stimulus: Staff from a specific workgroup views a record. Response: The system shall display the record if the suppression rules allow visibility to that workgroup.
functionalRequirements: The system shall provide customizable 'Rules of Suppression'.
The system shall specify whether patrons and staff can view authority, bibliographic, order, and item records in staff and public (OPAC) interfaces.
The system shall allow records to be visible to specific workgroups only.
The system shall allow records to be visible to all staff and patrons at specific locations.
The system shall allow records to be visible to all staff and all patrons.

systemFeatures.name: Requesting Rules Configuration
systemFeatures.description: The **library** system will enable the creation and modification of requesting rules that govern whether a patron can place a hold on an item. These rules can evaluate various criteria, including patron type, current number of holds, current patron account balance, item type, item status, and owning location code. For instance, rules might prohibit holds on on-order **CD** titles while allowing them for other on-order items. The system will also specify whether staff with particular privileges or roles can override specific requesting criteria, providing flexibility for exceptional circumstances. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Patron attempts to place a hold on an item. Response: The system shall evaluate requesting rules based on patron and item criteria.
Stimulus: Patron is prohibited from placing a hold due to requesting rules. Response: The system shall display a message explaining the restriction.
Stimulus: Staff with override privileges attempts to place a hold despite a restriction. Response: The system shall allow the override if the staff's role permits.
functionalRequirements: The system shall allow creation and modification of requesting rules.
Requesting rules shall determine whether a patron can place a hold on an item.
Requesting rules may evaluate patron type, current number of holds, current patron account balance, item type, item status, owning location code, and other criteria.
Requesting rules shall specify whether staff with specific privileges or roles can override specific criteria.

systemFeatures.name: Loan Rules Configuration
systemFeatures.description: The **library** system will support the creation and modification of loan rules to control item check-outs, calculate loan periods, and determine renewal limits. These rules can evaluate factors such as patron type, current number of items checked out, current patron account balance, item type, item status, owning location code, and check-out location code. For example, rules may prohibit checking out items with an unavailable status, such as those with a triggered hold or already checked out. Loan rules will also access check-out location open/closed schedules for accurate due date calculation and specify if specific criteria can be overridden by privileged staff. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Staff attempts to check out an item for a patron. Response: The system shall apply loan rules to determine if the check-out is allowed, calculate the due date, and enforce renewal limits.
Stimulus: A loan rule prohibits a check-out. Response: The system shall display a message indicating the reason for the prohibition.
Stimulus: Staff with override privileges attempts to check out a restricted item. Response: The system shall allow the override if the staff's role permits.
functionalRequirements: The system shall allow creation and modification of loan rules.
Loan rules shall allow or disallow check-out of items.
Loan rules shall calculate loan periods and determine renewal limits.
Loan rules may evaluate patron type, current number of items checked out, current patron account balance, item type, item status, owning location code, check-out location code, and other criteria.
Loan rules can access check-out location open/closed schedules in calculating due dates.
Loan rules shall specify whether a specific criteria may be overridden by staff with specific privileges or roles.

systemFeatures.name: Field-level Data Validation
systemFeatures.description: The **library** system will provide comprehensive data validation capabilities, allowing administrators to specify default values, validation rules, automatic formatting, and required status for any field. This feature ensures data accuracy and consistency across all records within the system. By enforcing these rules at the point of data entry or modification, the system reduces errors, improves data quality, and streamlines workflows for staff. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Staff enters data into a field with validation rules. Response: The system shall validate the input against the specified rules and prompt for correction if invalid.
Stimulus: Staff creates a new record. Response: The system shall apply default values and mark required fields for completion.
functionalRequirements: The system shall provide the ability to specify default value for any field.
The system shall provide the ability to specify data validation for any field.
The system shall provide the ability to specify automatic formatting for any field.
The system shall provide the ability to specify required status for any field.

systemFeatures.name: Live System Backup
systemFeatures.description: The **library** system will provide robust capabilities to perform live incremental and full backups of data and transaction logs. This ensures that critical system information is regularly protected against loss or corruption, even during continuous operation. The system will also support integration with third-party backup software, such as **EMC NetWorker**, offering flexibility and compatibility with existing backup infrastructure and strategies, enhancing data security and recoverability. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Administrator schedules a full system backup. Response: The system shall perform a live full backup of data and transaction logs.
Stimulus: Administrator integrates third-party backup software. Response: The system shall support the use of the specified third-party backup software for data backup.
functionalRequirements: The system shall provide capability to perform live incremental backups of data and transaction logs.
The system shall provide capability to perform live full backups of data and transaction logs.
The system shall support use of third-party backup software such as EMC NetWorker.

systemFeatures.name: Data Rollback and Revision Control
systemFeatures.description: The **library** system will log all data changes, such as record deletions, and provide 'undo' functionality to reverse unintended modifications. Ideally, the system will offer comprehensive revision control capabilities, allowing administrators to track changes over time and revert to previous states of records. This feature is crucial for maintaining data integrity, recovering from accidental errors, and auditing changes, providing a safety net for administrative actions. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Administrator accidentally deletes a record. Response: The system shall log the deletion and offer an 'undo' functionality.
Stimulus: Administrator initiates a data rollback to a previous state. Response: The system shall revert the data to the specified previous revision.
functionalRequirements: The system shall log data changes, such as record deletions.
The system shall provide 'undo' functionality for data changes.
The system shall ideally provide revision control capabilities.

systemFeatures.name: Server Clustering for Failover
systemFeatures.description: The **library** system will incorporate the ability to cluster servers, providing essential failover capability. This design ensures high availability and business continuity by allowing the system to automatically switch to a standby server in case of a primary server failure. Server clustering minimizes downtime, prevents service interruptions, and protects against data loss, which is critical for maintaining continuous **library** operations and access to resources. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Primary server experiences a critical failure. Response: The system shall automatically failover to a standby server within the cluster.
Stimulus: Administrator configures server clustering. Response: The system shall establish and manage the server cluster for failover.
functionalRequirements: The system shall provide the ability to cluster servers for failover capability.

systemFeatures.name: Patron Data Security in Transit
systemFeatures.description: The **library** system will ensure that all patron data remains secure during transfers to and from the system. This encompasses data in transit across networks, ensuring that personal and sensitive information is protected from unauthorized access or interception. Employing industry-standard encryption and secure communication protocols will be paramount to upholding patron privacy and maintaining compliance with data protection regulations, fostering trust in the **library**'s services. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Patron's personal data is transmitted from a client to the server. Response: The system shall encrypt the patron data during transfer to ensure security.
Stimulus: External system requests patron data via an API. Response: The system shall transmit patron data securely using established protocols.
functionalRequirements: The system shall ensure patron data is secure in all transfers to and from the system.

systemFeatures.name: Granular User Account Privilege Control
systemFeatures.description: The **library** system will empower administrative staff with full visibility and control over user privileges. This capability allows administrators to manage access rights for all users, defining what functions and data each user can access within the system. Granular control over privileges ensures that staff members only have the necessary permissions for their roles, enhancing system security, preventing unauthorized actions, and facilitating compliance with internal policies. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Administrator reviews a user's privileges. Response: The system shall display the full set of privileges assigned to the user.
Stimulus: Administrator modifies a user's privileges. Response: The system shall update the user's access rights based on the changes.
functionalRequirements: System administrative staff shall have full visibility and control of user privileges.

systemFeatures.name: Secure Communication Protocol Support
systemFeatures.description: The **library** system will provide robust support for various secure communication protocols, including **SFTP**, **SSL**, and **SSH**. **SFTP** will be supported in both active and passive modes, with configurability per vendor to accommodate different external systems. This comprehensive support for secure protocols ensures that all data exchanges, file transfers, and remote access operations are encrypted and protected, safeguarding sensitive information and maintaining the integrity of system communications. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: System initiates a file transfer with a vendor via SFTP. Response: The system shall establish an SFTP connection in the configured mode (active or passive).
Stimulus: User accesses the system remotely via SSH. Response: The system shall establish a secure SSH connection for remote access.
functionalRequirements: The system shall support secure protocols, including SFTP, SSL, and SSH.
SFTP shall be supported in both active and passive modes, configurable per vendor.

systemFeatures.name: MARC Record Import and Export
systemFeatures.description: The **library** system will support the import and export of **MARC bibliographic** and authority records. This functionality will allow for single or batch processing, enabling the transfer of all fields or selected fields to and from vendors, including **OCLC**. Imported records can overlay existing short or full bibliographic records, facilitating efficient updates. Furthermore, imported batches can be maintained and manipulated as selection lists, enhancing cataloging and collection management workflows. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Staff imports a batch of MARC bibliographic records from a vendor. Response: The system shall process the import, allowing for field selection and overlay options.
Stimulus: Staff exports selected MARC authority records. Response: The system shall generate the MARC file with the specified fields for export.
functionalRequirements: MARC bibliographic and authority records can be imported and exported.
Records can be imported/exported singly and in batch.
All fields or selected fields can be imported/exported to and from vendors including OCLC.
Imported records can overlay existing short or full bibliographic records.
Imported batches can be maintained and manipulated as selection lists.

systemFeatures.name: Record Sets Management
systemFeatures.description: The **library** system will support an unlimited number of record sets, providing flexible grouping of records for various administrative tasks. These sets will allow for batch import and export of members, enabling efficient bulk operations. Record sets can serve as the basis for batch field updates, as limiting scopes for queries, and for deleting original records. The system will provide review capabilities prior to deletion, write errors to a log file, and offer the ability to undo one or more deletions, ensuring data integrity and administrative control. Priority: Medium.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Administrator creates a new record set. Response: The system shall allow selection of records to be included in the set.
Stimulus: Administrator initiates a batch field update on a record set. Response: The system shall apply the updates to all members of the set, logging any errors.
Stimulus: Administrator requests to delete a record set. Response: The system shall prompt for review prior to deletion and provide undo functionality.

Record sets can be used as a limiting scope for queries.

The system shall write errors to a log file during batch deletion from a set.
The system shall provide the ability to undo one or more deletions from a set.

systemFeatures.name: Staff Account Creation Interface
systemFeatures.description: The **library** system will provide a dedicated interface for creating new staff accounts, streamlining the onboarding process for new personnel. The account creation process will include configurable templates for use by account administrators, ensuring consistency and efficiency. It will also offer granular privileges for account creation, modification, and deletion, allowing administrators to delegate account management tasks securely while maintaining control over sensitive operations. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Administrator initiates the creation of a new staff account. Response: The system shall display a dedicated interface with configurable templates.
Stimulus: Administrator modifies an existing staff account. Response: The system shall apply changes based on granular privileges for modification.

systemFeatures.name: Patron Account Creation Interface
systemFeatures.description: The **library** system will provide a dedicated interface for creating new patron accounts, simplifying the registration process for library users. This interface will offer configurable templates for staff use, ensuring consistency in data entry. It will support field validation and required fields to maintain data quality, and provide configurable default values to expedite the process. This streamlined approach to patron account creation enhances efficiency for staff and improves data accuracy. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Staff creates a new patron account. Response: The system shall display a dedicated interface with configurable templates and apply default values.
Stimulus: Staff enters invalid data into a required field during patron account creation. Response: The system shall perform field validation and prompt for correction.
functionalRequirements: The system shall provide a dedicated interface for creating new patron accounts.
The patron account creation process shall provide configurable templates for staff use.
The system shall support field validation for patron account creation.
The system shall support required fields for patron account creation.

systemFeatures.name: Scheduled Job Management
systemFeatures.description: The **library** system will support the scheduling of various maintenance tasks, reports, and data exports. This scheduling capability allows administrators to define job sequences, such as 'start job B when job A finishes,' ensuring dependencies are met. Furthermore, scheduled jobs can be modified or cancelled at any time prior to their starting, providing flexibility and control over automated processes. This feature optimizes resource utilization and automates routine operations, enhancing system efficiency. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Administrator schedules a data export job to run after a database backup. Response: The system shall sequence the jobs and execute the export only after the backup completes.
Stimulus: Administrator cancels a pending scheduled task. Response: The system shall prevent the scheduled task from starting.
functionalRequirements: The system shall support scheduling of maintenance tasks, reports, and data exports.
Jobs can be scheduled in sequence ('start job B when job A finishes').
Jobs can be modified or cancelled at any time prior to starting.

systemFeatures.name: Job Scheduling Delegation and Oversight
systemFeatures.description: The **library** system will allow staff to be granted permissions to schedule tasks, reports, and data exports, promoting distributed responsibility for routine operations. System administration staff will have the ability to view and manage all jobs scheduled by other staff members. This oversight ensures proper coordination, prevents conflicts, and allows for centralized monitoring and intervention by administrators, maintaining system integrity and efficiency across all scheduled activities. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: A staff member schedules a report. Response: The system shall allow the scheduling if the staff has appropriate permissions.
Stimulus: System administrator views all scheduled jobs. Response: The system shall display a comprehensive list of all jobs scheduled by any staff member.
functionalRequirements: Staff can be given permission to schedule tasks, reports, and data exports.
System administration staff can view and manage jobs scheduled by other staff.

systemFeatures.name: Keyboard Macros and Shortcuts
systemFeatures.description: The **library** system will support both administrator-programmable and user-programmable macros and/or keyboard shortcuts. These shortcuts can be assigned to macros (e.g., 'Insert Field') or to specific text strings, enhancing efficiency for repetitive tasks. Macros will be centrally managed on the server, allowing for import and export to individual users. Furthermore, their use and editing can be restricted through centrally-managed permissions, ensuring control and consistency across the system while offering personalized user experience. Priority: Medium.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Administrator creates a new macro for 'Insert Field'. Response: The system shall store the macro centrally and make it available for assignment to shortcut keys.
Stimulus: User attempts to edit a restricted macro. Response: The system shall prevent modification based on centrally-managed permissions.
functionalRequirements: The system shall support administrator-programmable macros and/or keyboard shortcuts.

Macros can be imported from and exported to individual users.

systemFeatures.name: Workstation-Independent Accounts
systemFeatures.description: The **library** system will ensure that staff and group accounts are independent of specific workstations, meaning client installations should not be tied to a particular physical location. This flexibility allows staff to log in from any authorized client machine within the network, enhancing mobility and operational continuity. It simplifies account management by decoupling user identity from hardware, providing a more versatile and scalable environment for library operations. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Staff member logs in from a different workstation. Response: The system shall authenticate the staff member and provide access to their account and privileges, regardless of the workstation's physical location.
Stimulus: Administrator assigns a staff account to a new group. Response: The system shall update the group membership and associated privileges, which will apply uniformly across all workstations.
functionalRequirements: Staff and group accounts shall be independent from workstations.
Client install should not be tied to a specific location.

systemFeatures.name: Centralized Client Software Updates
systemFeatures.description: The **library** system will enable centralized management of client software installation and updates. This will be achieved using standard or proprietary network management tools, allowing for streaming updates directly from the server to individual client machines. Administrators will have the ability to specify particular clients for updates, ensuring targeted deployment. Additionally, client software can be managed remotely using tools like **VNC** and **Remote Desktop**, streamlining maintenance and support processes across the entire client base. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Administrator initiates a client software update for specific workstations. Response: The system shall stream updates from the server to the designated clients.
Stimulus: Administrator uses VNC to troubleshoot a client workstation. Response: The system shall allow remote management of the client software via VNC.
functionalRequirements: Client software installation and updates must be centrally managed.
Centralized management shall use standard or proprietary network management tools.
Centralized management shall allow streaming updates from the server.
The system shall provide the ability to specify specific clients to be updated.
Client software can be managed with VNC and Remote Desktop.

systemFeatures.name: Server-based Client Configurations
systemFeatures.description: The **library** system will centralize all client configuration files on the server. This approach ensures consistency across all client installations and simplifies management. Administrators will have the capability to export and import configurations between clients, facilitating rapid deployment and standardization of settings. Centralized client configurations enhance maintainability, reduce setup time for new workstations, and ensure that all clients operate with consistent and approved settings. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: New client workstation is deployed. Response: The system shall retrieve its configuration files from the central server.
Stimulus: Administrator exports a client configuration. Response: The system shall generate a configuration file that can be imported to another client.

Configurations can be exported and imported between clients.

systemFeatures.name: Customizable Report Format and Output
systemFeatures.description: The **library** system will provide extensive capabilities to fully customize the layout and appearance of reports. This flexibility allows administrators and staff to design reports that meet specific informational and aesthetic requirements. Reports can be displayed, printed, emailed, or saved to standard formats such as **CSV** and **Excel**, in addition to customizable formats. This ensures broad utility and compatibility for sharing report data, catering to diverse needs for data presentation and integration. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Staff customizes a report layout. Response: The system shall apply the custom layout to the report output.
Stimulus: Administrator saves a report to CSV format. Response: The system shall generate the report in CSV format.
functionalRequirements: The system shall provide the ability to fully customize layout and appearance of reports.
The system shall provide the ability to display reports.
The system shall provide the ability to print reports.
The system shall provide the ability to email reports.
The system shall provide the ability to save reports to standard formats including CSV and Excel.

systemFeatures.name: User-Friendly Query Tool
systemFeatures.description: The **library** system will include a user-friendly interface for designing queries against all record types. This powerful query tool will allow staff to select specific fields to query, choose values from picklists, utilize regular expressions from drop-down menus, and employ a full range of **Boolean** operators for complex searches. Administrators will maintain control over staff access to tables and fields, ensuring that queries are performed within authorized boundaries and that sensitive data is protected. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Staff member designs a complex query. Response: The system shall provide a user-friendly interface with options for field selection, value picklists, regular expressions, and Boolean operators.
Stimulus: Staff executes a query on a restricted table. Response: The system shall prevent the query based on administrative access controls.

systemFeatures.name: Report Template Creation and Usage
systemFeatures.description: The **library** system will allow system administrators to create report templates, making standardized reports available to front-line staff. These templates can be run as-is for routine reporting or modified to meet a staff person's particular needs, providing both consistency and flexibility. This feature streamlines the reporting process, ensures adherence to common reporting standards, and empowers staff to generate relevant data insights without requiring extensive query building skills. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: System administrator creates a new report template. Response: The system shall store the template and make it available for front-line staff.

Report templates shall be available to front-line staff.
