systemFeatures.name: Fine-grained Report Permissions
systemFeatures.description: The **library** system will provide fine-grained permissions to allow or disallow staff from running specific reports. This includes controlling access to predefined reports and authorizing the execution of ad hoc reports on specific sets of data. This security measure ensures that sensitive or critical information is only accessible to authorized personnel, maintaining data confidentiality and integrity while still allowing flexibility for legitimate data analysis. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Staff member attempts to run a restricted report. Response: The system shall deny access based on fine-grained permissions.
Stimulus: Administrator grants permission for a staff member to run ad hoc reports on patron data. Response: The system shall update the staff member's permissions accordingly.
functionalRequirements: The system shall provide fine-grained permissions to allow or disallow staff to run specific reports.
The system shall provide fine-grained permissions to allow or disallow staff to run ad hoc reports on specific sets of data.

systemFeatures.name: Record Number Reporting
systemFeatures.description: The **library** system will generate reports detailing record numbers for various record types, including patron, bibliographic, item, order, and invoice. Each report will show the current record number, the total number of records, the number deleted, and the number purged. Access to these record number reports will be controlled at the user/group level, ensuring that sensitive metadata about system scale and data lifecycle is only viewable by authorized personnel for auditing and system management purposes. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Authorized administrator requests a record number report for item records. Response: The system shall generate a report showing current, total, deleted, and purged item record counts.
Stimulus: Unauthorized user attempts to access a record number report. Response: The system shall deny access based on user/group level control.
functionalRequirements: The system shall report for each record type: current record number, current number of records, number deleted, and number purged.
Record types shall include patron, bibliographic, item, order, invoice, etc.
Access to record numbers shall be controlled at the user/group level.

systemFeatures.name: Versatile Printer Support
systemFeatures.description: The **library** system will provide comprehensive support for defining and selecting four distinct types of printers: receipt printer, standard printer, label printer, and forms printer. The system will support all **Windows** printers, ensuring broad compatibility and ease of integration with existing hardware. This versatile printer support caters to the diverse printing needs of a **library** system, from small transaction receipts to large-scale reports and specialized labels. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Staff needs to print a receipt for a patron. Response: The system shall allow selection of a configured receipt printer for printing.
Stimulus: Administrator configures a new label printer. Response: The system shall detect and support the Windows printer, allowing it to be defined as a label printer.
functionalRequirements: The system shall provide the ability to define and select four types of printers: receipt printer, standard printer, label printer, and forms printer.
All Windows printers shall be supported.

systemFeatures.name: Multiple Print Output Options
systemFeatures.description: The **library** system will offer multiple print output options from any part of the application, enhancing flexibility for information dissemination. Users will be able to print to a file on the server, **FTP**, email, or directly to a physical printer. When applicable, the system will also provide the ability to select specific record fields and control their order when printing, allowing for tailored outputs that meet specific administrative or reporting requirements. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Staff generates a report and selects 'Print to File on Server'. Response: The system shall save the report as a file on the server.
Stimulus: Staff prints patron information and customizes field order. Response: The system shall print the selected record fields in the specified order.
functionalRequirements: The system shall provide the ability to print to a file on the server from any part of the application.
The system shall provide the ability to print via FTP from any part of the application.
The system shall provide the ability to print via email from any part of the application.
The system shall provide the ability to print to a printer from any part of the application.
When applicable, the system shall provide the ability to select record fields when printing.
When applicable, the system shall provide the ability to control order of fields when printing.

performanceRequirements: The **library** system shall support concurrent operations across 50 locations, processing up to 20 million circulations and over 500,000 item acquisitions per year without performance degradation. All searches and reports shall be processed during open hours without disrupting other critical system functions, ensuring real-time operational efficiency. Key system performance metrics, such as response times for record lookups and transaction processing, shall remain within 2 seconds for 95% of requests during peak usage periods, and batch operations must complete within their scheduled maintenance windows.
safetyRequirements: The **library** system shall ensure the integrity of all critical data, especially patron and collection records, during all operations, including updates, backups, and data transfers. In the event of a primary server failure, the system shall automatically failover to a clustered standby server, preserving all in-flight transactions and preventing data loss with an RTO of less than 15 minutes. Critical system operations, such as record deletion or bulk updates, shall include mandatory confirmation prompts, granular authorization, and robust rollback capabilities to prevent accidental and irreversible data corruption.
securityRequirements: The **library** system shall enforce strong authentication for all staff access, supporting individual login accounts with role-based access control to modules and data, implementing multi-factor authentication for administrative roles. Patron data shall be secured in transit and at rest using industry-standard encryption protocols, including **SSL**, **SFTP**, and **SSH**, to protect privacy and confidentiality, complying with all relevant data protection regulations. Administrative access to sensitive functions, such as root shell access and user privilege management, shall be strictly logged and audited, with alerts for suspicious activities, to prevent unauthorized access and modifications and ensure accountability.
softwareQualityAttributes: Usability: The **library** system's user interfaces, including administrative consoles and dashboards, shall be intuitive and accessible, catering to both technical and non-technical staff. It shall fully support screen-reading and screen-magnification software, adhering to accessibility standards (e.g., WCAG 2.1 AA) to ensure inclusivity for all users. The documentation shall be web-based, searchable, and organized by function to facilitate quick and easy information retrieval for all user classes, minimizing training overhead.

Maintainability: The **library** system shall be highly maintainable, providing comprehensive, version-specific technical documentation, including details on configuration files, log file structures, and clear upgrade guidelines. The system architecture shall support modular updates and patches, minimizing disruption during maintenance cycles and simplifying the deployment of new features. All custom developments shall follow established coding standards and design conventions (e.g., clear API specifications), facilitating future enhancements and efficient troubleshooting by internal and external development teams.

Reliability: The **library** system shall demonstrate high reliability through server clustering for failover, ensuring continuous availability with 99.9% uptime, even in the event of hardware failures. It shall implement live incremental and full backup capabilities, coupled with data rollback functionality, to protect against data loss and enable rapid recovery within a 4-hour RPO. Robust error logging and proactive monitoring with configurable alert thresholds shall identify and report system anomalies automatically, contributing significantly to overall system stability and consistent performance.

Portability: The **library** system shall be designed for portability, capable of operating on both **Linux** and **Solaris** server platforms, demonstrating flexibility in infrastructure deployment. Its client interfaces shall be accessible via standard **web browsers** (Microsoft Internet Explorer v6.0+ and Mozilla Firefox v2.0+) and a **Windows-compatible client**, ensuring broad compatibility across diverse user operating environments. The underlying **SQL-based database** and adherence to standard communication protocols (e.g., **SMTP**, **SFTP**) further enhance its ability to function across varied infrastructures with minimal re-engineering.

Scalability: The **library** system shall be scalable to support an expanding number of branches, patrons, and collection items, designed to handle significantly increased transaction volumes (e.g., a 50% increase in circulations over 3 years) without degradation in performance. Its architecture will allow for the horizontal scaling of components, such as application servers and database instances, to accommodate future growth and load spikes efficiently. The real-time processing capabilities will be maintained even as system load increases, ensuring consistent performance for critical operations.

Interoperability: The **library** system shall ensure high interoperability, integrating seamlessly with other **Integrated Library System (ILS)** modules, external vendor websites, and third-party applications. It will support standard data formats such as **USMARC21** and **EDIFACT** for cataloging and acquisitions, and communication protocols including **APIs**, **SIP2/NCIP2**, and **ODBC** for data exchange. This enables smooth data exchange and functional integration with diverse external systems and library-specific tools, facilitating a connected ecosystem.
businessRules: The **library** system shall strictly enforce all defined business rules governing circulation, record visibility, and data integrity across all operations. This includes preventing actions that violate item availability, patron eligibility criteria, or established collection policies, ensuring operational consistency. All configurable rules, such as suppression rules determining record visibility, requesting rules for holds, and loan rules for check-outs and renewals, shall be managed through dedicated administrative interfaces, allowing authorized staff to define and modify operational parameters. The system shall ensure that all data entries are validated against specified criteria, including default values, data types, automatic formatting, and required fields, to maintain consistent data quality and prevent errors at the source.

glossary.definition: Application Programming Interface, a set of defined methods for communication among software components.
Compact Disc, a common media type in library collections.
Comma-Separated Values, a common text file format for tabular data.
Central Processing Unit, the primary component of a computer that performs most of the processing inside a computer.
Electronic Data Interchange For Administration, Commerce and Transport, an international standard for electronic data interchange.
HyperText Markup Language, the standard markup language for documents designed to be displayed in a web browser.
Integrated Library System, an enterprise resource planning system for a library, managing operations like acquisitions, cataloging, circulation, and OPAC.
Internet Protocol address, a numerical label assigned to each device connected to a computer network that uses the Internet Protocol for communication.
The specific library system for which these requirements were developed.
The current project name, referring to the Integrated Library System's Administration Module.
A family of open-source Unix-like operating systems.
Machine-Readable Cataloging, a standard for the representation and communication of bibliographic and related information in machine-readable form.
A proprietary graphical web browser by Microsoft.
A free and open-source web browser developed by the Mozilla Foundation.
NISO Circulation Interchange Protocol, a standard for library systems to exchange messages about circulation, patrons, and items.
Open Database Connectivity, a standard application programming interface for accessing database management systems.
Online Computer Library Center, a global library cooperative providing shared technology services, research, and community programs.
Online Public Access Catalog, a computerized online catalog of materials held in a library.
A customer or user of the library.
SSH File Transfer Protocol, a secure file transfer protocol.
Standard Interchange Protocol, a protocol used to communicate between library systems and self-service devices.
Simple Mail Transfer Protocol, an internet standard for electronic mail transmission.
Simple Network Management Protocol, an Internet Standard protocol for collecting and organizing information about managed devices on IP networks.
A proprietary Unix operating system originally developed by Sun Microsystems.
Structured Query Language, a domain-specific language used to manage data in relational database management systems.
Software Requirements Specification, a document that describes the requirements for a software system.
Secure Shell, a cryptographic network protocol for operating network services securely over an unsecured network.
Secure Sockets Layer, a deprecated cryptographic protocol designed to provide communications security over a computer network.
Virtual Network Computing, a graphical desktop sharing system that uses the Remote Frame Buffer protocol to control another computer remotely.
Software application for accessing information on the World Wide Web.
A family of proprietary graphical operating systems developed by Microsoft.

flowchartDiagram.syntaxExplanation: Flowcharts visualize processes and decision points. 'TD' indicates Top-Down direction. Nodes represent steps or decisions. Square brackets define node shapes and labels; labels with spaces must be quoted. Alphanumeric IDs are used for nodes. Decisions are denoted by diamonds, while start/end points are often rounded rectangles or circles.
flowchartDiagram.code:
flowchart TD
    A1["Start User Account Creation"] --> B1{"Check Admin Privileges?"}
    B1 -- No --> C1["Display 'Access Denied'"]
    C1 --> E1("End Process")
    B1 -- Yes --> D1["Display Account Mgmt Console"]
    D1 --> F1["Select 'Create New User'"]
    F1 --> G1["Enter User Details"]
    G1 --> H1{"Input Valid?"}
    H1 -- No --> I1["Show Error Message"]
    I1 --> G1
    H1 -- Yes --> J1["Save User Data to Database"]
    J1 --> K1["Log Account Creation Event"]
    K1 --> E1
flowchartDiagram.caption: L_FLOW_User Account Creation Process

sequenceDiagram.syntaxExplanation: Sequence diagrams illustrate interactions between participants in a chronological order. Participants are defined at the top. Messages are sent between participants, showing the flow of control. `->>` denotes an asynchronous message, `->>+` indicates activation, and `-->>-` indicates deactivation and reply. `activate` and `deactivate` blocks denote the lifespan of a participant's execution context.
sequenceDiagram.code:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Admin as "Admin User"
    participant Monitor as "Monitor Service"
    participant AlertMgr as "Alert Manager"
    participant EmailGW as "Email Gateway"
    participant Dash as "Admin Dashboard"

    Monitor->>AlertMgr: "Resource Exceeded Alert"
    activate AlertMgr
    AlertMgr->>Dash: "Display Dashboard Alert"
    AlertMgr->>EmailGW: "Send Email Notification"
    activate EmailGW
    EmailGW-->>AlertMgr: "Email Sent Confirmation"
    deactivate EmailGW
    AlertMgr-->>Monitor: "Alert Acknowledged"
    deactivate AlertMgr
    Admin->>Dash: "View Alert Details"
    Dash-->>Admin: "Display Details"
sequenceDiagram.caption: L_SEQ_System Monitoring Alert Process

entityRelationshipDiagram.syntaxExplanation: Entity-Relationship Diagrams (ERDs) model the logical structure of a database, showing entities (tables) and their relationships. Entities are defined with attributes, specifying their type and keys (PK for Primary Key, FK for Foreign Key, UK for Unique Key). Relationships include cardinality (e.g., ||--o{ for one-to-many, }|--|| for many-to-one) and are labeled with quoted descriptions.
entityRelationshipDiagram.code:
erDiagram
    USER ||--o{ "STAFF ACCOUNT" : "manages"
    USER ||--o{ "PATRON ACCOUNT" : "owns"
    "STAFF ACCOUNT" }|--o{ "ITEM RECORD" : "administers"
    "PATRON ACCOUNT" }|--o{ LOAN : "takes"
    "ITEM RECORD" }|--o{ LOAN : "is_part_of"
    "BIBLIOGRAPHIC RECORD" ||--o{ "ITEM RECORD" : "describes"
    "LIBRARY BRANCH" ||--o{ "ITEM RECORD" : "holds"
    "STAFF ACCOUNT" }o--|| "LIBRARY BRANCH" : "works_at"

    USER {
        int id PK
        string username
        string email
    }
    "STAFF ACCOUNT" {
        int id PK
        int user_id FK
        string role
        datetime created_at
    }
    "PATRON ACCOUNT" {
        int id PK
        int user_id FK
        string library_card_number
        datetime registration_date
    }
    "LIBRARY BRANCH" {
        int id PK
        string name
        string address
    }
    "ITEM RECORD" {
        int id PK
        string barcode
        string status
        int bibliographic_record_id FK
        int current_branch_id FK
    }
    "BIBLIOGRAPHIC RECORD" {
        int id PK
        string title
        string author
        string isbn
    }
    LOAN {
        int id PK
        int patron_account_id FK
        int item_record_id FK
        datetime due_date
        datetime checkout_date
    }
entityRelationshipDiagram.caption: L_ERD_Core library Entities

introduction.purpose: The primary purpose of the **grid bgc** application is to establish a robust grid-based software infrastructure dedicated to supporting advanced bio-geo-chemical modeling. This system will leverage the Daymet surface weather interpolation engine to generate gridded surface weather datasets from diverse observation data records. Concurrently, it will integrate the Biome-BGC model to execute complex BGC modeling activities efficiently and accurately. The overarching goal is to provide a comprehensive platform for scientific users.
introduction.documentConventions: This Software Requirements Specification (SRS) adheres to the guidelines established by IEEE Standard 830-1998, 'Recommended Practice for Software Requirements Specifications'. All requirements are articulated in a clear, unambiguous, and testable manner to ensure precise communication. Key terms and acronyms are defined in the Glossary (Appendix A) to maintain consistency and clarity throughout the document. Specific notation for analysis models is detailed within Appendix B.
introduction.intendedAudience: This document is intended for a diverse audience, including system designers, developers, testers, project managers, and quality assurance personnel involved in the **grid bgc** project. It also serves as a critical reference for scientific users, portal administrators, and data users who require a detailed understanding of the system's capabilities and operational parameters. Stakeholders and potential integrators may also consult this SRS to comprehend the system's scope and interfaces.
introduction.productScope: The **grid bgc** application is a new, self-contained web-based software infrastructure designed to facilitate bio-geo-chemical modeling through grid technologies. Its scope encompasses user account management, comprehensive data organization, diverse object and project management functionalities, and sophisticated model execution capabilities using Daymet and Biome-BGC engines. The system will provide a graphical user interface for all functions, focusing on secure and reliable communication with remote computing resources. Excluded from this scope are the detailed internal implementations of the Daymet and Biome-BGC models themselves, beyond their integration points.
references: IEEE 830-1998 IEEE Recommended Practice for Software Requirements Specifications.
ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018 Systems and software engineering — Life cycle processes — Requirements engineering.

overallDescription.productPerspective: The **grid bgc** system is designed as a novel, self-contained application, acting as a crucial component within a broader scientific data ecosystem. It is envisioned to establish a robust grid-based software infrastructure, serving as a foundational platform for advanced bio-geo-chemical modeling. The application will not replace any existing system but rather augment current capabilities by providing a dedicated environment for complex simulations and data management. It stands as a new product leveraging established scientific models and infrastructure.
productFunctions: The **grid bgc** system shall manage comprehensive user accounts, including creation, role assignment, and administrative oversight.
The system shall provide robust data organization capabilities, managing various object types and projects for scientific modeling.
The system shall facilitate the execution of Daymet and Biome-BGC modeling runs, processing gridded surface weather and bio-geo-chemical data.
The system shall support the visualization and analysis of output datasets, offering tools for post-processing and model performance evaluation.
The system shall enable secure data download and publication functionalities for scientific community dissemination.
The system shall support template management, allowing users to leverage and contribute expert knowledge.
The system shall offer portal administration functionalities for monitoring, system consistency checks, and user support.

userClassesAndCharacteristics.characteristics: Scientific users are the primary and favored user class for the **grid bgc** system, characterized by a strong background in bio-geo-chemical modeling and data analysis. They possess technical expertise to manage input data, configure and run complex simulations, effectively visualize results, and manage the resulting output data. Their needs drive core system functionalities, prioritizing robust modeling capabilities and data accessibility.

Portal administrators are responsible for the day-to-day operational management of the **grid bgc** system. This user class requires administrative privileges to manage user accounts, oversee and troubleshoot user-initiated model runs, and configure general portal settings. They possess a deep understanding of the system's architecture and operational requirements, ensuring its stability and efficient performance for all users. System security and consistency are key concerns for this role.

Data users represent a secondary user class within the **grid bgc** ecosystem, possessing scientific research backgrounds but typically lacking the ability or need to initiate complex simulations themselves. Their primary interaction with the system involves accessing and utilizing simulation output data for further analysis or research. This class has the lowest priority, focusing mainly on data retrieval functionalities rather than active model manipulation or system configuration.

overallDescription.operatingEnvironment: The **grid bgc** web portal components are designed for seamless integration into the existing **Dataportal Web Server** infrastructure at NCAR. This ensures compatibility with established network configurations, security protocols, and existing user access mechanisms. The portal is expected to operate within the **NCAR** computing ecosystem, leveraging its robust and scalable web server capabilities. This environment facilitates centralized management and interoperability with other **NCAR** scientific applications and services.
designAndImplementationConstraints: The **grid bgc** system shall rigorously adhere to the **Globus toolkit** for all grid-based operations, including secure and reliable communication to remote computing resources. This mandate dictates the choice of middleware and ensures interoperability within existing grid environments. Leveraging **Globus** streamlines authentication, authorization, and data transfer mechanisms.

All aspects of the **grid bgc** system design, implementation, and operation shall strictly comply with **NCAR** Security policies and constraints. This includes data handling, access control, user authentication, and system monitoring. Adherence to these policies is non-negotiable to maintain institutional security standards and data integrity.

The system will solely utilize the **NCAR Mass Storage System** for all persistent file-based data storage, dictating the storage interface and protocols. This constraint ensures data longevity, accessibility, and integration with established **NCAR** archival practices.

The system shall be developed to operate on the **Hemisphere Linux cluster** at CU, requiring compatibility with its specific operating system version and available software libraries. This environment imposes constraints on deployment, resource allocation, and job scheduling mechanisms for computational tasks.
userDocumentation: The **grid bgc** system shall be delivered with a comprehensive Online Help Manual, providing users with in-depth guidance on all system functionalities. This manual will feature a logical Table of Contents (TOC) to facilitate easy navigation through major sections. It will serve as the primary reference for understanding complex modeling workflows and administrative tasks, ensuring users can independently resolve queries.

The **grid bgc** system shall incorporate a Context-Sensitive Online Help Manual, accessible directly from each web page via a dedicated link. This feature will display relevant help topics in a non-disruptive pop-up window, tailored to the specific context of the page currently in view. The context-sensitive view will omit the full Table of Contents to maintain focus on the immediate topic, enhancing usability and minimizing cognitive load.
assumptionsAndDependencies: The **grid bgc** system assumes continuous availability and stable performance of the **NCAR Mass Storage System** for all file-based storage operations. Any disruptions or performance degradation in the **MSS** would directly impact the system's ability to store and retrieve data, affecting modeling runs and data accessibility. Dependability on this external system is critical for operational continuity.

The system assumes the **NCAR Gatekeeper account system** will remain the authoritative source for user authentication and core user information. Changes to the **Gatekeeper** system's API, policies, or availability could necessitate significant modifications to the **grid bgc** user management module. The integrity and responsiveness of **Gatekeeper** are direct dependencies for user access.

A stable and performant network infrastructure, specifically for communication between the **Dataportal Web Server**, **Hemisphere Linux cluster**, and **NCAR Mass Storage System**, is assumed. Any latency or bandwidth limitations could severely impact the execution speed of modeling runs and overall user experience. High-speed, reliable connectivity is a foundational dependency.

The **grid bgc** system relies on the continued development and maintenance of the **Daymet** surface weather interpolation engine and the **Biome-BGC** model. Updates or changes in these core scientific models, or their APIs, will require corresponding adaptations within the **grid bgc** application to maintain functionality and accuracy. The scientific integrity of the system is directly dependent on these external modeling components.

externalInterfaceRequirements.userInterfaces: The **grid bgc** system shall provide a unified, web-based graphical user interface (GUI) accessible through standard web browsers. This interface will adhere to established web accessibility guidelines and **NCAR** user experience standards. Key GUI components will include consistent navigation elements, clear display of scientific data visualizations, and intuitive forms for parameterizing model runs. Error messages shall be displayed prominently and provide actionable feedback to users, facilitating self-correction. The system specifically requires users to enable browser cookies to maintain session state and authentication. All interactions requiring sensitive data entry, such as login credentials, shall be secured using industry-standard encryption protocols.
externalInterfaceRequirements.hardwareInterfaces: The **grid bgc** system shall interface primarily with the **NCAR Mass Storage System (MSS)** for all long-term data archival and retrieval operations. This interface will involve direct file transfer protocols compatible with **MSS**, ensuring efficient storage of large gridded datasets, model inputs, and simulation outputs. The system will manage the physical characteristics of these transfers, including data integrity checks and appropriate metadata handling, to ensure seamless interaction with the **MSS**'s underlying hardware infrastructure. No direct control of other hardware components is within the system's scope, aside from its interaction with compute nodes.
externalInterfaceRequirements.softwareInterfaces: The **grid bgc** system shall integrate with the **Dataportal Web Server** at NCAR, serving as its primary deployment environment and leveraging its existing authentication and authorization mechanisms. It will interface with the **Globus toolkit** for secure and reliable communication with remote **Hemisphere Linux cluster** compute nodes, enabling the submission and monitoring of computationally intensive modeling jobs. User authentication will primarily leverage the **NCAR Gatekeeper account system**, acting as the authoritative identity provider. Furthermore, the system will interact with the **Daymet** surface weather interpolation engine and the **Biome-BGC** model for core bio-geo-chemical processing, providing input data and retrieving processed outputs via well-defined APIs. A **PostgreSQL** database will serve as the backend for managing all persistent system metadata, user accounts, object definitions, and project configurations.
externalInterfaceRequirements.communicationsInterfaces: The **grid bgc** system shall utilize **HTTP/HTTPS** for all web-based client-server interactions, ensuring secure data transfer and communication over the internet. For communication with remote **Hemisphere Linux cluster** compute nodes and the **NCAR Mass Storage System**, the system will employ the **Globus toolkit**, specifically leveraging **GridFTP** for high-performance, secure, and reliable file transfers. All sensitive data, including user credentials and scientific data payloads, shall be encrypted using **TLS/SSL** protocols to maintain confidentiality and integrity. The system will implement robust error handling for network interruptions and manage session synchronization to prevent data inconsistencies during long-running operations. Messaging between internal components will also adhere to secure, authenticated protocols.

systemFeatures.name: User Account Management
systemFeatures.description: This feature provides comprehensive management of user accounts within the **grid bgc** system, enabling scientists and data users to access the platform and administrators to oversee user activity. It ensures secure authentication through integration with the **NCAR Gatekeeper account system** and allows for distinct user roles, enhancing the security and functionality partitioning of the system. The technical impact involves integrating with external authentication services and maintaining internal user states and permissions within the **grid bgc** database. This improves user access control and administrative oversight. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User attempts to apply for a **grid bgc** account. Response: System presents an account registration form, requiring **NCAR Gatekeeper** username and other details.
Stimulus: User attempts to log in with invalid credentials. Response: System displays an invalid credentials error and increments failed login attempts.
Stimulus: User's account reaches three failed login attempts. Response: System automatically locks the account, notifies portal administrators, and prevents further login attempts.
Stimulus: Portal administrator receives an email notification for a new account pending review. Response: System displays a list of pending accounts to the administrator for approval or rejection.
Stimulus: Portal administrator modifies a user's role. Response: System updates the user's permissions, which take effect upon their next session, and logs the change.
functionalRequirements: The system shall require all **grid bgc** users to possess valid **NCAR Gatekeeper** accounts for system access.
The system shall require portal administrators to approve or reject new user account requests.
The system shall retrieve user information, excluding passwords, from the **NCAR Gatekeeper account system**.
The system shall support the following account status states: Pending Confirmation, Pending Approval, Active, Locked, and Deleted.
The system shall support two primary user roles: 'User' (general end user) and 'Administrator' (system administration privileges).
The system shall provide a web portal interface for users to apply for new accounts.
The system shall validate that new account applications include a valid **NCAR Gatekeeper** username and all other required fields.
The system shall allow authenticated users to view their account details, excluding passwords.
The system shall provide a mechanism for users to request account deletion, notifying portal administrators upon submission.
The system shall enforce secure data channels (**HTTPS**) for all login attempts.
The system shall lock a user account after three consecutive failed login attempts.
The system shall authenticate regular users against the **NCAR Gatekeeper system** and administrative users against the internal **grid bgc** portal database.
The system shall send email notifications to portal administrators for new accounts awaiting review.
The system shall display a list of accounts pending review to portal administrators.
The system shall allow portal administrators to approve or reject specific user accounts, optionally including a message.
The system shall allow portal administrators to assign template submission privileges to specific users.
The system shall provide portal administrators with a comprehensive list of all user accounts, including filtered views for pending approval, deletion requests, and locked accounts.

The system shall allow portal administrators to permanently delete specific user accounts and associated data based on predefined rules (templates not deleted, shared objects deleted if unreferenced, other objects/projects deleted completely).
The system shall allow portal administrators to lock and unlock user accounts, sending email notifications to affected users.
The system shall ensure that currently executing jobs for a locked user continue until termination or completion.
The system shall provide portal administrators with a display of all running jobs, sorted by user, including their status and title.
The system shall allow portal administrators to terminate any user's running jobs.
The system shall assign the 'Scientist' role by default to all new user accounts.
The system shall allow portal administrators to change a user's assigned role.

systemFeatures.name: Data Organization and Integrity
systemFeatures.description: This feature defines the fundamental structure for organizing all data within the **grid bgc** system, categorizing elements into 'Objects' and 'Projects' to facilitate logical grouping and scientific workflows. It ensures data integrity by preventing unauthorized modifications to critical data once used in a model run, thereby preserving the scientific provenance of simulation outputs. This structural approach simplifies data management for scientists and enhances the reliability of research results by enforcing immutability after processing. Technically, it implements state management for data entities and enforces referential integrity rules to prevent corruption. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User attempts to modify an object that has been used in a completed model run. Response: System prevents the modification and displays a message indicating the object is locked.
Stimulus: A dependent object or project is modified, affecting a linked object. Response: System updates the linked object's status to 'Invalidated', indicating potential outdated inputs.
Stimulus: User views the status of a project. Response: System displays the current state of the project (Unlocked, Locked, or Invalidated).
functionalRequirements: The system shall categorize all logical grouping elements into 'Objects' and 'Projects'.
The system shall define 'Objects' as elements grouping related data for sharing and reuse.
The system shall define 'Projects' as elements grouping specific objects to perform a unit of work or simulation.
The system shall prevent users from changing object or project values once they have been used in a model run.
The system shall support the following states for objects and projects: Unlocked (editable), Locked (immutable after use in run), and Invalidated (dependencies modified).
The system shall implement specific logic rules for state transitions based on object and project types.

systemFeatures.name: Object Management
systemFeatures.description: This feature provides comprehensive capabilities for managing various scientific data entities, referred to as 'Objects', within the **grid bgc** system. It supports different object types, facilitates sharing among users, and enables the creation of new datasets through merging existing objects. This functionality empowers scientists to organize, reuse, and collaborate on diverse datasets, enhancing the efficiency and reproducibility of their research. The technical underpinnings involve robust data referencing, version control (through immutability after use), and efficient data replication mechanisms. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User selects an object to share. Response: System presents options to share with all users or specific users within the system.
Stimulus: User attempts to merge two objects of different types (e.g., a List object with a Grid object). Response: System displays an error message preventing the merge due to incompatible types.
Stimulus: A shared object is referenced by another user's project. Response: System locks the shared object, preventing direct modification by its owner to maintain referential integrity.
Stimulus: User initiates a merge operation. Response: System creates a full private copy of the source datasets and generates a new merged object, displaying metadata for review.
functionalRequirements: The system shall enable objects to manage datasets and references to other logically related datasets.
The system shall support three primary types of objects: List Objects (arbitrary data points), Grid Objects (rectangular grid datasets), and Parameterization Objects (model-specific parameter data).
The system shall allow users to share specific objects with other users.
The system shall provide options to share objects with 'All users' or 'Select specific users within the system'.
The system shall ensure that underlying datasets are not copied when a shared object is referenced.
The system shall transition a shared object to a 'Locked' state once it has been referenced elsewhere in the system.
The system shall allow users to create new datasets by merging existing objects.
The system shall restrict merging operations to objects of the same type (List or Grid).
The system shall ensure that merging always creates a new dataset from two existing source datasets.
The system shall allow merge sources to include the current user's existing objects or other users' shared objects.
The system shall perform no data validation (e.g., duplicate data, spatial relationships, missing values) during the initial merge operation.
The system shall create full copies of the source datasets during the creation of a merged dataset.
The system shall display dataset metadata to users prior to merge operations, enabling data screening.

systemFeatures.name: Project Management
systemFeatures.description: This feature provides a framework for managing 'Projects' within the **grid bgc** system, serving as both containers for supporting datasets and controls for simulation runs. It enables scientists to organize their research efforts by grouping related objects and defining the parameters for model execution. This functionality enhances the structured approach to scientific experimentation and data analysis, ensuring that simulation runs are traceable and reproducible. The technical components involve establishing strong associations between projects and objects, enforcing type compatibility, and providing tools for project creation and lifecycle management. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User attempts to create a new project. Response: System prompts the user to specify the project type (List or Grid).

Stimulus: User attempts to add an object of a different type to a project (e.g., a Grid object to a List project). Response: System prevents the addition and displays an error indicating a type mismatch.
Stimulus: User views a list of their projects. Response: System displays available projects, indicating their type and status.
functionalRequirements: The system shall define 'Projects' as having two main functions: containing references to supporting datasets and serving as the simulation run control element.
The system shall require users to specify the project type (List or Grid) during initial setup.
The system shall only allow users to reference datasets within a project that are of the same type as specified for the project.
The system shall enforce that projects cannot be shared or designated as templates.
The system shall allow users to create new projects, differentiating between List data projects and Grid projects.
The system shall ensure that referenced objects selected by the user are of the appropriate type, with strict enforcement for List and Grid type matching.

systemFeatures.name: Template Object Management
systemFeatures.description: This feature enables the creation and management of 'Template Objects' within the **grid bgc** system, providing a mechanism for sharing expert knowledge and predefined configurations among users. It allows privileged users to submit templates and provides all users with the ability to utilize them to create standardized projects, thereby promoting best practices and reducing setup time for complex simulations. The technical aspects include access control for template submission, ensuring template immutability post-submission, and managing full private copies upon template use. This significantly enhances collaborative research and streamlines scientific workflows. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User with template submission privileges attempts to submit a new template. Response: System processes the submission, making the template available for use and marking it as uneditable.
Stimulus: User without template submission privileges attempts to submit a template. Response: System denies the action and displays an authorization error.

Stimulus: Any user attempts to use an existing template. Response: System creates a full private copy of the template's data, prompting the user for a new project name and, if applicable, a new dataset for non-shared resources.
Stimulus: Portal administrator requests a list of all system templates. Response: System displays a categorized list of all templates by project type.
functionalRequirements: The system shall provide system templates as a means of sharing expert knowledge within the portal.
The system shall specify which object types support templates (detailed in specific object sections).
The system shall allow any user of the system to utilize a template.
The system shall restrict template submission privileges only to designated users.
The system shall prevent editing of a template once it has been submitted.
The system shall allow portal administrators and the original creator to delete templates from the system.
The system shall provide portal administrators with a list of all system templates, categorized by project type.
The system shall provide users with a list of their personally created templates in the system.
The system shall create a full private copy of all data when a template is used.
The system shall require the user to supply a new project name when utilizing a template before saving the project.
The system shall require the user to choose a new dataset if the template references a non-shared data resource before saving the project.

systemFeatures.name: Daymet Specific Object Management
systemFeatures.description: This feature provides specialized management for objects specifically utilized within the Daymet modeling framework, including Projection, Grid Registration, Surface Observation, Site Data, Daymet Parameterization, DEM, Analysis Mask, and Daymet Output objects. It ensures that Daymet models have appropriately structured input data and that their outputs are managed effectively. This enhances the precision and applicability of Daymet simulations for gridded surface weather interpolation. The technical aspects involve defining specific projection types, handling NetCDF file conventions for uploads, and managing object dependencies critical for Daymet's operation. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User attempts to define a new Projection Object. Response: System offers a choice of supported projection types (e.g., Geographic, UTM) and allows parameter input.
Stimulus: User uploads a surface observation data file. Response: System validates the file against required NetCDF conventions and archive structure, then creates a new Surface Observation Object.
Stimulus: User initiates a Daymet model run. Response: System creates a Daymet Output Object containing references to the generated datasets and makes it available for download.
Stimulus: User attempts to modify a Projection Object that is currently locked. Response: System denies the modification and displays an error message indicating the locked status.
functionalRequirements: The system shall enable 'Projection Objects' to define projection parameters for a dataset and be usable as templates.
The system shall support Geographic, UTM, Albers Conical Equal Area, Lambert Conformal Conic, Lambert Azimuthal Equal Area, Interrupted Goode Homolosine, and Interrupted Mollweide projection types.
The system shall allow creation of Projection Objects from new blank forms or by copying system templates.
The system shall allow viewing a list of Projection Objects and their detailed parameters.
The system shall permit updates to Projection Object parameters only when the object is in an unlocked state.
The system shall prevent changes to a Projection Object's type after creation.
The system shall allow deletion of unlocked Projection Objects and all related data.
The system shall, when deleting a locked Projection Object, prompt the user to delete all referencing projects.
The system shall not allow users to download Projection Object information.
The system shall enable 'Grid Registration Objects' to define grid registration parameters and be usable as templates.
The system shall allow creation of Grid Registration Objects from new blank forms or by copying system templates.
The system shall allow viewing a list of Grid Registration Objects and their detailed parameters.
The system shall permit updates to Grid Registration Object parameters only when the object is in an unlocked state.
The system shall allow deletion of unlocked Grid Registration Objects and all related data.
The system shall, when deleting a locked Grid Registration Object, prompt the user to delete all referencing objects.
The system shall not allow users to download Grid Registration Object information.
The system shall define 'Surface Observation Objects' as containing observed surface weather data, serving as a primary Daymet input, and being shareable.
The system shall support 'List' as the object type for Surface Observation Objects.
The system shall allow creation of Surface Observation Objects from new blank forms by uploading data files adhering to NetCDF file conventions and a specified archive structure.
The system shall allow creation of Surface Observation Objects by merging two discrete existing objects into one.
The system shall allow viewing a list of Surface Observation Objects, their details, and support search functionality.
The system shall permit updates to Surface Observation Objects (e.g., uploading new files) only when the object is in an unlocked state.
The system shall allow deletion of unlocked Surface Observation Objects and all related data.
The system shall, when deleting a locked Surface Observation Object, prompt the user to delete all referencing projects.
The system shall provide a visualization capability for Surface Observation Objects (Low Priority).
The system shall define 'Site Data Objects' for referenced datasets, allowing them to be shared.
The system shall allow creation of Site Data Objects from new blank forms by uploading data or by merging existing objects.
The system shall allow viewing a list of Site Data Objects and their details.
The system shall permit updates to Site Data Objects (e.g., uploading new files) only when the object is in an unlocked state.
The system shall allow deletion of unlocked Site Data Objects and all related data.
The system shall, when deleting a locked Site Data Object, prompt the user to delete all referencing projects.
The system shall provide a visualization capability for Site Data Objects (Low Priority).
The system shall enable 'Daymet Parameterization Objects' for defining Daymet model parameters.
The system shall allow creation, reading, updating, and deleting of Daymet Parameterization Objects.
The system shall enable 'DEM Objects' (Digital Elevation Model) for defining topographic data.
The system shall allow creation, reading, updating, and deleting of DEM Objects.
The system shall provide a visualization capability for DEM Objects (Low Priority).
The system shall enable 'Analysis Mask Objects' for defining spatial masks for analysis.
The system shall allow creation, reading, updating, and deleting of Analysis Mask Objects.
The system shall provide a visualization capability for Analysis Mask Objects (Low Priority).
The system shall define 'Daymet Projects' for organizing Daymet-specific referenced objects and templates.
The system shall allow creation, reading, updating, and deleting of Daymet Projects.
The system shall support initiating Daymet model runs from Daymet Projects.
The system shall define 'Daymet Output Objects' for storing output from Daymet model runs.
The system shall allow creation (after a model run), reading, and deletion of Daymet Output Objects.
The system shall allow users to download data contained within Daymet Output Objects.

systemFeatures.name: BiomeBGC Specific Object Management
systemFeatures.description: This feature provides specialized management for objects critical to the Biome-BGC modeling framework, encompassing Plant Functional Type, BiomeBGC Site Data, Output Specification, Nitrogen Deposition, Disturbance, and BiomeBGC Output objects. It ensures that input data for Biome-BGC simulations are correctly structured and that output data are effectively managed and shareable. This significantly contributes to the accuracy and flexibility of bio-geo-chemical modeling. Technical considerations include managing PFT IDs, supporting various object types for input, and ensuring data integrity for model runs. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User defines a new Plant Functional Type (PFT) object. Response: System processes the definition, assigning unique PFT IDs and making the object available for use in BiomeBGC projects.

Stimulus: User configures a BiomeBGC project, referencing various BiomeBGC-specific input objects. Response: System validates the compatibility of referenced objects and prepares the project for a model run.
Stimulus: A BiomeBGC model run completes. Response: System generates a BiomeBGC Output Object containing simulation results, making it shareable and downloadable.
Stimulus: User attempts to delete a Nitrogen Deposition Object that has dependent BiomeBGC projects. Response: System prevents deletion and notifies the user about invalidated dependent objects.
functionalRequirements: The system shall define 'Plant Functional Type Objects' (PFT) for classification, allowing sharing and template use.
The system shall allow creation, reading, updating, and deleting of Plant Functional Type Objects.
The system shall assign unique PFT IDs upon creation.
The system shall define 'BiomeBGC Site Data Objects' for specific site-related data in BiomeBGC models.
The system shall allow creation, reading, updating, and deleting of BiomeBGC Site Data Objects.
The system shall define 'Output Specification Objects' for configuring BiomeBGC model outputs, allowing collaboration.
The system shall provide a user interface for defining output specifications.
The system shall allow creation, reading, updating, and deleting of Output Specification Objects.
The system shall define 'Nitrogen Deposition Objects' for nitrogen input data, supporting various object types.
The system shall allow creation, reading, updating, and deleting of Nitrogen Deposition Objects.
The system shall manage the invalidation of objects dependent on a modified Nitrogen Deposition Object.
The system shall define 'Disturbance Objects' for representing environmental disturbances in BiomeBGC models.
The system shall allow creation, reading, updating, and deleting of Disturbance Objects.
The system shall define 'BiomeBGC Projects' for organizing BiomeBGC-specific referenced projects and defining simulation topologies.
The system shall allow creation, reading, updating, and deleting of BiomeBGC Projects.
The system shall support initiating BiomeBGC model runs from BiomeBGC Projects.
The system shall manage the invalidation of BiomeBGC Projects when dependencies change.
The system shall define 'BiomeBGC Output Objects' for storing output from BiomeBGC model runs, allowing sharing and template use.
The system shall allow creation (after a model run), reading, and deletion of BiomeBGC Output Objects.
The system shall allow users to download data contained within BiomeBGC Output Objects.

systemFeatures.name: Visualization and Evaluation Project Management
systemFeatures.description: This feature provides specialized project types for visualizing and evaluating the outputs of Daymet and BiomeBGC models. It allows scientists to create dedicated environments for post-processing, graphical representation, and performance assessment of simulation results. This significantly aids in the interpretation of complex scientific data and the validation of model accuracy. The technical components focus on integrating visualization tools and providing structured workflows for comparative analysis, enabling deeper insights into model behavior and environmental processes. Priority: Medium.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User creates a new Daymet Visualization Project. Response: System allocates a project environment tailored for visualizing Daymet output datasets and prompts for output object selection.

Stimulus: User initiates a model performance evaluation within an Evaluation Project. Response: System executes post-processing scripts on selected output data and displays statistical analysis or comparative visualizations.
Stimulus: User navigates to a BiomeBGC Visualization Project. Response: System presents available visualization tools and previously generated plots for BiomeBGC output data.
functionalRequirements: The system shall enable the creation and management of 'Daymet Visualization Projects' specifically for visualizing Daymet output objects.
The system shall enable the creation and management of 'BiomeBGC Visualization Projects' specifically for visualizing BiomeBGC output objects.
The system shall enable the creation and management of 'Evaluation Projects' for performing post-processing and model performance evaluation studies on output data.

systemFeatures.name: Portal Administration and Monitoring
systemFeatures.description: This feature empowers portal administrators to effectively manage the overall operation and health of the **grid bgc** system. It provides tools for monitoring system usage, reviewing performance metrics, and ensuring data consistency across various components. This capability is crucial for maintaining system stability, optimizing resource allocation, and proactively addressing potential issues, thereby guaranteeing a high level of service for all scientific users. The technical implementation involves backend logging, real-time metric collection, and automated consistency checks. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Portal administrator accesses the 'Portal Metrics' dashboard. Response: System displays real-time and historical data on user activity, job queue status, and resource utilization.

Stimulus: Portal administrator initiates a 'System Consistency Check'. Response: System performs a diagnostic scan across databases and storage, reporting any discrepancies in data linkages or states.
Stimulus: Portal administrator reviews the system's log files. Response: System provides access to categorized and searchable logs, detailing system events, errors, and user actions.
functionalRequirements: The system shall provide portal administrators with access to 'Portal Metrics' detailing system usage and performance.
The system shall implement a 'System Consistency Check' function to verify data integrity and linkages across all system components and storage.
The system shall provide tools for monitoring system logs and events.
The system shall allow administrators to manage compute node resources and queues.

performanceRequirements: GB-REQ-001: The system shall process Daymet and BiomeBGC model runs for average-sized datasets (up to 1 TB input) within 12 hours on designated compute nodes. Rationale: Scientific users require timely completion of simulations for iterative research, making prompt results critical for workflow efficiency.

GB-REQ-002: The web portal shall load all primary dashboards and data lists within 3 seconds for authenticated users under normal network conditions. Rationale: A responsive user interface is essential for a positive user experience, minimizing frustration and encouraging efficient interaction with complex scientific data.

GB-REQ-003: Data transfer operations to and from the **NCAR Mass Storage System** shall achieve a minimum sustained throughput of 100 MB/s for large files (exceeding 10 GB). Rationale: Large scientific datasets necessitate high-speed I/O to avoid bottlenecks in data preparation and result retrieval, ensuring that data movement does not impede computational progress.
safetyRequirements: GB-REQ-004: The system shall prevent accidental or unauthorized deletion of data that has been used in a published model run by enforcing data immutability for 'Locked' objects and projects. Rationale: Preserving the provenance and integrity of scientific data, especially for published results, is paramount to maintain research credibility and reproducibility.

GB-REQ-005: The system shall implement robust error handling and logging for all critical operations, including model runs and data transfers, to facilitate recovery from system failures. Rationale: In a scientific modeling environment, data loss or corrupted computational states can have severe consequences, requiring detailed logs for diagnosis and recovery procedures.

GB-REQ-006: The system shall validate all user-uploaded input files against predefined schemas and formats to prevent malformed data from corrupting model runs or internal data structures. Rationale: Input data quality directly impacts the accuracy of scientific models; preventing invalid inputs reduces computational errors and ensures reliable results.
securityRequirements: GB-REQ-007: The system shall authenticate all users against the **NCAR Gatekeeper account system** for standard users and the internal portal database for administrators, ensuring centralized and secure identity management. Rationale: Leveraging established **NCAR** infrastructure provides a strong, consistent security posture, preventing unauthorized access and maintaining institutional compliance.

GB-REQ-008: All communications between the client, web portal, compute nodes, and **NCAR Mass Storage System** shall be encrypted using **TLS/SSL** protocols to protect data in transit. Rationale: Scientific data can be sensitive, and protecting it from eavesdropping or tampering is critical for research integrity and compliance with data privacy policies.

GB-REQ-009: The system shall implement role-based access control (RBAC) to enforce granular permissions for accessing and manipulating objects, projects, and administrative functions. Rationale: RBAC ensures that users can only perform actions commensurate with their assigned roles (e.g., Scientist, Administrator), preventing privilege escalation and unauthorized data access.

GB-REQ-010: The system shall log all user login attempts, administrative actions, and critical data modifications for auditing and incident response purposes. Rationale: Comprehensive audit trails are necessary for accountability, identifying security breaches, and meeting compliance requirements in a research environment.
softwareQualityAttributes: GB-REQ-011: (Reliability) The system shall achieve an uptime of 99.5% for its web portal and core services, excluding scheduled maintenance. Rationale: Consistent availability is crucial for scientific users who may conduct long-running experiments or access data remotely, minimizing disruption to their research workflows.

GB-REQ-012: (Usability) The web-based graphical user interface (GUI) shall allow a new scientist user to successfully initiate their first Daymet model run within 30 minutes of initial login, with minimal recourse to the help documentation. Rationale: An intuitive and user-friendly interface reduces the learning curve, making the complex modeling capabilities accessible to a broader scientific audience.

GB-REQ-013: (Maintainability) The system's codebase shall adhere to established coding standards and be modularly designed to facilitate future enhancements, bug fixes, and integration of new scientific models with a projected effort of less than 2 person-weeks for minor updates. Rationale: Software maintainability is vital for the long-term viability of the project, ensuring it can evolve with scientific needs and technology changes without excessive cost.

GB-REQ-014: (Scalability) The system shall be capable of horizontally scaling its compute node capacity to accommodate an increase of up to 50% in concurrent model runs without significant degradation in performance. Rationale: As scientific demand grows, the system must be able to expand its computational resources to meet increasing user loads and larger datasets, ensuring continued productivity.

GB-REQ-015: (Interoperability) The system shall support data input and output formats (e.g., NetCDF, CSV) compatible with common scientific analysis tools and platforms. Rationale: Seamless integration with external tools is essential for scientists to perform post-processing, visualization, and further analysis outside the **grid bgc** environment, promoting data exchange and collaboration.
businessRules: GB-REQ-016: All data stored within the **grid bgc** system, once integrated into a model run, must be immutable to ensure the scientific reproducibility and auditability of results. Rationale: This rule is fundamental to scientific integrity, ensuring that any simulation output can be traced back to its exact input parameters without alteration.

GB-REQ-017: Access to the **grid bgc** system's full functionality requires adherence to **NCAR**'s security policies and procedures, as governed by the **NCAR Gatekeeper account system**. Rationale: Compliance with institutional security mandates is a prerequisite for operating within the **NCAR** infrastructure, protecting both system resources and sensitive scientific data.

GB-REQ-018: Template submission privileges shall be granted exclusively to designated expert users or administrators. Rationale: This rule ensures that shared templates, representing expert knowledge, maintain a high standard of quality and reliability, preventing the dissemination of erroneous or poorly configured models.

GB-REQ-019: Data merging operations shall only proceed if the source objects are of the same fundamental type (e.g., List with List, Grid with Grid). Rationale: This constraint prevents logical inconsistencies and errors that would arise from attempting to combine disparate data structures, ensuring the validity of newly created datasets.

otherRequirements: GB-REQ-020: The system shall review the necessity and feasibility of implementing resource quotas for users on compute nodes, prioritizing this evaluation if time permits at the end of the project. Rationale: Resource quotas could manage fair usage and prevent individual users from monopolizing computational resources, but the complexity of implementation requires careful assessment against core project deliverables.

glossary.definition: The current state of a user's account within the **grid bgc** system, including Pending Confirmation, Pending Approval, Active, Locked, and Deleted, governing access and functionality.
A user role with elevated privileges for managing user accounts, overseeing system operations, and configuring portal settings within the **grid bgc** system.
An object type defining spatial masks used for specific analysis purposes within scientific models.
A bio-geo-chemical model used by the **grid bgc** system to perform BGC modeling activities.
A server within a cluster (e.g., **Hemisphere Linux cluster**) dedicated to executing computationally intensive tasks and model runs for the **grid bgc** system.
The maintenance of accuracy and consistency of data over its entire lifecycle, ensured in **grid bgc** by preventing modification of data after use in model runs.
A user class within **grid bgc** primarily interested in utilizing simulation output data but without the ability to initiate simulations.
The existing web server infrastructure at **NCAR** into which the **grid bgc** web portal components integrate.
The surface weather interpolation engine used by **grid bgc** for generating gridded surface weather datasets from observation data records.
A Digital Elevation Model (DEM) object used to define topographic data within the **grid bgc** system for modeling.
An object type representing environmental disturbances, used as input for bio-geo-chemical models.
A set of software tools and services used by **grid bgc** for secure, reliable, and high-performance communication and data transfer across grid computing environments.
An object type containing rectangular grid datasets, representing spatial data within the **grid bgc** system.
The specific **Linux** cluster at CU where the **grid bgc** compute node software is developed and deployed.
An object type containing arbitrary points of data, without enforcing spatial constraints, used within the **grid bgc** system.
An execution of either the Daymet or Biome-BGC model, initiated through a **grid bgc** project, generating simulation outputs.
The external system used by **NCAR** for centralized user authentication and account management, integrated with **grid bgc**.
The central file-based storage system at **NCAR** utilized by **grid bgc** for all persistent data storage.
An object type representing nitrogen input data, used in bio-geo-chemical models.
A logical grouping element in **grid bgc** that groups related data elements, acting as a mechanism for sharing and reuse throughout the system.
An object type containing model-specific parameter data, used to configure simulations.
An acronym for Plant Functional Type, referring to classifications of plants based on their functional characteristics, managed as objects in **grid bgc**.
A specific user role responsible for the day-to-day operations and management of the **grid bgc** web portal.
An object type that defines the spatial projection parameters for a dataset within the **grid bgc** system.
A logical grouping element in **grid bgc** that groups specific objects together to perform a unit of work or control a simulation run.
The primary user class for the **grid bgc** system, engaging in data management, simulation, visualization, and analysis.
An object type containing site-specific data or referenced datasets, used as input for models.
An object type containing observed surface weather data, serving as a primary input dataset for the Daymet model.
A special type of object that provides a means of sharing expert knowledge and predefined configurations, enabling users to create standardized new projects or objects.
A classification of users (e.g., User, Administrator) that determines their permissions and access levels within the **grid bgc** system.

flowchartDiagram.syntaxExplanation: Flowcharts are directed graphs composed of nodes and edges, representing a process or workflow. Nodes define steps, decisions, or start/end points, and edges define the transitions between them. Node IDs are alphanumeric, without spaces or reserved words like 'end'. Labels for nodes with spaces must be enclosed in double quotes. The flow direction (TD for top-down, LR for left-right) is explicitly stated at the beginning of the diagram definition. Decision nodes typically use a diamond shape, process nodes use rectangles, and start/end nodes use rounded rectangles or ovals.
flowchartDiagram.code:
flowchart TD
    StartProcess["Initiate Account Application"]
    A["User Fills Application Form"] --> B{"Enter Gatekeeper Username?"}
    B -- Yes --> C["Validate Gatekeeper User"]
    C --> D{"Gatekeeper User Exists?"}
    D -- No --> E["Display Gatekeeper Error"]
    E --> A
    D -- Yes --> F["Account Status: Pending Confirmation"]
    F --> G["Send Confirmation Email to User"]
    G --> H{"User Confirms Email?"}
    H -- No --> I["Account Status: Locked (No Confirmation)"]
    H -- Yes --> J["Account Status: Pending Approval"]
    J --> K["Notify Portal Admin for Approval"]
    K --> L["Admin Reviews Account Request"]
    L --> M{"Admin Approves?"}
    M -- No --> N["Account Status: Rejected"]
    N --> AdminEnd["Admin Action Complete"]
    M -- Yes --> O["Account Status: Active"]
    O --> P["Assign Default Scientist Role"]
    P --> EndProcess["Account Active & Ready"]
    AdminEnd --> EndProcess
flowchartDiagram.caption: grid bgc User Account Creation Flow

sequenceDiagram.syntaxExplanation: Sequence diagrams illustrate the temporal order of messages exchanged between participants in a system. Participants represent entities or objects, and messages represent communication between them, showing the order of operations. Activations show when a participant is active. The shorthand `->>+` indicates a synchronous call with activation, and `-->>-` indicates a synchronous return with deactivation. Lifelines represent the existence of a participant over time. `alt` blocks represent conditional logic, and `else` provides an alternative path. Aliases for participants are simple alphanumeric IDs, never quoted.
sequenceDiagram.code:
sequenceDiagram
    participant User
    participant WebPortal
    participant Gatekeeper
    participant PortalDB
    participant Admin

    User->>WebPortal: Enter Credentials (Username, Password)
    WebPortal->>+Gatekeeper: Authenticate(Username, Password)
    Gatekeeper-->>-WebPortal: Authentication Result (Success/Fail)

    alt Authentication Success
        WebPortal->>+PortalDB: Retrieve Account Status(Username)
        PortalDB-->>-WebPortal: Account Status (Active/Locked/Pending)
        alt Account Active
            WebPortal-->>User: Display Dashboard
        else Account Locked
            WebPortal-->>User: Display Account Locked Message
        end
    else Authentication Failure
        WebPortal->>WebPortal: Increment Failed Attempts
        WebPortal->>PortalDB: Update Failed Login Count
        alt Failed Attempts >= 3
            WebPortal->>+Admin: Notify Locked Account(Username)
            Admin-->>-WebPortal: Acknowledge Notification
            WebPortal->>PortalDB: Set Account Status to Locked
            WebPortal-->>User: Display Account Locked Message
        else Failed Attempts < 3
            WebPortal-->>User: Display Invalid Credentials Error
        end
    end
sequenceDiagram.caption: grid bgc User Login Sequence

entityRelationshipDiagram.syntaxExplanation: Entity-Relationship Diagrams (ERDs) model the logical structure of a database, showing entities (tables), their attributes, and the relationships between them. Entities are represented as rectangles, and attributes are listed within the entity block, followed by their type and key designators (PK for Primary Key, FK for Foreign Key, UK for Unique Key). Multiple keys are comma-separated. Relationships are indicated by lines connecting entities, with cardinality symbols at each end and a quoted label describing the relationship. Cardinality symbols represent 'one and only one' (||), 'zero or one' (o|), 'one or many' (|{), and 'zero or many' (o{). Foreign key relationships are typically represented with the Entity having the FK using `}|--||` to point to the Entity holding the PK.
entityRelationshipDiagram.code:
erDiagram
    USER {
        int id PK
        string gatekeeper_username UK
        string email
        string name
    }

    ACCOUNT {
        int id PK
        int user_id FK
        string status
        datetime created_at
        datetime last_login_at
        int failed_login_attempts
    }

    ROLE {
        int id PK
        string name UK
    }

    ACCOUNT_ROLE {
        int account_id PK, FK
        int role_id PK, FK
    }

    OBJECT {
        int id PK
        int account_id FK
        string type
        string name
        string status
        datetime created_at
        datetime last_modified_at
        bool is_shared
        string description "For template objects"
    }

    PROJECT {
        int id PK
        int account_id FK
        string type
        string name
        string status
        datetime created_at
        datetime last_modified_at
    }

    MODEL_RUN {
        int id PK
        int project_id FK
        int account_id FK
        datetime start_time
        datetime end_time
        string status
        string output_location
    }

    OBJECT_DEPENDENCY {
        int primary_object_id PK, FK
        int dependent_object_id PK, FK
    }

    PROJECT_REFERENCES_OBJECT {
        int project_id PK, FK
        int object_id PK, FK
    }
    
    PROJECT_DEPENDENCY {
        int primary_project_id PK, FK
        int dependent_project_id PK, FK
    }

    USER ||--o{ ACCOUNT : "has"
    ACCOUNT }|--|| USER : "belongs to"

    ACCOUNT ||--o{ ACCOUNT_ROLE : "assigned"
    ACCOUNT_ROLE }|--|| ACCOUNT : "for"
    ACCOUNT_ROLE }|--|| ROLE : "grants"

    ACCOUNT ||--o{ OBJECT : "creates"
    OBJECT }|--|| ACCOUNT : "created by"

    ACCOUNT ||--o{ PROJECT : "creates"
    PROJECT }|--|| ACCOUNT : "created by"

    ACCOUNT ||--o{ MODEL_RUN : "executes"
    MODEL_RUN }|--|| ACCOUNT : "executed by"

    PROJECT ||--o{ MODEL_RUN : "initiates"
    MODEL_RUN }|--|| PROJECT : "for"

    OBJECT ||--o{ OBJECT_DEPENDENCY : "depends on"
    OBJECT_DEPENDENCY }|--|| OBJECT : "is a dependency of"

    PROJECT ||--o{ PROJECT_REFERENCES_OBJECT : "contains"
    PROJECT_REFERENCES_OBJECT }|--|| PROJECT : "part of"
    PROJECT_REFERENCES_OBJECT }|--|| OBJECT : "references"

    PROJECT ||--o{ PROJECT_DEPENDENCY : "depends on"
    PROJECT_DEPENDENCY }|--|| PROJECT : "is a dependency of"
entityRelationshipDiagram.caption: grid bgc Core Entities and Relationships

tbdList: GB-TBD-001: Review the detailed requirements for resource quotas if time permits at the end of the project (Ref: Section 3.1.6.8).
GB-TBD-002: Clarify the initial setup process for supporting data when a user first attempts a Daymet or BiomeBGC modeling run (Ref: Section 2.3.1, 2.3.2).
