introduction.purpose: The primary purpose of the **caiso** Black Start Capability Plan (BCP) is to outline procedures and requirements for establishing initial generation following a complete or major loss of system generation, often referred to as a blackout. This document ensures that a sufficient quantity and appropriately located set of Black Start generators are available to restore grid operations by self-starting and energizing isolated transmission facilities and auxiliary loads. It serves as a foundational guide for maintaining grid resilience and operational continuity.
introduction.documentConventions: This document adheres to the IEEE 830-1998 standard for Software Requirements Specifications. All requirements, features, and diagrams are identified using a stable prefix C- to ensure traceability and consistency throughout the document. Specific terminology relevant to the **caiso** energy market and grid operations is defined in the Glossary section to maintain clarity.
introduction.intendedAudience: This Software Requirements Specification is primarily intended for **caiso** internal stakeholders, including grid planning engineers, operations support staff, real-time grid operators, and contract managers. Additionally, it serves as a reference for external participants such as generator unit owners and operators who contract with **caiso** to provide Black Start services. Developers and quality assurance personnel responsible for implementing or verifying systems related to Black Start capabilities will also find this document essential.
introduction.productScope: The **caiso** system, as specified herein, encompasses the planning, testing, recordkeeping, and training processes necessary to manage Black Start capabilities within the **CAISO Controlled Grid**. It focuses on identifying, qualifying, and monitoring generating units capable of self-starting and supporting grid restoration efforts. This includes adherence to WECC and NERC reliability standards, management of RMR and Interim Black Start Contracts, and coordination with various stakeholders. The scope excludes the direct control systems of individual generators but mandates integration with **caiso**'s operational and communication protocols.
references: IEEE 830-1998 IEEE Recommended Practice for Software Requirements Specifications.
ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018 Systems and software engineering — Life cycle processes — Requirements engineering.
ISO Tariff Section 8.3.3 Certification and Testing Requirements
CAISO Operating Procedure G-203, Reliability Must Run Unit Commitment and Dispatch
CAISO Operating Procedure G-213 Generator Certification Testing
CAISO Operating Procedure G-213H Black Start Test Report Form
CAISO Operating Procedure G-213I Black Start Process Flowchart
NERC Reliability Standard EOP-005 System Restoration Plans
NERC Reliability Standard EOP-009 Documentation of Blackstart Generating Unit Test Results

overallDescription.productPerspective: The **caiso** Black Start Capability Plan (BCP) is a critical new self-contained product designed to enhance the reliability and resilience of the electrical grid. It specifically addresses the systemic challenge of restoring power after a complete system generation loss, acting as a vital component within the larger framework of **caiso**'s emergency response and grid management systems. This plan is directly integrated with and informed by the overall coordinated WECC BCP, ensuring regional and inter-utility compatibility and operational cohesion.
productFunctions: Determine the necessary amount and location of Black Start Generation through contingency studies.
Verify annually that Black Start generating units meet WECC restoration plan requirements.
Document and provide Cranking Paths between Black Start units and units to be started.
Evaluate Black Start planning, considering diverse fuel resources, cranking paths, communication, and unit capability.
Enter into RMR and Interim Black Start Contracts with qualified generators.
Manage the testing and certification process for Voluntary, Interim, and RMR Black Start units.
Record and maintain a comprehensive database of Black Start generators and their test results.
Provide annual training for Real-time Grid Operators on system restoration, including Black Start procedures and simulations.

userClassesAndCharacteristics.characteristics: These users are highly technical engineers responsible for conducting contingency studies, determining Black Start generation requirements, and verifying unit sufficiency. They require access to planning evaluation tools, grid models, and historical data to ensure compliance with WECC restoration plans. Their role involves strategic long-term planning and coordination with external entities.

These users manage the physical generating units designated for Black Start service. They are responsible for demonstrating unit capability through testing, submitting test documentation, and responding to test dispatch notices. They require clear communication channels with **caiso** and access to forms for reporting test results and requesting availability tests. Their technical expertise lies in power generation and local plant operations.

These individuals manage the administrative aspects of Black Start testing, including receiving test reports, dispatching notices, and determining unit availability based on test outcomes. They need access to contract details, testing criteria, and tools for calculating and updating unit availability limits. Accuracy and timely communication are critical for this role.

These are real-time operational staff who issue test dispatch notices and communicate directly with Black Start unit Scheduling Coordinators. They require up-to-date information on unit availability and the ability to rapidly send notifications. Their work is highly time-sensitive and critical during actual grid restoration events or test simulations.

These operators are responsible for the immediate command and control of the grid, including system restoration efforts. They undergo annual training on Black Start procedures and utilize system simulators. They need access to real-time grid status, Black Start unit information, and robust communication systems to coordinate restoration efforts effectively during emergencies.

overallDescription.operatingEnvironment: The system integrates with **SCADA** (Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition) systems for operational data exchange and includes robust communication platforms for voice and electronic messaging to support urgent notifications and reporting. These integrations ensure seamless data flow and real-time operational awareness, which are crucial for managing Black Start events.
designAndImplementationConstraints: The **caiso** system must strictly adhere to regulatory policies and reliability standards mandated by NERC (North American Electric Reliability Corporation) and WECC (Western Electricity Coordinating Council). Compliance with **caiso**'s own Tariff Sections 4.2, 4.3, and 8.3.3 is mandatory for all Black Start operations and testing.

Hardware limitations include the need for highly available communication infrastructure to support phone notifications and electronic dispatch notices, especially during critical testing or actual blackout scenarios. The system must accommodate data transfer rates necessary for SCADA integration and real-time operational updates.

The system will interface with existing **caiso** internal systems, such as the SLIC (Scheduling and Logging Information Center) system for updating unit availability. It must also accommodate integrations with various generator control systems for test data acquisition.

Specific technologies include the use of email for test report submissions (e.g., `ASNotifications@CAISO.com`) and adherence to established communication protocols for dispatch notices. The use of robust and secure database solutions is required for recordkeeping.

Existing RMR (Reliability Must Run) and Interim Black Start Contracts dictate specific testing frequencies, performance metrics, and reporting requirements that the system must enforce and manage. Any modifications to these contractual obligations must be reflected within the system's logic.

Security considerations mandate strict access controls to the Black Start database and secure transmission of sensitive operational data. Design conventions and programming standards must align with **caiso**'s enterprise architectural guidelines, ensuring maintainability and interoperability.
userDocumentation: The **caiso** system will be accompanied by comprehensive user documentation, including detailed procedures for **CAISO Grid Planners** on conducting studies and evaluations. **Generator Unit Owners/Operators** will receive user manuals for submitting test forms (**G-213H Black Start Test Report Form**) and interpreting test dispatch notices. **CAISO Operations Support Test Administrators** will have guides for managing availability tests and updating unit status. All user documentation will be developed in alignment with **caiso**'s internal documentation standards.
assumptionsAndDependencies: A key assumption is the continued validity and enforceability of Black Start contracts with generator owners, including RMR and Interim Black Start agreements. The project assumes the cooperation of participating transmission owners and neighboring Balancing Authorities in coordinated Black Start planning evaluations. Dependencies include the availability and proper functioning of third-party generator units equipped with Black Start capabilities, as well as the adherence of these units to specified technical requirements and operational protocols.

The system depends on the continued updates and adherence to NERC and WECC reliability standards, as these regulatory frameworks dictate many of the planning and testing requirements. Furthermore, it assumes the ongoing availability of the **caiso** Tariff and related operating procedures, which serve as the foundational rules for Black Start service.

externalInterfaceRequirements.userInterfaces: Key elements of the user interface will include clear navigation, standardized buttons for common actions (e.g., 'Submit', 'Approve', 'Generate Report'), and consistent error message displays. Visual aids such as **Cranking Path diagrams** may be integrated into the planning interface. For **Real-time Grid Operators**, interfaces will focus on displaying critical operational information and enabling rapid communication during system restoration scenarios.
externalInterfaceRequirements.hardwareInterfaces: Communication infrastructure, including dedicated phone lines and network equipment, represents a critical hardware interface for urgent voice notifications and electronic data transfer. The system must support robust and secure connections to ensure reliable communication with geographically dispersed generator units and other control centers, especially during emergency conditions when off-site electric power may be unavailable.
externalInterfaceRequirements.softwareInterfaces: External software interfaces include communication with the WECC and NERC reporting systems for sharing test results and compliance documentation, as required by EOP-009. The system will leverage standard operating system services and network protocols to facilitate data exchange. Furthermore, it will interact with email services for sending automated notifications and receiving test report forms, utilizing specific email addresses such as `ASNotifications@CAISO.com` for official correspondence.
externalInterfaceRequirements.communicationsInterfaces: Network communication protocols, such as **HTTP/HTTPS** for web-based interfaces and secure protocols for data transfer, will be essential for secure and reliable interaction with remote generator sites and other **caiso** systems. Data transfer rates must be sufficient to support real-time updates from SCADA systems and ensure timely dispatch of test notices. Encryption will be applied to all sensitive data in transit to maintain security and privacy during these critical operations.

systemFeatures.name: Black Start Grid Planning Management
systemFeatures.description: This feature provides the capability for **caiso** to systematically determine, verify, and manage the requirements for Black Start generation across the grid. It enhances grid resilience by ensuring an adequate quantity and strategic location of Black Start units, which is crucial for rapid system restoration after a major outage. The feature leverages contingency studies and complies with WECC restoration plans, directly supporting the stability and reliability of the **CAISO Controlled Grid**. Priority: High. This feature is fundamental to preventing prolonged blackouts and maintaining essential grid services.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: CAISO Grid Planner initiates a contingency study. Response: The system provides tools to define study parameters, including disturbance type, outage magnitude, and system demand level.

Stimulus: CAISO Grid Planner requests verification of Black Start unit sufficiency. Response: The system presents an analysis comparing current Black Start unit inventory against WECC restoration plan requirements.

Stimulus: CAISO Grid Planner requests documentation of Cranking Paths. Response: The system generates diagrams and textual descriptions of initial switching requirements between Black Start units and units to be started.

Stimulus: CAISO Grid Planner performs a Black Start planning evaluation. Response: The system facilitates evaluation based on diverse fuel resources, available cranking paths, communication adequacy, and unit capabilities.
functionalRequirements: The system shall enable **CAISO Grid Planners** to define and manage contingency study parameters, including initiating disturbance, outage magnitude, assumed generation status, interconnection status, system demand level, interconnection support, generator performance expectations, and expected transmission system damage.

The system shall annually verify that the number, size, availability, and location of system Black Start generating units are sufficient to meet WECC restoration plan requirements for the **CAISO Balancing Authority Area**.
The system shall store and retrieve documentation of Cranking Paths, including initial switching requirements, between each Black Start generating unit and the units to be started.

The system shall support the Black Start planning evaluation process by allowing assessment of diverse fuel resources, available cranking and transmission paths, communication adequacy, unit voltage and frequency regulation capability, and generator protection systems.
The system shall record and manage coordination activities with Black Start capability plans of transmission owners and neighboring Balancing Authorities.
The system shall facilitate the annual entry into RMR and Interim Black Start Contracts with generators that satisfy the results of the annual planning evaluation process.

systemFeatures.name: Black Start Generator Unit Testing Management
systemFeatures.description: This feature manages the comprehensive process for testing and certifying Black Start generating units, ensuring they meet specified performance requirements for grid restoration. By facilitating both voluntary and contractually obligated testing, it directly verifies the operational readiness of these critical assets, thereby reducing the risk of failure during an actual blackout event. The feature supports a systematic approach to compliance with NERC reliability standards and **caiso** Tariff protocols. Priority: High. Robust testing is essential for confirming the reliability of Black Start capabilities.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Voluntary Black Start unit owner requests Black Start status. Response: The system provides an interface for submitting test documentation and past reports.
Stimulus: Unit owner conducts a Black Start test. Response: The system receives notification of test success or failure via phone within 24 hours and a formal letter within 14 days.
Stimulus: CAISO requests an Availability Test for an RMR or Interim Black Start unit. Response: The system issues a Test Dispatch Notice to the owner's Scheduling Coordinator.

Stimulus: Unit owner submits a G-213H Black Start Test Report Form for an Availability Test. Response: The system processes the request, allows specification of above-contract MNDC, and updates the unit's availability in SLIC upon success.

Stimulus: Alhambra Generation Dispatcher initiates test communication. Response: The system enables dispatchers to notify the BS Unit SC via telephone and transmit electronic Availability Test Dispatch Notices.
Stimulus: BS Generating Unit Operator ramps unit for test. Response: The system facilitates monitoring of requested MW maintenance for four hours and records run time duration.
Stimulus: BS Generating Unit Operator submits ambient temperature data. Response: The system receives and utilizes ambient temperature information for accurate test outcome analysis.

Stimulus: CAISO Operations Support Test Administrator determines unit availability. Response: The system calculates availability based on actual MWh output and temperature correction factor, then declares unit availability or sets a new limit.
functionalRequirements: The system shall allow owners of Voluntary Black Start units to submit documentation of unit test results and analysis to **caiso**, WECC, or NERC upon request.
The system shall record notification of Black Start test success or failure provided by Participants to the **CAISO RT Dispatcher** by phone within 24 hours of conducting a test.
The system shall process formal letters from Participants regarding Black Start test success or failure within 14 days of test performance.
The system shall store Black Start unit testing records, including dates, durations, and indication of compliance with testing requirements.
The system shall verify that Black Start units demonstrate the ability to maintain voltage within emergency limits over a range of loading from no external load to full external load.
The system shall verify that Black Start units are equipped with governors capable of operating in an isochronous mode.
The system shall confirm the availability of adequate transmission capacity to connect the Black Start facility to its designated station services source.
The system shall verify Black Start unit capability to start and energize applicable transmission paths without assistance from the electrical system.
The system shall ensure Black Start tests include verification of key operating aids such as telephone communications and **SCADA**.

The system shall enforce start-up/synchronizing time limits for Black Start units: Hydroelectric and combustion gas turbines within 30 minutes, industrial gas turbines within 60 minutes, and hot, steam-driven turbines within 2.5 hours.
The system shall verify a minimum run time duration (generation time after synchronization) of 10 minutes for a Black Start test.
The system shall process **caiso** market schedules for any test energy generated during a Black Start test.
The system shall manage technical requirements specified by **caiso** for RMR and Interim Black Start Generating Units.
The system shall facilitate performance tests initiated by **caiso** for RMR and Interim Black Start units, with or without prior notification.
The system shall support **caiso**'s sole right to determine when Black Start Generating Unit operation is required.
The system shall track annual Black Start test requests from at least one-third of all RMR and Interim Black Start units.
The system shall assess Black Start units for their ability to self-provide start-up power for the required duration and to complete successive starts as specified in their contracts.
The system shall assess Black Start units for their ability to produce the range of reactive power resources required by their voltage support sections of their RMR or Interim Black Start contracts.
The system shall enable unit owners to request Availability Tests at any time via the **G-213H Black Start Test Report Form**.
The system shall allow unit owners to request testing above their contract MNDC or stated Availability via the **G-213H Black Start Test Report Form**.
The system shall update the Unit’s Availability in **SLIC** per the RMR Contract (Article 7.3 [b]) if a unit retests and passes requested MW.
The system shall enable the **Alhambra Generation Dispatcher** to notify the BS Unit SC via telephone prior to the first hour of the test.

The system shall enable the **Alhambra Generation Dispatcher** to transmit (electronically) an Availability Test Dispatch Notice to the RMR Unit Owner, PTO, and **CAISO Operations Support Test Administrator**.
The system shall require the BS Generating Unit Operator to ramp to the requested MW of the test prior to the start of the first hour.
The system shall require the BS Generating Unit Operator to maintain the requested MW for four full hours during the test.
The system shall allow the BS Generating Unit Operator to ramp back down or continue in Market Transaction after the end of the fourth hour.

The system shall require the RMR Generating Unit Operator to submit Ambient Temperature information relevant to the test timeframe to the **CAISO Operations Support Test Administrator** from **G-203G RMR Test Request Form Availability Test Ambient Temperature Supplement** by 11:00 the next business day after test completion.

The system shall determine the BS Unit’s Availability by averaging the actual MWh output (from the Unit’s Revenue Meter) during the four-hour test period, considering the Unit’s Temperature correction factor.
The system shall declare the BS Unit available at the current Unit Availability Limit if the Unit’s output is at least 99% of the Requested MW.
The system shall set the Availability Limit of the BS Unit to the Availability level achieved during the test if the Unit fails to satisfy the 99% Availability threshold.

systemFeatures.name: Black Start Recordkeeping and Data Management
systemFeatures.description: This feature ensures the diligent maintenance and accessibility of critical data pertaining to Black Start generators and their testing history. By centralizing this information in a regularly updated database, it provides **caiso** with accurate, historical, and real-time insights necessary for planning, operational decision-making, and regulatory compliance. Robust recordkeeping is paramount for accountability, auditing, and continuous improvement of Black Start capabilities. Priority: High. Accurate and current data is foundational to effective grid restoration planning.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: A Black Start unit is designated or updated. Response: The system records or modifies the unit's details in the Black Start database.
Stimulus: A Black Start test is completed. Response: The system updates the latest test date and status for the relevant unit in the database.
Stimulus: A Black Start unit fails to pass **caiso** criteria. Response: The system receives an explanation and a corrective action plan from the generator owner/operator.
Stimulus: WECC or NERC requests Black Start test results. Response: The system provides the requested documentation and justification within 30 days.
functionalRequirements: The system shall maintain a Black Start database that contains all Black Start generators designated for use during system restoration.
The Black Start database shall be reviewed and updated on at least an annual basis.
The Black Start database shall include the name, location, megawatt capacity, type of unit, latest date of test, and starting method for each designated generator.

The system shall receive and store an explanation and a plan to address and correct deficiencies from a generator owner/operator if a Black Start unit did not successfully pass the **caiso** Black Start criteria.
The system shall be capable of providing test results and justification for testing periodicity to WECC or NERC upon request within 30 days.

systemFeatures.name: Black Start Operator Training Management
systemFeatures.description: This feature ensures that **caiso** Real-time Grid Operators are adequately prepared for system restoration scenarios, including the effective utilization of Black Start capable units. Through annual training and simulations, it cultivates the necessary skills and coordination required to manage complex grid outages and restoration, thereby enhancing operational safety and efficiency. This commitment to ongoing education directly mitigates human error risk during high-stress emergency events. Priority: High. Well-trained operators are indispensable for successful grid restoration.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Annual training cycle begins. Response: The system facilitates the scheduling and delivery of training modules covering NERC and WECC requirements, and internal procedures.
Stimulus: A system restoration simulation is conducted. Response: The system provides simulation tools, records operator performance, and generates a critique report.
Stimulus: A system simulator becomes available. Response: The system integrates the simulator for advanced operator training on system restoration and Black Start unit use.
functionalRequirements: The system shall facilitate annual training for **Real-time Grid Operators** on system restoration, including the use of Black Start capable units.
The training program shall encompass integrated coordination of NERC requirements, WECC requirements, E-501 System Restoration, E-501A Black Start Capable Units, and the Black Start Capability Plan.
The system shall support simulations of full or partial system shutdowns and restoration, including the generation of critique reports for each simulation.
The system shall integrate a system simulator, upon availability, to conduct operator training for system restoration and Black Start unit use.

performanceRequirements: C-REQ-001: The system shall ensure that notifications for Black Start tests, including dispatch notices and success/failure reports, are communicated to relevant parties within specified timeframes (e.g., 24 hours for phone notification, 14 days for letter, immediate for dispatch). Rationale: Timely communication is critical for operational readiness and compliance during grid restoration efforts.

C-REQ-002: The Black Start database shall respond to queries for unit information (name, location, capacity, test dates) within 2 seconds for a single unit and 5 seconds for aggregate reports of all units. Rationale: Rapid access to accurate data is essential for planning and operational decision-making, particularly during emergency scenarios.

C-REQ-003: The system shall process submitted test report forms and update unit availability in the **SLIC** system within 1 hour of reception for an RMR unit. Rationale: Swift updates ensure that operational systems reflect the most current status of Black Start capabilities, preventing misdispatch or incorrect planning assumptions.
safetyRequirements: C-REQ-004: The system shall prominently display warnings to users regarding actions that could endanger personnel safety, damage equipment, or violate regulations during Black Start testing procedures. Rationale: Ensuring user awareness of safety protocols prevents accidents and operational hazards.

C-REQ-005: The system shall include validation checks on test parameters and results to identify potential inconsistencies that could indicate unsafe operating conditions or data entry errors. Rationale: Such validation helps prevent misinterpretation of test outcomes that could lead to dangerous operational decisions.

C-REQ-006: All operational procedures and system interfaces related to Black Start shall be designed to minimize the potential for human error, especially during high-stress restoration scenarios. Rationale: Reducing cognitive load and simplifying complex tasks directly enhances operational safety and efficiency.
securityRequirements: C-REQ-007: The Black Start database and all related configuration files shall be protected by role-based access control, ensuring that only authorized **caiso** personnel and external participants can view or modify specific data elements. Rationale: Restricting access prevents unauthorized alterations of critical grid restoration information.

C-REQ-008: All communications interfaces, including email and web-based portals, shall utilize industry-standard encryption protocols (e.g., **TLS 1.2+**) for data in transit to protect sensitive operational information from interception. Rationale: Encryption safeguards against espionage and maintains the integrity of commands and reports.

C-REQ-009: The system shall implement robust authentication mechanisms (e.g., multi-factor authentication) for all **CAISO Operations Support Test Administrators** and **CAISO Grid Planners** accessing administrative functions. Rationale: Strong authentication prevents unauthorized system access and maintains the security of grid planning and operational data.
softwareQualityAttributes: C-REQ-010: **Maintainability**: The system shall be designed with modular architecture and clear documentation to allow for efficient updates and bug fixes. Rationale: The dynamic nature of grid regulations and operational requirements necessitates a system that can be easily adapted and sustained over time.

C-REQ-011: **Reliability**: The system shall operate with 99.9% uptime, excluding planned maintenance, to ensure continuous availability of Black Start planning and testing data. Rationale: The criticality of grid restoration functions demands a highly dependable system that is accessible when needed most.

C-REQ-012: **Testability**: The system shall provide comprehensive logging and diagnostic capabilities to facilitate independent testing and verification of all functional requirements. Rationale: Rigorous testing ensures that the system accurately performs its intended functions and adheres to all compliance standards.

C-REQ-013: **Usability**: User interfaces for all user classes shall be intuitive and require minimal training, designed to reduce cognitive load during routine operations and emergency situations. Rationale: Ease of use minimizes errors and increases operational efficiency, especially for time-sensitive tasks performed by operators and dispatchers.
businessRules: C-REQ-014: All Black Start planning evaluations and generator unit testing procedures shall strictly adhere to the requirements outlined in the WECC Black Start Capability Plan. Rationale: Compliance with regional standards ensures interoperability and consistent reliability across the interconnected grid.

C-REQ-015: The **caiso** system shall enforce specific start-up/synchronizing time limits for different generator types (hydroelectric/combustion gas turbines: 30 minutes; industrial gas turbines: 60 minutes; hot, steam-driven turbines: 2.5 hours). Rationale: These time limits are critical operational parameters that dictate the speed of grid restoration.

C-REQ-016: Black Start units under RMR or Interim Black Start Contracts must satisfy technical requirements specified by **caiso**, including voltage maintenance and governor operation in isochronous mode. Rationale: These technical specifications ensure that contracted units possess the necessary capabilities for effective Black Start service.

C-REQ-017: The **caiso** system shall ensure that Black Start units are tested for their ability to self-provide start-up power for the period required to complete the start-up process and synchronize to the grid. Rationale: Verifying self-start capability is fundamental to the definition and purpose of a Black Start unit.

glossary.definition: A test called by the **caiso** or the RMR Unit Owner to establish and verify the RMR Unit’s Availability Limit.
The ability of a generating unit or station to go from a shutdown condition to an operating condition and start delivering energy to the grid without initial assistance from the electric system.
A generating unit with Black Start capability that enters into an Interim Black Start Contract with the **caiso**. This is not an RMR contract.
Those generator owners or operators that desire to test their generating units for Black Start capability.
A generating unit with Black Start capability that enters into an RMR Black Start Contract with the **caiso**.
A generating unit without a **caiso** Black Start contract that desires, for whatever reason, to be recognized as a **caiso** Black Start generating unit.
Western Electricity Coordinating Council, a regional entity of NERC responsible for coordinating and promoting electric system reliability in the western North American interconnection.
North American Electric Reliability Corporation, responsible for ensuring the reliability of the bulk power system in North America.
Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition, a system for real-time control and monitoring of industrial processes and infrastructure.
Scheduling and Logging Information Center, an internal **caiso** system used for managing and logging operational information, including unit availability.

flowchartDiagram.syntaxExplanation: A Flowchart diagram illustrates the sequence of steps and decision points within a process. Nodes represent process steps or decisions, and arrows indicate the flow of control. Node IDs must be alphanumeric without spaces, and labels with spaces must be double-quoted. The direction of the flow is specified at the beginning (TD for Top-Down, LR for Left-Right). This diagram specifically details the workflow for RMR and Interim Black Start Unit Availability Testing, showing interactions and decision outcomes.
flowchartDiagram.code:
flowchart TD
    A1["Unit Owner: Request Availability Test (G-213H)"] --> A2{"Test Above Contract MNDC?"}
    A2 -->|Yes| A3["Request in G-213H Form"]
    A2 -->|No| A4["Proceed with Standard Test"]
    A3 --> A5["CAISO Admin: Process Request"]
    A4 --> A5
    A5 --> A6["Alhambra Dispatcher: Notify BS Unit SC by Phone"]
    A6 --> A7["Alhambra Dispatcher: Transmit Electronic Test Notice"]
    A7 --> A8["BS Unit Operator: Ramp to Requested MW"]
    A8 --> A9["BS Unit Operator: Maintain MW for 4 Hours"]
    A9 --> A10["BS Unit Operator: Ramp Down / Market Transaction"]
    A10 --> A11["Unit Owner: Submit Ambient Temp Data"]
    A11 --> A12["CAISO Admin: Determine BS Unit Availability"]
    A12 --> A13{"Unit Output >= 99% of Requested MW?"}
    A13 -->|Yes| A14["CAISO Admin: Declare Unit Available at Current Limit"]
    A13 -->|No| A15["CAISO Admin: Set Availability Limit to Achieved Level"]
    A14 --> A16([End])
    A15 --> A16
flowchartDiagram.caption: RMR and Interim Black Start Unit Availability Test Flow

sequenceDiagram.syntaxExplanation: A Sequence Diagram illustrates the interactions between different participants in a system over time. Participants are declared at the top, and messages between them are shown as arrows. Activations indicate when a participant is performing an action. Arrows ending with `+` initiate activation, and arrows ending with `-` deactivate. Labels for messages should be concise. This diagram focuses on the Black Start Testing and Reporting process, showing the temporal flow of communications.
sequenceDiagram.code:
sequenceDiagram
    participant UO as "Voluntary BS Unit Owner"
    participant C as "CAISO"
    participant W as "WECC"
    participant N as "NERC"
    participant RD as "RT Dispatcher"

    UO->>C: Demonstrate Capability (Simulation/Test)
    C->>C: Store Test Requirements
    UO->>W: Submit Test Documentation (on request)
    UO->>N: Submit Test Documentation (on request)
    UO->>RD: Phone Notification: "Test Success/Failure" (within 24 hrs)
    RD->>C: Log Phone Notification
    UO->>C: Letter: "Test Success/Failure" (within 14 days)
    C->>C: Record Test Results and Analysis
    C->>W: Provide Test Records (on request)
    C->>N: Provide Test Records (on request)
sequenceDiagram.caption: Voluntary Black Start Unit Testing and Reporting Sequence

entityRelationshipDiagram.syntaxExplanation: An Entity Relationship Diagram (ERD) visually represents the structural design of a database by illustrating entities (tables), their attributes (columns), and the relationships between them. Entities are denoted by rectangles, and attributes are listed within them, specifying keys like Primary Key (PK) or Foreign Key (FK). Relationships show cardinality (e.g., one-to-one, one-to-many) and are labeled to describe their nature. This diagram models the core entities involved in Black Start management within the **caiso** system.
entityRelationshipDiagram.code:
erDiagram
    "UNIT_OWNER" ||--o{ "BLACK_START_UNIT" : "owns"
    "BLACK_START_UNIT" ||--o{ "CONTRACT" : "has"
    "BLACK_START_UNIT" ||--o{ "TEST_RECORD" : "generates"
    "CAISO_ADMIN" ||--o{ "TEST_RECORD" : "manages"

    "UNIT_OWNER" {
        int id PK
        string name
        string email
    }
    "BLACK_START_UNIT" {
        int id PK
        int unit_owner_id FK
        string name
        string location
        int mw_capacity
        string type
        string start_method
        date latest_test_date
    }
    "CONTRACT" {
        int id PK
        int black_start_unit_id FK
        string type "RMR or Interim"
        string start_time_limit
        date valid_from
        date valid_to
    }
    "TEST_RECORD" {
        int id PK
        int black_start_unit_id FK
        int c_admin_id FK
        date date_of_test
        int duration_minutes
        string status "Success/Failure"
        decimal mw_output
        string temperature_data
        string comments
    }
    "CAISO_ADMIN" {
        int id PK
        string name
        string role
        string email
    }
entityRelationshipDiagram.caption: caiso Black Start Management Data Model
