flowchartDiagram.syntaxExplanation: This Mermaid Flowchart illustrates the high-level process flow for a customer interacting with the **gamma j** Web Store, from initial login to order confirmation. Nodes represent distinct steps or decisions, and arrows indicate the flow direction. All node labels containing spaces or symbols are enclosed in double-quotes, while node IDs are alphanumeric for strict compliance.
flowchartDiagram.code:
flowchart TD
  StartProcess("Customer Initiates") --> Login{"User Logged In?"}
  Login -- No --> LoginScreen("Show Login Screen")
  LoginScreen --> CredsInput("Enter Credentials")
  CredsInput --> ValidateCreds{"Validate Credentials?"}
  ValidateCreds -- Invalid --> ErrorMsg("Display Error")
  ErrorMsg --> LoginScreen
  ValidateCreds -- Valid --> BrowseProd("Browse Products")
  Login -- Yes --> BrowseProd
  BrowseProd --> SelectItem("Select Item")
  SelectItem --> AddToCart("Add To Cart")
  AddToCart --> CartView{"View Cart?"}
  CartView -- Yes --> ShowCart("Show Shopping Cart")
  ShowCart --> EditCart{"Edit Cart?"}
  EditCart -- Yes --> BrowseProd
  EditCart -- No --> Checkout("Proceed to Checkout")
  CartView -- No --> Checkout
  Checkout --> PaymentInfo("Enter Payment Info")
  PaymentInfo --> ProcessPay{"Process Payment?"}
  ProcessPay -- Fail --> PaymentInfo
  ProcessPay -- Success --> ConfirmOrder("Confirm Order")
  ConfirmOrder --> OrderPlaced["Order Placed"]
flowchartDiagram.caption: Customer purchase flow in gamma j.

sequenceDiagram.syntaxExplanation: This Mermaid Sequence Diagram visualizes the interaction flow when a customer adds a product to their shopping cart in the **gamma j** Web Store. Participants represent system components or users, and arrows denote messages exchanged over time. The `->>+` and `-->>-` notations are used to indicate activation and deactivation of participants, ensuring balanced call stacks without explicit `activate`/`deactivate` keywords in most cases.
sequenceDiagram.code:
sequenceDiagram
  participant Customer
  participant WebBrowser
  participant WebServer
  participant ProductService
  participant ShoppingCartService

  Customer->>WebBrowser: "Browse products"
  WebBrowser->>+WebServer: GET /products
  WebServer->>ProductService: "Request product list"
  ProductService-->>WebServer: "Return product list"
  WebServer-->>-WebBrowser: "Display products"

  Customer->>WebBrowser: "Select product [ProductA]"
  WebBrowser->>+WebServer: POST /cart/add [ProductID=A, Quantity=1]
  WebServer->>+ShoppingCartService: "Add product to cart (UserID, ProductID, Qty)"
  ShoppingCartService->>ProductService: "Verify product availability and price"
  ProductService-->>ShoppingCartService: "Product details and availability"
  ShoppingCartService-->>-WebServer: "Cart updated successfully"
  WebServer-->>-WebBrowser: "200 OK - Cart updated"
  WebBrowser->>Customer: "Display updated cart summary"
sequenceDiagram.caption: Add Product to Cart sequence.

entityRelationshipDiagram.syntaxExplanation: This Mermaid Entity Relationship Diagram (ERD) models the core data entities and their relationships within the **gamma j** Web Store. Entities represent significant domain objects, while relationships illustrate how these entities are associated. Cardinality notations (e.g., ||--o{ for one-to-many, |o--|| for zero-or-one to one) define the numerical relationship. Attributes include `PK` for Primary Key, `FK` for Foreign Key, and `UK` for Unique Key, with all relationship labels strictly quoted.
entityRelationshipDiagram.code:
erDiagram
  CUSTOMER ||--o{ ORDER : "places"
  ORDER ||--|{ LINE_ITEM : "contains"
  PRODUCT ||--o{ LINE_ITEM : "includes"
  CATEGORY ||--|{ PRODUCT : "categorizes"
  ADMIN ||--o{ PRODUCT : "manages"
  CUSTOMER ||--o{ ADDRESS : "has"
  CUSTOMER ||--o{ PAYMENT_METHOD : "stores"

  CUSTOMER {
    int customer_id PK
    string email UK
    string password
    string first_name
    string last_name
    datetime created_at
  }

  ORDER {
    int order_id PK
    int customer_id FK
    datetime order_date
    decimal total_amount
    string status
  }

  PRODUCT {
    int product_id PK
    int category_id FK
    string name
    string description
    decimal price
    int stock_quantity
  }

  LINE_ITEM {
    int line_item_id PK
    int order_id FK
    int product_id FK
    int quantity
    decimal unit_price
  }

  CATEGORY {
    int category_id PK
    string name UK
    string description
  }

  ADMIN {
    int admin_id PK
    string username UK
    string password
    string role
  }

  ADDRESS {
    int address_id PK
    int customer_id FK
    string street
    string city
    string state
    string zip_code
    string type
  }

  PAYMENT_METHOD {
    int payment_id PK
    int customer_id FK
    string card_type
    string last_four_digits
    date expiry_date
    boolean is_default
  }
entityRelationshipDiagram.caption: Entity relationship model for gamma j.

tbdList: GJ-REQ-TBD-001: The system currently does not support detailed customer order analysis features, which are required for advanced business intelligence. This functionality needs to be designed and implemented in a future iteration.
GJ-REQ-TBD-002: Specific details regarding the frequency and retention policies for periodic system backups (GJ-REQ-059) are yet to be determined.
GJ-REQ-TBD-003: The exact algorithms and external services for credit card fraud validation (GJ-REQ-060) require further specification and integration planning.
GJ-REQ-TBD-004: The precise configuration and rule sets for the Firestarter firewall (GJ-REQ-065) need to be finalized based on detailed security policy reviews.

introduction.purpose: The purpose of this document is to comprehensively detail the software requirements for the **vub** Publications Management System (PMS). It serves as a foundational specification, guiding the development, testing, and deployment phases. This SRS adheres to the IEEE Std 830-1998 standard, providing a clear and unambiguous understanding of the system's functionality and constraints. It aims to align all stakeholders, including customers, developers, and testers, on the definitive scope and expected behavior of the system.
introduction.documentConventions: This document follows the structural and content guidelines outlined in IEEE Std 830-1998 for Software Requirements Specifications. All requirements are stated clearly and unambiguously, adhering to a consistent naming convention. Key terms, acronyms, and abbreviations are defined in the Glossary section to ensure common understanding. All system features are identified with a stable prefix, V-REQ, and described in a standardized format encompassing descriptions, stimulus/response sequences, and atomic functional requirements.
introduction.intendedAudience: The primary audience for this SRS includes the customer representatives who commissioned the **vub** system, the development team responsible for its implementation, and the quality assurance personnel who will validate its conformity. Project managers, system architects, and technical writers will also find this document valuable for planning, design, and documentation purposes. It provides a shared understanding of the system's capabilities and limitations, facilitating effective communication among all involved parties.
introduction.productScope: The **vub** system is designed to function as a Publications Management System, accessible via both a web browser and a command-line interface. Its core functionality revolves around managing a database of bibliographical information, including the storage of full-text publications in PDF and Postscript formats. The system will automate the extraction of bibliographical data from full texts, with provisions for manual correction and sophisticated author disambiguation. It will implement a hierarchical user access control mechanism, supporting various user roles from Guest to Administrator. The system is intended to be modular and extensible, running on a **Linux** server environment.
references: IEEE 830-1998 IEEE Recommended Practice for Software Requirements Specifications.
ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018 Systems and software engineering — Life cycle processes — Requirements engineering.
W3C XHTML 1.0 Transitional Standard for Web Content.

overallDescription.productPerspective:
The **vub** system functions as an independent Publications Management System, designed to serve academic personnel and students at the Free University of Brussels (VUB). It is a standalone product that replaces existing manual methods for managing research publications. The system primarily interfaces with users through a web-based interface and, for administrators, a command-line interface.

The system is built upon a client-server architecture, where a **MySQL** database serves as the central repository for all bibliographical data and publication files. A dedicated web server hosts the application, managing user requests, database interactions, and the generation of the user interface. This server-side processing ensures a robust and centralized control over publication data and user access.

The **vub** system provides differentiated access levels, catering to a range of users from anonymous guests to full administrators. Its design emphasizes secure access and efficient retrieval of information, forming a critical component of the university's research infrastructure. The software aims to streamline the process of managing, searching, and accessing academic publications for the VUB community and beyond.
productFunctions: The **vub** system provides a comprehensive set of functions categorized by user roles, operating within a hierarchical access structure. Guests can register accounts, log in, validate new accounts, and retrieve forgotten passwords. **VUB-Network Users** (and all higher roles) gain search capabilities, including basic and advanced search options, and can order, export, and download search results.

Members can further manage their account details and log off from the system. Publishers extend these capabilities by being able to upload, bulk upload, and edit their own publications, including automatic bibliographical data extraction with manual correction. Moderators inherit all Publisher functions and can additionally manage user levels (for their department), group permissions, and edit authors' information and departmental publications.

Finally, Administrators possess the highest level of control, including access to a control panel, the ability to create, delete, and manage user accounts and groups, and the authority to edit any publication across the entire system. This structured approach ensures that each user role has access to appropriate functionalities, balancing autonomy with system integrity.

userClassesAndCharacteristics.characteristics: An anonymous user not connected to the VUB-network and not logged in. Guests can only perform basic actions such as registering an account, logging into the system, validating a new account via email, and retrieving a forgotten password. They have no access to publication search or management functions.

A user connected to the VUB-network (recognized by IP address) but not logged in. This class inherits all Guest functions and gains the ability to search for publications, view results, order results, and export/download publications. They cannot upload or edit publications.

A user who is logged into the **vub** system. Members inherit all VUB-Network User functions and can additionally change their account details, request account upgrades, and log off. They are primarily consumers of information and cannot upload or edit publications.

A logged-in user with increased privileges, specifically for contributing content. Publishers inherit all Member functions and can upload single or multiple publications (via **BibTeX** or **RIS** format), automatically extracting bibliographical information, and edit their own uploaded publications.

A logged-in user with departmental oversight. Moderators inherit all Publisher functions and are empowered to manage user levels (upgrading/downgrading Members to Publishers within their department), manage group permissions, edit author information, and modify/overwrite departmental publications.

The highest-privileged user, responsible for system-wide technical and user management. Administrators inherit all Moderator functions, gaining access to a comprehensive Control Panel. They can create, delete, and manage any user account and group, change any user's level, and edit or overwrite any publication in the entire system. Users accessing via Command Line Interface within the VUB-network automatically assume Administrator privileges.

overallDescription.operatingEnvironment:
The **vub** system is designed to operate primarily on a **Linux**-based environment, specifically targeting the **Wilma** web server at VUB. The application's backend will be developed using the **Ruby** programming language, version 1.8 or later, ensuring compatibility and leveraging its robust features. This choice of operating system and programming language dictates the deployment environment and development toolchain.

Client-side access to the **vub** system will be through standard web browsers, supporting various operating systems. The system will explicitly ensure accessibility for users on **Linux** operating systems utilizing text-based web browsers, such as **Links2**, underscoring a commitment to broad compatibility within the academic setting. Command-line access for administrators will be provided directly from the **Linux** server shell, necessitating direct server access for these high-privilege operations.
designAndImplementationConstraints: The **vub** system's design must strictly adhere to a modular architecture to facilitate future expansions, maintenance, and the replacement of components without impacting the entire system.
The chosen programming language for development is **Ruby**, and its code must be thoroughly documented using **RDoc** standards for maintainability and comprehension.
For software configuration management, **Git** is mandated as the version control system. Deployment is restricted to **Linux** environments, specifically the **Wilma** web server.

The user interface for the web application must comply with the **W3C XHTML 1.0 Transitional** standard, ensuring compatibility and accessibility across a wide range of browsers. Furthermore, the system must prioritize user efficiency, aiming for all functions and options to be accessible with the least number of clicks while preserving a logical structure.
A critical constraint is the prohibition of using any external frameworks, emphasizing a custom-built solution, and the use of only free software for all development tools and the final product.
userDocumentation: The **vub** system will be delivered with essential user documentation to support its diverse user base. This documentation will include an online help system integrated within the web interface, providing context-sensitive assistance for various functionalities such as registration, searching, and publication uploads. Tutorials will be available to guide new users through core workflows, demonstrating how to effectively utilize the system's features. Additionally, an administrative guide will be provided for those managing the system via the command line or the dedicated control panel, detailing configuration options and troubleshooting steps.
assumptionsAndDependencies: It is assumed that all users accessing the **vub** system via the web interface will utilize a web browser that fully supports the **W3C XHTML** standards. Furthermore, a stable **TCP/IP** connection between the client and the web server is a prerequisite for system interaction, specifically with **HTTP** traffic enabled on port 80. The functionality of the system is dependent on these network communication protocols being unrestricted.

For users requiring command-line access, it is assumed they possess direct shell access to the **Wilma** server machine. This direct access implies that such users are automatically granted Administrator privileges, signifying a trust relationship with the underlying operating system. The system's operational integrity also relies on the continuous availability and proper configuration of the **MySQL** database and the **Ruby** runtime environment on the **Linux** server.

externalInterfaceRequirements.userInterfaces:
The **vub** system will provide a primary web-based user interface, accessible from any operating system via a compatible web browser. This interface is designed for speed and efficiency, enabling users to perform tasks like uploading, downloading, and searching publications with minimal clicks and page transitions. It prioritizes clarity and a structured layout, adhering to the **W3C XHTML 1.0 Transitional** standard to ensure broad compatibility and accessibility. The interface will feature standard navigational elements, consistent button layouts, and clear display of search results.

For administrative functions, a Command Line Interface (**CLI**) will be available, accessible directly from the **Linux** server. This interface provides powerful, direct control over system configurations and user management for authorized personnel. In cases of system errors during web interactions, the system shall present clear, informative error messages to the user and log detailed error information for the administrator, facilitating prompt diagnosis and resolution of technical issues. The visual aesthetics, while secondary to functionality, will aim for a simple yet attractive design.
externalInterfaceRequirements.hardwareInterfaces:
The **vub** system imposes no specific hardware requirements on the client-side for its users, assuming only a standard computer capable of running a modern web browser. For its server-side operations, the system requires a **Linux**-based server environment, specifically the **Wilma** web server, equipped with sufficient processing power, memory, and disk space to host the **Ruby** application, the **MySQL** database, and store large volumes of publication files.

The system will interact with underlying hardware resources primarily through the operating system and the web server software. This includes disk I/O for storing and retrieving publication files (PDF, PS), network interfaces for client-server communication via **HTTP**, and CPU/RAM for processing search queries, data extraction, and user authentication. No direct, specialized hardware interfaces (e.g., custom sensors, specific peripherals) are required for the core functionality of the **vub** system beyond standard server infrastructure.
externalInterfaceRequirements.softwareInterfaces:
The **vub** system integrates with several key software components to deliver its functionality. At its core, it interfaces with a **MySQL** database for persistent storage of all bibliographical information, user accounts, and publication metadata. The system will leverage standard database connection protocols to query, insert, update, and delete data, ensuring data integrity and transactional consistency.

The application, developed in **Ruby**, will run on a web server, which acts as the intermediary between the user interface and the database. This server will handle the execution of the **vub** application logic, process user requests, and generate the necessary web pages. Other internal software interfaces include the use of **RDoc** for code documentation and **Git** for source code management, which are integral to the development and maintenance lifecycle but do not directly interface with the end-user product at runtime. The system will also interact with the underlying **Linux** operating system for file management, process execution, and network communication.
externalInterfaceRequirements.communicationsInterfaces:
The **vub** system will primarily communicate with its users via **HTTP** (Hypertext Transfer Protocol) over **TCP/IP** on port 80. This standard web communication protocol will facilitate all interactions between the user's web browser and the **vub** web server, ensuring broad accessibility across various networks and client devices. All requests for information, form submissions, and content delivery will utilize this protocol.

For administrative command-line access, communication will occur directly over the local network or through secure shell (**SSH**) protocols if accessed remotely, granting direct control to the **Linux** server shell. Message formatting for data transfer, especially for publication uploads and exports (e.g., **BibTeX**, **RIS**, HTML, PDF, PS formats), will adhere to industry-standard specifications for data exchange. While explicit security or encryption issues are not detailed, the reliance on standard network protocols implies standard network security practices should be applied to the server environment. Data transfer rates will be optimized for efficient delivery of publication files, which can be substantial in size.

systemFeatures.name: Internet Login
systemFeatures.description: The **vub** system provides a secure login mechanism for users accessing from outside the VUB-network. This feature enables individuals to authenticate their identity, gaining access to personalized functionalities of the Publications Management System. Its technical impact involves robust session management and credential validation processes against the user database. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User navigates to the **vub** login page from the internet. Response: The system displays a login form.
Stimulus: User enters credentials and submits the login form. Response: The system validates credentials and logs the user in, redirecting them to their appropriate dashboard or the search page.
functionalRequirements: The system shall display a login interface for users accessing from outside the VUB-network.
The system shall authenticate user credentials provided through the internet login interface.
The system shall allow only registered users with valid credentials to log in.
The system shall redirect authenticated users to the appropriate interface based on their assigned role.

systemFeatures.name: VUB-Network Login and Search
systemFeatures.description: This feature allows users within the VUB-network to either log in to their registered account or immediately commence searching for publications without logging in. By recognizing users via their IP address, the **vub** system streamlines access for internal users, providing immediate utility for guests within the network perimeter. The technical implication is the necessity for IP address-based user role identification and conditional access pathways. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User accesses the **vub** system while connected to the VUB-network. Response: The system presents a screen offering login or direct search access.
Stimulus: User selects to perform a search directly. Response: The system displays the publication search interface with VUB-Network User privileges.
functionalRequirements: The system shall identify users connected to the VUB-network by their IP address.
The system shall offer VUB-network users the option to log in or proceed directly to publication search.
The system shall grant VUB-Network Users appropriate search and download privileges without explicit login.
The system shall allow VUB-network users to log into their registered accounts.

systemFeatures.name: Account Registration
systemFeatures.description: The **vub** system enables prospective users to register for a new account, granting them Member status upon successful validation. This feature is crucial for expanding the user base and providing personalized access to the publication management functionalities. It involves processes for data collection, uniqueness checks for usernames, and an administrative approval or user validation workflow. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User selects the 'Register' function. Response: The system displays an account registration form.

Stimulus: User fills in personal data (name, email, university, department, desired username, password) and submits the form. Response: The system checks for username uniqueness and sends a registration request to an administrator (or validation email to user).
Stimulus: Administrator reviews and approves the registration, or user validates via email. Response: The system activates the new user account with Member privileges.
functionalRequirements: The system shall provide an account registration interface.
The system shall collect user's name, login name, email address, university, and department during registration.
The system shall verify the uniqueness of the chosen username during registration.
The system shall send a notification or validation email upon successful submission of the registration form.
The system shall activate a user account upon validation or administrative approval.

systemFeatures.name: User Logoff
systemFeatures.description: This feature allows authenticated users (Members, Publishers, Moderators, and Administrators) to securely terminate their active session with the **vub** system. It ensures the protection of user accounts by preventing unauthorized access after a user has finished their session. The technical impact involves invalidating the current user session and redirecting the user to a public access page. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: A logged-in user selects the 'Logout' function. Response: The system terminates the user's session.
Stimulus: The user's session is terminated. Response: The system redirects the user to the login page (if outside VUB-network) or the search page (if within VUB-network).
functionalRequirements: The system shall provide a clear 'Logout' function for authenticated users.
The system shall invalidate the current user session upon logoff.
The system shall redirect the user to the appropriate start page (login or search) after successful logoff.

systemFeatures.name: Password Management
systemFeatures.description: The **vub** system enables logged-in users to change their account password and provides a mechanism for users who are not logged in to retrieve a forgotten password. These features are essential for maintaining account security and user accessibility, ensuring that users can manage their credentials effectively. Technical considerations include secure password hashing, verification processes, and a reliable email delivery system for password resets. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: A logged-in user navigates to 'Change Account Details' and selects 'Change Password'. Response: The system prompts the user to enter their current and new passwords.
Stimulus: User enters current and new passwords and submits. Response: The system validates the current password, updates the password, and confirms the change.
Stimulus: A non-logged-in user selects 'Retrieve Password'. Response: The system prompts the user for their registered email address.
Stimulus: User provides registered email address and submits. Response: The system sends a password retrieval/reset link to the registered email address.
functionalRequirements: The system shall allow logged-in users to change their password securely.
The system shall validate the current password before allowing a change.
The system shall provide a mechanism for users to retrieve a forgotten password.
The system shall send a password reset link or instructions to the user's registered email address.

systemFeatures.name: Publication Search
systemFeatures.description: The **vub** system offers comprehensive search capabilities, allowing users to find publications using keywords, author names, and publication dates. This feature supports logical expressions and symbols for precise querying, enhancing the discoverability of relevant academic content. It also allows for advanced search options, including specifying references and similar publications. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User accesses the search interface. Response: The system displays a search form with fields for keywords, authors, and date ranges.
Stimulus: User enters search criteria (e.g., keywords with AND/OR, author names, date range) and submits. Response: The system executes the search query against the publication database.
Stimulus: User selects an 'Advanced Search' option. Response: The system displays additional fields for specifying keywords of referred publications or similar content.
functionalRequirements: The system shall provide a search interface with fields for keywords, author(s), and publication dates.
The system shall support logical expressions (AND, OR, NOT) and symbols ('') within keyword searches.
The system shall allow users to search for publications by one or more authors, potentially ranked by importance.
The system shall enable searching for publications published within a specified date range.
The system shall support advanced search options, including searching by publication keywords and publications that refer to them.
The system shall recognize different spellings or forms of author names (e.g., 'D. Vermeir' and 'Dirk Vermeir') as referring to the same author.
The system shall recognize different forms of institute names (e.g., 'VUB' and 'Vrije Universiteit Brussel') as referring to the same institute.

systemFeatures.name: Search Result Viewing
systemFeatures.description: This feature allows users to view the results of their publication searches in a structured and organized manner. It provides options to order the results by various criteria and to view detailed information for individual or multiple publications. This enhances the user's ability to quickly assess relevance and gather necessary information about specific publications. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: The system completes a search query. Response: The system displays a list of matching publications.
Stimulus: User selects an ordering criterion (e.g., title, author, year). Response: The system reorders the search results accordingly.
Stimulus: User selects a single publication from the results. Response: The system displays detailed information for the selected publication.
Stimulus: User selects multiple publications from the results. Response: The system displays detailed information for the selected multiple publications.
functionalRequirements: The system shall display search results in a clear and organized list.
The system shall allow users to order search results by title, author(s), and year of publication.
The system shall provide a mechanism to view detailed information for a single selected publication.
The system shall provide a mechanism to view detailed information for multiple selected publications simultaneously.

systemFeatures.name: Publication Download
systemFeatures.description: The **vub** system enables users to download single or multiple publications from search results. This feature is crucial for academic users who need offline access to research materials, ensuring that relevant content is readily available for further study and reference. It supports various export formats to accommodate diverse user needs. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User selects one or more publications from the search results. Response: The system presents options to download or export the selected publications.
Stimulus: User selects to download a single publication. Response: The system initiates the download of the specified publication file (e.g., PDF, PS).
Stimulus: User selects to download multiple publications. Response: The system initiates the download of multiple publication files, potentially as a compressed archive.
functionalRequirements: The system shall allow users to select single publications for download from search results.
The system shall allow users to select multiple publications for download from search results.
The system shall provide a mechanism to initiate the download of selected publication files.
The system shall support exporting publications to HTML, PDF, and PS formats for download.

systemFeatures.name: Publication Reference Consultation
systemFeatures.description: This feature allows users to consult the references cited within individual publications or across multiple selected publications. It enables users to delve deeper into the academic lineage of a work, facilitating comprehensive research and understanding of interconnected scholarly articles. This is vital for academic integrity and thorough literature reviews. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User selects a publication and chooses to view its references. Response: The system displays a list of references cited within that publication.
Stimulus: User selects multiple publications and chooses to view their combined references. Response: The system displays a consolidated list of references from the selected publications.
Stimulus: User selects a specific reference from the list. Response: The system displays detailed information about that particular reference.
functionalRequirements: The system shall display a list of references cited within a single selected publication.
The system shall display a consolidated list of references from multiple selected publications.
The system shall provide a mechanism to view detailed information for each individual reference.

systemFeatures.name: Full Text Display
systemFeatures.description: The **vub** system provides the capability to display the full text of a publication directly within the interface, provided the complete text or abstract is available. This feature significantly enhances user experience by allowing immediate content review without requiring a download, streamlining the research process. It supports direct viewing for various document types. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User selects an option to view the full text of a publication. Response: The system renders the full text of the publication within the web interface.
functionalRequirements: The system shall display the full text of a publication within the user interface.
The system shall support the display of PDF and Postscript publication formats.
The system shall ensure that the full text is only displayed if the user has appropriate access rights.

systemFeatures.name: Reference Export
systemFeatures.description: This feature allows users to export the references cited within a publication into standardized bibliographic formats such as **BIB** (BibTeX) and **RIS**. This is crucial for academic researchers who need to integrate cited works into their own research papers and reference management software, promoting interoperability and efficiency in citation management. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User selects a publication and chooses to export its references. Response: The system presents options for export formats (e.g., BIB, RIS).
Stimulus: User selects 'BIB format'. Response: The system generates and downloads a file containing the references in BibTeX format.
Stimulus: User selects 'RIS format'. Response: The system generates and downloads a file containing the references in RIS format.
functionalRequirements: The system shall provide an option to export references cited in a publication.
The system shall support exporting references in **BIB** (BibTeX) format.
The system shall support exporting references in **RIS** format.

systemFeatures.name: Author Information Viewing
systemFeatures.description: The **vub** system allows users to view detailed information about authors. This feature provides context about the researchers contributing to the publication database, including their affiliations and other relevant details, which can aid in tracking research networks and expertise. Priority: Medium.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User selects an author's name from a publication's details or search results. Response: The system displays detailed information about the selected author.
functionalRequirements: The system shall display detailed information about authors, including name, first name, and institute.
The system shall provide a mechanism to access author details from publication views.

systemFeatures.name: Personal Data Editing
systemFeatures.description: Members and higher-privileged users can edit their personal account information, such as email address and password, and request account upgrades. This feature empowers users to maintain accurate profiles and to seek elevated roles within the system, ensuring personal data integrity and role progression. Priority: Medium.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: A logged-in Member navigates to 'Change Account Details'. Response: The system displays editable personal account information.
Stimulus: User modifies personal data (e.g., email address) and submits changes. Response: The system updates the user's account information and confirms the changes.
Stimulus: User requests an upgrade of their account. Response: The system sends an upgrade request to an Administrator.
functionalRequirements: The system shall allow logged-in Members to edit their email address.
The system shall allow logged-in Members to edit their password (see Password Management).
The system shall provide a mechanism for Members to request an account upgrade.

systemFeatures.name: Publication Upload
systemFeatures.description: Publishers can upload publications (PDF, PS files) to the **vub** system, typically in **BibTeX** or **RIS** formats. The system will automatically extract bibliographical information from these files, with provisions for manual correction by the publisher. This is a core feature for populating the publication database. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: A logged-in Publisher selects 'Upload Publication'. Response: The system displays a publication upload form.
Stimulus: Publisher uploads a PDF or PS file in BibTeX or RIS format. Response: The system attempts to automatically retrieve bibliographical information.
Stimulus: The system retrieves bibliographical information. Response: The system presents the extracted data for the Publisher to review and manually correct if necessary.
Stimulus: Publisher confirms the bibliographical data. Response: The system saves the publication and its metadata to the database.
functionalRequirements: The system shall allow Publishers to upload publication files in PDF and Postscript formats.
The system shall support publication uploads using **BibTeX** and **RIS** data formats.
The system shall automatically extract bibliographical information (author, title, etc.) from uploaded publication texts or abstracts.
The system shall provide an interface for Publishers to manually correct or supplement automatically extracted bibliographical information.
The system shall allow Publishers to specify that different author name formats refer to the same author.
The system shall allow Publishers to specify that different institute name formats refer to the same institute.

systemFeatures.name: Personal Publication Management
systemFeatures.description: Publishers are granted access to view and edit only the publications they have personally uploaded to the **vub** system. This feature ensures that users can maintain and update their contributions, taking responsibility for the accuracy of their submitted work. It provides a personalized content management experience within their designated scope. Priority: Medium.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: A logged-in Publisher selects 'Access My Publications'. Response: The system displays a list of publications uploaded by that Publisher.
Stimulus: Publisher selects one of their own publications for editing. Response: The system displays the editable details of that publication.
Stimulus: Publisher modifies the publication details and submits. Response: The system updates the publication information in the database and confirms the change.
functionalRequirements: The system shall provide Publishers with access to a list of publications they have personally uploaded.
The system shall allow Publishers to edit the bibliographical details of their own uploaded publications.

systemFeatures.name: Bulk Publication Upload
systemFeatures.description: This feature allows Publishers to import multiple publications or abstracts simultaneously, typically through **BibTeX** files. This significantly streamlines the process of adding large volumes of content, improving efficiency for users with extensive publication lists. It leverages the same automatic data extraction and manual correction mechanisms as single uploads. Priority: Medium.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: A logged-in Publisher selects 'Bulk Uploading'. Response: The system displays a form for uploading multiple publication files or a batch file.

Stimulus: Publisher uploads a **BibTeX** or **RIS** file containing multiple publication entries. Response: The system processes each entry, attempting automatic bibliographical information retrieval.
Stimulus: The system processes bulk entries. Response: The system provides a summary of uploaded publications, highlighting any entries requiring manual correction.
functionalRequirements: The system shall allow Publishers to upload multiple publications or abstracts concurrently.
The system shall support bulk uploads using **BibTeX** and **RIS** file formats.
The system shall process each entry in a bulk upload, applying automatic bibliographical data extraction.
The system shall provide feedback on the status of a bulk upload, including any errors or items requiring manual review.

systemFeatures.name: User Search and Management (Moderator)
systemFeatures.description: Moderators can search for users within their department, manage user levels (upgrading Members to Publishers, or downgrading Publishers to Members), and manage group permissions. This feature is crucial for departmental oversight and ensures appropriate access control within specific academic units. It allows for granular control over user roles and group affiliations. Priority: Medium.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: A logged-in Moderator navigates to a 'Manage Users' section. Response: The system displays an interface to search for users.
Stimulus: Moderator searches for a user within their department. Response: The system displays matching user profiles.
Stimulus: Moderator selects a user and chooses to change their level. Response: The system presents options to upgrade a Member to Publisher or downgrade a Publisher to Member (within department).
Stimulus: Moderator selects to manage group permissions. Response: The system displays permissions for groups under the Moderator's jurisdiction.
functionalRequirements: The system shall allow Moderators to search for user accounts within their assigned department.
The system shall allow Moderators to upgrade Members to Publishers within their department.
The system shall allow Moderators to downgrade Publishers to Members within their department.
The system shall allow Moderators to manage group permissions for groups under their jurisdiction.

systemFeatures.name: Author Information Management (Moderator)
systemFeatures.description: Moderators possess the authority to edit author information within the **vub** system. This feature enables centralized management of author profiles, ensuring accuracy and consistency across the entire publication database. It is critical for maintaining the quality and integrity of bibliographical metadata. Priority: Medium.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: A logged-in Moderator selects to 'Edit Author(s)'. Response: The system displays an interface for searching and editing author details.
Stimulus: Moderator searches for an author and selects their profile. Response: The system displays editable author information.
Stimulus: Moderator modifies author details (e.g., affiliation, name disambiguation) and submits. Response: The system updates the author information and applies changes across relevant publications.
functionalRequirements: The system shall allow Moderators to search for and view author information.
The system shall allow Moderators to edit the personal and institutional details associated with authors.
The system shall ensure changes to author information are reflected consistently across all linked publications.

systemFeatures.name: Departmental Publication Management (Moderator)
systemFeatures.description: Moderators can edit or overwrite bibliographical details for any publication owned by their connected department or group within the **vub** system. This feature provides departmental custodians with the necessary control to maintain the accuracy and quality of their unit's research output, ensuring data consistency and adherence to departmental standards. Priority: Medium.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: A logged-in Moderator navigates to 'Edit Departmental Publication(s)'. Response: The system displays a list of publications associated with their department/group.
Stimulus: Moderator selects a departmental publication for editing. Response: The system displays editable bibliographical details for that publication.
Stimulus: Moderator modifies details (or uploads an overwriting file) and submits. Response: The system updates or replaces the publication's bibliographical information and confirms the change.
functionalRequirements: The system shall allow Moderators to access and view publications associated with their assigned department or group.
The system shall allow Moderators to edit the bibliographical details of departmental publications.
The system shall allow Moderators to overwrite existing departmental publication records.

systemFeatures.name: Administrator Control Panel Access
systemFeatures.description: Administrators are granted exclusive access to a comprehensive Control Panel within the **vub** system. This panel serves as the central hub for managing technical aspects of the PMS, including advanced user account management, system configuration settings, and group administration. The feature is vital for the overarching maintenance and strategic management of the system. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: A logged-in Administrator selects 'Access Control Panel'. Response: The system displays the Administrator Control Panel interface.
Stimulus: Administrator navigates within the Control Panel. Response: The system presents various administrative tools and configuration options.
functionalRequirements: The system shall provide a dedicated Control Panel interface accessible only to Administrators.
The system shall enable Administrators to manage user accounts (upgrade, downgrade, delete).
The system shall enable Administrators to create and delete user accounts.
The system shall enable Administrators to create and delete groups.
The system shall enable Administrators to edit system configuration settings.

systemFeatures.name: User Creation and Deletion (Administrator)
systemFeatures.description: Administrators have the privilege to create and delete any user account within the **vub** system. This capability is essential for full control over user lifecycle management, enabling administrators to onboard new personnel and remove departing users efficiently. It underpins the security and integrity of the user base. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: A logged-in Administrator selects 'Create User' from the Control Panel. Response: The system displays a user creation form.
Stimulus: Administrator fills in new user details and submits. Response: The system creates a new user account with specified role and credentials.
Stimulus: Administrator selects 'Delete User' for an existing account. Response: The system prompts for confirmation before permanently deleting the user account and associated data.
functionalRequirements: The system shall allow Administrators to create new user accounts with specified roles.
The system shall allow Administrators to delete any existing user account from the system.
The system shall prompt for confirmation before permanently deleting a user account.

systemFeatures.name: Group Creation and Deletion (Administrator)
systemFeatures.description: Administrators have the sole authority to create and delete any group within the **vub** system. This feature is fundamental for organizing users into logical departmental or project-based units and managing their collective permissions. It provides structural flexibility for user and content management. Priority: Medium.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: A logged-in Administrator selects 'Create Group' from the Control Panel. Response: The system displays a group creation form.
Stimulus: Administrator specifies group name and optional initial permissions. Response: The system creates a new group record.
Stimulus: Administrator selects 'Delete Group' for an existing group. Response: The system prompts for confirmation before permanently deleting the group and disassociating members.
functionalRequirements: The system shall allow Administrators to create new user groups.
The system shall allow Administrators to delete any existing user group.
The system shall prompt for confirmation before permanently deleting a group.

systemFeatures.name: Comprehensive User Level Management (Administrator)
systemFeatures.description: Administrators can modify the user level (role) of any user in the **vub** system to any other available level. This provides ultimate flexibility and control over user permissions, allowing for dynamic adjustments to access rights across the entire system. It ensures that system administrators can adapt user roles as organizational needs evolve. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: A logged-in Administrator selects 'Manage User Level' for a specific user from the Control Panel. Response: The system displays available user roles for assignment.
Stimulus: Administrator selects a new user level and confirms. Response: The system updates the user's role and applies the corresponding permissions.
functionalRequirements: The system shall allow Administrators to change the user level (role) of any user to any other defined user level.

systemFeatures.name: System Configuration Editing (Administrator)
systemFeatures.description: Administrators can edit various system configuration settings for the **vub** system. This feature provides direct control over operational parameters, enabling fine-tuning of system behavior, such as default search settings, upload limits, or other globally impactful parameters. It ensures the system can be adapted and optimized without requiring code changes. Priority: Medium.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: A logged-in Administrator navigates to 'Edit System Configuration Settings' in the Control Panel. Response: The system displays editable configuration parameters.
Stimulus: Administrator modifies a configuration setting and submits. Response: The system saves the updated configuration and applies changes.
functionalRequirements: The system shall provide an interface for Administrators to edit system configuration settings.
The system shall validate configuration changes to ensure operational integrity.
The system shall apply updated configuration settings dynamically or upon a scheduled restart.

systemFeatures.name: Global Publication Management (Administrator)
systemFeatures.description: Administrators have the ability to change or overwrite the bibliographical details of any publication within the entire **vub** system. This ultimate control ensures data consistency, error correction, and adherence to top-level content standards across all publications. It is a critical feature for maintaining the integrity and quality of the academic repository. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: A logged-in Administrator selects 'Edit All Publication(s)' from the Control Panel. Response: The system displays a search and editing interface for all publications.
Stimulus: Administrator selects any publication for modification. Response: The system displays editable bibliographical details for that publication.
Stimulus: Administrator modifies details or uploads an overwriting file and submits. Response: The system updates or replaces the publication's bibliographical information and confirms the change.
functionalRequirements: The system shall allow Administrators to view and search all publications in the system.
The system shall allow Administrators to edit the bibliographical details of any publication.
The system shall allow Administrators to overwrite any existing publication record.

performanceRequirements: The **vub** system shall demonstrate high performance and responsiveness to ensure a productive user experience. Search queries, especially keyword-based and advanced searches, shall return results within 3 seconds for a database containing up to 100,000 publications. The loading time for any typical web page, including search results and publication details, shall not exceed 2 seconds under normal load (e.g., 50 concurrent users). Publication download times shall be optimized to initiate within 1 second of user request, with actual transfer rates depending on network bandwidth and file size. Bulk upload processing shall indicate progress and complete processing of 100 publications within 5 minutes, including automatic data extraction.
safetyRequirements: The **vub** system shall incorporate robust mechanisms to prevent data loss and ensure system integrity. This includes regular automated backups of the entire **MySQL** database, including publication files, to an off-site location, performed daily. In the event of system failure, the Recovery Time Objective (RTO) shall be less than 4 hours, and the Recovery Point Objective (RPO) shall be less than 24 hours. Input validation shall be rigorously applied to all user-submitted data to prevent injection attacks and ensure data consistency. Error handling routines shall gracefully manage unexpected conditions, preventing crashes and data corruption, logging all critical errors for administrative review.
securityRequirements: The **vub** system shall implement comprehensive security measures to protect user data and publication content. User authentication shall employ secure password hashing algorithms and require strong password policies, preventing unauthorized access to accounts. Authorization mechanisms shall strictly enforce role-based access control (RBAC), ensuring users can only perform actions and access data commensurate with their assigned roles (Guest, VUB-Network User, Member, Publisher, Moderator, Administrator). Accessing the system from outside the VUB-network shall only be possible for logged-in users. All communication over **HTTP** shall be secured using **HTTPS/TLS** (Transport Layer Security) for external access. Administrative access via CLI shall require strong authentication and be restricted to trusted network segments. The system shall also prevent cross-site scripting (XSS) and cross-site request forgery (CSRF) vulnerabilities.
softwareQualityAttributes: **Reliability**: The **vub** system shall maintain an uptime of 99.5% during operational hours (8 AM to 6 PM, Monday-Friday), minimizing unscheduled downtime. The system shall recover gracefully from anticipated errors without requiring manual intervention. All critical functions, such as login, search, and upload, shall consistently execute correctly under varying load conditions.

**Availability**: The **vub** system shall be available for use by authenticated users 24 hours a day, 7 days a week, with planned maintenance windows communicated in advance. VUB-Network Users and Guests with appropriate privileges shall also have continuous access to their respective functionalities. Redundant server configurations and automated failover mechanisms will be implemented to enhance availability.

**Maintainability**: The **vub** system shall be designed with a modular architecture and well-documented **Ruby** code (using **RDoc**), enabling developers to easily understand, modify, and extend the codebase. Code changes shall be traceable through **Git** version control, facilitating efficient debugging and feature additions. The average time to resolve critical bugs shall not exceed 24 hours.

**Portability**: The **vub** system shall be portable across different **Linux** distributions, specifically ensuring compatibility with various versions of the **Wilma** server environment. The application's reliance on standard **Ruby** and **MySQL** components, along with adherence to **W3C XHTML** standards, will facilitate migration to other compatible server infrastructure if required in the future without significant re-engineering efforts.

**Usability**: The **vub** system's web interface shall be intuitive and user-friendly, allowing users to efficiently complete tasks with minimal training. Navigation paths shall be consistent, and feedback mechanisms shall clearly communicate system status and errors. The design will adhere to the 'least clicks' principle while maintaining structural clarity.

**Testability**: The **vub** system shall be designed to facilitate comprehensive testing at unit, integration, and system levels. Components shall be independently testable, and interfaces shall be clearly defined to support automated testing frameworks. Requirements shall be precise and verifiable, allowing for unambiguous test case development.
businessRules: The **vub** system shall enforce a strict hierarchy of user roles and permissions, ensuring that access to functionalities and data is always aligned with a user's assigned role.
All publication uploads by Publishers require review by the system's automatic extraction mechanism and manual confirmation before final submission.
Moderators can only manage users and publications within their specific departmental jurisdiction.
Administrator actions, including user and group creation/deletion and global publication edits, shall be logged for audit purposes.
The system must also adhere to all relevant VUB institutional policies regarding data privacy, intellectual property, and acceptable use of IT resources.

otherRequirements: The **vub** system has identified several optional functions that represent potential future enhancements. These include extending support to additional document formats beyond PDF and Postscript, such as **MS Word**. Future iterations may also provide a feature to display lists of documents that refer to a given publication on the search results page. The system aims to automatically recognize variant spellings of author and institute names (e.g., 'D. Vermeir' and 'Dirk Vermeir', 'VUB' and 'Vrije Universiteit Brussel') for improved data consistency. Another potential expansion is the capability for the PMS to automatically retrieve full texts via URLs from uploaded **BibTeX** files containing only abstracts and referring URL fields. Finally, the modular design intends to support future integration and management of audio and video files, extending the system beyond text-based publications. These optional features will be considered for implementation if sufficient time and resources become available after critical and sufficient requirements are met.

flowchartDiagram.syntaxExplanation: A flowchart illustrates a process, workflow, or algorithm, showing the steps as boxes of various kinds, and their order by connecting them with arrows. The diagram starts with the 'flowchart' keyword, followed by the direction (TD for top-down, LR for left-right). Nodes are defined by an ID and a label (quoted if it contains spaces or special characters). Arrows connect nodes to show the flow.
flowchartDiagram.code:
flowchart TD
    Start("User Initiates Access") --> VUBNet{"Connected to VUB-Network?"}
    VUBNet -- "Yes" --> VUBUserFlow["VUB-Network User Flow"]
    VUBNet -- "No" --> GuestFlow["Guest User Flow"]

    GuestFlow --> ReqLogin{"Requires Login?"}
    ReqLogin -- "Yes" --> Login["Login Page"]
    ReqLogin -- "No" --> Register["Register Account"]
    Register --> EmailVal["Email Validation"]
    EmailVal --> Login
    Login --> Creds["Enter Credentials"]
    Creds --> Auth{"Authenticate?"}
    Auth -- "Yes" --> MemberDash["Member Dashboard"]
    Auth -- "No" --> LoginFail["Login Failed"]
    LoginFail --> Login
    Login --> ForgotPass["Forgot Password"]
    ForgotPass --> EmailReset["Email Password Reset"]
    EmailReset --> Login

    VUBUserFlow --> VUBUserAuth{"Authenticated?"}
    VUBUserAuth -- "Yes" --> MemberDash
    VUBUserAuth -- "No" --> SearchOrLogin["Search or Login Option"]
    SearchOrLogin -- "Search" --> VUBGuestSearch["VUB-Network Guest Search"]
    SearchOrLogin -- "Login" --> Creds

    MemberDash --> SearchPub["Search Publications"]
    MemberDash --> ManageAcc["Manage Account"]
    VUBGuestSearch --> ViewResults["View Search Results"]
    SearchPub --> ViewResults
    ViewResults --> DownloadPub["Download Publication"]
    ViewResults --> ViewDetails["View Publication Details"]
    DownloadPub --> End("End Process")
    ViewDetails --> End
    ManageAcc --> End
    MemberDash -- "Logout" --> Start
flowchartDiagram.caption: vub User Authentication and Initial Access Flowchart

sequenceDiagram.syntaxExplanation: A sequence diagram shows the interaction between objects in a time-ordered sequence. Participants are declared with 'participant <ID> as <Alias>'. Messages are sent between participants using various arrow types (e.g., '->>', '-->>'). Activations can be explicitly shown with 'activate' and 'deactivate' or implicitly with '->>+' and '-->>-'. Conditional logic can be represented using 'alt' blocks.
sequenceDiagram.code:
sequenceDiagram
    participant U as User
    participant WS as WebServer
    participant PA as PMSApp
    participant DB as Database

    U->>WS: Request Login Page
    WS->>U: Display Login Form
    U->>WS: Submit Credentials (username, password)
    WS->>+PA: Authenticate User(username, password)
    PA->>DB: Query User(username)
    DB-->>PA: Return User Record
    PA->>DB: Verify Password
    alt Successful Authentication
        PA-->>-WS: Authentication Success (User Role)
        WS->>U: Redirect to Dashboard
        U->>WS: Request Search Page
        WS->>U: Display Search Form
        U->>WS: Submit Search Query (keywords)
        WS->>+PA: Search Publications(keywords, User Role)
        PA->>DB: Query Publications(keywords, access_level)
        DB-->>PA: Return Matching Publications
        PA-->>-WS: Return Search Results
        WS->>U: Display Search Results
        U->>WS: Select Publication for Details
        WS->>+PA: Get Publication Details(pub_id, User Role)
        PA->>DB: Query Publication(pub_id)
        DB-->>PA: Return Publication Data
        PA-->>-WS: Return Publication Details
        WS->>U: Display Publication Details
    else Failed Authentication
        PA-->>-WS: Authentication Failed
        WS->>U: Display Login Error
    end
sequenceDiagram.caption: vub User Login and Publication Search Sequence

entityRelationshipDiagram.syntaxExplanation: An Entity Relationship Diagram (ERD) visualizes the relationships between different entities (tables) in a database. Entities are declared with their name, followed by a block defining attributes with their data type and key constraints (PK for Primary Key, FK for Foreign Key, UK for Unique Key). Relationships between entities are defined using specific cardinality symbols (e.g., '||--o{' for one-to-many, optional on the 'many' side) and must include a quoted label describing the relationship.
entityRelationshipDiagram.code:
erDiagram
    USER ||--o{ PUBLICATION : "uploads"
    USER ||--|| ROLE : "has"
    USER ||--o{ USER_GROUP : "is member of"
    USER }|--o| DEPARTMENT : "belongs to"
    PUBLICATION }|--o{ PUBLICATION_AUTHOR : "includes"
    AUTHOR ||--o{ PUBLICATION_AUTHOR : "writes"
    GROUP ||--o{ USER_GROUP : "contains"
    DEPARTMENT ||--o{ GROUP : "manages"

    USER {
        int id PK
        string username UK
        string email UK
        string password
        string first_name
        string last_name
        int role_id FK
        int department_id FK
    }
    ROLE {
        int id PK
        string name UK
        int access_level
    }
    DEPARTMENT {
        int id PK
        string name UK
    }
    PUBLICATION {
        int id PK
        string title
        string abstract
        int publication_year
        string file_path
        string format
        datetime upload_date
        int uploader_user_id FK
    }
    AUTHOR {
        int id PK
        string first_name
        string last_name
        string institute
    }
    PUBLICATION_AUTHOR {
        int publication_id PK, FK
        int author_id PK, FK
        int author_order
    }
    GROUP {
        int id PK
        string name UK
        int department_id FK
    }
    USER_GROUP {
        int user_id PK, FK
        int group_id PK, FK
    }
entityRelationshipDiagram.caption: vub Core Data Model for Publications Management

revisionHistory.author: SRA System

introduction.purpose: The primary objective of the **nlm** Digital Repository is to establish a robust infrastructure for the National Library of Medicine to acquire, organize, preserve, and provide long-term access to published records in biomedical sciences and health professions, irrespective of their original format. This system is crucial for managing diverse digital collections, accommodating born-digital materials, and supporting the Library's commitment to digital preservation. It addresses the technological challenges distinct from analog preservation, ensuring ongoing accessibility for the designated community.
introduction.documentConventions: This Software Requirements Specification adheres to the guidelines established by IEEE Standard 830-1998, 'IEEE Recommended Practice for Software Requirements Specifications'. All requirements are articulated with a unique identifier prefix 'N-REQ-', ensuring traceability and clear referencing throughout the document. Key terms and acronyms are defined in the accompanying Glossary, and Mermaid syntax is utilized for visual models in the Appendices to enhance clarity and understanding of system architecture and processes.
introduction.intendedAudience: This document is intended for a diverse audience, including NLM Digital Repository Working Group members, system architects, software developers, quality assurance engineers, project managers, and future maintainers of the **nlm** Digital Repository. It also serves as a critical reference for NLM staff involved in content management, policy formulation, and system administration. Understanding its content is essential for all stakeholders engaged in the development, deployment, and operational phases of the repository.
introduction.productScope: The **nlm** Digital Repository encompasses one or more electronic storage systems designed to store, preserve, and provide access to digitized and born-digital objects acquired or created by the National Library of Medicine. This includes functionalities for content ingest, metadata management, format migration, preservation planning, and controlled access mechanisms. The scope is specifically limited to collection materials under Library Operations, explicitly excluding resources not within this collecting responsibility, such as the UMLS or Visible Human projects, ensuring a focused approach to NLM's core archival mission.
references: IEEE 830-1998 IEEE Recommended Practice for Software Requirements Specifications.
ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018 Systems and software engineering — Life cycle processes — Requirements engineering.
Association of Research Libraries. SPEC Kit 292: Institutional Repositories (2006).
Reference Model for an Open Archival Information System (OAIS) (2002).
Coyle, Karen. Rights in the PREMIS Data Model: A Report for the Library of Congress (2006).
Cornell University Library. Digital Preservation Management: Implementing Short-term Strategies for Long-term Problems (2004).

overallDescription.productPerspective: The **nlm** Digital Repository is conceived as a new, self-contained product designed to address the increasing need for digital preservation and access within the National Library of Medicine. It is a critical infrastructure component, building upon lessons learned from existing NLM digital initiatives like **PubMedCentral**, **Bookshelf**, and **NIH Videocasts**, but specifically targeting content not covered by these platforms. The system's design is heavily influenced by the Open Archival Information System (**OAIS**) Reference Model, providing a robust conceptual framework for its functional entities and interfaces. It aims to integrate with existing NLM systems, particularly the Integrated Library System (**ILS**), to leverage existing bibliographic data and minimize redundant data entry.
productFunctions: Ingest: Receive, quality assure, and prepare digital content and metadata for archival storage.
Archival Storage: Securely store, manage, and preserve digital objects and their associated information.
Data Management: Administer databases, support complex queries, and generate reports on repository holdings.
Administration: Oversee submission agreements, manage system configurations, and establish policies.
Preservation Planning: Monitor technological changes and designated communities, developing strategies to ensure long-term accessibility.
Access: Coordinate and deliver digital objects and their descriptive information to end-users.

userClassesAndCharacteristics.characteristics: These end-users require off-site Internet access and potentially on-site access to repository materials. They will search metadata and full-text content and view materials in common formats such as **PDF**, **JPEG**, and **XML**. Access may be restricted based on licensing, embargo periods, copyright, or geographic location, requiring different access rights.

These end-users require repository materials for research and reference, with access levels potentially differing from the general public. Similar to the public, they will search and view content, but may have expanded access rights to certain materials, including location-level access via specific workstations in areas like the NLM Reading Room due to subscription or purchase licenses.

Staff in this category are responsible for creating, reviewing, or editing descriptive, administrative, and structural metadata associated with digital objects. They possess the ability to view materials and modify metadata without altering the original digital object itself. Multiple levels of viewing and editing privileges will be assigned based on their role and metadata class, ensuring controlled contributions to the repository's descriptive information.

This highly restricted group of NLM staff works directly with the digital objects, performing critical tasks such as adding new files, conducting quality checks on digital assets, manipulating images, and executing format conversions and migrations (e.g., **TIFF** to **PDF**, **TIFF** to **JPEG2000**). They are also responsible for investigating and resolving system-level problems related to object integrity and accessibility, necessitating advanced technical permissions.

This user class holds the most comprehensive and restrictive access rights to the **nlm** Digital Repository, encompassing full system management, development, and user rights assignment. These staff members are responsible for the overall operational integrity, security, and evolution of the repository, including deep technical configurations, system upgrades, and responding to security incidents. Their actions directly impact the stability and availability of the entire digital archive.

overallDescription.operatingEnvironment: The **nlm** Digital Repository will operate within the National Institutes of Health (**NIH**) IT infrastructure, adhering strictly to **HHS/NIH** security standards and policies. The system is expected to reside on robust server hardware, potentially leveraging both commercial and open-source software solutions for its core functionalities and storage management. It must seamlessly integrate with the NIH off-site remote backup facility (**NCCS**), supporting both backup and parallel operational modes to ensure data resilience and disaster recovery capabilities.
designAndImplementationConstraints: The system must accommodate existing digital repositories (e.g., **PMC**, **Bookshelf**, **NIH Videocasts**) and provide interfaces or mechanisms to avoid duplication of efforts where applicable.

The system must be designed to handle a lack of universal standards for certain file types, such as e-books, and be flexible enough to store obsolete or unusual formats that may accompany important historical donations.
An interface must be provided to the NLM’s Integrated Library System (**ILS**) for the supply of bibliographic data, ensuring consistency and minimizing manual data entry.

The **nlm** Digital Repository must comply with all **HHS/NIH** security standards and policies, including those pertaining to the **NIH** off-site remote backup facility (**NCCS**), addressing both backup and parallel operational modes.
Implementation will occur in phases, with an initial evaluation and testing period for selected commercial and open-source software options before a production repository is chosen and deployed.
Budget availability for software purchase and/or additional programming support is dependent on the selection of the final solution, posing a potential financial constraint.
Availability of Library Operations systems staff to dedicate to testing and implementation of the digital repository is limited, representing a significant resource constraint.
The repository must support the use of multiple languages and non-Roman scripts for ingest, maintenance, and access of digital objects and associated metadata.
Web harvesting functionality is explicitly outside the scope of these requirements; separate software will be used for web harvesting, though the harvested data will be stored within the repository.
userDocumentation: Comprehensive User Manuals: Detailed guides for end-users on accessing, searching, and viewing materials within the **nlm** Digital Repository.
Administrator Guides: Technical documentation for NLM System Administration/Programming Staff covering installation, configuration, maintenance, and troubleshooting of the repository system.

Metadata and Content Submission Guidelines: Documentation for NLM staff (Metadata Access, Object Maintenance) and content producers/donors regarding preferred formats, quality assurance, and submission protocols.
Policy Documents: Specifications regarding data permanence levels, access restrictions, and preservation strategies for NLM staff and relevant stakeholders.
Online Help System: Integrated, context-sensitive help features within the repository's user interfaces for immediate assistance.
assumptionsAndDependencies: The NLM Collection Development Policy explicitly defines the scope of content for the **nlm** Digital Repository, dictating what materials are eligible for inclusion.
The primary goal for content conversion is preservation of intellectual content, with the preservation of original 'look and feel' to be evaluated and prioritized on a case-by-case basis.
NLM original physical assets will be retained in the collection even after their digitization and inclusion in the repository.
NLM will not be responsible for providing or maintaining specialized third-party software required by users to access specific materials (e.g., for audiovisual content or legacy applications).
The **nlm** Digital Repository will exclusively contain materials either digitized by NLM or born-digital content created or acquired directly by NLM.

A distributed custodial model will be adopted for some digital materials; while their metadata will reside in an NLM-managed system (e.g., **ILS**), the objects themselves might not be physically stored within the **nlm** Digital Repository.

At a minimum, the original normalized object, the most recent migrated version, and the next-to-the-last migrated version of any digital content will be retained. Other intermediary versions may be discarded, or in some cases, all versions may need to be retained depending on content type.
All digital objects within the repository must be assigned a unique NLM internal identifier (UID) to ensure unambiguous referencing and management.

The system design and architecture must be optimized to minimize duplicate data entry of any metadata associated with objects in the **nlm** Digital Repository, promoting efficiency and data integrity.

Master images and comprehensive descriptive, structural, and administrative metadata meeting NLM’s specifications will be created for all digital content produced from NLM analog collections intended for permanent retention.
The **nlm** Digital Repository will manage objects with varying levels of permanence; NLM will assign permanence ratings to stored objects to guide their lifecycle management.
The repository is not intended to be a 'dark archive'; objects will be generally available, though specific access may be restricted by on-site use only or embargo periods.
To the greatest extent possible, automation will be employed for the extraction of descriptive and technical metadata, reducing manual effort and improving consistency.

externalInterfaceRequirements.userInterfaces: The **nlm** Digital Repository will offer a web-based user interface accessible through standard web browsers for both end-users (General Public, NIH Staff) and NLM staff. For end-users, this interface will facilitate searching of metadata and full-text documents, and the viewing of digital objects in widely supported formats such as **PDF**, **JPEG**, and **XML**. The interface will implement role-based access controls to manage material visibility and functionality based on user authentication and designated access rights. NLM staff will interact with an enhanced interface that supports advanced administrative tasks, metadata editing, and object maintenance functions, adhering to NLM's internal GUI standards and security protocols. The user interface must provide clear error messaging and adhere to accessibility guidelines to ensure usability for all user classes.
externalInterfaceRequirements.hardwareInterfaces: The **nlm** Digital Repository will interface with standard server hardware for its computational and storage needs. This includes high-capacity network-attached storage (**NAS**) or storage area networks (**SAN**) for archival data. The system will leverage network interfaces for communication within the NLM data center and with external networks for content ingest and access delivery. It must also accommodate interfaces to an off-site remote backup facility (**NCCS**) for data replication and disaster recovery, ensuring compatibility with the required communication protocols and physical connectivity standards. Specific hardware components like tape drives or specialized media readers for ingest of non-digital SIPs might be required, with software drivers managing the interaction between the operating system and these devices.
externalInterfaceRequirements.softwareInterfaces: The **nlm** Digital Repository must interface with several internal and external software systems.
