It will interact with NLM’s Integrated Library System (**ILS**) for the exchange of bibliographic data, requiring robust **REST API** or similar data transfer mechanisms. The system will integrate with existing digital repositories such as **PubMedCentral**, **Bookshelf**, and **NIH Videocasts** to coordinate content management and avoid duplication, potentially via custom **APIs** or standardized data interchange formats. Key metadata standards like **Dublin Core**, **MARC21**, **METS**, **MODS**, and **PREMIS** will dictate schema for data exchange. Communication protocols like **OAI-PMH** for metadata harvesting, **OpenSearch** and **OpenURL** for search and linking, and **SRU/SRW** for search and retrieval will be supported, alongside traditional file transfer protocols like **FTP** for content submission. The repository must also interact with underlying operating systems and database management systems (e.g., **PostgreSQL**, **MySQL**) for data persistence and transaction management.
externalInterfaceRequirements.communicationsInterfaces: The **nlm** Digital Repository will rely on standard network communication protocols, primarily **HTTP/HTTPS** for web-based access and administrative interfaces. **FTP** or secure **SFTP** protocols will be employed for the electronic transfer of Submission Information Packages (**SIPs**) from content producers. Network communication with the NIH off-site remote backup facility (**NCCS**) will adhere to specified **NIH** internal protocols to ensure secure and efficient data replication. All sensitive communications, including user authentication and data transfers, must utilize strong encryption protocols (**TLS v1.2** or higher) in accordance with **HHS/NIH** security policies. The system must support various message formatting standards, including **XML** for metadata exchange, and ensure appropriate data transfer rates for both bulk ingest and high-demand access scenarios, including synchronization mechanisms for distributed content.

systemFeatures.name: Ingest Management
systemFeatures.description: The Ingest Management feature facilitates the controlled reception and processing of all digital and digitized materials into the **nlm** Digital Repository. This functionality provides significant user value by streamlining the acquisition workflow for NLM staff and content producers, ensuring that diverse content types, from e-journals to images, are systematically evaluated for quality, format compliance, and authenticity. Technically, it forms the crucial entry point for all archival content, impacting the integrity and long-term usability of the entire collection. This feature is fundamental to the repository's mission, ensuring a well-organized and validated digital asset base.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: A Content Provider submits a Submission Information Package (SIP) via FTP. Response: The system initiates receipt protocols, performs initial virus scanning, and confirms receipt to the Content Provider.

Stimulus: NLM Staff manually initiates an ingest process for a local media submission. Response: The system provides an interface for metadata entry and file upload, guiding staff through quality assurance checks and SIP generation.

Stimulus: The system detects a non-preferred format during quality assurance. Response: The system flags the SIP for manual review, records the non-compliance, and prompts for a decision on acceptance or rejection.
functionalRequirements: The system shall provide mechanisms for receiving Submission Information Packages (SIPs) via electronic transfer (e.g., FTP/SFTP) and physical media mounts (e.g., CD-ROM).
The system shall confirm receipt of SIPs to Content Providers, including error messages for submission failures.
The system shall perform virus checking on all submitted digital content within a SIP.
The system shall review submitted materials for compliance with specified file formats and quality standards.
The system shall allow NLM staff to manually approve or reject submitted content based on review criteria.
The system shall record all actions and decisions made concerning submitted material, including reasons for rejection.
The system shall provide feedback to Content Providers regarding rejection reasons, enabling resubmission of corrected materials.
The system shall support the acceptance and preservation of material in non-preferred or non-standard formats, subject to NLM policy.
The system shall manage multiple ingest streams, including e-journals, e-books, harvested web sites, manuscripts, videos, images, and sound materials.
The system shall support both bulk ingest of materials and ingest of individual objects.
The system shall coordinate updates to Archival Storage and Data Management components upon successful ingestion.
The system shall integrate with NLM’s Integrated Library System (ILS) for existing bibliographic data where applicable, minimizing duplicate data entry.

systemFeatures.name: Archival Storage Management
systemFeatures.description: Archival Storage Management ensures the secure and persistent storage of digital objects within the **nlm** Digital Repository, along with their associated metadata. This feature offers critical user value by safeguarding NLM's invaluable digital assets against loss, degradation, and obsolescence, guaranteeing long-term accessibility and integrity. From a technical standpoint, it provides the core infrastructure for data resilience, including mechanisms for storage hierarchy management, media replacement, and robust error checking. Its design emphasizes disaster recovery and the reliable provision of data, making it an indispensable component for fulfilling NLM's preservation mandate.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: The Archival Storage component receives a newly generated Archival Information Package (AIP). Response: The system securely stores the AIP within the designated storage hierarchy, replicating it for redundancy.

Stimulus: A storage medium approaches its end-of-life or fails a routine integrity check. Response: The system initiates a media replacement procedure, migrating content to new media without interruption of service.

Stimulus: A daily error checking routine identifies data corruption in a stored digital object. Response: The system logs the error, attempts self-correction if possible, and triggers an alert for NLM staff to intervene and restore from a valid backup.
functionalRequirements: The system shall receive Archival Information Packages (AIPs) from the Ingest function and securely store them.
The system shall manage a hierarchical storage system, optimizing for access frequency and preservation requirements.
The system shall implement policies for data replication and redundancy across multiple storage locations.
The system shall support the replacement of storage media without data loss or significant service interruption.
The system shall perform regular error checking and data integrity validation on all stored digital objects.
The system shall provide disaster recovery capabilities to restore digital objects and metadata in case of catastrophic failure.
The system shall provide stored digital objects and associated data to the Access function upon request.
The system shall maintain audit trails for all data movement and integrity checks within the archival storage.

systemFeatures.name: Data Management and Reporting
systemFeatures.description: The Data Management and Reporting feature enables efficient administration of the repository's underlying database, supporting sophisticated queries and comprehensive report generation. This delivers significant value to NLM staff by providing deep insights into the repository's holdings, operational status, and compliance with preservation policies, thereby empowering informed decision-making. Technically, it underpins all search, retrieval, and administrative functionalities, ensuring that metadata and system information are accurately maintained, easily accessible, and can be analyzed for various strategic and operational purposes. Its robustness is key to the overall usability and accountability of the **nlm** Digital Repository.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: An NLM Administrator initiates a query to retrieve all digital objects with a 'High' permanence rating ingested within the last year. Response: The system executes the query against the database and presents a filtered list of digital objects meeting the criteria.

Stimulus: An NLM staff member requests a report on storage utilization by format type. Response: The system generates a formatted report detailing storage consumption, broken down by various digital object formats.

Stimulus: The Ingest function successfully generates new descriptive metadata for a digital object. Response: The system receives the metadata update and securely commits it to the appropriate database tables, ensuring data consistency.
functionalRequirements: The system shall administer the repository database, including schema management, indexing, and performance tuning.
The system shall support complex queries on descriptive, administrative, and structural metadata, allowing for filtering and sorting.
The system shall generate customizable reports on repository holdings, ingest statistics, access patterns, and preservation status.
The system shall receive and process database updates from other functional areas, such as Ingest and Administration.
The system shall ensure data integrity and consistency across all database operations.
The system shall provide interfaces for authorized NLM staff to perform database maintenance and troubleshoot data issues.
The system shall log all significant database access and modification events for auditing purposes.
The system shall export query results and generated reports in various standard formats (e.g., CSV, XML, PDF).

systemFeatures.name: Repository Administration
systemFeatures.description: Repository Administration encompasses the critical functions necessary for the strategic oversight and operational governance of the **nlm** Digital Repository. This feature offers high value by enabling NLM staff to negotiate submission agreements, manage system configurations, establish policies, and audit content, ensuring the repository operates in alignment with NLM's mission and regulatory requirements. Technically, it provides the control plane for the entire system, allowing for dynamic adaptation to evolving needs, precise control over content lifecycle, and rigorous accountability through auditing. Its robust implementation is vital for the long-term sustainability and trustworthiness of the digital archive.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: An NLM Administrator defines a new ingest policy regarding acceptable file formats. Response: The system updates the ingest configuration, and new submissions are validated against the updated policy.

Stimulus: An NLM staff member audits a specific submission to verify compliance with the submission agreement. Response: The system provides detailed records of the submission's processing, quality assurance, and metadata generation, flagging any discrepancies.

Stimulus: NLM Management approves a request for an archival information update, such as a metadata correction or content migration. Response: The system processes the update, applies it to the relevant digital objects and metadata, and records the change in the audit log.
functionalRequirements: The system shall provide tools for NLM staff to negotiate and record submission agreements with content producers and donors.
The system shall allow NLM System Administration staff to manage and configure system parameters, including ingest policies, storage policies, and access controls.
The system shall facilitate the update of archival information, including metadata corrections, format migrations, and content versioning.
The system shall provide mechanisms for establishing and applying new standards and policies/procedures for the repository's operation.
The system shall enable NLM staff to audit individual submissions and system activities for compliance and quality assurance.
The system shall activate and manage requests for specific administrative actions, such as content removal or metadata re-indexing.
The system shall maintain a comprehensive audit log of all administrative actions and policy changes.
The system shall support role-based access control for administrative functions, ensuring only authorized personnel can perform specific tasks.

systemFeatures.name: Preservation Planning
systemFeatures.description: The Preservation Planning feature is dedicated to the proactive management of digital object longevity within the **nlm** Digital Repository, addressing the challenges of technological obsolescence and format degradation. It provides immense user value by ensuring that the intellectual content of NLM's digital collection remains accessible and usable for future generations, thereby fulfilling NLM's long-term preservation mandate. From a technical perspective, this involves continuous monitoring of technology trends, evaluating content in the archive, and developing strategic migration plans, which are crucial for maintaining the repository's utility and relevance over extended periods. This feature is a cornerstone of digital stewardship, guaranteeing the enduring value of the collected materials.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: The system detects that a widely used digital format is becoming obsolete based on external technology monitoring. Response: The system flags all digital objects in that format for review by NLM staff and recommends potential migration strategies.

Stimulus: NLM staff initiates a review of the designated community's technology environment. Response: The system provides tools to assess common user software and hardware, informing decisions about preferred dissemination formats.

Stimulus: A new preservation standard is published and evaluated by NLM staff. Response: The system provides an interface for NLM staff to define and implement new preservation policies and procedures based on the adopted standard.
functionalRequirements: The system shall provide tools to monitor the technological environment relevant to digital preservation, including file formats, storage media, and software platforms.
The system shall support monitoring the designated community's technological capabilities and access requirements.
The system shall assist in developing and documenting preservation strategies, including format migration plans and emulation approaches.
The system shall provide functionality for developing packaging designs for Archival Information Packages (AIPs) and Dissemination Information Packages (DIPs).
The system shall support the evaluation of content in the archive for preservation risks and opportunities.
The system shall facilitate the participation in the development and adoption of digital preservation standards.
The system shall maintain a record of preservation actions taken for each digital object.
The system shall generate reports on preservation status and risks for digital objects within the repository.

systemFeatures.name: Access and Delivery
systemFeatures.description: The Access and Delivery feature is responsible for providing controlled and efficient access to the digital objects and their associated metadata stored within the **nlm** Digital Repository. This offers paramount user value by making NLM's rich digital collections discoverable and usable by diverse user classes, from the general public to specialized NIH staff, fulfilling the core mission of dissemination. Technically, it handles user authentication, authorization, search execution, and the dynamic generation and delivery of Dissemination Information Packages (**DIPs**) in appropriate formats. Its effectiveness directly impacts the usability and reach of the entire repository, requiring robust performance and adherence to security policies.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: An end-user searches the repository via the public web interface for a specific medical journal article. Response: The system performs a search across relevant metadata and full-text indexes, returning a list of matching results.

Stimulus: An authorized NIH staff member requests to view a restricted digital object. Response: The system verifies the user's credentials and access rights, then generates and delivers a Dissemination Information Package (DIP) containing the requested object in a viewable format.

Stimulus: A request for a digital object is denied due to an embargo period or licensing restriction. Response: The system displays an informative message to the user explaining the reason for denial and suggests alternative access points or future availability.
functionalRequirements: The system shall provide search capabilities across descriptive, administrative, and structural metadata, as well as full-text content where available.
The system shall implement role-based access control to enforce restrictions based on user class, licensing terms, embargo periods, copyright, and location.
The system shall coordinate access activities, including authentication, authorization, and logging of user requests.
The system shall generate Dissemination Information Packages (DIPs) on demand, packaging digital objects and relevant metadata for delivery.
The system shall deliver digital objects to users in readily available and widely supported formats (e.g., PDF, JPEG, XML).
The system shall provide mechanisms for delivering responses to user requests, including direct downloads or in-browser viewing.
The system shall integrate with NLM's user authentication systems (e.g., NIH network authentication) for staff access.
The system shall maintain audit logs of all access requests, including successful deliveries and denials.
The system shall provide mechanisms for NLM staff to activate or deactivate access to specific digital objects or collections.

performanceRequirements: N-REQ-050: The system shall support simultaneous access for at least 1,000 concurrent end-users accessing commonly requested materials, with a response time of less than 3 seconds for search queries. Rationale: High user demand for NLM resources necessitates robust responsiveness for effective public and research utility.

N-REQ-051: The system shall process bulk ingest streams of up to 1 terabyte per day, maintaining an average ingest rate of at least 100 gigabytes per hour. Rationale: NLM's continuous acquisition of large born-digital and digitized collections requires efficient and high-throughput ingest capabilities to prevent backlogs.

N-REQ-052: The system shall ensure 99.9% uptime for core access and administrative functionalities, excluding scheduled maintenance windows. Rationale: Consistent availability is paramount for a national digital library, supporting critical research and public information access without significant interruptions.

N-REQ-053: The system shall complete data integrity checks across the entire archival storage within a 24-hour cycle. Rationale: Regular and timely verification of data integrity is essential for detecting and mitigating data degradation or corruption, safeguarding the long-term preservation of digital assets.
safetyRequirements: N-REQ-054: The system shall implement automated checksum verification upon ingest and retrieval of all digital objects to detect and prevent data corruption. Rationale: Data integrity is non-negotiable for archival preservation; checksums provide a fundamental safeguard against silent data corruption during storage or transmission.

N-REQ-055: The system shall maintain at least three geographically dispersed copies of all Archival Information Packages (AIPs) to ensure resilience against single-point-of-failure events, including regional disasters. Rationale: Georedundancy is a critical component of disaster recovery strategies for an invaluable national digital heritage, minimizing the risk of catastrophic data loss.

N-REQ-056: The system shall provide a comprehensive audit trail for all modification, deletion, or preservation action performed on digital objects and their associated metadata. Rationale: Immutability and traceability of archival records are vital for maintaining authenticity and accountability, allowing for forensic analysis and recovery from accidental or malicious alterations.

N-REQ-057: The system shall integrate with **NIH** emergency alert systems to notify NLM System Administration/Programming staff immediately of any critical system failures, security breaches, or data integrity anomalies. Rationale: Rapid response to critical incidents is essential for minimizing potential damage, ensuring prompt recovery, and maintaining the trust placed in the repository.
securityRequirements: N-REQ-058: The system shall comply with all **HHS/NIH** security policies and standards, including those for data encryption (at rest and in transit), access control, and incident response. Rationale: Protecting sensitive NLM and user data from unauthorized access, modification, or disclosure is a legal and ethical imperative, aligning with federal cybersecurity mandates.

N-REQ-059: The system shall implement role-based access control (**RBAC**) for all user classes, strictly enforcing privileges based on predefined roles (e.g., General Public, NIH Staff, Metadata Access, Object Maintenance, System Administrator). Rationale: Granular control over system functions and digital objects is necessary to prevent unauthorized operations and maintain the integrity of the archival content and administrative processes.

N-REQ-060: The system shall support multi-factor authentication (**MFA**) for all NLM staff and administrative users. Rationale: **MFA** significantly enhances the security of administrative accounts, reducing the risk of unauthorized access through compromised credentials, which is critical for protecting the repository's core assets.

N-REQ-061: The system shall automatically log all access attempts, successful and failed, to digital objects and administrative functions, retaining logs for a minimum of one year for auditing and security analysis. Rationale: Comprehensive logging provides a critical forensic capability for detecting and investigating security incidents, ensuring accountability and compliance with audit requirements.

N-REQ-062: The system shall perform regular security vulnerability scans and penetration testing, with findings remediated within specified service level agreements (**SLAs**). Rationale: Proactive identification and remediation of security vulnerabilities are essential to maintain a robust security posture against evolving cyber threats, protecting the integrity and confidentiality of the digital archive.
softwareQualityAttributes: N-REQ-063: Adaptability: The system shall be adaptable to new digital formats and metadata standards without requiring a complete re-architecture. Rationale: The dynamic nature of digital content and evolving preservation best practices necessitates a flexible system design that can integrate new technologies efficiently.

N-REQ-064: Availability: The system shall be available 24/7, with planned maintenance windows occurring during off-peak hours and communicated in advance. Rationale: As a national resource, continuous access to **nlm** is crucial for researchers and the public across different time zones.

N-REQ-065: Maintainability: The system's codebase shall be well-documented and modular, facilitating efficient debugging, updates, and enhancements by NLM development staff. Rationale: A maintainable system reduces operational costs and allows for quicker responses to bugs or evolving requirements, ensuring long-term sustainability.

N-REQ-066: Reliability: The system shall consistently store, retrieve, and process digital objects without errors or data loss, even under peak load conditions. Rationale: Reliability is fundamental for an archival system, as any failure in data handling directly compromises the preservation mission.

N-REQ-067: Scalability: The system shall be capable of horizontally scaling its storage capacity and processing power to accommodate projected growth of digital collections over the next 10 years. Rationale: The volume of digital content at NLM is expected to grow significantly, requiring a system that can expand without proportional performance degradation.

N-REQ-068: Usability: The user interfaces for both end-users and NLM staff shall be intuitive and adhere to established accessibility guidelines. Rationale: A usable system maximizes user adoption and efficiency for all stakeholders, reducing training costs and improving overall satisfaction.
businessRules: N-BR-001: All digital content submitted for permanent retention must adhere to the NLM Collection Development Policy, which defines the scope and subject matter eligibility for the repository.
N-BR-002: Digital objects designated for long-term preservation must be assigned an NLM permanence rating, which dictates their storage, migration, and retention policies within the repository.
N-BR-003: Access to specific digital objects may be restricted based on valid licensing agreements, copyright regulations, or predefined embargo periods, as negotiated with content producers.
N-BR-004: Content acquired through web harvesting, while stored in the repository, will be processed via separate software and workflows distinct from the core Ingest Management feature.
N-BR-005: All system configuration changes and policy updates must be approved by designated NLM administrative personnel and recorded in the system's audit log.
N-BR-006: The system shall prioritize the preservation of intellectual content during format migrations, with consideration for preserving original 'look and feel' decided on a case-by-case basis.

N-BR-007: At least the original, most recent migrated, and next-to-last migrated versions of digital objects must be retained according to NLM's versioning policy, unless otherwise specified for specific content types.

otherRequirements: N-REQ-069: The system shall support comprehensive metadata requirements as detailed in Appendix A (Minimum Descriptive Metadata) and Appendix D (Technical Metadata). This includes the ability to ingest, store, manage, and retrieve metadata conforming to standards such as Dublin Core, MARC21, METS, MODS, and PREMIS. Rationale: Rich and standardized metadata is essential for discoverability, access, and long-term preservation of digital objects, enabling efficient management and compliance with archival best practices.

glossary.definition: Archival Information Package. A package of information that is preserved within an OAIS compliant archive, consisting of content information and associated preservation description information (PDI).
Dissemination Information Package. The information package delivered to the consumer in response to a request from that consumer. The DIP is derived from one or more AIPs.
Open Archival Information System. A reference model for an archive responsible for preserving information and making it available for a designated community.
Submission Information Package. An information package delivered by the producer to the archive. It includes content information and associated preservation description information (PDI).
National Library of Medicine. The world's largest biomedical library and a national resource for medical information.
Data about data. Descriptive, administrative, and structural information about a digital object that facilitates its discovery, management, and preservation.
Materials that originate in digital form and do not have an analog original.
An identified group of potential consumers who should be able to understand a particular set of information.

flowchartDiagram.syntaxExplanation: A Flowchart diagrams a sequential process or workflow, illustrating steps, decisions, and data flows using various node shapes and directed arrows. The 'flowchart TD' directive indicates a top-down flow. Nodes are defined by an alphanumeric ID followed by their label in double quotes. Decision points use diamond shapes. Input/Output steps typically use parallelogram shapes. Process steps use rectangular shapes. Start/End points use rounded or circular shapes. All node labels with spaces must be quoted, and node IDs must be unique and alphanumeric.
flowchartDiagram.code:
flowchart TD
    Start(("Start Ingest Process")) --> S1["Receive Submission (SIP)"]
    S1 --> S2{"Virus Scan & Initial Check?"}
    S2 --"No"--> S3["Notify Provider: Rejection"] --> S22("End Ingest")
    S2 --"Yes"--> S4{"Format Compliance & Quality Assurance?"}
    S4 --"No"--> S5["Record Non-Compliance, Await Decision"] --> S6{"Accept Non-Standard?"}
    S6 --"No"--> S3
    S6 --"Yes"--> S7["Proceed with Non-Standard Ingest"] --> S8["Generate Archival Information Package (AIP)"]
    S4 --"Yes"--> S8
    S8 --> S9["Generate Descriptive/Admin Metadata"] --> S10["Update Data Management System"]
    S10 --> S11["Store AIP in Archival Storage"]
    S11 --> S12{"Confirm Ingest to Provider?"}
    S12 --"Yes"--> S13["Send Confirmation"]
    S12 --"No"--> S14["Log Internal Confirmation"]
    S13 --> S22
    S14 --> S22
flowchartDiagram.caption: NLM Digital Repository Ingest Workflow

sequenceDiagram.syntaxExplanation: A Sequence Diagram illustrates the temporal order of interactions between participants to perform a specific function. Participants are defined with 'participant' keyword, optionally with an alias. Messages are represented by arrows, indicating synchronous (`->`) or asynchronous (`->>`) communication, and responses (`-->>`). Activations (`activate`/`deactivate` or `->>+`/`-->>-`) show when a participant is active. Alt blocks (`alt`/`else`) represent alternative message sequences. Participant names should be simple alphanumeric IDs, and message labels should be clear and concise. Activations must be balanced, and aliases should not be quoted.
sequenceDiagram.code:
sequenceDiagram
    participant CP as "Content Provider"
    participant NLM_I as "NLM Ingest System"
    participant QA as "Quality Assurance Service"
    participant MetaG as "Metadata Generator"
    participant DM as "Data Management System"
    participant AS as "Archival Storage"

    CP->>NLM_I: Submit SIP ("Digital Content & Metadata")
    NLM_I->>+QA: Request SIP Validation
    QA->>NLM_I: Report: "Virus Scan OK, Format Check Pending"
    NLM_I->>QA: Request Format & Quality Check
    alt SIP is compliant
        QA-->>-NLM_I: Report: "SIP Valid & Compliant"
        NLM_I->>MetaG: Trigger Metadata Extraction/Generation
        MetaG->>DM: Store "Descriptive & Admin Metadata"
        NLM_I->>DM: Coordinate "SIP ID to Metadata Record"
        NLM_I->>AS: Transfer "Validated Content (AIP)"
        AS-->>NLM_I: Confirm "AIP Stored & Replicated"
        NLM_I->>CP: Confirm "Submission Accepted"
    else SIP has issues
        QA-->>-NLM_I: Report: "SIP has Non-Compliance/Errors"
        NLM_I->>CP: Request "SIP Resubmission/Action (Error Details)"
    end
sequenceDiagram.caption: Content Provider SIP Submission Sequence

entityRelationshipDiagram.syntaxExplanation: An Entity Relationship Diagram (ERD) visually models the structure of a database, showing entities (tables) and their relationships. Entities are defined with 'entity { attributes }'. Attributes can have `PK` (Primary Key), `FK` (Foreign Key), or `UK` (Unique Key) constraints. Relationships are defined between entities, specifying cardinality (e.g., `||--o{` for one-to-many, `}|--||` for many-to-one) and an optional quoted label describing the relationship. Attribute names must be alphanumeric and not quoted. Entity names with spaces must be quoted.
entityRelationshipDiagram.code:
erDiagram
    "User" ||--o{ "Submission" : submits
    "Submission" ||--|{ "DigitalObject" : contains
    "DigitalObject" ||--o{ "MetadataRecord" : has
    "Policy" ||--o{ "DigitalObject" : applies
    "Format" ||--o{ "DigitalObject" : uses
    "DigitalObject" ||--o{ "Version" : includes
    "MetadataRecord" ||--o{ "MetadataField" : comprises
    "User" }|--|| "UserRole" : "is assigned"

    "User" {
        int user_id PK
        string username UK
        string email
        datetime created_at
    }

    "UserRole" {
        int role_id PK
        string role_name UK
    }

    "Submission" {
        int submission_id PK
        int user_id FK
        datetime submission_date
        string status
        string agreement_ref
    }

    "DigitalObject" {
        int object_id PK
        string nlm_uid UK "Unique NLM identifier"
        int format_id FK
        datetime ingest_date
        string permanence_rating
        string storage_path
    }

    "MetadataRecord" {
        int metadata_id PK
        int object_id FK
        string metadata_type
        datetime last_modified
    }

    "MetadataField" {
        int field_id PK
        int metadata_id FK
        string field_name
        string field_value
    }

    "Policy" {
        int policy_id PK
        string policy_name UK
        string policy_type
        string policy_description
    }

    "Format" {
        int format_id PK
        string format_name UK
        string mime_type
        string preservation_level
    }

    "Version" {
        int version_id PK
        int object_id FK
        int version_number
        datetime version_date
        string changes
    }
entityRelationshipDiagram.caption: NLM Digital Repository Core Entities

tbdList: N-TBD-001: The specific file formats for long-term preservation, categorized by preservation level (e.g., preferred, acceptable, at-risk), need to be fully defined and documented in a separate NLM Digital Repository Format Policy. (Reference: N-REQ-005, N-REQ-039)

N-TBD-002: Detailed technical specifications for the interface with NLM’s Integrated Library System (ILS), including data mapping and API protocols, require further definition. (Reference: N-REQ-010, N-SOFT-001)

N-TBD-003: The exact schema for descriptive, administrative, and structural metadata, including mandatory and optional fields for various content types, must be finalized in conjunction with NLM's Metadata Working Group. (Reference: N-REQ-008, N-REQ-069)
N-TBD-004: Specific retention periods for different permanence ratings and the criteria for content removal from the repository need to be established by NLM policy. (Reference: N-ASS-011, N-BR-002)
N-TBD-005: The precise methods and tools for automated metadata extraction from various digital object types must be evaluated and selected during the design phase. (Reference: N-ASS-016)

N-TBD-006: Performance benchmarks for search query response times under specific concurrent user loads require further refinement based on expected usage patterns and system architecture. (Reference: N-REQ-050)

N-TBD-007: The detailed disaster recovery plan, including specific recovery time objectives (RTO) and recovery point objectives (RPO) for different data categories, needs to be developed. (Reference: N-REQ-024, N-REQ-055)
N-TBD-008: Specific encryption algorithms and key management practices for data at rest and in transit must be defined in accordance with updated HHS/NIH security guidelines. (Reference: N-REQ-058)

revisionHistory.description: Initial Release of the telescope Software Requirements Specification.
revisionHistory.author: SRA System

introduction.purpose: This document defines the software requirements for the **telescope** X-Ray Telescope (XRT) Control Processor (XCP) Flight Software (FSW). The primary goal is to provide a comprehensive specification that guides the development, verification, and validation of the FSW, ensuring it meets the operational needs of the **Swift** Gamma Ray Burst Explorer mission. This specification serves as a foundational reference for all aspects of the software lifecycle.
introduction.documentConventions: The requirements within this document are structured according to IEEE 830-1998 Recommended Practice for Software Requirements Specifications. Functional requirements are uniquely identified with the prefix T-REQ-, followed by a sequential number. Use cases are identified as T-UC-. Diagram identifiers use the T_[TYPE] format. Items pending further definition are marked as TBD (To Be Determined), and those requiring review are marked as TBR (To Be Reviewed).
introduction.intendedAudience: This document is intended for a diverse audience, including **telescope** project managers, system engineers, software developers, quality assurance personnel, and testers at both Southwest Research Institute (SwRI) and Penn State University (PSU). It also serves as a critical reference for external stakeholders involved in the **Swift** mission who require a detailed understanding of the **telescope** FSW capabilities and constraints. The technical detail provided aims to facilitate clear communication and common understanding across all participating teams.
introduction.productScope: The **telescope** FSW product encompasses all software components necessary for the control, data acquisition, and health monitoring of the XRT. This includes managing the XRT Electronics Package (**XEP**) and Camera Head, processing commands from the **Swift** spacecraft, collecting and compressing science data, and managing thermal control systems. The scope specifically excludes the **Swift** spacecraft bus software, ground segment software, and the physical design of the XRT hardware components, focusing solely on the embedded flight software's functional and non-functional characteristics.
references: IEEE 830-1998 IEEE Recommended Practice for Software Requirements Specifications.
ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018 Systems and software engineering — Life cycle processes — Requirements engineering.
GSFC-410.4-SPEC-0004, Swift Missions Requirements Document.
Swift 1553 Bus Protocol Interface Control Document (Spectrum Astro document # 1143-EI-S19121).
XRT Interface Control Document (Pennsylvania State University [PSU] document # XRT-PSU-018).
XRT Thermal Design Specification (PSU document # XRT-PSU-012).

overallDescription.productPerspective: The **telescope** X-Ray Telescope (XRT) Control Processor (XCP) Flight Software (FSW) is a critical component of the larger **Swift** Gamma Ray Burst Explorer mission, which is categorized as a National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA) medium-class explorer (MIDEX) satellite. This FSW is not a standalone product but an embedded system designed to tightly integrate with and control the XRT instrument, providing high-resolution multi-wavelength observation capabilities following the detection of Gamma-Ray Bursts (GRBs) by the Burst Alert Telescope (BAT). Its role is to refine BAT positions and enable immediate spectroscopic observations of GRB afterglows.
productFunctions: Process and execute telecommands received from the **Swift** spacecraft.
Manage the XRT instrument states and operational modes.
Acquire, process, and compress science data from the Charge-Coupled Device (**CCD**) camera.
Control and monitor the XRT's thermal environment, including the Thermo-Electric Cooler (**TEC**) and various heater groups.
Interface with and manage data from the Telescope Alignment Monitor (**TAM**).
Perform internal health monitoring and diagnostics (Built-In Tests).
Handle real-time clock synchronization and timestamping of data packets.
Manage power distribution to various XRT components.
Provide error detection and correction for data integrity.

userClassesAndCharacteristics.characteristics: These users are responsible for the overall operation of the **Swift** mission and, by extension, the **telescope** instrument. They interact with the FSW indirectly by sending telecommands and receiving telemetry via the **Swift** spacecraft ground segment. Their expertise lies in mission planning and execution, and they require a reliable, predictable system that provides clear status and diagnostic information. They typically have a high understanding of spaceflight operations and scientific objectives.

This class of users is involved in the development, testing, maintenance, and potential future upgrades of the **telescope** FSW. They require detailed technical documentation, access to diagnostic interfaces (like the **Ethernet** board on Engineering Units), and an understanding of the FSW's internal architecture and component interactions. Their expertise is deep in embedded systems, real-time operating systems, and space-grade software development, needing robust debugging and verification capabilities.

overallDescription.operatingEnvironment: The **telescope** Flight Software operates within a highly constrained embedded system environment onboard the **Swift** Gamma Ray Burst Explorer satellite. The primary hardware platform for the XRT Electronics Package (**XEP**) is a **VME** enclosure housing a Lockheed-Martin Federal Systems (**LMFS**) single-board computer, which utilizes the **RAD6000** microprocessor. This processor is a radiation-hardened CPU designed for space applications, demanding specific software design considerations for robustness and fault tolerance.
designAndImplementationConstraints: The **telescope** FSW must operate within the strict power, mass, and volume limitations imposed by the **Swift** spacecraft and the XRT instrument design. Specific hardware limitations include the fixed voltage outputs of the low voltage power supply and the single string nature of the Survival Power Bus (**SPB**), which mandates careful power management and contingency planning. The physical connection between the **XEP** and Camera Head is a one-meter maximum length cable, influencing data transfer rates and signal integrity.

The choice of the **RAD6000** microprocessor dictates the use of specific development tools, compilers, and a **Real-Time Operating System (RTOS)** compatible with its architecture. This imposes constraints on programming languages, available libraries, and overall software complexity. The FSW must also comply with the **Swift Missions Requirements Document (GSFC-410.4-SPEC-0004)** and adhere to established space-grade software development standards, including stringent testing and verification protocols to ensure mission success.

A significant constraint is the reuse of components and design heritage from previous projects such as CUBIC, IMAGE, JET-X, and XMM. While this offers cost and schedule benefits, it necessitates careful integration of existing modules and adherence to their interface specifications. Specific communication protocols, such as the **MIL-STD-1553B** serial interface for spacecraft communication and **RS-422** for the Telescope Alignment Monitor (**TAM**), must be strictly implemented according to their respective Interface Control Documents (ICDs).
userDocumentation: The **telescope** FSW will be delivered with a comprehensive set of technical documentation to support its lifecycle. This includes detailed API specifications for external interfaces, an FSW Design Document outlining the internal architecture and component interactions, and a comprehensive Error Code Reference Manual to assist in diagnosing and resolving operational anomalies. Additionally, system configuration guides will be provided to support ground operations and potential in-flight software updates.
assumptionsAndDependencies: It is assumed that the **Swift** spacecraft provides stable and reliable power through its Operational Power Bus (**OPB**) and Survival Power Bus (**SPB**), as well as a fully compliant **MIL-STD-1553B** communication interface. The FSW depends on the spacecraft's ability to cycle the **OPB** for hardware resets and to provide accurate timing signals, including the 1PPS (One-Pulse-Per-Second) hardwired signal for clock synchronization.

The project depends on the timely availability and functionality of all third-party hardware components, including the Lockheed-Martin Federal Systems (**LMFS**) single-board computer and specific semiconductor devices for drivers and interfaces. Furthermore, ongoing input and detailed specifications from Penn State University (PSU) are crucial for defining outstanding requirements related to science data acquisition modes and specific verification procedures for certain sections of the FSW, as noted by the TBD/TBR items.

externalInterfaceRequirements.userInterfaces: For software development and diagnostic purposes on Engineering Units (**EU**), an **Ethernet** interface is provided. This interface facilitates debugging, uploading new software builds, and direct monitoring of FSW internal states. This **Ethernet** capability, however, is explicitly excluded from the Flight Units (**FU**) to conserve resources and reduce potential attack vectors in space. Standard debugging tools and custom diagnostic applications will be utilized via this interface.
externalInterfaceRequirements.hardwareInterfaces: Further hardware interfaces include the **CCD** interface CSC, which manages the intricate clock waveforms generated by the Sequencer board for **CCD** operation, along with the Clock board driving **CCD** bias voltage inputs. The Signal Chain board processes analog video output from the **CCD** into a digital bitstream. The Power Distribution Driver manages relays for heaters, **TAM** power, and camera door actuators, while the **Thermo-Electric Cooler (TEC)** power supply interface handles variable voltage outputs for temperature control. An **EEPROM** interface driver provides access to non-volatile memory for configuration and operational data.
externalInterfaceRequirements.softwareInterfaces: Specialized interface drivers abstract direct hardware interaction, such as the **MIL-STD-1553B** Driver, **RS-422** Driver, Analog I/O Driver, and the **CCD** Data Driver. These drivers provide standardized software interfaces to the underlying hardware, allowing higher-level CSCs to interact with the peripherals without needing to understand low-level hardware specifics. Other CSCs, like the Time Synchronization CSC, Sequencer Interface CSC, and various heater control CSCs (Tube Heater Control, Baffle Heater Control, Thermo-Electric Cooler CSC), ensure coordinated operation of critical subsystems. The Event Recognition Processor CSC monitors science data for significant events.
externalInterfaceRequirements.communicationsInterfaces: In addition to the **MIL-STD-1553B** bus, the FSW utilizes an **RS-422** serial interface. This interface serves two critical communication functions: receiving the 1PPS (One-Pulse-Per-Second) timing signal from the spacecraft for high-precision clock synchronization and exchanging image data with the Telescope Alignment Monitor (**TAM**). The 1PPS signal, in conjunction with At-The-Tone-The-Time-Will-Be messages over the **MIL-STD-1553B** bus, ensures the accuracy of the local Real Time Clock (**RTC**) within the **telescope** system.

systemFeatures.name: Command and Control Processing
systemFeatures.description: This feature provides the core functionality for the **telescope** Flight Software to receive, validate, and execute telecommands from the **Swift** spacecraft, as well as to generate and transmit telemetry data. It ensures that the XRT instrument can be configured, operated, and monitored remotely, aligning with mission objectives. The technical impact involves a robust message parsing engine, state machine management, and secure communication protocols to ensure the integrity and timely execution of all commands, thereby improving the efficiency and reliability of remote operations. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: **Swift** spacecraft sends a telecommand packet to the **telescope** FSW via **MIL-STD-1553B**. Response: The system shall receive, validate, and queue the telecommand.
Stimulus: The FSW detects a valid telecommand in its queue. Response: The system shall execute the specified command, update internal states, and generate an execution status telemetry message.

Stimulus: The FSW completes a periodic health check or data acquisition cycle. Response: The system shall compile relevant status and science data into **CCSDS** telemetry packets and transmit them to the **Swift** spacecraft via **MIL-STD-1553B**.
functionalRequirements: T-REQ-001: The system shall receive telecommands from the **MIL-STD-1553B** bus in accordance with the **Swift 1553 Bus Protocol**.
T-REQ-002: The system shall validate the integrity and format of all received telecommands.
T-REQ-003: The system shall execute valid telecommands, updating relevant instrument parameters or triggering specific operational sequences.
T-REQ-004: The system shall generate telemetry packets containing instrument status, health parameters, and science data in **CCSDS** format.
T-REQ-005: The system shall transmit telemetry packets to the **MIL-STD-1553B** bus at predefined intervals or upon specific events.
T-REQ-006: The system shall maintain a local copy of the spacecraft Real Time Clock (**RTC**) for timestamping telemetry data.

systemFeatures.name: Science Data Acquisition and Processing
systemFeatures.description: This feature manages the complete lifecycle of science data, from its collection by the **CCD** camera to its preparation for transmission. It encompasses controlling the **CCD** operational modes, acquiring raw pixel data, applying data compression techniques, and preparing the data for telemetry. This directly enables the **telescope** mission's scientific objectives by providing high-quality observational data. Technically, this requires precise timing control, efficient memory management for large data volumes, and robust algorithms for data handling, significantly impacting the data throughput and quality. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: FSW receives a telecommand to initiate **CCD** data acquisition in a specified mode. Response: The system shall configure the **CCD** clock sequencer and signal chain for the requested mode.

Stimulus: The **CCD** completes an image acquisition cycle. Response: The system shall read out the raw pixel data, perform any necessary initial processing (e.g., centroiding for **TAM**), and buffer it for compression.
Stimulus: Raw science data is available in the buffer. Response: The system shall apply a predefined data compression algorithm to the buffered data to reduce its volume for transmission.
functionalRequirements: T-REQ-007: The system shall control the **CCD** camera's operational modes, supporting up to sixty-four distinct modes.
T-REQ-008: The system shall acquire raw science data from the **CCD**'s video outputs.
T-REQ-009: The system shall apply data compression to science data to optimize telemetry bandwidth.
T-REQ-010: The system shall buffer acquired science data prior to compression and transmission.
T-REQ-011: The system shall support centroid algorithm processing for **TAM** image data to achieve sub-pixel resolution.
T-REQ-012: The system shall ensure synchronization between **CCD** clock waveforms and data readout operations.

systemFeatures.name: Thermal Management
systemFeatures.description: This feature is responsible for maintaining critical XRT components within their operational temperature ranges, specifically controlling the **Thermo-Electric Cooler (TEC)** for the **CCD** and managing various heater groups on the telescope tube and mirror baffle. Effective thermal management is crucial for the optimal performance and longevity of the sensitive XRT hardware, preventing damage from extreme temperatures and ensuring stable scientific data acquisition. Technically, this involves closed-loop control algorithms, sensor monitoring, and power distribution management for heater elements, directly contributing to system stability and reliability. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: FSW receives a telecommand to set the **CCD** temperature setpoint and ramp rate. Response: The system shall configure the **TEC** control loop with the new parameters.

Stimulus: A temperature sensor reports a value outside the desired hysteresis band for a heater group. Response: The system shall activate or deactivate the corresponding solid-state relay to control the heater element.
Stimulus: The **CCD** temperature deviates from its setpoint. Response: The system shall adjust the **TEC** power supply output to regulate the **CCD** temperature back to the setpoint.
functionalRequirements: T-REQ-013: The system shall provide closed-loop control of the **CCD** temperature via the **Thermo-Electric Cooler (TEC)**.
T-REQ-014: The system shall allow telecommand control of the **TEC** temperature setpoint, ramp rate, and operating mode (open or closed loop).
T-REQ-015: The system shall control 36 independent heater groups on the telescope tube with telecommand-configurable temperature set points and hysteresis.
T-REQ-016: The system shall control three heater groups on the Mirror Baffle, specifically Control 1 and Control 2, with telecommand-configurable temperature set points and hysteresis.
T-REQ-017: The system shall monitor and report all relevant temperature readings as housekeeping telemetry.
T-REQ-018: The system shall utilize a digital potentiometer with 100 wiper tap points for **TEC** temperature setpoint control.

performanceRequirements: T-REQ-019: The system shall acquire and process **CCD** science data from a 640-pixel output row within the timing constraints required by the specified operational modes, which may include double-clocking and overclocking. Rationale: Critical for ensuring complete and timely capture of scientific observations, especially during short-duration Gamma-Ray Bursts (GRBs) and their afterglows.

T-REQ-020: The system shall respond to telecommands from the **MIL-STD-1553B** bus within 100 milliseconds from reception to initial execution acknowledgment. Rationale: Ensures mission operators can confidently control the XRT in real-time and react quickly to dynamic mission events, such as GRB detections.

T-REQ-021: The system shall process and compress science data at a rate sufficient to support the science telemetry rates for a typical 30-minute observation (TBR). Rationale: Prevents data backlogs and ensures that high-priority science data is transmitted to the ground segment without significant delay, maximizing scientific yield.
safetyRequirements: T-REQ-022: The system shall prevent inadvertent opening of the camera door once it has been closed. Rationale: The camera door cannot be closed once opened; an unintended opening could expose the sensitive **CCD** to damaging environments, compromising the instrument and mission.

T-REQ-023: The system shall utilize the Survival Power Bus (**SPB**) to power critical heaters that prevent damage to the XRT from excessively low temperatures. Rationale: Ensures the long-term integrity of the XRT hardware, particularly during non-operational periods or in emergency power-down scenarios.

T-REQ-024: The system shall implement robust error detection and correction mechanisms for all critical data transfers, especially those involving telecommands and science data. Rationale: Guards against data corruption due to radiation effects or communication noise in the space environment, preserving operational control and scientific data fidelity.
securityRequirements: T-REQ-025: The system shall ensure that all telecommands received via the **MIL-STD-1553B** bus originate from an authenticated **Swift** spacecraft source. Rationale: Protects the XRT from unauthorized or malicious commands that could compromise its operation or cause damage. (Implicit via bus protocol, but explicit requirement).

T-REQ-026: The system shall maintain the confidentiality and integrity of science data through data compression and secure telemetry formatting before transmission to the spacecraft. Rationale: Ensures that valuable scientific data is not tampered with or exposed during transit, preserving its scientific value. (Implicit via CCSDS standards, but explicitly required).

T-REQ-027: The system shall employ robust memory protection mechanisms to prevent unauthorized access or modification of critical flight software code and data areas. Rationale: Essential for preventing software corruption from faults or unforeseen events, which could lead to loss of control or incorrect operation.
softwareQualityAttributes: T-REQ-028: **Reliability**: The system shall operate continuously without unscheduled restarts or data corruption for the planned three-year mission duration. Rationale: Given the mission's inaccessibility, high reliability is paramount to meet scientific objectives and avoid costly failures.

T-REQ-029: **Maintainability**: The system's software architecture shall be modular, allowing for independent testing and future modification of individual Computer Software Components (**CSCs**) with minimal impact on other parts of the system. Rationale: Facilitates debugging, updates, and future enhancements, extending the operational life and adaptability of the FSW.

T-REQ-030: **Testability**: The system shall incorporate built-in test (BIT) capabilities to allow for comprehensive verification of hardware interfaces and software functionality both pre-launch and in-orbit. Rationale: Ensures that the system's operational readiness can be continuously assessed and validated throughout its lifecycle.

T-REQ-031: **Fault Tolerance**: The system shall gracefully handle communication errors, sensor failures, and temporary hardware anomalies without requiring a full system reset, utilizing error detection and correction where feasible. Rationale: Maximizes mission uptime and data collection by allowing the system to recover from minor disruptions autonomously.

T-REQ-032: **Interoperability**: The system shall conform to the **MIL-STD-1553B** and **RS-422** communication protocols and **CCSDS** telemetry standards to ensure seamless integration and data exchange with the **Swift** spacecraft and ground segment. Rationale: Essential for effective command and control, and successful downlink of scientific data.
businessRules: T-REQ-033: All FSW development and documentation shall comply with relevant **NASA** standards and the **Swift Missions Requirements Document (GSFC-410.4-SPEC-0004)**.
T-REQ-034: The **telescope** FSW must adhere to the **Swift 1553 Bus Protocol Interface Control Document (Spectrum Astro document # 1143-EI-S19121)** for all bus communications.
T-REQ-035: The FSW shall implement the thermal control logic as specified in the **XRT Thermal Design Specification (PSU document # XRT-PSU-012)**.
T-REQ-036: All data packets for transmission to the spacecraft must conform to Consultative Committee for Space Data Systems (**CCSDS**) Source Packet standards.

otherRequirements: T-REQ-037: Some referenced document data is incomplete and will be provided as TBR.
T-REQ-038: Some abbreviations are TBR and require further definition.
T-REQ-039: Some information about science data acquisition modes is TBD and needs definition.
T-REQ-040: Some items in the Data Dictionary are TBD/TBR and require completion.

T-REQ-041: Requirements in sections 5.22 (Baffle Heater Control), 5.23 (Data Collection Control CSC), 5.24 (Event Recognition Processor CSC), 5.26 (Telescope Alignment Monitor CSC), and 5.27 (Thermo-Electric Cooler CSC) are TBD/TBR; PSU input is needed. Verification for these is TBD.

glossary.definition: One-Pulse-Per-Second: A hardwired timing signal from the spacecraft for clock synchronization.
MIL-STD-1553B: A military standard serial data bus for spacecraft communication.
Burst Alert Telescope: A wide-angle X-ray telescope on **Swift** that detects GRBs.
Charge-Coupled Device: An imaging spectrometer used in the XRT to collect science data.
Consultative Committee for Space Data Systems: An organization that develops standards for space data systems.
Computer Software Component: A modular unit of software functionality within the FSW.
Digital-to-Analog Converter: Used for controlling CCD voltages.
Electrically Erasable Programmable Read-Only Memory: Non-volatile memory for configuration and data storage.
Flight Software: The embedded software controlling the XRT instrument.
Gamma-Ray Burst: High-energy cosmic events studied by the **Swift** mission.
High-Output Paraffin: Actuators used for controlling camera doors and pressure relief valves.
Housekeeping: Telemetry data reporting the health and status of the XRT instrument.
Lockheed-Martin Federal Systems: Provider of the single-board computer using the **RAD6000** microprocessor.
Medium-Class Explorer: A category of NASA satellite missions.
National Aeronautics and Space Administration: The U.S. government agency overseeing space exploration.
Operational Power Bus: One of two 28VDC power buses from the spacecraft to the XRT.
Penn State University: Collaborating institution for the **Swift** mission and XRT development.
A radiation-hardened microprocessor used in the **XEP** single board computer.
Real-Time Operating System: Software that manages computational resources and provides deterministic timing for embedded applications.
Real Time Clock: A local clock maintained to timestamp data packets.
Spacecraft: Refers to the **Swift** satellite platform.
Survival Power Bus: One of two 28VDC power buses from the spacecraft, supplying power for critical heaters.
Southwest Research Institute: Preparing entity for this SRS document.
Telescope Alignment Monitor: A device used to measure changes in the XRT's mechanical alignment.
To Be Determined: An item not yet defined.
To Be Reviewed: An item that requires further review or validation.
Thermo-Electric Cooler: A device used to cool the **CCD** and control its temperature.
Universal Asynchronous Receiver-Transmitter: Used for serial communication, e.g., with the **TAM**.
Ultraviolet/Optical Telescope: One of the narrow-field telescopes on **Swift**.
VERSAmodule European: A bus standard used for the **XEP** enclosure.
X-Ray Telescope Control Processor: The processor responsible for controlling the XRT FSW.
XRT Electronics Package: One of two main electronic parts of the XRT, mounted on the spacecraft.
X-Ray Telescope: A sensitive, autonomous X-ray imaging spectrometer on the **Swift** mission.

flowchartDiagram.syntaxExplanation: Flowcharts are used to visualize processes, algorithms, and decision flows. Nodes represent steps or decisions, and arrows indicate the flow of control. Rectangular nodes denote a process step, diamond nodes denote a decision point, and rounded rectangular nodes denote start/end points. Direction is specified as Top-Down (TD) or Left-Right (LR). Node IDs are alphanumeric, and labels with spaces must be double-quoted.
flowchartDiagram.caption: telescope High-Level Observing Sequence

sequenceDiagram.syntaxExplanation: Sequence diagrams illustrate the temporal order of messages exchanged between participants. Participants are declared with 'participant' and can have an alias. Messages show communication direction (e.g., ->> for asynchronous, --> for synchronous). Activations can be shown with '->>+' and '-->>-'. Notes can be added to explain steps. Aliases must not be quoted.
sequenceDiagram.code:
sequenceDiagram
  participant SwiftS_C as Swift Spacecraft
  participant XRT_FSW as telescope FSW
  participant CmdCtrl as Command & Control CSC
  participant CCDCtrl as CCD Control CSC
  participant TMProc as Telemetry Processor

  SwiftS_C->>+XRT_FSW: Telecommand ("Set CCD Mode")
  XRT_FSW->>CmdCtrl: Validate Command
  CmdCtrl->>XRT_FSW: Command Validated
  XRT_FSW->>+CCDCtrl: Configure CCD(Mode: Spectroscopy)
  CCDCtrl->>CCDCtrl: Set Clock Sequencer Waveform
  CCDCtrl->>CCDCtrl: Set Signal Chain Gain
  CCDCtrl-->>-XRT_FSW: CCD Configuration Acknowledged
  XRT_FSW->>+TMProc: Update Status Telemetry
  TMProc->>TMProc: Generate CCSDS Packet
  TMProc-->>-XRT_FSW: Status Packet Ready
  XRT_FSW->>SwiftS_C: Telemetry ("CCD Mode Set OK")
sequenceDiagram.caption: telescope Telecommand Processing Sequence

entityRelationshipDiagram.syntaxExplanation: ER diagrams define entities, their attributes, and relationships. Entities are declared with 'entityName { attributeType attributeName [KEY_TYPE] "Comment" }'. Relationships are defined as 'ENTITY1 ||--o{ ENTITY2 : "label"', where '||--o{' specifies cardinality (e.g., one-to-many). Allowed key types are PK, FK, UK. Attribute names must be alphanumeric without spaces or quotes.
entityRelationshipDiagram.code:
erDiagram
  SPACECRAFT ||--|{ TELECOMMAND : "sends"
  TELECOMMAND ||--o{ COMMAND_LOG : "logs"
  TELECOMMAND ||--o{ FSW_STATE : "modifies"
  FSW_STATE ||--o{ TELEMETRY : "reports"
  TELEMETRY ||--|{ SENSOR_READING : "includes"
  TELEMETRY ||--|{ CCD_DATA : "includes"
  XRT_HW ||--o{ SENSOR_READING : "generates"
  XRT_HW ||--o{ ACTUATOR_CTRL : "receives"
  ACTUATOR_CTRL ||--o{ FSW_STATE : "driven by"
  CCD_SENSOR ||--o{ CCD_DATA : "captures"

  SPACECRAFT {
    int id PK
    string name
  }

  TELECOMMAND {
    int id PK
    int spacecraft_id FK
    string name
    string parameters
    datetime received_at
  }

  COMMAND_LOG {
    int id PK
    int telecommand_id FK
    string status
    datetime logged_at
  }

  FSW_STATE {
    int id PK
    string current_mode
    string sub_state
    datetime last_updated
  }

  TELEMETRY {
    int id PK
    int fsw_state_id FK
    string type
    bytes packet_data
    datetime timestamp
  }

  SENSOR_READING {
    int id PK
    int telemetry_id FK
    int xrt_hw_id FK
    string sensor_type
    decimal value
    string units
  }

  CCD_DATA {
    int id PK
    int telemetry_id FK
    int ccd_sensor_id FK
    string data_mode
    bytes raw_data
    bytes compressed_data
  }

  XRT_HW {
    int id PK
    string component_name
    string hw_type
  }

  ACTUATOR_CTRL {
    int id PK
    int xrt_hw_id FK
    string command_type
    string setting
  }

  CCD_SENSOR {
    int id PK
    string serial_number
    string model
  }
