externalInterfaceRequirements.softwareInterfaces: The **e-store** system integrates with several critical external software components to deliver its full functionality. It communicates with a dedicated **Configurator** system to identify and retrieve available product components for user configuration. Product specifications, offerings, and promotions are managed through an interface with a **Content Manager**. For financial transactions, the system interacts with a **BillPay system** to validate and process payments, and with a **Credit Management system** for handling financing options. Customer support functions rely on integration with a **CRM system**. Order management data flows to a **Sales system**, while shipment tracking and method updates are handled by a **Shipping system**. Tax calculations are delegated to an external **Tax system**, and export regulations are validated via an **Export Regulation system**. Additionally, the system integrates with a **Verisign-like software** for securing online transactions, typically a third-party trusted certificate authority.
externalInterfaceRequirements.communicationsInterfaces: The **e-store** system will primarily utilize the **HTTP** protocol for all communications over the internet, ensuring standard web accessibility and interaction. For internal communications within the intranet, the **TCP/IP protocol suite** will be employed, facilitating robust and reliable data exchange between system components. All external communications will occur over port 80, maintaining standard web traffic conventions. The system's logical address will adhere to the **IPv4 format**, providing a standardized network identification scheme. Specific security and encryption requirements for data transfer are detailed in the non-functional requirements section, ensuring confidential information remains protected during transmission.

systemFeatures.name: Product Configuration and Sales
systemFeatures.description: The **e-store** system provides users with the ability to configure products prior to purchase, enhancing the personalized shopping experience and ensuring product suitability. This feature allows users to select components, validates configurations, and guides them through conflict resolution, directly impacting customer satisfaction and reducing return rates. Priority: High. This feature is crucial for products like custom computers or modular electronics, allowing customers to tailor their purchases precisely to their needs.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User navigates to a configurable product page. Response: The system displays all products that can be configured.
Stimulus: User selects a product to configure. Response: The system displays all available components for the selected product.
Stimulus: User adds a component to the configuration. Response: The system enables the user to add one or more components to the configuration.
Stimulus: User attempts an invalid component combination. Response: The system notifies the user about any conflict in the current configuration and suggests resolutions.
Stimulus: User confirms the completed configuration. Response: The system allows the user to confirm the completion of the current configuration and proceeds with adding it to the cart.
functionalRequirements: The system shall display all the products that can be configured.
The system shall allow user to select the product to configure.
The system shall display all the available components of the product to configure.
The system shall enable user to add one or more component to the configuration.
The system shall notify the user about any conflict in the current configuration.
The system shall allow user to update the configuration to resolve conflict in the current configuration.
The system shall allow user to confirm the completion of current configuration.

systemFeatures.name: Comprehensive Product Details
systemFeatures.description: This feature ensures that customers have access to exhaustive information regarding each product, fostering informed purchasing decisions and enhancing trust in the **e-store**. By providing detailed specifications and browsing options, the system empowers users to thoroughly evaluate products, reducing ambiguity and potential post-purchase dissatisfaction. Priority: High. Clear and complete product data is fundamental to any successful online retail platform.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User selects a product from the catalog. Response: The system displays detailed information of the selected product.
Stimulus: User uses browsing options on a product page. Response: The system provides browsing options to see additional product details, such as images, videos, or extended specifications.
functionalRequirements: The system shall display detailed information of the selected products.
The system shall provide browsing options to see product details.

systemFeatures.name: Detailed Product Categorization
systemFeatures.description: The **e-store** provides detailed product categorization to help users navigate vast product catalogs efficiently, improving discoverability and overall user experience. This organization aids customers in quickly locating desired items and exploring related products, streamlining the shopping journey. Priority: Medium. Effective categorization is essential for large inventories, preventing user frustration and increasing sales potential.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User accesses the product catalog. Response: The system displays detailed product categorization to the user, allowing navigation through hierarchies.
functionalRequirements: The system shall display detailed product categorization to the user.

systemFeatures.name: Advanced Search Facility
systemFeatures.description: The **e-store** incorporates a robust search facility, enabling users to efficiently locate specific products within the extensive catalog using various criteria. This feature significantly enhances usability by minimizing the effort required to find items, supporting multiple search options and dynamic result display. Priority: High. An effective search function is critical for any e-commerce platform, directly influencing conversion rates and customer satisfaction.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User enters text into the search bar. Response: The system enables the user to enter the search text on the screen.
Stimulus: User selects multiple search filters. Response: The system enables the user to select multiple options on the screen to search.
Stimulus: User initiates a search. Response: The system displays all matching products based on the search criteria.
Stimulus: User scrolls through search results. Response: The system displays only 10 matching results on the current screen and enables navigation between search result pages.
Stimulus: User performs a search with no matches. Response: The system notifies the user when no matching product is found on the search.
functionalRequirements: The system shall enable user to enter the search text on the screen.
The system shall enable user to select multiple options on the screen to search.
The system shall display all the matching products based on the search.
The system shall display only 10 matching result on the current screen.
The system shall enable user to navigate between the search results.
The system shall notify the user when no matching product is found on the search.

systemFeatures.name: Customer Profile Management
systemFeatures.description: The **e-store** provides facilities for customers to create and maintain their personal profiles, centralizing user-specific data such as credentials and personal information. This feature allows for secure authentication and empowers users to manage their data, promoting a personalized shopping experience and fostering customer loyalty. Priority: High. Secure and manageable user profiles are foundational for building a trusted online retail environment.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: New user attempts to create an account. Response: The system allows the user to create a profile and set their credentials.
Stimulus: Registered user attempts to view their profile. Response: The system authenticates user credentials to view the profile.
Stimulus: User updates personal information in their profile. Response: The system allows the user to update the profile information securely.
functionalRequirements: The system shall allow user to create profile and set his credential.
The system shall authenticate user credentials to view the profile.
The system shall allow user to update the profile information.

systemFeatures.name: Personalized Profile Experience
systemFeatures.description: This feature enriches the customer's interaction with the **e-store** by offering a personalized view of their activities and preferences within their profile. Displaying order history, frequently searched items, and options for newsletter registration creates a tailored environment that enhances user engagement and encourages repeat visits. Priority: Medium. Personalization contributes to a more engaging and efficient shopping experience, helping users track past interactions and discover new content.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User accesses their profile. Response: The system displays both active and completed order history in the customer profile.
Stimulus: User selects an order from their history. Response: The system displays detailed information about the selected order.
Stimulus: User views their profile. Response: The system displays the most frequently searched items by the user in the profile.
Stimulus: User opts to subscribe to newsletters or surveys. Response: The system allows the user to register for newsletters and surveys within the profile.
functionalRequirements: The system shall display both the active and completed order history in the customer profile.
The system shall allow user to select the order from the order history.
The system shall display the detailed information about the selected order.
The system shall display the most frequently searched items by the user in the profile.
The system shall allow user to register for newsletters and surveys in the profile.

systemFeatures.name: Customer Support Services
systemFeatures.description: The **e-store** provides comprehensive customer support options, including online help, FAQs, and direct contact methods, ensuring users can quickly resolve queries and receive assistance. This feature is vital for maintaining customer satisfaction and trust, offering multiple channels for problem resolution and information access. Priority: High. Accessible and efficient customer support is a cornerstone of a reliable e-commerce platform.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User seeks assistance. Response: The system provides online help, FAQs, customer support contact information, and sitemap options for customer support.
Stimulus: User selects a support type. Response: The system allows the user to select the support type they want.
Stimulus: User enters support details. Response: The system allows the user to enter customer and product information for the support request.
Stimulus: User requests a callback. Response: The system displays customer support contact numbers on the screen and allows the user to enter a contact number for support personnel to call.
Stimulus: User requests online help or FAQs. Response: The system displays the requested online help or FAQs upon request.
functionalRequirements: The system shall provide online help, FAQ’s customer support, and sitemap options for customer support.
The system shall allow user to select the support type he wants.
The system shall allow user to enter the customer and product information for the support.
The system shall display the customer support contact numbers on the screen.
The system shall allow user to enter the contact number for support personnel to call.
The system shall display the online help upon request.
The system shall display the FAQ’s upon request.

systemFeatures.name: Order Email Confirmation
systemFeatures.description: This feature ensures customers receive timely and accurate email confirmations for their orders, reinforcing transaction security and providing a digital record of their purchase. Maintaining customer email information as a core part of the profile facilitates seamless communication, enhancing transparency and customer confidence in the **e-store**'s processes. Priority: High. Email confirmations are a standard and expected part of the online shopping experience.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User completes an order. Response: The system sends an order confirmation to the user through email.
Stimulus: User updates their profile. Response: The system maintains customer email information as a required part of the customer profile.
functionalRequirements: The system shall maintain customer email information as a required part of customer profile.
The system shall send an order confirmation to the user through email.

systemFeatures.name: Detailed Customer Invoice
systemFeatures.description: The **e-store** generates a detailed invoice for each confirmed order, providing customers with a comprehensive breakdown of their purchase. The option to print the invoice offers flexibility and convenience, ensuring customers have access to a physical record if needed. This feature supports financial transparency and is essential for post-purchase record-keeping. Priority: Medium. Detailed invoices are important for customer accounting and proof of purchase.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User confirms an order. Response: The system displays a detailed invoice for the current order once it is confirmed.
Stimulus: User requests to print an invoice. Response: The system optionally allows the user to print the invoice.
functionalRequirements: The system shall display detailed invoice for current order once it is confirmed.
The system shall optionally allow user to print the invoice.

systemFeatures.name: Shopping Cart Management
systemFeatures.description: The **e-store** provides a robust shopping cart facility, allowing users to collect and manage products before finalizing a purchase. This feature enhances the shopping experience by enabling users to add, remove, and review items dynamically, facilitating flexible and convenient order preparation. Priority: High. A functional shopping cart is a core component of any e-commerce platform, enabling multi-item purchases and thoughtful decision-making.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User adds a product to the cart. Response: The system provides a shopping cart during online purchase and allows the user to add products.
Stimulus: User removes a product from the cart. Response: The system allows the user to remove products from the shopping cart.
functionalRequirements: The system shall provide shopping cart during online purchase.
The system shall allow user to add/remove products in the shopping cart.

systemFeatures.name: Multiple Shipping Methods
systemFeatures.description: This feature offers customers a choice of various shipping methods, providing flexibility and catering to diverse delivery needs during the checkout process. By displaying shipping options, charges, and tentative durations, the **e-store** enhances transparency and allows customers to select the most suitable delivery service for their purchase. Priority: High. Offering multiple shipping options is crucial for meeting customer expectations regarding speed and cost.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User proceeds to checkout. Response: The system displays different shipping options provided by the shipping department.
Stimulus: User selects a shipping method. Response: The system enables the user to select the shipping method during the payment process.
Stimulus: User views selected shipping method. Response: The system displays the shipping charges and tentative duration for shipping.
functionalRequirements: The system shall display different shipping options provided by shipping department.
The system shall enable user to select the shipping method during payment process.
The system shall display the shipping charges.
The system shall display tentative duration for shipping.

systemFeatures.name: Online Shipment Tracking
systemFeatures.description: The **e-store** provides online tracking capabilities, allowing customers to monitor the status and location of their shipments by entering order information. This feature enhances customer confidence and reduces inquiries by providing real-time updates on delivery progress. Priority: High. Shipment tracking is a standard and highly valued service in online retail, improving transparency and reducing customer anxiety.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User enters order information for tracking. Response: The system allows the user to enter the order information for tracking.
Stimulus: User requests tracking information. Response: The system displays the current tracking information about the order.
functionalRequirements: The system shall allow user to enter the order information for tracking.
The system shall display the current tracking information about the order.

systemFeatures.name: Online Tax Calculations
systemFeatures.description: This feature ensures accurate and transparent tax calculations for all orders processed through the **e-store**. By automatically determining and displaying applicable taxes, the system provides customers with a clear understanding of the total cost, preventing surprises at checkout. Priority: High. Accurate tax calculation is a legal and operational necessity for any e-commerce business.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User adds items to cart or proceeds to checkout. Response: The system calculates tax for the order and displays tax information for the order.
functionalRequirements: The system shall calculate tax for the order.
The system shall display tax information for the order.

systemFeatures.name: Multiple Payment Methods
systemFeatures.description: The **e-store** supports a variety of payment methods, offering customers flexibility and convenience during the checkout process. By displaying available options and allowing users to select their preferred method, the system caters to a wider audience and enhances the overall purchasing experience. Priority: High. Diverse payment options are critical for minimizing cart abandonment and maximizing sales.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User proceeds to payment. Response: The system displays available payment methods for payment.
Stimulus: User selects a payment method. Response: The system allows the user to select the payment method for the order.
functionalRequirements: The system shall display available payment methods for payment.
The system shall allow user to select the payment method for order.

systemFeatures.name: Online Order Change or Cancellation
systemFeatures.description: This feature provides customers with the flexibility to modify or cancel eligible orders online, improving customer satisfaction and reducing the need for direct customer service interventions. The system clearly indicates which orders can be altered and ensures that users are notified of any changes made, promoting transparency and control. Priority: Medium. The ability to manage orders post-purchase is a significant convenience for customers.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User accesses their order history. Response: The system displays the orders that are eligible to change or cancel.
Stimulus: User selects an order to change. Response: The system allows the user to select the order to be changed and allows changes to shipping or payment method.
Stimulus: User selects an order to cancel. Response: The system allows the user to cancel the order.
Stimulus: User confirms changes to an order. Response: The system notifies the user about any changes made to the order.
functionalRequirements: The system shall display the orders that are eligible to change.
The system shall allow user to select the order to be changed.
The system shall allow user to cancel the order.
The system shall allow user to change shipping, payment method.
The system shall notify the user about any changes made to the order.

systemFeatures.name: Online Product Reviews and Ratings
systemFeatures.description: The **e-store** enables users to contribute and view product reviews and ratings, fostering a community-driven environment that aids purchasing decisions. This feature builds trust and provides valuable feedback for both potential buyers and the business, enriching the product pages with authentic user experiences. Priority: Medium. User-generated content like reviews is highly influential in online retail.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User selects a product. Response: The system displays the reviews and ratings of each product.
Stimulus: User submits a review or rating. Response: The system enables the user to enter their reviews and ratings for a product.
functionalRequirements: The system shall display the reviews and ratings of each product, when it is selected.
The system shall enable the user to enter their reviews and ratings.

systemFeatures.name: Financing Options
systemFeatures.description: The **e-store** offers various financing options, making high-value products more accessible to a broader customer base and encouraging larger purchases. By displaying available options and managing user requests, this feature improves purchasing power and financial flexibility for customers. Priority: Medium. Offering financing can significantly increase sales conversion for expensive items.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User views product or proceeds to checkout. Response: The system displays all available financing options.
Stimulus: User selects a financing option. Response: The system allows the user to select the financing option.
Stimulus: User submits a financing request. Response: The system notifies the user about the financing request status.
functionalRequirements: The system shall display all the available financing options.
The system shall allow user to select the financing option.
The system shall notify the use about the financing request.

systemFeatures.name: Detailed Sitemap Provision
systemFeatures.description: The **e-store** provides a detailed sitemap to enhance navigability and improve search engine optimization, making it easier for users to discover content and for search engines to index the site. This feature ensures comprehensive access to all sections of the website, improving overall user experience and site discoverability. Priority: Low. A sitemap is a key component for website structure and accessibility.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User requests to view the sitemap. Response: The system allows the user to view a detailed sitemap of the **e-store**.
functionalRequirements: The system shall allow user to view detailed sitemap.

systemFeatures.name: Online Promotions and Rewards
systemFeatures.description: This feature enables the **e-store** to offer various online promotions and rewards, stimulating sales and fostering customer loyalty. By displaying available promotions and allowing users to select them, the system can dynamically engage customers with incentives, driving repeat business and enhancing perceived value. Priority: Medium. Promotional tools are essential for marketing and sales growth in e-commerce.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User browses products or views cart. Response: The system displays all available promotions to the user.
Stimulus: User selects an available promotion. Response: The system allows the user to select an available promotion to apply to their order.
functionalRequirements: The system shall display all the available promotions to the user.
The system shall allow user to select available promotion.

systemFeatures.name: Online Product Purchase
systemFeatures.description: The **e-store** facilitates the complete online purchase process, allowing users to confirm their selection and securely enter payment information to finalize transactions. This core feature integrates all preceding steps—from product selection to payment—into a seamless workflow, enabling successful product acquisition for the customer. Priority: High. This is the ultimate goal of an e-commerce platform, enabling direct sales.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User proceeds to finalize purchase. Response: The system allows the user to confirm the purchase.
Stimulus: User enters payment details. Response: The system enables the user to enter the payment information securely.
functionalRequirements: The system shall allow user to confirm the purchase.
The system shall enable user to enter the payment information.

performanceRequirements: The **e-store** product shall be optimized for web-based access, with initial load times dependent on the user’s internet connection strength and the media from which it is run. The system’s responsiveness shall also be influenced by the client’s hardware components. The overall response time for loading the product page should take no longer than five minutes under typical network conditions, ensuring an acceptable user experience.

The back-end internal computers shall provide storage of all databases on redundant servers with automatic switchover capabilities to ensure continuous availability. Database replication to off-site storage locations shall be implemented, leveraging **RAID V Disk Stripping** on all database storage disks to enhance data integrity and access speeds. These measures collectively aim to deliver robust and efficient data handling.
safetyRequirements: The **e-store** system shall implement robust data validation and error handling mechanisms across all user input fields to prevent data corruption and maintain database integrity. Critical operations, such as order finalization and payment processing, shall include confirmation steps to mitigate accidental user actions, thereby preventing unintended financial transactions or order placements. All system logs containing sensitive operational data shall be securely stored and accessible only to authorized personnel, preventing unauthorized access and potential misuse.

In the event of system failures or data loss, the **e-store** shall ensure a well-defined disaster recovery plan is in place, including regular backups of all critical data to geographically diverse locations. The system shall employ transactional integrity controls for all financial operations, guaranteeing that either all steps of a transaction are completed successfully, or none are, preventing partial updates that could lead to inconsistent states. Furthermore, any external system integrations, particularly with payment gateways or shipping partners, shall adhere to secure communication protocols to prevent data interception or manipulation during transit.
securityRequirements: The **e-store** system shall employ **Secure Sockets Layer (SSL)** or **Transport Layer Security (TLS)** encryption for all transactions involving confidential customer information, protecting data during transfer over the internet. To prevent unauthorized access, the system shall automatically log out all customers after a predefined period of inactivity. All transactions initiated by the user will be explicitly confirmed with the customer’s web browser, adding an extra layer of user verification.

The system shall ensure that no cookies containing user passwords or any other confidential customer information are left on the customer’s computer post-session. When displaying sensitive data such as passwords, the customer’s web browser shall always echo special characters (e.g., asterisks) instead of the actual characters typed. Similarly, credit card numbers, once retrieved from the database, shall only display the last four digits to the customer.

On the back-end, customer passwords shall never be displayed in plain text to administrators; they may only be reset. The system’s back-end servers, which manage critical operational data, shall only be accessible to duly authenticated administrators with appropriate privilege levels. All back-end databases containing sensitive customer and transactional data shall be encrypted at rest, providing a strong defense against unauthorized data breaches. Regular security audits and penetration testing will be conducted to identify and remediate potential vulnerabilities.
softwareQualityAttributes: **Usability**: The **e-store** shall provide a uniform graphical user interface (GUI) across all web pages, featuring consistent navigation, iconography, and digital imagery for each product to enhance intuitiveness. It shall support accessibility features, including multi-language support and considerations for handicap access, ensuring a broad user base can effectively interact with the system.

**Reliability & Availability**: The system will guarantee high reliability through the use of redundant back-end internal computers for database storage with automatic failover capabilities. Databases will be replicated to off-site storage locations, employing **RAID V Disk Stripping** for enhanced data integrity and availability. A contractual agreement with an Internet Service Provider (ISP) for **T3 access** with 99.9999% availability will ensure continuous online presence for the **e-store**.

**Supportability**: The **e-store**'s source code shall be maintained within a robust configuration management tool, facilitating efficient version control, collaboration, and simplified maintenance. This ensures that the system can be easily updated, debugged, and extended by development and support teams. Comprehensive logging and monitoring capabilities will be implemented to assist in diagnosing issues and proactively managing system health, enhancing overall supportability.

**Maintainability**: The system shall be developed using standard web page development tools and adhere to established design conventions, such as IBM’s CUA or Microsoft’s GUI standards, to ensure code consistency and ease of future modifications. This approach, combined with clear documentation and modular architecture, will significantly reduce the effort required for maintenance and future enhancements, allowing for more agile adaptation to evolving business needs and technological advancements.
businessRules: All product pricing displayed on the **e-store** shall be inclusive of any applicable taxes at the point of sale, clearly itemized during the checkout process to ensure full transparency for the customer. Discounts and promotions offered through the system must be applied only once per eligible order and cannot be combined unless explicitly stated in the promotional terms. The system shall enforce a minimum order value for certain promotions or shipping methods as defined by Marvel Electronics and Home Entertainment's business policies.

Order cancellation eligibility shall be determined by the order's current processing status; orders already shipped or in advanced fulfillment stages will not be eligible for online cancellation. Customer reviews and ratings submitted to the **e-store** must undergo a moderation process to ensure compliance with content guidelines, preventing the publication of inappropriate or misleading information. All financing options presented to customers will be subject to approval by the associated third-party credit management system, with terms and conditions clearly communicated prior to application submission.

otherRequirements: **Licensing Requirements**: The **e-store** system shall adhere to all licensing agreements for any third-party software components, tools, or libraries utilized in its development and operation. Compliance with these licenses is paramount to ensure legal operation and avoid intellectual property infringements.

**Legal, Copyright, and Other Notices**: The **e-store** platform shall prominently display all disclaimers, copyright notices, word marks, trademarks, and product warranties pertinent to Marvel Electronics and Home Entertainment. This ensures legal compliance and clearly communicates legal ownership and product guarantees to users.

**Applicable Standards**: All aspects of the **e-store** system's design, development, and operation shall conform to relevant industry standards and best practices. This includes web accessibility standards, data protection regulations (e.g., GDPR, CCPA if applicable), and secure coding guidelines, ensuring a high-quality, compliant, and robust product.

glossary.definition: A product available from a catalogue that can be customized by the user.
Frequently Asked Questions, providing answers to common customer inquiries.
Customer Relationship Management, a system for managing a company's interactions with current and potential customers.
Redundant Array of Inexpensive Disks/Drives, a data storage virtualization technology that combines multiple physical disk drive components into a single logical unit.
Hypertext Transfer Protocol, the foundation of data communication for the World Wide Web.
Transmission Control Protocol/Internet Protocol, a suite of communication protocols used to interconnect network devices on the internet.
Graphical User Interface, a type of user interface that allows users to interact with electronic devices through graphical icons and visual indicators.
Secure Sockets Layer, a deprecated cryptographic protocol designed to provide communications security over a computer network. Modern systems use TLS.
Transport Layer Security, a cryptographic protocol designed to provide communications security over a computer network, superseding SSL.

flowchartDiagram.syntaxExplanation: Flowcharts illustrate processes and decision points. Nodes are identified by alphanumeric IDs. Labels with spaces are double-quoted. Arrows indicate flow direction. 'TD' specifies a top-down flow, 'LR' specifies left-to-right. Reserved words like 'end', 'subgraph', 'class' are not used as Node IDs, instead generic identifiers are preferred.
flowchartDiagram.caption: e-store Product Configuration and Purchase Flow

sequenceDiagram.syntaxExplanation: Sequence diagrams illustrate the temporal order of interactions between participants. Participants are declared with 'participant' and can have aliases. Messages are shown with arrows, and `->>+` denotes an asynchronous call with activation, while `-->>-` denotes an asynchronous response with deactivation. Lifelines represent the existence of a participant over time. `alt` blocks represent alternative paths in a sequence. Activations should be balanced.
sequenceDiagram.code:
sequenceDiagram
  participant User
  participant WebUI as "e-store Web UI"
  participant ProductCatalog as "Product Catalog Service"
  participant ShoppingCart as "Shopping Cart Service"
  participant OrderService as "Order Management Service"
  participant PaymentGateway as "Payment Gateway"
  participant ShippingService as "Shipping Service"

  User->>+WebUI: Browse products
  WebUI->>+ProductCatalog: Request product list
  ProductCatalog-->>-WebUI: Return product data
  WebUI->>User: Display products

  User->>+WebUI: Add configurable product "Product X" to cart
  WebUI->>ProductCatalog: Get product details and configuration options
  ProductCatalog-->>WebUI: Return product config details
  WebUI->>User: Display configuration interface
  User->>WebUI: Submit configured product "Config A"

  WebUI->>+ShoppingCart: Add item "Config A" to cart
  ShoppingCart-->>-WebUI: Cart updated with "Config A"
  WebUI->>User: Show updated cart

  User->>+WebUI: Proceed to Checkout
  WebUI->>User: Display login/registration or shipping form

  alt User is Guest
    User->>+WebUI: Enter guest shipping details
  else User is Logged In
    WebUI->>+OrderService: Retrieve saved addresses
    OrderService-->>-WebUI: Return saved addresses
    User->>WebUI: Select shipping address
  end

  WebUI->>+ShippingService: Calculate shipping options and costs
  ShippingService-->>-WebUI: Return options
  WebUI->>User: Display shipping options
  User->>WebUI: Select shipping option "Express"

  WebUI->>+WebUI: Display payment form
  User->>WebUI: Enter payment details (Credit Card)

  WebUI->>+PaymentGateway: Authorize payment "$199.99"
  PaymentGateway-->>-WebUI: Payment authorization success

  WebUI->>+OrderService: Create new order with "Config A" and "Express" shipping
  OrderService-->>-WebUI: Order ID E-ORDER-001 created

  WebUI->>+ShippingService: Initiate shipment for E-ORDER-001
  ShippingService-->>-WebUI: Shipment initiated

  WebUI->>User: Display Order Confirmation (E-ORDER-001)
  WebUI-->>-User: Send Order Confirmation Email
sequenceDiagram.caption: e-store Online Product Purchase Sequence

entityRelationshipDiagram.syntaxExplanation: ER diagrams define entities, their attributes, and relationships in a database schema. Entities are capitalized. Attributes specify their type and key constraints (PK for Primary Key, FK for Foreign Key, UK for Unique Key). Relationships denote cardinality using specific symbols: '||--o{ ' for one-to-many (optional many), '}||--||' for one-to-one (mandatory), and '}||--||' for many-to-one (mandatory one). Relationship labels are descriptive and enclosed in double quotes. Attribute names are alphanumeric.
entityRelationshipDiagram.code:
erDiagram
  USER ||--o{ ORDER : "places"
  ORDER ||--|{ ORDER_ITEM : "contains"
  PRODUCT }|--o{ ORDER_ITEM : "includes"
  USER }|--o{ REVIEW : "writes"
  PRODUCT }|--o{ REVIEW : "has"
  USER ||--o{ SHIPPING_ADDRESS : "has"
  USER ||--o{ PAYMENT_METHOD : "uses"
  CATEGORY }|--o{ PRODUCT : "categorizes"

  USER {
    int user_id PK
    string email UK
    string password_hash
    string first_name
    string last_name
    datetime created_at
  }

  ORDER {
    int order_id PK
    int user_id FK
    datetime order_date
    decimal total_amount
    string status
    int shipping_address_id FK
    int payment_method_id FK
  }

  PRODUCT {
    int product_id PK
    string name
    string description
    decimal price
    int category_id FK
    string image_url
    boolean configurable
  }

  ORDER_ITEM {
    int order_item_id PK
    int order_id FK
    int product_id FK
    int quantity
    decimal unit_price
    string configuration_details "JSON for custom config"
  }

  REVIEW {
    int review_id PK
    int product_id FK
    int user_id FK
    int rating
    string comment
    datetime review_date
  }

  SHIPPING_ADDRESS {
    int address_id PK
    int user_id FK
    string address_line1
    string address_line2
    string city
    string state
    string postal_code
    string country
  }

  PAYMENT_METHOD {
    int payment_method_id PK
    int user_id FK
    string method_type "e.g., Credit Card, PayPal"
    string last_four_digits
    datetime expiry_date
    string billing_address
  }

  CATEGORY {
    int category_id PK
    string category_name UK
    string description
  }
entityRelationshipDiagram.caption: e-store Core Entities and Relationships

introduction.purpose: This document formally defines the software requirements for the **rlcs** (Interstate-15 Reversible Lane Control System) Application. It outlines the essential functionalities, performance criteria, and interface specifications necessary for the development of a robust and reliable system. The primary goal is to ensure safe and efficient management of reversible traffic lanes, contributing to enhanced regional traffic flow and public safety.
introduction.documentConventions: This Software Requirements Specification (SRS) adheres strictly to the guidelines set forth in IEEE 830-1998, 'Recommended Practice for Software Requirements Specifications,' ensuring a comprehensive and standardized documentation approach. All functional and non-functional requirements are presented with clear, unambiguous language. Specific terminology and abbreviations are defined in the Glossary (Appendix A) to maintain consistency and clarity throughout the document.
introduction.intendedAudience: This document is primarily intended for the development team responsible for designing, implementing, and testing the **rlcs** Application software. It also serves as a critical reference for project managers, quality assurance personnel, and technical stakeholders within the Department of Transportation. Furthermore, it provides essential information for system operators and maintenance staff to understand the system's capabilities and operational parameters.
introduction.productScope: The **rlcs** Application software is designed to manage and control reversible lanes, primarily for peak traffic hours and special events on Interstate 15. This system will replace an existing, aging, and non-expandable application, integrating with new controller devices and field device interface cards. The scope includes a graphical user interface, process monitoring and control, sequencing of lane operations, data processing with security, and comprehensive reporting capabilities.
references: IEEE 830-1998 IEEE Recommended Practice for Software Requirements Specifications.
ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018 Systems and software engineering — Life cycle processes — Requirements engineering.
I-15 Reversible Lane Control System Existing Systems Documentation Report (Caltrans, June 1998).
I-15 Reversible Lane Control System System Requirements Document – Version D (Caltrans, March 2002).
I-15 Reversible Lane Control System Project – User Requirements Document – Version D (Caltrans, December 1998).
I-15 Reversible Lane Control System Feasibility Study Report (VIP, July 2001).
IEEE /EIA 12207.1 – 1997: Guide for Information Technology Software Life Cycle Processes – Life Cycle Data (IEEE SESC, 1997).

overallDescription.productPerspective: The **rlcs** Application software is a critical component within a larger traffic management ecosystem, serving as a direct replacement for an existing legacy system. It operates as a self-contained product responsible for the control and monitoring of reversible lanes on Interstate 15. The new system is designed to integrate seamlessly with updated hardware and contribute data to broader traffic intelligence platforms. This replacement initiative aims to overcome the limitations of the previous system, including its age and lack of expandability, by introducing modern controller devices and enhanced interfacing capabilities.
productFunctions: Graphical User Interface (GUI) for operator interaction and system visualization.
Process Control and Monitoring of all field devices and lane configurations.
Sequencing of opening, closing, and special event operations for reversible lanes.
Data Processing and Security to manage system data, user authentication, and audit logs.
Reporting module for generating operational summaries, event logs, and compliance reports.

userClassesAndCharacteristics.characteristics: Operators are highly trained individuals responsible for the real-time command and control of the **rlcs** during normal operational modes. They possess the authority to change the system mode and execute predefined operational sequences. Only one operator may be logged into the system at any given time to ensure singular control and accountability for critical lane management functions.

Management personnel require read-only access to the **rlcs** for oversight and strategic planning. Their primary interaction involves reviewing system status, event logs, and various reports to assess operational efficiency and compliance. This user class does not have direct control over reversible lane operations but relies on accurate data for decision-making processes.

Field Maintenance Staff are authorized to control the **rlcs** exclusively during maintenance mode from designated workstations or remote dial-in terminals. They possess specialized technical expertise for troubleshooting and repair of field devices. This user class is strictly prohibited from altering the system's operational mode, ensuring that maintenance activities do not inadvertently affect active traffic management.

The Electrical Systems staff requires access to the **rlcs** for reporting and status checking to diagnose hardware-related issues. Their role involves monitoring the health and performance of the electrical components and field devices. They do not possess command and control capabilities but are critical for maintaining the physical infrastructure of the reversible lane system.

Software Systems staff require access for reporting and status checking purposes, focusing on software performance, error logs, and system diagnostics. They are responsible for the ongoing maintenance, updates, and troubleshooting of the **rlcs** Application. Like Electrical Systems staff, they do not directly control lane operations but ensure the software's continuous and correct functioning.

System Administrators are members of the District 11 technical staff, typically from Software Systems, with elevated privileges. They are authorized to create, modify, and manage system configuration data, including user accounts, device parameters, and scheduled operations. System Administrators can also change system modes and are responsible for the overall security setup and integrity of the **rlcs** Application.

overallDescription.operatingEnvironment: The **rlcs** Application software will operate on a robust hardware platform designed to ensure continuous availability and performance. This platform must be compatible with either **Microsoft Windows NT** or **Unix-based operating systems** to accommodate diverse infrastructure strategies. The system will leverage a private network for secure and efficient communication between its workstations and intelligent controllers, minimizing external vulnerabilities.
designAndImplementationConstraints: The **rlcs** Application must operate within hardware limitations primarily dictated by the chosen intelligent controller, with particular attention to memory constraints for the **2070 controller** as specified in the TEES document. All application activity must be meticulously logged and stored in audit files, ensuring traceability and accountability for all operations. The system's architecture will emphasize the use of high-level programming languages to facilitate cost-effective maintenance and enhance long-term supportability, without specific language mandates. The software is constrained by the strict safety requirements inherent in managing reversible lanes, demanding fail-safe mechanisms to prevent catastrophic events such as opposing traffic flows.
userDocumentation: The **rlcs** Application will be delivered with a comprehensive suite of user documentation to support various user classes. This includes a detailed User Manual providing step-by-step instructions for system operation, configuration, and reporting functionalities. An online Help system will offer context-sensitive assistance for operators and administrators. Additionally, a Quick Reference Guide will be provided for common operational sequences and emergency procedures, ensuring efficient system utilization and rapid response capabilities.
assumptionsAndDependencies: The **rlcs** project assumes the successful deployment of a robust **Fiber Optic Network** along the I-15 Reversible Lane corridor, which will serve as the primary communication backbone. While the primary communication will be fiber, secondary communication through **ISDN** is assumed to provide failover capabilities that are transparent to the **rlcs** application. Communication between the FCU (Field Control Unit) and DCU (Device Control Unit) locations is dependent on existing copper infrastructure, maintaining current field connectivity. Remote access to the **rlcs** is assumed to be facilitated via a secure dial-up connection, requiring appropriate network configurations and security protocols to be in place.

externalInterfaceRequirements.userInterfaces: The **rlcs** Application will present a sophisticated Graphical User Interface (**GUI**) designed for intuitive and efficient operator interaction. The GUI shall adhere to established usability standards, featuring clear visual representations of system status, device configurations, and operational sequences. Standard elements such as navigation menus, control buttons, data display panels, and alert indicators will be consistently implemented across all screens. The interface will provide real-time feedback on commands issued and system responses received, ensuring operators have a comprehensive understanding of lane status.
externalInterfaceRequirements.hardwareInterfaces: The **rlcs** Application software shall interface with a variety of intelligent controllers and field devices through specialized Input/Output (**I/O**) cards. These hardware interfaces include Manual Control Units (**MCU**), Barrier Gates, Draw Lights, Wrong Way Lights, Entrance Pop-Ups (EL-1 through EL-5, ET-1), Wrong Way Pop-Ups (WL-1 through WL-5, WT-1), and Changeable Message Signs (**CMS 1-4**). The software will utilize dedicated I/O driver software to send control commands to these devices and receive real-time status feedback. Compatibility with whichever intelligent controller is selected for the system is a critical requirement, ensuring seamless integration and control over the diverse field infrastructure.
externalInterfaceRequirements.softwareInterfaces: The **rlcs** Application will interact with several critical software components to fulfill its operational requirements. These interfaces include the underlying Workstation Operating System (**O/S**), Network Operating System (**O/S**), and Controller Operating System (**O/S**), which provide the foundational execution environment. The system will integrate with a **database management system** for persistent data storage and retrieval, ensuring all operational data, configurations, and logs are securely maintained. A dedicated **reporting tool** will also interface with the **rlcs** Application to facilitate the generation of various operational and audit reports, enabling effective system analysis and oversight.
externalInterfaceRequirements.communicationsInterfaces: The **rlcs** Application requires robust communication interfaces to ensure reliable data exchange both internally and with authorized external systems. A one-way data transfer mechanism will provide access to system status data for external systems, such as **ATMS-2**, **ValuePricing/FasTrak**, and **ATIS**, through a secure firewall. This transfer will occur approximately every 30 seconds to an external server data store, ensuring timely updates for public consumption. A separate one-way serial data transfer capability will also be provided for specific device communication needs. Internally, **rlcs** workstations and controllers will communicate over a dedicated private network utilizing **fiber**, **Cat 5 wiring**, **leased lines**, and **dial-up lines**, with the TMC communicating with DCU controllers via FCU controllers. Wireless connections are explicitly excluded due to security and interference considerations.

systemFeatures.name: Graphical User Interface (GUI)
systemFeatures.description: The Graphical User Interface (GUI) provides operators with a centralized, visual platform for monitoring and controlling the **rlcs**. It enhances user experience by presenting complex system information in an intuitive format, allowing for quick comprehension of lane status, device states, and operational sequences. From a technical standpoint, the GUI acts as the primary interface layer, abstracting the underlying complexity of hardware interactions and data processing. It ensures that all operational commands are safely validated and accurately transmitted, improving both efficiency and safety in lane management. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Operator logs into the **rlcs** GUI. Response: The system validates credentials and displays the main dashboard with current lane status and device states.
Stimulus: Operator selects a specific field device for status inquiry. Response: The system retrieves and displays the real-time operational status and historical data for the selected device.
Stimulus: Operator attempts to initiate an unauthorized action. Response: The system displays an 'Access Denied' message and logs the attempted action for audit purposes.
Stimulus: Operator requests an overview of active operational sequences. Response: The system presents a summary of all running sequences, their progress, and any pending actions.
functionalRequirements: The system shall provide secure operator login and logout functionalities, adhering to defined authentication protocols.
The system shall display a real-time, graphical representation of all reversible lanes, showing their current status (open, closed, transitional).
The system shall present the operational status of all interconnected field devices, including gates, lights, and pop-ups.
The system shall allow operators to query the status of individual field devices.
The system shall provide visual and audible alerts for critical system events or device failures.
The system shall display historical event logs and operational data through the GUI.
The system shall restrict access to specific commands and configurations based on the authenticated operator's role and privileges.

systemFeatures.name: Process Monitoring and Control
systemFeatures.description: Process Monitoring and Control is central to the safe and efficient operation of the **rlcs**, allowing for constant oversight and precise management of all field devices. This feature directly addresses the user need for reliable and responsive control over reversible lane infrastructure, preventing unsafe conditions and optimizing traffic flow. Technically, it involves continuous polling of device statuses, validation of operational commands against safety rules, and the execution of control signals to field hardware. This tight integration ensures that the physical state of the lanes accurately reflects the commanded state, thereby enhancing public safety and system integrity. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Operator issues a command to change a barrier gate status. Response: The system validates the command against safety rules, transmits it to the relevant controller, and updates the GUI with the device's new status.

Stimulus: A field device reports an unexpected status change or failure. Response: The system detects the anomaly, logs the event, triggers an alarm in the GUI, and initiates a predefined fallback procedure.
Stimulus: The system performs a scheduled diagnostic check on a controller. Response: The system records the diagnostic results and alerts an operator if any irregularities are detected.
Stimulus: Field Maintenance Staff initiates a maintenance sequence. Response: The system verifies maintenance mode, executes the sequence, and prevents conflicting commands from normal operators.
functionalRequirements: The system shall continuously monitor the operational status of all connected field devices (gates, lights, pop-ups, CMS).
The system shall allow operators to issue direct commands to individual field devices, subject to safety interlocks.
The system shall implement safety logic to prevent conflicting or unsafe device commands (e.g., opening opposing lanes simultaneously).
The system shall detect and report abnormal device statuses or communication failures in real-time.
The system shall automatically log all device status changes and control commands.
The system shall provide override capabilities for emergency situations, accessible only by authorized personnel and subject to strict logging.
The system shall transmit control commands to field devices via I/O driver software and intelligent controllers.

systemFeatures.name: Sequencing
systemFeatures.description: The Sequencing feature enables automated execution of complex operational flows for opening, closing, or reconfiguring reversible lanes, significantly reducing manual effort and minimizing human error. Users benefit from predefined, validated sequences that ensure consistent and safe transitions across the lane system. From an architectural perspective, this involves orchestrating multiple device commands and status checks into a coherent, time-bound process. The system ensures each step of a sequence is completed and verified before proceeding, thereby upholding the critical safety requirements for reversible lane operations and maintaining operational efficiency. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Operator initiates a 'Morning Peak Opening' sequence. Response: The system loads the predefined sequence, begins execution by sending commands to activate relevant devices, and monitors progress.
Stimulus: A step within an active sequence fails to complete. Response: The system halts the sequence, notifies the operator of the failure, and offers options for manual intervention or rollback.

Stimulus: System Administrator creates a new special event lane configuration sequence. Response: The system saves the sequence, validates its steps for logical consistency and safety, and makes it available for future execution.
Stimulus: Operator requests to pause an ongoing sequence. Response: The system brings the sequence to a controlled halt at the next safe state, allowing for intervention or resumption.
functionalRequirements: The system shall allow System Administrators to define, modify, and store multiple operational sequences for lane opening, closing, and special events.
The system shall validate each step of a newly defined sequence for logical consistency and safety interlocks prior to saving.
The system shall allow authorized operators to initiate, pause, resume, and terminate predefined sequences.
The system shall execute sequence steps in the specified order, awaiting confirmation of successful completion for each critical step.
The system shall provide real-time status updates on the progress of any active sequence.
The system shall automatically handle and report failures during sequence execution, offering appropriate recovery options.
The system shall ensure that only one critical sequence (e.g., opposing lane opening) can be active at any given time.

systemFeatures.name: Data Processing and Security
systemFeatures.description: Data Processing and Security provides the foundational integrity and protection for all information within the **rlcs**, from operational parameters to user credentials and audit logs. Users are assured that their actions are recorded, configurations are protected, and access is strictly controlled, preventing unauthorized system manipulation or data loss. This feature encompasses data validation, encryption where appropriate, user authentication, authorization mechanisms, and comprehensive logging. Technically, it underpins all other system features by managing the secure flow and storage of critical traffic control data, making it indispensable for maintaining operational reliability and regulatory compliance. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Operator enters login credentials. Response: The system processes the credentials against stored, hashed data, and grants access based on successful authentication and assigned role.
Stimulus: An external system attempts to access secured **rlcs** network data. Response: The firewall blocks the unauthorized access attempt, and the system logs the security event.

Stimulus: System Administrator modifies system configuration data. Response: The system validates the changes, updates the database, and records the modification in an audit log with user and timestamp.
Stimulus: The system detects an integrity compromise in a critical data file. Response: The system flags the data as compromised, isolates it, and alerts the System Administrator.
functionalRequirements: The system shall implement robust user authentication mechanisms for all access points, including GUI and remote connections.
The system shall enforce role-based access control (RBAC) to restrict user actions based on their assigned privileges.
The system shall securely store all sensitive data, including user credentials and configuration parameters, using industry-standard encryption or hashing.
The system shall maintain comprehensive audit logs of all user actions, system events, and configuration changes, including timestamps and user identifiers.
The system shall ensure data integrity for all stored and transmitted operational data, detecting and reporting any unauthorized alterations.
The system shall facilitate secure, one-way data transfer to external systems through a firewall for public information dissemination.
The system shall protect its private network from unauthorized external access through appropriate network security measures.

systemFeatures.name: Reporting
systemFeatures.description: The Reporting feature provides a critical capability for analyzing **rlcs** operations, identifying trends, and ensuring compliance with regulatory and operational standards. Users, particularly Management and Maintenance staff, gain insights into system performance, event histories, and operator activities, supporting informed decision-making and accountability. Technically, this involves querying the central database, aggregating diverse data points, and generating customizable reports in various formats. It transforms raw operational data into actionable intelligence, enabling continuous improvement of reversible lane management and providing necessary documentation for audits. Priority: Medium.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Management requests a 'Daily Operational Summary' report. Response: The system queries historical data, compiles the summary, and presents it in a predefined format through the GUI.

Stimulus: Field Maintenance Staff filters the event log for all 'device failure' events in the last month. Response: The system retrieves and displays a chronological list of all relevant device failure events, including timestamps and affected devices.
Stimulus: Operator attempts to generate a report for which they lack permissions. Response: The system denies the request and informs the operator of insufficient privileges.
Stimulus: System Administrator schedules an 'Annual Audit Report' for automatic generation. Response: The system creates a scheduled task to generate and store the report at the specified interval.
functionalRequirements: The system shall generate comprehensive operational summary reports, including lane usage, device status changes, and sequence executions.
The system shall provide detailed event logs, filterable by date, event type, device, and user, for forensic analysis.
The system shall generate reports on system performance metrics, such as response times and uptime for critical components.
The system shall support the generation of custom reports based on user-defined criteria and data ranges.
The system shall allow reports to be exported in common formats (e.g., PDF, CSV) for external analysis.
The system shall restrict access to report generation and viewing based on user roles and data sensitivity.
The system shall enable System Administrators to schedule recurring report generation at predefined intervals.

performanceRequirements: The **rlcs** Application shall process and transmit critical field device control commands within 500 milliseconds, from operator input to device activation, to ensure real-time responsiveness. This requirement is paramount for safety-critical operations, minimizing latency in reversible lane changes and emergency responses.

The system shall update external system interfaces (e.g., **ATMS**, **ATIS**) with current lane status data every 30 seconds to provide timely information for public dissemination. This frequency is essential for maintaining accurate traveler information and supporting integrated traffic management strategies.

The Graphical User Interface (**GUI**) shall render and refresh all critical real-time status displays within 1 second of data receipt, ensuring operators have immediate visual feedback. Rapid GUI updates are crucial for effective monitoring and swift decision-making in dynamic traffic scenarios.

The system shall log all operational events and user actions to the audit trail database within 100 milliseconds of occurrence, maintaining a complete and accurate historical record. This low latency for logging is vital for forensic analysis, accountability, and compliance with regulatory requirements.

The system shall be capable of supporting up to 5 concurrent read-only users (Management, Electrical, Software Systems staff) without noticeable degradation in performance for primary operator functions. This ensures that oversight and diagnostic activities do not impede critical lane control operations.
safetyRequirements: The **rlcs** Application shall prevent the simultaneous opening of reversible lanes in opposing directions under all operational conditions, including system failures or operator error. This is a paramount safety requirement to avert catastrophic head-on collisions, safeguarding both human life and property.

The system shall detect the unintended lowering of any barrier gate across an otherwise open entrance and immediately trigger an audible and visual alarm. This safeguard is critical to prevent severe accidents and entrapment of vehicles, particularly at high-speed entry points.

The **rlcs** Application shall implement a multi-level interlocking mechanism for all lane change sequences, requiring positive confirmation of each step before proceeding to the next. This ensures that any device failure during a transition will halt the sequence, allowing for safe recovery and preventing partially configured hazardous states.

The system shall continuously monitor for control system integrity, including communication failures between controllers and field devices, and report any anomalies within 100 milliseconds. Rapid detection of integrity issues is essential for initiating emergency procedures or reverting to a safe, default state before a dangerous condition can fully develop.

In the event of a critical system failure (e.g., loss of communication with central control), the **rlcs** shall automatically revert all field devices to a predetermined safe state (e.g., all lanes closed, all warning lights active). This fail-safe mechanism ensures that public safety is prioritized even when the system cannot function optimally.
securityRequirements: The **rlcs** Application shall implement a robust user authentication system requiring unique usernames and strong passwords for all system access. This measure is fundamental to preventing unauthorized access and ensuring that only verified personnel can interact with the critical traffic control system.

The system shall employ role-based access control (**RBAC**) to restrict specific functionalities and data views based on the authenticated user's assigned role. This ensures that operators, maintenance staff, and administrators only have access to the privileges necessary for their duties, minimizing the risk of accidental or malicious misuse.

All sensitive data, including user credentials and configuration parameters, shall be stored using industry-standard hashing and encryption algorithms. This protects critical information from compromise, even if the underlying database is breached, thereby maintaining the integrity and confidentiality of system operations.

The **rlcs** Application shall establish and maintain a secure private network for internal communication between workstations and controllers, isolated from public networks. This isolation is vital to protect against external cyber threats and unauthorized remote intrusions, ensuring the operational stability of the traffic control infrastructure.

Remote access to the **rlcs** Application shall only be permitted via a secure dial-up connection, incorporating virtual private network (**VPN**) technology and multi-factor authentication. This provides a secure tunnel for authorized off-site personnel, mitigating risks associated with external network exposure while allowing necessary remote management capabilities.
softwareQualityAttributes: Availability: The **rlcs** Application shall be available 24 hours per day, 7 days per week, 365 days per year, with a targeted uptime of 99.99%. This extremely high availability is critical due to the continuous nature of traffic management and the severe consequences of system downtime on public safety and regional commerce.

Maintainability: The software architecture and codebase shall be designed for high maintainability, allowing for expedient diagnosis, repair, and modification by a single, competent software engineer within 4 hours. This is achieved through clear modularity, comprehensive documentation, and adherence to established coding standards, minimizing operational disruption and long-term support costs.

Reliability: The **rlcs** Application shall operate without unrecoverable errors or system crashes for a minimum Mean Time Between Failures (**MTBF**) of 1 year. This high reliability is crucial to ensure consistent, uninterrupted control over reversible lanes, as system failures can lead to dangerous traffic conditions and operational inefficiencies.

Testability: The software shall be designed to facilitate comprehensive and automated testing of all functional requirements and safety-critical interlocks. This ensures that new features or modifications can be thoroughly validated before deployment, reducing the risk of introducing defects into a live, safety-critical system.

Usability: The Graphical User Interface (**GUI**) shall be intuitive for trained operators, requiring no more than 4 hours of dedicated training for basic operational proficiency. This ensures that operators can quickly and effectively manage reversible lanes, particularly during high-pressure situations, minimizing errors due to complex interfaces.

Robustness: The **rlcs** Application shall gracefully handle unexpected inputs, device malfunctions, and communication interruptions without crashing or entering an unsafe state. The system must recover automatically from transient errors and provide clear diagnostic information, ensuring continuous operation even under adverse conditions.
businessRules: Only one operator shall be actively logged into the **rlcs** Application GUI at any given time to maintain singular control and accountability for critical lane operations.
All operational sequences for reversible lane changes (opening, closing) must be predefined and validated for safety by a System Administrator prior to execution.
Any command or sequence that could potentially lead to conflicting traffic flows in reversible lanes shall be automatically rejected by the system's safety interlock logic.
Audit logs for all user actions and system events shall be immutable and retained for a minimum of five years for regulatory compliance and forensic analysis.
Field Maintenance Staff are authorized to control devices only when the **rlcs** is in 'maintenance mode', which must be explicitly set by an Operator or System Administrator.

otherRequirements: The **rlcs** Application shall not be apportioned across multiple releases; the complete set of specified requirements will be delivered in a single, comprehensive release. This ensures that all interdependent functionalities are developed and deployed cohesively, eliminating compatibility issues and ensuring full system capability from initial deployment.

glossary.definition: Advanced Traveler Information System; an external system receiving data from **rlcs**.
Advanced Traffic Management System; an external system receiving data from **rlcs**.
California Department of Transportation; the administering agency for the **rlcs** project.
Changeable Message Sign; field devices controlled by **rlcs** to display traffic messages.
Commercial Off-The-Shelf; refers to readily available commercial software components.
Device Control Unit; controllers responsible for interfacing with individual field devices.
Field Control Unit; controllers that manage communication between the TMC and DCU controllers.
Graphical User Interface; the primary visual interface for operators to interact with the **rlcs** Application.
Input/Output Card; hardware interfaces between the intelligent controller and field devices.
Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers standard for Software Requirements Specifications.
Integrated Services Digital Network; a communication technology used as a secondary link for **rlcs**.
Manual Control Unit; a field device providing localized manual control capabilities.
Mean Time Between Failures; a measure of system reliability indicating average operational time between failures.
Interstate-15 Reversible Lane Control System; the application software being specified in this document.
Software Requirements Specification; the document detailing the requirements for the **rlcs** Application.
Transportation Electrical Equipment Specification; a document providing specifications for transportation electrical equipment, including the 2070 controller.
Traffic Management Center; the central facility where the **rlcs** operators are located.
An external system related to managed lanes and tolling, receiving data from **rlcs**.
Vendor Information Packet; referenced in the Feasibility Study Report.

flowchartDiagram.syntaxExplanation: The flowchart uses standard Mermaid syntax to represent a process flow. Nodes are defined with unique alphanumeric IDs and descriptive labels. Node shapes indicate different process steps. Arrows depict the flow of control or data between nodes. `TD` indicates a top-down flow direction. Labels with spaces or special characters are enclosed in double-quotes.
flowchartDiagram.code:
flowchart TD
    TOPM["Traffic Operations Program Management"] --> Report
    RLCSOM["rlcs Operations & Maintenance"] --> GUI
    RLCSOM --> Report
    TravelP["Traveling Public"] --> GUI
    HOVDev["HOV Field Devices"] -- "Status" --> ProcMon
    HOVDev -- "RLCS Data" --> Seq
    ExtSys["External Systems (ValuePricing, ATMS-2, Travelers Info)"] --> ProcMon
    ExtSys --> DataSec

    GUI("Graphical User Interface") -- "Macro commands/Sequencing" --> Seq
    GUI -- "Commands" --> ProcMon
    GUI -- "RLCS Data" --> DataSec

    ProcMon("Process Control & Monitoring") -- "Commands" --> HOVDev
    ProcMon -- "Status and Event Data" --> DataSec
    ProcMon -- "Current System Status" --> GUI

    Seq("Sequencing") -- "Macro Commands" --> ProcMon
    Seq -- "RLCS Data" --> DataSec

    DataSec("Data Processing and Security") -- "RLCS Data" --> GUI
    DataSec -- "Device and Security Data" --> HOVDev
    DataSec -- "RLCS Data" --> Seq
    DataSec -- "Current System Status" --> ProcMon
    DataSec -- "RLCS Data" --> Report

    Report("COTS Reporting") -- "Current Command Sum'ry, Event Log, Current System Status, Schedule, Failure Summary, Other Management Reports, PIER Report" --> TOPM
    Report -- "RLCS Data" --> RLCSOM
flowchartDiagram.caption: rlcs Product Functions Data Flow Overview

sequenceDiagram.syntaxExplanation: This sequence diagram illustrates the temporal interactions between different participants in the system. Participants are declared with unique IDs and optional aliases. Messages flow between participants, showing the order of operations. Arrows denote synchronous (->>) or asynchronous (->) communication, and `+` and `-` indicate activation and deactivation of lifelines. `alt` blocks represent alternative paths based on conditions. All participant aliases are unquoted, and message labels avoid special characters.
sequenceDiagram.code:
sequenceDiagram
    participant O as Operator
    participant G as GUI
    participant D as "Data Processing & Security"
    participant P as "Process Monitoring & Control"
    participant FCU as "FCU Controller"
    participant FD as "Field Device"

    O->>+G: "Login Request (username, password)"
    G->>+D: "Verify Credentials (username, password)"
    D-->>-G: "Authentication Result (Success/Fail)"
    alt Authentication Success
        G->>O: "Display Dashboard"
        O->>G: "Select Device 'Gate 1'"
        G->>O: "Display Device Info & Control Options"
        O->>+G: "Issue Command 'Lower Gate'"
        G->>+D: "Validate Command 'Lower Gate' for 'Gate 1'"
        D-->>-G: "Validation Result (Success)"
        G->>+P: "Send Control Command 'Lower Gate' to 'Gate 1'"
        P->>+FCU: "Forward Control Command 'Lower Gate'"
        FCU->>FD: "Activate Lowering Mechanism"
        FD-->>FCU: "Lowering Complete / Status Update"
        FCU-->>-P: "Device 'Gate 1' Status: Lowered"
        P-->>-G: "Update GUI with 'Gate 1' Status: Lowered"
        G->>O: "Command 'Lower Gate' for 'Gate 1' Executed"
        D->>D: "Log Command Execution (Operator, Device, Action, Timestamp)"
    else Authentication Fail
        G->>O: "Display 'Invalid Credentials'"
    end
sequenceDiagram.caption: Operator Issues Device Control Command

entityRelationshipDiagram.syntaxExplanation: The ER Diagram models the relationships between entities within the **rlcs** system. Entities are declared with their attributes, including primary (PK), foreign (FK), and unique (UK) keys. Relationships between entities are defined with cardinality (e.g., `||--o{` for one-to-many, `||--|{` for one-to-many mandatory) and descriptive, quoted labels. Attribute names are alphanumeric and unquoted, while entity names with spaces are quoted.
entityRelationshipDiagram.code:
erDiagram
    User ||--|{ UserRole : "has role"
    User ||--o{ Command : "issues"
    User ||--o{ SystemEvent : "generates"
    User ||--o{ Report : "generates"
    User ||--o{ SystemConfiguration : "modifies"

    UserRole {
        int id PK
        string name UK
    }

    User {
        int id PK
        string username UK
        string password_hash
        string email
        int role_id FK
    }

    Device ||--o{ Command : "receives"
    Device ||--o{ SystemEvent : "causes"
    Device ||--o{ SequenceStep : "participates in"

    Device {
        int id PK
        string name UK
        string type
        string location
        string status
        int controller_id FK
    }

    Command {
        int id PK
        int user_id FK
        int device_id FK
        string command_type
        string status
        datetime timestamp
    }

    SystemEvent {
        int id PK
        string event_type
        string description
        datetime timestamp
        int user_id FK
        int device_id FK
    }

    Sequence ||--|{ SequenceStep : "contains"

    Sequence {
        int id PK
        string name UK
        string type
        string status
    }

    SequenceStep {
        int id PK
        int sequence_id FK
        int device_id FK
        int step_order
        string action
    }

    Report {
        int id PK
        string name UK
        int generated_by_user_id FK
        datetime generation_timestamp
        text report_data
    }

    SystemConfiguration {
        int id PK
        string param_name UK
        string param_value
        int last_modified_by_user_id FK
        datetime last_modified_timestamp
    }
entityRelationshipDiagram.caption: rlcs Core System Entity Relationship Diagram

tbdList: R-REQ-TBD-001: Specific performance metrics for report generation times are TBD, pending database design and data volume analysis.
