The **pnnl** system shall provide a capability for users to adjust the sensitivity of the diagnostic algorithms. This allows for fine-tuning the balance between detecting subtle diagnostic events and concealing detected events, including false detections, based on operational requirements. This adjustment capability shall be restricted to **Administrator** roles and require appropriate authentication.

The **pnnl** system will adhere to all documentation requirements specified by the client, including the delivery of this Software Requirements Specification, user manuals, and any other technical reports identified throughout the project lifecycle.

glossary.definition: The process by which the tool establishes the identity and privileges of the user by obtaining an identifier and password from the user and checking it against a database of predefined users. The user becomes authenticated when the identifier and password match one of the predefined users in the tool’s database.

One of the parts that make up a software system. A component may be subdivided into other components [IEEE Std 610.12-1990]. For the purpose of this specification, the term 'component' will be used in preference to the term 'module'.
The output of the diagnostic processing. This can either be an identified fault condition or the confirmation of the absence of a fault condition.

Data describing the characteristics of the unit under diagnosis, for example, specifications and design information, operating characteristics, set points, installation and configuration data, and operation data (such as schedules) that do not change on a regular basis.

A requirement that specifies a function that a software system or software component must be able to perform [IEEE Std 610.12-1990]. In this requirements specification, functional requirements specify how the inputs to the software product should be transformed into outputs [IEEE Std 830-1984].

A requirement that specifies an external item with which a software system or software component must interact, or that sets forth constraints on formats, timing, or other factors caused by such an interaction [IEEE Std 610.12-1990].
(for this document) a software system or software component - along with any necessary data and documentation - for which requirements are specified in a requirements specification.

(1) a condition or capability needed by a customer to solve a problem or achieve an objective; (2) a condition or capability that must be met or possessed by a software system or software component to satisfy a contract, standard, specification, or other formally imposed document; (3) a documented representation of a condition or capability as in (1) or (2) [adapted from IEEE Std 610.12-1990].
A document of the essential requirements (functions, design constraints, and attributes) of the software and/or hardware and their external interfaces [adapted from IEEE Std 610.12-1990].
Requirements Specification.

Data that are measured through electronic or mechanical detection. Typically this data varies at such a rate, e.g., in real time, hourly, daily, weekly, or is otherwise difficult to estimate or model such that an accurate characterization of it can only be gained through such detection. This data includes temperatures, pressures, electrical current, machine state, etc.
A collection of software components related in such a way as to produce a result greater than what their parts, separately, could produce.
A discrete part of an HVAC system, e.g., chiller or cooling tower.
A complete HVAC system.
The ease with which a user can learn to operate, prepare inputs for, and interpret outputs of a software system or software component [IEEE Std 610.12-1990].

flowchartDiagram.syntaxExplanation: Flowcharts use nodes to represent processes, decisions, or data, and arrows to show the flow of control or data. Direction is always specified as either `TD` (top-down) or `LR` (left-right). Node IDs must be alphanumeric and unique. Node labels with spaces or special characters must be enclosed in double-quotes. Decision nodes typically use diamond shapes (`{}`), while process steps use rectangular shapes (`[]`) or rounded rectangles (`()`) for start/end. Data nodes can use parallelogram shapes (`[/ /]`). It's crucial to use alphanumeric IDs and quote all labels to avoid syntax errors.
flowchartDiagram.code:
flowchart TD
    A["Sensed Data Acquisition Devices"] --> B["Raw Sensed Data"] 
    FixedData["Fixed HVAC Unit Data"] --> B
    B --> C("Data Preprocessing Component")
    C --> D("Diagnostic Component")
    D --> E("Output Component")
    E --> F["Display Diagnostic Results"] 
    E --> G["Store Diagnostic Results"] 
    F --> H["pnnl User Interface"] 
    G --> I["Permanent Data Storage"] 
    H --> J["Building Operator / Technician"]
flowchartDiagram.caption: pnnl Automated Diagnostic Process Flow

sequenceDiagram.syntaxExplanation: Sequence diagrams illustrate the temporal order of interactions between participants. Participants are declared with `participant Alias as "Full Name"` where 'Alias' is a simple ID and 'Full Name' is the descriptive label; alias IDs must NOT be quoted. Messages between participants show synchronous (`->>`) or asynchronous (`->`) communication. Activations (`->>+` and `-->>-`) denote when a participant is active. `alt` blocks represent alternative paths, with conditions specified, and `loop` blocks indicate repetitive actions. All message labels with spaces or symbols must be double-quoted. Avoid `activate`/`deactivate` keywords inside `alt` or `loop` blocks, preferring the `+`/`-` shorthand with arrows.
sequenceDiagram.code:
sequenceDiagram
    participant User
    participant UI as "pnnl User Interface"
    participant App as "pnnl Application Logic"
    participant AuthSvc as "Authentication Service"
    participant DiagEng as "Diagnostic Engine"

    User->>+UI: "Select 'Start Diagnostics'"
    UI->>+App: "Request Start Diagnostics"
    App->>+AuthSvc: "Authenticate Administrator(Credentials)"
    AuthSvc-->>-App: "Return User Role: Administrator"

    alt Administrator Confirmed
        App->>+UI: "Request Confirmation to Start"
        UI->>+User: "Display 'Confirm Start?' (Yes/No)"
        User->>+UI: "Select 'Yes'"
        UI->>+App: "Confirm Start Action"
        App->>+DiagEng: "Initiate Diagnostic Processing"
        DiagEng-->>-App: "Diagnostic Session Started"
        App-->>-UI: "Display Active Status"
    else Unauthorized User
        App-->>-UI: "Display Unauthorized Access Message"
    end

    UI-->>-User: "Display Operational Status"
sequenceDiagram.caption: pnnl Diagnostic Start and Authentication Flow

entityRelationshipDiagram.syntaxExplanation: Entity Relationship Diagrams (ERDs) depict entities (tables) and their relationships. Entities are defined with their attributes, where `PK` denotes a Primary Key, `FK` a Foreign Key, and `UK` a Unique Key. Attributes are defined as `type name [PK|FK|UK] ["comment"]`; attribute names must be alphanumeric, without spaces or quotes. Entity names with spaces must be quoted. Relationships use `||--o{` for one-to-many, `||--||` for one-to-one, `}|--||` for many-to-one (where FK is in the 'many' entity), and `}|--o{` for many-to-many (requiring a junction table not directly shown by this syntax). Relationship labels MUST be double-quoted.
entityRelationshipDiagram.code:
erDiagram
    USER {
        int id PK
        string username UK
        string password_hash
        string role
    }

    HVAC_UNIT {
        int id PK
        string name
        string type
        string location
    }

    FIXED_DATA {
        int id PK
        int hvac_unit_id FK
        string param_name
        string param_value
        datetime last_updated
    }

    SENSED_DATA {
        int id PK
        int hvac_unit_id FK
        datetime timestamp
        string sensor_type
        float reading
    }

    DIAGNOSTIC_RESULT {
        int id PK
        int hvac_unit_id FK
        datetime timestamp
        string fault_type
        string description
        string corrective_action
        string severity
        int generated_by_user_id FK "Optional user who initiated diagnostic run"
    }

    DIAGNOSTIC_CONFIG {
        int id PK
        string config_name
        float sensitivity
        int last_updated_by_user_id FK
    }

    USER ||--o{ DIAGNOSTIC_CONFIG : "configures"
    USER ||--o{ DIAGNOSTIC_RESULT : "views/generates"
    HVAC_UNIT ||--o{ FIXED_DATA : "has fixed data"
    HVAC_UNIT ||--o{ SENSED_DATA : "produces sensed data for"
    HVAC_UNIT ||--o{ DIAGNOSTIC_RESULT : "is subject of"
    DIAGNOSTIC_CONFIG }|--|| USER : "last updated by"
    DIAGNOSTIC_RESULT }|--|| HVAC_UNIT : "for unit"
    SENSED_DATA }|--|| HVAC_UNIT : "from unit"
    FIXED_DATA }|--|| HVAC_UNIT : "belongs to"
entityRelationshipDiagram.caption: pnnl Core Entities and Relationships

introduction.purpose: The primary purpose of the **tcs** is to establish a unified command and control framework for tactical unmanned aerial vehicles (**UAVs**). This system is designed to facilitate comprehensive data receipt, processing, export, and dissemination capabilities. It ensures interoperability across the full spectrum of present and future **UAV** platforms, encompassing both the Tactical Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (**TUAV**) and Medium Altitude and Endurance (**MAE**) **UAV**s such as Outrider and Predator.
introduction.documentConventions: This Software Requirements Specification (SRS) adheres strictly to the guidelines set forth by the IEEE 830-1998 Recommended Practice for Software Requirements Specifications. The document employs a consistent terminology throughout, with key terms and acronyms formally defined in the Appendix A: Glossary. All requirements are uniquely identified using the T-REQ-[NUMBER] format for clear traceability and management.
introduction.intendedAudience: This document is intended for a diverse audience, including **tcs** program managers, system engineers, software developers, quality assurance personnel, and operational stakeholders. It serves as a foundational reference for understanding the system's capabilities and design constraints. The SRS supports communication and alignment among all parties involved in the development, testing, and deployment of the **tcs**.
introduction.productScope: The scope of the **tcs** encompasses the full lifecycle management of **UAV** missions, from initial planning through real-time execution and post-mission analysis. It includes interfaces for diverse payloads and network communication systems, enabling command and control of the **UAV** itself, its onboard sensors, and the processing of collected intelligence. The system's architecture is specifically designed to integrate seamlessly with existing Joint **C4I Systems**, ensuring a cohesive operational environment for military forces.
references: IEEE 830-1998 IEEE Recommended Practice for Software Requirements Specifications.
ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018 Systems and software engineering — Life cycle processes — Requirements engineering.

overallDescription.productPerspective: The **tcs** is conceived as a groundbreaking, self-contained product within the military’s evolving command, control, communications, computers, and intelligence (**C4I**) ecosystem. It represents a strategic shift from disparate **UAV** control mechanisms to a consolidated, interoperable platform. The system is designed to function as a crucial component of larger defense infrastructure, providing critical reconnaissance and strike capabilities by integrating various **UAV** assets.
productFunctions: The **tcs** will facilitate comprehensive mission planning capabilities, enabling operators to define flight routes, payload utilization, and communication parameters. It will also provide real-time command and control functionalities for air vehicles and their payloads, alongside continuous monitoring of operational status. The system supports advanced payload product management, encompassing data processing, display, and exploitation to derive actionable intelligence.

Furthermore, the **tcs** integrates targeting functions, including coordinate development and accuracy estimation, to support precision operations. It manages extensive interfaces with external **C4I Systems** for seamless information exchange. The system incorporates robust maintenance functions for **UAVs**, payloads, data terminals, and workstations, complemented by sophisticated fault detection, location, and software management capabilities.

userClassesAndCharacteristics.characteristics: Highly skilled military personnel responsible for mission planning, real-time control, and monitoring of **UAVs** and their payloads. They require extensive training in **UAV** operations, tactical procedures, and system interface interaction. Operators are critical decision-makers under operational pressure, necessitating a highly responsive and intuitive user interface.

Specialized personnel tasked with diagnosing, troubleshooting, and repairing **tcs** hardware and software components, including **UAV** systems, payloads, data terminals, and workstations. They possess in-depth technical knowledge of electronic systems, software diagnostics, and military maintenance protocols. Their role ensures the continuous operational readiness of the **tcs** platform.

Experts responsible for the deployment, configuration, security, and ongoing management of the **tcs** software and its underlying infrastructure. This class handles software upgrades, debugging, system monitoring, and ensuring compliance with military security policies. They require advanced knowledge of operating systems, network protocols, and cybersecurity best practices.

overallDescription.operatingEnvironment: The **tcs** is designed to operate within a demanding military environment, requiring robust hardware and secure network infrastructure. It will be deployed on specialized, ruggedized workstation platforms that are capable of withstanding various environmental stressors encountered in tactical settings. These workstations will be equipped with high-resolution displays and dedicated peripheral equipment to support complex mission planning and real-time monitoring tasks.
designAndImplementationConstraints: The design and implementation of the **tcs** are subject to several critical constraints, primarily driven by military operational requirements and existing infrastructure. The system must strictly adhere to the **Defense Information Infrastructure/Common Operating Environment** (**DII/COE**) specifications and the **Joint Technical Architecture** (**JTA**), ensuring seamless integration within the broader **DoD** enterprise. All components must also comply with relevant government and industry standards, including those from **NIST** and **FIPS**, where applicable.

Hardware limitations, such as processing power, memory requirements, and mass storage capacity, as outlined in the resource requirements, will directly influence software design choices. The **tcs** must support specific interfaces to other applications and hardware components, including various **C4I systems** and **UAV** platforms, necessitating strict adherence to defined interface protocols. Security considerations, encompassing **Computer Security**, **Communications Security**, and **Physical Security**, impose rigorous design requirements to protect sensitive operational data and prevent unauthorized access or manipulation.

The system development shall leverage **Commercial-Off-The-Shelf** (**COTS**) products where feasible, provided they meet military ruggedization and security standards. Design conventions and programming standards mandated by the acquiring organization must be followed to ensure maintainability and future extensibility of the software. Furthermore, the system is constrained by responsiveness requirements, demanding near-real-time performance for critical control and monitoring functions, especially during active mission phases.
userDocumentation: The **tcs** will be delivered with a comprehensive suite of user documentation tailored for different user classes. This includes detailed User Manuals providing step-by-step instructions for mission planning, control, and data exploitation. An integrated Online Help system will offer context-sensitive assistance and troubleshooting guides to operators during live operations.

Additionally, dedicated Training Manuals will be developed to support formal instruction for **tcs** operators and maintenance personnel, covering system setup, operational procedures, and emergency protocols. All documentation will adhere to relevant military standards for technical publications, ensuring clarity, accuracy, and ease of use in operational environments.
assumptionsAndDependencies: The successful operation of the **tcs** assumes the continuous availability and operational readiness of the various **UAV** platforms and their associated payloads, including both current and future models. It is also assumed that communication links, both line-of-sight (**LOS**) and satellite communication (**SATCOM**), will maintain sufficient bandwidth and reliability to support real-time data exchange and control commands without significant latency or interruption.

The system is critically dependent on the interoperability and stable functioning of external **C4I Systems** and other integrated military applications, whose interfaces are external to the **tcs** control. Any changes or failures in these external systems could impact the **tcs**'s ability to disseminate intelligence or receive mission directives. Furthermore, the accuracy and timely provision of geopositional data, such as **GPS** signals and digital terrain elevation data (**DTED**), are assumed to be consistently available and reliable for navigation and targeting functions.

externalInterfaceRequirements.userInterfaces: The **tcs** will feature a sophisticated graphical user interface (GUI) designed to optimize human-computer interaction (**HCI**) for military operators in high-stress tactical environments. The interface shall employ a consistent layout across all operational modes, adhering to established military GUI standards and product family style guides to ensure intuitiveness and reduce training overhead. Key operational screens, such as map displays for flight route planning and **UAV** monitoring displays, will provide real-time visual feedback and actionable controls.
externalInterfaceRequirements.hardwareInterfaces: The **tcs** will interface with various hardware components crucial for **UAV** operations and system functionality. This includes direct control interfaces to Data Terminals (**DT Control**) for managing communication links with **UAVs**, encompassing antenna and pedestal control, as well as transmitter and receiver settings. These interfaces must support precise, real-time command and telemetry data exchange with the **UAV** and its sensors, utilizing defined communication protocols.
externalInterfaceRequirements.softwareInterfaces: The **tcs** is designed for extensive interoperability with a broad spectrum of external military software components and specialized systems. This includes critical connections to various **C4I Systems** such as **All Source Analysis System** (**ASAS**), **Joint Standoff Target Attack Radar System** (**JSTARS GSM**), **Joint Maritime Command Information System** (**JMCIS**), **Advanced Field Artillery Tactical Data System** (**AFATDS**), and **Theater Battle Management Core System** (**TBMCS**). Data items exchanged with these systems will include intelligence reports, target nominations, mission tasking orders, and operational status updates, utilizing standard military message formats like **USMTF** and **VMF**.
externalInterfaceRequirements.communicationsInterfaces: The **tcs** requires robust and secure communication interfaces to support its mission-critical functions, encompassing both digital and analog data links. Digital communication will primarily be facilitated through secure military networks, adhering to protocols such as **TCP/IP** (implied for modern military networks) and specialized message formats like **Variable Message Format** (**VMF**) and **United States Message Text Format** (**USMTF**). These protocols will govern the exchange of mission plans, **UAV** control commands, telemetry data, and intelligence products with **UAVs**, **C4I systems**, and other integrated platforms.

systemFeatures.name: Mission Planning
systemFeatures.description: This feature provides operators with comprehensive tools for defining every aspect of a **UAV** mission, from flight trajectories and operational parameters to payload utilization and communication settings. It significantly enhances an operator's ability to strategize and prepare for diverse tactical scenarios, improving mission success rates and operational efficiency. Technically, this involves complex geospatial processing, resource allocation, and validation against operational constraints, impacting the data persistence and communication subsystems. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Operator initiates new mission planning session. Response: **tcs** displays an empty mission planning canvas and loads available map data.
Stimulus: Operator defines a flight route on the map display. Response: **tcs** calculates flight parameters, checks for airspace restrictions, and highlights potential conflicts.

Stimulus: Operator assigns specific payloads and defines their operational modes for the mission. Response: **tcs** validates payload compatibility and resource allocation, updating mission resource estimates.
Stimulus: Operator configures communication parameters for the mission. Response: **tcs** verifies network availability and secure channel configurations.
Stimulus: Operator saves the completed mission plan. Response: **tcs** stores the plan securely and makes it available for deployment.
functionalRequirements: T-REQ-001: The system shall provide a graphical map display for flight route planning, supporting various map data formats (e.g., **ADRG**, **CADRG**, **DTED**).
T-REQ-002: The system shall allow operators to define and modify waypoints, altitudes, speeds, and loiter parameters for **UAV** flight paths.
T-REQ-003: The system shall calculate estimated flight times, fuel consumption, and range based on the defined flight route and **UAV** characteristics.
T-REQ-004: The system shall enable operators to select and configure payloads (e.g., **EO/IR**, **SAR**) for a given mission segment.
T-REQ-005: The system shall validate payload configurations against **UAV** capabilities and mission objectives.
T-REQ-006: The system shall allow operators to define communication frequencies, data link types, and encryption settings for mission execution.
T-REQ-007: The system shall store mission plans in a secure, persistent data store, accessible for deployment and archival.

systemFeatures.name: Mission Control and Monitoring
systemFeatures.description: This feature enables real-time command, control, and comprehensive monitoring of **UAVs**, their payloads, and associated data terminals during active missions. It ensures operators can dynamically adapt to changing tactical situations, issue direct commands, and continuously assess **UAV** status and performance. This capability is foundational to effective **UAV** operations, relying on robust real-time data processing and secure communication links, with significant impact on system responsiveness and data integrity. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Operator issues a flight path modification command. Response: **tcs** transmits the command to the **UAV** and displays the updated flight path on the map.
Stimulus: **UAV** transmits telemetry data (e.g., altitude, speed, GPS coordinates). Response: **tcs** updates **UAV** status displays in near real-time.
Stimulus: Operator commands a payload to change sensor mode or focus. Response: **tcs** transmits the command to the payload and displays confirmation of the mode change.
Stimulus: Payload transmits sensor data (e.g., video, SAR imagery). Response: **tcs** receives, processes, and displays the payload data on dedicated monitoring screens.
Stimulus: Operator initiates **UAV** launch sequence. Response: **tcs** executes pre-flight checks, communicates with the **UAV**, and initiates launch.
Stimulus: Operator initiates **UAV** recovery sequence. Response: **tcs** guides the **UAV** to the recovery point, facilitating controlled landing procedures.
functionalRequirements: T-REQ-008: The system shall provide real-time control over **UAV** flight parameters, including heading, altitude, speed, and mission profile.
T-REQ-009: The system shall display real-time **UAV** telemetry data, including **GPS** coordinates, airspeed, altitude, and system health status.
T-REQ-010: The system shall allow operators to command **UAV** payloads to activate, change modes, or adjust sensor parameters (e.g., zoom, gain).
T-REQ-011: The system shall display real-time video and imagery feeds from **UAV** payloads with minimal latency.
T-REQ-012: The system shall control the Air Data Terminal (**ADT**) for communication link establishment and maintenance.
T-REQ-013: The system shall support both automated and manual **UAV** launch and recovery procedures.
T-REQ-014: The system shall initiate an emergency recovery procedure for the **UAV** upon detecting critical failures or operator command.
T-REQ-015: The system shall monitor and display the status of data terminal components, including antenna position and transmitter/receiver health.

systemFeatures.name: Payload Product Management
systemFeatures.description: This feature is dedicated to the robust processing, display, and exploitation of data collected by **UAV** payloads, transforming raw sensor feeds into actionable intelligence products. It enables operators to quickly analyze, interpret, and disseminate critical information, enhancing situational awareness and decision-making for military command. This capability significantly taxes the system's processing, storage, and display resources, demanding high performance in imagery and video processing, as well as secure data handling. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: **UAV** payload transmits raw sensor data (e.g., **SAR** imagery, **EO/IR** video). Response: **tcs** processes the raw data, applying necessary conversions and enhancements.
Stimulus: Operator selects a processed imagery product for display. Response: **tcs** renders the imagery on a dedicated display, allowing for manipulation (zoom, pan).
Stimulus: Operator uses exploitation tools to analyze imagery for specific objects or patterns. Response: **tcs** provides tools for measurement, annotation, and feature extraction.

Stimulus: Operator designates a processed product for export to an external **C4I system**. Response: **tcs** formats the product according to relevant standards (e.g., **NITF**) and initiates secure transmission.
functionalRequirements: T-REQ-016: The system shall process raw **UAV** payload data (e.g., **SAR**, **EO/IR**) into standardized product formats.
T-REQ-017: The system shall display payload products on dedicated high-resolution monitors with full pan, zoom, and rotation capabilities.
T-REQ-018: The system shall provide tools for imagery exploitation, including measurement, annotation, and geo-referencing.
T-REQ-019: The system shall export processed payload products to external systems in formats compliant with **USIS** and **NITF** standards.
T-REQ-020: The system shall archive raw and processed payload products to external data storage systems.

systemFeatures.name: Targeting
systemFeatures.description: The Targeting feature provides critical capabilities for identifying, locating, and estimating the accuracy of potential targets using **UAV** sensor data. This directly supports precision strike and reconnaissance missions, enabling operators to provide highly accurate target coordinates to weapon systems or other **C4I** assets. This function demands extremely precise geospatial calculations, integration with **GPS** and other navigation aids, and robust error propagation models, affecting computational performance and data integrity. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Operator designates a point of interest on a payload imagery display. Response: **tcs** calculates the precise geographic coordinates of the designated point.
Stimulus: Operator requests a target accuracy estimation for a calculated coordinate. Response: **tcs** computes and displays the probable error ellipse based on sensor, **UAV**, and **GPS** data.
Stimulus: Operator confirms a target coordinate and associated accuracy. Response: **tcs** prepares the target information for dissemination to external **C4I** or weapon systems.
functionalRequirements: T-REQ-021: The system shall calculate target coordinates (e.g., **MGRS**, latitude/longitude) from **UAV** sensor data.
T-REQ-022: The system shall estimate target accuracy based on sensor resolution, **UAV** position, and environmental factors.
T-REQ-023: The system shall display target coordinates and accuracy estimations on the user interface.
T-REQ-024: The system shall format and disseminate target information to designated external **C4I systems** (e.g., **AFATDS**, **ATHS**).

systemFeatures.name: C4I System Interface
systemFeatures.description: This feature manages all interactions between the **tcs** and external **Command, Control, Communications, Computers, and Intelligence (C4I)** systems. It ensures seamless and secure exchange of critical operational data, intelligence products, and mission directives, thereby integrating the **tcs** into the broader military operational network. This capability is paramount for collaborative warfare and significantly impacts system security, communication protocols, and data transformation services. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: **C4I system** transmits a mission tasking order to **tcs**. Response: **tcs** receives, parses, and displays the tasking order to the operator.
Stimulus: Operator commands **tcs** to send an intelligence report to a **C4I system**. Response: **tcs** formats the report according to **C4I** standards and securely transmits it.
Stimulus: **tcs** detects a critical **UAV** status change. Response: **tcs** logs the event and, if configured, generates an alert to relevant **C4I systems**.
functionalRequirements: T-REQ-025: The system shall establish and maintain secure digital interfaces with designated **C4I systems** (e.g., **ASAS**, **JMCIS**, **TBMCS**).
T-REQ-026: The system shall process incoming digital data from **C4I systems**, including mission tasking, intelligence feeds, and situational awareness updates.
T-REQ-027: The system shall transmit outgoing digital data to **C4I systems**, including **UAV** status, intelligence products, and mission reports.
T-REQ-028: The system shall monitor the status and health of all **C4I** digital and analog communication links.
T-REQ-029: The system shall support analog interfaces for specific **C4I** systems or data streams as required.
T-REQ-030: The system shall process incoming and outgoing analog data for seamless integration with **C4I** systems.

systemFeatures.name: System Maintenance
systemFeatures.description: The System Maintenance feature provides tools and procedures for diagnosing and rectifying issues across all **tcs** components, including **UAVs**, payloads, data terminals, and workstations. This ensures the long-term operational readiness and reliability of the entire system by enabling proactive and reactive maintenance activities. This capability streamlines logistical support and reduces downtime, impacting system diagnostics, modularity, and human-computer interface design for technicians. Priority: Medium.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Maintenance technician initiates an **AV** diagnostic routine. Response: **tcs** runs diagnostics on the **Air Vehicle** subsystems and reports findings.
Stimulus: Technician replaces a faulty payload component. Response: **tcs** allows for re-calibration and verification of the new payload.
Stimulus: Technician performs a diagnostic on the Data Terminal. Response: **tcs** provides status and error logs for Data Terminal components.
Stimulus: Technician accesses workstation diagnostic tools. Response: **tcs** displays workstation hardware and software health metrics.
functionalRequirements: T-REQ-031: The system shall provide diagnostic tools for **Air Vehicle** subsystems, including flight control, navigation, and power.
T-REQ-032: The system shall facilitate maintenance procedures for **UAV** payloads, including calibration and testing.
T-REQ-033: The system shall offer diagnostic and configuration utilities for Data Terminal hardware and software.
T-REQ-034: The system shall provide tools for monitoring and maintaining the **tcs** workstation and peripheral equipment health.

systemFeatures.name: Fault Detection and Location (FD/L)
systemFeatures.description: This feature continuously monitors the **tcs** and its integrated components for anomalies and failures, proactively identifying and localizing faults to minimize operational disruption. It encompasses detection during startup, periodic checks, and extensive diagnostics, significantly improving system resilience and reducing mean time to repair (**MTTR**). This capability is deeply integrated into core system processes, impacting system performance, logging, and error reporting mechanisms. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: **tcs** initiates during startup. Response: **tcs** performs a **Startup FD/L** routine and reports system readiness or identified faults.
Stimulus: **tcs** runs a scheduled periodic diagnostic check. Response: **tcs** logs system health and alerts operators to any anomalies.
Stimulus: Operator initiates an extensive fault diagnosis. Response: **tcs** executes deep-level diagnostics across subsystems and provides detailed fault location information.
functionalRequirements: T-REQ-035: The system shall perform a **Startup FD/L** routine upon initialization to verify critical system components.
T-REQ-036: The system shall conduct **Periodic FD/L** checks during normal operation to monitor system health and detect emerging faults.
T-REQ-037: The system shall provide an **Extensive FD/L** capability for in-depth fault diagnosis and location upon operator request.
T-REQ-038: The system shall log all detected faults with timestamps, severity levels, and proposed corrective actions.
T-REQ-039: The system shall alert operators to critical faults through visual and auditory indicators.

systemFeatures.name: Software Management
systemFeatures.description: This feature provides essential functionalities for maintaining the software integrity and evolving the **tcs** platform, including secure software upgrades, debugging, and continuous monitoring. It ensures the system remains current, secure, and performant, adapting to new threats and requirements, while minimizing downtime. This capability directly impacts system security, configuration management, and the overall maintainability lifecycle of the software. Priority: Medium.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Administrator initiates a software upgrade process. Response: **tcs** verifies the upgrade package, backs up current configuration, and installs the new software version.
Stimulus: Developer activates a software debug session. Response: **tcs** provides tools for monitoring internal states, logging, and stepping through code execution (in a controlled environment).
Stimulus: Operator checks software component status. Response: **tcs** displays versions, resource usage, and health of key software modules.
functionalRequirements: T-REQ-040: The system shall provide a secure mechanism for installing software upgrades and patches.
T-REQ-041: The system shall allow for configuration backup and restoration during software upgrade processes.
T-REQ-042: The system shall incorporate debugging and monitoring tools for software components, accessible in a controlled development/maintenance environment.
T-REQ-043: The system shall log software errors and exceptions for diagnostic purposes.

performanceRequirements: The **tcs** shall exhibit high responsiveness for mission-critical control functions. Specifically, commands transmitted to **UAVs** must be executed within 100 milliseconds from operator input, reflecting the real-time nature of tactical operations. This responsiveness is critical for flight control, payload adjustments, and emergency recovery, directly impacting mission safety and effectiveness. The system's processing architecture and communication protocols are designed to meet this demanding latency requirement.

The system shall support a minimum data transfer rate of 50 Mbps for high-bandwidth sensor data, such as **SAR** imagery and **EO/IR** video streams, from **UAVs** to the ground control station. This throughput is essential for near-real-time intelligence exploitation and is predicated on robust communication interfaces and efficient data compression algorithms. The internal processing capabilities of the **tcs** must also be capable of rendering high-resolution imagery and video at a minimum of 30 frames per second without perceptible lag.

Mass storage subsystems shall provide sufficient capacity to archive at least 72 hours of continuous high-bandwidth sensor data and mission logs. This ensures comprehensive post-mission analysis and compliance with data retention policies. The power subsystem must guarantee continuous operation for a minimum of 30 minutes following a primary power failure, utilizing the **Uninterruptible Power** system to allow for an orderly shutdown or transition to alternative power sources.
safetyRequirements: The **tcs** shall incorporate comprehensive safety measures to protect both Air Vehicle assets and human operators. For **Air Vehicle Safety**, the system shall implement automated fail-safes and emergency recovery protocols, including flight termination capabilities, to mitigate uncontrolled flight or potential damage. These measures are designed to prevent accidental loss of **UAVs** and minimize collateral damage during operational anomalies, adhering to **Federal Aviation Administration** (**FAA**) guidelines for unmanned aircraft where applicable.

Regarding **Human Safety**, the **tcs** user interfaces and operational procedures shall be designed following Human Factors Engineering (**HFE**) principles to minimize operator error and reduce cognitive load, especially in critical situations. All warning and alert mechanisms shall be distinct and unambiguous, providing clear instructions for operators to react safely to system failures or external threats. The system will explicitly identify and mitigate System Safety and Health Hazard Assets, ensuring that operational scenarios do not pose undue risks to personnel.
securityRequirements: The **tcs** shall implement robust Computer Security measures to protect against unauthorized access, data manipulation, and denial-of-service attacks. This includes multi-factor authentication for all user access, role-based access control (**RBAC**) to system functionalities and data, and cryptographic protection for all sensitive data at rest and in transit. These measures are essential to maintain the integrity, confidentiality, and availability of classified mission data and control instructions, complying with **Trusted Computer Security Evaluation Criteria** (**TCSEC**) guidelines.

Communications Security is a paramount requirement for the **tcs**, demanding the use of military-grade encryption for all data links with **UAVs**, **C4I Systems**, and other external interfaces. The system shall employ secure communication protocols and frequency hopping techniques to prevent interception, jamming, or spoofing of control signals and intelligence feeds. This ensures the integrity of command chains and the confidentiality of reconnaissance data, critical for tactical advantage.

Physical Security considerations require the **tcs** hardware components to be hardened against environmental threats and tampering, suitable for deployment in tactical military environments. Access to system components shall be restricted to authorized personnel. Furthermore, Personnel Security mandates that all operators and maintenance staff possess appropriate security clearances. Privacy Requirements dictate that all personal data handled by the system, if any, shall be anonymized or protected according to **DoD** privacy regulations, preventing unauthorized disclosure.
softwareQualityAttributes: **Functionality**: The **tcs** shall provide a comprehensive and accurate set of capabilities as defined in Section 4, System Features, enabling full mission planning, control, monitoring, and intelligence exploitation for all supported **UAV** platforms. This attribute is paramount as the system's core purpose is to deliver specific tactical capabilities, directly supporting military objectives. All specified functions must perform correctly under all defined operational conditions, reflecting the critical nature of **UAV** operations.

**Reliability**: The system shall demonstrate a high level of reliability, with a calculated Mean Time Between Failure (**MTBF**) of at least 1,000 operational hours for critical components, and a Mean Time To Repair (**MTTR**) not exceeding 30 minutes for common faults. This is crucial for maintaining continuous **UAV** operations in tactical environments where system downtime can have severe consequences. Redundancy and fault tolerance mechanisms will be employed to ensure mission continuity even in the event of component failures.

**Maintainability**: The **tcs** software and hardware shall be designed for ease of maintenance, enabling efficient diagnosis and resolution of faults. This includes modular software architecture, clear documentation, and built-in diagnostic tools (**Built In Test Equipment - BITE**). High maintainability ensures that system repairs and upgrades can be performed rapidly, minimizing logistical overhead and maximizing system uptime in demanding military operational theaters.

**Availability**: The **tcs** shall achieve an operational availability of 99.9% during mission execution, ensuring the system is ready for use when required. This requires rapid startup, robust fault recovery mechanisms, and minimal downtime for scheduled maintenance. The ability to quickly resume operations after an anomaly is critical for maintaining persistent surveillance and immediate response capabilities.

**Flexibility**: The system shall be highly flexible, capable of adapting to new **UAV** platforms, payloads, communication protocols, and evolving **C4I system** interfaces with minimal software modification. This is essential for future-proofing the **tcs** within a rapidly changing technological and operational landscape. The architecture will support configurable modules and parameter-driven adaptations rather than requiring extensive code changes for each new integration.

**Portability**: The **tcs** software components shall be designed with portability in mind, enabling deployment on different hardware platforms and operating system versions within the **DII/COE** framework, given the tactical nature of its use. This minimizes re-engineering efforts when transitioning to upgraded military hardware or adapting to diverse operational deployment scenarios. Adherence to open standards and well-defined interfaces will facilitate this portability.

**Reusability**: The system shall be developed using modular, well-documented software components that are designed for potential reuse in other military command and control systems. This attribute promotes efficiency in future development efforts across the **DoD**, leveraging existing, proven functionalities. Emphasizing reusability reduces overall development costs and accelerates the deployment of new capabilities.

**Testability**: The **tcs** shall be highly testable, with features to support comprehensive verification and validation throughout the development lifecycle. This includes accessible internal states, logging mechanisms, and interfaces for automated testing tools. High testability is critical for ensuring that the complex interactions and mission-critical functionalities of the **tcs** perform reliably and meet all specified requirements before and during deployment.

**Usability**: The **tcs** user interface shall be highly usable for its intended audience of military operators and maintenance personnel, requiring minimal training to achieve proficiency. This is measured by factors such as task completion time, error rates, and user satisfaction, as evaluated through Human Factors Engineering (**HFE**) studies. An intuitive interface ensures operators can focus on critical mission decisions rather than struggling with system interaction, enhancing overall operational effectiveness.
businessRules: All operational activities within the **tcs** shall comply with **Department of Defense (DoD)** directives and military operational doctrine for **UAV** deployment and intelligence gathering. The system must enforce established command and control protocols, ensuring that mission tasking and execution adhere to the authorized chain of command. Data handling and dissemination must follow strict classification guidelines, preventing unauthorized access or leakage of sensitive information.

The **tcs** shall operate in accordance with all applicable international laws and treaties regarding armed conflict and the use of unmanned systems. Any targeting decisions or intelligence products generated by the system must be auditable, providing a clear trail of data sources, processing steps, and operator actions. Furthermore, the system must support dynamic adaptation to evolving Rules of Engagement (ROE) as directed by military leadership, ensuring operational flexibility while maintaining legal compliance.

otherRequirements: The **tcs** shall adhere to stringent Human Factors Engineering (**HFE**) principles during its design and development, optimizing the human-computer interface and operational workflows for maximum effectiveness and minimal cognitive load for operators and maintenance personnel. This includes considerations for display clarity, control placement, feedback mechanisms, and environmental factors in tactical settings.

Comprehensive Training Related Requirements dictate the provision of all necessary materials and facilities for operator and maintenance personnel training. This encompasses simulated operational environments, instructional modules for mission planning and control, and hands-on experience with diagnostic tools, ensuring personnel are fully proficient before deployment. The training program will be modular and scalable to accommodate varying skill levels and operational needs.

Logistics-Related Requirements specify that the **tcs** must be fully Transportable across various military logistical platforms, including ground vehicles, air transport (e.g., C-130, C-17), sea vessels, and rail. This includes requirements for robust packaging, shock absorption, and environmental protection. Preparation Time for deployment and redeployment must be minimized, ensuring rapid operational readiness in diverse geographic locations.

Packaging Requirements demand that all **tcs** hardware and software components be packaged in rugged, environmentally controlled containers suitable for military transport and field deployment. This packaging must protect against extreme temperatures, humidity, vibration, and electromagnetic interference (**EMI**). The Precedence And Criticality Of Requirements are established by the **TCS** Program Manager, with mission-critical operational requirements taking precedence over non-critical enhancements, ensuring focus on essential capabilities.

flowchartDiagram.syntaxExplanation: This flowchart illustrates the high-level operational states and transitions of the **tcs**. Nodes represent distinct states or processes, and arrows indicate transitions between them. Decision points are used to branch based on conditions. All node labels are quoted if they contain spaces or special characters, and node IDs are alphanumeric for strict compliance.
flowchartDiagram.code:
flowchart TD
    Start(["System Power On"]) --> Init["Initialize Hardware/Software"]
    Init --> ValidateConfig{"Configuration Valid?"}
    ValidateConfig -- No --> RecoveryMode["Recovery Startup Mode"]
    ValidateConfig -- Yes --> NormalStartup["Normal Startup Mode"]
    RecoveryMode --> R1{"Recovery Success?"}
    R1 -- No --> Fail["System Failure"]
    R1 -- Yes --> Operations["Operations State"]
    NormalStartup --> Operations["Operations State"]

    Operations --> Plan["Mission Planning Mode"]
    Operations --> ControlMonitor["Control & Monitoring Mode"]
    Operations --> Maintenance["Maintenance Mode"]
    Operations --> ShutdownRequest{"Shutdown Initiated?"}

    Plan -- "Plan Complete" --> ControlMonitor
    ControlMonitor -- "Mission Concludes" --> Operations
    Maintenance -- "Exit Maintenance" --> Operations

    ShutdownRequest -- Yes --> Shutdown["Shutdown State"]
    Shutdown -- "Power Off" --> End(["System Off"])
    Fail --> End
flowchartDiagram.caption: tcs Operational States and Modes Flow.

sequenceDiagram.syntaxExplanation: This sequence diagram visualizes the temporal interactions between key participants during a typical mission execution within the **tcs**. Participants are represented by vertical lifelines, and messages passed between them are depicted by horizontal arrows, showing the order of events. Activations illustrate the active period of a participant, where processing is occurring.
sequenceDiagram.code:
sequenceDiagram
    participant O as Operator
    participant T as "tcs System"
    participant AV as "Air Vehicle"
    participant P as Payload
    participant C as "C4I System"

    O->>T: "Initiate Mission Plan"
    activate T
    T->>O: "Display Planning Interface"
    O->>T: "Define Flight Route & Payload Config"
    T->>AV: "Upload Mission Parameters"
    deactivate T
    AV-->>T: "Mission Upload Confirmation"

    O->>T: "Start Mission Execution"
    activate T
    T->>AV: "Launch Command"
    AV->>T: "Launch Acknowledge"
    loop Mission Execution
        AV->>P: "Activate Sensor"
        P-->>AV: "Sensor Active Confirmation"
        AV-->>T: "Telemetry Data Stream"
        activate T
        P-->>T: "Raw Sensor Data Stream"
        T->>O: "Display Telemetry & Sensor Feeds"
        O->>T: "Issue AV Control / Payload Adjust"
        T->>AV: "Control Command"
        AV-->>T: "Command Acknowledge"
        deactivate T
        T->>C: "Disseminate Intelligence Products"
        C-->>T: "Acknowledgement"
    end
    T->>AV: "Initiate Recovery Command"
    AV-->>T: "Recovery Sequence Started"
    deactivate T
    AV-->>O: "Mission Complete"
sequenceDiagram.caption: tcs Mission Execution Sequence.

entityRelationshipDiagram.syntaxExplanation: This Entity Relationship Diagram (ERD) models the core data entities within the **tcs** and their relationships. Entities represent significant data objects, with attributes describing their properties. Relationship lines indicate how entities are connected, and cardinality notations (e.g., `||--o{`) specify the number of instances involved in a relationship. Primary Keys (PK) and Foreign Keys (FK) define unique identifiers and linkages.
entityRelationshipDiagram.code:
erDiagram
    TCS_OPERATOR ||--o{ MISSION : "manages"
    MISSION ||--|{ WAYPOINT : "comprises"
    AIR_VEHICLE ||--o{ PAYLOAD : "carries"

    MISSION ||--|{ MISSION_AV_ASSIGNMENT : "includes"
    AIR_VEHICLE ||--|{ MISSION_AV_ASSIGNMENT : "is assigned to"

    MISSION ||--|{ MISSION_C4I_INTERACTION : "logs interaction"
    C4I_SYSTEM ||--|{ MISSION_C4I_INTERACTION : "is involved in"

    TCS_OPERATOR {
        int operator_id PK
        string username
        string name
        string clearance_level
    }

    MISSION {
        int mission_id PK
        int operator_id FK
        string mission_name
        datetime start_time
        datetime end_time
        string status
    }

    AIR_VEHICLE {
        int av_id PK
        string call_sign
        string type
        string current_status
        string home_base
    }

    PAYLOAD {
        int payload_id PK
        int av_id FK
        string type
        string serial_number
        string status
    }

    WAYPOINT {
        int waypoint_id PK
        int mission_id FK
        decimal latitude
        decimal longitude
        decimal altitude
        int sequence_order
    }

    C4I_SYSTEM {
        int c4i_id PK
        string system_name
        string system_type
        string interface_protocol
    }

    MISSION_AV_ASSIGNMENT {
        int mission_id PK, FK
        int av_id PK, FK
        datetime assignment_date
        string role_in_mission
    }

    MISSION_C4I_INTERACTION {
        int mission_id PK, FK
        int c4i_id PK, FK
        datetime interaction_timestamp
        string interaction_type
    }
entityRelationshipDiagram.caption: tcs Core Data Model ERD.

T-REQ-012: Detailed sequence of events and decision points for automated **UAV** launch and recovery procedures.

introduction.purpose: This document serves as the Software Requirements Specification for peazip, version 2.7.1. peazip is developed as a comprehensive file and archive manager application, designed to offer a cross-platform graphical interface for various Open Source archiving and compression utilities. Its primary goal is to empower users to efficiently manage a wide array of archiving formats, including popular ones like 7z, RAR, and ZIP. The specification adheres to the 'Software Requirements Specification' template by Karl E. Wiegers, with minor adaptations to suit project specific needs.
introduction.documentConventions: This document targets both software engineers involved in the ongoing development of peazip and its end-users or clients. It represents the singular, authoritative source for peazip's software requirements as of its release. Its guidelines are crucial for developers building the software and for formalizing the product delivered to clients. Any future modifications to these requirements will strictly follow a formal change management and acceptance procedure. Developers are encouraged to seek clarifications as needed and must not implement changes without explicit client approval.
introduction.intendedAudience: The intended audience for this document includes project programmers, software engineers responsible for future development efforts, and the application's end-users. Its objective is to articulate the features of peazip version 2.7.1 and to provide essential information regarding the software's goals and its proper utilization. While sections 3, 4, and 6 are particularly relevant for end-users, the entire document is pertinent for software engineers and programmers seeking comprehensive understanding.
introduction.productScope: The software product specified herein is a file and archive manager application designed for computer systems, enabling users to perform archiving, compression, and extraction (decompression) operations on files and archives. Core functionalities of peazip include the creation and updating of compressed archives, extraction of archive contents, and a suite of file and archive management tools. These tools encompass robust file copying, splitting and joining, fast or secure deletion, byte-to-byte comparison, and calculation of various checksums and hashes. The system also supports appending timestamps to archive names for backup purposes and offers two-factor authentication (password and keyfile) for managed archives. All operational parameters are customizable through a dedicated settings interface, allowing adaptation to user preferences.
references: IEEE 830-1998 IEEE Recommended Practice for Software Requirements Specifications.
ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018 Systems and software engineering — Life cycle processes — Requirements engineering.
http://sourceforge.net/projects/peazip/
http://peazip.sourceforge.net/
http://peazip.sourceforge.net/peazip-help.html
http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/PeaZip

overallDescription.productPerspective: peazip was initially conceived as a frontend for the **Pea archiving utility**, developed by Giorgio Tani, focusing on file and archive management within a computer system. Following the completion of the **peazip** user interface, support was expanded to encompass a broader array of archiving and compression formats. This evolution positioned **peazip** as a unified, consistent graphical user interface that consolidates and exposes the functionalities of multiple underlying open-source applications, thereby offering a comprehensive solution for diverse archiving needs. This design strategy emphasizes interoperability and a rich feature set.
productFunctions: Creation of compressed archives in various supported formats.
Updating existing archives by adding or removing content.
Appending timestamps to archive names for organization and backup.
Two-factor authentication (password and keyfile) for securing archives.
Extraction of contents from archives compressed in supported formats.
Secure deletion of files and archives from storage units.
Byte-to-byte comparison between two files for integrity verification.
Checking files for duplicates and detecting corruption.
Splitting large files into multiple volumes and merging them back.
Displaying comprehensive information lists for selected files/archives.
Viewing file content as hexadecimal values for advanced inspection.
Graphical monitoring of all executed functions and processes.
Access to system management tools and utilities.
Customization of all functions and parameters via a settings menu.
Execution of any feature as a command prompt command.

userClassesAndCharacteristics.characteristics: This class encompasses any user of a computer system interested in managing (archiving, compressing, extracting) files and archives. There are no inherent technical limitations on who can use peazip. Users might range from those experienced with similar archiving applications to novices. While the peazip interface is designed to be user-friendly, all users are encouraged to consult available tutorials and help documentation to fully leverage the application's extensive features. The system is designed to be intuitive for common tasks, yet powerful for advanced operations.

This segment includes users with greater experience and technical knowledge of computer systems, potentially including developers who wish to contribute to the open-source project. These users are more likely to utilize advanced functionalities such as executing peazip's functions via the command prompt. While a precise distinction based on experience level is challenging, this group benefits from the deeper customization and integration capabilities offered by the software. Their feedback is also valuable for identifying bugs and suggesting enhancements due to the open-source nature of peazip.

overallDescription.operatingEnvironment: peazip is designed for broad operating system compatibility, ensuring it functions across various computing environments. It supports all 64-bit versions of **Microsoft Windows**, all 32-bit versions of **Microsoft Windows** (including **95/98/NT/2000/XP/Vista**), and all **POSIX**-compliant systems, such as **Linux**, **BSD**, and other **UNIX**-like operating systems. Furthermore, **peazip** is available as source code, which enhances its adaptability to nearly any operating system, underscoring its independence from specific OS platforms. This ensures maximum reach and flexibility for users. The application's minimum hardware requirements include an **x-86 compatible CPU** for optimal performance, particularly due to certain performance-critical sections implemented in **ASM**. System **RAM** and **CPU** demands dynamically scale with the chosen compression algorithm and level, ranging from minimal usage for basic operations to over a gigabyte for complex algorithms like **LZMA**, **PPMd**, and **PAQ**. The choice of algorithm significantly influences processing speed, with advanced algorithms often demonstrating better scalability in multi-core environments. All necessary software components invoked by the **peazip** frontend are self-contained within the application's packages, which are provided under suitable open-source or royalty-free licenses. This design principle eliminates the need for users to install custom external packages. The only potential exceptions are standard **gtk/gdk related libraries** required for running **peazip**, **Gwrap**, and **Pea binaries**, which are widely available and well-known trusted components often present in many computer systems.
designAndImplementationConstraints: Development of **peazip** requires proficiency in **Delphi/Kylix**, **Object Pascal**, or **Pascal** programming languages, as these are the core technologies used in its implementation. Any further development or maintenance efforts must adhere to these language prerequisites, ensuring consistency and compatibility with the existing codebase.

The software is distributed under the **LGPL** license, making **peazip** 100% Open Source and entirely free. This license permits linking with any other software, irrespective of whether it is free or proprietary. All developers and users are obligated to agree to and fully accept the terms stipulated by the **LGPL** license. This licensing model encourages community contributions and broad adoption.

**peazip** is developed within the **Lazarus IDE** (http://sourceforge.net/projects/lazarus/). This Integrated Development Environment dictates certain development practices and toolchains. Adherence to this development environment is a constraint for maintainers and contributors to ensure seamless integration and consistent development workflows. The choice of **Lazarus IDE** also influences the cross-platform capabilities of the application.
userDocumentation: Comprehensive user documentation for **peazip** is provided through a dedicated Help Document (titled 'PeaZip Help – Revision: 14/10/2009') and a Mini Tutorial, both bundled directly within the application. These resources offer essential guidance on installation, initial setup, and effective utilization of the software's features. Users are encouraged to consult these integrated materials for immediate assistance.

Additionally, external online resources serve as supplementary documentation, providing broader context and updated information. These include the official **peazip** project website at http://peazip.sourceforge.net/index.html and a dedicated help portal at http://peazip.sourceforge.net/peazip-help.html. These online sources ensure users have access to continuous support and community knowledge, enhancing the overall user experience.
assumptionsAndDependencies: It is assumed that the underlying operating system provides standard system libraries for graphical user interface rendering, such as **gtk/gdk related libraries**, which are necessary for running **peazip**, **Gwrap**, and **Pea binaries**. While these libraries are common and widely available, their absence on a specific system might necessitate manual installation. This assumption ensures the standalone nature of the **peazip** package, minimizing additional software prerequisites for the end-user beyond these common dependencies.

externalInterfaceRequirements.userInterfaces: The **peazip** system provides a rich graphical user interface (GUI) designed for intuitive file and archive management. The primary interface is the **File Manager Interface**, serving as the central hub for browsing files, selecting objects, and initiating various operations. This interface includes a navigation bar with standard 'back', 'forward', and 'up' buttons, alongside a 'refresh' function to ensure displayed content is current. Menu buttons such as 'File', 'Edit', 'Browser', and 'Tools' provide access to submenus, organizing functionalities logically.
externalInterfaceRequirements.hardwareInterfaces: The **peazip** system interacts directly with standard computer hardware components to perform its core functions. It requires an **x-86 compatible CPU**, as specific performance-critical sections of the application are optimized with **ASM** code. This ensures efficient execution of compression and decompression algorithms. The application also interfaces with the system's **RAM** and storage units (e.g., hard drives, SSDs) for processing and storing files and archives. The exact **CPU** and **RAM** requirements dynamically adjust based on the selected compression algorithm and level, ranging from minimal consumption for simple tasks to significant demands for complex, high-compression operations. The system assumes standard input devices (e.g., mouse, keyboard) for user interaction and selection.
externalInterfaceRequirements.softwareInterfaces: **peazip** acts as a unified frontend for a collection of underlying open-source archiving and compression utilities. It integrates with these external binaries and libraries, which are bundled within **peazip** packages under suitable open-source or royalty-free licenses. This modular design means **peazip** leverages existing, robust compression engines without requiring their separate installation by the user. The system also interfaces with the host operating system's file system API to perform file browsing, creation, modification, deletion, and extraction operations. This deep integration allows **peazip** to manage files and archives across various supported formats seamlessly.
externalInterfaceRequirements.communicationsInterfaces: The **peazip** system does not inherently feature direct network communication protocols for peer-to-peer file sharing or remote archiving. However, it can implicitly interact with communication mechanisms when managing files located on network drives or cloud storage mapped as local file system paths by the operating system. In such scenarios, **peazip** relies on the underlying OS and network stack to handle communication protocols like **SMB**, **NFS**, or **HTTP/HTTPS** for remote file access. Any security or encryption related to data transfer in these contexts is managed by the operating system and network infrastructure, not directly by **peazip** itself. The application's core functionality is centered on local file and archive manipulation.

systemFeatures.name: File System Browsing
systemFeatures.description: This feature provides users with comprehensive capabilities to browse and access various areas and objects (files and archives) within the computer system where **peazip** is running. Users can navigate through storage units, desktop, user documents, and other system locations. The value to the user lies in the effortless discovery and selection of files for management tasks, streamlining workflow by centralizing file access within the application. Technically, this involves interfacing with the host operating system's file system APIs to retrieve directory listings and file metadata efficiently. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User selects 'File' button. Response: A submenu with browsing and access options appears.
Stimulus: User chooses 'Filesystem' from the submenu. Response: The system displays an object list organized hierarchically, allowing navigation.
Stimulus: User chooses 'Bookmarks' from the submenu. Response: The system displays a list of user-favorited objects for quick access.
Stimulus: User chooses 'Recent archives' from the submenu. Response: The system displays a list of recently browsed archives.
Stimulus: User selects 'Open path' or 'Open archive'. Response: The system prompts for a path or file, then provides direct access.
Stimulus: User interacts with the navigation bar buttons (back, forward, up). Response: The system navigates through the file system history or moves up one directory level.
Stimulus: User presses the 'refresh' button. Response: The system updates the currently displayed content in the file manager interface.
functionalRequirements: P-REQ-001: The peazip system shall provide a graphical interface for browsing the local file system heirarchy.
P-REQ-002: The peazip system shall allow users to navigate to specific directories and view their contents.
P-REQ-003: The peazip system shall maintain and display a list of user-defined bookmarks for quick access.
P-REQ-004: The peazip system shall maintain and display a list of recently accessed archives.
P-REQ-005: The peazip system shall support direct access to files or paths via user input.
P-REQ-006: The peazip system shall provide navigation controls for moving backward, forward, and up in the directory history.
P-REQ-007: The peazip system shall allow users to refresh the display of current directory contents.

systemFeatures.name: Advanced Object Selection
systemFeatures.description: This feature empowers users to flexibly manage the selection of files and archives displayed within the file manager interface, offering a variety of methods beyond simple single-item clicks. This improves user productivity by enabling efficient mass management of objects, especially when dealing with large quantities where manual selection would be error-prone and time-consuming. From a technical standpoint, this requires robust internal object representation and efficient algorithms for filtering and sorting collections of files based on various criteria. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User selects 'Edit' button. Response: A submenu with object selection options appears.
Stimulus: User chooses 'Select all' from the submenu. Response: All objects currently displayed in the file manager interface are selected.
Stimulus: User chooses 'Invert selection' from the submenu. Response: The selection status of all displayed objects is toggled (selected become deselected, and vice-versa).
Stimulus: User chooses an option from 'All objects...' (e.g., 'by extension'). Response: The system displays a dialog for specifying criteria (e.g., file extension) to filter and select objects.
Stimulus: User confirms selection criteria. Response: Objects matching the criteria are selected, and others are deselected.
Stimulus: User chooses 'Sort by selection status'. Response: The system reorders displayed objects, grouping selected items together.
functionalRequirements: P-REQ-008: The peazip system shall provide an 'Edit' menu for object selection options.
P-REQ-009: The peazip system shall allow users to select all currently displayed objects.
P-REQ-010: The peazip system shall allow users to invert the current selection of objects.
P-REQ-011: The peazip system shall enable selection of objects based on file extension.
P-REQ-012: The peazip system shall enable selection of objects based on file attributes (e.g., read-only, hidden).
P-REQ-013: The peazip system shall enable selection of objects based on file size.
P-REQ-014: The peazip system shall enable selection of objects based on modification date.
P-REQ-015: The peazip system shall allow sorting of displayed objects by their selection status.

systemFeatures.name: Interface Navigation and View Mode
systemFeatures.description: This feature provides users with direct access to **peazip's** primary functional interfaces beyond the file manager, specifically the archive creation and extraction layouts. It also enables users to modify the view mode for objects within the file manager, such as toggling between standard and flat views. This capability significantly enhances user navigation efficiency and improves the overview of complex file structures, making it easier to manage large sets of files. Technically, this requires robust state management and rendering capabilities for switching between different UI contexts and dynamically adjusting file display logic. Priority: Medium.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User selects 'Browser' button. Response: A submenu with interface transfer and view mode options appears.
Stimulus: User chooses 'Go to archiving layout'. Response: The system transitions to the archive creation interface.
Stimulus: User chooses 'Go to extraction layout'. Response: The system transitions to the archive extraction interface.
Stimulus: User chooses 'Toggle browse/flat view'. Response: The system alternates between a hierarchical browse view and a flat view displaying all contained objects.
Stimulus: User chooses 'Refresh'. Response: The system reloads and updates the content of the currently displayed interface.
functionalRequirements: P-REQ-016: The peazip system shall provide a 'Browser' menu for interface navigation and view options.
P-REQ-017: The peazip system shall allow direct navigation to the archive creation interface.
P-REQ-018: The peazip system shall allow direct navigation to the archive extraction interface.
P-REQ-019: The peazip system shall support toggling between hierarchical and flat views of directory contents.
P-REQ-020: The peazip system shall provide a refresh function to update displayed content.

systemFeatures.name: System Utilities Access
systemFeatures.description: This feature allows users to access and utilize various computer system management tools directly through the **peazip** application. This encompasses utilities for storage unit maintenance and general system administration. The user benefits from a centralized point of access for common system tasks, reducing the need to switch between multiple applications. This integration streamlines system diagnostics and optimization directly from the file management context. Technically, this involves launching external system executables or querying system information via OS-specific APIs, ensuring proper handling of command-line parameters and process management. Priority: Medium.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User selects 'Tools' button. Response: A submenu with system management utilities appears.

Stimulus: User selects 'System tools' from the submenu. Response: A secondary menu appears, offering disk utilities (clean, defrag, manage, remove), system management tools (control panel, computer management, task manager), and environment variable display options.
Stimulus: User selects a disk utility (e.g., 'Clean'). Response: The system launches the corresponding operating system utility for disk management.
Stimulus: User selects a system management tool (e.g., 'Task Manager'). Response: The system launches the operating system's task manager.
Stimulus: User selects 'System benchmark'. Response: The system performs a benchmark test and displays performance metrics (MIPS, Core 2 Duo equivalent speed in MHz).
functionalRequirements: P-REQ-021: The peazip system shall provide a 'Tools' menu for accessing system management utilities.
P-REQ-022: The peazip system shall provide direct access to operating system disk utilities (e.g., clean, defrag).
P-REQ-023: The peazip system shall provide direct access to operating system management tools (e.g., control panel, task manager).
P-REQ-024: The peazip system shall display operating system environment variables.
P-REQ-025: The peazip system shall execute a system benchmark and display MIPS and Core 2 Duo equivalent speed results.

systemFeatures.name: Non-Archiving File Utilities
systemFeatures.description: This feature provides a suite of advanced file management tools that extend beyond traditional archiving functions. Users can securely delete files, perform byte-to-byte comparisons, check for duplicates or corruption, split and merge files, and view file information or hexadecimal content. This enhances data integrity, security, and specialized file manipulation, offering users powerful capabilities for detailed file control. Technically, these utilities require direct file I/O operations, checksum calculations, and low-level byte manipulation, often implemented with optimized algorithms for efficiency and reliability. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User selects one or more files in the file manager or within the archive/extraction interfaces. Response: Contextual menu or toolbar options for non-archiving utilities become available.
Stimulus: User selects 'Secure file deletion'. Response: The system prompts for confirmation, then securely overwrites and deletes the selected files, preventing data recovery.
Stimulus: User selects 'Byte to byte file comparison' for two files. Response: The system performs a byte-level comparison and highlights differences.
Stimulus: User selects 'Check files'. Response: The system performs checks to identify duplicate files or verify integrity against known checksums.
Stimulus: User selects 'Split file'. Response: The system prompts for split parameters, then divides the selected file into multiple smaller volumes.
Stimulus: User selects 'Merge split volumes'. Response: The system prompts for the parts of a split file, then recombines them into the original file.
Stimulus: User selects 'Information display' for a file/archive. Response: The system presents detailed metadata including file count, dates, sizes, and total space occupation.
Stimulus: User selects 'Hexadecimal preview' for a file. Response: The system displays the raw byte content of the file in hexadecimal format.
functionalRequirements: P-REQ-026: The peazip system shall provide a secure file deletion utility that prevents data recovery.
P-REQ-027: The peazip system shall perform byte-to-byte comparison between two selected files and report differences.
P-REQ-028: The peazip system shall identify duplicate files within a specified scope.
P-REQ-029: The peazip system shall verify file integrity using checksums or hash values.
P-REQ-030: The peazip system shall split a selected file into multiple user-defined volumes.
P-REQ-031: The peazip system shall merge multiple split volumes back into their original file.
P-REQ-032: The peazip system shall display detailed information for selected files or archives, including size, dates, and content statistics.
P-REQ-033: The peazip system shall provide a hexadecimal preview of the content of any selected file.

systemFeatures.name: Archive Extraction
systemFeatures.description: This feature enables users to decompress and extract the contents of selected compressed archives into a specified folder on their computer system. Users have full control over the destination folder's name and location, ensuring flexibility in organizing extracted data. The original archive remains intact, preserving its contents and name at its initial storage location. This capability is paramount for accessing stored data and making it usable. Technically, this requires integration with multiple decompression engines, robust error handling for corrupted archives, and secure decryption for password-protected or keyfile-authenticated archives. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User selects one or more suitable archives in the file manager and clicks the 'Extract' button in the toolbar. Response: The system opens the extraction interface, pre-populating with selected archives.
