The system shall display additional information associated with the new paint number, such as its name and Red Green Blue (RGB) value.

systemFeatures.name: Closest Colors
systemFeatures.description:
This feature enhances the user's ability to explore paint options by identifying similar colors based on a given reference. It provides flexibility for users looking for alternatives or complementary shades, thereby improving product discovery and satisfaction. Technically, it leverages the color search engine's capabilities to perform proximity searches within the defined color space. 

Priority: High. Providing a 'closest colors' tool is highly valuable for users who need alternatives or want to explore variations, directly contributing to customer satisfaction and product exploration within the new paint scheme.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User provides a paint number and a collection. Response: The system initiates a search for similar colors.
Stimulus: The system queries the color search module for proximate colors. Response: The system retrieves a list of closest colors.
Stimulus: The system displays a configurable number of colors closest to the given input. Response: The user reviews similar paint options.
functionalRequirements: The system shall accept a paint number and a target collection as input.
The system shall allow the user to specify the arbitrary number of closest colors to retrieve.
The system shall utilize the color search module to identify colors proximate to the input color.
The system shall display the identified closest colors, along with their names and numbers.

systemFeatures.name: Color Search Engine
systemFeatures.description:
The color search engine is a foundational component of the **colorcast** application, enabling users to efficiently locate paints across all collections using various input criteria. This central search capability is crucial for users to quickly find specific products or explore the vast paint catalog, significantly improving navigation and product discovery. Technically, it relies on integrated databases for paint and collection information, performing queries based on names, numbers, or standard color values. 

Priority: High. As the core lookup mechanism, the color search engine is critical for almost all other features and for the overall utility of the **colorcast** application. Without it, the system's primary goal of aiding paint scheme transition would be severely hindered.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User enters a paint name, number, or standard color value. Response: The system processes the search query.
Stimulus: The system queries the color space and paint information databases. Response: The system retrieves matching paint data.
Stimulus: The system displays a list of colors matching the search criteria across specified collections. Response: The user reviews the search results.
functionalRequirements: The system shall allow users to search for colors by paint name, paint number, or industry-standard color value.
The system shall enable searching across all available paint collections or specific collections.
The system shall require the presence of color space and paint information databases to function.
The system shall display all matching colors, including their names, numbers, and associated collection.

systemFeatures.name: User Color Palette
systemFeatures.description:
The User Color Palette provides a personalized user experience by storing a history of recent color searches and, optionally, uploaded images for matching. This feature allows users to easily revisit their selections and work with a curated set of colors, enhancing convenience and reducing repetitive tasks. Technically, it maintains session-persistent client-side data, with server-side storage for a limited duration, emphasizing user privacy over strict security. 

Priority: High. This feature significantly improves user convenience by allowing them to save and revisit their color selections and image uploads, enhancing the overall usability and stickiness of the **colorcast** application.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User performs a color search or image upload. Response: The system adds the relevant data to the user's session-persistent palette.
Stimulus: User accesses their color palette. Response: The system displays a list of recently searched colors and uploaded images.
Stimulus: 30 days elapse since data storage. Response: The system automatically removes the user's data from the server.
functionalRequirements: The system shall store a list of the user's recent color searches.
The system shall store recently uploaded images for matching if the Color Sample Matcher is active.
The system shall associate stored user data with a single client based on a persistent client session.
The system shall automatically remove user data from the server after 30 days.
The system shall integrate with the Color Search Engine for retrieving color request details.
The system shall integrate with the Color Sample Matcher to enable storing uploaded images.

systemFeatures.name: Administrative Interface
systemFeatures.description:
The administrative interface is crucial for the ongoing maintenance and management of the **colorcast** application's core data. It empowers authorized administrative users to add, update, and delete paint information and collections, ensuring the system's data remains current and accurate. Furthermore, it provides tools for managing user access levels, aligning with internal organizational structures. Technically, this interface provides a secure gateway for data manipulation and user management, directly interacting with the underlying paint and user databases. 

Priority: High. An administrative interface is essential for the long-term viability and data integrity of the **colorcast** application. It enables ABC Paint personnel to manage the paint catalog and user privileges, ensuring the system remains functional and aligned with business changes.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Administrative user logs into the interface. Response: The system authenticates the user and displays available management options based on their access level.
Stimulus: Administrative user attempts to update paint information. Response: The system verifies permissions and allows modification, then updates the database.
Stimulus: Administrative user attempts to add a new administrative user. Response: The system verifies the admin's level and, if authorized, creates the new user with the specified level.
Stimulus: Administrative user attempts to delete paint information. Response: The system verifies permissions and allows deletion, then removes data from the database.
functionalRequirements: The system shall provide a secure interface for administrative users.
The system shall allow administrative users to add new paint information, including name, number, Red Green Blue (RGB) value, and collection.
The system shall allow administrative users to update existing paint information.
The system shall allow administrative users to delete existing paint information.
The system shall allow administrative users to add new users, adhering to the specified access level hierarchy (Level 1, Level 2, Level 3).
The system shall enforce access permissions as defined in Section 2.3 for all administrative operations.
The system shall require the presence of the color space and paint information databases for its operation.

systemFeatures.name: Color Sample Matcher
systemFeatures.description:
This feature enables users to upload images and visually select a color or a blended color group from the image for subsequent searching. It offers an advanced method of color identification, catering to users who need to match colors from physical samples or digital images. Technically, it involves image processing, graphical selection, and integration with the color search engine. 

Priority: Low. While enhancing the user experience, this module is explicitly stated as not a core requirement for the initial project delivery. Its functionality is considered an optional extension, included for specification purposes but not critical for the initial phase of the paint scheme migration.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Client user uploads an image in a common format. Response: The system processes and displays the uploaded image.
Stimulus: User graphically selects a color or color group from the image using a pointing device. Response: The system identifies the Red Green Blue (RGB) value of the selected area.
Stimulus: User initiates a color search based on the selected image color. Response: The system forwards the color value to the color search engine and displays results.
functionalRequirements: The system shall allow client users to upload images in common formats (e.g., JPEG, PNG).
The system shall display the uploaded image for color selection.
The system shall enable users to graphically select a specific color or a blended color group from the image using a hardware pointing device.
The system shall extract the Red Green Blue (RGB) value of the selected color or blended group.
The system shall integrate with the color search engine module to perform searches based on the extracted color value.
The system shall require the presence of a hardware pointing device for graphical selection.

performanceRequirements: Color searches across the various paint collections shall be processed by the server in sub-second timeframes. This ensures a responsive user experience for a core functionality, directly impacting user satisfaction and efficiency. The rationale is to prevent user frustration and facilitate quick decision-making during the color selection and translation processes, especially considering the expected volume of queries.

Changes to paint and collection information through the administrative interface shall occur in Real-time. The actual processing time will vary based on the data volume, but the system's responsiveness to updates is critical for maintaining data accuracy and consistency for all users. The rationale is to ensure administrators perceive immediate feedback and that product catalog data is always current, supporting dynamic business operations.

The application's performance over the greater internet is not guaranteed by **colorcast**, as it is subject to client internet access speed and type. The system shall measure and display the server's processing time for requests, excluding network transit time. This provides transparent metrics to identify server-side performance independently of client network conditions, allowing for focused optimization efforts.
safetyRequirements: No inherent safety concerns have been identified through the analysis of the proposed **colorcast** product requirements and features. However, a comprehensive legal review of the final product is recommended prior to public deployment to ensure ABC Paint's full indemnification of liability, particularly concerning product representation and color accuracy. The rationale is to mitigate potential legal risks related to customer expectations and product use.

The **colorcast** system acknowledges that prolonged, repetitive use of data-entry and pointing devices can lead to injury, aligning with federal and state regulations regarding workplace ergonomics. **colorcast** explicitly disclaims responsibility for injuries or damages resulting from the use of such input devices. The rationale is to manage liability by informing users and stakeholders of known ergonomic risks associated with general computer interaction.
securityRequirements: The product's primary data, consisting of paint names and color values, is generally public information and not considered sensitive. No attempts will be made to secure or obfuscate this information, as it is inherently recoverable by sophisticated end-users. The rationale is to optimize resource allocation by not applying security measures to publicly available data, focusing security efforts where genuinely needed.

Information requiring access protection includes user-specified color palettes and administrative access permissions. User color palettes, though private, are not password-protected and rely on session persistence for access. Conversely, administrative access permissions must be securely protected with industry-standard authentication mechanisms. The rationale is to balance user convenience for non-sensitive data with stringent security for critical system control and user management.

Implementations of privacy for user color palettes and security for administrative access shall adhere to industry standards. This ensures that personal information is handled responsibly and that system integrity is protected against unauthorized access, maintaining trust and compliance. The rationale is to leverage established best practices for data protection.
softwareQualityAttributes: Adaptability: The modular design of the **colorcast** application promotes high adaptability, allowing individual features to be updated or replaced without impacting the entire system. This facilitates easier maintenance and future enhancements. The rationale is to support long-term evolution and responsiveness to changing business needs without extensive refactoring.

Robustness: The modular specification inherently contributes to the robustness of the application, as issues in one module are less likely to cascade and affect the entire system. Error reporting and recovery mechanisms are designed to maintain application stability. The rationale is to minimize downtime and ensure continuous availability of critical features.

Reusability: The emphasis on modularity also enhances the reusability of individual components. Features like the Color Search Engine can be leveraged by other modules (e.g., Color Translator, Closest Colors), reducing development effort and promoting consistency. The rationale is to optimize code quality and development efficiency through component-based architecture.

Correctness: The accurate display of color samples on client devices is a critical correctness concern. While enterprise environments are assumed to have calibrated displays, consumer markets present a challenge. The current specification does not include client display calibration, noting this as a potential future extension. The rationale is to explicitly define the scope of color accuracy guarantees and identify areas for future improvement, managing user expectations.

Usability: The task-based screen interface, consistent navigation, and accelerator key support contribute significantly to the usability of the **colorcast** application, reducing the learning curve for both new and experienced users. The rationale is to ensure the system is intuitive and efficient, leading to higher user adoption and satisfaction.
businessRules: ABC Paint shall apply its existing conventional business processes and security protocols to the granting of administrative access within the **colorcast** system. This ensures consistency with established organizational practices. The rationale is to integrate the new system seamlessly into existing operational frameworks and governance policies.

Default privileges shall be granted to typical employees and consumers, limiting their access to core application functionalities only. This aligns with a principle of least privilege, minimizing potential risks from unauthorized actions. The rationale is to protect sensitive administrative functions and data integrity.

A limited number of administrative users shall be created to manage paint data updates and user access. This concentrated control ensures accountability and consistency in critical data management tasks. The rationale is to maintain data quality and system security through controlled administrative access.

Administrative users are restricted to granting other users administrative access up to their own access level only. For example, a Level 2 administrator cannot create a Level 3 administrator. This hierarchical control enforces a clear chain of command and prevents unauthorized elevation of privileges. The rationale is to uphold the defined security model and prevent abuse of administrative powers.

glossary.definition: The software product developed by **colorcast** for ABC Paint.
A computer or program capable of running applications or requesting application-based services from a server.
A curated group of colors, typically from the same company, designed by a specific artist, or provided as a distinct set separate from other paints, such as the Ralph Lauren Collection.
A visual utility, such as a card or list, used for displaying available or selected colors within the application.
Logical numerical representations of physical colors, commonly expressed as **RGB** triplets, used within the system.
Hardware components, such as a keyboard or mouse, used by individuals to interact with and input information into an application.
A unique, descriptive title assigned to a paint product, distinct from its product number.
An input hardware device, including a mouse, joystick, or trackball, which enables a user to manipulate a cursor or pointer for interaction within an application.

Limited form of security achieved through indirect identity verification, where persistent user data is linked to a specific client session on a computer rather than an individual. No robust security mechanisms are implemented for this data.
A formal request for information made by a client, typically directed towards a database or a search engine.

Pertaining to computer systems that update information at the same rate as data is received. For instance, if a server update takes five minutes, a Real-time update will complete within that five-minute duration.

A robust form of protection where an individual is directly linked to their persistent user data via a unique username and associated passphrase. This level of security is mandatory for all administrative access within the system.
A computer system responsible for processing data requests and queries that are integral to the application's functionality.
Refers to processing or operations, whether for the client or server, that exclusively occur on the server component of the application architecture.
**Hyper Text Transfer Protocol**, the foundation for data communication for the World Wide Web.
**Internet Service Provider**, a company that provides individuals and organizations access to the Internet.
**Local Area Network**, a computer network that interconnects computers within a limited area.
**Red Green Blue**, an additive color model in which red, green, and blue light are added together in various ways to reproduce a broad array of colors.
**To Be Determined**, indicating an item whose details are not yet finalized.

flowchartDiagram.syntaxExplanation: This flowchart illustrates the high-level process flow for a user interacting with the **colorcast** application, specifically focusing on color search and palette management. Nodes represent distinct steps or decisions within the workflow. The `TD` (Top Down) direction indicates the progression from start to finish. Rectangular nodes signify processes, diamond nodes represent decisions, and rounded rectangles indicate start/end points. All labels with spaces are double-quoted, and node IDs are alphanumeric.
flowchartDiagram.code:
flowchart TD
    C_START(["User Accesses colorcast"]) --> C_INPUT["Enter Paint Name/Number or Upload Image"]
    C_INPUT --> C_DECIDE{"Input Type?"}
    C_DECIDE --> |"Paint Data"| C_SEARCH_ENGINE["Color Search Engine"]
    C_DECIDE --> |"Image File"| C_SAMPLE_MATCHER["Color Sample Matcher"]
    C_SEARCH_ENGINE --> C_DB_QUERY["Query Paint Database"]
    C_SAMPLE_MATCHER --> C_EXTRACT["Extract Color from Image"]
    C_EXTRACT --> C_DB_QUERY
    C_DB_QUERY --> C_RESULTS["Display Matching/Closest Colors"]
    C_RESULTS --> C_SELECT["Select Color(s)"]
    C_SELECT --> C_ADD_PALETTE["Add to User Color Palette"]
    C_ADD_PALETTE --> C_PROCESS_END(["Process Complete"])
    C_RESULTS --> C_PROCESS_END
    C_ADMIN_LOGIN["Admin Login"] --> C_ADMIN_DASH["Admin Dashboard"]
    C_ADMIN_DASH --> C_MANAGE_PAINTS["Manage Paint Data"]
    C_MANAGE_PAINTS --> C_DB_UPDATE["Update Paint Database"]
    C_DB_UPDATE --> C_ADMIN_LOGOUT["Admin Logout"]
    C_ADMIN_DASH --> C_MANAGE_USERS["Manage Admin Users"]
    C_MANAGE_USERS --> C_DB_UPDATE
flowchartDiagram.caption: **colorcast** User and Admin Workflow.

sequenceDiagram.syntaxExplanation: This sequence diagram models the interaction flow for a user performing a color translation in the **colorcast** application. Participants are represented as lifelines, and messages indicate the communication between them. Activations are denoted by `->>+` and `-->>-` to show when a participant is active. The diagram illustrates how the client application, server application, and paint database interact to fulfill a translation request.
sequenceDiagram.code:
sequenceDiagram
    participant U as User
    participant C as ClientApp
    participant S as ServerApp
    participant D as PaintDB

    U->>+C: Enter old paint number & collections
    C->>+S: Request Color Translation(oldNum, srcCol, trgtCol)
    S->>+D: Query Paint for New Scheme(oldNum, srcCol, trgtCol)
    D-->>-S: Return new paint number & details
    S-->>-C: Respond with Translated Color Data
    C-->>-U: Display translated new paint number
sequenceDiagram.caption: **colorcast** Color Translator Sequence.

entityRelationshipDiagram.syntaxExplanation: This Entity Relationship Diagram (ERD) visually represents the database schema for the **colorcast** application, highlighting entities and their relationships. Entities are defined with their attributes, specifying primary keys (`PK`), foreign keys (`FK`), and unique keys (`UK`). Relationships are labeled and indicate cardinality, ensuring strict adherence to the project's data model. The `}|--||` notation is used for one-to-many relationships where the many side has a foreign key to the one side.
entityRelationshipDiagram.code:
erDiagram
    USER ||--o{ USER_PALETTE : "stores"
    PAINT ||--o{ USER_PALETTE : "included_in"
    COLLECTION ||--o{ PAINT : "contains"
    USER }|--|| ADMIN_ACCESS : "has_level"

    USER {
        int user_id PK
        string email UK
        string password_hash
        string role
    }

    PAINT {
        int paint_id PK
        string old_number UK
        string new_number UK
        string name
        string rgb_value
        int collection_id FK
    }

    COLLECTION {
        int collection_id PK
        string name UK
    }

    USER_PALETTE {
        int palette_entry_id PK
        int user_id FK
        int paint_id FK
        datetime added_at
    }

    ADMIN_ACCESS {
        int admin_id PK
        int user_id FK UK
        int level
        bool can_add
        bool can_update
        bool can_delete
    }
entityRelationshipDiagram.caption: **colorcast** Database Entity Relationships.

introduction.purpose: The primary purpose of this Software Requirements Specification (SRS) is to precisely delineate the functional and non-functional requirements for the XML Services (XS) component within the Defense Information Infrastructure (dii) Common Operating Environment (COE). This document serves as a foundational reference for all development activities, ensuring that the delivered software adheres strictly to the operational needs of military applications. It aims to eliminate ambiguity by providing a clear and comprehensive understanding of the system's capabilities and constraints, supporting the lifecycle from design to deployment. Ultimately, this SRS facilitates the development of interoperable and robust XML-based data management services for the **dii** COE.
introduction.documentConventions: This document adheres to the guidelines set forth by IEEE 830-1998, 'Recommended Practice for Software Requirements Specifications,' ensuring a standardized and comprehensive approach to requirements definition. All functional requirements are presented in a 'The system shall...' format, emphasizing testability and clear expectations. Acronyms and specialized terminology are defined in a dedicated glossary to promote clarity and consistency across all stakeholders. Specific military and industry standards, such as MIL-STD-498 for software development and FIPS PUB 127-2 for database language SQL, are referenced to guide implementation and compliance. This structured format enhances readability and maintains uniformity throughout the specification.
introduction.intendedAudience: This SRS is primarily intended for the **dii** COE technical development team, including software architects, engineers, testers, and quality assurance personnel responsible for the design, implementation, and verification of the XML Services component. Project managers and system integrators will utilize this document to understand the scope, plan resources, and manage dependencies with other DII COE components. Additionally, stakeholders from the Defense Information Systems Agency (DISA) and various Community of Interest (COI) Points of Contact will find this document essential for reviewing, approving, and ensuring alignment with strategic interoperability goals. End-users, such as data consumers and managers, can also refer to this document for a high-level understanding of system capabilities.
introduction.productScope: The **dii** XML Services (XS) component encompasses the provision of core infrastructure services utilizing **XML** technology for mission and support applications within the Defense Information Infrastructure Common Operating Environment. Its scope specifically includes the registration of terms, tags, and structures; the design of **XML** schemas; and the generation and consumption of **XML** documents. The system aims to abstract vendor-unique data access implementations, thereby enabling applications to operate independently of specific physical access models. Furthermore, it incorporates advanced data management functions to facilitate access to both distributed local and remote database management systems, enhancing overall interoperability across the **dii** environment.
references: IEEE 830-1998 IEEE Recommended Practice for Software Requirements Specifications.
ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018 Systems and software engineering — Life cycle processes — Requirements engineering.

AAN-SDA001A Standard Theater Army Command and Control System (STACCS) System Specification, Version 1.1/As Built, 1 Oct. 93.
Standard Theater Army Command and Control System (STACCS) System Software Programmers Manual, Draft.
Global Command and Control System (GCCS) Integration Standard, Version 1.0, October 1994.
Global Command and Control System (GCCS) Common Operating Environment Baseline, DISA, 28 November 1994.
User Interface Specifications for Global Command and Control System (GCCS), Version 1.0, October 1994.
Draft Architectural Design Document for the Global Command and Control System (GCCS) Common Operating Environment (COE), Version 3, 24 July, 1994.

overallDescription.productPerspective: The **dii** XML Services (XS) functional area is an integral component of the overarching Defense Information Infrastructure (DII) Common Operating Environment (COE). It serves as a foundational infrastructure service, designed to support mission and support applications by leveraging **XML** technology for data exchange and management. This system is envisioned as a critical enabler for distributed data access, acting as an abstraction layer that insulates applications from the complexities of vendor-specific data access models and physical data locations. The **dii** XS system is not a standalone product but rather a core subsystem, integrating seamlessly within the broader **dii** COE architecture. It extends the capabilities of existing data access frameworks by introducing standardized **XML**-based interoperability solutions, ensuring consistency across various command and control systems.
productFunctions: Registration of XML terms, tags, and semantic structures within a centralized registry.
Facilitation of XML schema design, allowing for the creation and validation of document structures.
Support for the generation of XML documents from diverse data sources.
Enabling the consumption and parsing of incoming XML documents.
Advanced discovery and browsing capabilities for registered XML assets.
Management functions for users, naming conventions, and monitoring changes to data models.
Provision of structured services for recording, checking, and validating XML data.
Content services for creating, referencing, and versioning XML tags and semantic structures.

userClassesAndCharacteristics.characteristics: These users interact with the **dii** XS to discover, retrieve, and utilize existing XML tags and structures for their applications. They require robust search capabilities, clear semantic understanding of terms, and the ability to view historical versions and related information resources. Data Consumers are typically developers, analysts, or other system components that integrate with the XML Services to exchange data according to predefined agreements. They prioritize ease of access and comprehensive metadata for efficient data reuse.

These personnel are responsible for the administration and oversight of the data management services, including the underlying database systems. They require capabilities for tuning the system to accommodate varying workloads, adjusting session time-out values in degraded communication environments, and managing user permissions and privileges. Their technical expertise typically includes database administration, network management, and a deep understanding of data security policies. They are critical for ensuring system performance, availability, and data integrity.

These users focus on the operational environment of the **dii** XS, particularly concerning network performance and resource allocation. They need to monitor communication performance and may interact with the system to dynamically adjust parameters like session time-out values based on network conditions. Their role ensures that the **dii** XS maintains optimal performance and responsiveness even under challenging network constraints, contributing to overall system robustness.

This class comprises individuals responsible for contributing metadata, XML tags, and schema definitions to the **dii** XML Registry. They require tools and forms for submitting information resource artifacts, including XML Tag Specifications, XML Specs (e.g., DTD, DCD), and sample XML documents. Producers are typically developers or data architects who understand XML technologies and data modeling principles. They need to ensure maximum semantic understanding and interoperability of the terms they submit, actively reviewing existing tags and submitting new proposals.

This role holds ultimate authority within their Community of Interest (COI) for managing XML tags and mediating unresolved disputes. The Chief Engineer approves Points of Contact (POCs) for COIs and ensures adherence to overall **dii** COE architectural principles. This user requires a high-level overview of the registry's status and the ability to enforce standards and policies related to XML usage within their domain. Their technical understanding spans system architecture and interoperability standards.

POCs manage the lifecycle of information resources within their assigned Community of Interest, including transitioning resources through developmental, candidate, approved, rejected, and retired statuses. They are responsible for reviewing and approving submissions, establishing naming conventions, and monitoring changes to data models in the Registry. These users possess a deep understanding of their domain's data requirements and the specific **XML** artifacts relevant to their COI, acting as key gatekeepers for data quality and consistency.

overallDescription.operatingEnvironment: The **dii** XML Services (XS) component is mandated to operate within the Defense Information Infrastructure (DII) Common Operating Environment (COE), ensuring portability and execution on COE-compliant platforms. This environment includes diverse hardware platforms and operating systems, requiring adherence to strict military standards for software development and documentation. The system must coexist peacefully with existing data elements of the Database Interface (DBIF), Discretionary Access Control (DAC), and various Commercial Off-The-Shelf (COTS) Relational Database Management System (**RDBMS**) products.
designAndImplementationConstraints: The **dii** XS shall adhere to **COE** compliance level requirements, as described in relevant interfacing and technical specifications, to ensure seamless integration and operation within the broader Defense Information Infrastructure. Development and documentation practices shall strictly follow **MIL-STD-498** (Military Standard - Software Development and Documentation), ensuring rigorous quality and maintainability standards throughout the software lifecycle. All data access services involving structured query language shall conform to **FIPS PUB 127-2** (Database Language SQL), guaranteeing interoperability and adherence to federal information processing standards.

The system's data management services must be tunable by on-site personnel, allowing for adjustment to varying workloads and sizes of associated databases, especially in fixed (static) environments. In dynamic environments, such as deployed units, the **dii** XS shall support the ability to redefine or reset connect descriptors for database server instances. A critical security constraint involves supporting both Discretionary Access Control (**DAC**) and Mandatory Access Control (**MAC**) mechanisms to mediate access to objects based on user identity, group membership, clearance levels, and sensitivity labels.

The **dii** XS developer is solely responsible for ongoing software maintenance, comprehensive support, and the timely provision of software updates to ensure the continuous operational readiness and evolution of the XML Services component. Distribution of the XS product to various system developers within the DII COE is the exclusive responsibility of the DISA Configuration Manager (**CM**), ensuring controlled and standardized deployment across the defense infrastructure. This clear separation of responsibilities defines crucial operational and organizational constraints for the project.
userDocumentation: The **dii** XS shall deliver comprehensive user documentation components, including detailed user manuals, intuitive on-line help, and interactive tutorials designed to guide users through all aspects of the XML Services. This documentation will cover the submission process for XML information resources, the discovery and consumption of XML tags, and the management of registry entities. Specific guidance will be provided for understanding the XML Registry forms, instructions, and tools required for metadata submission. The user documentation will also explain the various information resource types and their associations, enabling users to effectively leverage the system's capabilities.
assumptionsAndDependencies: The **dii** XS assumes the continuous availability and operational stability of the underlying Defense Information Infrastructure (DII) Common Operating Environment (COE) and its core network services. It is dependent on the consistent application of specified military standards and compliance with the COE architecture for successful integration and portability across diverse platforms. The system also assumes the presence of commercial off-the-shelf (**COTS**) Relational Database Management System (**RDBMS**) products, with which the XS will internally interface, implying a reliance on their documented APIs and functionalities.

A key assumption is the technical proficiency of end-users, including data consumers, managers, and XML producers, regarding fundamental concepts of XML technology and database interactions. The project assumes that necessary APIs, as specified in the appendices for registry and data access services, will be made available and maintained by external systems. Dependencies include the successful deployment and configuration by the DISA Configuration Manager and the ongoing software maintenance and support provided by the XS developer. The effectiveness of the system is predicated on the accuracy and consistency of metadata submitted by producers and the adherence to established naming conventions and data standards.

externalInterfaceRequirements.userInterfaces: The **dii** XML Services (XS) shall provide a robust web-based user interface for interacting with the XML Registry, enabling users to produce and display submittal forms for information resource packages. This interface will support interactive input for metadata submission and the downloading of forms, instructions, and tools necessary for producers. For consumers, the interface shall offer intuitive navigation, including tree/hierarchy and tabular views of XML tags, complemented by multiple search options to facilitate discovery. Each information resource will have its dedicated web page to display pertinent details, ensuring comprehensive access to metadata and related artifacts. Error messages and system notifications shall follow a consistent display standard, providing clear feedback to the user.
externalInterfaceRequirements.hardwareInterfaces: The **dii** XML Services (XS) shall interface with standard server hardware platforms that are compliant with the Common Operating Environment (**COE**), ensuring broad compatibility across the Defense Information Infrastructure. This includes interactions with network interface controllers for communication protocols such as **FTP** and **HTTP**, supporting both local and remote database access. The system must also interact with mass storage devices, including those configured with Redundant Array of Inexpensive Disks (**RAID**) for data persistence and reliability. Physical data and control interactions will adhere to established operating system and network stack interfaces, leveraging existing **DII COE** infrastructure for robust hardware connectivity and data transfer.
externalInterfaceRequirements.softwareInterfaces: The **dii** XML Services (XS) shall integrate with a variety of internal and external software components within the **dii** Common Operating Environment. It will provide standard Application Programming Interfaces (**APIs**) for registry services, enabling programmatic interaction with the XML Registry for submission, query, and management of information resources. Internally, the XS function shall interface transparently with existing data elements of the Database Interface (**DBIF**), Discretionary Access Control (**DAC**) systems, and Commercial Off-The-Shelf (**COTS**) Relational Database Management System (**RDBMS**) products. Communication with these systems will involve the exchange of structured data, potentially leveraging **SQL** commands as specified in **FIPS PUB 127-2**, to perform data management and retrieval operations. The system must also support integration with other design tools and environments for schema development and content creation.
externalInterfaceRequirements.communicationsInterfaces: The **dii** XML Services (XS) shall support various communication protocols for data exchange and system interactions within the Defense Information Infrastructure. It will utilize standard network protocols such as **FTP** and **HTTP** for the submission and downloading of information resource packages, ensuring secure and reliable file transfers. The system shall support both on-line interactive processes and off-line batch processes for metadata submission, accommodating diverse operational scenarios. In environments with degraded communications, the XS must allow for dynamic adjustment of session time-out values to maintain service continuity and user accessibility. For distributed database access, the system will leverage the Remote Database Access (**RDA**) standard, conforming to ISO (9579) application layer protocols and supporting two-phase commit transaction processing where updates to multiple remote databases require coordination. All communications shall adhere to specified **DII COE** security standards, including potential encryption mechanisms like **DES**, to protect data in transit.

systemFeatures.name: XML Registry Management
systemFeatures.description: This feature enables users to register, manage, and approve XML terms, tags, and semantic structures within a centralized registry, ensuring maximum semantic understanding and interoperability across the **dii** COE. It allows producers to contribute metadata and related artifacts, facilitating a collaborative environment for standardizing XML usage. The feature improves the user's ability to formalize data definitions and reduces redundancy across different systems. Technically, it involves robust data persistence, validation mechanisms, and workflow management for submissions and approvals. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: A Producer user initiates the submission of new XML metadata via a web form. Response: The system displays the Information Resource Submittal Package form, pre-populating fields where possible and validating initial input.

Stimulus: A Producer submits a compressed package of XML artifacts (Tag Spec, XML Spec, Sample XML) via email or FTP. Response: The system receives the package, parses the submitted XML Registry specification forms, and queues them for processing and populating the XML Registry database.

Stimulus: A COI Point of Contact (POC) reviews a pending submission for an XML Tag. Response: The system presents the submission details, allowing the POC to approve, reject, or forward the request to another registry.

Stimulus: A COI Point of Contact (POC) modifies the definition or status of an existing Information Resource. Response: The system updates the XML Registry database and records the change, notifying subscribed users or producers of the modification.

Stimulus: A user subscribes for notification of changes to a Community of Interest (COI) or specific Information Resources. Response: The system registers the subscription and automatically sends notifications when relevant changes are approved and published.
functionalRequirements: D-REQ-001: The system shall provide web forms for producers to submit XML Tag Specifications, XML Specs (e.g., DTD, DCD), and sample XML documents.
D-REQ-002: The system shall allow submission of Information Resource Submittal Packages via email or FTP.
D-REQ-003: The system shall parse submitted XML Registry specification forms and validate their content against predefined schema rules.
D-REQ-004: The system shall populate the XML Registry database with submitted metadata and related information resource artifacts.
D-REQ-005: The system shall support modification of specified terms, definitions of metadata, and status of Information Resources by authorized personnel.
D-REQ-006: The system shall enable the association of Information Resources based on predefined relationship types (e.g., uses/used_by, is_newer_version_of).
D-REQ-007: The system shall provide a mechanism for approving and rejecting submissions, including the ability to annotate reasons for rejection.
D-REQ-008: The system shall allow authorized users to forward submission requests to different federated registries based on predefined agreements.
D-REQ-009: The system shall provide a capability to post planned changes to a registry for preview by relevant stakeholders.
D-REQ-010: The system shall notify subscribed users or authorized producers of changes to Information Resources.
D-REQ-011: The system shall support both on-line interactive and off-line batch processes for metadata submission.
D-REQ-012: The system shall provide capabilities to create and manage usernames and superusers for registry administration.
D-REQ-013: The system shall establish and enforce acceptable naming conventions for XML tags that do not conflict with DOD data standards.

systemFeatures.name: XML Document Discovery and Consumption
systemFeatures.description: This feature provides consumers with comprehensive tools to discover, browse, and retrieve XML tags and associated semantic structures for reuse in their applications. It enhances interoperability by making standardized data elements easily accessible and understandable across the **dii** COE. Users can efficiently search for relevant information, view complex relationships, and access amplifying documentation, significantly improving data reuse and reducing development time. Technically, this requires robust indexing, search algorithms, and a flexible data presentation layer to support various viewing formats. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: A Consumer user searches for an XML Tag by providing a keyword. Response: The system displays a list of matching XML Tags, allowing the user to refine the search or view details.

Stimulus: A Consumer user selects an XML Tag to view its details. Response: The system displays a dedicated web page for the Information Resource, showing its attributes, relationships (e.g., Ancestor/Descendant, Uses/Used by), data type information, and versioning history.

Stimulus: A Consumer user requests to download an XML Tag or other selected Information Resources. Response: The system provides a downloadable package containing the requested resources in a specified format.

Stimulus: A Consumer user navigates the XML Tags via a tree/hierarchy structure. Response: The system dynamically renders the hierarchical view, allowing expansion and collapse of nodes to explore relationships.

Stimulus: A Consumer user needs to view the XML Tag Specification form for a specific information resource. Response: The system displays the original submission form, complete with all requesting information as provided by the author.
functionalRequirements: D-REQ-014: The system shall provide multiple search options to find specific XML Tags, including substring search within names and definitions.
D-REQ-015: The system shall enable searching for XML Tags by subscriber or author.
D-REQ-016: The system shall display XML Tags with their Ancestor/Descendant relationships.
D-REQ-017: The system shall display XML Tags with their Uses/Used by relationships, showing parent/child dependencies for complex container tags.
D-REQ-018: The system shall provide data type information for XML Tags, including related details and constraints.
D-REQ-019: The system shall display versioning relationships for Information Resources, showing different iterations and changes.
D-REQ-020: The system shall provide access to reference sets related to the domain values of an element or attribute.
D-REQ-021: The system shall display amplifying information resources, such as **ERwin** models, **DTDs**, and documents, associated with XML Tags.
D-REQ-022: The system shall provide a dedicated web page for each Information Resource, displaying all pertinent information according to its type.
D-REQ-023: The system shall allow users to view XML Tags via a tree/hierarchy structure or tabular format.
D-REQ-024: The system shall provide capabilities to download selected XML Tags or other Information Resources.
D-REQ-025: The system shall display the XML Tag Specification form for a given information resource author upon request.
D-REQ-026: The system shall catalog entities and attributes within servers to enable browsing, searching, and retrieving data related to XML sources.
D-REQ-027: The system shall establish rules to ensure maximum semantic understanding of a term as it appears in an XML document.
D-REQ-028: The system shall provide capabilities to link to **DTDs** and **DCDs** associated with XML resources.
D-REQ-029: The system shall support the concept of namespaces for unique identification within the Registry.

systemFeatures.name: XML Schema and Document Design
systemFeatures.description: This feature provides services for designing XML schemas, recording structures, and ensuring conformance to established standards. It empowers users to create and manage the structural definitions of XML documents, promoting consistency and validity across data exchanges. By offering validation capabilities and facilitating the reuse of existing tags, this feature streamlines the development of new XML applications and enhances data integrity within the **dii** COE. Technically, it involves schema definition languages, validation engines, and version control for structural metadata. Priority: Medium.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: A designer creates a new XML schema definition. Response: The system provides tools for recording the structure and checks it against standard style guides or conventions.
Stimulus: A designer attempts to validate an XML document against a defined schema. Response: The system performs the validation and reports any conformance errors.

Stimulus: A designer attempts to create a new XML tag or semantic structure. Response: The system provides an interface for defining the new tag and records its semantic structure in a **JTA**-compliant formalism.

Stimulus: A designer wishes to incorporate existing XML tags into a new schema. Response: The system provides mechanisms to browse and reference existing tags and their associated semantic structures from the Registry.
Stimulus: A designer modifies a schema or related metadata object. Response: The system versions the metadata object, maintaining a historical record of changes.
functionalRequirements: D-REQ-030: The system shall provide tools and services for designing the schema for an XML document.
D-REQ-031: The system shall record XML document structures and associated metadata.
D-REQ-032: The system shall check XML structures against established standards, such as style guides for tags.
D-REQ-033: The system shall validate XML documents and schemas for conformance to specifications.
D-REQ-034: The system shall enable the use and referencing of existing XML tags and their associated semantic structures from the Registry.
D-REQ-035: The system shall allow users to create new XML tags and associated semantic structures.
D-REQ-036: The system shall support interchangeability with other XML design tools and environments.
D-REQ-037: The system shall record semantic structures in a **JTA**-compliant formalism.
D-REQ-038: The system shall generate standard views of XML metadata objects.
D-REQ-039: The system shall provide versioning capabilities for metadata objects, tracking changes over time.

systemFeatures.name: XML Document Generation and Interrogation
systemFeatures.description: This feature facilitates the dynamic generation of XML documents and schemas, along with advanced capabilities for interrogating and extracting data views from existing XML content. It allows applications to produce standards-compliant XML output and to flexibly transform and query XML data for various purposes. This improves the adaptability of the system to diverse reporting and data exchange requirements. Technically, it involves XML serialization, transformation languages (**XSL**), and query processing for hierarchical data structures. Priority: Medium.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: An application requests the generation of an XML document based on a specified schema and data. Response: The system generates an XML document that includes (by reference or by value) the schema and data.

Stimulus: A user requests to extract specific data views from an existing XML document. Response: The system processes the document and materializes different query results into alternate tree structures or other representations.

Stimulus: The system processes an incoming XML document for conformance checking. Response: The system verifies if the document is well-formed and valid according to its schema, reporting any discrepancies.

Stimulus: An application needs to transmit metadata to construct an alternate, semantically-valid view of XML data. Response: The system transmits sufficient metadata, possibly using **XSL**, to enable the reconstruction of diverse views.
Stimulus: The system renders an XML document for display. Response: The system processes the XML and associated stylesheets (if any) to produce a human-readable representation.
functionalRequirements: D-REQ-040: The system shall generate XML documents that include (by reference or by value) a specified schema.
D-REQ-041: The system shall develop a scheme to permit dynamic cross-referencing and indexing of XML objects.
D-REQ-042: The system shall generate language (natural language) and charset values for XML documents.
D-REQ-043: The system shall check XML documents for well-formedness and validity against their schemas.
D-REQ-044: The system shall extract views of data from XML documents based on defined queries or transformations.
D-REQ-045: The system shall disassemble XML documents to materialize different query results into different tree structures.
D-REQ-046: The system shall render XML documents for display or further processing.
D-REQ-047: The system shall interrogate XML documents to retrieve specific data or metadata.
D-REQ-048: The system shall transmit sufficient metadata to construct alternate, semantically-valid views (e.g., using **XSL**).
D-REQ-049: The system shall express the version of generated XML documents or schemas.

systemFeatures.name: XML Document Consumption and Validation
systemFeatures.description: This feature focuses on the robust consumption, parsing, and validation of incoming XML and schema documents, ensuring that all received data adheres to predefined structural and semantic rules. It provides core parsing capabilities (DOM, SAX) and enforces validation constraints to maintain data integrity and enable meaningful sharing of XML-based information. This significantly enhances the reliability of data ingestion and processing within the **dii** COE. Technically, it relies on XML parsers, schema processors, and transformation engines for converting hierarchical data. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: The **dii** XS receives an incoming XML document. Response: The system utilizes **DOM** or **SAX** parsers to process the XML document.
Stimulus: The system needs to check the conformance of a received XML document. Response: The system verifies if the document is well-formed and valid against its schema, flagging any errors.
Stimulus: A system component requires semantically-valid views from an XML document. Response: The system extracts the requested views, potentially using **XSL**, for further application processing.

Stimulus: The system needs to convert a hierarchical XML view into a relational representation. Response: The system disassembles the hierarchical view and transforms it into a relational structure suitable for database storage or relational queries.

Stimulus: The system identifies a need for validation constraints to enable shared XML schemas. Response: The system creates validation constraints that support meaningful sharing of XML-based schemas and related information, ensuring data consistency across disparate systems.
functionalRequirements: D-REQ-050: The system shall consume XML and schema documents using industry-standard parsers (e.g., **DOM**, **SAX**).
D-REQ-051: The system shall check the conformance of incoming XML documents for well-formedness.
D-REQ-052: The system shall validate incoming XML documents against their specified schemas.
D-REQ-053: The system shall create validation constraints to enable meaningful sharing of XML-based schemas and related information.
D-REQ-054: The system shall extract semantically-valid views from an XML document (e.g., using **XSL**).
D-REQ-055: The system shall disassemble a hierarchical XML view into a relational representation.
D-REQ-056: The system shall render consumed XML documents for internal processing or external display.

performanceRequirements: D-REQ-057: The **dii** XS shall support variable workloads, dynamically tuning its data management services to accommodate diverse operational demands in fixed environments, ensuring optimal resource utilization and response times. Rationale: Military operations require robust and adaptable systems that can handle fluctuating data traffic without degradation in service quality.

D-REQ-058: The **dii** XS shall maintain acceptable performance in a degraded communications environment by allowing user-definable and resettable session time-out values prior to initialization of a user session. Rationale: Deployed units often operate under challenging network conditions, necessitating flexible system behavior to prevent service interruptions and ensure continuous access to critical data.

D-REQ-059: The system shall process ANSI standard **SQL** as specified in **FIPS PUB 127-2** with a query response time of under 500ms for typical read operations on up to 1 million records. Rationale: Efficient data retrieval is crucial for mission-critical applications within the **dii** COE, directly impacting operational effectiveness and decision-making speed.
safetyRequirements: D-REQ-060: The **dii** XS shall implement robust transaction journaling for all data modifications to ensure data recoverability in the event of system failures. Rationale: In a military context, data integrity and availability are paramount; transaction journaling provides a critical mechanism for restoring data to a consistent state after unexpected interruptions. The system shall support commit/rollback mechanisms for individual transactions.

D-REQ-061: The system shall leverage **RAID** (Redundant Array of Inexpensive Disks) storage configurations for all persistent data stores to minimize data loss risk and maximize data availability. Rationale: **RAID** provides hardware-level data redundancy, which is essential for maintaining continuous operation and protecting sensitive military information from disk failures. The system shall integrate with disk mirroring technologies.

D-REQ-062: The **dii** XS shall provide capabilities for mirroring databases on a transaction basis, supporting rapid error or failure recovery through **COTS RDBMS** utilities. Rationale: Ensuring high availability and disaster recovery is critical for military systems, and real-time database mirroring is a key strategy to achieve minimal downtime and data loss.
securityRequirements: D-REQ-063: The **dii** XS shall enforce Discretionary Access Controls (**DAC**) to restrict access to objects based on the identity of subjects or groups to which they belong, allowing authorized subjects to pass permissions. Rationale: This provides flexibility in access management for various user classes while maintaining a baseline of controlled access, allowing for granular control over data sharing.

D-REQ-064: The **dii** XS shall implement Mandatory Access Controls (**MAC**) to mediate access to objects based on the clearance level of the subject and the sensitivity label of the object, ensuring stricter hierarchical security enforcement. Rationale: **MAC** is crucial for military systems dealing with classified information, guaranteeing that data sensitivity and user clearance levels are consistently respected regardless of user discretion.

D-REQ-065: The system shall provide capabilities for creating and managing usernames, superusers, user permissions, and privileges to control access to **dii** XS functionalities and data. Rationale: A robust user management system is fundamental for maintaining accountability, preventing unauthorized access, and ensuring that only authorized personnel can perform specific actions within the XML Services. The system shall support secure authentication mechanisms.

D-REQ-066: All communication interfaces handling sensitive data shall utilize established **DII COE** security protocols, including encryption techniques such as **DES** (Data Encryption Standard), to protect data in transit. Rationale: Protecting military data from interception and unauthorized access during transmission is paramount to national security and operational integrity. The system shall comply with all relevant **DOD** security standards.
softwareQualityAttributes: D-REQ-067: Portability: The **dii** XS software shall be portable and required to execute on all **COE**-compliant platforms. Rationale: Given the diverse and evolving hardware and software landscapes within the DII, portability ensures that the XML Services can be deployed and operated across various environments without significant modifications, maximizing its utility and longevity.

D-REQ-068: Reliability: The system shall support managing various types of data, database architectures, and platforms, providing high availability even in the presence of distributed database management systems and network degradations. Rationale: Mission-critical applications depend on continuous and error-free operation, requiring a reliable system that can adapt to environmental challenges and maintain data accessibility under adverse conditions.

D-REQ-069: Maintainability: The **dii** XS developer shall be responsible for software maintenance, software support, and software updates. Rationale: Clear assignment of maintenance responsibility ensures that the system can be efficiently updated, bug-fixed, and evolved over its lifecycle, preventing technical debt and sustaining operational effectiveness.

D-REQ-070: Interoperability: The **dii** XS shall isolate vendor-unique implementations of data access, providing applications a means of avoiding dependencies on physical access models and supporting data management functions for distributed DBMS. Rationale: High interoperability is a core requirement for the DII COE, enabling seamless data exchange and integration across disparate military systems and reducing vendor lock-in.

D-REQ-071: Testability: The **dii** XS shall be qualified through formal validation tests of the **SRS** level requirements, applying methods including test, demonstration, analysis, and inspection (**T, D, A, I**). Rationale: Rigorous testing and qualification are essential for military software to ensure it meets all specified requirements and performs reliably under operational conditions, minimizing risks and ensuring mission success.
businessRules: D-REQ-072: The **dii** XS shall adhere to the principle of maximum semantic understanding of a term as it appears in an XML document, achieved through coordinated registration and approval processes among the Community of Interest. Rationale: This ensures that all XML data exchanged within the **dii** COE is unambiguously interpreted, preventing miscommunication and enabling effective data reuse.

D-REQ-073: The system shall establish acceptable naming conventions for XML elements and attributes that do not conflict with existing Department of Defense (**DOD**) data standards conventions. Rationale: Consistency in naming is crucial for maintainability, discoverability, and long-term data governance across the vast **DOD** enterprise.

D-REQ-074: The **dii** XS shall support federation agreements for passing requests to different registries, allowing for a distributed and interconnected network of XML information resources. Rationale: This enables scalability and broader collaboration by allowing different communities or agencies to manage their own registries while still participating in a larger interoperable framework.

D-REQ-075: The system shall enforce a workflow for information resource status transitions (developmental, candidate, approved, rejected, retired) managed by the Community of Interest Point of Contact (POC). Rationale: A formal lifecycle management process ensures quality control and orderly evolution of shared XML assets, maintaining the integrity and relevance of the registry over time.

otherRequirements: D-REQ-076: Logistics Requirements: The **dii** XS developer is responsible for software maintenance, software support, and software updates for the XML Services component. Rationale: Centralized responsibility ensures consistent quality, expertise, and timely delivery of critical updates.

D-REQ-077: Packaging Requirements: The **dii** XS software shall be delivered in accordance with **DII COE** guidelines. Rationale: Adherence to packaging standards ensures proper integration, deployment, and configuration management within the Defense Information Infrastructure.

D-REQ-078: Precedence and Criticality: All requirements in this document shall be assigned a precedence and criticality code (Essential, Desirable, or Optional) to guide development prioritization. Rationale: Prioritization is crucial for managing resources effectively and focusing on the most critical functionalities and quality attributes for mission success.

flowchartDiagram.syntaxExplanation: Flowcharts visualize processes and decision points. Nodes represent steps or decisions, and arrows indicate the flow. Shapes denote specific types of steps: rounded rectangles for start/end, rectangles for processes, diamonds for decisions, and parallelograms for input/output. Direction (`TD` for Top-Down or `LR` for Left-Right) must be specified. Node IDs must be alphanumeric; labels with spaces must be double-quoted. Avoid reserved words like 'end' as node IDs.
flowchartDiagram.code:
flowchart TD
    StartDII["Start dii XML Services"] --> XSInit("Initialize XS Components")
    XSInit --> EnvMode{"Operating Environment Mode?"}
    EnvMode -->|Fixed/Static| FixedProc["Tune Data Management Services"] --> DataMgmt(Manage Distributed DBMS)
    EnvMode -->|Changing/Dynamic| DynProc["Redefine Connect Descriptors"] --> DataMgmt
    EnvMode -->|Degraded Comms| DegradedProc["Adjust Session Time-outs"] --> DataMgmt

    DataMgmt --> UserRole{"User Role?"}

    UserRole -->|Producer| ProducerFlow(Produce & Submit XML Resources)
    ProducerFlow --> ProducerAction1["Prepare Submittal Package"]
    ProducerAction1 --> ProducerAction2["Submit Metadata (Online/Batch)"]
    ProducerAction2 --> RegistryProc(Process Submission)

    RegistryProc --> RegistryValidation{"Valid Submission?"}
    RegistryValidation -->|No| Reject(Reject & Annotate) --> ProducerFlow
    RegistryValidation -->|Yes| Approve(Approve Submission)
    Approve --> RegistryUpdate["Populate/Modify XML Registry"]
    RegistryUpdate --> NotifySubscribers["Notify Subscribers of Changes"]
    NotifySubscribers --> RegistryEnd(Registry Ready)

    UserRole -->|Consumer| ConsumerFlow(Discover & Consume XML Resources)
    ConsumerFlow --> ConsumerAction1["Search/Browse XML Tags"]
    ConsumerAction1 --> ConsumerAction2["View/Download Resources"]
    ConsumerAction2 --> ConsumerEnd(Resource Consumed)

    UserRole -->|Manager| ManagerFlow(Administer & Monitor Registry)
    ManagerFlow --> ManagerAction1["Manage Users & Naming Conventions"]
    ManagerAction1 --> ManagerAction2["Monitor Data Model Changes"]
    ManagerAction2 --> ManagerEnd(Registry Managed)

    RegistryEnd --> EndDII["End dii XML Services"]
    ConsumerEnd --> EndDII
    ManagerEnd --> EndDII
flowchartDiagram.caption: dii XML Services Core Process Flow

sequenceDiagram.syntaxExplanation: Sequence diagrams illustrate the interaction between participants over time. Participants (actors, systems, components) are defined first. Messages are shown as arrows, indicating synchronous (`->>`) or asynchronous (`->`) calls. Activations (`->>+` for start, `-->>-` for end) represent periods a participant is active. Use `participant <ID> as "<Label>"` for readability. Avoid `activate`/`deactivate` pairs directly within `alt`/`opt`/`loop` blocks; prefer arrow notation.
sequenceDiagram.code:
sequenceDiagram
    participant XMLProducer as "XML Producer User"
    participant WebUI as "Web User Interface"
    participant DIIXS as "dii XML Services"
    participant XSRegistry as "XML Registry Database"
    participant COIPOC as "COI Point of Contact"
    participant Subscriber as "Registered Subscriber"

    XMLProducer->>+WebUI: "Prepare & Submit XML Package"
    WebUI->>DIIXS: "Upload: Information Resource Package (Compressed)"
    activate DIIXS
    DIIXS->>DIIXS: "Parse XML Registry Forms"
    DIIXS->>+XSRegistry: "Propose: New Information Resource"
    XSRegistry-->>-DIIXS: "Submission Recorded (Pending Approval)"
    DIIXS-->>-WebUI: "Submission Confirmation"
    WebUI-->>-XMLProducer: "Display: Submission Acknowledged"

    COIPOC->>+DIIXS: "Request: Review Submissions"
    DIIXS->>DIIXS: "Retrieve Pending Items"
    DIIXS->>COIPOC: "Display: Submission Details"
    deactivate DIIXS

    alt Approval Path
        COIPOC->>+DIIXS: "Action: Approve Submission"
        DIIXS->>+XSRegistry: "Update: Status to Approved"
        XSRegistry-->>-DIIXS: "Status Updated"
        DIIXS->>Subscriber: "Notify: New Resource Approved"
        deactivate DIIXS
    else Rejection Path
        COIPOC->>+DIIXS: "Action: Reject Submission (with Annotation)"
        DIIXS->>+XSRegistry: "Update: Status to Rejected"
        XSRegistry-->>-DIIXS: "Status Updated"
        DIIXS->>XMLProducer: "Notify: Submission Rejected (Reason)"
        deactivate DIIXS
    end

    DIIXS->>XSRegistry: "Continuously Monitor for Changes"
sequenceDiagram.caption: XML Information Resource Submission and Approval

entityRelationshipDiagram.syntaxExplanation: ER diagrams model database schemas, showing entities (tables) and their relationships. Entities are defined with attributes, specifying primary keys (PK), foreign keys (FK), and unique keys (UK). Relationships are defined between entities, including cardinality (e.g., `||--o{` for one-to-many, `||--||` for one-to-one) and a quoted label describing the relationship. Attribute names must be alphanumeric without spaces, while entity names with spaces must be quoted. FK relationships should imply `}|--||` or `}|--o{` based on the 'many' side. Avoid `NN` for Not Null constraints, use only `PK`, `FK`, `UK` for keys.
