  participant AgentB as Receiver
  participant MulticastGroup as Group
  participant EncryptionSvc as EncryptionService

  AgentA->>+EncryptionSvc: Request Key (GroupKey)
  EncryptionSvc-->>-AgentA: Provide Key (GroupKey)

  AgentA->>AgentA: Prepare Multicast Message
  AgentA->>+EncryptionSvc: Encrypt Message
  EncryptionSvc-->>-AgentA: Encrypted Message

  AgentA->>Group: Send Multicast Message
  note over Group: Distributed to subscribed agents

  Group->>+AgentB: Receive Multicast Message
  AgentB->>+EncryptionSvc: Decrypt Message
  EncryptionSvc-->>-AgentB: Decrypted Message
  AgentB-->>-Group: Process Message
sequenceDiagram.caption: Secured Multicast Communication Sequence

entityRelationshipDiagram.syntaxExplanation: ER diagrams model the relationships between entities in a system. Entities represent tables or objects, with attributes (properties). Relationships define how entities are connected, including cardinality (e.g., one-to-one `||--||`, one-to-many `||--o{`, many-to-one `}|--||`). `PK` (Primary Key), `FK` (Foreign Key), and `UK` (Unique Key) denote attribute types. Attribute names must be alphanumeric without spaces or quotes. Entity names with spaces must be quoted. Relationship labels must be double-quoted.
entityRelationshipDiagram.code:
erDiagram
  AGENT ||--o{ MESSAGE : "sends"
  MESSAGE }|--|| AGENT : "sent by"

  AGENT ||--o{ MESSAGE : "receives unicast"
  MESSAGE }o--|| AGENT : "targets unicast agent"
  
  "MULTICAST_GROUP" ||--o{ MESSAGE : "includes multicast message"
  MESSAGE }o--|| "MULTICAST_GROUP" : "targets group"
  
  AGENT ||--o{ GROUP_MEMBERSHIP : "participates in"
  "MULTICAST_GROUP" ||--o{ GROUP_MEMBERSHIP : "manages"
  GROUP_MEMBERSHIP }|--|| AGENT : "member is"
  GROUP_MEMBERSHIP }|--|| "MULTICAST_GROUP" : "group is"
  
  ENCRYPTION_DETAIL }|--|| MESSAGE : "encrypts"

  AGENT {
    int agent_id PK
    string agent_name
    string network_address
  }

  MESSAGE {
    int message_id PK
    int sender_id FK
    int unicast_receiver_id FK "Optional (unicast only)"
    int multicast_group_id FK "Optional (multicast only)"
    string content
    string message_type
    datetime timestamp
  }

  "MULTICAST_GROUP" {
    int group_id PK
    string group_address
    string group_name UK
  }

  GROUP_MEMBERSHIP {
    int membership_id PK
    int agent_id FK
    int group_id FK
    datetime join_date
    datetime leave_date
  }

  ENCRYPTION_DETAIL {
    int encryption_id PK
    int message_id FK
    string encryption_algo
    string key_id
    bool is_encrypted
  }
entityRelationshipDiagram.caption: Agent Communication Domain Model

revisionHistory.description: Initial Release based on PEPPOL Deliverable D2.1 functional and non-functional requirements specification for the VCD.
revisionHistory.author: SRA System

introduction.purpose: The primary purpose of the **peppol** project is to address the significant market fragmentation and lack of interoperability within the European public eProcurement infrastructure. This initiative aims to establish common principles and technical solutions that enable pan-European eProcurement across Member States. By facilitating the electronic exchange of company information and evidence, **peppol** seeks to reduce administrative burdens and transaction costs for economic operators and public sector awarding entities. The overarching goal is to foster a more competitive, transparent, and efficient single market by 2010, aligning with the Lisbon objectives for a dynamic knowledge-based economy.
introduction.documentConventions: This document adheres to the IEEE Recommended Practice for Software Requirements Specifications (IEEE 830-1998) standard for structuring and presenting requirements. Key terms, acronyms, and abbreviations are defined in the Glossary (Appendix A) to ensure clarity and consistency throughout. All functional requirements are articulated using the 'The system shall...' construct, specifying atomic, testable behaviors. Mermaid syntax is utilized for visual analysis models in Appendix B, following strict diagramming guidelines.
introduction.intendedAudience: This Software Requirements Specification (SRS) is intended for a diverse audience, including project managers, software developers, quality assurance personnel, and technical stakeholders involved in the **peppol** project. Business analysts and future system maintainers will also find this document valuable for understanding the system's intended functionality and design constraints. Furthermore, policy makers and legal experts involved in pan-European eProcurement initiatives may consult this document to understand the technical implications and requirements for interoperability.
introduction.productScope: The **peppol** Virtual Company Dossier (VCD) system encompasses a phased approach to developing an interoperable solution for electronic public procurement across Europe. Its scope includes mechanisms for economic operators to utilize company information already registered in one Member State and submit it electronically to awarding entities in another. The system is designed to support different maturity levels, starting from pre-VCD mapping to advanced networked package exchange, focusing on the evidence required for qualification and selection criteria in public tenders. It is strictly constrained to the functionalities directly related to VCD creation, management, and secure cross-border submission within the defined European legal and technical frameworks.
references: IEEE 830-1998 IEEE Recommended Practice for Software Requirements Specifications.
ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018 Systems and software engineering — Life cycle processes — Requirements engineering.

overallDescription.productPerspective: The **peppol** Virtual Company Dossier (VCD) system is conceived as a pivotal component within the broader European eProcurement infrastructure. It is not a standalone application but rather an interoperable solution designed to augment and connect existing national eProcurement systems, which are currently characterized by high fragmentation and a lack of cross-border interoperability. The **peppol** VCD acts as a bridge, enabling economic operators to seamlessly manage and submit company information and evidence across different Member States without encountering the technical and legal barriers prevalent in current systems. It represents a significant advancement over legacy, country-specific procedures by introducing standardized electronic data exchange.
productFunctions: Facilitate the mapping of diverse national company data formats to a common Virtual Company Dossier (VCD) taxonomy.
Enable economic operators to compile and manage a simple VCD package for submission in public tenders.
Support the creation of advanced VCD packages, incorporating dynamic updates and potentially automated evidence retrieval.
Establish a networked VCD exchange capability for secure, pan-European transmission of company dossiers between authorized entities.
Provide mechanisms for public sector awarding entities to verify and assess VCDs submitted by economic operators from various Member States.
Ensure compliance with relevant legal, organizational, and semantic frameworks governing public procurement and data exchange within the European Union.

userClassesAndCharacteristics.characteristics: These users represent companies or individuals bidding for public contracts across Europe. They typically possess varying levels of technical expertise but require an intuitive interface to prepare, manage, and submit their Virtual Company Dossiers (VCDs). Their primary need is to reduce administrative burden, ensure compliance, and participate in cross-border tenders with ease. They are sensitive to data accuracy, security, and the overall efficiency of the submission process, often operating under strict deadlines.

These users are governmental or public organizations responsible for issuing tenders and awarding contracts. They require robust functionality to receive, verify, and assess VCDs submitted by economic operators, potentially from different Member States. Their key concerns include legal compliance, data integrity, transparency, and the ability to efficiently process a high volume of tenders. They typically have established internal procurement processes and require the **peppol** system to integrate smoothly with their existing workflows.

These entities, such as national business registers or tax authorities, are responsible for maintaining official company data and issuing evidence documents. They act as data sources for the VCD system, providing verified information upon request or through automated interfaces. Their primary characteristic is adherence to national legal frameworks and data protection regulations, requiring secure and authorized access mechanisms for data dissemination. Technical expertise for integration with the **peppol** network is expected.

These users are responsible for the technical operation, maintenance, and configuration of the **peppol** VCD system. They possess advanced technical knowledge, including database management, network administration, and system security. Their needs include monitoring system performance, managing user access rights, troubleshooting issues, and ensuring system uptime and data integrity. They are critical for implementing updates and ensuring the system's continuous compliance with evolving standards and regulations.

overallDescription.operatingEnvironment: The **peppol** Virtual Company Dossier (VCD) system is designed to operate within a complex, distributed, and pan-European Information and Communication Technology (ICT) environment. It is expected to integrate seamlessly with existing national eProcurement infrastructures, which may include various hardware platforms, operating systems, and database technologies. The system will primarily function as a **Web**-based application, accessible via standard **Web** browsers across different client operating systems such as **Windows**, **macOS**, and **Linux**.
designAndImplementationConstraints: The **peppol** VCD system must adhere strictly to the legal frameworks governing public procurement within the European Union, including directives such as 2004/18/EC, and relevant national public procurement acts. It must also comply with data protection regulations, particularly regarding the handling of sensitive company information and personal data. This includes adherence to principles of data minimization, purpose limitation, and secure processing, ensuring legal validity of electronic evidence.

Implementation must align with specified semantic frameworks, including adherence to common terminologies, taxonomies, and data representation patterns. Relevant standards such as Universal Business Language (**UBL**), extensible Business Reporting Language (**XBRL**), and Web Ontology Language (**OWL**) for data exchange and description must be adopted where appropriate. This ensures semantic interoperability across different Member States and eProcurement platforms.

The system is constrained by the need for technical interoperability with a variety of existing national eProcurement systems and official registers, requiring the use of open standards and well-defined Application Programming Interfaces (**APIs**). Communication protocols must conform to recognized European standards, with a strong emphasis on security and reliable data transfer. The overall architecture must also align with the broader **peppol** infrastructure guidelines and initiatives, such as the CEN BII workshop for business interoperability interfaces.
userDocumentation: The **peppol** system shall deliver a comprehensive User Manual providing detailed instructions for Economic Operators on how to create, manage, and submit Virtual Company Dossiers. This manual will include step-by-step guides for data mapping, package compilation, and submission, along with troubleshooting tips.

An Administration Guide shall be provided for System Administrators, outlining system configuration, monitoring procedures, security settings, and backup/recovery processes. This guide will cover technical aspects necessary for the efficient and secure operation of the VCD platform.

Online Help functionality shall be integrated into the **Web** interface, offering context-sensitive assistance and quick access to information regarding specific features or fields. This will enhance usability and reduce the learning curve for all user classes, ensuring immediate support during VCD preparation and submission.

Developer API Documentation shall be made available for third-party developers and Member State Authorities wishing to integrate their systems with the **peppol** VCD platform. This documentation will detail all available **APIs**, data models, communication protocols, and authentication mechanisms required for seamless interoperability and evidence exchange.
assumptionsAndDependencies: It is assumed that Member States will continue to evolve their national eProcurement infrastructures, but will remain committed to adopting European interoperability standards and participating in the **peppol** network. The success of the VCD system heavily depends on the willingness of national authorities to provide access to official company registers and evidence data through agreed-upon interfaces. Any significant divergence in national legal or technical frameworks from the envisioned pan-European approach could impede the system's effectiveness and broad adoption.

The **peppol** VCD system relies on the continued development and stability of core European interoperability initiatives and underlying technical standards, such as those from CEN and the European Interoperability Framework (**EIF**). The availability of secure and reliable communication channels between Member States, including robust network infrastructure and agreed-upon security protocols, is a critical dependency. Furthermore, the system assumes that economic operators and contracting authorities possess adequate ICT literacy and infrastructure to engage with the electronic VCD processes.

externalInterfaceRequirements.userInterfaces: The **peppol** Virtual Company Dossier (VCD) system shall provide a consistent and intuitive **Web**-based user interface for Economic Operators to manage and submit their company dossiers. This interface will adhere to modern usability standards, employing clear navigation, consistent iconography, and accessible design principles to minimize the learning curve. Key functionalities like data input forms, document upload mechanisms, status dashboards, and submission confirmations will be presented clearly. Visual cues and structured workflows will guide users through the process of mapping national data, compiling evidence packages, and monitoring the status of their VCD submissions to various tenders.
externalInterfaceRequirements.hardwareInterfaces: The **peppol** VCD system will primarily interact with standard server hardware for its backend processing, database storage, and network communication. No direct, specialized hardware interfaces are anticipated for the core VCD application. However, client-side interactions will rely on conventional computing devices such as desktop computers, laptops, and potentially tablets, utilizing standard input devices like keyboards and pointing devices. These client devices must support modern **Web** browser functionality and possess adequate processing power and network connectivity to interact efficiently with the VCD system.
externalInterfaceRequirements.softwareInterfaces: The **peppol** VCD system shall interface with various external software components crucial for its pan-European operation. This includes integration with national company registers and other official evidence-providing systems in different Member States, primarily through **RESTful APIs** or **SOAP Web Services**. Data exchange will utilize agreed-upon semantic standards such as **UBL** or **XBRL**, with **XML** as a common serialization format. The system will also interface with an Identity and Access Management (**IAM**) system for secure authentication and authorization of Economic Operators and Contracting Authorities. Furthermore, it will interact with underlying database management systems (**DBMS**) for persistent data storage and potentially document management systems for handling evidence files.
externalInterfaceRequirements.communicationsInterfaces: The **peppol** VCD system requires robust communications interfaces to enable secure and efficient cross-border data exchange. It shall support standardized **HTTPS** protocol for all **Web**-based client-server interactions, ensuring data encryption in transit. For inter-system communication, such as with national registers or other **peppol** infrastructure components, secure **B2B** messaging protocols based on **AS4** or similar standards shall be utilized. Message formatting will adhere to agreed-upon **XML** schemas and semantic standards to ensure machine readability and semantic interoperability. All communications must implement strong cryptographic measures to ensure confidentiality, integrity, and authenticity, complying with European security and data protection regulations, and must be capable of handling varying data transfer rates to support diverse network conditions across Member States.

systemFeatures.name: Pre-VCD Mapping Tool
systemFeatures.description: This feature provides economic operators with a utility to map their existing national company data into a standardized **peppol** Virtual Company Dossier (VCD) taxonomy. Its user value lies in significantly reducing the initial effort and complexity associated with preparing company information for pan-European tenders, by abstracting away the idiosyncrasies of national data formats. Technically, this impacts the system by requiring robust data transformation engines and extensible schema management capabilities, forming the foundational layer for subsequent VCD package creation. The priority for this feature is High, as it is the initial gateway for data ingestion and standardization.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Economic operator uploads a company data file in a national format. Response: The system parses the file and presents a visual representation of the mapped data against the **peppol** VCD taxonomy for review.

Stimulus: Economic operator identifies a discrepancy in the mapped data fields. Response: The system provides an interface for manual correction or re-mapping of individual data elements, offering suggestions based on semantic analysis.

Stimulus: Economic operator confirms the mapping process. Response: The system stores the standardized **VCD** data in a temporary repository, ready for package compilation, and generates a mapping report.
functionalRequirements: P-REQ-001: The system shall provide an interface for economic operators to upload company data files in supported national formats.
P-REQ-002: The system shall automatically parse uploaded national data files and attempt to map relevant fields to the **peppol** VCD taxonomy.
P-REQ-003: The system shall display a visual representation of the mapped data, highlighting any unmapped fields or potential discrepancies.
P-REQ-004: The system shall allow economic operators to manually adjust or correct mapped data fields prior to finalization.
P-REQ-005: The system shall validate the mapped data against the **peppol** VCD schema to ensure structural and semantic correctness.
P-REQ-006: The system shall generate and store a standardized VCD data set following successful mapping and validation.
P-REQ-007: The system shall provide a report detailing the mapping process, including any warnings or errors encountered.

systemFeatures.name: VCD Simple Package Creation
systemFeatures.description: This feature enables economic operators to compile a basic Virtual Company Dossier (VCD) package, bundling all necessary evidence and company information for submission to a specific public tender. Its user value is the simplification of tender submission by providing a single, verifiable, and standardized electronic document that can be reused across tenders. The technical impact involves robust data aggregation, digital signing capabilities, and secure storage for compiled packages. The priority for this feature is High, as it represents the core deliverable for tender applications.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Economic operator selects previously mapped company data and a specific tender. Response: The system presents a customizable checklist of required evidence based on tender criteria, pre-populating with available data.

Stimulus: Economic operator uploads additional evidence documents or links to external proofs. Response: The system integrates the new evidence into the VCD, performing basic validation checks on format and size.
Stimulus: Economic operator initiates the VCD package compilation. Response: The system digitally signs the VCD package, encrypts it, and stores it in a secure repository, ready for submission.
functionalRequirements: P-REQ-008: The system shall allow economic operators to select a tender and associated mapped company data for VCD package creation.
P-REQ-009: The system shall present a dynamic checklist of required evidence based on the selected tender's criteria.
P-REQ-010: The system shall enable economic operators to upload supporting evidence documents in specified formats (e.g., PDF, XML).
P-REQ-011: The system shall allow economic operators to link to external, verifiable evidence sources (e.g., national registers).
P-REQ-012: The system shall compile the selected company data and evidence into a single, standardized VCD package.
P-REQ-013: The system shall digitally sign the VCD package to ensure authenticity and integrity.
P-REQ-014: The system shall encrypt the VCD package to protect sensitive information during storage and transmission.
P-REQ-015: The system shall store the compiled VCD package in a secure, tamper-evident repository.

systemFeatures.name: VCD Advanced Package Management
systemFeatures.description: This feature enhances the simple VCD functionality by introducing dynamic management and re-compilation capabilities, allowing economic operators to update existing dossiers or tailor them for specific tenders more efficiently. The user value lies in maintaining up-to-date and compliant evidence with reduced manual oversight, crucial for complex and multi-stage procurement processes. Technically, this requires sophisticated version control for VCD components, advanced business rules for evidence validity periods, and potentially automated integration with evidence providers for real-time updates. The priority for this feature is Medium, offering significant workflow improvements beyond basic functionality.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Economic operator reviews an existing VCD package for an upcoming tender. Response: The system highlights outdated evidence or changed tender requirements, suggesting necessary updates.

Stimulus: Economic operator requests to re-compile a VCD package with updated information. Response: The system dynamically fetches the latest available evidence, updates the VCD, and notifies the operator of any new discrepancies or data gaps.

Stimulus: Economic operator applies specific filters or criteria to an advanced VCD. Response: The system generates a customized VCD variant tailored to the exact requirements, ensuring optimal relevance and compliance.
functionalRequirements: P-REQ-016: The system shall allow economic operators to view and manage previously created VCD packages.
P-REQ-017: The system shall automatically identify and flag outdated evidence within a VCD package based on predefined validity rules.
P-REQ-018: The system shall enable economic operators to initiate a re-compilation of an existing VCD package.
P-REQ-019: The system shall automatically retrieve updated evidence from integrated national registers or designated sources during re-compilation.
P-REQ-020: The system shall notify economic operators of any issues or missing evidence during the re-compilation process.
P-REQ-021: The system shall provide version control for VCD packages, allowing rollback to previous validated states.
P-REQ-022: The system shall enable customization of VCD packages based on specific tender requirements or criteria.

systemFeatures.name: VCD Network Exchange
systemFeatures.description: This feature facilitates the secure, pan-European exchange of Virtual Company Dossiers (VCDs) between authorized economic operators, public sector awarding entities, and evidence providers across the **peppol** network. The user value is the realization of true cross-border eProcurement, eliminating geographical barriers and significantly streamlining the submission and verification processes for tenders involving multiple Member States. From a technical standpoint, this necessitates robust, distributed access control mechanisms, secure inter-system communication protocols, and a high degree of semantic interoperability across all participating entities. The priority for this feature is High, as it embodies the ultimate vision of the **peppol** project.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: A Contracting Authority from one Member State requests a VCD from an Economic Operator in another Member State. Response: The system securely transmits the authorized VCD package from the operator's designated repository to the requesting authority, logging the transaction.

Stimulus: A Contracting Authority initiates the verification of evidence within a received VCD. Response: The system communicates with the relevant national evidence provider systems to retrieve and cross-reference evidence, providing a verification report to the authority.

Stimulus: An Economic Operator revokes access to their VCD for a specific Contracting Authority. Response: The system immediately terminates access to the VCD for the specified authority, ensuring data privacy and control.
functionalRequirements: P-REQ-023: The system shall provide secure mechanisms for Economic Operators to authorize the sharing of their VCD packages with specific Contracting Authorities.
P-REQ-024: The system shall enable Contracting Authorities to securely request and receive VCD packages from Economic Operators across Member States.
P-REQ-025: The system shall implement robust access control policies to ensure only authorized entities can access specific VCDs or evidence components.
P-REQ-026: The system shall support secure, tamper-evident transfer of VCD packages between authorized participants over the **peppol** network.
P-REQ-027: The system shall facilitate automated verification of VCD evidence by communicating with designated national evidence provider systems.
P-REQ-028: The system shall provide audit trails for all VCD access, sharing, and verification activities.
P-REQ-029: The system shall enable Economic Operators to revoke access to their VCDs from specific Contracting Authorities at any time.
P-REQ-030: The system shall ensure that all cross-border VCD exchanges comply with applicable data protection and privacy regulations.

performanceRequirements: P-NFR-001: The **peppol** VCD system shall achieve an average response time of less than 3 seconds for all VCD package compilation and submission operations, even under peak loads of 1,000 concurrent users. This is critical to ensure a fluid user experience and avoid delays in time-sensitive tender processes. Failure to meet this requirement could lead to user frustration and missed tender deadlines.

P-NFR-002: The system shall be capable of processing up to 10,000 VCD package submissions per hour during peak periods. This throughput is necessary to support a high volume of tenders across multiple Member States, particularly towards submission deadlines, without degradation of service. Insufficient processing capacity would create bottlenecks and hinder pan-European eProcurement adoption.

P-NFR-003: The VCD system shall maintain an availability of 99.9% over a 24/7 operational week, excluding scheduled maintenance windows. High availability is paramount for a critical eProcurement infrastructure, as downtime can prevent economic operators from submitting bids and contracting authorities from assessing them, leading to significant economic and legal repercussions.
safetyRequirements: P-NFR-004: The system shall ensure data integrity for all VCD contents and associated evidence, employing cryptographic hashing and digital signatures to detect any unauthorized modification during storage or transmission. This is vital for maintaining the legal validity and trustworthiness of submitted tender information, preventing fraudulent alterations. Compromised data integrity could lead to erroneous contract awards or legal disputes.

P-NFR-005: The system shall implement robust error handling and recovery mechanisms to prevent data loss or corruption in the event of system failures, including transaction rollback capabilities for VCD compilation processes. This protects critical business data and ensures continuity of tender operations. Data loss would have severe legal and financial consequences for all stakeholders.

P-NFR-006: The system shall provide clear, unambiguous error messages and user guidance for all critical operations to prevent user-induced errors that could compromise tender submissions. Clear guidance reduces the risk of accidental data submission errors or incorrect evidence provision, which are crucial for legal compliance in public procurement.
securityRequirements: P-NFR-007: The **peppol** VCD system shall implement strong multi-factor authentication for all Economic Operators and Public Sector Awarding Entities accessing sensitive VCD data. This is essential to prevent unauthorized access to confidential company information and ensure that only legitimate users can create, submit, or review VCDs, thereby upholding the integrity of the procurement process.

P-NFR-008: All VCD data, both in transit and at rest, shall be encrypted using industry-standard cryptographic protocols (e.g., **TLS 1.2+**, **AES-256**). This protects sensitive company and personal data from eavesdropping or unauthorized disclosure, complying with strict European data protection regulations. A failure in encryption could lead to massive data breaches with severe legal and reputational damage.

P-NFR-009: The system shall provide a comprehensive audit trail of all actions performed on VCDs, including creation, modification, access, and submission, with timestamps and user identities. This enables accountability, supports forensic analysis in case of security incidents, and provides verifiable records for legal compliance. Lack of detailed auditing would make dispute resolution and compliance verification impossible.
softwareQualityAttributes: P-NFR-010: Interoperability: The **peppol** VCD system shall achieve a high degree of semantic and technical interoperability with national eProcurement systems and official registers across all EU Member States. This is the cornerstone of the **peppol** project, enabling seamless cross-border data exchange and reducing fragmentation. Without high interoperability, the system fails to deliver its core value proposition.

P-NFR-011: Reliability: The system shall consistently provide accurate VCD compilation and evidence verification results, with data integrity checks and validation mechanisms ensuring submitted information is correct and valid. High reliability builds trust among economic operators and contracting authorities. Inaccurate results could lead to incorrect tender evaluations and legal challenges.

P-NFR-012: Maintainability: The system shall be designed with a modular architecture and utilize well-documented **APIs** and code, facilitating efficient updates and modifications in response to evolving legal requirements or technical standards. Good maintainability ensures the system can adapt economically over its lifecycle. Poor maintainability would lead to high operational costs and slow adaptation to change.

P-NFR-013: Usability: The user interfaces for Economic Operators and Contracting Authorities shall be intuitive and easy to learn, requiring minimal training. Usability ensures broad adoption and reduces errors, crucial for users with varying technical proficiencies. A complex or difficult-to-use system would deter participation in cross-border eProcurement.

P-NFR-014: Portability: The core **peppol** VCD platform shall be capable of deployment across different underlying cloud infrastructure providers or national data centers, given its pan-European nature. This flexibility supports various national digital sovereignty requirements and ensures resilience against vendor lock-in, crucial for a collaborative European initiative.
businessRules: P-NFR-015: All VCD data and evidence submissions shall comply with the legal requirements outlined in Directive 2004/18/EC on public procurement, as well as relevant national public procurement acts of the participating Member States. Any deviation shall result in immediate flagging or rejection of the VCD.

P-NFR-016: The system shall enforce data retention policies for VCDs and associated audit trails as mandated by national and European legal frameworks, ensuring data is not stored beyond its necessary lifecycle.

P-NFR-017: The system shall recognize and process official digital certificates and electronic signatures issued by accredited authorities within the EU, ensuring the legal validity of electronic evidence and documents.

P-NFR-018: Access to sensitive company information within a VCD shall be strictly controlled based on explicit authorization granted by the Economic Operator and verified against the role of the requesting Contracting Authority.

otherRequirements: P-NFR-019: The system shall provide comprehensive logging capabilities for debugging, monitoring, and auditing purposes, ensuring all significant system events, data accesses, and errors are recorded for operational and legal compliance.

P-NFR-020: The **peppol** VCD system shall support at least the official languages of all participating Member States for its user interfaces and documentation, ensuring accessibility and ease of use across Europe.

flowchartDiagram.syntaxExplanation: Flowcharts illustrate a process or workflow, showing sequential steps, decisions, and outcomes. Nodes represent actions or states, while arrows indicate transitions. Decisions are typically diamond-shaped, and processes are rectangular. Direction (TD for Top-Down, LR for Left-Right) must be specified. Node IDs must be alphanumeric and unique; labels with spaces or symbols must be double-quoted.
flowchartDiagram.code:
flowchart TD
    StartProcess["Economic Operator Starts Tender Process"] --> MapNationalData{"National Data Mapped to VCD Taxonomy?"}
    MapNationalData -- No --> UploadNational["Upload National Company Data"]
    MapNationalData -- Yes --> CreateSimpleVCD["Create Simple VCD Package"]
    UploadNational --> MapNationalData
    CreateSimpleVCD --> AddEvidence["Add/Link Supporting Evidence"]
    AddEvidence --> ReviewVCD["Review & Validate VCD Package"]
    ReviewVCD --> SignVCD{"VCD Validated & Signed?"}
    SignVCD -- No --> ReviseVCD["Revise VCD Contents"]
    SignVCD -- Yes --> SubmitVCD["Submit VCD Package to Tender"]
    SubmitVCD --> VCDTransmitted["VCD Transmitted to Contracting Authority"]
    VCDTransmitted --> VerifyVCD{"Contracting Authority Verifies VCD?"}
    VerifyVCD -- No --> RequestClarification["Request Clarification/Correction"]
    VerifyVCD -- Yes --> AwardProcess["Proceed with Award Process"]
    RequestClarification --> ReviseVCD
    AwardProcess --> EndProcess["Tender Process Concluded"]
flowchartDiagram.caption: P_FLOW_001: Peppol VCD Tender Submission Process Flow

sequenceDiagram.syntaxExplanation: Sequence diagrams depict the interactions between objects or participants in a sequential order, emphasizing the time ordering of messages. Participants are defined at the top, and messages are represented by arrows. Synchronous messages are `->>`, asynchronous are `->>`. Activations `->>+` and `-->>-` indicate the start and end of a participant's active period. Loop, alternative (`alt`), and optional (`opt`) blocks can represent conditional or repetitive logic. Participant aliases (e.g., `participant A as Actor`) should not have quotes around the alias ID.
sequenceDiagram.code:
sequenceDiagram
    participant EO as "Economic Operator"
    participant VCD_System as "Peppol VCD System"
    participant NatReg as "National Register/Evidence Provider"
    participant CA as "Contracting Authority"

    EO->>VCD_System: Authenticate
    VCD_System->>EO: Authentication Success

    EO->>+VCD_System: Request VCD Package Creation
    VCD_System->>VCD_System: Compile VCD Data (Local)
    alt Evidence from National Register Needed
        VCD_System->>+NatReg: Request Company Evidence(companyId)
        NatReg-->>-VCD_System: Return Evidence(data)
    end
    VCD_System->>EO: Present VCD for Review
    EO->>VCD_System: Confirm/Adjust VCD
    VCD_System->>VCD_System: Generate Digital Signature
    VCD_System-->>-EO: VCD Package Ready for Submission

    EO->>+VCD_System: Submit VCD to Tender(tenderId)
    VCD_System->>+CA: Transmit VCD Package
    CA-->>-VCD_System: Acknowledge Receipt
    VCD_System-->>-EO: Submission Confirmation

    CA->>+VCD_System: Request VCD Verification(vcdId)
    VCD_System->>+NatReg: Verify Evidence(vcdId, evidenceRef)
    NatReg-->>-VCD_System: Verification Result(status)
    VCD_System-->>-CA: Provide Verification Report
sequenceDiagram.caption: P_SEQ_001: VCD Package Creation and Verification Sequence

entityRelationshipDiagram.syntaxExplanation: Entity-Relationship Diagrams (ERDs) model the logical structure of a database, showing entities (tables), their attributes (columns), and the relationships between them. Entities are denoted by names, with attributes listed below. Primary Keys (PK), Foreign Keys (FK), and Unique Keys (UK) identify attributes. Relationships are defined with cardinality (e.g., ||--o{ for one-to-many, }|--|| for many-to-one) and must include a quoted label describing the relationship. Many-to-many relationships must be resolved through a junction entity. Attribute names must be alphanumeric without spaces or quotes.
entityRelationshipDiagram.code:
erDiagram
    Company ||--o{ "Virtual_Company_Dossier" : "creates"
    "Virtual_Company_Dossier" ||--o{ Evidence : "contains"
    "Contracting_Authority" ||--o{ Tender : "issues"
    Tender ||--o{ Tender_VCD_Submission : "has_submissions"
    "Virtual_Company_Dossier" ||--o{ Tender_VCD_Submission : "submitted_to_tender"
    National_Register ||--o{ Evidence : "provides_source_for"

    Company {
        string company_id PK
        string legal_name
        string VAT_number
        string country_code
        datetime created_at
    }
    "Virtual_Company_Dossier" {
        string vcd_id PK
        string company_id FK
        string vcd_version
        datetime compilation_date
        string status
    }
    Evidence {
        string evidence_id PK
        string vcd_id FK
        string register_id FK
        string evidence_type
        string evidence_reference
        datetime issue_date
        datetime expiry_date
    }
    "Contracting_Authority" {
        string ca_id PK
        string ca_name
        string country_code
    }
    Tender {
        string tender_id PK
        string ca_id FK
        string tender_title
        datetime submission_deadline
        string tender_status
    }
    Tender_VCD_Submission {
        string submission_id PK
        string tender_id FK
        string vcd_id FK
        datetime submission_date
        string verification_status
    }
    National_Register {
        string register_id PK
        string register_name
        string country_code
    }
entityRelationshipDiagram.caption: P_ERD_001: Peppol VCD Core Entities and Relationships

introduction.purpose: The primary purpose of this document is to comprehensively delineate the external behavior of the **viper** Supply Chain Management (SCM) system, articulating its functional and non-functional requirements. It also specifies critical design constraints and adherence to established standards that govern the development of the **viper** system, ensuring a robust and compliant software solution.
introduction.documentConventions: This Software Requirements Specification adheres to the guidelines established by the IEEE 830-1998 Recommended Practice for Software Requirements Specifications. Key terms and abbreviations are defined in the Glossary (Appendix A), and all functional requirements are presented in a 'The system shall...' format. Diagramming conventions follow Mermaid syntax as detailed in Appendix B.
introduction.intendedAudience: This document is primarily intended for the **viper** development team, project managers, quality assurance personnel, and relevant stakeholders at Ejada company. It serves as a foundational reference for design, implementation, testing, and acceptance, ensuring a shared understanding of the system's requirements and scope.
introduction.productScope: This document defines the requirements for the **viper** SCM system, focusing on managing Ejada company's IT products, business consultation, and other IT services. The system's scope is strictly limited to Ejada's specific operational requests, encompassing customer service management, procurement, product development, manufacturing flow, physical distribution, outsourcing, partnerships, and performance measurement. It is intended to be a baseline for subsequent software development life cycle phases.
references: IEEE 830-1998 IEEE Recommended Practice for Software Requirements Specifications.
ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018 Systems and software engineering — Life cycle processes — Requirements engineering.
SCM vision document v.1.2.
Use Case & STD Documentation v1.5.
SCM Conceptual Class Model and Sequence Diagram document v.2.0.
SCM Screen layouts document v.1.2.
SWE 417-SRS Template-USE.

overallDescription.productPerspective: The **viper** system is designed to streamline the delivery of Ejada company's diverse product portfolio, including IT products, business consultation, and various IT services, with an emphasis on speed and cost-efficiency. It operates as a new, self-contained product, yet it is conceptually aligned with established enterprise SCM solutions, albeit with a scope specifically tailored to Ejada's operational needs and requests. This product will serve as a foundational component within Ejada's broader framework.
productFunctions: Provide customer service management.
Determine mutually satisfying goals between the organization and customers.
Establish and maintain customer rapport.
Produce positive feelings in the organization and the customers.
Maintain procurement processes.
Manage product development and commercialization.
Coordinate with customer relationship management to identify customer needs.
Select materials and suppliers in conjunction with procurement.
Develop production technology in manufacturing flow.
Integrate into the best supply chain flow for product/market combinations.
Maintain manufacturing flow management processes.
Manage physical distribution.
Maintain outsourcing and partnerships.
Maintain measurement performance.
Maintain cost performance.
Maintain customer service performance.
Maintain productivity measures performance.
Maintain asset measurement performance.
Maintain quality performance.

userClassesAndCharacteristics.characteristics: These users are responsible for managing various aspects of the supply chain, including requests, items, customers, and suppliers. They require full access to administrative functionalities and are expected to possess high school level education or higher, with basic computer literacy and English proficiency. Their roles demand frequent interaction with the system's management modules.

Customers interact with the **viper** system primarily to view and add requests, as well as to manage their personal profiles. They are expected to have a high school education or higher, basic computer usage skills, and English language proficiency. Their access is limited to their own request history and profile information, emphasizing ease of use and clear navigation.

Suppliers utilize the **viper** system to view incoming supply requests, submit feedback, and update their profiles. Similar to customers, they are assumed to have at least a high school education, basic computer skills, and English proficiency. Their system interactions are focused on fulfilling requests and maintaining their supplier information within the system.

overallDescription.operatingEnvironment: The **viper** system is designed to operate within a web-based environment, necessitating a server machine running a suitable **Microsoft OS** with consistent **Internet** connectivity. This architecture ensures broad accessibility and leverages familiar enterprise infrastructure.
designAndImplementationConstraints: The system must be entirely web-based, ensuring accessibility through standard web browsers without requiring specific client-side installations beyond a browser.

All development tools and technologies employed must be compliant with the **.Net** framework, specifically requiring the use of **ASP.NET** for web development and **C#** as the primary programming language.
The chosen Database Management System (DBMS) for **viper** must be **MS SQL**.
Development must adhere to Ejada's existing framework and programming standards, ensuring seamless integration and maintainability within the company's broader technical ecosystem.
The **viper** system is slated for future integration with two other existing modules within Ejada's framework, requiring adherence to defined interface protocols and data structures.
userDocumentation: A comprehensive online help system will be provided, integrated directly into the **viper** web application, offering contextual assistance for all features.
A digital user manual, accessible through the system, will detail all functionalities, user roles, and operational procedures.
Tutorials and quick-start guides will be available for new users, covering initial login, profile management, and basic request/item interactions.
assumptionsAndDependencies: It is assumed that the server machine hosting the **viper** system will operate on a compatible **Microsoft OS** and possess a stable, high-bandwidth connection to the **Internet** for continuous operation and user access.
The project depends on the availability and stability of the selected **MS SQL Server** for database operations, ensuring data integrity and performance.

Dependencies exist on the continued support and functionality of web browsers such as **Internet Explorer** (versions 6 and 7) and **Mozilla Firefox** (versions 2 and 3) as the primary user interaction platforms.

externalInterfaceRequirements.userInterfaces: The **viper** system will feature a web-based graphical user interface, designed for intuitive navigation and interaction. Upon initial access, users will encounter an introduction page, providing a direct link to the login interface. The login page will distinctly feature two text fields for 'username' and 'password' and a list box for 'domain' selection, accommodating 'coordinator', 'customer', and 'supplier' roles.
externalInterfaceRequirements.hardwareInterfaces: The **viper** system is explicitly designed without direct hardware interface requirements. Its web-based architecture abstracts away the need for specific hardware integrations, relying solely on standard client devices (e.g., desktops, laptops) capable of running supported web browsers and server infrastructure.
externalInterfaceRequirements.softwareInterfaces: The **viper** system will primarily interface with **MS SQL Server 7.0.1** as its backend database, leveraging its capabilities for data storage, retrieval, and management. This is a critical interface for all persistent data operations, ensuring transactional integrity and data consistency. Furthermore, the system must render correctly and function across specific versions of popular web browsers. These include **Internet Explorer** versions 6 and 7, and **Mozilla Firefox** versions 2 and 3. Compatibility with these browsers is essential for the user interface to be fully functional and visually accurate for the intended user base.
externalInterfaceRequirements.communicationsInterfaces: The **viper** system will exclusively utilize **TCP/IP** as its foundational communication protocol across the **Internet** or internal networks, facilitating all data exchange between client-side interfaces and the server. While currently self-contained, the system is architected to allow for future integration with external systems, such as **Customer Relationship Management (CRM)** and **Human Resources (HR)** platforms, through customizable communication interfaces.

systemFeatures.name: User Authentication and Authorization
systemFeatures.description: This feature provides secure access to the **viper** system by verifying user credentials and assigning appropriate domain-specific access rights. It improves user experience by offering a clear entry point to tailored functionalities, while technically enforcing role-based security. The primary user value is secure and personalized access, impacting system architecture by segmenting user interactions based on roles. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User navigates to the system's introduction page. Response: The system displays an introduction page with a link to the login page.
Stimulus: User clicks 'Login' link. Response: The system displays the login page with fields for username, password, and domain selection.
Stimulus: User enters credentials and selects a domain (Coordinator, Customer, or Supplier). Response: The system validates credentials against stored user data.
Stimulus: Valid credentials and domain are provided. Response: The system directs the user to their respective domain's main page.
Stimulus: Invalid credentials or incorrect domain are provided. Response: The system directs the user to an error page, providing a 'Try again' link.
functionalRequirements: The system shall display an introductory page with navigation to the login interface.
The system shall present a login interface comprising text fields for username and password, alongside a dropdown for domain selection (Coordinator, Customer, Supplier).
The system shall validate submitted user credentials against registered user accounts and their associated domains.
The system shall authenticate users based on correct username, password, and domain selection.
The system shall redirect authenticated users to their designated domain's dashboard (Coordinator, Customer, or Supplier).
The system shall display an error page upon failed login attempts, offering an option to retry authentication.
The system shall allow users to log out from any page, returning them to the login or introductory page.

systemFeatures.name: Coordinator: Manage Customer Information
systemFeatures.description: This feature empowers Coordinators to comprehensively manage customer data, including viewing, adding, editing, and deleting customer records. It enhances operational efficiency by providing centralized control over customer relations and accurate data maintenance. From a technical perspective, it ensures data integrity and consistency across the customer database, directly supporting the customer service management process. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Coordinator navigates to the 'Customer management' section. Response: The system displays the last 5 new customers and options to view details, view all, or add a new customer.
Stimulus: Coordinator clicks 'add new customer'. Response: The system displays a form with 4 information fields for new customer details.
Stimulus: Coordinator fills the form and clicks 'add'. Response: The system saves the new customer information and updates the customer list.
Stimulus: Coordinator clicks 'view detail' for a specific customer. Response: The system displays the customer's detailed information with 'edit' and 'delete' options.
Stimulus: Coordinator clicks 'edit' for a customer. Response: The system displays an editable form with 4 information fields pre-filled with customer data.
Stimulus: Coordinator modifies information and clicks 'edit'. Response: The system updates the customer record with the new information.
Stimulus: Coordinator clicks 'delete' for a customer. Response: The system displays a confirmation page for deletion.
Stimulus: Coordinator confirms deletion. Response: The system removes the customer record from the database.
functionalRequirements: The system shall allow Coordinators to view a summary of the last 5 newly added customers.
The system shall provide an option for Coordinators to view a comprehensive list of all registered customers.
The system shall enable Coordinators to add new customer records by filling out a designated form with four information fields.
The system shall store and associate newly added customer information within the database.
The system shall display detailed information for a selected customer upon request by a Coordinator.
The system shall allow Coordinators to modify existing customer details through an editable form.
The system shall update customer records in the database with the modified information.
The system shall provide a confirmation step before permanently deleting a customer record.
The system shall remove the specified customer record from the database upon Coordinator confirmation.

systemFeatures.name: Coordinator: Manage Item Inventory
systemFeatures.description: This feature provides Coordinators with tools to manage the system's item inventory, including adding new items, viewing existing ones, editing their details, and removing them. It ensures accurate tracking of products available for supply to customers, which is crucial for inventory management and order fulfillment. Technically, it maintains the integrity of item data, preventing discrepancies in product availability. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Coordinator navigates to the 'Items management' section. Response: The system displays the last 5 new items and options to view details, view all, or add a new item.
Stimulus: Coordinator clicks 'add new Items'. Response: The system displays a form for adding new item details.
Stimulus: Coordinator fills the form and clicks 'save'. Response: The system saves the new item information.
Stimulus: Coordinator clicks 'view detail' for a specific item. Response: The system displays the item's detailed information with 'edit' and 'delete' options.
Stimulus: Coordinator clicks 'edit' for an item. Response: The system displays an editable form with 2 information fields pre-filled with item data.
Stimulus: Coordinator modifies information and clicks 'save'. Response: The system updates the item record and sends notifications if necessary.
Stimulus: Coordinator clicks 'delete' for an item. Response: The system displays a confirmation page for deletion.
Stimulus: Coordinator confirms deletion. Response: The system removes the item record from the inventory.
functionalRequirements: The system shall present Coordinators with a list of the last 5 newly added items.
The system shall enable Coordinators to view a complete list of all items in the inventory.
The system shall provide a form for Coordinators to add new items, including necessary item attributes.
The system shall store new item information and associate it with the inventory.
The system shall display detailed information for any selected item.
The system shall allow Coordinators to modify item details through an editable form containing two information fields.
The system shall update the item record in the database upon modification.
The system shall send notifications to suppliers or customers if an item modification impacts ongoing processes.
The system shall provide a confirmation dialogue before an item is permanently deleted.
The system shall remove the specified item record from the inventory upon Coordinator confirmation.

systemFeatures.name: Coordinator: Manage Supplier Information
systemFeatures.description: This feature allows Coordinators to manage supplier records within the **viper** system, encompassing functionalities to view, add, edit, and delete supplier details. It streamlines the procurement process by ensuring up-to-date and accessible supplier information, thereby improving supply chain efficiency. Technically, it maintains a consistent and reliable supplier database for operational use. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Coordinator navigates to the 'Supplier management' section. Response: The system displays the last 5 new suppliers and options to view details, view all, or add a new supplier.
Stimulus: Coordinator clicks 'add new supplier'. Response: The system displays a form with 4 information fields for new supplier details.
Stimulus: Coordinator fills the form and clicks 'add'. Response: The system saves the new supplier information.
Stimulus: Coordinator clicks 'view detail' for a specific supplier. Response: The system displays the supplier's detailed information with 'edit' and 'delete' options.
Stimulus: Coordinator clicks 'edit' for a supplier. Response: The system displays an editable form with 4 information fields pre-filled with supplier data.
Stimulus: Coordinator modifies information and clicks 'edit'. Response: The system updates the supplier record with the new information.
Stimulus: Coordinator clicks 'delete' for a supplier. Response: The system displays a confirmation page for deletion.
Stimulus: Coordinator confirms deletion. Response: The system removes the supplier record from the database.
functionalRequirements: The system shall present Coordinators with a summary of the last 5 newly added suppliers.
The system shall provide an option for Coordinators to access a comprehensive list of all registered suppliers.
The system shall enable Coordinators to add new supplier records via a form with four distinct information fields.
The system shall securely store new supplier information within the database.
The system shall display detailed information for a selected supplier upon Coordinator request.
The system shall allow Coordinators to modify existing supplier details through an editable form.
The system shall update supplier records in the database with the revised information.
The system shall provide a confirmation prompt before a supplier record can be permanently deleted.
The system shall remove the specified supplier record from the database upon Coordinator confirmation.

systemFeatures.name: Coordinator: Manage Requests
systemFeatures.description: This feature centralizes the management of all customer and supplier requests for Coordinators, enabling them to add, view, edit, and delete requests. It improves responsiveness and coordination across the supply chain, ensuring that customer demands are met and supplier interactions are managed effectively. Technically, it provides a unified platform for request lifecycle management, crucial for efficient workflow orchestration. Priority: Top.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Coordinator presses 'Manage Requests'. Response: The system displays a management page for all requests, showing the last 5 new customer requests and the last 5 requests to suppliers, along with management functions.
Stimulus: Coordinator clicks 'add new Requests'. Response: The system displays a form for adding a new request.
Stimulus: Coordinator fills the request form and clicks 'send'. Response: The system creates the new request and sends it to the relevant supplier.
Stimulus: Coordinator clicks 'view detail' for a specific request. Response: The system displays the request's detailed information with 'edit' and 'delete' options.
Stimulus: Coordinator clicks 'edit' for a request. Response: The system displays an editable form with 2 information fields pre-filled with request data.
Stimulus: Coordinator modifies information and clicks 'save'. Response: The system updates the request record and sends a notification to the supplier.
Stimulus: Coordinator clicks 'Delete Request'. Response: The system displays the request information for confirmation.
Stimulus: Coordinator presses 'Delete'. Response: The system deletes the request and sends a notification to the supplier.
functionalRequirements: The system shall display a main page for Coordinators to manage requests, showing the last 5 new customer requests and the last 5 requests to suppliers.
The system shall enable Coordinators to add new requests by filling out a dedicated form.
The system shall send newly added requests to the appropriate supplier for fulfillment.
The system shall allow Coordinators to view a comprehensive list of all customer and supplier requests.
The system shall display detailed information for a selected request.
The system shall allow Coordinators to modify existing request information through an editable form with two fields.
The system shall notify the relevant supplier when a request is edited.
The system shall provide a confirmation step before a request is deleted.
The system shall permanently delete the specified request from the system.
The system shall notify the relevant supplier when a request is deleted.

systemFeatures.name: Customer: Manage Requests
systemFeatures.description: This feature allows Customers to manage their personal requests within the **viper** system, including adding new requests, viewing their status, and editing existing ones. It improves customer satisfaction by providing direct control and visibility over their engagements with Ejada. Technically, it ensures customer-specific request data is accurately captured and updated. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Customer logs in and navigates to their main page. Response: The system displays the last 5 new requests and options to view details, view all, or add a new request.
Stimulus: Customer clicks 'add new request'. Response: The system displays a form with 2 information fields for a new request.
Stimulus: Customer fills the form and clicks 'send'. Response: The system saves the new request information.
Stimulus: Customer clicks 'view detail' for a specific request. Response: The system displays the request's detailed information with 'edit' and 'delete' options.
Stimulus: Customer clicks 'edit' for a request. Response: The system displays an editable form with 2 information fields pre-filled with request data.
Stimulus: Customer modifies information and clicks 'save'. Response: The system updates the request record with the new information.
functionalRequirements: The system shall display the last 5 new requests submitted by the logged-in Customer on their main page.
The system shall allow Customers to view a comprehensive list of all their submitted requests.
The system shall provide a form with two information fields for Customers to add new requests.
The system shall store newly added customer requests in the database.
The system shall display detailed information for a selected customer request.
The system shall enable Customers to modify their existing request information via an editable form with two fields.
The system shall update the customer's request record in the database upon modification.

systemFeatures.name: Customer: Manage Profile
systemFeatures.description: This feature enables Customers to view and update their personal profile information within the **viper** system. It provides customers with autonomy over their data, ensuring accuracy and currency of contact and personal details. From a technical standpoint, it ensures that customer master data is kept up-to-date and accessible for relevant system processes. Priority: Medium.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Customer clicks 'edit profile' link. Response: The system displays an editable form with 4 information fields pre-filled with customer profile data.
Stimulus: Customer modifies information and clicks 'save'. Response: The system updates the customer's profile information.
functionalRequirements: The system shall provide a link for Customers to edit their profile information.
The system shall display an editable form with four information fields for the Customer's profile.
The system shall update the Customer's profile record in the database with the modified information.

systemFeatures.name: Supplier: Manage Requests
systemFeatures.description: This feature provides Suppliers with the ability to view incoming supply requests and provide feedback on them within the **viper** system. It facilitates efficient communication and collaboration between Ejada and its suppliers, ensuring timely responses to procurement needs. Technically, it supports the flow of request information to suppliers and captures their responses, critical for managing the supply chain effectively. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Supplier logs in and navigates to their main page. Response: The system displays the last 5 new requests directed to them.
Stimulus: Supplier clicks 'view detail' for a specific request. Response: The system displays the request's detailed information and a feedback box.
Stimulus: Supplier enters feedback and sends it. Response: The system records the feedback and redirects to a confirmation page.
functionalRequirements: The system shall display the last 5 new requests specifically directed to the logged-in Supplier on their main page.
The system shall allow Suppliers to view a comprehensive list of all supply requests assigned to them.
The system shall display detailed information for a selected supply request.
The system shall provide a feedback box for Suppliers to enter comments or responses regarding a request.
The system shall record and store the feedback provided by the Supplier.
The system shall redirect the Supplier to a confirmation page after successfully sending feedback.

systemFeatures.name: Supplier: Manage Profile
systemFeatures.description: This feature allows Suppliers to view and update their profile information within the **viper** system. It ensures that Ejada has access to current and accurate supplier details, which is vital for effective communication and partnership management. Technically, it enables self-service updates to supplier master data, reducing administrative overhead. Priority: Medium.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Supplier clicks 'edit profile' link. Response: The system displays an editable form with 4 information fields pre-filled with supplier profile data.
Stimulus: Supplier modifies information and clicks 'save'. Response: The system updates the supplier's profile information.
functionalRequirements: The system shall provide a link for Suppliers to edit their profile information.
The system shall display an editable form with four information fields for the Supplier's profile.
The system shall update the Supplier's profile record in the database with the modified information.

performanceRequirements: V-REQ-001: The system shall display request lists within 10 seconds, even under peak load conditions, to ensure timely access to critical information.

V-REQ-002: The system shall complete 'Add Request' operations within 10 minutes, including sending notifications, to support efficient workflow. This requirement is crucial for maintaining rapid response times within the supply chain.

V-REQ-003: The system shall ensure 'Edit Request' operations are processed in real-time ('on time change') with immediate notification delivery, critical for instantaneous updates in dynamic supply scenarios.

V-REQ-004: The system shall process 'Delete Request' operations within 1 minute, including notification to the supplier, to facilitate swift adjustments in procurement. This ensures that changes can be enacted without significant delay.

V-REQ-005: The system shall display item management functions within 5 seconds, providing quick access to inventory control. Rapid display of these functions is essential for efficient inventory oversight.
safetyRequirements: V-REQ-006: The **viper** system shall implement robust input validation mechanisms for all user-submitted data to prevent SQL injection, cross-site scripting (XSS), and other common web vulnerabilities. This is critical to safeguard against data corruption or unauthorized access that could compromise operational integrity.

V-REQ-007: All critical data operations, such as deleting customer, item, or supplier records, shall require explicit user confirmation. This prevents accidental data loss and provides an additional layer of data integrity protection.

V-REQ-008: The system shall log all administrative actions, including data modifications and deletions, with timestamps and user identifiers. This logging is necessary for audit trails, ensuring accountability and facilitating recovery in case of operational errors or misuse.
securityRequirements: V-REQ-009: The **viper** system shall enforce role-based access control, ensuring that users can only access functionalities and data relevant to their assigned roles (Coordinator, Customer, Supplier). This is fundamental for maintaining data confidentiality and integrity by preventing unauthorized privilege escalation.

V-REQ-010: All sensitive data, including user credentials (username, password) and personal customer/supplier information, shall be encrypted during transmission using **HTTPS/TLS** protocols. This requirement protects data from eavesdropping and tampering during network communication.

V-REQ-011: User passwords shall be stored as salted and hashed values in the database, never in plain text. This measure protects against unauthorized access to user accounts even if the database is compromised, adhering to industry best practices for credential security.

V-REQ-012: The system shall implement session management that automatically terminates inactive user sessions after a predefined period (e.g., 30 minutes). This helps mitigate the risk of unauthorized access to unattended active sessions.
softwareQualityAttributes: V-REQ-013: Reliability: The system shall maintain an uptime of 99.9% during operational hours (8 AM - 6 PM local time, Monday - Friday), excluding scheduled maintenance. This ensures continuous availability of critical SCM functions, minimizing disruption to Ejada's operations.

V-REQ-014: Availability: The system shall be available 24/7 for all users, with planned downtime for maintenance communicated 48 hours in advance. This maximizes user access and operational continuity, supporting global business activities.

V-REQ-015: Maintainability: The system shall be developed using clear, modular code with comprehensive internal documentation and adherence to Ejada's programming standards. This facilitates future updates, bug fixes, and enhancements, reducing long-term maintenance costs.

V-REQ-016: Portability: The system's web-based architecture shall allow it to be deployed on various **Microsoft Windows Server** environments without significant modifications, provided the necessary **.NET** framework and **MS SQL Server** are present. This ensures flexibility in hosting and deployment options.

V-REQ-017: Usability: The system shall provide an intuitive and consistent user interface across all modules, designed for users with basic computer literacy. This minimizes training effort and increases user adoption and efficiency.
businessRules: V-REQ-018: All requests, whether from customers or to suppliers, must be approved by a Coordinator before being finalized or forwarded.
V-REQ-019: Only active customers and suppliers can be associated with new requests or items. Deactivated entities will be excluded from selection lists.
V-REQ-020: Item inventory levels must be updated automatically upon successful fulfillment of a request by a supplier.
V-REQ-021: Any modification to an existing request must trigger an automatic notification to the involved supplier or customer, ensuring all parties are informed of changes.

otherRequirements: V-REQ-022: The **viper** system requires that all functional requirements are fully implemented and integrated prior to the first delivery. No partial functional delivery will be accepted. This ensures a complete and operational system is provided from the outset.

V-REQ-023: A formal Change Management Process, as defined in Section 4 of the original SRS, shall govern all proposed modifications to the **viper** system. This ensures that changes are systematically evaluated, approved, and tracked, maintaining control over the project's evolution and preventing scope creep.

flowchartDiagram.syntaxExplanation: Flowcharts illustrate processes and decision logic. Nodes represent steps or decisions, connected by arrows indicating the flow. Start/End nodes are typically rounded rectangles, processes are rectangles, and decisions are diamonds. Labels for nodes with spaces or symbols must be enclosed in double quotes. Node IDs must be alphanumeric and unique, without spaces or special characters, and 'end', 'subgraph', or 'class' are forbidden as IDs.
flowchartDiagram.caption: User Login and Domain Routing Flow

sequenceDiagram.syntaxExplanation: Sequence diagrams depict interactions between participants in a temporal order. Participants are declared with 'participant' keyword. Messages are shown as arrows, with '->>' for asynchronous messages and '->' for synchronous. Activations show when a participant is active. The '->>+' and '-->>-' notation can be used to indicate activation and deactivation implicitly. Alias IDs must not be quoted. Avoid special characters like '{}' in messages and do not use 'activate' inside 'alt'/'opt'/'loop' blocks.
sequenceDiagram.code:
sequenceDiagram
  participant User as "Coordinator User"
  participant System as "viper System"
  participant Database as "MS SQL Database"

  User->>System: "Login(username, password, domain)"
  System->>+Database: "Query User Credentials"
  Database-->>-System: "Return User Data (Valid/Invalid)"
  alt Valid Credentials
    System->>User: "Redirect to Coordinator Dashboard"
    User->>System: "Click 'Add Request'"
    System->>User: "Display 'Add Request' Form"
    User->>System: "Submit Request Details"
    System->>+Database: "Save New Request"
    Database-->>-System: "Confirmation of Save"
    System->>User: "Display Confirmation/Success Message"
    System->>User: "Send Notification to Supplier"
  else Invalid Credentials
    System->>User: "Display Login Error Page"
  end
sequenceDiagram.caption: Coordinator Add Request Sequence

entityRelationshipDiagram.syntaxExplanation: Entity Relationship Diagrams illustrate the logical structure of a database, showing entities (tables) and their relationships. Relationships are defined with cardinality (e.g., one-to-one, one-to-many) and labeled descriptions. Attributes define the properties of each entity, with primary keys (PK), foreign keys (FK), and unique keys (UK) explicitly marked. Multiple keys must be comma-separated (e.g., PK, FK), but 'NN' (Not Null) is forbidden. Entity names with spaces must be quoted, while attribute names must be alphanumeric and unquoted.
entityRelationshipDiagram.code:
erDiagram
  USER ||--o{ CUSTOMER : "is_associated_with"
  USER ||--o{ SUPPLIER : "is_associated_with"
  USER ||--o{ COORDINATOR_ROLE : "has"

  CUSTOMER ||--o{ REQUEST : "initiates"
  SUPPLIER ||--o{ REQUEST : "receives"
  REQUEST ||--|{ REQUEST_ITEM : "contains"
  ITEM ||--o{ REQUEST_ITEM : "is_part_of"
  COORDINATOR_ROLE ||--o{ REQUEST : "manages"
  COORDINATOR_ROLE ||--o{ ITEM : "manages"
  COORDINATOR_ROLE ||--o{ CUSTOMER : "manages"
  COORDINATOR_ROLE ||--o{ SUPPLIER : "manages"

  USER {
    int user_id PK
    string username UK
    string password_hash
    string user_type
    datetime created_at
  }

  CUSTOMER {
    int customer_id PK
    int user_id FK
    string name
    string contact_email
    string address
  }

  SUPPLIER {
    int supplier_id PK
    int user_id FK
    string name
    string contact_email
    string address
  }

  COORDINATOR_ROLE {
    int coordinator_id PK
    int user_id FK
    string department
    string employee_id UK
  }

  REQUEST {
    int request_id PK
    int customer_id FK
    int supplier_id FK
    int coordinator_id FK
    string status
    string description
    datetime created_at
    datetime due_date
  }

  ITEM {
    int item_id PK
    string name
    string description
    decimal price
    int stock_quantity
  }

  REQUEST_ITEM {
    int request_item_id PK
    int request_id FK
    int item_id FK
    int quantity
  }

introduction.purpose: The purpose of this Software Requirements Specification (SRS) is to precisely delineate the requirements for the **ctc network** project, which facilitates Center-to-Center (C2C) Communications within the Dallas/Ft. Worth (DFW) Regional Metroplex. This document establishes the functional and non-functional requirements necessary for developing a robust, extensible, and standards-compliant system for traffic data exchange and device control among various traffic management centers. The scope encompasses the core C2C infrastructure and its interfaces to diverse ITS field devices and external systems, ensuring a unified approach to regional traffic management.
introduction.documentConventions: This document adheres to the IEEE 830-1998 standard for Software Requirements Specifications, ensuring a structured and comprehensive presentation of system requirements. All functional requirements are articulated as explicit 'The system shall...' statements to maintain clarity and testability. Technical terms and acronyms are defined within Appendix A, the Glossary, for consistent understanding across all stakeholders. Diagramming notations, specifically Mermaid for flowcharts, sequence diagrams, and entity-relationship diagrams, are explained within Appendix B to ensure proper interpretation of visual models.
introduction.intendedAudience: This SRS is primarily intended for the development team responsible for implementing the **ctc network** project, providing a definitive guide for design and coding efforts. It also serves as a critical reference for project managers for planning and tracking progress, and for quality assurance personnel to design comprehensive test cases. Furthermore, stakeholders, including the Software Task Force, the North Central Texas Council of Governments, and participating regional agencies, will utilize this document to validate that the specified system meets their operational needs and strategic objectives.
introduction.productScope: The **ctc network** system provides a foundational infrastructure for interconnecting dissimilar traffic management systems across the DFW Metroplex, enabling the exchange of traffic data and control information. It supports a common repository for aggregated traffic information and offers mechanisms for command and control of various Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITS) field devices. The system is designed to convert diverse proprietary data formats into a standardized format based on the evolving ITS Traffic Management Data Dictionary (**TMDD**) standard, promoting reusability and extensibility.
