Furthermore, the system is required to interface with the **Driver Safety Device (DSD)**, an on-train system monitoring driver alertness, and **Train-borne recorders** for logging operational data. It must also accommodate interfaces to train control systems, such as those employing **Balises** for track-to-train communication, particularly as a bearer service for **ERTMS/ETCS** data. These interfaces necessitate robust communication protocols and signal handling capabilities to ensure reliability and safety-critical data integrity.
externalInterfaceRequirements.softwareInterfaces: The **eirene fun 7** system is designed to integrate with a variety of external software components and networks to fulfill its operational mandate. It requires interfaces to private railway networks, enabling seamless communication within and between different railway organizations. Connectivity to public operator networks is also necessary, allowing railway personnel to make **Public emergency calls** and potentially other general communications. Crucially, the system must interface with specialized railway systems such as **Train Control Systems (TCS)**, including **ERTMS/ETCS** Level 2 or 3 applications, providing a reliable data bearer. This necessitates well-defined **Application Programming Interface (API)** protocols for data items and messages related to train control, timetable information, maintenance, and remote database access. Shared data mechanisms, such as those for subscriber management, numbering plans, and access matrices, must be securely maintained across these interconnected software components.
externalInterfaceRequirements.communicationsInterfaces: The **eirene fun 7** system relies heavily on robust communication interfaces, primarily leveraging the **GSM-R** network for its core functionalities. It must support various voice communication protocols for point-to-point, broadcast, group, and multi-party calls, ensuring high-quality audio transmission. For data services, protocols for text messaging, general data applications, and automatic fax are required, capable of transmitting data at rates of at least 2.4 kbit/s, with provisions for higher rates as needed by applications like **ERTMS/ETCS**. All communications must ensure fast and guaranteed call set-up times. Security and encryption protocols are paramount for critical railway communications, particularly for **Railway Emergency Calls** and train control data, to prevent unauthorized access or interference. The system will also handle communication standards like **HTTP** for data exchanges, alongside specific message formatting for railway-centric information, ensuring reliable synchronization mechanisms across the network.

systemFeatures.name: Voice Communication Services
systemFeatures.description: This feature provides a comprehensive suite of voice communication capabilities essential for railway operations, enabling various interaction models between fixed and mobile users. It enhances operational efficiency by allowing direct communication, mass notifications, and group coordination, ensuring that all necessary personnel can communicate effectively under diverse scenarios. Technically, it requires robust network infrastructure capable of managing simultaneous calls, arbitrating talk requests, and dynamically adjusting call group compositions and geographical areas. The system must support high-priority voice traffic without compromise, critical for safety and operational continuity. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User initiates a point-to-point call to another user. Response: The system establishes a direct voice connection, allowing simultaneous two-way communication.

Stimulus: Controller initiates a broadcast voice call to a predefined group in a local area. Response: The system establishes a one-way voice channel where only the controller can speak, and all group members can listen.
Stimulus: Mobile user attempts to speak during an active group call. Response: The system arbitrates access, allowing one mobile user to speak at a time while controllers retain priority.
functionalRequirements: The system shall support point-to-point voice calls between any two call parties, fixed or mobile.
The system shall support simultaneous two-way conversation in point-to-point calls.
The system shall support public emergency point-to-point voice calls, such as '112' calls.
The system shall support broadcast voice calls, providing one-way communication from a single user to multiple users in a predefined local area.
The system shall allow modification of call group compositions and local areas for broadcast calls within the network.
The system shall support group voice calls for multiple users in a predefined local area.
The system shall provide a mechanism to arbitrate speech access for mobile users within a group call.
The system shall allow controllers to speak at any time during a group call.
The system shall support multi-party voice communications among up to six different parties.
The system shall allow any party involved in a multi-party voice call to talk simultaneously.

systemFeatures.name: Data Communication Services
systemFeatures.description: This feature provides essential data exchange capabilities, crucial for modern railway operations, enabling the transmission of information beyond voice. It supports critical applications such as **ERTMS/ETCS** for train control, as well as general operational data like timetable information, diagnostics, and e-mail. From a technical perspective, it requires robust data bearer services with specified data rates, ensuring that data transmission does not interfere with high-priority voice calls. The system must also support specific data application types like text messaging and automatic fax, adhering to the same priority schemes as voice calls to maintain operational integrity. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Ground system sends a point-to-point text message to a mobile user. Response: The system transmits the text message to the specified mobile user.
Stimulus: Train control application sends data via the **eirene fun 7** bearer service. Response: The system prioritizes and transmits the data to the trackside **ERTMS/ETCS** components.
Stimulus: User initiates an automatic fax transmission from a mobile unit. Response: The system transmits the fax, allowing interruption for higher-priority voice or data calls.
functionalRequirements: The network should support the transmission of point-to-point and point-to-multipoint text messages from the ground to mobile users.
The network should support the receipt of mobile-originated text messages by the ground.
The text message facility, if implemented, shall not interfere with the ability of users to make or receive high priority voice or data calls.
The network shall support point-to-point data communications for general data applications.
The network shall support data rates of at least 2.4 kbit/s for general data applications.
The network should support fax transmissions between the ground and mobile users.
Where fax functionality is provided, it shall be possible to interrupt the fax to make or receive a high priority voice or data call.
Where **ERTMS/ETCS** level 2 or 3 is implemented, the network shall be capable of supporting data communications for that train control system.
The priority scheme for calls described in section 10.2 shall be valid for data applications and train control applications.

systemFeatures.name: Call Management Services
systemFeatures.description: This feature provides advanced call handling functionalities that enhance user control and operational flexibility within the **eirene fun 7** network. It includes services like identity display and restriction, priority and pre-emption for critical communications, closed user groups for controlled access, and various call forwarding options. These services improve user experience by providing transparency (e.g., caller ID) and control (e.g., barring calls), while ensuring that high-priority railway communications can always take precedence. Technically, this requires sophisticated network mechanisms for call routing, subscriber authorization, and dynamic call state management, ensuring security and efficient resource allocation. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: An incoming call arrives. Response: The system displays the identity of the calling party, either as a standard telephone number or a functional description.
Stimulus: A user initiates a call with a higher priority than an ongoing call. Response: The system pre-empts the lower priority call, disconnecting it and connecting the new, higher priority call.
Stimulus: A user's line is busy, and call forwarding (busy) is active. Response: The system automatically forwards the incoming call to the designated alternative user.
Stimulus: Network management attempts to bar a user from making calls to a specific external network. Response: The system prevents the user from initiating calls to that specific external network.
functionalRequirements: The equipment shall display the identity of the called or calling party as a standard telephone number.
The equipment shall display the identity of the called or calling party as a textual description of their function.
The network should prevent the identity of certain users from being displayed on the mobile when being called, calling, or both.
The network shall provide a mechanism for calls to be assigned different priority levels.
The mechanism shall allow higher priority calls to pre-empt existing lower priority calls.
Pre-empted calls shall be discontinued, and the new higher priority call shall be connected.
The group of users who may access the **eirene fun 7** network facilities shall be limited.
Any user not within the list of allowed **eirene fun 7** users shall not gain access to network functions and services.
It shall be possible for an incoming call or data message for one user to be forwarded to another user using network functionality.
It shall be possible for the user forwarding a voice call to converse with the intended recipient prior to forwarding.
The network shall support unconditional call forwarding, call forwarding if busy, call forwarding if no reply (Optional), and call forwarding if not reachable (Optional).
The network shall allow the user to temporarily exit from an existing call by putting it on hold.
It shall be possible for the user to rejoin a call that is on hold at any time.
The network shall provide the ability to inform a user, who is in an existing call, of attempts by other users to contact them.
Where network services are chargeable, the network should provide information about call rates and ongoing call charges (Optional).
It shall be possible, using network management or maintenance facilities, to prevent individual users from making or receiving calls based on network, number type, or specific telephone numbers.

systemFeatures.name: Railway Emergency Call Management
systemFeatures.description: This critical feature provides a highly prioritized and rapid response mechanism for informing railway personnel of immediate dangers, necessitating the cessation of all railway movements within a predefined area. It significantly enhances safety by ensuring quick dissemination of vital information during emergencies. Technically, it requires robust network capabilities for immediate call setup, area identification, and guaranteed message delivery to specific groups of users (drivers, controllers, and other concerned personnel). The system must distinguish between 'Train emergency calls' and 'Shunting emergency calls' and ensure that these calls always override other communications, reflecting their paramount importance in railway safety protocols. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Driver initiates a **Railway Emergency Call**. Response: The system immediately establishes a high-priority call, identifying the calling driver's location and initiating notification procedures.

Stimulus: Network receives a **Railway Emergency Call**. Response: The system automatically determines the affected geographical area and prioritizes the call for all relevant personnel within that area.

Stimulus: Controller receives a **Railway Emergency Call** notification. Response: The system provides an immediate visual and auditory alert, requiring the controller to acknowledge and potentially confirm the emergency message.
Stimulus: System confirms a **Railway Emergency Call**. Response: The system broadcasts an explicit confirmation message to all previously notified personnel, reinforcing the emergency status.
functionalRequirements: The system shall enable a user to initiate a **Railway Emergency Call**.
The system shall differentiate between 'Train emergency calls' and 'Shunting emergency calls'.
The system shall ensure that **Railway Emergency Calls** are given the highest priority, overriding all other communications.
The system shall automatically determine the predefined area affected by a **Railway Emergency Call** based on its initiation point.
The system shall immediately notify all relevant drivers, controllers, and personnel within the affected area of an incoming **Railway Emergency Call**.
The system shall provide a mechanism for controllers to acknowledge receipt of a **Railway Emergency Call**.
The system shall provide a mechanism for the originator of a **Railway Emergency Call** to receive confirmation that the call has been received and processed by the network.
The system shall allow for the management of active **Railway Emergency Calls** by authorized controllers, including termination or area adjustments.

systemFeatures.name: Functional and Location Dependent Addressing
systemFeatures.description: This feature revolutionizes how users are addressed within the **eirene fun 7** network, shifting from fixed terminal IDs to dynamic functional roles or current geographical locations. This significantly enhances operational flexibility, as communications can be directed to the 'driver of train X' or the 'controller for area Y', regardless of who or what equipment is currently fulfilling that role or where they are physically located. Technically, it requires a robust subscriber management system that dynamically maps functional identities and geographical coordinates to active users and their equipment. This involves sophisticated registration procedures and location services, making communication more intuitive and adaptable to the fluid nature of railway operations. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User attempts to place a call using a functional number (e.g., 'Driver of Train 123'). Response: The system resolves the functional number to the currently assigned user and their active mobile equipment, then places the call.
Stimulus: Mobile equipment registers its current location with the network. Response: The system updates its internal location database, enabling location-dependent addressing.

Stimulus: Controller initiates a call to a specific function based on the caller's current location (e.g., 'nearest shunter'). Response: The system identifies the nearest shunter based on reported locations and routes the call accordingly.
functionalRequirements: The **eirene fun 7** network shall provide support for functional addressing.
The system shall allow users to be addressed by a functional number representing their role or function (e.g., train number, engine number, coach number).
The system shall support registration procedures for functional identities.
The **eirene fun 7** network shall provide support for location dependent addressing.
The system shall allow calls to be addressed to a particular function (e.g., controller) based on the current location of the user (e.g., train).
The system shall maintain and update location information for mobile users to enable location-dependent addressing.
The system shall provide mechanisms for users to register and deregister their functional identities within the network.

systemFeatures.name: Shunting Mode Operations
systemFeatures.description: This feature provides specialized communication and safety mechanisms for shunting operations, which are inherently critical due to the complex and often hazardous maneuvering of trains. It enhances safety by ensuring reliable, dedicated communication within a shunting team and providing a 'Link Assurance Signal' to detect transmission breaks during crucial maneuvers. Technically, this requires dedicated group membership management for shunting teams and a robust, unidirectional signaling mechanism for the link assurance. The feature isolates shunting communications from the broader network traffic, ensuring focus and minimizing potential distractions or interferences during these high-risk activities. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Shunting team member activates 'Shunting Mode' on their **Operational radio**. Response: The system registers the user into a dedicated shunting group and initiates the 'Link Assurance Signal' for that group.

Stimulus: Driver is pushing a train backwards, and the lookout's radio transmission breaks. Response: The system detects the loss of the 'Link Assurance Signal' and alerts the driver to a potential communication breakdown.
Stimulus: Authorized personnel modify the group membership of a shunting team. Response: The system updates the shunting group configuration, reflecting the new team composition.
functionalRequirements: The **eirene fun 7** network shall provide support for a specific mode for shunting operations.
The system shall support dedicated group membership for shunting teams.
The system shall provide a 'Link Assurance Signal' (LAS) transmitted periodically or constantly from one radio to another during shunting operations.
The receiving radio shall detect a break in radio transmission during critical manoeuvres via the LAS.
The system shall define and enforce constraints specific to shunting mode operations to ensure safety.
The system shall allow for dynamic modification of shunting group membership via subscriber management facilities.

performanceRequirements: The **eirene fun 7** system shall achieve a call set-up time that is fast and guaranteed, ensuring immediate connectivity for operational and emergency communications. This is critical for maintaining safety during time-sensitive railway activities and minimizing delays in critical information exchange. The network shall support seamless communication for train speeds up to 500 km/h, requiring advanced handover mechanisms and signal integrity across cell boundaries. Data services shall provide rates of at least 2.4 kbit/s, with provisions for higher rates to support demanding applications like **ERTMS/ETCS** efficiently. The overall reliability and availability of the network and mobile equipment must meet stringent railway operational standards, minimizing downtime and ensuring continuous service delivery. The system shall maintain communication integrity in areas with high densities of trains requiring simultaneous communications.
safetyRequirements: The **eirene fun 7** system shall incorporate robust safeguards to ensure the highest level of safety for railway operations. **Railway Emergency Calls** shall have absolute priority over all other communications, with guaranteed delivery and immediate notification mechanisms to relevant personnel in defined areas. The 'Shunting Mode' shall include a 'Link Assurance Signal' to provide real-time detection of communication breaks during critical maneuvering, preventing accidents due to lost contact. Interfaces with the **Driver Safety Device (DSD)** and **Train Control Systems (TCS)**, particularly **ERTMS/ETCS**, must adhere to strict safety integrity levels, ensuring reliable transmission of safety-critical data. The system shall implement mechanisms to prevent unauthorized access or manipulation of safety-related communications and data. Mandatory compliance with the **EIRENE FRS** and **SRS** on interoperability lines is a core safety requirement.
securityRequirements: The **eirene fun 7** system shall implement comprehensive security measures to protect communications and network integrity. Access to the **EIRENE network** facilities shall be restricted to authorized users through a 'Closed User Group' mechanism, preventing unauthorized entities from gaining access to services. Call barring functionalities shall allow for granular control over incoming and outgoing calls based on network, number type, or specific numbers, enhancing operational security and preventing misuse. All critical railway communications, especially those involving train control data and **Railway Emergency Calls**, shall be secured through appropriate encryption and authentication protocols to ensure confidentiality and integrity. The system shall prevent unauthorized display of user identities where restricted, preserving privacy and operational discretion. Robust network management and maintenance facilities shall be secured against unauthorized configuration changes.
softwareQualityAttributes: **Interoperability:** The **eirene fun 7** system shall provide seamless communication across national or other borders between compliant **EIRENE networks** and systems. This is critical for the fluid movement of international trains and staff, ensuring a unified communication standard across Europe. The system achieves this by defining a mandatory set of requirements for network infrastructure and mobile equipment. **Reliability:** The system shall operate without failure for extended periods, especially for safety-critical voice and data services. This is crucial for maintaining operational continuity and ensuring emergency response capabilities during adverse conditions. **Availability:** The **EIRENE network** and mobile equipment shall be operational and accessible to users for a very high percentage of the time. This ensures that communication resources are consistently ready for use, supporting railway operations around the clock. **Maintainability:** The system architecture and components shall be designed for ease of diagnosis, repair, and modification. This facilitates efficient upkeep, reduces operational costs, and allows for future enhancements and adaptations to evolving railway needs. **Robustness:** The system shall gracefully handle unexpected inputs, environmental disturbances, and partial failures without catastrophic breakdown. This ensures continued operation under challenging railway conditions, such as high train density or signal interference. **Testability:** All functionalities and non-functional requirements of the **eirene fun 7** system shall be verifiable through defined testing procedures. This is essential for validating compliance with the specification and ensuring that the implemented system meets all operational and safety standards.
businessRules: All requirements marked with '(M)' are mandatory and must be implemented by each railway on lines where interoperability is required. Requirements marked with '(O)' are optional; however, if an optional feature is selected, its implementation method, as defined in the **EIRENE SRS** and **FRS**, becomes mandatory to ensure consistency and achieve manufacturing economies of scale. Statements marked with '(I)' are informational, providing explanatory notes without imposing direct implementation requirements. The allocation of priorities for calls and data applications must be consistently applied across the network, as defined in section 10.2 of the source document. Subscriber management, including the allocation of priorities, call restriction, group membership, and network selection lists, must conform to established operational policies. **Functional addressing** registration procedures must adhere to specified principles to ensure accurate user identification by function or role.

glossary.definition: A passive or active device normally mounted in proximity to the track for communications with passing trains. A standard for balises has been devised within the EUROBALISE project.
A call made to all members of a pre-defined group within a local geographical area. Only the initiator of the call may talk, with all other group members listening only.

The radio and associated user and other interfaces installed in the cab of a locomotive and for use principally by the locomotive driver. Definition also includes those radios supporting train control.
A member of the train crew with overall responsibility for passenger-related Railway activities on-board the train.
A number assigned to an item of rolling stock on a permanent basis. The coach number may form a component of a functional number used to address users/systems on an item of rolling stock.

An individual responsible for the conduct of some aspect of train operations (also known as dispatcher). Functional roles defined include primary controller, secondary controller, traffic controller, and power supply controller.
Centralised Train Signalling.
The term for back-to-back or set-to-set radio communications without the use of any ground infrastructure.
An on-train system which monitors the alertness of the driver and provides warnings and alarms to other systems as appropriate.
Driver Safety Device.

A railway telecommunications network, based on the ETSI GSM standard, which complies with all related mandatory requirements specified in the EIRENE FRS and SRS. An EIRENE network may also include optional features and these shall then be implemented as specified. The EIRENE network excludes terminals.

A railway telecommunications system based on the ETSI GSM standard, which complies with all related mandatory requirements as specified in the EIRENE FRS and SRS. An EIRENE system may also include optional features and these shall then be implemented as specified. The EIRENE System includes terminals.
Electromagnetic Compatibility.
A number assigned to an item of traction stock on a permanent basis. The engine number may form a component of a functional number used to address users/systems on an item of traction stock.
European Rail Traffic Management System.
European Train Control System.

A UIC fiche or leaflet is a document adopted by UIC members. Statements within the fiche may comprise specifications which are binding on UIC members (‘obligatory’ specifications) or optional (‘recommended’ specifications).
A term used to describe the process of addressing a call using a number representing the function a user is performing, rather than a number identifying the user’s terminal equipment.
A description of the function performed by a called or calling party. The functional identity can include characters and numbers.
The full number used within the functional addressing scheme to identify an end user/system by function or role rather than by a specific item of radio equipment or user subscription.
A standard radio closely based on commercially available units for general use.
Global Positioning System.
A call made to all members of a pre-defined group within a local geographical area. Only one member of the group may talk at any instant, with all other group members listening only.

A form of unidirectional signalling transmitted periodically or constantly from one radio to another to allow the receiving user to detect a break in radio transmission during critical manoeuvres (eg during shunting).
A term used to describe the process of addressing a particular function (typically a controller) based on the current location of the user (typically a train).
Man-Machine Interface.
A voice communication method whereby a number of parties defined by the call initiator may participate in the call. All parties may talk simultaneously.

glossary.term: Multiple driver communications within the same train
glossary.definition: A term used to describe communications between the drivers of each active cab in a train comprising multiple traction vehicles.
The part of the functional identity of a user which defines the functional role performed by the user on a specific train, engine or coach.
These are railway communications directly concerned with train and shunting movements or train operation. For example, controller-driver communications.
A handheld radio suitable for use by people involved in railway operations (eg shunting team members).
A controller responsible for the management of the traction power supply.

The location and direction of movement of any particular train permits the unique identification of a Primary Controller. The Primary Controller is currently the co-ordinator of train emergency calls. The Primary Controller is normally responsible for the operation of a designated area of track. The exact responsibilities of the Primary Controller are determined on a national basis.
A point-to-point voice call which is used to notify non-railway authorities (such as Police and Ambulance services) of an emergency situation.
Push-To-Talk.
An ERTMS/ETCS term referring to a centralised safety unit to establish and control train separation using radio as the train-to-ground communication medium.

A high priority call for informing drivers, controllers and other concerned personnel of a level of danger requiring all Railways movements in a pre-defined area to stop. Two types are defined: Train emergency calls and Shunting emergency calls.
Radio Block Centre.
The use of a mobile on any communications network other than the user’s home network.
A Secondary Controller is a train controller who holds responsibility for the safe running of trains on a designated area of track (e.g. a signaller).
A group of people manoeuvring trains in order to change their location or composition.
A number assigned to an item of traction or rolling stock on a permanent basis.

These are railway communications which are not directly concerned with train movements or train operation. For example, such communications might involve the passage of catering, maintenance or timetable information.
Train Control System.
A controller who has responsibility for the safe movement of trains.

The process by which the movement of a train is influenced without any action by the driver. This encompasses automatic train protection, automatic train operation and in-cab signalling, with ERTMS/ETCS being the interoperable application.
A number given to a train by operational staff for a particular journey.
A controller who has responsibility for the scheduling of trains and the ‘flow’ of trains over the network. The traffic control function has no formal safety responsibility.
Union Internationale des Chemins de Fer.
Voice Broadcast Service.
Voice Group Call Service.

flowchartDiagram.syntaxExplanation: Flowcharts illustrate processes, algorithms, or workflows using nodes and directed connections. Nodes can represent steps, decisions, or start/end points. Node IDs must be alphanumeric and unique. Labels for nodes or connections with spaces or symbols must be enclosed in double quotes. The flowchart must declare its direction (TD for top-down, LR for left-right). Decision nodes are represented by diamond shapes, and process steps by rectangles.
flowchartDiagram.code:
flowchart TD
    A1["Initiate Emergency Call EF7-UC-001"] --> B2{"User Authenticated?"}
    B2 -->|No| C3["Display Access Denied EF7-REQ-001"]
    B2 -->|Yes| D4["Select Call Type EF7-REQ-002"]
    D4 -->|'Railway Emergency Call'| E5["Determine Affected Area EF7-REQ-003"]
    D4 -->|Other Call Type| F6["Route Normal Call EF7-REQ-004"]
    E5 --> G7["Notify Relevant Personnel EF7-REQ-005"]
    G7 --> H8{"Personnel Acknowledge?"}
    H8 -->|No| I9["Re-notify Personnel EF7-REQ-006"]
    H8 -->|Yes| J10["Confirm Emergency Call EF7-REQ-007"]
    J10 --> K11["Monitor Emergency Status EF7-REQ-008"]
    C3 --> L12[End Process]
    F6 --> L12
    K11 --> L12
flowchartDiagram.caption: EF7_FLOW: Railway Emergency Call Process Flow

sequenceDiagram.syntaxExplanation: Sequence diagrams illustrate the temporal order of interactions between participants in a system. Participants represent entities or objects involved in the interaction. Messages show the flow of information between participants, with arrows indicating the direction and type of message. Aliases can be used for participant IDs but must not be quoted. Message labels should be concise and avoid special characters like curly braces. Activations can be indicated using '->>+' and '-->>-' to show when a participant is active.
sequenceDiagram.code:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Driver as "Locomotive Driver"
    participant EF7_NETWORK as "eirene fun 7 Network"
    participant Controller as "Primary Controller"
    participant OtherUsers as "Other Users in Area"

    Driver->>+EF7_NETWORK: EF7-UC-001: "Initiate Railway Emergency Call"
    EF7_NETWORK->>EF7_NETWORK: EF7-REQ-003: "Determine Affected Area & Priority"
    EF7_NETWORK->>+Controller: EF7-REQ-005: "Notify Emergency Call EF7-REQ-009"
    Controller->>-EF7_NETWORK: EF7-REQ-006: "Acknowledge Emergency Notification EF7-REQ-010"
    EF7_NETWORK->>Driver: EF7-REQ-007: "Confirm Emergency Call Initiation EF7-REQ-011"
    EF7_NETWORK->>OtherUsers: EF7-REQ-005: "Broadcast Emergency Alert EF7-REQ-012"
sequenceDiagram.caption: EF7_SEQ: Railway Emergency Call Sequence Interaction

entityRelationshipDiagram.syntaxExplanation: Entity-Relationship Diagrams (ERD) model data structures by defining entities, their attributes, and relationships. Entities represent significant concepts, with attributes describing their properties. Keys like Primary Key (PK), Foreign Key (FK), and Unique Key (UK) are used. Relationships between entities are defined with cardinality (e.g., one-to-one, one-to-many, many-to-many) and must be labeled with a descriptive, quoted string. Many-to-many relationships require an explicit junction table.
entityRelationshipDiagram.code:
erDiagram
    USER ||--o{ CALL : "initiates"
    CALL ||--o{ CALL_PARTICIPANT : "includes"
    CALL_PARTICIPANT }|--|| USER : "participates_in"
    USER ||--o{ MESSAGE : "sends"
    MESSAGE ||--o{ MESSAGE_RECIPIENT : "targets"
    MESSAGE_RECIPIENT }|--|| USER : "receives_by"
    USER }|--o| MOBILE_EQUIPMENT : "uses"
    MOBILE_EQUIPMENT ||--|| "EIRENE Network" : "connects_to"
    USER ||--o{ FUNCTIONAL_IDENTITY : "has"
    FUNCTIONAL_IDENTITY ||--o| USER : "identifies"
    USER ||--o{ USER_GROUP_MEMBERSHIP : "is_member_of"
    USER_GROUP_MEMBERSHIP }|--|| "EIRENE Group" : "in_group"

    USER {
        int user_id PK
        string username
        string user_type
        string password
        string email
    }
    CALL {
        int call_id PK
        int initiator_user_id FK
        string call_type
        datetime start_time
        datetime end_time
        string status
    }
    CALL_PARTICIPANT {
        int call_id FK
        int user_id FK
        string participant_role
        PK, FK
    }
    MESSAGE {
        int message_id PK
        int sender_user_id FK
        string message_content
        datetime timestamp
        string message_type
    }
    MESSAGE_RECIPIENT {
        int message_id FK
        int receiver_user_id FK
        datetime received_time
        PK, FK
    }
    MOBILE_EQUIPMENT {
        int equipment_id PK
        string equipment_type
        string serial_number UK
        string current_location
        int user_id FK "assigned_to"
    }
    FUNCTIONAL_IDENTITY {
        int identity_id PK
        string function_name UK
        string associated_number UK
        int user_id FK
    }
    "EIRENE Group" {
        int group_id PK
        string group_name UK
        string group_type
        string area_definition
    }
    USER_GROUP_MEMBERSHIP {
        int user_id FK
        int group_id FK
        datetime membership_start_date
        PK, FK
    }
    "EIRENE Network" {
        int network_id PK
        string network_name UK
        string coverage_area
    }
entityRelationshipDiagram.caption: EF7_ERD: eirene fun 7 Core Entities

introduction.purpose: The primary objective of this Functional Requirements Specification (FRS) report is to provide a comprehensive and detailed description of the functionalities required for the initial version of the **cctns** (Crime & Criminal Tracking Network and Systems). This document focuses on outlining the critical features designed to enhance the operational capabilities of police personnel, thereby improving outcomes in crime investigation and criminal detection within the e-governance framework. It serves as a foundational reference for all subsequent development and testing activities related to the **cctns** project.
introduction.documentConventions: This document adheres to the guidelines specified in IEEE 830-1998, 'Recommended Practice for Software Requirements Specifications,' ensuring a standardized and unambiguous representation of requirements. All functional and non-functional requirements are presented with clear, concise language, utilizing a consistent numbering scheme and terminology. Key terms, acronyms, and abbreviations are defined within the dedicated glossary section to maintain clarity and avoid misinterpretation throughout the document.
introduction.intendedAudience: This document is primarily intended for project stakeholders, including but not limited to, software developers, quality assurance engineers, project managers, system architects, and government officials from the Ministry of Home Affairs. It also serves as a critical reference for system administrators, trainers, and future maintenance teams responsible for the **cctns** system. The detailed functional and non-functional specifications aim to facilitate a shared understanding of the system's capabilities and operational parameters among all involved parties.
introduction.productScope: The **cctns** V1.0 system is designed as a mission-mode e-governance project to create a comprehensive network for tracking crime and criminals across India. Its scope encompasses the digitization and automation of core police functions, including complaint registration, criminal investigation, case prosecution, and an extensive search capability. The system provides a dedicated citizen interface for seamless interaction and information exchange, along with robust navigation and configuration modules to support role-based operations and system adaptability. While focusing on critical functionalities, this initial version lays the groundwork for future enhancements and broader integration within the national criminal justice system.
references: IEEE 830-1998 IEEE Recommended Practice for Software Requirements Specifications.
ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018 Systems and software engineering — Life cycle processes — Requirements engineering.

overallDescription.productPerspective: The system's requirements are fundamentally influenced by the need to enhance the efficiency and effectiveness of police operations, particularly in crime investigation and criminal detection. Key interfaces with other systems will include judicial court systems for prosecution data exchange, national identity databases for citizen verification, and potentially forensic labs for evidence management. Its strategic importance dictates a robust, secure, and highly available architecture capable of supporting operations across diverse geographical and organizational units.
productFunctions: Registration: Facilitates the formal registration of citizen complaints and initial incident reporting.
Investigation: Manages and automates critical tasks throughout the criminal investigation process.
Prosecution: Supports police personnel in interacting with judicial courts for case prosecution.
Search: Provides comprehensive capabilities for basic and advanced searching of cases, persons, and property.
Citizen Interface: Enables secure information exchange and communication between citizens and police.
Navigation: Offers role-based access and personalized dashboards for efficient application navigation.
Application Configuration: Allows for customization and management of state-specific data elements and rules.
Help Module: Delivers context-sensitive and accessible help material for all system users.
Support Module: Provides a mechanism for users to log, track, and receive alerts on defects and enhancement requests.
Audit Module: Maintains an unalterable record of all critical user and system actions for accountability.
Access Control Module: Enforces granular, role-based security to protect sensitive case and user information.

userClassesAndCharacteristics.characteristics: These users are the primary operational beneficiaries of **cctns**, directly involved in crime investigation, data entry, and case management. They possess varying levels of technical expertise but require intuitive interfaces to minimize training overhead. Their roles demand high levels of access to case details, suspect information, and evidence, with specific privileges determined by their rank and duties. They rely on the system for daily operations, case planning, and reporting, expecting high performance and availability.

Citizens interact with the **cctns** through the dedicated Citizen Interface module to register complaints, seek information, and receive acknowledgments from the police. These users may have minimal technical expertise and expect a simple, user-friendly, and secure platform. Their primary needs include ease of access, clear communication, and timely updates regarding their submissions. Data privacy and the secure exchange of personal information are paramount for this user class.

These users are responsible for managing and resolving support requests submitted by other system users. They possess technical proficiency related to the **cctns** application and its underlying infrastructure. Their responsibilities include viewing, tracking, categorizing, and generating reports on submitted defects and enhancement requests. They require elevated privileges to access support-related modules and communicate effectively with reporting users.

Admin-users are responsible for system-level configurations, user profile management, and group allocations. They possess advanced technical and administrative knowledge of the **cctns** system. Their privileges include setting up new user accounts, modifying user groups, and managing state-specific data and rules within the Configuration module. They must ensure data integrity and system security through proper administrative practices.

Super-users represent the highest level of administrative authority within the **cctns** system, possessing comprehensive control over security attributes and system-wide parameters. They are responsible for critical security management functions such as modifying access rights, security levels, privileges, and password policies. This user class requires the deepest technical understanding and adherence to strict security protocols, as their actions have significant system-wide implications, including audit trail management.

External auditors periodically review the system's operational integrity, data accuracy, and compliance with regulations, particularly through the Audit Module. They require secure, read-only access to audit trails and the ability to export audit data for independent analysis without modifying system records. These users possess specialized knowledge in auditing and compliance standards, and require intuitive reporting capabilities for their specific oversight functions.

overallDescription.operatingEnvironment: Integration with existing government systems and external judicial platforms will be facilitated through well-defined **REST API**s and secure communication protocols. The system must coexist with and potentially integrate with document management systems, biometric authentication services, and alert notification services such as **SMS** gateways and email servers. All interactions must adhere to stringent security standards and government IT policies.
designAndImplementationConstraints: The **cctns** system must comply with all relevant Indian government e-governance policies, IT acts, and data privacy regulations, including those pertaining to sensitive personal data and national security information. All development must adhere to security best practices for public sector applications.

The system must integrate with existing police department hardware infrastructure where feasible, avoiding proprietary hardware dependencies that could hinder widespread deployment. This includes compatibility with standard biometric devices for user authentication and identification, if applicable.

Interfacing with external court management systems and national identity databases is mandatory, requiring the adoption of specified data exchange formats and communication protocols, potentially including **SOAP** or **RESTful Web Services**.

The database schema design and implementation must prioritize data integrity, auditability, and long-term archival capabilities, specifically supporting unalterable audit trails as mandated. Use of a **PostgreSQL** or **Oracle** database is preferred.
The system shall be developed using open-source technologies where appropriate, to reduce licensing costs and promote flexibility, while still meeting performance and security benchmarks.
All user interfaces must adhere to government-mandated accessibility standards, including ISO 9241 and WCAG 1.0 guidelines, to ensure usability for citizens and police personnel with special needs.
userDocumentation: The **cctns** system will be delivered with a comprehensive set of user documentation components. This includes detailed user manuals for police personnel, covering all functional modules from Registration to Configuration, with specific sections for IOs and records room staff. On-line help will be context-sensitive, available both within the application and offline, providing immediate assistance for all actions and scenarios.

Additionally, administrator guides will be provided for Admin-users and Super-users, detailing system configuration, user management, security attribute modifications, and reporting functionalities. Tutorial videos and frequently asked questions (FAQs) will be made available to facilitate quick onboarding and continuous learning for all user classes, especially for the Citizen Interface module. All documentation will be available in digital formats, adhering to government standards for accessibility and readability.
assumptionsAndDependencies: It is assumed that necessary physical infrastructure, including reliable power supply, network connectivity, and appropriate hardware for server deployment, will be provided and maintained by the respective government entities at district and state levels. The availability of consistent high-speed internet connectivity for remote police stations and citizen access points is a critical assumption.

The successful implementation of **cctns** is dependent on the active participation and cooperation of various police departments, judicial bodies, and other government agencies for data sharing and process integration. Availability of qualified personnel for system administration and support at various levels is also assumed.

The project assumes the availability of updated and standardized master data (e.g., acts, sections, castes, tribes, property types) from relevant government sources for initial system configuration and ongoing updates. Any changes in government policies or legal frameworks related to crime reporting and investigation are considered external dependencies that may require system adjustments.

externalInterfaceRequirements.userInterfaces: Key elements like navigation menus, search bars, and standard action buttons (e.g., Save, Submit, Cancel, Help) will be consistently placed across all modules. Error messages will be meaningful, providing explanatory text and actionable advice to users. The interface will allow for user customization of displayed columns, layout, and appearance (colors, fonts), with these preferences persistently saved in user profiles. The application logo will serve as a consistent link back to the home page from any screen.
externalInterfaceRequirements.hardwareInterfaces: Input device independence will be maintained, allowing controls to be activated by various input methods, including keyboard navigation, to support accessibility. No direct, low-level hardware control requirements are anticipated for the application itself; interactions will be mediated through the operating system and network layers.
externalInterfaceRequirements.softwareInterfaces: Critical integrations include interfaces with external judicial court systems to exchange case prosecution data, likely via **RESTful APIs** or secure file transfer protocols, ensuring legal admissibility. The system may also interface with national identity databases for citizen verification and criminal record checks. All software interfaces will adhere to secure communication standards, with data encryption in transit and at rest, to protect sensitive crime and personal data.
externalInterfaceRequirements.communicationsInterfaces: SMS alerts, such as those for support ticket updates or citizen interface acknowledgements, will leverage third-party **SMS** gateway services, requiring secure **API** integrations. Message formatting for all communications will be standardized to ensure clarity and consistency. Data transfer rates must be optimized to support responsive user interactions and efficient data synchronization across distributed police stations, especially in areas with limited bandwidth. Secure socket layer (**SSL/TLS**) protocols will be strictly enforced for all external communications.

systemFeatures.name: Complaint Registration
systemFeatures.description: This feature provides citizens with a streamlined and accessible mechanism to formally register complaints with the police. It enhances transparency and ease of interaction between law enforcement and the public, reducing reliance on physical visits and paperwork. Technically, this module acts as the primary data ingress point for new cases, requiring robust validation and secure storage of initial complaint data, subsequently linking to the Investigation module for further processing. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Citizen navigates to the C-CitizenInterface and selects 'Register Complaint'. Response: The system displays a complaint registration form.
Stimulus: Citizen fills required fields and submits the form. Response: The system validates input, creates a new complaint record, and provides an acknowledgement with a unique tracking ID.
Stimulus: Police personnel (IO) views new complaints awaiting assignment. Response: The system displays a list of unassigned complaints in the C-Navigation dashboard.
Stimulus: Police personnel assigns an Investigating Officer (IO) to a complaint. Response: The system updates the complaint status, notifies the assigned IO, and initiates the investigation workflow.
functionalRequirements: The system shall allow citizens to register new complaints through a dedicated web interface.
The system shall capture essential complaint details including complainant information, incident type, date, time, and location.
The system shall validate all submitted complaint data for completeness and format before saving.
The system shall generate a unique identification number for each registered complaint.
The system shall provide an immediate acknowledgment to the citizen upon successful complaint registration.
The system shall enable police personnel to view and filter newly registered complaints.
The system shall allow authorized police personnel to assign an Investigating Officer (IO) to a complaint.
The system shall automatically notify the assigned IO of new case assignments.

systemFeatures.name: Criminal Investigation Management
systemFeatures.description: This feature automates and facilitates the entire criminal investigation process, from initial complaint intake to evidence collection and suspect tracking. It significantly improves operational efficiencies for Investigating Officers (IOs) by centralizing all case-related information and automating tedious administrative tasks. Architecturally, this module integrates deeply with the Registration module and serves as a central repository for evolving case data, enabling real-time updates and collaborative investigation efforts. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: IO accesses an assigned complaint from their C-Navigation dashboard. Response: The system displays the detailed investigation dashboard for the selected case.

Stimulus: IO records new findings, evidence, or suspect details. Response: The system updates the case file, logs the action in the C-Audit module, and makes the information available to authorized personnel.
Stimulus: IO generates a preliminary investigation report. Response: The system compiles relevant case data and outputs a formatted report for review and submission.
Stimulus: IO closes the investigation or escalates for prosecution. Response: The system updates the case status and, if escalated, transfers relevant data to the C-Prosecution module.
functionalRequirements: The system shall enable IOs to view all details of assigned complaints.
The system shall allow IOs to record investigation progress, including steps taken, findings, and evidence collected.
The system shall support the attachment of digital evidence such as images, videos, and documents to case files.
The system shall provide functionality to track the status of suspects, including arrest, bail, or absconding.
The system shall automatically link new suspect or property entries to existing records if a match is found.
The system shall enable IOs to generate various investigation-related reports.
The system shall provide a mechanism to mark an investigation as complete.
The system shall allow for the escalation of a completed investigation to the prosecution stage.

systemFeatures.name: Case Prosecution Interface
systemFeatures.description: This feature provides police personnel with a dedicated platform to manage interactions with judicial courts during the prosecution of cases. It streamlines the often complex communication and documentation exchange between police stations and the legal system, enhancing accountability and efficiency. Technically, this module requires robust data synchronization capabilities to ensure that court records accurately reflect police case files and vice-versa, facilitating the seamless flow of legal information. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Designated constable accesses the C-Prosecution module for a case. Response: The system displays relevant case details and a list of upcoming court dates.
Stimulus: Constable records proceedings of a court hearing. Response: The system captures the hearing details, including outcomes and next steps, and updates the case record.
Stimulus: Constable generates a court-mandated document. Response: The system populates a template with case-specific data and prepares the document for printing or digital submission.
Stimulus: Judicial system updates case status. Response: The system automatically reflects the change in the C-Prosecution module and notifies relevant police personnel.
functionalRequirements: The system shall allow police personnel to record details of court interactions and proceedings for each case.
The system shall track and display upcoming court hearing dates and deadlines.
The system shall enable the attachment of court orders and judgments to case files.
The system shall support the generation of standardized prosecution documents, such as charge sheets and witness summons.
The system shall provide a chronological log of all prosecution-related events for a case.
The system shall allow for the updating of a case's disposition status (e.g., acquitted, convicted, dismissed).
The system shall alert police personnel about critical prosecution deadlines.
The system shall facilitate data exchange with external court management systems where applicable.

systemFeatures.name: Advanced Search and Reporting
systemFeatures.description: This feature empowers police personnel with advanced capabilities to search, filter, and report on diverse datasets within the **cctns** system. It significantly improves investigative efficiency by allowing users to quickly retrieve relevant information on persons, crimes, modus operandi, and property. From a technical standpoint, this module requires a highly optimized search engine and indexing capabilities to deliver rapid results across vast amounts of structured and unstructured data, supporting complex query logic. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Police personnel initiates a basic search query (e.g., suspect name). Response: The system returns a filtered list of matching records within 5-8 seconds.
Stimulus: Police personnel initiates an advanced search using multiple criteria (e.g., crime type, location, date range). Response: The system returns a refined list of results within 10-15 seconds.
Stimulus: User customizes the display columns in the search results view. Response: The system re-renders the results table with the selected columns and saves the preference to the user profile.
Stimulus: Police personnel requests a monthly crime report. Response: The system executes the query, aggregates data, and generates a formatted report.
functionalRequirements: The system shall provide a basic search functionality for quick retrieval of records by keywords.
The system shall provide an advanced search functionality allowing multiple search criteria (e.g., person, crime type, modus operandi, property, date range).
The system shall allow users to customize the columns displayed in search result lists.
The system shall save user-specific search result view customizations.
The system shall enable the execution of pre-defined queries, such as monthly reporting and RTI-related requests.
The system shall export search results into common formats such as CSV or PDF.
The system shall provide search response times of 5-8 seconds for simple searches.
The system shall provide search response times of 10-15 seconds for advanced searches, regardless of data volume.

systemFeatures.name: Citizen Information Interface
systemFeatures.description: This feature provides a secure and accessible conduit for information exchange between citizens and police units, fostering greater transparency and trust. It enables citizens to receive acknowledgements, updates, and general information, while allowing police to respond efficiently, significantly reducing administrative overhead associated with traditional paperwork. From a technical perspective, this module requires robust public-facing infrastructure, secure messaging capabilities, and integration with core police data to deliver timely and accurate information. Priority: Medium.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Citizen logs into the C-CitizenInterface using their tracking ID. Response: The system displays the current status and updates for their registered complaints.
Stimulus: Citizen requests an acknowledgement of a previous submission. Response: The system retrieves and displays the official acknowledgement, offering a download option.
Stimulus: Police personnel sends a response to a citizen's query. Response: The system delivers the response securely to the citizen's interface and sends an alert (email/SMS) if opted in.
Stimulus: Citizen submits feedback or asks a general query. Response: The system routes the query to the appropriate police unit and initiates a response workflow.
functionalRequirements: The system shall provide a secure online portal for citizens to access information related to their registered complaints.
The system shall display the real-time status and progress updates of citizen-registered complaints.
The system shall allow citizens to download official acknowledgments and documents.
The system shall enable police personnel to send direct responses and updates to citizens through the interface.
The system shall notify citizens via email or SMS when an action has been taken on their queries or complaints, if configured.
The system shall provide a facility for citizens to submit general queries or feedback.
The system shall maintain a secure communication log between citizens and police personnel.

systemFeatures.name: Role-Based Navigation and Dashboard
systemFeatures.description: This feature ensures that police personnel receive personalized and relevant information immediately upon logging into the **cctns** application through role-based landing pages. It enhances individual productivity by providing quick access to assigned cases, alerts, and pending tasks, enabling more efficient planning and execution of duties. Architecturally, this module relies on the underlying role management and task assignment systems to dynamically generate dashboard content, optimizing user experience and operational focus. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Police personnel logs into the C-cctns application. Response: The system identifies the user's role and displays a customized landing page (dashboard).
Stimulus: User views the 'Cases Assigned' section on the dashboard. Response: The system lists all cases currently assigned to the logged-in user with their current status.
Stimulus: User clicks on a pending task alert. Response: The system navigates to the relevant module and task details, allowing immediate action.
Stimulus: System generates a critical alert for the user. Response: The system displays the alert prominently on the dashboard and potentially sends a notification.
functionalRequirements: The system shall provide role-based landing pages upon successful user login.
The system shall display a personalized dashboard containing cases assigned to the user.
The system shall show alerts and notifications relevant to the user's role and assigned tasks.
The system shall list pending tasks and actions requiring the user's attention.
The system shall allow users to navigate directly from dashboard items to the respective functional modules.
The system shall provide real-time updates for critical information displayed on the dashboard.
The system shall ensure that only information pertinent to the user's role and permissions is displayed.
The system shall allow users to dismiss or archive non-critical alerts.

systemFeatures.name: Application Configuration and Master Data Management
systemFeatures.description: This feature enables administrators to configure the **cctns** application according to state-specific requirements and maintain up-to-date master data. It ensures the system's adaptability to diverse regional legal frameworks and administrative structures, promoting accuracy and consistency across the network. Technically, this module provides a secure administrative interface for managing critical lookup tables and configuration parameters, impacting data validation and reporting across all other modules. Priority: Medium.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Admin-user accesses the C-Configuration module. Response: The system displays a list of configurable data elements and rules.
Stimulus: Admin-user adds a new 'Act' or 'Section' to the legal framework. Response: The system validates the entry, updates the master data, and makes it available system-wide.
Stimulus: Admin-user modifies state-specific data or property information. Response: The system applies the changes, ensuring all relevant modules use the updated information.
Stimulus: Admin-user views the audit trail for configuration changes. Response: The system displays a log of all modifications made within the C-Configuration module.
functionalRequirements: The system shall provide an administrative interface for configuring application parameters.
The system shall enable the creation, update, and deletion of 'Act' and 'Section' definitions.
The system shall allow for the management of state-specific data elements, such as castes, tribes, and geographical units.
The system shall support the configuration of property types and their associated attributes.
The system shall ensure that all configuration changes are logged in the C-Audit module.
The system shall provide validation rules for all master data entries to ensure consistency.
The system shall allow for the dynamic application of configuration changes without requiring system downtime.
The system shall enable the import and export of configuration data for backup and migration purposes.

systemFeatures.name: Context-Sensitive Help System
systemFeatures.description: This feature provides users with immediate, detailed, and context-sensitive help material directly within the **cctns** application. It significantly improves user-friendliness and reduces the learning curve, empowering police personnel and citizens to utilize the system effectively without extensive external training. Technically, this module requires a comprehensive help content management system that integrates seamlessly with the UI, delivering both online and offline access to relevant documentation based on the user's current screen or action. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User clicks the 'Help' icon on any screen. Response: The system displays context-sensitive help specific to that screen or current action.
Stimulus: User searches for a keyword within the help documentation. Response: The system presents a list of relevant help articles and guides.
Stimulus: User accesses help documentation in an offline mode. Response: The system provides pre-downloaded or cached help content for continuous support.
Stimulus: User navigates through help topics. Response: The system displays detailed explanations, examples, and troubleshooting steps.
functionalRequirements: The system shall provide context-sensitive help material for all user interfaces and actions within the application.
The system shall make help documentation accessible to users in both online and offline modes.
The system shall provide a search function within the help module to locate specific topics.
The system shall offer detailed explanations and scenarios for each help topic.
The system shall ensure that help content is continuously updated and consistent with application functionality.
The system shall allow users to print or save help articles.
The system shall provide a feedback mechanism within the help module for users to suggest improvements.

systemFeatures.name: Integrated Support and Feedback System
systemFeatures.description: This feature offers an integrated mechanism for users to log defects, submit enhancement requests, and track their resolution directly within or outside the **cctns** application. It enhances user satisfaction and system maintainability by providing a formal channel for feedback and issue resolution, fostering continuous improvement. From a technical perspective, this module requires a ticketing system, automated alert capabilities (email/SMS), and reporting tools to manage support workflows and provide insights to help-desk personnel. Priority: Medium.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User logs a defect or enhancement request via the Support interface. Response: The system creates a support ticket with a unique ID and sends an acknowledgment to the user.
Stimulus: Help-desk user updates the status of a support ticket. Response: The system notifies the original requester via email or SMS (if opted in) about the status change.
Stimulus: User tracks the progress of a submitted request. Response: The system displays the current status, assigned personnel, and resolution notes for the ticket.
Stimulus: Help-desk user generates a report on support tickets. Response: The system provides categorized reports (e.g., by status, age, category) to identify trends and backlogs.
functionalRequirements: The system shall provide an interface for users to log defects or enhancement requests.
The system shall assign a unique identifier to each submitted request.
The system shall allow users to track the status and progress of their submitted requests.
The system shall send alerts (email, SMS) to users when actions are taken on their requests, if configured.
The system shall enable help-desk users to view reports on submitted requests categorized by status, type, and age.
The system shall be accessible to users both from within the application and through a separate browser interface.
The system shall allow help-desk users to assign and reassign support tickets to other personnel.
The system shall store all communication related to a support ticket within the ticket itself.

systemFeatures.name: Unalterable Audit Trail
systemFeatures.description: This feature ensures the integrity and legal admissibility of all actions performed within the **cctns** system by maintaining a comprehensive and unalterable audit trail. It is crucial for accountability, compliance, and forensic analysis, recording every significant interaction with critical entities. Technically, this module must automatically capture and securely store details of all CRUD operations, user identities, and timestamps, with strict safeguards against any modification or deletion of audit records. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User performs a 'create', 'read', 'update', or 'delete' action on a case or suspect record. Response: The system automatically records the action, user ID, date, and time in the unalterable audit trail.
Stimulus: Administrator attempts to modify an audit trail entry. Response: The system detects and prevents any modification, logging the attempted violation in the audit trail itself.

Stimulus: Authorized external personnel requests an audit report for a specific case. Response: The system generates a chronological report of all actions related to that case without affecting the stored audit trail.
Stimulus: System detects an attempted unauthorized access. Response: The system logs the violation, including user, timestamp, and attempted resource, in the audit trail.
functionalRequirements: The system shall automatically capture and store an unalterable audit trail for all actions on critical entities (case, suspect, property).
The system shall record the user initiating the action, the date, and the time of the event for every audit entry.
The system shall prevent any modification or deletion of audit trail data by any user or administrator.
The system shall maintain audit trails for at least the entire lifecycle of the associated case.
The system shall make audit trail data available for inspection by authorized personnel on request.
The system shall export audit trails for specified cases without impacting the stored audit data.
The system shall capture and store violations of access control mechanisms, including attempted unauthorized access.
The system shall provide audit reports organized by case, by user, and in chronological sequence.

systemFeatures.name: Granular Access Control
systemFeatures.description: This feature implements robust, role-based access control mechanisms to limit user access to sensitive cases, files, records, and functionalities within the **cctns** system. It is critical for ensuring data confidentiality, privacy, and compliance with security regulations, preventing unauthorized information disclosure or manipulation. Architecturally, this module underpins all user interactions, requiring efficient permission checks at every access point and providing administrators with fine-grained control over user privileges. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Super-user assigns specific access rights to a user group. Response: The system updates the security attributes for the group, enforcing new permissions across the application.

Stimulus: User attempts to access a case to which they are denied access. Response: The system either displays a filtered view, indicates existence without details, or entirely conceals the case based on configured security settings.
Stimulus: Admin-user creates a new user profile and assigns them to groups. Response: The system registers the new user with their allocated permissions and makes them active.

Stimulus: User performs a search. Response: The system automatically filters the search results to include only records the user is authorized to access, without indicating the existence of restricted records.
functionalRequirements: The system shall allow users to limit access to cases to specified users or user groups.
The system shall provide for role-based access control for all functionalities within the system.
The system shall allow a user to be a member of more than one user group.
The system shall allow only admin-users to set up user profiles and allocate users to groups.
The system shall allow users to stipulate which other users or groups can access specific cases.
The system shall allow changes to security attributes (access rights, privileges, passwords) only by super-users.
The system shall provide configurable responses for users attempting to access unauthorized cases (display metadata, indicate existence, or hide completely).
The system shall never include records in search results if the user does not have the right to access them.
The system shall log unauthorized access attempts in the C-Audit module.
The system shall store all access to cases and related activities in the C-Audit module for legal admissibility.

performanceRequirements: The **cctns** system shall provide adequate response times for commonly performed functions under both standard and peak load conditions, ensuring a responsive user experience. Standard conditions are defined as 500 concurrent users, while peak conditions are defined as 2000 concurrent users during business hours. This requirement ensures that critical police operations are not hindered by system latency.

The system shall complete a simple search query within 5-8 seconds, regardless of the overall database size or number of cases. This includes the time taken to return a result list, excluding the retrieval of individual records. This response time is crucial for rapid information retrieval during investigations.

The system shall execute an advanced search query (involving multiple search criteria) within 10-15 seconds, irrespective of the storage capacity or total number of cases. This performance metric is vital for complex analytical tasks and reporting, supporting efficient decision-making by police personnel.

The system shall process and store audit trail entries with negligible impact on the performance of the core functional modules, ensuring that the auditing mechanism does not introduce significant latency for operational users. Audit logging operations must occur asynchronously to maintain system responsiveness.
safetyRequirements: The **cctns** system shall implement robust data backup and recovery mechanisms to prevent data loss in the event of hardware or software failures. Full database backups shall be performed daily, with incremental backups hourly, ensuring minimal data loss and rapid restoration capabilities. This is critical for maintaining the continuity of police operations and the integrity of sensitive crime data.

The system shall incorporate disaster recovery protocols, including replication to an off-site data center, to ensure business continuity in the event of a major regional outage. The Recovery Time Objective (RTO) shall not exceed 24 hours, and the Recovery Point Objective (RPO) shall not exceed 1 hour. This safeguards against catastrophic events affecting system availability and data accessibility.

The system shall employ data validation rules at all input points and database constraints to maintain the integrity and consistency of crime and criminal data. Automated checks will prevent the entry of invalid or malformed data, reducing errors that could compromise investigations or legal proceedings. This is paramount for the legal admissibility of system-generated information.
securityRequirements: The **cctns** system shall enforce strong authentication mechanisms for all users, including multi-factor authentication for administrative and super-user roles. Passwords shall adhere to complex policies, including minimum length, character types, and expiration, to prevent unauthorized access. This protects sensitive crime data from malicious actors.

The system shall implement granular, role-based access control, ensuring that users can only access data and functionalities strictly necessary for their assigned duties. This principle of least privilege is fundamental to protecting sensitive information and maintaining data confidentiality.

The system shall maintain an unalterable audit trail of all critical actions, including data creation, modification, deletion, and access attempts on sensitive entities. This audit trail is crucial for forensic analysis, accountability, and demonstrating compliance with legal and regulatory requirements, safeguarding against insider threats.

All data transmitted across networks, both internal and external, shall be encrypted using industry-standard protocols (e.g., **TLS 1.2+** for **HTTPS**). Data at rest, particularly sensitive personal information and case details, shall also be encrypted to protect against unauthorized access to storage media. This ensures the confidentiality of information throughout its lifecycle.

The system shall protect against common web vulnerabilities, including SQL injection, cross-site scripting (XSS), and cross-site request forgery (CSRF), through secure coding practices and regular security audits. This minimizes potential attack vectors that could compromise system integrity or data confidentiality.
softwareQualityAttributes: **Usability**: The system shall adhere to ISO 9241-110, ISO 9241-171, and WCAG 1.0 standards for user interface design and accessibility. It will feature a consistent look and feel, context-sensitive help, customizable interfaces, and clear, actionable error messages. This ensures that police personnel and citizens, including those with special needs, can use the system efficiently and effectively.

**Availability**: The system shall be available to users from 08:00 to 20:00 on all weekdays, and 10:00 to 17:00 on weekends. Planned downtime shall not exceed 4 hours per rolling three-month period, and unplanned downtime shall not exceed 2 hours per rolling three-month period, with no more than 2 incidents. This guarantees that police operations can proceed without significant interruption.

**Reliability**: The system shall maintain a high level of data integrity through robust validation and audit logging, ensuring that recorded information is accurate and trustworthy. In the event of software or hardware failure, the system must be fully restorable (with inline synchronization) within no more than 6 hours. This prevents data corruption and ensures system resilience under operational stress.

**Maintainability**: The system shall be designed with modular architecture, clear code documentation, and adherence to established coding standards to facilitate ease of maintenance and future enhancements. The audit and support modules contribute to maintainability by providing comprehensive logs and a structured feedback mechanism for issue resolution.
