All system data (configuration, default parameters, planning, and usage data) will be backed up daily at a technician-defined time, and in case of failure, the recovery mechanism shall restore data from the most recent backup. This ensures data persistence and rapid system restoration after outages.

H1-REQ-053: Reliability: All **home 1.3** operations shall incorporate comprehensive exception handling. The system will respond to users with clear, descriptive messages when errors or exceptional conditions occur, improving usability and guiding users through issues effectively.

H1-REQ-054: Maintainability: The development of the **home 1.3** system shall adhere to methods and techniques that support system maintenance, including thorough documentation of requirements, design, and code. This promotes long-term understanding and ease of modification. The system design will emphasize abstraction, information hiding, and module independence to facilitate future updates and reduce the risk of unintended side effects.

H1-REQ-055: Maintainability: The prototype version shall be designed and implemented such that all modules and components can be readily incorporated into a fully specified commercial version of the **DigitalHome System**. This foresight ensures that development efforts for the prototype contribute directly to future product evolution without requiring extensive refactoring.

H1-REQ-056: Usability: The system shall provide an intuitive web interface, catering to general users who are familiar with basic web operations. Clear visual feedback and understandable navigation paths will reduce the learning curve and minimize user errors, enhancing overall user satisfaction and efficiency.

H1-REQ-057: Testability: The **home 1.3** prototype, operating in a simulated environment, shall be designed with clear interfaces and modular components to facilitate comprehensive testing. This enables isolated testing of features and behaviors, ensuring that the system functions correctly before potential integration with real hardware.

H1-REQ-058: Portability: The system shall employ widely used and accepted hardware and software technologies and standards, ensuring that it can be adapted to various operating environments and platforms with minimal effort. This approach enhances the long-term viability and potential market reach of the **DigitalHome** product line.
businessRules: H1-REQ-059: All system documents (Software Requirements Specification, Architectural Design Specification, Module Detailed Design, Module Source Code, and all Test Plans) shall be current, utilize the **Homeowner document format [HO2305]**, and reside in the **HomeOwner Document Archive** upon project completion. This ensures consistent documentation standards and centralized knowledge management.

H1-REQ-060: **HomeOwner** has designated object-oriented development, using **UML 2.0**, as the preferred method for software development for **HomeOwner** products. Any exceptions to this rule must be formally approved by the **CIO**. This establishes a clear architectural and methodological standard for all software initiatives within the organization.

otherRequirements: No additional specific requirements beyond those detailed in the functional and non-functional sections are identified at this time for the **home 1.3** prototype.

glossary.definition: American Society of Heating, Refrigerating and Air-Conditioning Engineers.
Any process by which it is verified that someone is who they claim they are. This typically involves submission and verification of a username and a password.
A high data rate Internet access, typically contrasted with slower dial-up access.
The name of the prototype smart home product that is being produced by **DigitalHomeOwner**.
The division of **HomeOwner Inc.** responsible for producing **DigitalHome** products.
A family of technologies that provides digital data transmission over the wires of a local telephone network.

The process of transforming information (referred to as plaintext) using an algorithm (called cipher) to make it unreadable to anyone except those possessing special knowledge, usually referred to as a key.
Frequently Asked Question.
A home networking device used to connect devices in the home to the Internet or other Wide Area Networks.
The largest national retail chain serving the needs of home owners in building, furnishing, repairing, and improving their homes.
Heating, Ventilation and Air Conditioning.
An instrument that measures and controls relative humidity.
Internet Service Provider.
Personal Digital Assistant.
Radio Frequency.

An external act that bypasses or contravenes the digital home security system. For example, if an intruder opens a door where the contact sensor is set to “on,” the system will sense a 'security breach'.
A numeric valuable that establishes the desire value of an environmental quality (e.g., a temperature value of 70° F).
Software Requirements Specification.
Cryptographic protocols that provide security for communications over networks such as the Internet.

flowchartDiagram.syntaxExplanation: Flowcharts illustrate processes, workflows, or decision paths. Nodes represent steps or decisions, connected by arrows indicating flow. Node IDs are alphanumeric, and labels with spaces are enclosed in double quotes. The chart direction (TD for Top-Down, LR for Left-Right) is declared at the beginning. Decision nodes are typically diamond-shaped, while process steps are rectangular, and start/end points are rounded rectangles or circles.
flowchartDiagram.code:
flowchart TD
    Login["User Login"] --> SelectDevice{"Select Device Type?"}
    SelectDevice -->|Thermostat| C1["View/Set Temperature"]
    SelectDevice -->|Humidistat| D1["View/Set Humidity"]
    SelectDevice -->|Security| E1["Monitor Sensors/Alarms"]
    SelectDevice -->|Appliance| F1["View/Change Appliance State"]

    C1 --> UpdateDB["Update System/Database"]
    D1 --> UpdateDB
    E1 --> UpdateDB
    F1 --> UpdateDB

    UpdateDB --> DisplayStatus["Display Updated Status"]
    DisplayStatus --> EndProcess["End Session"]
flowchartDiagram.caption: home 1.3 Device Management Workflow

sequenceDiagram.syntaxExplanation: Sequence diagrams show interactions between participants in chronological order, emphasizing the sequence of messages exchanged. Participants are declared with unique aliases, and messages are depicted with arrows indicating the sender, receiver, and message content. Activations show when a participant is active in a process. Lifelines represent the existence of a participant over time. Asynchronous messages are represented by open arrowheads, synchronous by solid arrowheads. `->>+` indicates activation, `-->>-` indicates deactivation. Participant aliases, if used (e.g., `participant U as User`), must not have quoted display names (e.g., `participant U as "User"` is forbidden); the display name must be an unquoted string (e.g., `participant U as User`).
sequenceDiagram.code:
sequenceDiagram
    participant HomeUser
    participant WebApp
    participant HomeWebServer
    participant Gateway
    participant ThermostatDevice
    participant HomeDatabase

    HomeUser->>+WebApp: Request "Thermostat Control Page"
    WebApp->>HomeWebServer: Authenticate User
    HomeWebServer-->>WebApp: "Authentication OK"
    WebApp->>HomeUser: Display "Thermostat Control UI"
    HomeUser->>WebApp: Input "New Temperature Value (e.g., 72°F)"
    WebApp->>+HomeWebServer: POST "/thermostat/set" (DeviceId, TempValue)
    HomeWebServer->>+Gateway: "Wireless Command (DeviceId, TempValue)"
    Gateway->>+ThermostatDevice: "RF Signal (Set TempValue)"
    ThermostatDevice-->>-Gateway: "ACK: Temp Set Successfully"
    Gateway-->>-HomeWebServer: "Response: Device Updated"
    HomeWebServer->>+HomeDatabase: Save "Thermostat Log (DeviceId, TempValue, Timestamp)"
    HomeDatabase-->>-HomeWebServer: "DB Save OK"
    HomeWebServer-->>-WebApp: "200 OK: Temperature Updated"
    WebApp-->>-HomeUser: Display "Updated Temperature on UI"
sequenceDiagram.caption: home 1.3 Thermostat Temperature Update Sequence

entityRelationshipDiagram.syntaxExplanation: Entity Relationship Diagrams (ERD) illustrate the logical structure of a database, showing entities (tables), their attributes, and the relationships between them. Each entity has a name and a list of attributes. Keys (Primary Key - PK, Foreign Key - FK, Unique Key - UK) are explicitly denoted. Relationships specify cardinality (e.g., ||--o{ for one-to-many, }|--|| for many-to-one to represent FK relation), and labels describing the relationship are enclosed in double quotes. Only 'PK', 'FK', and 'UK' are permitted for attribute keys; 'NN' is forbidden. When an attribute serves as both PK and FK, it is explicitly listed as 'PK, FK'.
entityRelationshipDiagram.code:
erDiagram
    USER ||--o{ HOME : "manages"
    HOME ||--o{ DEVICE : "contains"
    DEVICE ||--|{ THERMOSTAT : "is a"
    DEVICE ||--|{ HUMIDISTAT : "is a"
    DEVICE ||--|{ SECURITY_SENSOR : "is a"
    DEVICE ||--|{ APPLIANCE_SWITCH : "is a"
    HOME ||--o{ SCHEDULE : "plans for"
    SCHEDULE ||--o{ SCHEDULE_ENTRY : "includes"
    DEVICE ||--o{ SCHEDULE_ENTRY : "scheduled for"
    DEVICE ||--o{ REPORT_ENTRY : "generates"

    USER {
        int id PK
        string username
        string password_hash
        string role
        datetime created_at
    }

    HOME {
        int id PK
        string name
        int user_id FK
        datetime created_at
    }

    DEVICE {
        int id PK
        int home_id FK
        string type
        string name
        string location
        string status
        datetime last_update
    }

    THERMOSTAT {
        int device_id PK, FK
        float current_temp
        float set_point_temp
    }

    HUMIDISTAT {
        int device_id PK, FK
        float current_humidity
        float set_point_humidity
    }

    SECURITY_SENSOR {
        int device_id PK, FK
        string sensor_type
        string current_state
    }

    APPLIANCE_SWITCH {
        int device_id PK, FK
        string current_state
    }

    SCHEDULE {
        int id PK
        int home_id FK
        int user_id FK
        int month
        int year
        datetime created_at
    }

    SCHEDULE_ENTRY {
        int id PK
        int schedule_id FK
        int device_id FK
        int day_of_week
        int hour
        string target_value
    }

    REPORT_ENTRY {
        int id PK
        int device_id FK
        datetime timestamp
        string value_read
        string event_type
    }
entityRelationshipDiagram.caption: home 1.3 Entity Relationship Diagram

introduction.purpose: The primary objective of this Software Requirements Specification (SRS) is to provide a comprehensive overview of the **ijis** initiative's Indigent Defense Services module. This document delineates the core business processes, use cases, and supplementary requirements essential for the effective administration of indigent defense within Tarrant County. It serves as a foundational reference for all stakeholders involved in the system's development and deployment, ensuring a shared understanding of its scope and functionality.
introduction.documentConventions: This document adheres to the guidelines set forth in IEEE Standard 830-1998, 'Recommended Practice for Software Requirements Specifications,' ensuring consistency and clarity in its presentation. All terminology, acronyms, and abbreviations used herein are either defined within the document's glossary or are universally understood within the domain of criminal justice and software engineering. Specific formatting conventions, such as bolding for system names and key technologies, are applied consistently to enhance readability.
introduction.intendedAudience: The intended audience for this SRS includes project sponsors, legal and technical stakeholders from Tarrant County, software development teams, quality assurance personnel, and system integrators. It provides a detailed blueprint for developers, a verification basis for testers, and a contractual reference for project management. Furthermore, it informs members of the Criminal Justice Community about the system's capabilities and operational impact.
introduction.productScope: The scope of the **ijis** Indigent Defense Services module is to define and implement enhanced business processes and technology solutions for managing appointed counsel requests and related judicial activities. This system will facilitate rapid information sharing, streamline attorney appointments, manage compensation, and generate compliance reports for indigent defense. While integrating with existing justice systems, it focuses specifically on the workflow from defendant arrest and indigency determination through attorney compensation and regulatory reporting within Tarrant County, Texas.
references: IEEE 830-1998 IEEE Recommended Practice for Software Requirements Specifications.
ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018 Systems and software engineering — Life cycle processes — Requirements engineering.

overallDescription.productPerspective: This module will interact with several external systems, acting as an essential hub for indigent defense data, and is not a standalone application. It will interface with the Court Case Management System for defendant and case information, and an external Accounting System for attorney compensation. The requirements detailed herein delineate the specific functionalities and interfaces necessary for the **ijis** Indigent Defense Services module to achieve its integration and operational objectives.
productFunctions: Manage the submission and processing of requests for appointed counsel.
Track and manage initial contacts and subsequent interactions between defense attorneys and defendants.
Manage notifications regarding case filing decisions from the District Attorney.
Administer the scheduling and notification of court settings for defense attorneys.
Handle the compensation process for appointed defense attorneys, including payment requests and approvals.
Maintain and update the official list of qualified defense attorneys for appointment rotations.
Generate comprehensive reports to ensure compliance with indigent defense standards and regulations.

userClassesAndCharacteristics.characteristics: Responsible for managing county business and financial information. This user class requires access to payment records and compensation approvals to ensure financial compliance and accurate disbursement of funds. They interact with the system to process approved attorney compensation claims and ensure fiscal integrity.

The governing body of the County, consisting of the County Judge and four Commissioners. This user class will review and approve payment claims for indigent defense services, holding ultimate fiscal authority. Their interaction is primarily for high-level oversight and final authorization of financial transactions within the system.

Manages the schedule of County or District Courts. This user class needs to enter and update court setting information, including results and hearing outcomes, into the system. They require an efficient interface for scheduling and managing judicial events related to indigent defense cases.

Presides over trial courts with misdemeanor criminal jurisdiction. Judges review and approve attorney applications for appointment lists, authorize payment requests, and rule on advance payment of expenses. They require secure access to attorney qualifications and payment records for decision-making.

Individuals arrested and/or charged with a criminal offense. Defendants interact with the system indirectly through magistrates and defense attorneys, primarily concerning their indigency status, counsel appointments, and case notifications. Their personal and financial data are central to the system's core functions.

Qualified and appointed to represent indigent defendants. This user class submits applications for appointment lists, logs defendant contacts, and submits requests for payment. They require timely notifications of appointments, case filings, and court settings, as well as a portal for managing their case-related activities.

Responsible for processing defendants into custody. This user class captures initial defendant, incident, and financial information, schedules indigency hearings, and witnesses financial affidavits. They are crucial for initiating the request for appointed counsel process promptly after an arrest.

Represents the State of Texas in adversarial judicial proceedings. This user class provides notice of case filing decisions and charge details to the system. They require mechanisms for communicating prosecution decisions and receiving setting notifications, contributing to the overall case lifecycle management.

Presides over trial courts with felony criminal jurisdiction. Similar to County Court Judges, they approve attorney applications, authorize payments, and play a critical role in capital felony case attorney selections. Their elevated jurisdiction requires specific system functionalities for complex legal scenarios.

Law Enforcement Agencies investigating and presenting charges to the District Attorney. While they don't directly use the **ijis** system, their actions (dropping charges) can trigger notifications within the system related to attorney compensation. They are an external entity whose actions impact system workflows.

Appointed by the Presiding Judge to adopt standards for capital case attorneys. This committee's role is primarily advisory and standard-setting, impacting the qualifications and selection process for attorneys handling death penalty cases. The system will incorporate their established qualification criteria.

Responsible for informing defendants of their right to counsel and determining indigent status. Magistrates review financial affidavits, interview defendants, and submit requests for appointed counsel or waivers to the Office of Attorney Appointments. They are critical entry points for defendants seeking indigent defense.

Manages the indigent defense appointment process. This user class is the primary administrative user, responsible for processing requests, managing attorney lists, notifying parties, and generating reports. They require comprehensive system access to oversee and execute all core functions of the indigent defense program.

Appointed by the Governor to preside over the administrative judicial region. This judge acts as an appellate authority for attorney compensation disputes, reviewing and determining appropriate payment amounts. They require specialized access to review contested payment claims and issue binding orders.

A standing committee responsible for establishing standards for Indigent Defense Services. This external entity receives comprehensive reports from the system to monitor compliance and evaluate the effectiveness of indigent defense services. The system's reporting capabilities are designed to meet their information requirements.

overallDescription.operatingEnvironment: Database operations will be critical for storing sensitive legal and financial data, requiring a highly available and secure database solution. The system will interact with various external applications, including the **Court Case Management System**, **District Attorney Electronic Case Filing System**, and **external Accounting System(s)**. Compatibility and secure communication protocols with these external systems are paramount to ensure seamless operation and data exchange within the broader justice community.
designAndImplementationConstraints: The **ijis** system must strictly adhere to the Texas Code of Criminal Procedure (CCP), including Article 1.051 (Right to representation by Counsel), Article 15.17 (Duties of arresting officer and magistrate), Article 26.04 (Procedures for Appointing Counsel), Article 26.05 (Compensation of Counsel Appointed to Defend), and Article 26.052 (Appointment of counsel in death penalty case; reimbursement of investigative expenses).

Compliance with the Texas Uniform Commercial Code, specifically Chapter 43 Uniform Electronic Transactions Act (UETA), is mandatory for all electronic transactions and signed records within the system. The system must also align with Texas Government Code Section 71.0351, related to Indigent Defense Information, and Chapter 203 of the Texas Local Government Code, concerning the management and preservation of records.

Technical implementation must conform to the Texas Department of Information Resources Guidelines for the Management of Electronic Transactions and Signed Records. Additionally, the system must support data exchange utilizing the United States Department of Justice – Office Justice Programs' Global Justice XML Data Model (GJXDM) for interoperability with other justice systems.

The system must integrate seamlessly with the existing **Court Case Management System** for defendant and case information, the **District Attorney Electronic Case Filing System (ECFS)** for charge details, and an **external Accounting System(s)**, such as **SAP**, for managing county financial information and attorney compensation. These integrations are critical and will define data exchange formats and protocols.

All development activities and system documentation must conform to the standards outlined in the Tarrant County Indigent Defense Plan and its supplements dated December 27, 2001, June 5, 2003, and June 25, 2003. These plans provide specific procedural and operational guidelines that the **ijis** system must encapsulate and enforce.
userDocumentation: A comprehensive User Manual providing step-by-step instructions for all system functionalities, tailored to the specific roles of the Office of Attorney Appointments staff, Magistrates, and Defense Attorneys.
An Online Help System integrated directly into the user interface, offering contextual assistance and searchable documentation for immediate support.
An Administration Guide detailing system setup, configuration, user management, and troubleshooting procedures for IT support personnel.
Training materials and tutorials, including quick-start guides and video demonstrations, to facilitate efficient onboarding for new users of the **ijis** system.
assumptionsAndDependencies: It is assumed that the external **Court Case Management System**, **District Attorney Electronic Case Filing System**, and the **external Accounting System(s)** will be fully operational and accessible via defined interfaces throughout the lifecycle of the **ijis** project. Any changes or unavailability of these systems could significantly impact integration efforts and system functionality.

The legal frameworks, including the Texas Code of Criminal Procedure and relevant Texas Government Codes, are assumed to remain stable or any updates will be communicated in a timely manner to allow for system adaptation. Continued adherence to the Tarrant County Indigent Defense Plan and its supplements is also a core assumption.

The availability of qualified technical personnel within Tarrant County and the development team is assumed for the successful design, development, deployment, and ongoing maintenance of the **ijis** system. Adequate training and resources for end-users are also assumed to ensure effective adoption and utilization of the new system.

It is dependent on the cooperation and active participation of all stakeholders from the Criminal Justice Community in providing feedback and validating requirements. The accuracy and completeness of initial data migration from existing systems, if applicable, are crucial for the integrity of the new **ijis** system.

externalInterfaceRequirements.userInterfaces: Each screen will incorporate standard buttons for actions such as 'Save', 'Submit', 'Cancel', and 'Help', consistently positioned for ease of use. Error messages will be displayed prominently and clearly, guiding users to correct invalid inputs or understand system states. The user interface design will prioritize accessibility and efficiency, minimizing clicks and data entry fields while providing comprehensive information presentation for complex legal data.
externalInterfaceRequirements.hardwareInterfaces: The **ijis** system is primarily software-based and does not require specialized hardware interfaces beyond standard server infrastructure, network equipment, and end-user workstations (desktops, laptops). Input will be primarily via keyboards and mice, with output to standard monitors and printers. No direct interaction with custom peripheral hardware, such as biometric scanners or specialized legal recording devices, is anticipated for the core functionalities of the indigent defense module.
externalInterfaceRequirements.softwareInterfaces: Communication with the **external Accounting System(s)**, such as **SAP**, will be established for the processing and remittance of attorney compensation payments. This interface will involve secure **batch data transfers** or **API** calls for payment request submissions and status updates. The system must also be capable of exchanging data in the **Global Justice XML Data Model (GJXDM)** format to ensure interoperability with other justice programs as mandated by the United States Department of Justice.
externalInterfaceRequirements.communicationsInterfaces: Secure network communication protocols, specifically **HTTPS/TLS**, will be employed for all **Web**-based user interactions and inter-system data exchanges to protect sensitive legal and personal information. Data transfer rates will be optimized to support timely notifications and report generation, ensuring that critical information is disseminated within required legal timeframes. Synchronization mechanisms will be implemented for external system integrations to maintain data consistency across interconnected platforms.

systemFeatures.name: Submit Request for Appointed Counsel
systemFeatures.description: This feature enables law enforcement agencies and magistrates to efficiently capture defendant, incident, and financial information and submit requests for appointed counsel. It significantly improves the timeliness and accuracy of initiating indigent defense services, ensuring defendants' rights are upheld within strict legal deadlines. Technically, it centralizes data capture, reducing manual errors and providing a structured input for the appointment process. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Detention Facility Officer captures Defendant, Incident, and Financial Information. Response: The system records the information and generates a Financial Affidavit.
Stimulus: Defendant swears to and signs Financial Affidavit. Response: The system records the affidavit and captures the signature.
Stimulus: Detention Facility Officer witnesses and signs Financial Affidavit. Response: The system records the officer's attestation.
Stimulus: Detention Facility Officer schedules Indigency Hearing. Response: The system records the scheduled hearing details.
Stimulus: Magistrate informs Defendant of right to request counsel. Response: The system presents options for requesting counsel.
Stimulus: Defendant requests appointment of a Court Appointed Attorney. Response: The system initiates the Request for Appointed Counsel process.
Stimulus: Magistrate reviews Financial Affidavit. Response: The system displays the Defendant's financial information.
Stimulus: Magistrate interviews/questions Defendant. Response: The system allows the Magistrate to record interview notes.
Stimulus: Magistrate makes determination that Defendant is indigent. Response: The system updates the Defendant's indigency status.
Stimulus: Defendant and Magistrate sign Request for Appointed Counsel. Response: The system captures the necessary signatures for the request.
Stimulus: Magistrate submits Request for Appointed Counsel to the Office of Attorney Appointments. Response: The system routes the request for processing.
Stimulus: Defendant waives right to Court Appointed Attorney. Response: The system initiates the Waiver of Appointed Counsel process.
Stimulus: Defendant signs Waiver of Appointed Counsel. Response: The system captures the Defendant's waiver signature.
Stimulus: Magistrate submits Waiver of Appointed Counsel to the Office of Attorney Appointment. Response: The system records and routes the waiver.
Stimulus: Magistrate makes determination that Defendant is not indigent. Response: The system updates the Defendant's indigency status accordingly.
Stimulus: Magistrate submits notice of indigency determination to the Office of Attorney Appointments. Response: The system records and routes the notice.
functionalRequirements: The system shall enable Detention Facility Officers to capture defendant, incident, and financial information within 48 hours of arrest.
The system shall generate a digital Financial Affidavit for defendant signature.
The system shall record the witnessing and signing of the Financial Affidavit by a Detention Facility Officer.
The system shall allow Detention Facility Officers to schedule an Indigency Hearing.
The system shall provide Magistrates with access to defendant financial affidavits for review.
The system shall enable Magistrates to record their determination of a defendant's indigency status.
The system shall capture digital signatures from both the Defendant and Magistrate on the Request for Appointed Counsel.
The system shall transmit the Request for Appointed Counsel to the Office of Attorney Appointments upon submission by a Magistrate.
The system shall capture digital signatures for the Waiver of Appointed Counsel.
The system shall notify the Office of Attorney Appointments if a defendant waives their right to counsel.
The system shall notify the Office of Attorney Appointments if a defendant is determined not to be indigent.

systemFeatures.name: Process Request for Appointed Counsel
systemFeatures.description: This feature streamlines the critical process of appointing legal counsel to indigent defendants, ensuring timely and compliant attorney assignments. By automating research and selection, it enhances administrative efficiency for the Office of Attorney Appointments and accelerates the provision of legal representation. Technically, it integrates with the Court Case Management System for data validation and implements a rules-based attorney selection algorithm, reducing manual workload and error. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Magistrate submits a Request for Appointed Counsel. Response: The system receives the request and flags it for review by the Office of Attorney Appointments.
Stimulus: Office of Attorney Appointments reviews the Request for Appointed Counsel. Response: The system displays the request details and pending status.

Stimulus: Office of Attorney Appointments researches Defendant/Incident information in Court Case Management System. Response: The system queries the Court Case Management System and displays relevant data.
Stimulus: Office of Attorney Appointments confirms Defendant has not been released from custody. Response: The system verifies custody status, denying if released.

Stimulus: Office of Attorney Appointments confirms Defendant does not have a Defense Attorney appointed on a pending case. Response: The system checks for existing appointments, considering denial or special handling.
Stimulus: Office of Attorney Appointments determines the appropriate appointment rotation list. Response: The system applies offense-based criteria to identify the correct attorney list.
Stimulus: Office of Attorney Appointments selects next Defense Attorney in the appointment rotation list. Response: The system assigns the attorney and updates the rotation list.

Stimulus: Office of Attorney Appointments notifies Defendant, Defense Attorney, and Detention Facility Officer of appointment. Response: The system sends notifications via facsimile and/or electronic mail.
Stimulus: Defendant has been released from custody. Response: The system automatically denies the request for appointed counsel.
Stimulus: Office of Attorney Appointments records denial due to release from custody. Response: The system logs the denial reason.
Stimulus: Previously appointed Defense Attorney is on appropriate list. Response: The system selects the existing attorney for the new appointment.
Stimulus: Previously appointed Defense Attorney is not on appropriate list. Response: The system notifies the original attorney of new charges and removes them, then appoints a new attorney.
Stimulus: Charge is capital felony case with death penalty sought. Response: The system routes the Request for Counsel to the presiding District Judge.
Stimulus: District Court Judge selects two qualified attorneys. Response: The system records the judge's selection.
Stimulus: Office of Attorney Appointments appoints selected Defense Attorney(s). Response: The system finalizes the appointment of selected capital case attorneys.
functionalRequirements: The system shall receive Request for Appointed Counsel submissions from Magistrates.
The system shall enable the Office of Attorney Appointments to review requests and associated defendant/incident data.
The system shall integrate with the **Court Case Management System** to research defendant and incident information.
The system shall verify the defendant's custody status and automatically deny requests if the defendant has been released.
The system shall check for existing defense attorney appointments on pending cases for the defendant.
The system shall determine the appropriate appointment rotation list based on the highest level and degree of offense.
The system shall select the next available Defense Attorney from the determined rotation list.
The system shall notify the Defendant, Defense Attorney, and Detention Facility Officer of an appointment via facsimile and/or electronic mail within 24 hours of appointment.
The system shall record the denial reason if a defendant has been released from custody.
The system shall allow for the selection of a previously appointed Defense Attorney if qualified for a new charge.
The system shall notify a previously appointed Defense Attorney of new charges and remove them if not qualified for the new appointment.
The system shall route capital felony case requests to the presiding District Judge for attorney selection.
The system shall enable District Court Judges to select two qualified attorneys for capital felony cases, considering Local Selection Committee qualifications.
The system shall finalize the appointment of judge-selected attorneys for capital felony cases.

systemFeatures.name: Manage Defendant Contact
systemFeatures.description: This feature facilitates the tracking and reporting of defense attorney contact with their assigned defendants, ensuring compliance with legal requirements for timely client interaction. It provides accountability for attorneys and enables the Office of Attorney Appointments to monitor service delivery, enhancing the quality of indigent defense. Technically, it offers a dedicated interface for attorneys to log contact details and triggers alerts for non-compliance, supporting proactive oversight. Priority: Medium.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Defense Attorney is appointed to represent Defendant. Response: The system initiates a contact logging requirement for the attorney.
Stimulus: Defense Attorney contacts Defendant within one working day of appointment. Response: The system records the date and time of the initial contact.
Stimulus: Defense Attorney notifies Office of Attorney Appointments of date and time of initial contact. Response: The system updates the appointment record with contact details.
Stimulus: Defense Attorney interviews Defendant in person. Response: The system records the date and time of the in-person visit.
Stimulus: Defense Attorney notifies Office of Attorney Appointments of date and time of in person visit(s). Response: The system updates the appointment record with in-person visit details.
Stimulus: Defense Attorney fails to contact Defendant within one working day. Response: The system flags the appointment as non-compliant and notifies the Office of Attorney Appointments.
Stimulus: Defense Attorney fails to interview Defendant in person as appropriate. Response: The system flags the appointment as non-compliant and notifies the Office of Attorney Appointments.
functionalRequirements: The system shall enable Defense Attorneys to record the date and time of initial contact with a defendant within one working day of appointment.
The system shall enable Defense Attorneys to record the date and time of in-person visits with a defendant.
The system shall notify the Office of Attorney Appointments if a Defense Attorney fails to make initial contact with a defendant by the end of the first working day after appointment.
The system shall track repeated contacts and interviews until case disposition or attorney relief.
The system shall update appointment records with all recorded defendant contact details.

systemFeatures.name: Manage Notice of Case Filing Decision
systemFeatures.description: This feature ensures that appointed defense attorneys and the Office of Attorney Appointments are promptly informed of the District Attorney's case filing decisions, including whether charges were accepted, modified, or rejected. This critical information allows attorneys to prepare appropriately and the OAA to maintain accurate case records. Technically, it integrates with the District Attorney's Electronic Case Filing System to receive updates and automate notifications. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: District Attorney makes the decision to proceed with prosecution. Response: The system awaits notification from the District Attorney's office.
Stimulus: District Attorney notifies Office of Attorney Appointments of accepted charge(s). Response: The system receives the charge details.

Stimulus: Office of Attorney Appointments enters Defense Attorney appointment into Court Case Management System. Response: The system integrates and updates the Court Case Management System with appointment details.
Stimulus: Office of Attorney Appointments notifies Defense Attorney that charge(s) have been filed. Response: The system sends an email or facsimile notification to the attorney.
Stimulus: District Attorney creates additional charge. Response: The system processes this as a new charge, potentially requiring re-evaluation of attorney qualifications.
Stimulus: District Attorney modifies original charge. Response: The system updates the charge details, potentially requiring re-evaluation of attorney qualifications.
Stimulus: District Attorney rejects original charge. Response: The system records the rejection.
Stimulus: Office of Attorney Appointments notifies Defense Attorney of rejected charges. Response: The system sends an email or facsimile notification to the attorney.
functionalRequirements: The system shall receive notifications from the District Attorney regarding accepted charges for prosecution.
The system shall integrate with the **Court Case Management System** to record Defense Attorney appointments and charge details.
The system shall notify the appointed Defense Attorney of newly filed charges via electronic communication.
The system shall process additional or modified charges from the District Attorney, potentially triggering reassessment of attorney qualifications.
The system shall record instances where the District Attorney rejects original charges.
The system shall notify the Defense Attorney when charges against their client are rejected by the District Attorney.

systemFeatures.name: Manage Defense Attorney Setting Notification
systemFeatures.description: This feature manages the creation and updating of court settings for indigent defense cases, encompassing initial settings, calendared cases, and the recording of hearing outcomes. It is vital for ensuring all parties are informed of court dates and for maintaining comprehensive records of case progression. Technically, it integrates with the Court Case Management System for setting information and automates notifications, improving operational transparency. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: District Attorney has accepted and filed charges against Defendant. Response: The system flags the case for potential court settings.
Stimulus: Court Coordinator determines that a setting is required. Response: The system awaits input for a new court setting.
Stimulus: Court Coordinator enters setting information into Court Case Management System. Response: The system receives setting details from the Court Case Management System.
Stimulus: Office of Attorney Appointments notifies Defendant, Defense Attorney, and District Attorney of setting. Response: The system sends automated notifications via electronic means.
Stimulus: Defendant, Defense Attorney, and District Attorney appear before Judge. Response: The system notes the occurrence of the setting.
Stimulus: Court Coordinator enters setting result into Court Case Management System. Response: The system receives and records the outcome of the hearing.
Stimulus: Defendant is in custody (Alternative Path 1). Response: The system handles notification of setting to a defendant in custody.
Stimulus: Defendant fails to appear (Alternative Path 2). Response: The system records the non-appearance and triggers appropriate alerts or follow-up actions.
Stimulus: Defense Attorney fails to appear (Alternative Path 3). Response: The system records the non-appearance and triggers appropriate alerts or follow-up actions.
functionalRequirements: The system shall receive court setting information from the **Court Case Management System** initiated by a Court Coordinator.
The system shall notify the Defendant, Defense Attorney, and District Attorney of court settings via electronic means.
The system shall receive and record the results and outcomes of court hearings from the **Court Case Management System**.
The system shall track repeated court settings until case disposition, acquittal, or attorney relief.
The system shall capture and flag instances of defendant or defense attorney failure to appear for court settings.

systemFeatures.name: Manage Defense Attorney Compensation
systemFeatures.description: This feature manages the entire lifecycle of defense attorney compensation, from initial payment requests to final approvals and remittances, including handling appeals. It ensures that attorneys are compensated fairly and in compliance with legal stipulations, while providing the county with clear audit trails for financial management. Technically, it integrates with an external accounting system and supports a multi-level approval workflow, reducing payment processing delays. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Defense Attorney submits a request for payment itemizing services performed. Response: The system receives the request and assigns a unique identifier.
Stimulus: Office of Attorney Appointments forwards request for payment to Judge. Response: The system routes the request through an internal workflow to the assigned Judge.
Stimulus: Judge reviews/approves request for payment and forwards to Office of Attorney Appointments. Response: The system records the Judge's decision and updates the request status.
Stimulus: Office of Attorney Appointments forwards order to remit payment to Auditor. Response: The system transmits the approved payment order to the Auditor.
Stimulus: Auditor presents claim to Commissioners Court. Response: The system makes the claim available for review by the Commissioners Court.
Stimulus: Commissioners Court approves payment of claim. Response: The system records the final approval.
Stimulus: Auditor remits payment to Defense Attorney. Response: The system triggers payment processing through an external accounting system.
Stimulus: Auditor notifies Judge that payment has been remitted. Response: The system sends an automated notification to the Judge.
Stimulus: Judge disapproves amount of payment without Appeal. Response: The system records the disapproval and reason, allowing the Judge to make a written finding of the approved amount.
Stimulus: Judge disapproves amount of payment with Appeal. Response: The system records the disapproval and reason, initiating an appeal process.
Stimulus: Defense Attorney files motion with Presiding Administrative Judge. Response: The system receives and logs the appeal motion.
Stimulus: Presiding Administrative Judge reviews disapproval of payment. Response: The system provides relevant payment history and justifications to the judge.
Stimulus: Presiding Administrative Judge determines the appropriate amount of payment. Response: The system records the final decision on the payment amount.
Stimulus: Presiding Administrative Judge orders Auditor to remit payment to Defense Attorney. Response: The system generates a directive for the Auditor.
Stimulus: Defense Attorney files request for advance payment of expenses. Response: The system receives the ex parte request.
Stimulus: Judge denies request (in whole or part) for advance payment. Response: The system records the denial and reasoning.
Stimulus: Filing Agency drops charges against Defendant. Response: The system is notified, potentially impacting attorney compensation processing.
Stimulus: Charges against Defendant are rejected by District Attorney. Response: The system is notified, potentially impacting attorney compensation processing.
functionalRequirements: The system shall enable Defense Attorneys to submit itemized requests for payment of services performed.
The system shall route payment requests from the Office of Attorney Appointments to the appropriate Judge for review and approval.
The system shall record judicial approval or disapproval of payment requests, including reasons for modifications.
The system shall transmit approved payment orders from the Office of Attorney Appointments to the Auditor.
The system shall facilitate the presentation of claims by the Auditor to the Commissioners Court for final approval.
The system shall integrate with an **external Accounting System(s)** for remittance of approved payments to Defense Attorneys.
The system shall notify the Judge of payment remittance by the Auditor.
The system shall support a judicial finding for approving payment amounts different from the requested amount.
The system shall manage an appeal process for disapproved payment amounts, routing motions to the Presiding Administrative Judge.
The system shall enable the Presiding Administrative Judge to review appeals and determine appropriate payment amounts.
The system shall process requests for advance payment of expenses (ex parte) and record judicial decisions.
The system shall be capable of adjusting or halting compensation processes upon notification of charges being dropped by the Filing Agency.
The system shall be capable of adjusting or halting compensation processes upon notification of charges being rejected by the District Attorney.

systemFeatures.name: Manage Attorney List
systemFeatures.description: This feature maintains and updates the official appointment rotation list for defense attorneys, ensuring that only qualified individuals are assigned to indigent defense cases. It provides a structured process for attorney application, review, approval, and management of their status on the list. Technically, it centralizes attorney qualification data and automates list management, upholding the integrity and fairness of the appointment process. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Defense Attorney requests to be placed on appointment rotation list. Response: The system provides an application interface.
Stimulus: Defense Attorney submits application for public appointments. Response: The system receives and registers the application.
Stimulus: Office of Attorney Appointments reviews application. Response: The system displays application details and qualification criteria.
Stimulus: Office of Attorney Appointments forwards application to Judges. Response: The system routes the application for judicial review.
Stimulus: Judges review/approve application. Response: The system records the judicial decision.
Stimulus: Judges forward application to Office of Attorney Appointments. Response: The system returns the application with the judicial decision.
Stimulus: Office of Attorney Appointments adds Defense Attorney to appropriate appointment rotation list(s). Response: The system updates the attorney's status and adds them to the list.
Stimulus: Office of Attorney Appointments notifies Defense Attorney of addition to list. Response: The system sends an automated confirmation.
Stimulus: Defense Attorney fails to meet minimum qualifications. Response: The system records the qualification issues.
Stimulus: Office of Attorney Appointments notifies Defense Attorney of qualification issues. Response: The system sends a notification detailing the deficiencies.
Stimulus: Judges do not approve application. Response: The system records the disapproval.
Stimulus: Office of Attorney Appointments notifies Defense Attorney of disapproval. Response: The system sends a notification of the judicial decision.
Stimulus: Judges order permanent removal of Defense Attorney. Response: The system initiates the removal process.
Stimulus: Office of Attorney Appointments removes Defense Attorney from appointment rotation list(s). Response: The system deactivates the attorney's eligibility for appointments.
Stimulus: Office of Attorney Appointments notifies Defense Attorney of permanent removal. Response: The system sends a formal notification.
Stimulus: Judges order reinstatement of Defense Attorney. Response: The system initiates the reinstatement process.
Stimulus: Office of Attorney Appointments adds Defense Attorney to appointment rotation list(s). Response: The system reactivates the attorney's eligibility.
Stimulus: Office of Attorney Appointments notifies Defense Attorney of reinstatement. Response: The system sends a formal notification.
Stimulus: Defense Attorney requests temporary removal from appointment rotation list(s). Response: The system receives the request for temporary suspension.
Stimulus: Office of Attorney Appointments suspends appointments for 30 days. Response: The system updates the attorney's status to temporarily inactive.
Stimulus: Office of Attorney Appointments notifies Defense Attorney of temporary removal. Response: The system sends a notification of the suspension.
functionalRequirements: The system shall provide an application interface for Defense Attorneys to request placement on appointment rotation lists.
The system shall enable the Office of Attorney Appointments to review attorney applications against qualification criteria.
The system shall facilitate judicial review and approval of attorney applications.
The system shall add approved Defense Attorneys to the appropriate appointment rotation lists.
The system shall notify Defense Attorneys of their acceptance or rejection from the appointment lists.
The system shall record and track qualification issues for attorneys not meeting minimum standards.
The system shall allow for the permanent removal of Defense Attorneys from appointment lists based on judicial orders.
The system shall support the reinstatement of Defense Attorneys to appointment lists based on judicial orders.
The system shall allow Defense Attorneys to request temporary removal from appointment lists.
The system shall suspend appointments for Defense Attorneys for a specified period (e.g., 30 days) upon request or judicial order.
The system shall notify Defense Attorneys of any changes to their status on the appointment rotation lists.

systemFeatures.name: Manage Reporting
systemFeatures.description: This feature provides the Office of Attorney Appointments with comprehensive reporting capabilities to monitor compliance with the Texas Fair Defense Act and other regulatory requirements. It allows for detailed analysis of appointment metrics, denials, processing times, and financial expenditures, supporting transparency and accountability. Technically, it queries various data points within the **ijis** system and external integrations to generate customizable reports. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Task Force on Indigent Defense requires a report. Response: The system is prompted to generate a report.
Stimulus: Office of Attorney Appointments selects the report type. Response: The system displays available report templates.
Stimulus: Office of Attorney Appointments enters/selects report parameters (e.g., date range, offense type). Response: The system applies the parameters to the data query.
Stimulus: Office of Attorney Appointments distributes report to Task Force on Indigent Defense. Response: The system generates and provides the report for distribution.
functionalRequirements: The system shall enable the Office of Attorney Appointments to select various report types based on indigent defense metrics.
The system shall allow users to specify report parameters, including date ranges (day, week, month, quarter, annual, ad hoc) and appointment/offense types.
The system shall generate reports on the number of appointments made by date range and type.
The system shall generate reports on the number of denials of counsel by date range and type of denial.
The system shall calculate and report minimum, maximum, and average hours between arrest and magistration by date range.
The system shall calculate and report minimum, maximum, and average hours between magistration and request for counsel by date range.
The system shall calculate and report minimum, maximum, and average hours between request for appointment and actual appointment/denial by date range.
The system shall calculate and report minimum, maximum, and average hours between appointment of counsel and Defense Attorney first contact by date range.
The system shall calculate and report minimum, maximum, and average days between appointment and case filing by date range.
The system shall calculate and report minimum, maximum, and average days between case filing and disposition by date range.
The system shall calculate and report minimum, maximum, and average days between appointment and disposition by date range.
The system shall generate reports on minimum, maximum, and average total hours billed by Defense Attorney and Offense Type by date range.
The system shall generate reports on minimum, maximum, and average number of court appearances by Defense Attorney and Offense Type by date range.
The system shall generate reports on minimum, maximum, and average total payments made to Defense Attorney by Offense Type by date range.
The system shall generate reports on minimum, maximum, and average cost of reimbursement types (e.g., Court Appearances, Investigation) by Offense Type and date range.
The system shall generate reports on minimum, maximum, and average number of appointments for Defendants with special needs by date range.
The system shall generate reports on minimum, maximum, and average number of duplicate appointments and associated payments by date range.
The system shall generate reports on minimum, maximum, and average number of non-disposed cases/charges and associated payments by date range.
The system shall generate reports on minimum, maximum, and average number of pleas, trials, and dispositions by Defense Attorney, Disposition Type, and Offense Type by date range.
The system shall comply with Texas Government Code Section 71.0351 requirements for Indigent Defense Information reporting.

performanceRequirements: I-REQ-001: The **ijis** system shall process initial defendant and incident information capture within 48 hours of arrest to support timely indigency hearings. This ensures compliance with legal mandates and prevents undue delays in the initial stages of legal representation.

I-REQ-002: The **ijis** system shall enable magistrates to electronically review and submit requests for counsel within 24 hours of an indigency hearing. This accelerates the formal request process, directly impacting the speed at which indigent defendants receive legal counsel.

I-REQ-003: The **ijis** system shall process requests for appointed counsel as soon as possible, but not later than the end of the first working day after receiving the request. This stringent requirement minimizes the duration defendants spend without legal representation, aligning with justice system efficiency goals.

I-REQ-004: The **ijis** system shall generate all standard compliance reports within 5 minutes for any specified date range up to one year. This ensures that reporting to the Task Force on Indigent Defense and other stakeholders is timely, supporting rapid analysis and decision-making.
safetyRequirements: I-REQ-005: The **ijis** system shall implement robust data validation mechanisms to prevent the entry of incomplete or inconsistent legal and financial information. This minimizes the risk of errors that could lead to incorrect attorney appointments or compensation issues, directly impacting the fairness and accuracy of justice administration.

I-REQ-006: The **ijis** system shall maintain a comprehensive audit trail of all actions performed by users, including data creation, modification, and deletion, timestamped and linked to the user. This ensures accountability and provides an immutable record for legal scrutiny or system recovery, safeguarding against malicious activity or accidental data loss.
securityRequirements: I-REQ-007: The **ijis** system shall enforce role-based access control, ensuring that users can only access functionalities and data relevant to their specific roles (e.g., Magistrate, Office of Attorney Appointments, Defense Attorney). This protects sensitive defendant information and restricts administrative functions to authorized personnel, preventing unauthorized access and data breaches.

I-REQ-008: The **ijis** system shall encrypt all sensitive data at rest (e.g., defendant financial information, case details) and in transit (e.g., during inter-system communication using **HTTPS/TLS**). This ensures the confidentiality and integrity of personal and legal data, complying with privacy regulations and preventing eavesdropping.

I-REQ-009: The **ijis** system shall implement strong user authentication mechanisms, including complex password policies and account lockout after multiple failed attempts. This prevents unauthorized users from gaining access to the system, protecting the integrity of the justice information managed by **ijis**.

I-REQ-010: The **ijis** system shall comply with all relevant security guidelines from the Texas Department of Information Resources, especially those concerning electronic transactions and signed records. This ensures that all digital processes and records meet governmental security standards.
softwareQualityAttributes: I-REQ-011: Reliability: The **ijis** system shall operate with 99.9% uptime during core business hours (7:00 AM - 6:00 PM CST, Monday to Friday). This ensures continuous availability of critical indigent defense services, minimizing disruptions to court proceedings and attorney appointments.

I-REQ-012: Usability: The **ijis** system's user interfaces shall be intuitive and require minimal training (less than 4 hours for basic functions) for users familiar with justice system operations. This reduces onboarding time and increases user adoption efficiency, particularly for intermittent users like Magistrates and Defense Attorneys.

I-REQ-013: Maintainability: The **ijis** system's codebase shall be well-documented and follow established coding standards to allow new developers to understand and modify the system with reasonable effort. This ensures the system can adapt to evolving legal requirements and integrate new features without significant redevelopment costs.

I-REQ-014: Interoperability: The **ijis** system shall seamlessly exchange data with the **Court Case Management System**, **District Attorney Electronic Case Filing System**, and **external Accounting System(s)** using defined **API**s and **GJXDM** standards. This facilitates an integrated justice information flow, eliminating data silos and redundant data entry.

I-REQ-015: Auditability: The **ijis** system shall provide comprehensive logging and reporting features that allow administrators to trace all system events, including user actions, data changes, and system integrations, for a period of at least 7 years. This is crucial for legal compliance, financial audits, and investigation of discrepancies.

I-REQ-016: Testability: The **ijis** system shall be designed with clear separation of concerns, modular components, and accessible interfaces to facilitate automated and manual testing of all functional and non-functional requirements. This ensures the quality and correctness of the software prior to deployment and throughout its lifecycle.
businessRules: I-REQ-017: All appointments of counsel must comply with Texas Code of Criminal Procedure (CCP) Article 26.04, detailing procedures for appointing counsel.
I-REQ-018: Compensation for appointed counsel shall strictly adhere to CCP Article 26.05, governing the compensation of counsel appointed to defend.
I-REQ-019: In death penalty cases, the appointment of counsel and reimbursement of investigative expenses must follow the specific guidelines outlined in CCP Article 26.052.
I-REQ-020: The system must uphold the right to representation by counsel as defined in CCP Article 1.051 for all eligible defendants.
I-REQ-021: The duties of arresting officers and magistrates regarding defendant rights and indigency hearings, as per CCP Article 15.17, must be supported and documented by the system.
I-REQ-022: All electronic transactions and signed records within the **ijis** system must comply with Chapter 43 of the Texas Uniform Commercial Code, the Uniform Electronic Transactions Act (UETA).
I-REQ-023: The management and preservation of records within the **ijis** system must adhere to Chapter 203 of the Texas Local Government Code.

I-REQ-024: The system's operation and reporting must align with the standards and requirements established by the Task Force on Indigent Defense Plans, including the Tarrant County Indigent Defense Plan and its supplements.
I-REQ-025: The system shall provide indigent defense information in accordance with Texas Government Code Section 71.0351.

otherRequirements: I-REQ-026: The system shall provide an intuitive search and filtering capability across all defendant, attorney, appointment, and case records to facilitate quick retrieval of information.
I-REQ-027: The system shall support customizable notification templates for emails and facsimiles, allowing for flexibility in messaging while maintaining a consistent tone.
I-REQ-028: The system shall be capable of generating data extracts in various common formats (e.g., CSV, XML, PDF) for ad-hoc analysis and external use.
I-REQ-029: All legal documents generated or managed by the system shall be viewable, printable, and exportable in a standard, unalterable format (e.g., PDF).

flowchartDiagram.syntaxExplanation: A flowchart visually represents the sequential steps and decision points within a process, using nodes for activities and diamonds for decisions. Arrows indicate the flow of control, and direction (`TD` for top-down or `LR` for left-right) is specified. Node IDs are alphanumeric and unique, and all labels with spaces or symbols must be enclosed in double-quotes.
flowchartDiagram.code:
flowchart TD
  StartProcess["Start Indigent Defense Process"] --> RequestAppt("Submit Request for Appointed Counsel")
  RequestAppt --> IndigencyHearing["Conduct Indigency Hearing"]
  IndigencyHearing --> MagistrateDecision{"Defendant Indigent?"}
  MagistrateDecision -- No --> DenyRequest("Deny Request: Not Indigent")
  MagistrateDecision -- Yes --> ApptProcess("Process Request for Appointed Counsel")
  
  ApptProcess --> CheckCustody{"Defendant in Custody?"}
  CheckCustody -- No --> DenyRequestCustody("Deny Request: Not in Custody")
  CheckCustody -- Yes --> CheckPrevAppt{"Prev. Attorney for Case?"}
  CheckPrevAppt -- Yes --> AttorneySelectionA("Select Prev. Attorney")
  CheckPrevAppt -- No --> DetermineApptList("Determine Appt. Rotation List")
  DetermineApptList --> AttorneySelectionB("Select Next Attorney")
  
  AttorneySelectionA --> NotifyParties("Notify Parties of Appointment")
  AttorneySelectionB --> NotifyParties
  
  NotifyParties --> ContactDefendant("Manage Defendant Contact")
  ContactDefendant --> ContactMade{"First Contact Made?"}
  ContactMade -- No --> EscalateContact("Escalate: No Contact")
  ContactMade -- Yes --> CaseFiling("Manage Notice of Case Filing Decision")
  
  CaseFiling --> CaseAccepted{"Charges Filed?"}
  CaseAccepted -- No --> NotifyAttorneyChargesDropped("Notify Attorney: Charges Dropped")
  CaseAccepted -- Yes --> CourtSetting("Manage Defense Attorney Setting")
  
  CourtSetting --> SettingDone{"Setting Concluded?"}
  SettingDone -- No --> CourtSetting
  SettingDone -- Yes --> PaymentProcess("Manage Defense Attorney Compensation")
  
  PaymentProcess --> PaymentApproved{"Payment Approved?"}
  PaymentApproved -- No --> HandleAppeal("Handle Payment Appeal")
  HandleAppeal --> PaymentApproved
  PaymentApproved -- Yes --> RemitPayment("Remit Payment to Attorney")
  RemitPayment --> EndProcess["End Indigent Defense Process"]
flowchartDiagram.caption: **ijis** Indigent Defense Process Flow

sequenceDiagram.syntaxExplanation: A sequence diagram illustrates the temporal order of interactions between system participants. Participants are declared with `participant <ID> as <Name>`. Messages are shown with arrows, indicating synchronous (`->`) or asynchronous (`->>`) communication. Activations (`->>+` and `-->>-`) represent an object's period of activity, and must be balanced. Loops, alternatives (`alt`), and options (`opt`) allow for conditional or repetitive flows. Alias IDs are not quoted.
sequenceDiagram.code:
sequenceDiagram
  participant Magistrate
  participant OAA as "Office of Attorney Appointments"
  participant CCMS as "Court Case Management System"
  participant Defendant
  participant DefAttorney as "Defense Attorney"
  participant DetentionFac as "Detention Facility Officer"
  participant DistrictJudge as "District Court Judge"
  participant LSC as "Local Selection Committee"

  Magistrate->>OAA: "Submit Request for Appointed Counsel"
  OAA->>+OAA: "Review Request"
  OAA->>CCMS: "Query Defendant/Incident Info"
  CCMS-->>OAA: "Return Case Data"

  alt Defendant Released from Custody
    OAA->>Defendant: "Notify Denial of Counsel"
    OAA->>OAA: "Record Denial Reason"
  else Defendant in Custody
    OAA->>OAA: "Confirm Defendant in Custody"
    OAA->>CCMS: "Check for Previous Appointments"
    CCMS-->>OAA: "Return Previous Appointment Status"

    alt Capital Felony Case
      OAA->>DistrictJudge: "Forward Capital Case Request"
      DistrictJudge->>LSC: "Consult for Qualified Attorneys"
      LSC-->>DistrictJudge: "Provide Qualified Attorney List"
      DistrictJudge->>OAA: "Select Two Attorneys (Qualified)"
      OAA->>DefAttorney: "Appoint Selected Attorneys"
    else Standard Case
      OAA->>OAA: "Determine Appointment Rotation List"
      OAA->>OAA: "Select Next Defense Attorney"
      OAA->>DefAttorney: "Notify Appointment"
    end

    OAA->>Defendant: "Notify Appointment Confirmation"
    OAA->>DetentionFac: "Notify Appointment Details"
  end
  OAA-->>-Magistrate: "Request Processed"
sequenceDiagram.caption: **ijis** Process Request for Appointed Counsel

entityRelationshipDiagram.syntaxExplanation: An Entity Relationship Diagram (ERD) models the structure of a database, showing entities (tables) and the relationships between them. Entities are defined with their attributes, specifying primary keys (PK), foreign keys (FK), and unique keys (UK). Relationships are indicated by lines with specific cardinality notations at each end, describing how many instances of one entity can be associated with instances of another. Relationship labels are always quoted.
entityRelationshipDiagram.code:
erDiagram
  DEFENDANT ||--o{ REQUEST : "submits"
  REQUEST ||--|| APPOINTMENT : "leads to"
  ATTORNEY ||--o{ APPOINTMENT : "receives"
  APPOINTMENT ||--o{ CONTACT_LOG : "generates"
  DEFENDANT ||--o{ CHARGE : "accused of"
  CHARGE ||--o{ SETTING : "has"
  ATTORNEY ||--o{ PAYMENT : "receives"
  REQUEST ||--o{ OAA_ACTIVITY : "involves"
  ATTORNEY ||--o{ ATTORNEY_LIST_ENTRY : "is on"
  OAA_ACTIVITY ||--o{ REPORT : "generates"

  DEFENDANT {
    int id PK
    string name
    string financial_status
    datetime created_at
  }

  REQUEST {
    int id PK
    int defendant_id FK
    string status
    datetime submitted_date
  }

  APPOINTMENT {
    int id PK
    int request_id FK
    int attorney_id FK
    datetime appointment_date
    string case_type
  }

  ATTORNEY {
    int id PK
    string name
    string qualifications
    string bar_number UK
  }

  CONTACT_LOG {
    int id PK
    int appointment_id FK
    string contact_type
    datetime contact_date
  }

  CHARGE {
    int id PK
    int defendant_id FK
    string charge_details
    string status
  }

  SETTING {
    int id PK
    int charge_id FK
    datetime setting_date
    string outcome
  }

  PAYMENT {
    int id PK
    int attorney_id FK
    decimal amount
    string status
    datetime request_date
  }

  OAA_ACTIVITY {
    int id PK
    string activity_type
    datetime activity_date
    int performed_by_user_id FK
  }

  REPORT {
    int id PK
    string report_type
    datetime generated_date
    string parameters
  }

  ATTORNEY_LIST_ENTRY {
    int id PK
    int attorney_id FK
    string list_type
    datetime added_date
    string status
  }
entityRelationshipDiagram.caption: **ijis** Indigent Defense Data Model
