Office of the Chief Information Officer, typically responsible for information technology strategy and operations within an organization.
Soil and Water Assessment Tool, a river basin scale model developed to quantify the impact of land management practices in large, complex watersheds.
Annualized Agricultural Non-Point Source Pollution Model, a pollutant loading model used to simulate nutrient and sediment transport from agricultural watersheds.
An area or ridge of land that separates waters flowing to different rivers, basins, or seas.
United States Department of Agriculture.

flowchartDiagram.syntaxExplanation: This flowchart illustrates the high-level process of data ingestion, quality assurance, and user access within the **stewards** system. Nodes represent distinct steps or decisions in the workflow, with arrows indicating the flow direction. Rectangular nodes signify processes, diamond nodes denote decisions, and rounded rectangles indicate start/end points. All labels containing spaces are enclosed in double quotes for clarity and adherence to syntax rules.
flowchartDiagram.caption: High-level stewards data flow.

sequenceDiagram.syntaxExplanation: This sequence diagram illustrates the core interaction flow for the 'Data Upload Process' within the **stewards** system. Participants represent distinct entities or system components involved in the process, ordered horizontally. Messages show the flow of information and actions between these participants, with solid lines indicating synchronous calls and dashed lines representing asynchronous responses. Arrows are used to show the direction of communication. Each message label clearly defines the action or information being exchanged.
sequenceDiagram.code:
sequenceDiagram
  participant WS as WatershedStaff
  participant SCA as StewardsClientApp
  participant SDS as StewardsDatabaseService
  participant SM as StewardsMetadata

  WS->>SCA: Collect & QA Local Data
  SCA->>SCA: Prepare Data & Metadata Files
  SCA->>SDS: Upload Data(dataset)
  SDS->>SDS: Validate Data Format & Integrity
  alt Data Validated
    SDS->>SDS: Store Data in DB
    SCA->>SM: Upload Metadata(metadata_file)
    SM->>SM: Store Metadata in DB
    SM-->>SCA: Metadata Stored Confirmation
    SDS-->>SCA: Data Stored Confirmation
    SCA-->>WS: Upload Success Notification
  else Data Invalid
    SDS-->>SCA: Data Validation Error
    SCA-->>WS: Upload Failure Notification
  end
sequenceDiagram.caption: Stewards data upload process sequence.

entityRelationshipDiagram.syntaxExplanation: This Entity Relationship Diagram (ERD) models key entities and their relationships within the **stewards** data system. Entities represent core data objects, attributes define their properties, and lines illustrate relationships with their respective cardinalities. 'PK' denotes a Primary Key, 'FK' denotes a Foreign Key, and 'UK' denotes a Unique Key. Relationships use standard Crow's Foot notation where '||' represents one and only one, 'o{' represents zero or more, and '}o' represents zero or one.
entityRelationshipDiagram.code:
erDiagram
  WATERSHED ||--o{ STATION : "contains"
  STATION ||--o{ DATA_POINT : "records"
  DATA_POINT ||--|| DATA_SERIES_TYPE : "is_of_type"
  WATERSHED ||--o{ METADATA : "has"
  USER ||--o{ METADATA : "creates"
  USER ||--o{ DATA_UPLOAD_LOG : "performs"
  DATA_UPLOAD_LOG }|--|| WATERSHED : "for"

  WATERSHED {
    int watershed_id PK
    string name
    string location
    string description
  }

  STATION {
    int station_id PK
    int watershed_id FK
    string name
    string latitude
    string longitude
  }

  DATA_SERIES_TYPE {
    int type_id PK
    string type_name UK
    string unit
  }

  DATA_POINT {
    int data_point_id PK
    int station_id FK
    int type_id FK
    datetime timestamp
    decimal value
    string uncertainty_code
  }

  METADATA {
    int metadata_id PK
    int watershed_id FK
    int user_id FK
    string title
    string standard_version
    text content
    datetime creation_date
  }

  USER {
    int user_id PK
    string username UK
    string password_hash
    string email
    string role
  }

  DATA_UPLOAD_LOG {
    int log_id PK
    int user_id FK
    int watershed_id FK
    datetime upload_timestamp
    string status
    string file_name
  }
entityRelationshipDiagram.caption: Stewards core data entity relationships.

tbdList: TBD-001: Detailed use case scenarios for all system features need to be elicited from user class members to accurately and completely describe expected uses and functionality (Ref: Section 3.1.2).

TBD-002: Real-world estimates of data storage needs, specifically the potential for gigabytes of data capacity, require further requirements gathering to refine hardware requirements (Ref: S-REQ-039).
TBD-003: Specific scenarios for load balancing, such as fail-over versus session-managed load balancing, need to be addressed in the functional requirements specification (Ref: S-REQ-040).

introduction.purpose: The purpose of this Software Requirements Specification (SRS) is to precisely define the functional and non-functional requirements for the **esa** (ASPERA-3 Processing and Archiving Facility) ground data system. This system is integral to the Mars Express mission, focusing on the comprehensive management of data from the ASPERA-3 instrument package. The document outlines the capabilities necessary for data processing, archiving, and dissemination to support scientific research and public outreach.
introduction.documentConventions: This document adheres to the IEEE 830-1998 Recommended Practice for Software Requirements Specifications. Requirements are systematically identified with project-unique identifiers and are rigorously defined to be necessary, verifiable, attainable, and clear. Each requirement includes explicit qualification methods, such as demonstration or analysis, along with a documented rationale and clear traceability to its originating source, ensuring a structured approach to requirements management.
introduction.intendedAudience: The primary audience for this SRS includes the **esa** development team, project managers, quality assurance personnel, and system architects responsible for the design, implementation, and verification of the system. Additionally, stakeholders from the Swedish Institute of Space Physics (IRF) and Southwest Research Institute (SwRI), along with the ASPERA-3 science team, will utilize this document to ensure alignment with mission objectives and scientific data requirements. The detailed specifications herein serve as a foundational reference for all project activities.
introduction.productScope: The **esa** ground data system is specifically designed to acquire, process, store, and distribute all telemetry data originating from the ASPERA-3 instrument package aboard the Mars Express mission. Its scope encompasses the acquisition of telemetry via **NISN**, the transformation of raw data into Instrument Data File Set (**IDFS**) formats, local archiving at SwRI, and long-term archival submission to the **NASA Planetary Data System (PDS)**. Furthermore, the system is responsible for providing web-based interfaces for both public data displays and secure science team analysis.

overallDescription.productPerspective:
The **esa** system operates as a critical component of the ground segment for the European Space Agency's Mars Express mission, specifically supporting the ASPERA-3 instrument package. It functions as a new, self-contained product responsible for managing scientific data from acquisition to long-term archival. This system replaces or augments previous manual data handling processes by providing an automated and standardized facility for processing complex spacecraft telemetry.
The **esa** system interfaces extensively with external entities critical to its operation. It receives raw telemetry and orbit/attitude data from the **European Science Operations Center (ESOC)**, which acts as the primary data disposition system. Furthermore, processed scientific data is prepared and submitted to the **NASA Planetary Data System (PDS)** for long-term preservation and broader scientific access, establishing a key interface for data exchange with the global scientific community. The system also interacts with various internal software components that define and manage the Instrument Data File Sets.
productFunctions: Telemetry Data Acquisition: It receives and processes raw telemetry and orbit/attitude data from the Mars Express spacecraft via **ESOC**.
Science Data Processing: It transforms raw telemetry into calibrated and validated **IDFS** data sets suitable for scientific analysis.
Data Archival: It maintains both local short-term and **PDS**-compliant long-term archives for all processed and raw data.
Data Distribution: It distributes **IDFS** data sets and related software tools to the ASPERA-3 Co-Investigators and the broader scientific community.
Web-based Data Visualization: It offers secure web interfaces for scientific team analysis and public displays of current ASPERA-3 data.

userClassesAndCharacteristics.characteristics: The primary users of the **esa** system are the ASPERA-3 Science Team members, including Co-Investigators (Co-I's), who require timely access to processed **IDFS** data for in-depth scientific analysis. These users possess advanced technical expertise in space physics and data interpretation, necessitating tools for data access, analysis, and visualization. They require secure and often password-protected access to proprietary data before public release.

This user group accesses validated ASPERA-3 data from the **NASA PDS** for independent research and analysis. This community may have varying levels of technical proficiency but requires standardized, well-documented data products that are readily accessible. Their interaction is primarily with the archived data rather than the direct processing system.

The Public represents a broader audience, engaging with web-based displays that present current ASPERA-3 data in an easily understandable format. This group primarily uses the system for educational and public outreach purposes, requiring intuitive interfaces and clear visualizations without needing in-depth technical understanding of the data processing.

System Operators are responsible for the daily operation, monitoring, and maintenance of the **esa** data system. These users possess specialized knowledge of system administration, data processing workflows, and troubleshooting procedures. Their interaction focuses on ensuring data integrity, system uptime, and compliance with data acquisition and archival schedules.

overallDescription.operatingEnvironment:
The **esa** system operates primarily within a **UNIX**-based computing environment. This includes both development and production server instances, located at Southwest Research Institute (**SwRI**). The chosen operating system facilitates robust data processing, system automation, and integration with specialized scientific software tools. The production environment will incorporate an archival system designed for secure and reliable long-term data storage.
Network connectivity is a critical aspect of the operating environment, requiring a secure and high-bandwidth connection to the **NASA Integrated Services Network (NISN)** for data transfers and distribution. The system also necessitates a dedicated web server to host both public and password-protected data display interfaces. This infrastructure ensures continuous data flow from **ESOC** and efficient dissemination of processed data to end-users and **PDS** archives.
designAndImplementationConstraints:
The **esa** system must adhere to several key design and implementation constraints to ensure compatibility, data integrity, and mission success. Data products must be generated in the specific Instrument Data File Set (**IDFS**) format, as defined by individual Software Design Documents for each ASPERA-3 component. Furthermore, all data submitted to the **NASA Planetary Data System (PDS)** must strictly comply with **PDS** archiving standards, including formatting and metadata requirements, within a six-month window from data acquisition.
The system's development and operational infrastructure are constrained to a **UNIX**-based platform, leveraging existing **SwRI** resources and expertise. Integration with external systems like **ESOC** for telemetry acquisition and **NISN** for network communications mandates adherence to specified interface control documents and protocols. These constraints dictate choices in programming languages, data management solutions, and overall system architecture.
userDocumentation: The **esa** system will be accompanied by comprehensive user documentation to support its diverse user base. This includes user manuals detailing the operation of data processing and archival tools for system operators and the **ASPERA-3** science team. Online help features will be integrated into the web-based data displays, providing guidance for both public users and science team members on data navigation and interpretation. Tutorials may also be developed to facilitate the use of **SwRI-developed** data access and science analysis software by **Co-I’s**.
assumptionsAndDependencies:
The successful operation of the **esa** system assumes the continuous availability and timely delivery of ASPERA-3 telemetry data and Mars Express Orbit/Attitude data from **ESOC** via **NISN**. It is also assumed that the definitions for the **IDFS** data sets, including instrument parameters, spacecraft parameters, and science information, will be fully documented and provided in the respective Software Design Documents from the ASPERA-3 team components (**DPU**, **ELS**, **IMA**, **NPD**, **NPI**, **SU**, **OA**).
Key dependencies include the availability and stability of the **NASA Planetary Data System (PDS)** for long-term data archival and the adherence of **PDS** to its published interface standards. The system also relies on the provision of **SwRI-developed** IDFS data access and science analysis software, which are essential for Co-Investigator data utilization. The overall project timeline and budget are dependent on these external entities and deliverables remaining consistent with current plans.

externalInterfaceRequirements.userInterfaces:
The **esa** system will present several user interfaces designed to facilitate data access and visualization for different user classes. A public-facing web interface will provide visual displays of the most current ASPERA-3 data, offering an intuitive and easily navigable experience for general users. This interface will conform to standard web accessibility guidelines and employ responsive design principles to ensure usability across various browsers and devices.
For the ASPERA-3 science team, a separate, password-protected web interface will be available, offering access to comprehensive ASPERA-3 data for in-depth scientific analysis. This interface will allow team members to query, visualize, and potentially download specific data sets. The design will emphasize advanced charting capabilities, data filtering options, and adherence to established scientific data visualization standards to support complex research activities.
externalInterfaceRequirements.hardwareInterfaces:
The **esa** system interacts with underlying hardware through its deployment on dedicated **UNIX**-based servers at **SwRI**. This includes interfaces to server-grade processors, memory modules, and high-capacity storage devices forming the local archive. Communication with these hardware components will be managed via standard operating system drivers and kernel interfaces, ensuring efficient data input/output and system resource utilization.
Network hardware interfaces, specifically network interface cards and associated infrastructure, are critical for establishing reliable connections to the **NASA Integrated Services Network (NISN)**. These interfaces must support the required data transfer rates for telemetry acquisition and data distribution, employing standard network protocols such as **TCP/IP**. There are no direct interfaces to specialized instrument hardware, as data acquisition occurs through established network protocols from **ESOC**.
externalInterfaceRequirements.softwareInterfaces:
The **esa** system integrates with several external software components to fulfill its mission objectives. It acquires ASPERA-3 and Mars Express Orbit/Attitude telemetry data directly from the **ESOC Data Disposition system**, as defined by the **DDID** and **ESA Mars Express Space / Ground Interface Control Document (SGICD)**. This interface is crucial for the initial ingestion of raw mission data into the processing pipeline. The system also processes packet definitions supplied by **FMI** and **UMU** for data interpretation.
For long-term archival, the **esa** system interfaces with the **NASA Planetary Data System (PDS)**, requiring adherence to **PDS**-compliant data formats and submission protocols. Internally, the system relies on detailed Virtual Instrument Descriptions and associated Software Design Documents (e.g., **ELS-SDD**, **IMA-SDD**) which specify the structure and parameters of the **IDFS** data sets. Output interfaces include system logs, local archive configuration files, and the `www.ASPERA-3.org` web server for data presentation.
externalInterfaceRequirements.communicationsInterfaces:
The **esa** system relies extensively on robust communication interfaces for data exchange and dissemination. Telemetry data acquisition from **ESOC** is facilitated through a secure network connection to the **NASA Integrated Services Network (NISN)**. This communication utilizes established protocols for reliable data transfer, ensuring the integrity and completeness of incoming ASPERA-3 and Mars Express Orbit/Attitude data. Message formatting will adhere to the specifications outlined in relevant interface control documents like the **DDID**.
For data distribution to ASPERA-3 Co-Investigators, the system supports electronic data transfer, potentially leveraging **FTP** or secure file transfer protocols over the internet. The public and science team web-based displays will utilize **HTTP/HTTPS** protocols for secure client-server communication. All communication channels will incorporate appropriate security measures, including encryption where sensitive data is transmitted, to protect intellectual property and ensure data privacy.

systemFeatures.name: Data Acquisition and Processing
systemFeatures.description: This feature enables the **esa** system to automatically acquire raw telemetry and orbit/attitude data from external sources and transform it into standardized Instrument Data File Set (**IDFS**) products. It significantly improves data availability for scientific analysis by automating a complex, error-prone manual process. Technically, this involves robust network communication protocols and data parsing algorithms, forming the initial and most critical stage of the data pipeline. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: **ESOC** provides new ASPERA-3 telemetry data. Response: The **esa** system initiates acquisition of the telemetry data.
Stimulus: The **esa** system acquires ASPERA-3 science data. Response: The **esa** system processes the data into **IDFS** data sets.
Stimulus: Raw telemetry contains errors or is uncleaned. Response: The **esa** system generates intermediate files of cleaned-up telemetry.
functionalRequirements: The system shall acquire ASPERA-3 experiment and Mars Express Orbit/Attitude telemetry data from **ESOC**.
The system shall process all ASPERA-3 science data into **IDFS** data sets.
The system shall process engineering and ancillary information necessary for calibration and science validation into **IDFS** data sets.
The system shall generate intermediate files of cleaned-up ASPERA-3 and MEX OA telemetry if cleaned-up telemetry is not provided by **ESOC**.

systemFeatures.name: Data Archiving
systemFeatures.description: This feature ensures the secure and persistent storage of all raw, intermediate, and processed data products generated by the **esa** system. It provides long-term data availability for future scientific endeavors, re-processing, and compliance with archival mandates. Technically, it requires a robust local storage infrastructure and adherence to specific data management plans for integrity and accessibility. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: **esa** system acquires telemetry data. Response: The **esa** system stores the raw ASPERA-3 and MEX OA telemetry data on a local **SwRI** archive.
Stimulus: **esa** system generates **IDFS** data sets. Response: The **esa** system stores the ASPERA-3 and MEX OA **IDFS** data sets on a local **SwRI** archive.

Stimulus: **esa** system generates intermediate cleaned-up telemetry files. Response: The **esa** system stores any **esa**-generated intermediate files of ASPERA-3 and MEX OA cleaned-up telemetry on a local **SwRI** archive.
functionalRequirements: The system shall store the ASPERA-3 and MEX OA telemetry data on a local **SwRI** archive.
The system shall store the ASPERA-3 and MEX OA **IDFS** data sets on a local **SwRI** archive.
The system shall store any **esa**-generated intermediate files of ASPERA-3 and MEX OA cleaned-up telemetry on a local **SwRI** archive.

systemFeatures.name: Data Distribution and PDS Archival
systemFeatures.description: This feature manages the dissemination of processed ASPERA-3 data and associated software to various stakeholders, including Co-Investigators and the **NASA Planetary Data System (PDS)**. It facilitates collaborative scientific research and ensures long-term public access to validated mission data, aligning with open science principles. Technically, this involves implementing efficient data transfer mechanisms and strict compliance with **PDS** standards for format, calibration, and validation. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: New **IDFS** data sets are available. Response: The **esa** system provides **IDFS** data and intermediate files to all ASPERA-3 **Co-I’s**.
Stimulus: New ASPERA-3 **IDFS** data is available. Response: The **esa** system electronically distributes ASPERA-3 **IDFS** data to **Co-I’s** within 24 hours.
Stimulus: Processed data is calibrated and validated. Response: The **esa** system provides ASPERA-3 **IDFS** data to **NASA PDS** in **PDS**-compliant form within 6 months of acquisition.
Stimulus: **Co-I** requires data access software. Response: The **esa** system makes **IDFS** data access software available to **Co-I’s**.
functionalRequirements: The system shall provide ASPERA-3 and MEX OA **IDFS** data and any **esa**-generated intermediate files of ASPERA-3 and MEX OA cleaned-up telemetry to all ASPERA-3 **Co-I’s**.
The system shall electronically distribute ASPERA-3 **IDFS** data to **Co-I’s** within 24 hours of acquiring ASPERA-3 telemetry, given error-free transmission and processing.
The system shall electronically distribute MEX OA **IDFS** data to **Co-I’s** within 24 hours of acquiring MEX OA telemetry, given error-free transmission and processing.

The system shall electronically distribute any **esa**-generated intermediate files of ASPERA-3 and MEX OA cleaned-up telemetry to **Co-I’s** within 24 hours of acquisition, given error-free transmission and processing.
The system shall provide ASPERA-3 **IDFS** data and MEX OA **IDFS** data to **NASA PDS**.
The system shall provide ASPERA-3 data to **NASA PDS** in **PDS**-compliant form.
The system shall ensure ASPERA-3 data is calibrated and validated prior to depositing in the **NASA PDS**.
The system shall provide ASPERA-3 data to **NASA PDS** no later than 6 months after acquisition.
The system shall provide **IDFS** data processing algorithms to **IRF**.
The system shall integrate science analysis software into the **NASA** approved data repository.
The system shall make **IDFS** data access software developed by **SwRI** available to the ASPERA-3 **Co-I’s**.
The system shall make science analysis software developed by **SwRI** to analyze **IDFS**-formatted data available to the ASPERA-3 **Co-I’s**.
The system shall determine the distribution mechanisms for ASPERA-3 and MEX OA data to each **Co-I** six months prior to MEX launch, and define these in the **esa** Operations Procedures Document.

systemFeatures.name: Web-based Data Displays
systemFeatures.description: This feature offers interactive web interfaces for visualizing ASPERA-3 data, catering to both public outreach and specialized scientific analysis by the team. It enhances accessibility to mission results and fosters engagement by providing a dynamic view of current and historical data. Technically, this requires a robust web server, dynamic data retrieval from the archive, and secure authentication mechanisms for protected content. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: A public user accesses the **esa** web portal. Response: The **esa** system provides web-based displays of the most current ASPERA-3 data for public view.

Stimulus: An ASPERA-3 team member requests advanced data displays. Response: The **esa** system provides web-based displays defined by the ASPERA-3 team, allowing use of any available ASPERA-3 data for science analysis.
Stimulus: An ASPERA-3 team member attempts to access a protected science analysis display. Response: The **esa** system prompts the user for authentication credentials.
functionalRequirements: The system shall provide web-based displays of the most current ASPERA-3 data for public view.
The system shall provide web-based displays, defined by the ASPERA-3 team, where any available ASPERA-3 data can be used for science analysis.
The system shall password protect the web-based displays for science analysis until the ASPERA-3 data is made public.

systemFeatures.name: Error Handling and System Maintenance
systemFeatures.description: This feature ensures the reliability and maintainability of the **esa** system by incorporating mechanisms for detecting and managing errors, alongside providing continuous software support. It minimizes data corruption risks and ensures long-term operational stability, which is crucial for a mission-critical ground system. Technically, this involves implementing robust error detection routines, logging mechanisms, and a defined support framework. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: An error occurs during data processing. Response: The **esa** system activates its built-in error handling mechanism.
Stimulus: A system fault is detected. Response: **SwRI** provides **esa** system maintenance.
Stimulus: A software issue is reported. Response: **SwRI** provides software support for the **esa** system.
functionalRequirements: The system shall have built-in error handling.
**SwRI** shall provide **esa** system maintenance.
**SwRI** shall provide software support for the **esa** system.

systemFeatures.name: Access Control
systemFeatures.description: This feature implements security measures to protect sensitive data and intellectual property within the **esa** system, particularly regarding restricted access to pre-public scientific data and system operations. It safeguards proprietary information and ensures data integrity before public release. Technically, this necessitates a robust authentication system and granular access control mechanisms for different user roles. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: An unauthorized user attempts to access password-protected data. Response: The **esa** system web server denies access.
functionalRequirements: The **esa** system web server shall be password protected where appropriate to allow only pertinent ASPERA-3 team members access.

performanceRequirements: The **esa** system shall acquire, process, and distribute ASPERA-3 **IDFS** data to Co-Investigators within 24 hours of raw telemetry acquisition, provided transmission and processing are error-free. This requirement ensures timely access for ongoing scientific analysis and rapid response to mission events. The system shall also complete the processing of daily telemetry volumes within a 24-hour cycle to maintain data currency.
safetyRequirements: There are no specific safety requirements directly associated with the **esa** ground data system, as it primarily handles scientific data and is not involved in real-time spacecraft operations or command and control. However, the system shall ensure the integrity of all processed and archived data to prevent corruption or loss of scientific information, thereby indirectly contributing to the reliability of scientific outcomes.
securityRequirements: The **esa** system shall implement password protection for all web-based interfaces and data access points intended for the ASPERA-3 science team, as specified by E-REQ-027. This ensures that sensitive pre-public scientific data and intellectual property are protected from unauthorized access. Communication channels used for data acquisition and distribution shall employ encryption where appropriate to prevent eavesdropping and data tampering, upholding data privacy and confidentiality.
softwareQualityAttributes: The **esa** system shall prioritize maintainability, ensuring that its codebase is well-documented, modular, and adheres to established coding standards to facilitate future enhancements and bug fixes. Reliability is critical, requiring the system to operate continuously and without significant data loss or corruption, supported by robust error handling (E-REQ-024). Testability shall be inherent in the design, allowing for comprehensive verification of all functional requirements. Usability for both scientific users and system operators shall be emphasized, with intuitive interfaces and clear operational procedures.
businessRules: The **esa** system shall ensure that all ASPERA-3 data provided to **NASA PDS** is compliant with **PDS** standards for data formatting, metadata, and scientific validation. A critical business rule dictates that this submission to **PDS** must occur no later than six months after data acquisition, as per mission requirements. Furthermore, access to science analysis web displays and certain data sets shall be restricted to authorized ASPERA-3 team members until the data is officially made public, safeguarding intellectual property rights.

otherRequirements: The **esa** system shall be adaptable to evolving data formats or processing algorithms by ensuring a modular design that allows for updates to specific virtual instrument descriptions or processing routines without requiring a complete system overhaul. This flexibility supports the long-term scientific objectives of the mission, accommodating unforeseen changes in instrumentation parameters or scientific analysis methodologies over the Mars Express mission duration.

flowchartDiagram.syntaxExplanation: The **esa** flowchart uses `flowchart TD` for a top-down orientation. Nodes are represented by alphanumeric IDs, with labels enclosed in double quotes if they contain spaces or special characters. Decision nodes use diamond shapes, while process steps use rectangular shapes. Arrows indicate the flow of control or data.
flowchartDiagram.code:
flowchart TD
    StartProcess(["Start"]) --> AcquireTelemetry["Acquire Telemetry from ESOC (E-REQ-001)"]
    AcquireTelemetry --> CleanTelemetryCheck{"Clean Telemetry Provided? (E-REQ-004)"}
    CleanTelemetryCheck --No--> GenerateCleaned["Generate Cleaned-up Telemetry (E-REQ-004)"]
    GenerateCleaned --> ProcessData["Process to IDFS Data Sets (E-REQ-002, E-REQ-003)"]
    CleanTelemetryCheck --Yes--> ProcessData
    ProcessData --> StoreLocal["Store on Local SwRI Archive (E-REQ-005, E-REQ-006, E-REQ-007)"]
    StoreLocal --> DistributeData["Distribute to Co-Is (E-REQ-008, E-REQ-009, E-REQ-010, E-REQ-011)"]
    DistributeData --> PublicWebDisplay["Provide Public Web Displays (E-REQ-021)"]
    DistributeData --> ScienceWebDisplay{"Provide Team Science Web Displays? (E-REQ-022)"}
    ScienceWebDisplay --> PWSecureCheck{"Password Protected? (E-REQ-023, E-REQ-027)"}
    PWSecureCheck --Yes--> AuthenticateUser["Authenticate User"]
    AuthenticateUser --> DisplayScienceData["Display Science Data"]
    PWSecureCheck --No--> DisplayScienceData
    DisplayScienceData --> PDSArchival["Submit to NASA PDS (E-REQ-012, E-REQ-013, E-REQ-014, E-REQ-015)"]
    PDSArchival --> EndProcess(["End"])
flowchartDiagram.caption: esa system data processing flow.

sequenceDiagram.syntaxExplanation: The **esa** sequence diagram illustrates the temporal interactions between participants. `participant` declarations define interacting entities, with aliases for brevity. Messages are represented by arrows, showing the direction and type of communication. Activations (`->>+` and `-->>-`) denote active periods for participants during message processing, ensuring a balanced representation of call stacks.
sequenceDiagram.code:
sequenceDiagram
    participant User as "ASPERA-3 Co-I"
    participant WebServer as "esa Web Server"
    participant AuthSystem as "Authentication System"
    participant esaSystem as "esa Data System"
    participant LocalArchive as "Local Archive"

    User->>+WebServer: Request Protected Science Display (E-REQ-022)
    WebServer->>AuthSystem: Request Authentication (E-REQ-023, E-REQ-027)
    AuthSystem->>WebServer: Send Login Form
    WebServer->>User: Display Login Form
    User->>WebServer: Submit Credentials
    WebServer->>AuthSystem: Verify Credentials
    AuthSystem-->>-WebServer: Authentication Result (Success/Failure)
    alt Authentication Success
        WebServer->>+esaSystem: Request Science Data
        esaSystem->>LocalArchive: Retrieve IDFS Data
        LocalArchive-->>esaSystem: Return IDFS Data
        esaSystem-->>-WebServer: Return Processed Data
        WebServer-->>User: Display Science Data
    else Authentication Failure
        WebServer-->>User: Display "Access Denied" Error
    end
sequenceDiagram.caption: ASPERA-3 Co-I secure data access.

entityRelationshipDiagram.syntaxExplanation: The **esa** Entity Relationship Diagram (ERD) visualizes key entities and their relationships within the system's data model. Entities are declared with attributes, including primary (PK), foreign (FK), and unique (UK) keys. Relationships between entities define how they are associated, using standard crow's foot notation to specify cardinality, such as one-to-many (`||--o{`) or many-to-one (`}|--||`). All relationship labels are enclosed in double quotes for clarity.
entityRelationshipDiagram.code:
erDiagram
    TELEMETRY_DATA {
        int id PK
        int location_id FK
        string source
        string raw_content
        datetime acquisition_time
    }
    IDFS_PRODUCT {
        int id PK
        int telemetry_id FK
        int location_id FK
        string processed_content
        datetime creation_time
        string pds_status
    }
    LOCAL_STORAGE_LOCATION {
        int location_id PK
        string name UK
        string path
    }
    ASPERA_TEAM_USER {
        int user_id PK
        string email UK
        string password_hash
        string user_role
    }
    WEB_VIEW_DEFINITION {
        int view_id PK
        string view_name UK
        string access_level
        string configuration
    }

    IDFS_PRODUCT }|--|| TELEMETRY_DATA : "derived_from"
    TELEMETRY_DATA }|--|| LOCAL_STORAGE_LOCATION : "stored_in"
    IDFS_PRODUCT }|--|| LOCAL_STORAGE_LOCATION : "stored_in"
    ASPERA_TEAM_USER ||--o{ WEB_VIEW_DEFINITION : "accesses"
entityRelationshipDiagram.caption: esa core data entities relationships.

E-TBD-003: Detailed specifications for internal data requirements of the **esa** system, which are left to be defined during the design phase.

E-TBD-004: The exact mechanisms and specific data sets for distributing ASPERA-3 and MEX OA data to each **Co-I**, pending determination six months prior to MEX launch and documentation in the **esa** Operations Procedures Document.
E-TBD-005: The detailed **ELS Software Design Document (ELS-SDD)** specifications, pending its release on December 15, 2001.
E-TBD-006: The detailed **IMA Software Design Document (IMA-SDD)** specifications, pending its release on December 15, 2001.
E-TBD-007: The detailed **NPD Software Design Document (NPD-SDD)** specifications, pending its release on December 15, 2001.
E-TBD-008: The detailed **NPI Software Design Document (NPI-SDD)** specifications, pending its release on December 15, 2001.
E-TBD-009: The detailed **DPU Software Design Document (DPU-SDD)** specifications, pending its release on September 1, 2002.
E-TBD-010: The detailed **SU Software Design Document (SU-SDD)** specifications, pending its release on September 1, 2002.
E-TBD-011: The detailed **OA Software Design Document (OA-SDD)** specifications, pending its release on September 1, 2002.

introduction.purpose: This document details the software requirements specification for the **split merge** v2.1.0 open source project. It will later be used as a base for the extension of the existing software itself. This document follows the IEEE standard for software requirements specification documents. The PDF file format is widely used for secure and dependable electronic information exchange across various platforms such as **Macintosh**, **Windows**, **UNIX**, and **LINUX**, as well as numerous mobile devices. However, efficient manipulation of these files often comes at a cost. The primary purpose of this project is to provide an easy, free, and efficient way to handle PDF files through a simple graphical interface.
introduction.documentConventions: **split merge** was initially implemented prior to the creation of this document, meaning all stated requirements are already satisfied. It is critically important to maintain consistency by updating this document with every future requirement and clearly articulating its priority. This practice ensures the document remains a valuable and current resource for development. Due to the existing implementation of **split merge**, certain sections of this document adopt a style that is similar to a user manual, offering practical insights. Technical information is included where necessary, and readers are encouraged to consult Appendix A for a comprehensive glossary of defined terms.
introduction.intendedAudience: This Software Requirements Specification document is specifically designed for multiple key stakeholders. It targets software **Developers**, offering them a clear overview of the project's capabilities and guiding their efforts to improve or expand features, thus setting future development guidelines. **Project Testers** can leverage this document as a foundational resource for structuring their testing strategies, facilitating a more methodical and organized approach to identifying bugs. Additionally, **End Users** of the application who wish to understand the functionalities and capabilities of the **split merge** project can refer to this document for comprehensive information.
introduction.productScope: **split merge** is a Free and Open Source Software (FOSS) tool designed for comprehensive manipulation of PDF documents, including splitting, merging, and various other transformations. The system provides both a Graphical User Interface (GUI) and a Command-Line Interface (Console) to cater to diverse user preferences. It is available in basic and enhanced versions, both released under open-source licenses. The GUI integrates functionality through a modular plugin architecture, where each plugin performs a specific task and loads within the main interface. The basic version of the GUI includes six core plugins: Alternate Mix, Merge/Extract, Rotate, Split, Visual document composer, and Visual reorder. The **Console** application represents the core functionality, providing both basic and enhanced PDF manipulation features accessible via command-line prompts.
references: IEEE 830-1998 IEEE Recommended Practice for Software Requirements Specifications.
ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018 Systems and software engineering — Life cycle processes — Requirements engineering.
The official website of the project: http://www.pdfsam.org/
Project's development and distribution website at Sourceforge: http://sourceforge.net/projects/pdfsam/
PDFsam wiki: http://www.pdfsam.org/mediawiki/

overallDescription.productPerspective: **split merge** stands as a multi-functional, free, and open-source utility specifically engineered for the manipulation of PDF files, operating under a **GNU General Public License (GPL)**. Unlike numerous PDF viewing applications, **split merge** distinguishes itself by offering a broad spectrum of manipulation features not commonly found in a single, accessible tool. Its open-source nature provides a strong incentive for technically proficient users to contribute to the project's ongoing development, fostering a community-driven approach. Furthermore, an enhanced version of the software is also available, extending its capabilities to meet more complex user demands.
productFunctions: Splitting PDF documents into various segments (e.g., chapters, single pages).
Merging multiple PDF documents or specific subsections from them.
Extracting designated sections from a PDF document into a new, single PDF.
Mixing alternate pages from two PDF documents, with options for straight or reverse order.
Rotating pages within selected PDF documents according to user specifications.
Visually reordering individual pages of a chosen PDF document via a graphical interface.
Visually composing a new document by dragging and dropping pages from multiple selected PDF documents.
Saving and loading a complete working environment to automate recurrent tasks and restore previous configurations.
Managing **split merge** settings and defining a startup environment for customized application launch.
Executing any of these features as command-line operations through the **Console** application.

userClassesAndCharacteristics.characteristics: This group encompasses individuals across various age groups with diverse levels of computer experience. The **split merge** interface is designed to be pleasant and user-friendly, making its core functions accessible even to those with limited technical expertise. More experienced computer users can leverage advanced features, such as executing functions via the command line **Console** application, which is particularly beneficial for batch processing or server-side manipulation of larger PDF files.

userClassesAndCharacteristics.userClass: Open Source Software Developers and Contributors
userClassesAndCharacteristics.characteristics: This category includes individuals with strong programming language knowledge keen on understanding and extending the project's source code. It also includes **Translators** proficient in languages not yet supported, enabling the localization of the application. Fundamentally, this class welcomes anyone enthusiastic about contributing to the Free and Open Source Software (FOSS) community, aligning with the project's core ethos of collaborative development and open contribution.

overallDescription.operatingEnvironment: **split merge** is engineered for platform independence, ensuring it can operate on any system equipped with a **Java Virtual Machine (JVM)** version 1.6 or higher. The software has undergone extensive testing and demonstrated full compatibility across major operating systems. These tested environments include **Microsoft Windows**, various **GNU/Linux** distributions, and **Mac OS X**. The typical hardware requirements for **split merge** involve a maximum default memory usage of 254MB, although this can vary based on the size of processed PDF files and is configurable by the user. The application requires approximately 13.9MB of disk space for installation and operation. Users handling exceptionally large PDF files have the option to adjust the default memory allocation using specific commands to optimize performance.
overallDescription.designAndImplementationConstraints: **split merge** is fundamentally designed as a platform-independent application, meticulously developed using the **Java** programming language. Its user interface, constructed with **Java Swing**, mandates that any future developers intending to contribute to the project possess a solid understanding of **Java** and **Swing** development. The software is released under the **GNU GPLv2** license, with the **PDFsam-console** component additionally dual-licensed under **GPLv2** and **LGPLv2**. All individuals involved in the development, usage, or distribution of **split merge** are required to acknowledge and fully comply with the terms stipulated by these licenses.
overallDescription.userDocumentation: Comprehensive user documentation components for **split merge** are readily available and maintained on the project's official website. These resources are housed within the dedicated Resources section of the website. For further detailed information regarding the availability and specific content of these documentation materials, readers should refer to Section 1.5 of this document, which provides a list of relevant references.
overallDescription.assumptionsAndDependencies: The successful operation and deployment of **split merge** are predicated on a crucial assumption: the availability of a functional **Java Runtime Environment (JRE)**, specifically version 1.6 or a later iteration. This dependency is fundamental, as the application is built upon the **Java** platform. The project assumes that this prerequisite **JRE** will be present and correctly configured within the user's operating environment. Any deviation from this assumption could impact the application's functionality.

externalInterfaceRequirements.userInterfaces: The **split merge** application primarily interacts with users through a Graphical User Interface (GUI), which is designed to be intuitive and modular. This GUI leverages a plugin-based architecture, where distinct functional panels, such as those for splitting, merging, or visual reordering, are dynamically loaded. Key elements of the GUI include a selection panel for input document management, various option panels for configuring operations, and a destination folder selection for output. Visual manipulation plugins, like 'Visually reorder' and 'Visually compose', feature interactive subpanels that display PDF page thumbnails. Users can select, drag, reorder, delete, and rotate these thumbnails directly within the interface. A built-in image viewer is provided for previewing individual pages. Standard GUI controls such as buttons (e.g., 'RUN', 'Move Up', 'Move Down', 'Reverse'), text fields for input (e.g., passwords, page ranges), and dropdowns for options are consistently employed across all plugins. Error messages are displayed clearly to guide the user. The interface also supports keyboard shortcuts, such as 'Alt + S' for saving and 'Alt + L' for loading the working environment, enhancing user efficiency.
externalInterfaceRequirements.hardwareInterfaces: The **split merge** application does not necessitate direct interaction with specialized hardware interfaces beyond standard computing peripherals. It relies on the underlying operating system and **Java Virtual Machine** to manage access to common hardware components such as displays for rendering the GUI, keyboards and mice for user input, and disk drives for file storage and retrieval. No specific custom hardware interfaces or unique device protocols are required for the functionality of this software.
externalInterfaceRequirements.softwareInterfaces: The **split merge** system extensively interfaces with the **Java Virtual Machine (JVM)**, which serves as its primary execution environment, and utilizes the **Java Swing** toolkit for rendering its graphical user interface. A critical software interface is its interaction with PDF file format specifications, allowing it to parse, manipulate, and generate new PDF documents. The application also provides a **Command-Line Interface (Console)**, enabling external scripts or advanced users to programmatically invoke core PDF manipulation functions. Data exchange primarily involves reading and writing PDF files from the local file system. For saved working environments, the system generates and consumes **XML** files, providing a structured means of configuration persistence. The software relies on standard operating system file I/O operations for all document management tasks.
externalInterfaceRequirements.communicationsInterfaces: The **split merge** application does not inherently require any specialized communications interfaces for its core PDF manipulation functionalities. Its primary operations are localized to the user's machine, involving file system access for input and output. While the project's official website and source code repositories are mentioned as references, the application itself does not include active communication protocols for data transfer, network-based processing, or remote functionality. Any communication requirements, such as updates or external data retrieval, would typically be handled outside the application's direct operational scope, likely through a web browser.

systemFeatures.name: Split PDF Document
systemFeatures.description: This feature enables users to divide a single PDF document into multiple smaller documents based on a variety of configurable options. The primary user value lies in efficiently segmenting large documents into manageable parts, such as splitting a book into individual chapters or extracting specific page ranges. From a technical perspective, this involves parsing the input PDF, applying the chosen splitting logic, and generating new PDF files, potentially with compression and version control. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User selects "Split" option from the plugins tree or presses 'S' key. Response: The system displays the Split plugin panel.
Stimulus: User selects a PDF document from a directory. Response: The system imports the file, displays information (pages, version), and prompts for a password if the document is protected.
Stimulus: User enters password for a protected PDF and reloads. Response: The system reloads the PDF, granting access for splitting.

Stimulus: User selects one of the seven splitting types (Burst, Split every 'n' pages, Split even/odd pages, Split after specific pages, Split at specific size, Split by bookmark level). Response: The system configures the splitting logic according to the selected type.
Stimulus: User selects a destination folder for output files and chooses compression/PDF version. Response: The system configures the output directory, compression setting, and target PDF version.

Stimulus: User defines a filename pattern using variables like `[CURRENTPAGE]`, `[TIMESTAMP]`, `[FILENUMBER]`, `[BASENAME]`, `[BOOKMARK_NAME]`. Response: The system prepares to generate output filenames according to the specified pattern.
Stimulus: User presses the "RUN" button. Response: The system initiates the PDF splitting operation and saves the resulting files to the destination.
functionalRequirements: SM-REQ-001: The system shall allow the user to split only one document at a time for this feature.
SM-REQ-002: The system shall require PDF version 1.5 or above for the compression of output files.
SM-REQ-003: The system shall limit the number of digits used for the unique `[FILENUMBER]` prefix to 10 or less.

systemFeatures.name: Merge and Extract PDF Documents
systemFeatures.description: This feature enables users to combine multiple PDF documents, or selected subsections of them, into a single new PDF document. Conversely, it also allows users to extract specific sections from a single PDF into a standalone document. The user value lies in flexibly compiling or isolating content from various sources, such as assembling chapters from different reports or extracting specific exhibits. Technically, this involves reading multiple input PDFs, identifying specified page ranges, and sequentially concatenating them into a new output PDF. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User selects "Merge/Extract" option or presses 'M' key. Response: The system displays the Merge/Extract panel.
Stimulus: User selects one or more files to handle. Response: The system inserts selected files into a selection table with details.
Stimulus: User changes the order of files in the table using "Move Up" / "Move Down" or clicking column headers. Response: The system reorders the files in the selection table.

Stimulus: User specifies page selections for documents (e.g., "All", "8-15", "page-", single page number, comma-separated values). Response: The system configures the page ranges to be merged for each document.
Stimulus: User enters a password for a protected document and reloads. Response: The system reloads the file with access.
Stimulus: User opts to export the list of selected files as an **XML** file. Response: The system generates an **XML** file suitable for the console `concat` command with the `-l` option.
Stimulus: User indicates that PDF documents contain forms in the merge options. Response: The system adjusts merging logic to properly handle PDF forms.
Stimulus: User selects the output file path or accepts the default, and chooses compression/PDF version. Response: The system configures the output file destination, compression, and PDF version.
Stimulus: User presses the "RUN" button. Response: The system initiates the merging or extraction operation and saves the resultant PDF.
functionalRequirements: SM-REQ-004: The system shall ensure that page numbers specified in the page selection are valid and within the document's bounds.
SM-REQ-005: The system shall require PDF version 1.5 or above for the compression of output files.

systemFeatures.name: Alternate Mix PDF Documents
systemFeatures.description: This feature allows users to combine two PDF documents by interleaving their pages, taking them alternately from each source document. This is particularly useful for consolidating documents scanned as one-sided pages or for specific content arrangement needs. The user benefits from effortlessly creating a unified document with interleaved content. Technically, the system reads pages from two input PDFs based on configurable step sizes and order (straight or reverse) and then constructs a new PDF with this interleaved sequence. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User selects "Alternate Mix" option. Response: The system displays the Alternate Mix plugin panel.
Stimulus: User selects two PDF documents from a directory. Response: The system imports files, displays information (pages, version), and prompts for passwords if protected.
Stimulus: User enters passwords for protected PDFs and reloads. Response: The system reloads the files with access.
Stimulus: User changes the order of the two selected files using "Move Up" / "Move Down" buttons or column header clicks. Response: The system reorders the files in the selection table.
Stimulus: User selects "Reverse first document" or "Reverse second document" options. Response: The system configures page retrieval for the respective document to be in reverse order.

Stimulus: User sets the "Number of pages to switch document" parameter. Response: The system adjusts the interleaving step size, determining how many pages are taken from one document before switching to the other.

Stimulus: User selects the destination folder for the output file or accepts the default, and chooses compression/PDF version. Response: The system configures the output file destination, compression, and PDF version.
Stimulus: User presses the "RUN" button. Response: The system initiates the alternate mixing operation and saves the combined PDF.
functionalRequirements: SM-REQ-006: The system shall allow the user to mix exactly two documents at a time.

systemFeatures.name: Rotate PDF Document Pages
systemFeatures.description: This feature provides the capability to batch rotate pages within one or more PDF documents. It's designed for global adjustments, affecting either all, even, or odd pages of the selected documents. Users benefit from quickly correcting page orientations across entire documents or specific page sets. From an architectural standpoint, the system processes input PDFs, applies a uniform rotation transformation to selected pages, and then generates new PDF files with the adjusted orientation. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User selects "Rotate" option. Response: The system displays the Rotate plugin panel.
Stimulus: User selects one or more documents to rotate from a directory. Response: The system imports files, displays information (pages, version), and prompts for passwords if protected.
Stimulus: User enters passwords for protected PDFs and reloads. Response: The system reloads the files with access.
Stimulus: User selects clockwise rotation degrees (e.g., 90, 180, 270). Response: The system configures the rotation angle.
Stimulus: User selects which pages to rotate (All, Even, or Odd). Response: The system configures the page selection for rotation.

Stimulus: User selects the destination folder for output files or accepts the default, and chooses compression/PDF version. Response: The system configures the output directory, compression setting, and target PDF version.
Stimulus: User defines a filename pattern for output files using available prefixes. Response: The system prepares to generate output filenames according to the specified pattern.
Stimulus: User presses the "RUN" button. Response: The system initiates the page rotation operation and saves the resultant PDF files.
functionalRequirements: SM-REQ-007: The system shall require PDF version 1.5 or above for the compression of output files.
SM-REQ-008: The system shall limit the number of digits used for the unique `[FILENUMBER]` prefix to 10 or less.

systemFeatures.name: Visually Reorder PDF Document Pages
systemFeatures.description: This feature offers a highly intuitive graphical interface for manipulating individual pages within a single PDF document. Users can visually reorder, rotate, delete, and preview specific pages using an interactive thumbnail view. This provides significant user value by enabling precise, page-level adjustments and corrections to a PDF's structure and content. Technically, the system generates visual thumbnails, allows for interactive manipulation of their order and properties, and then reconstructs the PDF based on the final arrangement. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User selects "Visually reorder" option. Response: The system displays the Visual reorder plugin panel.
Stimulus: User selects a document from a directory. Response: The system generates and displays thumbnails for each page of the PDF document in a subpanel.
Stimulus: User clicks on specific page thumbnails. Response: The system selects the clicked pages for manipulation.
Stimulus: User drags selected pages, uses "Move Up" / "Move Down", or "Reverse" button. Response: The system visually reorders the pages as specified.
Stimulus: User adjusts the zoom level of the thumbnails preview. Response: The system changes the visual scale of the page thumbnails.

Stimulus: User selects "Delete" / "Undelete" or "Rotate left" / "Rotate right" actions on selected pages. Response: The system marks pages for deletion/inclusion or rotates their visual representation and applies the change to the output.
Stimulus: User right-clicks on selected pages. Response: The system displays a context menu with manipulation options.
Stimulus: User selects "Preview" on a selected page. Response: The system displays the selected page in a built-in Image viewer.

Stimulus: User selects the destination folder for the output file or accepts the default, and chooses compression/PDF version. Response: The system configures the output file destination, compression, and PDF version.
Stimulus: User presses the "RUN" button. Response: The system initiates the page reordering and manipulation operation, generating a new PDF.
functionalRequirements: SM-REQ-009: The system shall require PDF version 1.5 or above for the compression of output files.
SM-REQ-010: The system shall allow the user to manipulate only one document at a time for this feature.

systemFeatures.name: Visually Compose PDF Document
systemFeatures.description: This feature empowers users to create a new PDF document by visually assembling pages from multiple existing PDF documents. It offers a user-friendly graphical interface where users can open several input files, preview their pages, and then drag-and-drop or otherwise arrange selected pages into a composition panel to form the final document. The significant user benefit is the ability to custom-build complex documents from disparate sources. Technically, the system manages multiple input PDF streams, renders their pages as thumbnails, tracks user-defined page compositions, and then generates a new PDF from the compiled page sequence. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User selects "Visually compose" option. Response: The system displays the Visual document composer panel, divided into input and composition areas.
Stimulus: User opens one or more input PDF documents in the input panel. Response: The system generates and displays thumbnails for each page of the input documents.
Stimulus: User adjusts zoom level in the input panel. Response: The system changes the scale size of the input document thumbnails.
Stimulus: User selects "Preview" for an input page. Response: The system displays the selected page in the Image Viewer.
Stimulus: User drags pages from the input panel to the composition panel. Response: The system adds the dragged pages to the new document's composition.

Stimulus: User uses "Move Up" / "Move Down", "Delete", "Rotate clockwise/anticlockwise", or "Reverse" functions in the composition panel. Response: The system manipulates the pages within the new document's composition as specified.
Stimulus: User selects "Preview" for a composed page. Response: The system displays the selected page in the Image Viewer.

Stimulus: User selects the destination folder for the output file or accepts the default, and chooses compression/PDF version. Response: The system configures the output file destination, compression, and PDF version.
Stimulus: User presses the "RUN" button. Response: The system initiates the document composition operation and saves the new PDF.
functionalRequirements: SM-REQ-011: The system shall require PDF version 1.5 or above for the compression of output files.
SM-REQ-012: The system shall provide an intuitive drag-and-drop interface for composing documents.

systemFeatures.name: Save and Load Working Environment
systemFeatures.description: This feature provides users with the ability to persist and restore the complete state of the **split merge** application, including all current settings and configurations for each plugin. The primary user value is the significant time saving for recurrent tasks, as users can quickly resume their work without needing to manually reconfigure options. From a technical standpoint, the system serializes the application's current state into an **XML** file and can deserialize it to restore the previous setup. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User presses "Alt + S" or selects "Save" from the "File" menu. Response: The system prompts the user for a location to save the current working environment as an **XML** file.
Stimulus: User selects a save location and confirms. Response: The system saves the complete application state, including plugin settings, to the specified **XML** file.
Stimulus: User presses "Alt + L" or selects "Load" from the "File" menu. Response: The system prompts the user to select an existing working environment **XML** file.
Stimulus: User selects an **XML** file and confirms. Response: The system loads the specified working environment, restoring all plugin configurations and application settings.
functionalRequirements: SM-REQ-013: The system shall provide a mechanism to save the complete working environment to an **XML** file.
SM-REQ-014: The system shall provide a mechanism to load a previously saved working environment from an **XML** file.
SM-REQ-015: The system shall restore all plugin-specific settings and loaded file lists upon loading a working environment.

systemFeatures.name: Log Panel
systemFeatures.description: This feature integrates a log panel within the **split merge** GUI, providing real-time feedback on operations, errors, and informational messages. This offers user value by enhancing transparency, aiding in troubleshooting, and confirming the successful execution of tasks. Technically, the system collects runtime events, such as file processing progress, warnings, and errors, and displays them in a dedicated user interface component. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User initiates a PDF manipulation operation (e.g., Split, Merge). Response: The system displays progress messages and status updates in the log panel.
Stimulus: An error occurs during an operation (e.g., invalid file, password mismatch). Response: The system displays an error message with relevant details in the log panel.
Stimulus: A critical warning or informational message is generated. Response: The system displays the warning or informational message in the log panel.
Stimulus: User wants to review past operations. Response: The system retains a history of log entries within the panel for user inspection.
functionalRequirements: SM-REQ-016: The system shall record and display informational messages related to application operations in the log panel.
SM-REQ-017: The system shall record and display warning messages in the log panel.
SM-REQ-018: The system shall record and display error messages, including details of the error, in the log panel.
SM-REQ-019: The system shall maintain a visible history of log entries for the duration of the application session.

systemFeatures.name: Application Settings Management
systemFeatures.description: This feature allows users to configure various application-wide settings and define a default working environment to be loaded upon startup. The user value lies in customizing the application's behavior to suit individual preferences and streamlining the launch process for common workflows. Technically, the system provides a dedicated settings interface for modifying configuration parameters and persists these preferences locally, likely in a configuration file, for retrieval at startup. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User accesses the "Settings" panel (e.g., via a menu option). Response: The system displays configurable application settings.
Stimulus: User modifies a setting (e.g., default output folder, memory allocation, default startup environment). Response: The system updates the corresponding configuration parameter.
Stimulus: User saves the modified settings. Response: The system persists the updated settings for future application sessions.
Stimulus: The application starts. Response: The system loads the configured default environment and applies saved settings.
functionalRequirements: SM-REQ-020: The system shall provide a user interface for configuring application-wide settings.
SM-REQ-021: The system shall allow users to specify a default working environment to load on startup.
SM-REQ-022: The system shall persist user-defined settings between application sessions.
SM-REQ-023: The system shall apply all saved settings and load the default working environment upon application launch.

performanceRequirements: SM-REQ-024: The **split merge** system shall initialize and be ready for user interaction within 5 seconds on a typical desktop environment. Rationale: Efficient startup and processing times are critical for user satisfaction, especially for a desktop utility. SM-REQ-025: The system shall complete the splitting of a 100-page PDF document into single pages within 10 seconds. Rationale: Efficient processing is vital for handling common PDF manipulation tasks. SM-REQ-026: The system shall consume no more than 512MB of RAM when processing PDF files up to 500 pages in size, assuming default memory allocation. Rationale: Resource efficiency ensures the application runs smoothly without adversely impacting other system processes, particularly on machines with limited resources. SM-REQ-027: The system shall perform file operations (load, save) for documents up to 200 pages within 3 seconds. Rationale: Responsive file handling contributes significantly to a fluid user experience.
safetyRequirements: SM-REQ-028: The **split merge** system shall ensure that original PDF documents are never modified or corrupted during any operation. All output shall be written to new files. Rationale: Data integrity is paramount, ensuring users' source documents remain untouched. SM-REQ-029: The system shall handle corrupted input PDF files gracefully, providing informative error messages without crashing or leading to data loss. Rationale: Robust error handling prevents application failure and guides users in resolving issues. SM-REQ-030: The system shall provide clear visual feedback during long-running operations, such as a progress bar or status indicator, to prevent user frustration and perceived application unresponsiveness. Rationale: Transparency during processing enhances usability and manages user expectations.
securityRequirements: SM-REQ-031: The **split merge** system shall correctly handle password-protected PDF documents by prompting the user for the correct password before allowing manipulation. Rationale: Adherence to PDF security standards is essential for handling sensitive documents. SM-REQ-032: The system shall not store user-provided passwords for protected PDF documents persistently after the application session ends. Rationale: Preventing the storage of sensitive credentials minimizes security risks. SM-REQ-033: The system shall not transmit any user document content or application data to external servers. All processing shall occur locally. Rationale: Ensuring local processing safeguards user privacy and data confidentiality.
softwareQualityAttributes: SM-REQ-034: Portability: The **split merge** system shall be runnable on any operating system equipped with a **Java Virtual Machine** version 1.6 or higher, including **Microsoft Windows**, **GNU/Linux**, and **Mac OS X**. Rationale: Platform independence broadens the user base and simplifies deployment. SM-REQ-035: Usability: The system shall provide a consistent and intuitive Graphical User Interface (GUI) across all plugin functionalities, adhering to standard **Java Swing** conventions. Rationale: A user-friendly interface reduces learning curves and increases user satisfaction. SM-REQ-036: Reliability: The system shall consistently produce valid and correctly formatted PDF output documents for all supported operations, even under varied input conditions. Rationale: Correct and dependable output is fundamental to a document manipulation tool. SM-REQ-037: Maintainability: The system's codebase, being open source and written in **Java**, shall follow established coding standards to facilitate future enhancements and bug fixes by community developers. Rationale: Good code hygiene is vital for long-term project viability and community contributions. SM-REQ-038: Testability: The system's modular plugin architecture shall allow for independent testing of each core functionality. Rationale: Modular design simplifies the testing process, enabling more comprehensive coverage and easier identification of defects.
businessRules: SM-REQ-039: The **split merge** application shall be distributed under the terms of the **GNU General Public License (GPLv2)**. Rationale: This license choice promotes free use, study, sharing, and modification of the software. SM-REQ-040: The **split merge** **Console** component shall additionally be dual-licensed under **GPLv2** and **LGPLv2**. Rationale: The dual-licensing offers greater flexibility for integration into other software projects, potentially expanding its utility. SM-REQ-041: All contributions to the **split merge** project shall adhere to the licensing terms, ensuring legal compliance for all derivative works. Rationale: Maintaining consistent licensing ensures the project's legal standing and fosters a clear framework for contributions.

otherRequirements: The **split merge** project is subject to the **GNU GPLv2** license, and the **PDFsam-console** component is dual-licensed under **GPLv2** and **LGPLv2**. All users and developers must comply with these licensing terms. This open-source model governs the rights to use, copy, modify, and distribute the software, ensuring its continued availability as a free tool.

glossary.definition: Portable Document Format. A file format used to present documents in a manner independent of application software, hardware, and operating systems.
Graphical User Interface. A type of user interface that allows users to interact with electronic devices through graphical icons and visual indicators, as opposed to text-based interfaces.
Free and Open Source Software. Software that is both free (as in freedom) and open source (source code is available).
GNU General Public License. A free software license that guarantees end users the freedom to run, study, share, and modify the software.
GNU Lesser General Public License. A free software license that is less restrictive than the GPL, often used for libraries, allowing them to be linked with non-free software.
Java Virtual Machine. An abstract machine that enables a computer to run **Java** programs as well as programs written in other languages that are also compiled to **Java** bytecode.
Extensible Markup Language. A markup language that defines a set of rules for encoding documents in a format that is both human-readable and machine-readable.
A software component that adds a specific feature to an existing computer program.
A command-line interface (CLI) application that provides core functionalities of **split merge** through text commands.

flowchartDiagram.syntaxExplanation: Flowcharts represent processes and decision points. Nodes are identified by alphanumeric IDs and can have various shapes. Labels for nodes with spaces or symbols must be enclosed in double quotes. Arrows indicate the flow direction. `TD` or `LR` specify top-down or left-right orientation.
flowchartDiagram.code:
flowchart TD
    A1["Start Split Operation"] --> A2["User Selects PDF Document"]
    A2 --> A3{"Document Protected?"}
    A3 -- Yes --> A4["Prompt for Password"]
    A4 --> A5["User Enters Password"]
    A5 --> A6["Reload Document"]
    A3 -- No --> A7["Select Split Options"]
    A6 --> A7
    A7 --> A8["Define Output Settings"]
    A8 --> A9["User Presses 'RUN'"]
    A9 --> A10["Perform Splitting Logic"]
    A10 --> A11{"Splitting Successful?"}
    A11 -- No --> A12["Display Error Message"]
    A11 -- Yes --> A13["Save Output PDF Files"]
    A13 --> A14["Operation Completed"]
    A12 --> A14
flowchartDiagram.caption: SM_FLOWCHART: PDF Document Splitting Process.

sequenceDiagram.syntaxExplanation: Sequence diagrams illustrate object interactions arranged in a time sequence. Participants represent entities or objects involved in the interaction. Messages passed between participants are shown as arrows. `->>` denotes an asynchronous message, `->>`+ for activation start, `-->>-` for activation end. Notes provide additional context.
sequenceDiagram.code:
sequenceDiagram
    participant EndUser
    participant GUISplitMerge as "split merge GUI"
    participant CoreLogic as "split merge Core Logic"
    
    EndUser->>+GUISplitMerge: Select "Visually Reorder" plugin
    GUISplitMerge->>CoreLogic: Request PDF Document
    CoreLogic-->>GUISplitMerge: Display File Selection Dialog
    EndUser->>GUISplitMerge: Choose PDF Document
    GUISplitMerge->>+CoreLogic: Load Document & Generate Thumbnails
    CoreLogic-->>-GUISplitMerge: Display Page Thumbnails
    EndUser->>GUISplitMerge: Select Pages & Manipulate (Drag, Rotate, Delete)
    GUISplitMerge->>+CoreLogic: Process Page Manipulations
    CoreLogic-->>-GUISplitMerge: Update Visuals
    EndUser->>GUISplitMerge: Press "RUN" Button
    GUISplitMerge->>+CoreLogic: Initiate Output Generation
    CoreLogic-->>-GUISplitMerge: Return Status
    GUISplitMerge-->>EndUser: Display "Operation Complete" / Errors
sequenceDiagram.caption: SM_SEQUENCE: Visual Page Reordering Workflow.

entityRelationshipDiagram.syntaxExplanation: Entity-Relationship Diagrams (ERDs) depict the structural design of a database by showing entities, their attributes, and the relationships between them. Entities are named in uppercase. Attributes are listed with their data type and key types (PK for Primary Key, FK for Foreign Key, UK for Unique Key). Relationships are labeled and indicate cardinality (e.g., `||--o{` for one-to-many, optional many).
entityRelationshipDiagram.code:
erDiagram
    USER ||--o{ SETTING_PROFILE : "configures"
    SETTING_PROFILE ||--|{ PLUGIN_SETTING : "contains"
    PLUGIN ||--o{ PLUGIN_SETTING : "defines defaults for"
    DOCUMENT }|--|| PAGE : "comprises"
    OPERATION }|--|| DOCUMENT : "processes"
    OPERATION }|--o{ SETTING_PROFILE : "uses"
    
    USER {
        string user_id PK
        string username
    }
    
    SETTING_PROFILE {
        string profile_id PK
        string user_id FK
        string profile_name UK
        datetime created_at
        datetime last_modified
    }
    
    PLUGIN {
        string plugin_id PK
        string plugin_name UK
        string description
    }
    
    PLUGIN_SETTING {
        string setting_id PK
        string profile_id FK
        string plugin_id FK
        string setting_key
        string string_value
    }
    
    DOCUMENT {
        string doc_id PK
        string filename
        string filepath
        int page_count
        string pdf_version
    }
    
    PAGE {
        string page_id PK
        string doc_id FK
        int page_number
        string thumbnail_path
        int rotation_angle
        bool is_deleted
    }

    OPERATION {
        string operation_id PK
        string operation_type
        datetime start_time
        datetime end_time
        string status
    }
entityRelationshipDiagram.caption: SM_ERD: Core Data Model for **split merge**.

introduction.purpose: The primary objective of the **warc III** project is to enhance the existing **WARC Tools** framework by delivering advanced capabilities for web archive management. This phase focuses on facilitating the widespread adoption of the WARC file format among web development communities. The system aims to provide robust open-source tools, a comprehensive software library, and essential technical documentation. These enhancements will empower users to efficiently manipulate, manage, and explore web archive content without requiring complex low-level coding expertise.
introduction.documentConventions: This Software Requirements Specification (SRS) adheres to the guidelines established by IEEE 830-1998, ensuring a standardized and comprehensive documentation of requirements. All functional and non-functional requirements are meticulously detailed to provide clarity and precision.
