Streaming audio and video for live lectures and conferences.
Customizable user profiles for personalized identification and information sharing.
Secure file hosting and comprehensive document management within individual and class-specific spaces.
Online testing and examination tools with automated grading capabilities.
Enhanced collaboration features including application sharing for whiteboards and co-editing.
Accessibility of class lectures as downloadable podcasts for flexible learning.

userClassesAndCharacteristics.characteristics: This user class possesses overarching system privileges, enabling them to maintain the entire **virtual ed** platform, manage user enrollments, and create virtual classes. These users are expected to have comprehensive technical expertise and a deep understanding of the system's architecture and operational intricacies, allowing for full control over backend configurations and security settings.

This user class holds domain-specific class privileges, empowering them to manage and support particular classes they are enrolled in, alongside other users within those classes. While these users have no knowledge of the system's backend, they possess proficient front-end application skills, enabling them to facilitate class activities, moderate discussions, and assist students with feature utilization. This role typically includes professors and teaching assistants.

This user class has restricted privileges, allowing them to interact with the front-end of the system primarily with read-only access. Their primary functionalities involve accessing learning materials, participating in discussions, and submitting assignments. Users in this class, typically students, are limited to making modifications only to their personal profile sections and engaging with features permitted by their instructors, requiring basic computer literacy.

overallDescription.operatingEnvironment: The **virtual ed** server-side software is designed to operate on a robust server infrastructure, specifically requiring **Microsoft Server 2003** as a minimum platform. The server hardware must include at least a Dual Core 3.6 MHz CPU, a minimum of 2 gigabytes of storage space, and at least 4 gigabytes of RAM to ensure efficient performance for concurrent users and resource-intensive operations.
designAndImplementationConstraints: The **virtual ed** application development will occur during active university sessions, necessitating system rollouts and validation to be scheduled strictly during low usage periods to minimize downtime.

The existing NJIT user database system, integral to this application, may be outdated or require maintenance, potentially limiting concurrent user capacity to 250 users during initial deployment phases.
The online system documentation will not support all languages; initially, it will be available in English only.

Functional access to the application will be restricted to web browsers from **Microsoft** (Internet Explorer), **Apple** (Safari), and **Mozilla** (Firefox) to ensure compatibility and consistent user experience.
userDocumentation: Enrolled students and faculty will receive printable manuals via email and gain access to online tutorials demonstrating key system functionalities. Additionally, an integrated online help feature will provide readily accessible, easy-to-read 'how-to' instructions for various tasks and features within the **virtual ed** platform.

NJIT administration personnel will be provided with a comprehensive, in-depth manual detailing the entire system. This documentation is crucial for enabling the administration to effectively support and maintain the **virtual ed** system post-development, ensuring long-term operational continuity.
assumptionsAndDependencies: It is assumed that current university systems and databases, necessary for user authentication and course data, will remain available for use and archiving throughout the project lifecycle.

The existing database will undergo necessary modifications to accommodate **virtual ed**'s requirements, including the addition of new entities and relationships to support user profiles, course structures, and collaborative features.

The university staff and faculty are assumed to be willing to accept, learn, and actively utilize the new **virtual ed** system, critical for its successful adoption and integration into existing educational workflows.

Users are assumed to possess adequate computing hardware, stable network connections, and essential peripherals such as webcams and microphones to fully leverage the system's interactive features like video conferencing and profile videos.
Users are also assumed to have access to common software programs, including media players, which are necessary for streaming audio and video content provided by the **virtual ed** platform.

externalInterfaceRequirements.userInterfaces: The **virtual ed** system will feature a clean, intuitive graphical user interface, accessible via standard web browsers. It will conform to established web accessibility guidelines to ensure usability for all students and faculty, including those with disabilities. Key interface elements will include a persistent navigation menu, standardized buttons for common actions (e.g., 'Home', 'Help', 'Logout'), and consistent screen layouts across all modules.
externalInterfaceRequirements.hardwareInterfaces: The **virtual ed** system will interface with standard user hardware components essential for interactive features. This primarily includes **webcams** and **microphones** for streaming audio and video communication, particularly for live lectures and video conferencing. The system's client-side software components will utilize standard audio/video input APIs provided by the operating system, facilitating seamless integration with these peripheral devices. High-speed internet connectivity, while a network interface, also implies hardware interaction with modems and network cards, which must support robust data transfer rates for multimedia content.
externalInterfaceRequirements.softwareInterfaces: The **virtual ed** system will interface with several critical software components to deliver its full functionality. It will connect with the university’s existing user database for authentication and enrollment data, requiring a well-defined **API** for secure data exchange. The backend will rely on **Apache server** for web services and **IIS 6.0** or later for specific services, leveraging the **.NET Framework 2.0** or later. Furthermore, integration with a dedicated video and audio streaming server will be necessary, utilizing industry-standard streaming protocols to manage live and on-demand multimedia content. File management will interface with **FTP management software** on the server side, ensuring secure file transfers and storage.
externalInterfaceRequirements.communicationsInterfaces: The **virtual ed** platform will employ robust communication protocols to facilitate its diverse features. For general web access and data exchange, **HTTP** and **HTTPS** protocols will be used, with **HTTPS** mandatory for all secure transactions, including login and personal data transfer. Real-time communication features, such as instant messaging and video conferencing, will leverage specialized protocols designed for low-latency, high-throughput data streams. File transfer functionalities will utilize **FTP** protocols for secure uploading and downloading of documents and assignments. Email communication, for notifications and manual delivery of documentation, will adhere to **SMTP** standards. All communication channels will incorporate encryption to ensure data privacy and integrity.

systemFeatures.name: User Authentication and Access
systemFeatures.description: This foundational feature manages user logins and ensures secure access to the **virtual ed** platform. It provides a streamlined entry point for all user classes, validating credentials against the system's database to grant appropriate permissions and present relevant functionalities. This enhances user security and ensures that sensitive educational content is protected, contributing to a trusted learning environment. This feature is of High Priority, as it is a prerequisite for all other system interactions and data access.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User attempts to access the **virtual ed** platform. Response: The system displays the login page.
Stimulus: User enters username and password and clicks 'Login'. Response: The system authenticates the credentials against the user database.
Stimulus: User provides valid credentials. Response: The system logs the user in and redirects to the appropriate main dashboard based on their role (e.g., student, professor, admin).
Stimulus: User provides invalid credentials. Response: The system displays an error message indicating invalid username or password.
functionalRequirements: VE-REQ-001: The system shall provide a secure login page for user authentication.
VE-REQ-002: The system shall validate user credentials against the stored user database.
VE-REQ-003: The system shall enforce strong password policies, including minimum length and character complexity requirements.
VE-REQ-004: The system shall redirect users to a role-specific dashboard upon successful login.
VE-REQ-005: The system shall display clear error messages for failed login attempts.
VE-REQ-006: The system shall implement session management to maintain user login state securely.

systemFeatures.name: Instant Messaging
systemFeatures.description: The Instant Messaging feature within **virtual ed** enables students and professors to engage in real-time text-based communication, both individually and in groups. This functionality significantly improves communication efficiency by allowing users to see online contacts and initiate immediate conversations, addressing the common complaint of delayed responses in online learning. It fosters a more interactive and responsive classroom-like atmosphere, facilitating quick questions, answers, and collaborative discussions. This feature is of High Priority, as it directly impacts engagement and immediate support.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User logs in to **virtual ed**. Response: The user is logged in, and all available features are shown in the menu.
Stimulus: User clicks on the 'Instant Messaging' tool. Response: A new pop-up window displays a list of contacts, showing online users marked green and offline users marked red.
Stimulus: User double-clicks on the name of an online user. Response: A new window opens, initiating a real-time conversation between the two parties.
Stimulus: User clicks the smiley button in the chat window. Response: A list of available emojis for conversation is displayed.
Stimulus: User clicks the plus button in the chat window. Response: A list of other available online users appears, who can be invited to join the current conversation.
Stimulus: User double-clicks on a name from the 'add user' list. Response: The selected user joins the conversation, establishing a multi-party conference.
Stimulus: User clicks the file button in the chat window. Response: A new window prompts the user to browse their system for a file to send to the other party.
Stimulus: User selects a file. Response: The selected file is highlighted.
Stimulus: User clicks the 'send file' button. Response: The selected file begins transmitting to the other party.
functionalRequirements: VE-REQ-007: The system shall support client operating systems including **Windows XP**, **Windows Vista**, and **Mac OS**.
VE-REQ-008: The system shall ensure full functionality on **Internet Explorer**, **Mozilla Firefox**, and **Safari** web browsers.
VE-REQ-009: The system shall require client web browsers to have pop-up windows enabled for the Instant Messaging interface.
VE-REQ-010: The system shall require **JavaScript** to be enabled on client systems for the proper operation of the Instant Messaging software.
VE-REQ-011: The system shall provide visual indicators (e.g., green/red status) for online and offline contacts.
VE-REQ-012: The system shall allow users to send and receive text messages in real-time.
VE-REQ-013: The system shall support multi-party chat conferences by inviting additional online users.
VE-REQ-014: The system shall allow users to send files to other participants within a chat session.

systemFeatures.name: Streaming Audio and Video
systemFeatures.description: The Streaming Audio and Video feature enables students and professors to conduct real-time audio and/or video calls, fostering a highly interactive virtual classroom experience. Professors can host video lectures for the entire class or engage in one-on-one meetings, enhancing the sense of presence and direct interaction often missing in online courses. This capability supports immediate question-and-answer sessions and allows for flexible learning through the availability of recorded lectures. This feature is of Medium Priority, as instant messaging offers an alternative for real-time interaction, though audio/video greatly enriches the experience.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User logs in to **virtual ed**. Response: The user is logged in, and all available features are shown in the menu.
Stimulus: User clicks on the 'Instant Messaging' tool. Response: A new pop-up window displays a list of contacts.
Stimulus: User double-clicks on the name of an online user. Response: A new window opens for real-time text conversation.
Stimulus: User double-clicks on the 'Audio Call' button. Response: The system attempts to call the other user's computer and awaits a response.
Stimulus: The receiving user clicks the 'Accept' button. Response: An audio connection is established, and the conversation begins.
Stimulus: The receiving user clicks the 'Deny' button. Response: The connection is not established, and the initiating user receives a 'user busy' message.
Stimulus: User double-clicks on the 'Video Call' button. Response: The system attempts to call the other user's computer and awaits a response.
Stimulus: The receiving user clicks the 'Accept' button. Response: A video and audio connection is established, initiating real-time communication.
Stimulus: User clicks the 'Hang Up' button. Response: The audio/video conversation between users ends.
Stimulus: User clicks the 'Host a Conference' button. Response: A new window appears, allowing the user to select and invite online users to a multi-party conference.
Stimulus: Invited user clicks the 'Accept' button for a conference. Response: The user joins the video conference and can participate in the online audio/video lecture.
functionalRequirements: VE-REQ-015: The system shall support client operating systems including **Windows XP**, **Windows Vista**, and **Mac OS**.
VE-REQ-016: The system shall ensure full functionality on **Internet Explorer**, **Mozilla Firefox**, and **Safari** web browsers.
VE-REQ-017: The system shall require client web browsers to have pop-up windows enabled for audio/video calling.
VE-REQ-018: The system shall require **JavaScript** to be enabled on client systems for proper installation and operation of the audio/video calling software.
VE-REQ-019: The system shall require users to have a high-speed internet connection (e.g., **DSL**, **Cable**) to ensure optimal audio and video streaming performance.
VE-REQ-020: The system shall require client machines to be equipped with functional microphones and web cameras for audio and video communication features.
VE-REQ-021: The system shall enable one-on-one audio and video calls between users.
VE-REQ-022: The system shall support multi-party audio and video conferences for group lectures or meetings.
VE-REQ-023: The system shall provide real-time connection status and feedback during call attempts.

systemFeatures.name: Customizable User Profile
systemFeatures.description: The Customizable User Profile feature empowers users to personalize their presence within the **virtual ed** environment, allowing them to update personal information, change passwords, and modify the aesthetic elements of their profile page. This personalization fosters a stronger sense of ownership and identity within the online learning community, making interactions more engaging and meaningful. Users can express individuality through background colors, menu layouts, font choices, and even introductory videos, which can enhance team performance by facilitating better association among students. This feature is of Medium Priority, as its primary impact is on user experience and personalization rather than core functionality.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User logs in to **virtual ed**. Response: The user is logged in, and all available features are shown in the menu.
Stimulus: User clicks on the 'Setting Preferences' button. Response: A new menu displays all available customization features.
Stimulus: User clicks on the 'Contact Information' button. Response: A text area appears, allowing users to input contact details such as phone numbers and addresses.
Stimulus: User clicks on the 'Background Color' button. Response: A new menu displays all available colors for the profile page background.
Stimulus: User clicks on the 'Video' button. Response: A pop-up window appears, offering the opportunity to start video recording an introductory video for their profile page.
Stimulus: User clicks on the 'Info' button. Response: A text area appears where users can input additional personal information, strengths, weaknesses, or favorite links.
Stimulus: User clicks on the 'Font' button. Response: A new menu displays choices for different fonts to be used on the profile page.
Stimulus: User clicks on the 'Font Size' button. Response: A new menu displays various font sizes for selection.
Stimulus: User clicks on the 'Layout' menu. Response: A menu with different layout choices for the profile page appears.
Stimulus: User clicks on the 'Password' button. Response: A new window pops up, providing the user with the ability to reset or change their password.
functionalRequirements: VE-REQ-024: The system shall support client operating systems including **Windows XP**, **Windows Vista**, and **Mac OS**.
VE-REQ-025: The system shall ensure full functionality on **Internet Explorer**, **Mozilla Firefox**, and **Safari** web browsers for profile customization.
VE-REQ-026: The system shall require client web browsers to have pop-up windows enabled for profile customization features.
VE-REQ-027: The system shall require users to have a **webcam** and **microphone** if they wish to create and upload an introductory video to their profile page.
VE-REQ-028: The system shall allow users to update their personal contact information.
VE-REQ-029: The system shall enable users to customize background colors, menu layouts, and font styles on their profile pages.
VE-REQ-030: The system shall provide an interface for users to record and upload introductory videos to their profiles.
VE-REQ-031: The system shall allow users to add additional descriptive information and links to their profile pages.
VE-REQ-032: The system shall provide a secure mechanism for users to change or reset their passwords.

systemFeatures.name: Virtual-Space (File Uploads V1)
systemFeatures.description: Virtual-Space (V1) provides a dedicated folder space for each student, primarily supporting the secure uploading of files for assignment submission. This initial release focuses on core upload functionality, offering a reliable alternative to email for document sharing. While limited, it establishes a foundational component for file management, with each class section allocated specific space on an **FTP server**. The cost implication includes ensuring sufficient storage and management overhead. This feature is of Medium Priority, as email can still serve as a temporary alternative for basic document sharing.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User logs in to **virtual ed**. Response: The user is logged in, and all available features are shown in the menu.
Stimulus: User clicks on the 'File Hosting' software tool – Virtual-Space. Response: A window displays a file tree diagram of the user’s uploaded files.

Stimulus: User clicks the 'Browse' button. Response: A window prompts the user to select a file from their local machine. Once selected, the browse window closes, and the file path appears in a text box.
Stimulus: User clicks the 'Upload' button without selecting a file. Response: A window informs the user that they must use the 'Browse' button to select a file.

Stimulus: User clicks the 'Upload' button with a selected file that would cause the account to exceed its disk quota. Response: The file is allowed to upload, and an email notification is sent to the administrator and the professor regarding the overage. The file tree diagram updates to include the newly added file.
functionalRequirements: VE-REQ-033: The system shall utilize **FTP management software** on the server to manage file storage.
VE-REQ-034: The system shall allocate a minimum of 1GB of quota space per student per class section for file uploads.
VE-REQ-035: The system shall allow file uploads even if they exceed the assigned disk quota for a student.
VE-REQ-036: The system shall send an email notification to the administrator and relevant professor when a student exceeds their disk quota during an upload.
VE-REQ-037: The system shall restrict student access to their assigned folders with 'Write' only privileges for V1.
VE-REQ-038: The system shall provide professors with 'Read', 'Write', and 'Delete' privileges for files within their class folders.
VE-REQ-039: The system shall require configuration of firewall ports to permit necessary **FTP** file transfers.
VE-REQ-040: The system shall support client operating systems including **Windows XP**, **Windows Vista**, and **Mac OS**.
VE-REQ-041: The system shall ensure full functionality on **Internet Explorer**, **Mozilla Firefox**, and **Safari** web browsers for file uploads.
VE-REQ-042: The system shall require client web browsers to have pop-up windows enabled for the file upload interface.

systemFeatures.name: Virtual-Space V2 (Full File Management)
systemFeatures.description: Virtual-Space V2 significantly enhances file management capabilities within **virtual ed**, offering full control over uploaded files and a student-centric designated **FTP** space. This iteration introduces comprehensive features such as folder arrangement, file deletion, and stricter disk quota enforcement to optimize resource utilization. Each student will receive a dedicated 2GB quota, preventing uploads that exceed this limit. While incurring higher costs for increased disk space and administrative management, this version provides robust tools for sharing with team members and submitting assignments. This feature is of Low Priority, as basic functionality is covered by V1, and email remains an alternative.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User logs in to **virtual ed**. Response: The user is logged in, and all available features are shown in the menu.

Stimulus: User clicks on the 'File Hosting' software tool – Virtual-Space. Response: A window displays a file tree diagram of the user’s files and shared class files, with buttons to upload, share, or download.

Stimulus: User clicks the 'Browse' button. Response: A window prompts the user to select a file from their local machine. Once selected, the browse window closes, and the file path appears in a text box.

Stimulus: User clicks the 'Upload' button. Response: The program provides a window for the user to select a destination folder. After selection, the file uploads. If successful, a confirmation window appears, and the file name is added to the file tree diagram in the appropriate folder.
Stimulus: User clicks the 'Upload' button without selecting a file. Response: A window informs the user that they need to use the 'Browse' button to select a file.
Stimulus: User attempts to upload a file that would exceed their disk space quota. Response: A window informs the user that the upload cannot proceed without first deleting existing files.

Stimulus: User selects a file and clicks 'Delete' or presses the Delete key. Response: A verification window asks the user to confirm deletion. If 'Yes', the file is removed from the server and the file tree diagram.

Stimulus: User selects a file and drags it to another folder in the file tree. Response: The file moves to the new destination folder, is removed from the previous folder, and the file tree diagram updates.

Stimulus: User selects a parent folder and clicks 'New Folder'. Response: A window prompts the user to enter a name for the new folder. After input, the new folder is created on the server, and the file tree diagram updates.
Stimulus: User selects a file and clicks 'Download'. Response: A window prompts the user to select a local folder for download. A copy of the file is placed in the chosen folder.

Stimulus: User selects a file and clicks 'Open'. Response: The operating system prompts the user to 'Save' or 'Open'. If 'Save' is selected, a window prompts for a download location. If 'Open' is selected, the document opens with the appropriate program or prompts the user to select one.
functionalRequirements: VE-REQ-043: The system shall utilize **FTP management software** on the server, configured with advanced quota management, user authentication, and appropriate rights for comprehensive file management.
VE-REQ-044: The system shall allocate a dedicated quota space of 2GB per student for file storage.
VE-REQ-045: The system shall prevent file uploads that would cause a student to exceed their assigned disk quota.
VE-REQ-046: The system shall provide students with 'Read', 'Write', and 'Delete' privileges for files within their personal file spaces.
VE-REQ-047: The system shall require configuration of firewall ports to permit necessary **FTP** file transfers.
VE-REQ-048: The system shall support client operating systems including **Windows XP**, **Windows Vista**, and **Mac OSX**.
VE-REQ-049: The system shall ensure full functionality on **Internet Explorer**, **Mozilla Firefox**, and **Safari** web browsers for advanced file management.
VE-REQ-050: The system shall require client web browsers to have pop-up windows enabled for file management operations.
VE-REQ-051: The system shall allow users to create and manage folders within their assigned file space.
VE-REQ-052: The system shall enable users to move files between folders.
VE-REQ-053: The system shall provide functionality for users to download files to their local machines.
VE-REQ-054: The system shall facilitate opening files directly from the browser using appropriate local applications.

systemFeatures.name: Test Admin – Virtual-Exam (V1)
systemFeatures.description: The Virtual-Exam (V1) feature provides students with a real-time method for taking quizzes and exams online, simulating a traditional classroom testing environment. This initial version supports various question types, including multiple choice, true/false, short answer, and essay questions, delivered via downloadable Excel spreadsheets or editable PDF files. Students complete these files and upload them through the file management system within a specified time frame. This feature aims to integrate online assessment seamlessly into distance learning, improving the integrity and structure of evaluations. This feature is of High Priority, as online assessment is critical for academic evaluation.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User logs in to **virtual ed**. Response: The user is logged in, and all available features are shown in the menu.

Stimulus: User clicks on the 'Testing' software tool – Virtual-Exam. Response: A window displays a list of untaken tests posted by professors as clickable links, and for taken tests, it lists the name, class, and score/grade.

Stimulus: User clicks on an untaken test. Response: A prompt asks the student to confirm they want to take the test. If 'Yes', a window prompts to download the test file, and a timer for the allotted duration begins. If 'No', the user is returned to the Virtual-Exam home screen.
Stimulus: During an active test, user clicks 'Upload Test'. Response: A browse window appears, allowing the student to select the completed test file and upload it to the professor's **FTP** folder.
functionalRequirements: VE-REQ-055: The system shall utilize **FTP management software** on the server for secure storage and submission of virtual exams.
VE-REQ-056: The system shall allocate a private folder for each class with 'Write' permissions for student exam submissions.
VE-REQ-057: The system shall ensure appropriate user authentication and authorization for accessing and submitting virtual exams.
VE-REQ-058: The system shall display a list of available (untaken) and completed tests for each student.
VE-REQ-059: The system shall allow students to download exam files in formats such as **Excel spreadsheets** or editable **PDFs**.
VE-REQ-060: The system shall initiate a timer once a student confirms starting an exam, enforcing the professor-allotted duration.
VE-REQ-061: The system shall enable students to upload completed exam files to a designated professor's folder via a browse interface.
VE-REQ-062: The system shall support client operating systems compatible with **Microsoft Office** or similar applications for Mac users to complete exams.

systemFeatures.name: Test Admin – Virtual-Exam V2 (Automated Grading)
systemFeatures.description: Virtual-Exam V2 introduces enhanced online assessment capabilities with automated grading for supported question types and more secure testing environments. This advanced version allows professors to configure diverse question formats directly within the platform, including randomized question pools, and provides immediate feedback to students upon submission for objectively gradable questions. It aims to reduce manual grading effort for instructors and provide timely learning reinforcement for students. This feature is of Medium Priority, as V1 provides essential assessment, but automated grading significantly improves efficiency.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User logs in and navigates to 'Virtual-Exam'. Response: The system displays a list of assigned tests with their status.
Stimulus: User selects an untaken test with automated grading. Response: The system displays the test interface within the browser, starting a timer.
Stimulus: Student answers questions and clicks 'Submit Exam'. Response: The system processes the submission, performs automated grading for applicable questions, and displays a preliminary score.
Stimulus: Professor accesses completed test submissions. Response: The system displays student submissions, grades for automated questions, and areas for manual grading of essay questions.
Stimulus: Professor finalizes grades and publishes. Response: The system updates student records and notifies students of their final grades.
functionalRequirements: VE-REQ-063: The system shall support multiple question types, including multiple choice, true/false, fill-in-the-blank, and short answer.
VE-REQ-064: The system shall provide automated grading for objectively gradable question types.
VE-REQ-065: The system shall allow professors to create and manage question banks and assemble exams from these banks.
VE-REQ-066: The system shall support timed exams with automatic submission upon time expiry.
VE-REQ-067: The system shall provide professors with an interface to manually grade subjective questions.
VE-REQ-068: The system shall display detailed results and feedback to students after exam submission, based on professor settings.
VE-REQ-069: The system shall integrate with the grade book system to record exam scores.

systemFeatures.name: Clean GUI (Initial Interface)
systemFeatures.description: The initial Clean GUI feature provides users with a straightforward and intuitive graphical interface for interacting with the **virtual ed** platform. It focuses on clarity, ease of navigation, and a logical layout of system functionalities, ensuring that users can quickly locate and utilize features without unnecessary complexity. This design principle enhances the overall user experience, reducing the learning curve and improving productivity for students and faculty alike. This feature is of High Priority, as a usable interface is paramount for user adoption and efficient system interaction.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User accesses the **virtual ed** login page. Response: The system displays a clean, responsive login form with clear input fields.
Stimulus: User logs in successfully. Response: The system presents a dashboard with clearly labeled navigation elements to access core features (e.g., 'Messages', 'Files', 'Exams', 'Profile').
Stimulus: User clicks a main navigation link. Response: The system loads the corresponding module's interface, maintaining consistent header and footer elements.
Stimulus: User hovers over an interactive element. Response: The system provides a subtle visual cue (e.g., highlight, tooltip) indicating interactivity.
functionalRequirements: VE-REQ-070: The system shall provide a consistent and clear visual design across all user interfaces.
VE-REQ-071: The system shall utilize intuitive navigation menus and clear labeling for all features.
VE-REQ-072: The system shall ensure a responsive design that adapts to various screen sizes and resolutions.
VE-REQ-073: The system shall minimize visual clutter and prioritize content readability.
VE-REQ-074: The system shall adhere to established web usability guidelines (e.g., clear call-to-actions, consistent iconography).

systemFeatures.name: Clean GUI V2 (Enhanced User Experience)
systemFeatures.description: Clean GUI V2 represents an evolution of the user interface, incorporating enhanced aesthetic design, improved responsiveness, and advanced accessibility features to deliver a superior user experience. This iteration focuses on optimizing visual appeal, streamlining workflows, and ensuring the platform is usable for a broader audience, including those with specific accessibility needs. By refining interaction patterns and visual feedback, it contributes to a more engaging and efficient learning environment. This feature is of Medium Priority, building upon the essential usability provided by V1 to offer a more polished and inclusive interface.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User accesses any **virtual ed** page. Response: The system renders the page with improved visual aesthetics and faster load times.
Stimulus: User resizes the browser window. Response: The interface elements dynamically adjust and reflow to maintain optimal readability and functionality.
Stimulus: User activates screen reader software. Response: The system's interface elements are properly tagged and navigable via assistive technologies.
Stimulus: User interacts with a form element. Response: The system provides immediate, clear visual feedback (e.g., validation errors, success messages).
functionalRequirements: VE-REQ-075: The system shall implement a refined visual design, including updated color schemes and typography, to enhance user engagement.
VE-REQ-076: The system shall ensure full responsiveness and adaptability across all major devices and screen sizes (desktop, tablet, mobile).
VE-REQ-077: The system shall conform to **WCAG 2.1 AA** accessibility guidelines to support users with disabilities.
VE-REQ-078: The system shall provide enhanced visual feedback for user interactions and system status.
VE-REQ-079: The system shall optimize page load times for all major interfaces.

systemFeatures.name: Enhanced File Sharing and Document Collaboration
systemFeatures.description: This feature significantly expands upon basic file management by introducing advanced file sharing capabilities and real-time document collaboration. It enables multiple users to work concurrently on the same document, track changes, and manage versions effectively, fostering a highly interactive and productive group work environment. This functionality is crucial for projects, group assignments, and joint research, directly addressing the need for efficient teamwork in distance learning. This feature is of High Priority for promoting collaborative learning and project-based work.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User selects a file in Virtual-Space and clicks 'Share'. Response: The system displays options to share the file with specific users or groups, with configurable permissions.
Stimulus: User shares a document with editing permissions. Response: Invited users receive a notification and gain access to the document.
Stimulus: Multiple users open the same shared document for editing. Response: The system enables real-time co-editing, displaying cursors and changes from all active collaborators.
Stimulus: User saves changes to a collaborative document. Response: The system updates the document, creates a new version, and notifies collaborators of changes.
Stimulus: User views the version history of a collaborative document. Response: The system displays a list of all saved versions with associated timestamps and authors.
functionalRequirements: VE-REQ-080: The system shall allow users to share files and folders with other users and groups within the **virtual ed** platform.
VE-REQ-081: The system shall provide configurable sharing permissions (e.g., read-only, edit, co-own).
VE-REQ-082: The system shall support real-time collaborative editing for compatible document types.
VE-REQ-083: The system shall implement version control for collaboratively edited documents, allowing rollback to previous versions.
VE-REQ-084: The system shall notify users of changes made to shared documents.
VE-REQ-085: The system shall integrate with external office productivity suites for document viewing and editing (e.g., **Microsoft Office Online**, **Google Docs**).

systemFeatures.name: Class Lecture Video/Audio as Podcasts
systemFeatures.description: This feature provides flexibility for students by making class lecture videos and audio recordings available as downloadable podcasts. It allows students to access lecture content at their convenience, review complex topics, and catch up on missed sessions, accommodating diverse learning paces and schedules. This significantly enhances the adaptability of the learning experience, ensuring that crucial educational material is always accessible. This feature is of Medium Priority, as live streaming covers primary content delivery, but podcasting offers valuable supplementary access.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Professor records a live lecture or uploads a pre-recorded lecture. Response: The system processes and transcodes the multimedia content.
Stimulus: Professor designates a lecture as a podcast. Response: The system makes the lecture available in podcast format (e.g., **MP3**, **MP4**) for student access.
Stimulus: Student navigates to the 'Podcasts' section for their class. Response: The system displays a list of available lecture podcasts.
Stimulus: Student selects a podcast. Response: The system allows the student to either stream the podcast or download it to their device.
Stimulus: Student subscribes to a class podcast feed. Response: The system provides an **RSS** feed URL for external podcast clients.
functionalRequirements: VE-REQ-086: The system shall allow professors to designate recorded lectures as downloadable podcasts.
VE-REQ-087: The system shall encode audio/video lectures into standard podcast formats (e.g., **MP3**, **MP4**).
VE-REQ-088: The system shall provide a centralized repository for all class lecture podcasts.
VE-REQ-089: The system shall allow students to stream or download podcasts to their devices.
VE-REQ-090: The system shall offer **RSS** feed functionality for students to subscribe to class podcasts using external podcast clients.
VE-REQ-091: The system shall ensure podcasts are accessible offline once downloaded.

systemFeatures.name: Application Sharing for Whiteboards
systemFeatures.description: The Application Sharing for Whiteboards feature enables real-time collaborative drawing and annotation during live sessions, mimicking a traditional whiteboard experience in a virtual setting. This allows professors and students to visually explain concepts, solve problems collaboratively, and brainstorm ideas in an interactive manner. It significantly enhances engagement for subjects requiring visual demonstration and problem-solving, making complex topics more comprehensible. This feature is of High Priority for highly interactive and visually oriented online instruction.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User initiates a live video conference or a collaborative session. Response: The system provides an option to activate the 'Whiteboard Sharing' feature.
Stimulus: User selects 'Whiteboard Sharing'. Response: The system displays a shared digital whiteboard interface to all participants.
Stimulus: User draws, types, or annotates on the whiteboard. Response: All participants see the changes reflected in real-time on their whiteboards.
Stimulus: Multiple users simultaneously draw or type on the whiteboard. Response: The system displays all concurrent inputs and annotations in real-time.
Stimulus: User saves the whiteboard content. Response: The system saves the current state of the whiteboard as an image or PDF for later review.
functionalRequirements: VE-REQ-092: The system shall provide a shared digital whiteboard functionality during live collaborative sessions.
VE-REQ-093: The system shall support real-time drawing, typing, and annotation by multiple participants on the whiteboard.
VE-REQ-094: The system shall allow users to select various drawing tools (e.g., pens, shapes, colors).
VE-REQ-095: The system shall provide a mechanism to clear the whiteboard content.
VE-REQ-096: The system shall allow participants to save the whiteboard content as an image file (**PNG**, **JPEG**) or **PDF**.

performanceRequirements: VE-REQ-NFR-001: The system shall support a minimum of 250 concurrent active users participating in real-time activities such as instant messaging and video conferencing with acceptable latency (under 200ms). This ensures that peak usage periods, especially during live lectures or exam sessions, do not degrade the user experience.

VE-REQ-NFR-002: The system shall load all critical user interface pages within 3 seconds under normal network conditions for typical user scenarios. This prevents user frustration and ensures efficient navigation throughout the platform.

VE-REQ-NFR-003: The system shall process file uploads for assignments (up to 2GB per student) within a reasonable timeframe, completing 95% of uploads within 60 seconds given a stable high-speed internet connection. This ensures students can submit assignments reliably and promptly without undue delays.

VE-REQ-NFR-004: The system shall maintain video streaming quality at or above 720p for group conferences and 1080p for individual calls, with minimal buffering (less than 1% of playback time). This is crucial for clear communication and effective delivery of visual content in lectures.
safetyRequirements: VE-REQ-NFR-005: The system shall implement robust data backup and recovery procedures for all user data, including profiles, messages, files, and exam submissions, with daily automated backups. This ensures the integrity and availability of critical academic information in the event of system failures or data corruption.

VE-REQ-NFR-006: The system shall log all administrative actions and critical system events, maintaining an audit trail for a minimum of one year. This provides accountability and aids in forensic analysis for any security incidents or operational discrepancies.

VE-REQ-NFR-007: The system shall incorporate error handling and validation mechanisms to prevent data inconsistencies or system crashes due to malformed input or unexpected user actions. This improves system stability and protects the integrity of the academic environment.
securityRequirements: VE-REQ-NFR-008: The system shall enforce strong authentication mechanisms, including multi-factor authentication (MFA) for administrative and faculty accounts, to protect against unauthorized access. This safeguards sensitive data and ensures that only authorized personnel can access critical system functions and student records.

VE-REQ-NFR-009: The system shall encrypt all data in transit using **TLS 1.2** or higher for all communication channels (web, instant messaging, audio/video, file transfer). This protects user data from eavesdropping and tampering during network transmission.

VE-REQ-NFR-010: The system shall implement role-based access control (RBAC) to ensure that users can only access features and data consistent with their assigned roles (Student, Professor, Administrator). This prevents unauthorized access to academic resources and personal information.

VE-REQ-NFR-011: The system shall regularly undergo security audits and penetration testing by independent third parties at least annually. This proactively identifies and remediates potential vulnerabilities, maintaining the overall security posture of the platform.
softwareQualityAttributes: VE-REQ-NFR-012: **Reliability**: The system shall maintain an uptime of 99.9% excluding scheduled maintenance, ensuring continuous access to learning resources. This is crucial for an online learning platform where students and faculty depend on constant availability.

VE-REQ-NFR-013: **Usability**: The system shall feature an intuitive graphical user interface that allows first-time users to complete common tasks (e.g., sending a message, uploading a file) without requiring prior training. This minimizes the learning curve and promotes widespread adoption across diverse user groups.

VE-REQ-NFR-014: **Maintainability**: The system shall be developed using modular architecture and adhere to industry-standard coding practices, facilitating easy updates, bug fixes, and feature enhancements. This reduces long-term operational costs and ensures the system can evolve with changing educational needs.

VE-REQ-NFR-015: **Scalability**: The system shall be capable of accommodating a 50% increase in user base and concurrent activity without significant degradation in performance by utilizing cloud-based infrastructure and load balancing. This prepares the platform for future growth and increased demand without requiring major architectural overhauls.

VE-REQ-NFR-016: **Interoperability**: The system shall integrate seamlessly with existing NJIT student information systems for user authentication and course enrollment data exchange. This streamlines administrative processes and provides a unified experience for users.
businessRules: VE-RULE-001: All course content and communication within **virtual ed** must adhere to NJIT's academic integrity policies and codes of conduct.
VE-RULE-002: Student data, including grades and personal information, must be handled in compliance with **FERPA** regulations and university privacy policies.
VE-RULE-003: Professors retain full intellectual property rights over their original course materials and lectures hosted on **virtual ed**.
VE-RULE-004: All online tests administered through **virtual ed** must be designed and conducted to uphold fair assessment practices, with clear guidelines for proctoring and dispute resolution.

otherRequirements: VE-OTHER-001: The system shall support localization for date and time formats based on user preferences or geographical region, enhancing global usability.
VE-OTHER-002: The system shall provide comprehensive logging of user activity for auditing and troubleshooting purposes, while respecting user privacy settings.
VE-OTHER-003: The system shall integrate with the university's official notification system to send alerts regarding critical announcements or system outages.

glossary.definition: The name of the distance learning software system being developed for NJIT.
New Jersey Institute of Technology, the university for which the system is being developed.
The instant messaging client integrated within the **virtual ed** system for real-time text communication.
The file hosting and management module within **virtual ed** for uploading, sharing, and organizing documents.
The online testing and examination module within **virtual ed** for administering and grading academic assessments.
File Transfer Protocol, used for transferring files between a client and a server, specifically for file management in Virtual-Space.
Graphical User Interface, the visual part of the software system that allows users to interact with it.
Software Requirements Specification, a document that describes the requirements for a software system.
A small graphical user interface window that appears on top of other content, often used for specific system interactions or notifications.
A high-level, interpreted programming language primarily used for client-side scripting to create interactive web pages.
Digital audio or video files that are made available for download or streaming, typically as a series, for asynchronous consumption.

flowchartDiagram.syntaxExplanation: This flowchart illustrates the primary process flow within the **virtual ed** system, from user authentication through accessing various core functionalities. Nodes represent distinct steps or decisions, and arrows indicate transitions. Rectangular nodes signify processes, diamond nodes represent decisions, and rounded rectangles denote start/end points. All labels containing spaces are enclosed in double-quotes to ensure proper rendering.
flowchartDiagram.caption: Overall virtual ed System Process Flow

sequenceDiagram.syntaxExplanation: This sequence diagram illustrates the temporal interactions during an Instant Messaging (IM) session within the **virtual ed** platform. Participants (User, WebApp, IMChatService, Database) represent distinct entities, and arrows denote messages exchanged over time. The `->>` and `-->>` arrows signify synchronous and asynchronous messages, respectively. Activations are implicitly handled by the `+` and `-` on the arrows to denote start and end of activity duration.
sequenceDiagram.code:
sequenceDiagram
  participant User as "Client User"
  participant WebApp as "virtual ed Web App"
  participant IMChatService as "IM Chat Service"
  participant DB as Database

  User->>+WebApp: "Login Request"
  WebApp->>DB: "Authenticate User Credentials"
  DB-->>WebApp: "User Details / Auth Result"
  WebApp-->>-User: "Login Success / Dashboard"

  User->>+WebApp: "Open IM Client"
  WebApp->>+IMChatService: "Request Online Contacts (User ID)"
  IMChatService->>DB: "Query User Status & Contacts (User ID)"
  DB-->>IMChatService: "Online Contacts List"
  IMChatService-->>-WebApp: "Return Online Contacts"
  WebApp-->>-User: "Display Contact List"

  User->>+IMChatService: "Select Contact (Recipient ID)"
  IMChatService->>IMChatService: "Create Chat Session (Sender ID, Recipient ID)"
  IMChatService->>User: "Open Chat Window"
  IMChatService->>User as Recipient: "Notify Incoming Chat (Sender ID)"

  User->>IMChatService: "Send Message (Text)"
  IMChatService->>DB: "Log Message (Session ID, Sender ID, Text)"
  IMChatService->>Recipient: "Deliver Message (Text)"
sequenceDiagram.caption: Instant Messaging Session Sequence

entityRelationshipDiagram.syntaxExplanation: This Entity Relationship Diagram (ERD) models the core data entities and their relationships within the **virtual ed** system. Entities (rectangles) represent key data objects, and their attributes are listed below them, specifying data types and primary (PK), foreign (FK), or unique (UK) keys. Relationships (lines with crow's feet, circles, and vertical bars) indicate cardinality and participation. Relationship labels are enclosed in double-quotes. Attribute names are alphanumeric and not quoted, while entity names with spaces are quoted.
entityRelationshipDiagram.code:
erDiagram
  USER ||--o{ ENROLLMENT : "participates in"
  COURSE ||--o{ ENROLLMENT : "includes"
  USER ||--o{ COURSE : "teaches"
  USER ||--o{ CHAT_SESSION : "creates"
  CHAT_SESSION ||--o{ MESSAGE : "contains"
  USER ||--o{ MESSAGE : "sends"
  USER ||--o{ FOLDER : "manages"
  COURSE ||--o{ FOLDER : "hosts"
  FOLDER ||--o{ FILE : "stores"
  USER ||--o{ FILE : "uploads"
  COURSE ||--o{ VIRTUAL_EXAM : "administers"
  VIRTUAL_EXAM ||--o{ QUESTION : "has"
  USER ||--o{ EXAM_SUBMISSION : "submits"
  VIRTUAL_EXAM ||--o{ EXAM_SUBMISSION : "receives"

  USER {
    int id PK
    string email UK
    string password_hash
    string username
    text profile_data
    datetime last_login
  }

  COURSE {
    int id PK
    string name
    text description
    int instructor_id FK
    datetime created_date
  }

  ENROLLMENT {
    int user_id PK,FK
    int course_id PK,FK
    string role
    datetime enrollment_date
  }

  CHAT_SESSION {
    int id PK
    string name
    string type
    int created_by FK
  }

  MESSAGE {
    int id PK
    int sender_id FK
    int receiver_id FK
    int chat_session_id FK
    text content
    datetime timestamp
  }

  FOLDER {
    int id PK
    string name
    int created_by_id FK
    int course_id FK
    int parent_folder_id FK
  }

  FILE {
    int id PK
    string filename
    string filepath
    int filesize
    datetime upload_date
    int uploaded_by_id FK
    int course_id FK
    int parent_folder_id FK
  }

  VIRTUAL_EXAM {
    int id PK
    int course_id FK
    string title
    text description
    int duration_minutes
    datetime start_time
    datetime end_time
    int max_score
  }

  QUESTION {
    int id PK
    int exam_id FK
    text question_text
    string question_type
    text options
    text correct_answer
    int points
  }

  EXAM_SUBMISSION {
    int id PK
    int exam_id FK
    int student_id FK
    datetime submission_date
    int score
    json answers_data
  }
entityRelationshipDiagram.caption: virtual ed Entity Relationship Diagram

tbdList: VE-REQ-017: The specific handling mechanism for files causing quota overage in Virtual-Space V1 (allow and notify vs. prevent upload) requires clarification as there is conflicting information in the source text.

introduction.purpose: The primary purpose of this Software Requirements Specification (SRS) is to detail the functional and non-functional requirements for the **opensg 0.1** Enterprise Information Management (EIM) system. This document serves as a foundational blueprint, outlining the system's capabilities to manage, integrate, and share information within and beyond the Smart Grid ecosystem. It is designed to ensure a shared understanding among all stakeholders regarding the system's objectives and scope.
introduction.documentConventions: This document adheres strictly to the guidelines and recommendations set forth by the IEEE 830-1998 standard for Software Requirements Specifications. All requirements are systematically identified using a unique prefix, 'O0-REQ-', followed by a sequential number to ensure traceability and easy reference. Technical terms and acronyms are defined within the dedicated glossary section to maintain clarity and consistency throughout the specification.
introduction.intendedAudience: This SRS is intended for a diverse audience, including enterprise architects, system designers, software developers, quality assurance engineers, project managers, and business stakeholders. It provides a comprehensive reference for technical implementation details, quality assurance planning, and strategic business alignment. Each group will utilize this document to ensure their respective contributions align with the overall project vision and technical specifications.
introduction.productScope: The **opensg 0.1** system encompasses an Enterprise Information Management (EIM) framework designed to address the complexities of information handling in Smart Grid environments. Its scope includes defining a reference architecture, specifying business processes, outlining data models, and detailing technical infrastructure. The system aims to establish a consistent framework for interoperable information exchange, supporting various architectural views as defined by the TOGAF framework.
references: IEEE 830-1998 IEEE Recommended Practice for Software Requirements Specifications.
ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018 Systems and software engineering — Life cycle processes — Requirements engineering.
The Open Group Architecture Framework (TOGAF) 9.0.

overallDescription.productPerspective: The **opensg 0.1** system represents a new, self-contained product aimed at establishing a robust Enterprise Information Management framework within the Smart Grid domain. It serves as a critical enabler for interoperable Smart Grid operations, addressing existing challenges in data sharing and semantic consistency. This system is foundational, designed to support various architectural domains including Business, Data, Application, and Technology, laying the groundwork for future Smart Grid innovations and integrations.
productFunctions: Facilitate secure and standardized data model sharing with internal and external entities.
Provide unified management capabilities for Smart Grid, non-Smart Grid, and hybrid data types.
Support the definition and implementation of persistent data stores, incorporating industry standards like **IEC Common Information Model (CIM)**.
Enable the management of an enterprise semantic model, including common data concept definitions and aliases.
Oversee the technical architecture view, encompassing infrastructure components and analytics capabilities.
Provide resources and governance for an EIM Competency Center, fostering knowledge distribution and best practices.

userClassesAndCharacteristics.characteristics: These users possess deep knowledge of enterprise architecture principles, including **TOGAF**. They will primarily utilize **opensg 0.1** to define and validate architectural views, ensuring alignment with business strategy and technical standards. Their focus is on model consistency and architectural governance.

Data Stewards are responsible for data quality, governance, and semantic consistency. They will interact with the system to manage data models, define semantic concepts, and ensure data integrity across various data sources. A strong understanding of data modeling and standards like **IEC CIM** is expected.

These users develop and integrate applications that consume or produce data managed by **opensg 0.1**. They require clear APIs and service definitions for seamless interaction with the EIM system. Their technical expertise lies in software development and integration patterns.

Operators require reliable and timely access to Smart Grid operational data to monitor grid status, identify anomalies, and manage resources effectively. While not direct configurators, their operational needs drive many of the data integration and availability requirements of the system.

overallDescription.operatingEnvironment: The **opensg 0.1** system is anticipated to operate within a distributed enterprise IT environment, supporting both cloud-native and on-premises deployments. It will interact with various operating systems, including **Linux** and **Windows Server**, and utilize virtualization technologies for scalable deployment. Compatibility with modern container orchestration platforms such as **Kubernetes** is a key consideration for its operational flexibility.
designAndImplementationConstraints: The system shall adhere to the architectural guiding principles derived from **TOGAF 9.0** for all architectural views (Business, Data, Application, Technology).

All data models within the system shall be designed to be compatible with, and incorporate, the **IEC Common Information Model (CIM)** to ensure semantic interoperability within the Smart Grid domain.

The system's security architecture shall be intrinsically integrated into all architectural components and processes, rather than being treated as a separate, isolated module, as mandated by the EIM Task Force.

The choice of database technologies must support highly available, scalable, and secure persistent data storage for both structured and semi-structured data, accommodating collocated Smart Grid and non-Smart Grid data.

The system must integrate with existing enterprise middleware and communication protocols, defining clear patterns for interfacing new technologies with legacy systems without compromising data integrity or security.
userDocumentation: The **opensg 0.1** system shall be delivered with comprehensive user manuals detailing configuration, administration, and operational procedures for all EIM components.
Online help and context-sensitive tutorials shall be provided for key functionalities, guiding users through complex data model creation, management, and sharing processes.
Developer guides and API documentation will be made available, outlining the interfaces and services offered by the EIM system for integration with other applications.
assumptionsAndDependencies: The development of **opensg 0.1** assumes the availability of skilled personnel proficient in enterprise architecture, data modeling, and Smart Grid technologies. Training programs may be required for new team members.

The system is dependent on the stability and adherence to specifications of external standards, particularly **TOGAF 9.0** and the **IEC Common Information Model (CIM)**, for its architectural integrity and interoperability.

It is assumed that the underlying IT infrastructure, including network connectivity and basic security services, will be adequately provisioned and maintained to support the operational requirements of the EIM system.

externalInterfaceRequirements.userInterfaces: The user interfaces for **opensg 0.1** will primarily consist of web-based **Admin Dashboards** and **Data Steward Portals**, designed for enterprise architects and data stewards. These interfaces shall provide intuitive graphical tools for defining, visualizing, and managing data models, semantic concepts, and architectural views. Emphasis will be placed on adherence to modern web accessibility standards and responsive design principles, ensuring usability across various display devices and resolutions. Standard UI components like navigation menus, search bars, and configurable widgets will be consistently applied.
externalInterfaceRequirements.hardwareInterfaces: The **opensg 0.1** system shall logically interface with various Smart Grid hardware components, including smart meters, sensors, and remote terminal units (RTUs), primarily through intermediary data collection systems. Direct hardware interaction is not within the scope of this core EIM system, but it relies on standardized data streams originating from these devices. Communication with underlying server hardware will be managed via standard operating system interfaces and network protocols, ensuring efficient resource utilization and reliable data transfer for persistent data stores.
externalInterfaceRequirements.softwareInterfaces: The **opensg 0.1** system will expose a suite of **REST API**s for integration with other enterprise applications, databases, and third-party tools. Key interfaces include those for publishing and consuming data models, retrieving information objects, and managing semantic concepts. Compatibility with **PostgreSQL** and other industry-standard databases is required for persistent data storage. Furthermore, it will integrate with message queuing systems like **Apache Kafka** or **RabbitMQ** to facilitate asynchronous data exchange with Smart Grid operational systems and analytical platforms.
externalInterfaceRequirements.communicationsInterfaces: The **opensg 0.1** system shall leverage standard **HTTP/S** protocols for all web-based user interactions and **REST API** communications, ensuring secure and encrypted data transfer. For internal service-to-service communication and data streaming, it will utilize robust message queuing protocols such as **AMQP** or **MQTT**, particularly for handling high-volume Smart Grid data. All communication interfaces must incorporate **TLS/SSL** for encryption and support **OAuth2** or **JWT** for authentication and authorization, adhering to enterprise security policies and data privacy regulations.

systemFeatures.name: Enterprise Model Sharing
systemFeatures.description: This feature enables the secure and standardized sharing of data models with various internal and external entities, including business-to-business (B2B) partners and consumer (B2C) applications. It is crucial for fostering collaboration and ensuring semantic interoperability across the **opensg 0.1** ecosystem. The capability facilitates the widespread distribution and consistent interpretation of architectural and data definitions, making it fundamental for integrated Smart Grid operations. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Enterprise architect requests to publish a validated data model to an external partner. Response: The system initiates a secure transfer process, making the model accessible to authorized external entities while maintaining version control.

Stimulus: A consumer-facing application attempts to retrieve a B2C-specific subset of a data model. Response: The system provides the requested model subset via a controlled interface, applying predefined access policies and data transformation rules.
functionalRequirements: O0-REQ-001: The system shall allow authorized users to publish and share data models securely with external business entities.
O0-REQ-002: The system shall support standardized exchange formats for data models in business-to-business (B2B) scenarios.
O0-REQ-003: The system shall provide controlled access mechanisms for consumer (B2C) applications to retrieve specific data model subsets.
O0-REQ-004: The system shall maintain an audit log of all data model sharing activities, including access requests and successful transfers.

systemFeatures.name: Unified Information Management
systemFeatures.description: This feature provides comprehensive capabilities for managing diverse information types, specifically addressing common enterprise data, Smart Grid-specific data, and hybrid data sets that combine both. It ensures a consistent approach to data governance, lifecycle management, and access across the **opensg 0.1** environment. This unified management approach is essential for deriving holistic insights and maintaining data integrity throughout complex Smart Grid operations. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: A data steward initiates the onboarding process for a new Smart Grid data stream from an operational sensor. Response: The system guides the steward through data source configuration, schema mapping, and initial validation against existing Smart Grid data models.

Stimulus: An analytical query is issued requesting aggregated performance metrics from both traditional IT systems and Smart Grid infrastructure. Response: The system retrieves, harmonizes, and presents the data from various underlying repositories, providing a unified view for analysis.
functionalRequirements: O0-REQ-005: The system shall provide CRUD (Create, Read, Update, Delete) capabilities for all managed information objects.
O0-REQ-006: The system shall manage data specific to Smart Grid operations, including real-time telemetry and control commands.
O0-REQ-007: The system shall manage general non-Smart Grid enterprise data.
O0-REQ-008: The system shall support the seamless integration and management of data objects containing both Smart Grid and non-Smart Grid attributes.

systemFeatures.name: EIM Data Architecture Definition
systemFeatures.description: This feature supports the meticulous definition and practical implementation of the **opensg 0.1** data architecture, encompassing requirements for persistent data stores, the strategic use of both localized and centralized storage, and the crucial incorporation of industry standards such as the **IEC Common Information Model (CIM)**. It ensures data consistency, semantic fidelity, and architectural alignment across the entire enterprise, which is paramount for a robust Smart Grid infrastructure. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: An architect specifies a new requirement for a persistent data store to archive historical Smart Grid event data. Response: The system allows the architect to define storage characteristics, retention policies, and linkages to the relevant data models for this new store.

Stimulus: A system administrator configures a messaging endpoint to enable **IEC CIM**-based communication with a new utility application. Response: The system applies predefined **CIM** transformation rules to incoming and outgoing messages, ensuring compliance and semantic interoperability for the communication channel.
functionalRequirements: O0-REQ-009: The system shall enable the definition and management of persistent data store architectural requirements.
O0-REQ-010: The system shall support persistent data stores capable of accommodating collocated Smart Grid and non-Smart Grid data.
O0-REQ-011: The system shall facilitate the use of localized data stores for specific business units or application domains.
O0-REQ-012: The system shall support the utilization of centralized data stores for enterprise-wide data governance and access.
O0-REQ-013: The system shall provide mechanisms for incorporating standard models, including the **IEC Common Information Model (CIM)**, into the enterprise information management framework.
O0-REQ-014: The system shall enable **IEC CIM**-based messaging for interoperable data exchange.
O0-REQ-015: The system shall support the creation and management of **IEC CIM**-based persistent data store schemas.

systemFeatures.name: Enterprise Semantic Model Management
systemFeatures.description: This feature manages the enterprise semantic model by rigorously defining common data concepts and enabling the creation and management of aliases, thereby ensuring robust semantic interoperability across diverse systems within **opensg 0.1**. It provides a structured and guided approach to both initiating and maintaining enterprise-wide semantic consistency, which is critical for accurate data interpretation and integration in complex Smart Grid environments. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: A data architect defines a new common data concept, 'CustomerMeterPoint', within the enterprise semantic model. Response: The system records the definition, validates its uniqueness, and makes it available for mapping and cross-referencing across various data models and business units.

Stimulus: A business analyst from the billing department seeks to reconcile a local term, 'ConsumptionMeterID', with the standardized 'CustomerMeterPoint' concept. Response: The system assists the analyst in identifying 'ConsumptionMeterID' as an alias for 'CustomerMeterPoint', facilitating alignment and consistent data reporting.
functionalRequirements: O0-REQ-016: The information model shall define common definitions of data concepts for the enterprise.
O0-REQ-017: The information model shall allow for the creation and management of aliases for defined data concepts.
O0-REQ-018: The system shall provide tools and processes to initiate and maintain enterprise semantic management.
O0-REQ-019: The system shall utilize a defined reference architecture to create and evolve the enterprise information architecture.
O0-REQ-020: The system shall explicitly model how semantic modeling supports and enhances information modeling activities.
O0-REQ-021: The system shall define and document patterns for introducing business units to Smart Grid semantic modeling principles and practices.

systemFeatures.name: EIM Technical Architecture View Management
systemFeatures.description: This feature is dedicated to the comprehensive management of the technical architecture view for **opensg 0.1**, meticulously defining essential infrastructure components such as the metadata repository and advanced analytics capabilities. It explicitly supports the specification of how various services interact to enable seamless, end-to-end **opensg 0.1** business processes, including crucial patterns for integrating cutting-edge technologies with existing legacy systems. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: A technical architect identifies the need to integrate a new real-time analytics engine into the EIM framework. Response: The system allows the architect to specify the engine's components, integration points, and dependencies within the technical architecture, ensuring proper alignment.

Stimulus: A developer requires guidance on integrating a legacy SCADA system with the modern EIM platform using new interface technologies. Response: The system provides access to predefined integration patterns and best practices for bridging disparate systems, including code examples and configuration templates.
functionalRequirements: O0-REQ-022: The system shall define and manage core infrastructure components, including a centralized metadata repository.
O0-REQ-023: The system shall incorporate and manage various analytics capabilities within the EIM framework.
O0-REQ-024: The system shall define and document how services interact to support end-to-end EIM business processes.
O0-REQ-025: The system shall provide support for process-oriented information perspectives. (TBD: O0-TBD-001 - Resolve EIM support for process-oriented information perspectives)
O0-REQ-026: The system shall include self-healing and self-discovery capabilities for dynamic adaptation. (TBD: O0-TBD-002 - Include self healing and self discovery capabilities)
O0-REQ-027: The system shall define patterns for using new technologies to create interfaces with older systems.

systemFeatures.name: EIM Competency Center Facilitation
systemFeatures.description: This feature provides essential support for the functions of an **opensg 0.1** Competency Center, specifically by enabling the definition of robust governance structures, facilitating resource management, and ensuring effective knowledge distribution. This encompasses introducing critical Smart Grid EIM lessons learned, documenting data movement patterns and tools, and actively enhancing the organization's collective ability to create, maintain, and reuse logical data models. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: A Competency Center manager needs to document new best practices derived from a Smart Grid EIM pilot project. Response: The system provides a structured platform for capturing, categorizing, and publishing these lessons learned, making them searchable and accessible across the organization.

Stimulus: A data modeler is searching for reusable logical data models to accelerate a new project initiative. Response: The system provides access to a searchable repository of approved and version-controlled logical data models, complete with usage guidelines and associated documentation.
functionalRequirements: O0-REQ-028: The system shall provide tools for defining and managing EIM governance structures and policies.
O0-REQ-029: The system shall facilitate the distribution of EIM knowledge, resources, and best practices.
O0-REQ-030: The system shall support the introduction and dissemination of Smart Grid EIM lessons to relevant organizational units.
O0-REQ-031: The system shall document and categorize various data movement patterns.
O0-REQ-032: The system shall introduce and document data movement tools and methodologies.
O0-REQ-033: The system shall provide mechanisms for enhancing the organization’s ability to create, maintain, and reuse logical data models.

performanceRequirements: O0-REQ-034: The system shall support a minimum throughput of 10,000 data model metadata queries per second with a latency not exceeding 500 milliseconds for 95% of requests. This is crucial to ensure architects and data stewards can efficiently access model definitions without significant delays, supporting dynamic design and operational decisions.

O0-REQ-035: The data ingestion subsystem for Smart Grid data shall process real-time streams at a rate of 50,000 events per second, with an end-to-end processing latency of less than 2 seconds for critical operational data. This ensures the system can handle the high volume and velocity of sensor data, which is essential for timely grid monitoring and control actions.
safetyRequirements: O0-REQ-036: The system shall implement robust data validation and integrity checks to prevent the propagation of erroneous or corrupted Smart Grid data into persistent stores. This is critical to maintain the reliability of grid operations, as faulty data could lead to incorrect decisions and potential system instabilities.

O0-REQ-037: The system shall incorporate fault tolerance mechanisms, including redundant components and automatic failover, to ensure continuous operation and data availability in the event of hardware or software failures. Uninterrupted access to enterprise information and Smart Grid data is paramount for operational safety and regulatory compliance.
securityRequirements: O0-REQ-038: The system shall enforce role-based access control (RBAC) for all EIM functions, ensuring that users can only perform actions and access data for which they have explicit authorization. This prevents unauthorized modification or disclosure of sensitive enterprise and Smart Grid information, adhering to the principle of least privilege.

O0-REQ-039: All data, both in transit and at rest, shall be encrypted using industry-standard algorithms (e.g., TLS 1.3 for transit, AES-256 for at-rest storage). This protects sensitive Smart Grid operational data and confidential enterprise information from eavesdropping and unauthorized access, fulfilling critical data privacy and security mandates.

O0-REQ-040: The system shall provide comprehensive audit logging of all significant user actions, data access, and system configuration changes. These logs must be immutable and centrally stored to support forensic analysis, compliance audits, and detection of security incidents.
softwareQualityAttributes: O0-REQ-041: Interoperability: The system shall support widely recognized industry standards such as **IEC Common Information Model (CIM)** and **TOGAF** for data modeling and architectural definitions, enabling seamless integration with existing and future Smart Grid systems. This ensures the system can exchange and interpret information effectively with diverse external entities.

O0-REQ-042: Reliability: The system shall achieve an uptime of 99.95% during operational hours, ensuring continuous availability of critical EIM services and Smart Grid data. This is vital for maintaining consistent business operations and supporting real-time decision-making in the Smart Grid.

O0-REQ-043: Scalability: The system shall be capable of scaling horizontally to accommodate a 50% increase in data volume and user concurrency within a six-month period without significant performance degradation. This adaptability ensures the system can evolve with the growing demands of Smart Grid expansion and increasing data loads.

O0-REQ-044: Maintainability: The system's codebase shall adhere to established coding standards, comprehensive documentation, and modular architecture to facilitate ease of maintenance, bug fixing, and feature enhancements. This minimizes the total cost of ownership and ensures the longevity of the EIM platform.
