O0-REQ-045: Usability: The user interfaces for data architects and stewards shall be intuitive and provide clear visual feedback, minimizing training requirements and reducing the likelihood of operational errors. An effective user experience is critical for efficient data model and semantic concept management.
businessRules: O0-REQ-046: All data models developed and managed within the **opensg 0.1** system shall conform to the semantic definitions established by the enterprise semantic model, ensuring consistency across all integrated systems.

O0-REQ-047: Data sharing agreements with external entities shall be digitally documented and verifiable within the system, adhering to all legal and regulatory compliance requirements for data exchange.

O0-REQ-048: Changes to core data definitions or architectural patterns must follow a defined governance workflow, requiring approval from designated data stewards or architectural review boards before deployment to production.

glossary.definition: Common Information Model: An open standard for describing electrical power systems, facilitating the exchange of information between different applications.
Enterprise Information Management: A discipline that manages the full information lifecycle, from creation to disposal, across an enterprise.
International Electrotechnical Commission: A global organization that prepares and publishes international standards for all electrical, electronic, and related technologies.

National Institute of Standards and Technology: A physical sciences laboratory and non-regulatory agency of the United States Department of Commerce, promoting innovation and industrial competitiveness.
Role-Based Access Control: A method of restricting system access to authorized users based on their roles within an organization.
Software Requirements Specification: A document that describes the requirements for a software system.

The Open Group Architecture Framework: A framework for enterprise architecture that provides a comprehensive approach to the design, planning, implementation, and governance of an enterprise information architecture.
Utility Communications Architecture International User Group: An international user group promoting utility enterprise integration and interoperability through the deployment of open standards.

flowchartDiagram.syntaxExplanation: This flowchart illustrates the core process for integrating a new data model into the **opensg 0.1** EIM system. Nodes represent steps or decisions, and arrows indicate the flow of control. Decision points are diamond-shaped, and processes are rectangular. The direction of flow is from top to bottom.
flowchartDiagram.code:
flowchart TD
  StartProcess["Initiate Model Integration"] --> A1["Submit New Data Model"]
  A1 --> A2["Validate Model Syntax"]
  A2 --> D1{"Is Model Standard Compliant?"}
  D1 -- "No" --> A3["Rectify Model & Re-validate"]
  A3 --> A2
  D1 -- "Yes" --> A4["Integrate with Enterprise Semantic Model"]
  A4 --> A5["Perform Data Mapping & Transformation"]
  A5 --> D2{"Mapping Validated?"}
  D2 -- "No" --> A5
  D2 -- "Yes" --> A6["Publish Model to Metadata Repository"]
  A6 --> EndProcess["Model Integrated & Published"]
flowchartDiagram.caption: O0_FLOW_001: EIM Data Model Integration Process

sequenceDiagram.syntaxExplanation: This sequence diagram depicts the high-level interaction flow for Smart Grid Data Ingestion within the **opensg 0.1** system. Participants represent system components or external entities, and arrows indicate messages exchanged between them. Activation bars show the period of active processing for a participant.
sequenceDiagram.code:
sequenceDiagram
  participant SGD as SmartGridDevice
  participant MDC as "Message Data Collector"
  participant EVS as "EIM Validation Service"
  participant ESS as "EIM Semantic Service"
  participant PDS as "Persistent Data Store"

  SGD->>+MDC: "Send Real-time Telemetry Data"
  MDC->>+EVS: "Forward Data for Validation"
  EVS-->>-MDC: "Validation Status (OK/Error)"

  alt Valid Data
    MDC->>+ESS: "Forward Valid Data for Enrichment"
    ESS->>+PDS: "Store Enriched Data"
    PDS-->>-ESS: "Storage Confirmation"
    ESS-->>-MDC: "Enrichment & Storage Success"
  else Invalid Data
    MDC->>MDC: "Log Data Ingestion Error"
    MDC->>ESS: "Notify Error & Discard Data"
  end

  MDC-->>-SGD: "Data Ingestion Acknowledgment"
sequenceDiagram.caption: O0_SEQ_001: Smart Grid Data Ingestion Flow

entityRelationshipDiagram.syntaxExplanation: This Entity Relationship Diagram (ERD) illustrates the core entities and their relationships within the **opensg 0.1** EIM system. Entities are depicted as boxes with attributes, and relationships are shown with cardinality notations. Attributes are listed with their types and key constraints (PK for Primary Key, FK for Foreign Key, UK for Unique Key). Relationship labels are strictly quoted.
entityRelationshipDiagram.caption: O0_ERD_001: Core EIM Data Entities

tbdList: O0-TBD-001: Resolve EIM support for process-oriented information perspectives (Ref: O0-REQ-025)
O0-TBD-002: Include self healing and self discovery capabilities for dynamic adaptation (Ref: O0-REQ-026)

introduction.purpose: This Software Requirements Specification (SRS) precisely details the qualification requirements for **themas**, The Energy Management System. It furnishes a comprehensive technical description of all software requirements pertinent to the system, establishes traceability for software capability requirements against the Statement of Work, and meticulously identifies all external interfaces. This document is specifically structured for utilization by the Design Requirements team, the Principle Software Architect, and all other stakeholders with an interest in the development and ongoing progression of **themas**.
introduction.documentConventions: This document adheres to the standards outlined in the IEEE 830-1998 Recommended Practice for Software Requirements Specifications. All requirements are delineated using imperative language, specifically employing 'shall' to denote binding conditions. Terminology and abbreviations are defined within Section 1.3, ensuring clarity and consistency throughout the specification. Specific identifiers for requirements, such as T-REQ-XXX, are used to facilitate traceability and management of individual stipulations.
introduction.intendedAudience: The primary audience for this Software Requirements Specification includes the Design Requirements team, who will utilize it as the foundational blueprint for architectural and detailed design. The Principle Software Architect will leverage this document to ensure design integrity and compliance. Furthermore, any other parties involved in the development, quality assurance, or project management phases of **themas** will find this document indispensable for understanding the system's intended capabilities and constraints.
introduction.productScope: The scope of this document is rigorously confined to detailing solely the software requirements for the **themas** system, excluding any hardware-specific stipulations which are handled in the overarching system specification. Only statements explicitly using the imperative 'shall' are to be interpreted as non-negotiable, binding requirements within this SRS. This document provides an exhaustive definition of all internal system requirements but intentionally refrains from providing granular details regarding the precise external systems that will interface with **themas**. Additionally, this SRS outlines the requirements for a simulated thermostat prototype, essential for the verification and validation of **themas**'s overall reliability, forming the cornerstone for subsequent software design activities.
references: IEEE 830-1998 IEEE Recommended Practice for Software Requirements Specifications.
ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018 Systems and software engineering — Life cycle processes — Requirements engineering.
THEMAS_SOW_001 Statement of Work.

overallDescription.productPerspective: The **themas** system is conceived as an independent operational entity, designed to function autonomously from other systems and from the intrinsic components of the heating and cooling (H/C) infrastructure to which it is connected. This self-contained characteristic ensures minimized external dependencies for its core logic. The architecture of **themas** is fundamentally divided into distinct hardware and software components, delineating clear boundaries for development and maintenance responsibilities. This SRS is exclusively dedicated to specifying the stringent requirements governing the software portion, with any underlying assumptions or critical dependencies related to the hardware articulated within this document to provide comprehensive context.
productFunctions: Monitor Temperature: Continuously receives temperature data, validates it, and determines system status based on defined thresholds.
Determine Utilization: Manages the operational status of H/C units, processes requests, and ensures efficient resource allocation within defined limits.
Initialize System: Establishes the system's initial operational state by loading parameters and bringing H/C units to a known configuration.
System Reports: Collects and stores event data, enabling supervisors to generate reports for system analysis and maintenance.

userClassesAndCharacteristics.characteristics: The System Supervisor is the primary user, responsible for maintaining the heating and cooling systems within a building. This user requires access to system status, warnings regarding faulty temperatures, and performance reports. The supervisor is expected to have a foundational understanding of building climate control but should not require extensive technical expertise specific to **themas**. The system's interface and reporting mechanisms are designed to present critical information clearly and concisely, avoiding excessive detail that could lead to confusion. Their interaction primarily involves monitoring and responding to system-generated alerts or reports, as well as making adjustments to thermostat settings when necessary.

overallDescription.operatingEnvironment: The **themas** system is mandated to operate on the **Microsoft Windows NT** operating system, establishing a fundamental platform constraint for its deployment and execution. All internal process communications within the software architecture are designed and optimized for this specific operating system, ensuring seamless interaction and data exchange between modules. External communications with connected thermostats and heating/cooling units are facilitated through dedicated hardware interfaces, which are also required to function compatibly within the **Windows NT** environment. This dependency on a specific operating system influences system design choices, including threading models, inter-process communication mechanisms, and peripheral device drivers.
designAndImplementationConstraints: The **themas** system is constrained by the inherent limitations and feedback mechanisms of the external hardware devices. Thermostats are only capable of providing current temperature values and desired temperature settings, without additional diagnostic or status feedback. Heating and cooling units offer no direct operational feedback to **themas**; once a command signal is transmitted, the system cannot independently verify if the unit has successfully realized the command. This necessitates design considerations that account for inferring unit status based on temperature responses and predefined operational parameters. The system is also required to run exclusively on the **Microsoft Windows NT** operating system, dictating technology stack choices and development practices for core system components and interface drivers.
userDocumentation: The **themas** system shall be delivered with a comprehensive User Manual, providing detailed instructions on system operation, configuration, and troubleshooting for the System Supervisor. An online help system shall be integrated within the supervisor interface, offering context-sensitive assistance for various functions and reports. Additionally, a series of quick-start guides will be provided to facilitate rapid onboarding for new supervisors, covering essential monitoring and configuration tasks. All documentation will adhere to established internal corporate guidelines for technical writing and formatting.
assumptionsAndDependencies: A fundamental assumption is that the thermostat hardware consistently and without significant real-time delay provides accurate current temperature and desired temperature settings to the **themas** system. These thermostats are also assumed to possess the capability of being remotely controlled and configured by authorized users of the **themas** system, responding reliably to setting changes. Furthermore, all data transmitted from the thermostats is assumed to be pre-formatted correctly, eliminating the need for extensive data transformation within **themas**. Conversely, the heating/cooling units are assumed to lack the ability to return their current operational (ON/OFF) status to **themas**, requiring the system to manage and infer their states based on commands issued and environmental feedback. The units are expected to respond to ON/OFF commands without real-time delay.

externalInterfaceRequirements.userInterfaces: The primary user interface for **themas** shall be a dedicated **Supervisor Dashboard**, accessible via a standard **Windows NT** desktop application. This interface shall provide a clear, real-time overview of the overall building's temperature status, individual thermostat readings, and the operational status of all heating and cooling units. The layout will adhere to a consistent graphical user interface (GUI) standard, featuring intuitive controls for navigating reports, adjusting thermostat settings, and acknowledging system alarms. Critical system warnings and error messages will be displayed prominently, utilizing a consistent color scheme and messaging standard to ensure immediate recognition and understanding by the supervisor. User input mechanisms, such as keyboard shortcuts and mouse interactions, will be optimized for efficiency in a control room environment.
externalInterfaceRequirements.hardwareInterfaces: The **themas** system shall interface with various hardware components, including digital thermostats and heating/cooling (H/C) units. Communication with thermostats will involve receiving real-time temperature data and transmitting updated temperature settings. The interface will support multiple thermostat devices, each with a unique identifier. For H/C units, **themas** will transmit control signals to switch units ON or OFF; however, no feedback mechanism from the units to confirm signal receipt or operational status is expected. These hardware interactions will be managed through a dedicated hardware abstraction layer within **themas**, designed to accommodate specific communication protocols, potentially involving serial or network-based connections, as dictated by the hardware vendors. The system must be robust to intermittent communication issues with these external devices.
externalInterfaceRequirements.softwareInterfaces: The **themas** system will integrate with an internal event logging **database** for persistent storage of all system events and operational data, enabling historical analysis and report generation. This database interface shall utilize industry-standard protocols, likely **ODBC** or **JDBC** given the **Windows NT** environment, to ensure reliable data persistence and retrieval. Initialization parameters, including definitions for H/C units, thermostats, utilization limits, trigger values, and overtemperature thresholds, will be loaded from structured initialization files. These files will be processed during system startup and configuration, requiring a robust file parsing mechanism within **themas**. Data sharing between internal software components, such as the Monitor Temperature and Determine Utilization modules, will occur via shared memory or message queues managed by the **Windows NT** operating system, optimizing performance for real-time operations.
externalInterfaceRequirements.communicationsInterfaces: The communication interfaces for **themas** are primarily focused on reliable, low-latency data exchange with attached thermostat and heating/cooling hardware. Specific communication protocols, such as **RS-485** or a proprietary **Modbus** variant, are anticipated for these interactions, ensuring robust data integrity over potentially long distances within a building. The system will handle periodic polling of thermostat devices for current temperature and setting values. Control signals to H/C units will be transmitted as discrete ON/OFF commands, requiring specific electrical signaling or packet structures. All communication data will adhere to defined message formats, although specific encryption for internal hardware communication is not initially mandated, ensuring minimal overhead. Any network communications, such as for remote access (if introduced in future phases), would utilize **TCP/IP** with appropriate security layers like **TLS**.

systemFeatures.name: Initialize Operational Parameters
systemFeatures.description: This feature is paramount for establishing the foundational configuration of the **themas** system upon startup, ensuring all operational limits and definitions are correctly loaded. It directly enhances system reliability and consistency by ensuring that critical parameters, such as heating/cooling unit definitions, thermostat identifiers, utilization thresholds, temperature triggers, overtemperature limits, and valid temperature ranges, are derived from a single, authoritative source. From a technical standpoint, this involves robust file parsing and validation mechanisms to ingest initialization data, which then populates internal data structures, safeguarding the integrity of subsequent system operations. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: System initiates startup sequence. Response: **themas** begins loading operational parameters.

Stimulus: Initialization Data file is parsed by the system. Response: **themas** extracts H/C unit definitions, thermostat identifiers, utilization parameters, trigger values, overtemperature values, and valid temperature ranges.
Stimulus: All required parameters are successfully loaded. Response: **themas** makes operational parameters available for other system components.
functionalRequirements: The system shall load heating/cooling unit definitions from an initialization file.
The system shall identify one heating/cooling unit corresponding to one thermostat in each of four quadrants across three floors.
The system shall load unique thermostat identifiers from an initialization file for all monitored thermostats.
The system shall load the maximum number of concurrently running heating or cooling units from an initialization file.
The system shall establish upper and lower temperature trigger values (LT, UT) based on initialization data.
The system shall establish lower and upper overtemperature values (LO, UO) based on initialization data, ensuring a maximum deviation of 3 degrees Fahrenheit.
The system shall establish a valid temperature range (min, max) from initialization data.

systemFeatures.name: Initialize System State
systemFeatures.description: The Initialize System State feature ensures that **themas** always begins its operational cycle from a controlled and known configuration, which is critical for preventing erroneous H/C unit behavior. This directly benefits system stability and the efficiency of energy management by bringing all controlled units to a uniform 'off' state before assessing individual zone needs. Technically, this involves issuing commands to all connected H/C units, followed by a comprehensive polling of all thermostats to determine their current conditions. Subsequently, it intelligently re-activates necessary H/C units based on the latest readings and pre-defined operational rules, thereby ensuring immediate environmental comfort while maintaining initial system control. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: System initialization is triggered (e.g., power-on, restart). Response: **themas** issues commands to turn off all heating and cooling units.
Stimulus: All H/C units are confirmed off (conceptually, as no feedback). Response: **themas** reads current temperature values and settings from all thermostats.
Stimulus: Thermostat readings indicate a need for heating or cooling. Response: **themas** determines the appropriate H/C unit and initiates a request to turn it on.
Stimulus: All thermostats have been evaluated. Response: **themas** transitions to active monitoring, with relevant H/C units potentially engaged.
functionalRequirements: The system shall first issue a command to turn off all heating and cooling units upon initialization.
The system shall read the current temperature values and setpoints from all thermostats after H/C units are off.
The system shall determine if any thermostat's settings require a heating or cooling unit to be turned on.
The system shall issue a request to turn on appropriate H/C units based on current readings and operational parameters during initialization.
The system shall determine H/C unit activation needs according to rules defined in T-REQ-009 and T-REQ-010.

systemFeatures.name: Validate Temperature Data
systemFeatures.description: This feature provides a critical quality assurance step by ensuring that all temperature data received from thermostats falls within an acceptable and reasonable operational range, thereby preventing spurious or faulty sensor readings from negatively impacting system behavior. It directly improves the reliability of **themas** by filtering out invalid data, safeguarding against erroneous heating or cooling activations. From a technical perspective, this involves comparing incoming current temperature values and temperature settings against predefined minimum and maximum valid range parameters. Any data falling outside this range is flagged, preventing its use in subsequent decision-making processes and ensuring system stability. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: **themas** receives a current temperature value from a thermostat. Response: **themas** compares the value to the valid temperature range.
Stimulus: Current temperature value is outside the valid range. Response: **themas** identifies and outputs an invalid temperature status.
Stimulus: Current temperature value is within the valid range. Response: **themas** identifies and outputs a valid temperature status.
Stimulus: **themas** receives a temperature setting value from a thermostat. Response: **themas** compares the setting to the valid temperature range.
Stimulus: Temperature setting value is outside the valid range. Response: **themas** identifies and outputs an invalid temperature status.
Stimulus: Temperature setting value is within the valid range. Response: **themas** accepts and outputs the valid temperature setting.
functionalRequirements: The system shall only respond to temperature values from thermostats that are within the specified valid range.
The system shall compare a received current temperature value to the lower and upper bounds of the valid temperature range.
The system shall identify a current temperature value as invalid if it is strictly less than the lower range value or strictly greater than the upper range value.
The system shall output an invalid temperature status if the current temperature is outside the valid range.
The system shall output a valid temperature status if the current temperature is within the valid range.
The system shall compare a received temperature setting value to the lower and upper bounds of the valid temperature range.
The system shall identify a temperature setting value as invalid if it is strictly less than the lower range value or strictly greater than the upper range value.
The system shall output an invalid temperature status if the temperature setting is outside the valid range.
The system shall realize and use a temperature setting value if it is within the valid range.

systemFeatures.name: Monitor Temperature and Determine H/C Mode
systemFeatures.description: This critical feature continuously monitors environmental conditions and makes real-time decisions on whether to engage heating or cooling, ensuring optimal comfort and energy efficiency. It directly provides user value by maintaining desired temperatures within acceptable fluctuations, proactively responding to changes. Technically, it involves calculating temperature deltas against setpoints and trigger values, subsequently determining if an H/C unit needs to be activated or deactivated. This feature also includes logic for identifying overtemperature conditions, which trigger alarm events to notify the supervisor of potential issues, ensuring system safety and responsiveness. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: **themas** receives valid temperature data (current temp, setpoint, trigger, overtemp values). Response: **themas** determines the temperature status relative to overtemperature limits.
Stimulus: Reported temperature exceeds overtemperature limits. Response: **themas** outputs a 'Temperature Limit Exceeded' status.
Stimulus: Reported temperature is within overtemperature limits. Response: **themas** outputs the current 'Temperature Trigger Exceeded' status.
Stimulus: Temperature Trigger Exceeded status is received. Response: **themas** determines the appropriate H/C mode (turn on heat/cool, or turn off both).
Stimulus: Current temperature is within the satisfaction range (LT <= T <= UT). Response: **themas** outputs a request to turn off both heating and cooling units.

Stimulus: Current temperature is outside the satisfaction range but within overtemp limits (LO <= T < LT or UT < T <= UO). Response: **themas** outputs a request to turn on the appropriate heating (T < LT) or cooling (T > UT) unit.
functionalRequirements: The system shall determine when a reported temperature or changed temperature setting exceeds overtemperature values.
The system shall define Lower Overtemperature Value (LO) as TSET - OD and Upper Overtemperature Value (UO) as TSET + OD.
The system shall recognize a 'Temperature Limit Exceeded' condition if the current temperature (T) is less than LO or greater than UO.
The system shall output 'Temperature Limit Exceeded' if the condition is met.
The system shall output 'Temperature Trigger Exceeded' if LO <= T <= UO.
The system shall activate the appropriate heating or cooling unit when the current temperature exceeds the current temperature setting by a predefined amount.
The system shall define Lower Trigger Value (LT) as TSET - TD and Upper Trigger Value (UT) as TSET + TD.
The system shall output a request to turn off both heating and cooling units if LT <= T <= UT.
The system shall output a request to turn on the heating unit if LO <= T < LT.
The system shall output a request to turn on the cooling unit if UT < T <= UO.

systemFeatures.name: Determine Utilization and Generate H/C Request/Event
systemFeatures.description: This feature manages the active state of all heating and cooling units, enforcing operational limits to prevent system overload and ensuring efficient energy consumption. It provides value by optimizing resource allocation and preventing excessive simultaneous unit operation. Technically, it maintains the ON/OFF status of each H/C unit and processes incoming requests against a maximum concurrency limit. If a request cannot be immediately honored, it is queued using a Last-In, First-Out (LIFO) mechanism, and a 'Unit Unavailable' event is generated for logging. This ensures that operational decisions are made within predefined constraints while providing clear auditing of resource contention. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: **themas** receives an H/C request (ON/OFF). Response: **themas** determines if the request can be honored based on utilization parameters.
Stimulus: Request to turn on an H/C unit is received, and maximum units are already running. Response: **themas** adds the request to a LIFO queue.
Stimulus: Request to turn on an H/C unit is received, and maximum units are not running. Response: **themas** generates a request to turn on the specified unit.
Stimulus: Request to turn off an H/C unit is received. Response: **themas** checks the LIFO queue for waiting requests.

Stimulus: LIFO queue is not empty after an H/C unit is turned off. Response: **themas** removes one request from the queue, re-evaluates the associated thermostat's need, and potentially submits a new ON request.
Stimulus: Request for an H/C unit to be turned on is denied due to utilization limits. Response: **themas** generates a 'Unit Unavailable' event.
Stimulus: 'Unit Unavailable' event is generated. Response: **themas** records the event, including event type, thermostat designation, and H/C unit designation.
functionalRequirements: The system shall maintain the ON/OFF status for each heating and cooling unit.
The system shall limit the number of heating or cooling units running simultaneously based on operational parameters.
The system shall add an ON request to a LIFO queue if the maximum number of H/C units is already running.
The system shall generate a request to turn on an H/C unit if the maximum number of units is not running.
The system shall remove one request from the LIFO queue when an H/C unit is turned off and the queue is not empty.
The system shall re-evaluate the associated thermostat's need and potentially submit a new ON request if a queued request is processed.
The system shall generate a 'Unit Unavailable' event when an H/C unit ON request is denied.
The system shall record 'Unit Unavailable' events, including event type, thermostat, and H/C unit information.
The system shall generate an 'Approved H/C Request' containing unit designation and ON/OFF flag when a request is approved.

systemFeatures.name: Generate H/C Signal
systemFeatures.description: This feature is responsible for translating logical heating or cooling requests into physical control signals that directly interact with the building's H/C units. Its core value lies in the seamless execution of system decisions into tangible actions, impacting environmental control. Technically, it processes approved H/C requests, determines the necessary electrical or digital signal, and transmits it to the corresponding hardware unit. Additionally, it ensures that an event is logged for every status change of an H/C unit, providing an invaluable audit trail for operational history and statistical reporting, critical for fault diagnosis and performance analysis. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: **themas** receives an 'Approved H/C Request' (unit, ON/OFF status). Response: **themas** generates the necessary control signal for the specified heating or cooling unit.
Stimulus: Control signal is generated. Response: **themas** transmits the signal to the respective heating or cooling unit.
Stimulus: An H/C unit's status is changed (ON to OFF, or OFF to ON). Response: **themas** generates a system event detailing the unit's identification and new status.
functionalRequirements: The system shall generate the necessary control signal for an approved heating or cooling unit request.
The system shall provide an indication of the H/C unit's status back to the requesting thermostat (TBD).
The system shall generate a system event each time a change is made to the status of a heating or cooling unit.
The system shall include the identification of the heating or cooling unit whose status is being changed in the generated event.

systemFeatures.name: Generate Alarm Data
systemFeatures.description: The Generate Alarm Data feature is vital for proactively alerting the System Supervisor to critical operational anomalies, ensuring timely intervention and preventing potential equipment damage or discomfort. It directly supports the safety and reliability aspects of the system by immediately highlighting conditions that exceed predefined safe operational parameters. From a technical standpoint, it is activated by specific event triggers, such as invalid temperature readings or severe overtemperature conditions, generating structured alarm data that can be consumed by the reporting and notification mechanisms. This ensures that abnormal states are not only detected but also effectively communicated to human operators. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: **themas** detects an invalid temperature value from a thermostat. Response: **themas** generates an alarm condition.
Stimulus: **themas** detects a temperature exceeding overtemperature limits. Response: **themas** generates an alarm condition.
Stimulus: An alarm condition is generated. Response: **themas** creates structured alarm data for logging and potential display.
functionalRequirements: The system shall generate an alarm condition if an invalid temperature value is reported from a thermostat.
The system shall generate an alarm condition if a temperature exceeds overtemperature limits.
The system shall log alarm conditions as system events (TBD detailed output).

systemFeatures.name: Generate Event Data
systemFeatures.description: This feature provides a fundamental auditing and diagnostic capability for **themas**, systematically recording all significant system occurrences to build a comprehensive operational history. It is invaluable for post-incident analysis, performance monitoring, and compliance verification, ensuring transparency in system behavior. Technically, it involves collecting contextual data from various system modules, such as timestamps, event types, and associated entities (thermostats, H/C units), and formatting it for persistent storage in the event database. This ensures that every critical action or anomaly within the system is captured, providing the raw data for all subsequent reporting and analytical needs. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: A significant system activity or anomaly occurs (e.g., H/C unit status change, unit unavailable, alarm). Response: **themas** collects relevant data pertaining to the event.
Stimulus: Event data is collected. Response: **themas** structures the data into a standardized system event format.
Stimulus: System event data is generated. Response: **themas** transmits the event data to the event database for storage.
functionalRequirements: The system shall generate structured event data for all significant system activities.
The system shall include event type, timestamp, and relevant entity identifiers (thermostat, H/C unit) in event data.
The system shall store generated event data persistently in the system's event database.
The system shall support the generation of events for H/C unit status changes, utilization denials, and temperature alarms.

systemFeatures.name: Change Thermostat Setting
systemFeatures.description: This feature allows the System Supervisor to remotely adjust the desired temperature setting for any thermostat managed by **themas**, providing essential control and flexibility over environmental conditions. It directly enhances user convenience and the system's adaptability by enabling dynamic adjustments without physical interaction at each thermostat. From a technical standpoint, this involves receiving a new setting, validating it against system parameters, and transmitting the command to the specified thermostat. The system must confirm the successful application of the new setting, ensuring that the remote command is realized by the hardware. Priority: Medium.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Supervisor requests to change a thermostat setting. Response: **themas** receives the new temperature setting and the target thermostat identifier.
Stimulus: New setting is received. Response: **themas** validates the new setting against the valid temperature range (T-REQ-008).
Stimulus: Setting is valid. Response: **themas** transmits the updated setting to the target thermostat.
Stimulus: Thermostat confirms setting change. Response: **themas** updates its internal state and logs an event for the change.
functionalRequirements: The system shall accept requests from the supervisor to change a thermostat's temperature setting.
The system shall validate the requested temperature setting against the established valid temperature range.
The system shall transmit the validated new temperature setting to the specified thermostat hardware.
The system shall (TBD) confirm successful application of the new setting by the thermostat.

systemFeatures.name: Generate Reports
systemFeatures.description: The Generate Reports feature empowers the System Supervisor with comprehensive insights into **themas**'s operational performance, energy utilization, and historical events, facilitating informed decision-making and efficient system maintenance. It directly provides analytical value by transforming raw event data into meaningful, aggregated information, enabling trend analysis and identification of potential issues. Technically, this involves querying the event database, processing vast amounts of historical data, and formatting the results into structured, human-readable reports. This capability is crucial for optimizing energy consumption, scheduling maintenance, and ensuring compliance with operational policies. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Supervisor requests a specific report (e.g., daily events, utilization summary). Response: **themas** retrieves relevant event data from the event database.
Stimulus: Event data is retrieved. Response: **themas** processes and aggregates the data according to the requested report type.
Stimulus: Report data is processed. Response: **themas** generates the report in a predefined format.
Stimulus: Report is generated. Response: **themas** presents the report to the supervisor via the dashboard interface.
functionalRequirements: The system shall receive requests from the supervisor to generate various system reports.
The system shall query the event database to retrieve relevant historical event data for report generation.
The system shall process and aggregate event data to create comprehensive operational and statistical reports.
The system shall output reports in a human-readable format suitable for analysis by the System Supervisor.
The system shall support reports detailing unit unavailability events, H/C unit status changes, and alarm conditions.

performanceRequirements: T-REQ-019: The **themas** system shall process incoming thermostat temperature data and determine H/C mode within 500 milliseconds of data reception. This is crucial for maintaining real-time environmental control and ensuring timely responses to temperature fluctuations, directly impacting occupant comfort and energy efficiency. Any delay beyond this threshold could lead to overshooting desired temperatures.

T-REQ-020: The **themas** system shall store event data with a throughput of at least 100 events per second during peak operational periods. This ensures that all critical system activities and anomalies are captured without data loss or significant processing backlog, which is essential for accurate historical analysis and auditing. A lower throughput could result in incomplete event logs, compromising diagnostic capabilities.

T-REQ-021: The **themas** system shall generate standard operational reports, summarizing daily events and utilization, within 30 seconds of a supervisor request. This allows supervisors to quickly access critical performance metrics for timely decision-making regarding energy management and system maintenance. Longer generation times would impede the supervisor's ability to react effectively to developing trends.
safetyRequirements: T-REQ-022: The **themas** system shall immediately generate an alarm and log an event if any reported temperature falls outside the established valid temperature range (T-REQ-008). This safeguard prevents potential equipment damage from extreme temperatures and ensures the safety of the building occupants. Delayed alarm generation could lead to dangerous environmental conditions.

T-REQ-023: The **themas** system shall prioritize turning off all heating/cooling units as the first step of its initialization sequence (T-REQ-007). This fail-safe mechanism ensures that the system starts from a known, non-active state, preventing unintended heating or cooling operations during startup, especially after a power interruption or system reset. Failure to do so could lead to uncontrolled climate conditions.

T-REQ-024: The **themas** system shall limit the number of concurrently operating heating or cooling units to a predefined maximum (T-REQ-003). This ensures that the building's electrical infrastructure is not overloaded and prevents excessive energy consumption or potential equipment failure due to simultaneous operation beyond design limits. Exceeding this limit could cause electrical circuit trips or damage to H/C units.
securityRequirements: T-REQ-025: The **themas** system shall require unique user credentials for all supervisor access to the **Supervisor Dashboard** functionality. This ensures that only authorized personnel can monitor, configure, and generate reports from the system, protecting against unauthorized manipulation of building climate controls. Without proper authentication, the system is vulnerable to malicious or accidental misconfiguration.

T-REQ-026: The **themas** system shall log all attempts at supervisor login, including successful and failed attempts, with associated timestamps. This provides an audit trail for security monitoring and helps detect potential unauthorized access attempts or suspicious activity. A lack of such logging would obscure security breaches.

T-REQ-027: The **themas** system shall implement data integrity checks for all incoming temperature and setting data from thermostats. This ensures that environmental data used for critical decision-making has not been corrupted or tampered with during transmission, maintaining the reliability of system operations. Corrupted data could lead to erroneous H/C unit activations.
softwareQualityAttributes: T-REQ-028: **Reliability**: The **themas** system shall operate continuously for at least 99.9% of the time, excluding scheduled maintenance. This is essential for an energy management system that continuously monitors and controls environmental conditions, ensuring consistent comfort and optimal energy usage. Frequent downtime would lead to manual interventions and loss of control.

T-REQ-029: **Maintainability**: The **themas** system's codebase shall be structured to allow for the implementation of minor functional enhancements or bug fixes within a maximum of 5 person-days for an average complexity task. This promotes long-term cost-effectiveness and adaptability, ensuring the system can evolve with changing building requirements or address issues promptly. Poor maintainability would lead to increased operational costs and system rigidity.

T-REQ-030: **Usability**: The **Supervisor Dashboard** for **themas** shall enable a trained supervisor to understand system status and perform routine tasks (e.g., viewing reports, changing thermostat settings) with fewer than 3 errors per hour. This ensures that the system is intuitive and efficient for its target users, minimizing training time and operational mistakes. A complex or confusing interface would lead to operational inefficiencies and frustration.

T-REQ-031: **Robustness**: The **themas** system shall gracefully handle temporary loss of communication with individual thermostats or H/C units without crashing or affecting the operation of other connected devices. It shall attempt to re-establish communication every 60 seconds. This ensures system stability in the face of transient hardware or network issues, preventing cascading failures. A lack of robustness could lead to total system failure due to isolated hardware problems.

T-REQ-032: **Testability**: The **themas** system shall provide internal logging and diagnostic interfaces to facilitate automated testing of core functional modules (e.g., temperature validation, H/C mode determination). This ensures that new features or changes can be thoroughly verified before deployment, reducing the risk of introducing defects into the production environment. A system with low testability is prone to undetected bugs and costly manual verification.
businessRules: T-REQ-033: The **themas** system shall ensure that no more than the predefined maximum number of heating or cooling units, as specified in the utilization parameters (T-REQ-003), are active simultaneously.

T-REQ-034: The **themas** system shall enforce that temperature values reported by any given thermostat do not deviate by more than 3 degrees Fahrenheit from the last valid reading before being flagged as an anomaly or alarm condition.

T-REQ-035: The **themas** system shall process temperature data and make H/C unit activation decisions based on a hierarchy of operational parameters, starting with valid temperature range, then overtemperature limits, and finally trigger values.

flowchartDiagram.syntaxExplanation: This flowchart illustrates the high-level process for the 'Initialize System' feature within **themas**. Nodes represent distinct steps or decisions, with arrows indicating the flow of control. Rectangular nodes signify processes, diamond nodes represent decisions, and rounded rectangles denote start/end points. All labels containing spaces or special characters are enclosed in double quotes for strict Mermaid syntax compliance.
flowchartDiagram.code:
flowchart TD
    A1["Start System Initialization"] --> B1["Issue 'All H/C Off' Command"]
    B1 --> C1["Load Operational Parameters"]
    C1 --> D1["Fetch All Thermostat Readings"]
    D1 --> E1{"Is H/C Required for Thermostat?"}
    E1 -->|Yes| F1["Determine Specific H/C Mode"]
    F1 --> G1["Queue/Process H/C Unit Request"]
    G1 --> H1["Update System State"]
    H1 --> I1("Log Event Data")
    I1 --> D1
    E1 -->|No| D1
    D1 -- "No more thermostats" --> J1["Initialization Complete"]
flowchartDiagram.caption: themas System Initialization Process Flow

sequenceDiagram.syntaxExplanation: This sequence diagram models the interaction flow for an H/C Request and Control process in **themas**. Participants represent system components or external entities. Messages flow chronologically from top to bottom, with `->>+` indicating an asynchronous call that increases the activation bar, and `-->>-` representing an asynchronous reply that decreases it. `alt` blocks represent conditional logic. Aliases for participants are used without quotes, and all message labels are double-quoted.
sequenceDiagram.code:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Th as Thermostat
    participant TM as "themas (Monitor Temperature)"
    participant TU as "themas (Determine Utilization)"
    participant HCU as "Heating/Cooling Unit"
    participant DB as "themas (Event Database)"
    participant Sup as Supervisor

    Th->>+TM: "Send Temperature Data (T, TSET)"
    TM->>TM: "Validate Temperature (T-REQ-008)"
    TM->>TM: "Determine Temperature Status (T-REQ-009)"
    TM->>TM: "Determine H/C Mode (T-REQ-010)"
    alt H/C Request Needed
        TM->>+TU: "Generate H/C Request (T-REQ-013)"
        TU->>TU: "Determine Status of All H/C Units (T-REQ-011)"
        alt Unit Available
            TU->>+HCU: "Approved H/C Request (T-REQ-014)"
            HCU-->>-TU: "Signal Received Confirmation"
            TU->>DB: "System Event Data (H/C Change)"
        else Unit Unavailable
            TU->>DB: "Generate Unit Unavailable Event (T-REQ-012)"
        end
        TU-->>-TM: "Utilization Result"
    else No H/C Request Needed
        TM-->>Th: "No Action Needed"
    end
    TM-->>-Th: "Status Update"

    Sup->>DB: "Request System Reports (T-REQ-018)"
    DB-->>Sup: "Return Report Data"
sequenceDiagram.caption: themas H/C Request and Control Sequence

entityRelationshipDiagram.syntaxExplanation: This Entity Relationship Diagram (ERD) visualizes the data model for **themas**. Entities are defined with their attributes, including primary keys (PK) and foreign keys (FK). Relationships between entities are strictly depicted following the 'FK implies Many-to-One' rule, where '}|--||' denotes many-to-one and '||--o{' denotes one-to-many. Relationship labels are double-quoted, and attribute names are alphanumeric, while entity names are capitalized. Only PK, FK, and UK are allowed for key specifications.
entityRelationshipDiagram.code:
erDiagram
    THERMOSTAT {
        int thermostat_id PK
        string location
        decimal current_temperature
        decimal setting_temperature
    }

    HEATING_COOLING_UNIT {
        int unit_id PK
        string unit_type
        string quadrant
        int floor_number
        string current_status
    }

    EVENT {
        int event_id PK
        int thermostat_id FK
        int unit_id FK
        timestamp event_timestamp
        string event_type
        string description
    }

    REPORT {
        int report_id PK
        string report_name
        timestamp generated_at
        text report_content
    }

    OPERATIONAL_PARAMETER {
        int param_id PK
        string param_name
        string param_value
        string param_type
    }

    EVENT }|--|| THERMOSTAT : "logged for"
    EVENT }|--|| HEATING_COOLING_UNIT : "logged against"
entityRelationshipDiagram.caption: themas Core Data Model ERD

tbdList: T-REQ-014: Provide an indication of the H/C unit's status back to the requesting thermostat.
T-REQ-015: Define specific outputs for alarm conditions generated by the system.
T-REQ-017: Define the mechanism for confirming successful application of a new thermostat setting by the hardware.

introduction.purpose: The purpose of this document is to comprehensively capture the functional and non-functional requirements for the **puget sound** enhancements. These enhancements aim to significantly improve the existing **Moodle** courseware system, addressing specific educational needs of the University of Puget Sound. This specification provides a detailed framework for understanding the required software functionalities, ensuring alignment with institutional objectives and stakeholder expectations.
introduction.documentConventions: This document adheres to the IEEE 830-1998 Recommended Practice for Software Requirements Specifications. All requirements and features are uniquely identified with the prefix 'PS-' to ensure clear traceability and management throughout the project lifecycle. Analysis models, including flowcharts, sequence diagrams, and entity-relationship diagrams, are presented using **Mermaid** syntax, facilitating visual understanding of the system's design and interactions. Consistent terminology and formatting are maintained to enhance readability and clarity.
introduction.intendedAudience: This SRS is intended for a diverse audience, including software developers, quality assurance engineers, project managers, and university stakeholders. Developers and testers will utilize this document as a definitive guide for implementation and verification, ensuring that the software meets specified criteria. Project managers and university administrators can leverage this SRS for strategic planning, resource allocation, and validating that the developed system aligns with the University of Puget Sound's educational goals.
introduction.productScope: The **puget sound** project focuses on developing core functionalities currently absent or inadequate in the existing **Moodle** courseware system, which is being considered as a replacement for **Blackboard**. This includes redesigns to achieve consistency with other university practices and to enhance the user interface for a more intuitive data flow. The scope is confined to specific enhancements that leverage existing **Moodle** APIs, aiming to refine parts of the platform to elevate the learning experience for students and provide professors with advanced material presentation tools.
references: IEEE 830-1998 IEEE Recommended Practice for Software Requirements Specifications.
ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018 Systems and software engineering — Life cycle processes — Requirements engineering.
Moodle Requirements Brainstorming.pdf

overallDescription.productPerspective: The **puget sound** project represents a significant enhancement effort for **Moodle**, positioning it as a viable and improved courseware system for the University of Puget Sound. This product is being developed as a potential replacement for the existing **Blackboard** system, aiming to address identified functional gaps and enhance user experience. The enhancements are designed to integrate seamlessly within the **Moodle** framework, utilizing its established architecture and available APIs.
productFunctions: Allow for multiple file transfers on a per-page configurable basis.
Enable recording, organization, and management of audio clips in a portfolio.
Display and manage web feeds (e.g., RSS 2.0) for various course elements.
Provide comprehensive search functionality across course pages.
Facilitate online grading of assignments and maintenance of grade history.
Integrate social networking applications like wikis and blog engines.
Offer email and SMS notifications for page changes and updates.

userClassesAndCharacteristics.characteristics: Students are the primary consumers of the **puget sound** courseware system. Their interactions primarily involve accessing information posted by professors, uploading assignments and project files, and participating in online discussions. They generally possess varying levels of technical proficiency but expect an intuitive and responsive interface to manage their coursework efficiently.

Professors serve as the primary content administrators within the **puget sound** system. Their responsibilities include uploading educational materials, managing course content, creating new discussion forums, and grading assignments. They require robust tools for content creation, organization, and student assessment, with a focus on ease of use and flexibility in managing diverse teaching methods. They often act as Course Administrators.

System Administrators are responsible for the overall maintenance, configuration, and updating of the **puget sound** courseware system. Their engagement with individual courses is minimal, focusing instead on system-wide management tasks, security, and ensuring optimal performance. They possess advanced technical expertise and require comprehensive tools and documentation for effective system oversight and troubleshooting.

Course Administrators are a specific role, often fulfilled by professors or system administrators, endowed with the capability to manage courses and individual course pages. This role is critical for configuring page-specific functionalities, such as enabling multiple file uploads or toggling notification settings. They need fine-grained control over course content and student interactions within their designated courses.

overallDescription.operatingEnvironment: The **puget sound** enhancements will operate within the existing **Moodle** ecosystem, primarily functioning as a web-based application. This implies deployment on a **Linux** server environment, likely utilizing **Apache** or **Nginx** as the web server, and a **MySQL** or **PostgreSQL** database for data storage. The application layer will predominantly be based on **PHP**, consistent with **Moodle**'s architecture.
designAndImplementationConstraints: The **puget sound** enhancements must be implemented to seamlessly integrate with the existing **Moodle** core system, strictly utilizing its established Application Programming Interfaces (APIs). This ensures compatibility and minimizes the risk of breaking core functionalities.

All new features and user interface redesigns must maintain consistency with other existing university practices and branding guidelines. This promotes a unified user experience across the University of Puget Sound's digital platforms.

The system must adhere to university IT security policies, including data privacy regulations and access control standards. Specific technologies like **PHP** for development and **MySQL** for database management are expected to be used, aligning with the current **Moodle** stack.

The system will leverage open-source components where appropriate, consistent with **Moodle**'s licensing model. Licensing requirements for any third-party integrations must also be strictly followed, ensuring legal compliance and sustainability of the software.
userDocumentation: Relevant, context-sensitive online documentation shall be available on each page of the **puget sound** system, tailored to the specific functionality presented. This immediate access to help minimizes the need for external support and improves user autonomy.

Comprehensive and searchable documentation for system administrators must be provided, covering all routine maintenance tasks, common error resolutions, and advanced troubleshooting procedures. This resource is crucial for efficient system upkeep and problem diagnosis by the Office of Information Services staff.
assumptionsAndDependencies: It is assumed that **Moodle** will be officially adopted by the University of Puget Sound as its primary courseware system, replacing **Blackboard**. The project's success is contingent upon this strategic institutional decision.

The **puget sound** enhancements depend entirely on the availability and stability of **Moodle**'s existing APIs for integration. Any changes or limitations in these APIs could significantly impact the development timeline and scope.

Sufficient resources, including trained personnel for development, testing, deployment, and ongoing maintenance, will be available from the University's Office of Information Services. This ensures the long-term viability and supportability of the enhanced courseware system.

The underlying infrastructure (servers, network, database) supporting the **Moodle** instance will meet the necessary performance and reliability standards to accommodate the additional functionalities and user load introduced by the **puget sound** enhancements.

externalInterfaceRequirements.userInterfaces: The **puget sound** system will primarily feature a **web-based Graphical User Interface (GUI)** accessible via standard modern web browsers on **desktop**, **tablet**, and **mobile** devices. The UI will undergo a redesign to ensure a more natural data flow, improved responsiveness, and reduced page clutter, offering a consistent experience across all user roles. All pages, post-login, will prominently display a search box, adhering to usability best practices for quick navigation.
externalInterfaceRequirements.hardwareInterfaces: The **puget sound** system will interact with user-facing hardware components, primarily **microphones** for the audio recording feature. This interaction will be facilitated through standard web browser APIs, capturing audio input for student portfolios. Additionally, the system will interface with local file systems on client devices to enable the upload and download of various file types, including assignment submissions and audio clips.
externalInterfaceRequirements.softwareInterfaces: The **puget sound** system will interface extensively with the **Moodle** Core API for all fundamental course management operations, user authentication, and data storage. It will integrate with a **MySQL** (or compatible) database for persistent data storage. External third-party services will include **SMTP** servers for email notifications and **SMS** gateways for mobile notifications. The social networking features will involve integrating external **wiki** and **blog** engines, which will share **Moodle**'s authentication. Optionally, it may interface with external web search engines like **Google**.
externalInterfaceRequirements.communicationsInterfaces: The **puget sound** system will rely heavily on **HTTP/HTTPS** protocols for all client-server communications, ensuring secure and encrypted data exchange across the network. For notifications, it will use **SMTP** for email delivery and specific **SMS** protocols for mobile text messages. Web feeds will be published using the **RSS 2.0** standard. Internal communications between system components, such as the application server and database, will utilize standard network protocols, ensuring efficient and reliable data transfer within the university's IT infrastructure.

systemFeatures.name: Multiple File Transfer
systemFeatures.description: This feature empowers course administrators and professors within the **puget sound** system to configure specific pages, such as assignment submission or project pages, to allow for multiple file uploads. This significantly enhances the flexibility for students to submit comprehensive multi-part assignments or collaborative project work, fostering richer educational interactions. From a technical standpoint, it extends **Moodle**'s existing file management capabilities, requiring adjustments to data models and user interface components to handle batch file operations efficiently. The priority for this feature is set to Medium.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Course Administrator navigates to an assignment page editing interface. Response: The system displays an option to enable multiple file uploads for that specific page, as per PS-REQ-001.

Stimulus: Student attempts to upload multiple files to a configured assignment submission page. Response: The system accepts and processes all selected files as a single, cohesive submission, adhering to PS-REQ-003.
functionalRequirements: PS-REQ-001: The system shall allow course administrators to enable or disable multiple file uploads on a per-page basis.
PS-REQ-002: The system shall present the multiple file upload configuration option during a page’s creation and editing stages.
PS-REQ-003: The system shall support the upload of multiple files for assignment submissions, forum posts, and wiki pages.
PS-REQ-004: The system shall manage the storage of multiple uploaded files associated with a single submission or content item.

systemFeatures.name: Audio Recording
systemFeatures.description: This feature addresses a critical need, particularly within foreign language departments, by enabling students to record, organize into portfolios, download, and delete voice clips directly within the **puget sound** system. This functionality provides professors with a longitudinal view of student progress, facilitating more effective assessment of language acquisition and oral communication skills. Technically, it necessitates browser-based audio capture, server-side storage optimization for speech, and on-demand conversion to widely compatible formats like **MPEG Audio (mp3)**. The priority for this feature is designated as Medium.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Student initiates an audio recording session within an assignment page. Response: The system activates the user's microphone and begins capturing audio, as specified in PS-REQ-005.

Stimulus: Student accesses their audio clip portfolio. Response: The system displays a categorized list of their recorded voice clips, providing options to play, download, or delete, in accordance with PS-REQ-006 and PS-REQ-015.
functionalRequirements: PS-REQ-005: The system shall allow actors to record voice clips directly within the application.
PS-REQ-006: The system shall provide an archive, referred to as a portfolio, for actors to organize their voice clips.
PS-REQ-007: The system shall allocate equal and finite drive space to each professor or administrator for the storage of student voice clips.
PS-REQ-008: The system shall allow professors or system administrators to partition this storage space among students.
PS-REQ-009: The system shall provide an option for actors to preview voice clips before they are archived.
PS-REQ-010: The system shall allow voice clips to be automatically archived upon creation if the preview option is not selected.
PS-REQ-011: The system shall allow actors to download their voice clips in **MPEG Audio (mp3)** format.
PS-REQ-012: The system shall store audio clips in a format optimized for speech recordings.
PS-REQ-013: The system shall convert stored audio clips to **MPEG Audio (mp3)** format only upon download.
PS-REQ-014: The system shall provide a cross-platform viewer for listening to voice clips directly within **Moodle**.
PS-REQ-015: The system shall allow actors to delete recorded clips from their portfolio.
PS-REQ-016: The system shall allow actors to optionally attach one of their archived voice clips to any page where file uploads are permitted.

systemFeatures.name: Web Feeds
systemFeatures.description: This feature integrates **RSS 2.0** web feed functionality across various content-rich pages within the **puget sound** courseware system, including forums, assignment postings, and announcements. By enabling web feeds, the system allows users to efficiently stay informed about new content and updates without constantly navigating the platform, significantly improving content discovery and engagement. Technically, this involves generating and managing **RSS 2.0** XML feeds for configurable pages, ensuring adherence to standard web syndication protocols. The priority for this feature is classified as High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Course Administrator publishes a new announcement on a page with web feeds enabled. Response: The system automatically updates the **RSS 2.0** feed for the announcements page, reflecting the new content, as per PS-REQ-017.
Stimulus: A user's **RSS** reader client polls the course's web feed for updates. Response: The system provides the latest content updates, including the new announcement, adhering to PS-REQ-017.
functionalRequirements: PS-REQ-017: The system shall generate and display web feeds, specifically **RSS 2.0**, for pages where content elements such as forum posts, assignment postings, and announcements are added.
PS-REQ-018: The system shall allow course administrators to determine which specific page components have associated web feeds.
PS-REQ-019: The system shall allow course administrators to control the audience or scope to whom these web feeds should pertain.
PS-REQ-020: The system shall allow web feeds to be turned on or off on a per-page basis by course administrators.
PS-REQ-021: The system shall provide the toggle functionality for web feed availability during the creation and editing stages of a page.

systemFeatures.name: Search
systemFeatures.description: This feature introduces robust search functionality within the **puget sound** system, offering an alternative and more efficient navigation method than traditional hierarchical links. Users will be able to quickly locate specific pages, assignments, or forum posts across their courses, thereby reducing navigational friction and improving overall user satisfaction. Technically, this requires a comprehensive indexing mechanism for course content and a retrieval system capable of delivering relevant, categorized results. The priority for this feature is rated as Medium.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Actor types a query into the search box displayed on any page after login. Response: The system displays search results relevant to the actor's current course or all courses, grouped by category, in accordance with PS-REQ-023 and PS-REQ-029.
Stimulus: Actor clicks on a search result link within the search results page. Response: The system navigates the actor directly to the corresponding page or content item, as specified in PS-REQ-028.
functionalRequirements: PS-REQ-022: The system shall allow an actor to search through pages within a course.
PS-REQ-023: The system shall display a search box and a 'Search' button prominently on every page after an actor has logged in.
PS-REQ-024: The system shall provide a dedicated search page that includes advanced filtering options for search results.
PS-REQ-025: The system shall allow actors to filter search results by specific content categories, such as forum posts or wiki pages.
PS-REQ-026: The system shall allow actors to search specific sections or components within the course management system.
PS-REQ-027: The system shall display search results categorized (e.g., Assignments, Wiki Pages, Forum Posts) based on their relevance to the search terms.
PS-REQ-028: The system shall navigate the actor directly to the corresponding page when a search result link is clicked.
PS-REQ-029: If an actor is hierarchically outside of a particular course, the system shall search all of their enrolled courses and group the results by course.
PS-REQ-030: If an actor is hierarchically inside of a course, the system shall search that specific course with an explicit option to extend the search to all courses.

systemFeatures.name: Gradebook
systemFeatures.description: This feature provides course administrators within the **puget sound** system with the capability to post, manage, and modify grades for assignments, along with attaching textual or file-based feedback. It ensures that students can view their individual performance, averages, and overall grades, while maintaining a comprehensive history of grade changes for transparency and dispute resolution. Technically, it requires secure database storage for grade data, robust timestamping for modifications, and strict role-based access controls to ensure privacy. The priority for this feature is categorized as High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Course Administrator accesses an assignment and enters a score and feedback for a student. Response: The system records the grade, feedback, and a timestamp of the modification, displaying it appropriately, as per PS-REQ-032 and PS-REQ-034.

Stimulus: Student views their academic progress for a specific course. Response: The system displays their individual assignment grades, averages, and overall course grade, ensuring no other student's information is visible, consistent with PS-REQ-035 and PS-REQ-036.
functionalRequirements: PS-REQ-032: The system shall allow a course administrator to assign a grade for an assignment based on the ratio of points earned to points possible.
PS-REQ-033: The system shall allow course administrators to attach feedback in the form of text or an uploaded file to a grade.
PS-REQ-034: The system shall automatically attach timestamps to record the last modification time of a grade.
PS-REQ-035: The system shall display grade information, including individual assignment grades, averages, and overall grade, to the appropriate actor.
PS-REQ-036: The system shall ensure that no actor can view another's grades, except for a course administrator for the course to which the grades belong.
PS-REQ-037: The system shall maintain a comprehensive history of grades for a particular assignment, including all changes made by the course administrator.

systemFeatures.name: Social Networking Applications
systemFeatures.description: This feature aims to bolster the collaborative capabilities of the **puget sound** system by integrating robust wiki and blog functionalities directly into **Moodle**. It provides platforms for students and professors to collaboratively create documents, share insights, and express ideas through personal blogs, fostering a more interactive and engaged learning community. From a technical perspective, this involves integrating third-party wiki and blog engines while ensuring seamless authentication and notification mechanisms with **Moodle**. The priority for this feature is set to Low.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Actor creates a new wiki page within a course. Response: The system provides a simple markup language interface for collaborative document creation and linking to other wiki pages, as per PS-REQ-038 and PS-REQ-039.

Stimulus: Actor publishes a new blog post via the integrated blog engine. Response: The system records the post and makes it publicly or privately available, sharing authentication and notification services with **Moodle**, in accordance with PS-REQ-041 and PS-REQ-043.
functionalRequirements: PS-REQ-038: The system shall provide a wiki where actors can collaboratively create networks of documents.
PS-REQ-039: The wiki shall provide a simple markup language that actors can use to style their input and link to other pages within the wiki.
PS-REQ-040: The system shall integrate the wiki within **Moodle** to provide consistent authentication and notification services.
PS-REQ-041: The system shall provide a blog engine with modern blogging functionality.
PS-REQ-042: The blog engine shall support features such as tagging, drafting, and comments.
PS-REQ-043: The blog engine shall share authentication and notification mechanisms with **Moodle**.

systemFeatures.name: Notifications
systemFeatures.description: This feature introduces a comprehensive notification system within the **puget sound** platform, enabling users to receive timely alerts via **email** and **SMS** for significant changes to **Moodle** pages, such as assignment updates or new announcements. This proactive communication ensures students and professors remain informed, enhancing responsiveness and engagement with course content. Users will have granular control over their notification preferences and delivery methods. Technically, this requires integration with **email** and **SMS** gateways, coupled with configurable subscription management within the user profile. The priority for this feature is classified as Low.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Course Administrator updates an assignment page where notifications are enabled. Response: The system sends **email** and/or **SMS** notifications to subscribed students, as per PS-REQ-044.

Stimulus: Student accesses their user preferences to modify notification settings. Response: The system displays options to select preferred delivery methods (**email** or **SMS**) and manage individual page subscriptions, in accordance with PS-REQ-048 and PS-REQ-049.
functionalRequirements: PS-REQ-044: The system shall provide both **email** and **SMS** notifications for changes to pages.
PS-REQ-045: When a page is created, the actor in the role of course administrator shall be able to toggle whether notifications are turned on for that page.
PS-REQ-046: Notifications shall be turned on by default for announcement pages.
PS-REQ-047: If notifications are initially turned off by the administrator, actors in the capacity of student shall be able to manually subscribe to notifications for that specific page.
PS-REQ-048: Students shall be able to select their preferred notification delivery method (mobile telephone via **SMS** or **email** account) within their user preferences.
PS-REQ-049: Students shall be able to manage their notification subscriptions, including the ability to remove themselves from specific notifications.

performanceRequirements: PS-REQ-050: The **puget sound** system shall support at least 1000 concurrent users without any noticeable degradation in response time, especially during peak academic periods such as midterm and finals weeks. This ensures consistent accessibility and responsiveness, even under significant load, maintaining a smooth user experience for a large student and faculty population.
safetyRequirements: PS-REQ-051: The **puget sound** system shall implement comprehensive data validation and integrity checks for all user inputs and data processing operations. This measure prevents data corruption, ensures the accuracy of academic records and course content, and mitigates risks associated with malicious or erroneous data entry.

PS-REQ-052: All backup procedures for the **puget sound** system shall include encryption of data both in transit and at rest, with copies stored in geographically dispersed, secure off-site locations. This robust strategy safeguards sensitive university and personal information against unauthorized access, data breaches, and catastrophic data loss events.

PS-REQ-053: The **puget sound** system shall maintain a detailed, immutable audit trail for all critical administrative actions, including grade modifications, user account changes, and system configuration updates. This provides accountability, supports forensic investigations, and ensures compliance with regulatory and university policies.
securityRequirements: PS-REQ-054: The **puget sound** system shall enforce strong user authentication mechanisms, potentially integrating with the University's existing **Single Sign-On (SSO)** system. This ensures that only authenticated and authorized individuals can access the system, leveraging centralized identity management for improved security and user convenience.

PS-REQ-055: All communication between the client devices and the **puget sound** server shall be encrypted using **HTTPS** (TLS 1.2 or higher). This protects sensitive data, such as login credentials, personal information, and academic records, from interception and tampering during transmission across public and private networks.

PS-REQ-056: The **puget sound** system shall implement a robust Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) model, ensuring that users can only access functionalities and data pertinent to their assigned roles (e.g., Student, Professor, System Administrator). This granular control prevents unauthorized access to sensitive information, such as other students' grades, and restricts system administration capabilities to authorized personnel.

PS-REQ-057: Sensitive user data stored within the **puget sound** system, including personal identifiable information and academic records, shall be protected through encryption at rest within the database. This provides an additional layer of security, safeguarding data even in the event of unauthorized access to the underlying storage infrastructure.
softwareQualityAttributes: PS-REQ-058: The **puget sound** system shall provide a simple, intuitive, and highly responsive user interface across all its functionalities for students, professors, and administrators. This design approach aims to minimize learning curves, reduce user errors, and maximize efficiency in task completion, enhancing overall user satisfaction.

PS-REQ-059: The **puget sound** system shall offer a centralized administrative interface that allows for easy configuration of UI elements, themes, and feature settings across the entire courseware system. This streamlines system management, enabling administrators to efficiently customize the platform without requiring deep technical expertise.

PS-REQ-060: The **puget sound** system shall be backed up on a configurable schedule, with assignment submission data receiving nightly backups. This critical measure ensures comprehensive data protection, allowing for swift recovery in the event of system failures or data loss.

PS-REQ-061: The **puget sound** system shall maintain an availability target of 99% uptime, operating 24 hours a day, 7 days a week, excluding planned maintenance windows. This commitment ensures consistent access for users across various time zones and study schedules, supporting uninterrupted academic activities.
