PS-REQ-062: The **puget sound** system shall be capable of restoring all critical backed-up data within six hours of a system outage or data corruption event. This fast recovery time is essential to minimize service disruption and ensure academic continuity.

PS-REQ-063: The **puget sound** system shall be designed for maintainability, requiring minimal substantial modifications from the Office of Information Services staff. This architectural approach aims to reduce the long-term operational burden and associated costs.

PS-REQ-064: The **puget sound** system shall be largely self-sustaining in its operation and maintenance, reducing the necessity for extensive manual intervention by IT personnel. This optimizes resource allocation, allowing staff to focus on more strategic technological initiatives.

PS-REQ-065: The internal architecture of the **puget sound** system shall adhere to established software engineering coding standards, design patterns, and best practices. This ensures that the codebase is easily understandable, extensible, and maintainable by current and future development teams, facilitating long-term evolution and bug fixing.

PS-REQ-066: The **puget sound** system shall provide comprehensive, searchable documentation for system administrators, covering all regular maintenance tasks, common errors, and advanced troubleshooting procedures. This resource empowers IT staff to efficiently manage and troubleshoot the system, reducing reliance on external support and increasing operational independence.

PS-REQ-067: The **puget sound** system shall maintain compatibility and consistent functionality across a range of modern web browsers and common operating systems, including **Windows**, **macOS**, **Linux**, **iOS**, and **Android**. This broad compatibility ensures that all users can access and utilize the system regardless of their chosen device or platform.

PS-REQ-068: The **puget sound** system shall be designed with modular components and clearly defined interfaces, enabling independent testing of individual features and integrations. This facilitates efficient and comprehensive testing processes, which are crucial for ensuring the overall quality, reliability, and correctness of the software.
businessRules: PS-REQ-069: All student academic records and personal data managed by the **puget sound** system shall be retained and protected in strict accordance with the University of Puget Sound's data retention policies and relevant educational privacy regulations, such as **FERPA**. This ensures legal compliance and ethical handling of sensitive student information.

PS-REQ-070: Access to administrative functions, grade modification capabilities, and sensitive system configuration within the **puget sound** system shall be exclusively restricted to authorized university personnel who possess the appropriate security clearances and have undergone necessary training. This rule upholds the integrity of academic processes and prevents unauthorized operational changes.

glossary.definition: A user performing actions within use cases, often used synonymously with 'user' in the context of the system.
A recorded voice segment, typically created by students, managed within a portfolio for language learning or other purposes.
The existing courseware system currently in use at the University of Puget Sound, which the **puget sound** enhancements to **Moodle** are intended to replace.
An actor who is either a professor or a system administrator, possessing elevated privileges to manage courses and individual course pages within the system.
A software application designed to facilitate electronic classroom management, including functionalities for electronic grading, assignment submission, discussions, and other learning tools.
Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act, a United States federal law that governs the access to educational information and records by public entities and private institutions.
A common digital audio encoding format known for its wide compatibility across various web browsers and operating systems, used for downloading audio clips.
The existing open-source course management system that the **puget sound** project aims to enhance and for which new functionalities are being developed.
Refers to any distinct upload point, content block, or functional view within the system, such as an assignment, a wiki page, a forum post, or an announcement.
An organized archive of an actor's voice clips, intended for tracking progress over time or for presentation purposes.
A widely used web feed format for publishing frequently updated works such as blog entries, news headlines, and course updates, enabling content syndication.
Short Message Service, a text messaging service component of most telephone, Internet, and mobile device systems, used for mobile notifications.
Refers collectively to the enhanced courseware system, encompassing **Moodle** and all modifications and new functionalities introduced by the **puget sound** project.
Single Sign-On, an authentication scheme that allows a user to log in with a single ID and password to gain access to multiple related but independent software systems.

flowchartDiagram.syntaxExplanation: This flowchart depicts a high-level process for a student submitting an assignment within the **puget sound** system. Nodes represent states or actions, with rectangular nodes for processes, diamond nodes for decisions, and rounded rectangles for start/end points. Arrows indicate the flow of control, moving from one stage to the next based on user input or system logic. Quoted labels are used for clarity and readability.
flowchartDiagram.code:
flowchart TD
    A1["Student accesses PS-UC-001: Submit Assignment page"]
    A1 --> A2{"Multiple Files Configured? (PS-REQ-001)"}
    A2 -- No --> A3["Upload single file (PS-REQ-003)"]
    A2 -- Yes --> A4["Upload multiple files (PS-REQ-003)"]
    A3 --> A5["Submit Assignment"]
    A4 --> A5
    A5 --> A6{"Submission Successful?"}
    A6 -- Yes --> A7["System records submission & timestamp (PS-REQ-004)"]
    A7 --> A8{"Notifications Enabled? (PS-REQ-045)"}
    A8 -- Yes --> A9["System sends PS-REQ-044: Email/SMS notification to Professor"]
    A8 -- No --> A10["No notification sent"]
    A9 --> A11(["End: Assignment Submitted Successfully"])
    A10 --> A11
    A6 -- No --> A12["Display PS-Error-001: Error message to student"]
    A12 --> A1["Retry submission"]
flowchartDiagram.caption: **puget sound**: Assignment Submission Flow

sequenceDiagram.syntaxExplanation: This sequence diagram illustrates the temporal interactions and message flow involved in a student submitting an assignment with the option for multiple files within the **puget sound** system. Participants are listed at the top, representing different entities in the system. Solid lines with filled arrowheads denote synchronous calls, while dashed lines represent asynchronous responses. `alt` blocks denote conditional logic. Participants are labeled with aliases, and message labels clearly describe the communication.
sequenceDiagram.code:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Student
    participant UI as "puget sound Web UI"
    participant App as "puget sound Application"
    participant MoodleAPI as "Moodle Core API"
    participant DB as Database
    
    Student->>UI: Access "Submit Assignment" page
    UI->>App: Request Assignment Page (AssignmentID)
    App->>MoodleAPI: Fetch Assignment Details (AssignmentID)
    MoodleAPI->>DB: Query Assignment_TBL
    DB-->>MoodleAPI: Assignment Details (PS-SF-001)
    MoodleAPI-->>App: Assignment Details
    App-->>UI: Render Assignment Page (PS-REQ-002)
    UI-->>Student: Display Assignment Form
    
    Student->>UI: Upload Files (PS-REQ-003)
    UI->>App: Send Files, AssignmentID, StudentID
    App->>MoodleAPI: Store Submission Files (Files, AssignmentID, StudentID)
    MoodleAPI->>DB: Save Submission_TBL, File_TBL (PS-REQ-004)
    DB-->>MoodleAPI: Confirmation
    MoodleAPI-->>App: Submission Confirmation
    
    alt Notifications Enabled (PS-REQ-045)
        App->>App: Check Professor Preferences (PS-REQ-048)
        App->>MoodleAPI: Send Notification (Message, ProfessorID, Type)
        MoodleAPI->>App: Notification Acknowledged
    else No Notifications
        App->>App: Log no notification sent
    end
    App-->>UI: Display Success Message
    UI-->>Student: Submission Confirmation
sequenceDiagram.caption: **puget sound**: Assignment Submission Sequence

entityRelationshipDiagram.syntaxExplanation: This Entity Relationship Diagram (ERD) illustrates the core data entities and their relationships within the **puget sound** system. Entities are depicted as rectangles, attributes are listed within entities, and relationships are shown with lines indicating cardinality and labels. Primary Keys (PK), Foreign Keys (FK), and Unique Keys (UK) are explicitly marked. The relationship notation adheres to the 'crow's foot' style, with '}|--||' indicating a many-to-one relationship from the perspective of the foreign key-holding entity. Entity names and relationship labels are quoted for clarity when containing spaces.
entityRelationshipDiagram.code:
erDiagram
    COURSE_ENROLLMENT["Course Enrollment"] }|--|| USER : "enrolls"
    COURSE_ENROLLMENT["Course Enrollment"] }|--|| COURSE : "for course"
    COURSE }|--|| USER : "taught by"
    ASSIGNMENT }|--|| COURSE : "belongs to"
    SUBMISSION }|--|| ASSIGNMENT : "for"
    SUBMISSION }|--|| USER : "by"
    GRADE }|--|| SUBMISSION : "evaluates"
    GRADE }|--|| USER : "given by"
    AUDIO_CLIP["Audio Clip"] }|--|| USER : "owned by"
    NOTIFICATION }|--|| USER : "sent to"
    NOTIFICATION }|--|| ASSIGNMENT : "triggered by"

    USER {
        int user_id PK
        string email_address UK
        string username
        string role
        datetime created_at
    }

    COURSE {
        int course_id PK
        string course_name
        string course_code UK
        int instructor_user_id FK "Instructor"
    }
    
    COURSE_ENROLLMENT["Course Enrollment"] {
        int enrollment_id PK
        int user_id FK
        int course_id FK
        datetime enrollment_date
    }

    ASSIGNMENT {
        int assignment_id PK
        int course_id FK
        string title
        text description
        datetime due_date
        bool multiple_files_enabled "PS-REQ-001"
        bool notifications_enabled "PS-REQ-045"
    }

    SUBMISSION {
        int submission_id PK
        int assignment_id FK
        int student_user_id FK
        datetime submission_date
        string file_paths "JSON or delimited paths"
        text feedback_text "PS-REQ-033"
    }

    GRADE {
        int grade_id PK
        int submission_id FK
        int grader_user_id FK "Course Administrator"
        decimal score
        decimal max_score
        datetime grade_date
        datetime last_modified "PS-REQ-034"
    }

    AUDIO_CLIP["Audio Clip"] {
        int clip_id PK
        int owner_user_id FK
        string clip_name
        string storage_format "PS-REQ-012"
        string file_path
        datetime upload_date
    }

    NOTIFICATION {
        int notification_id PK
        int target_user_id FK
        int related_assignment_id FK
        string notification_type
        string message_content
        datetime sent_date
        bool is_read
    }
entityRelationshipDiagram.caption: **puget sound**: Core Entity Relationship Diagram

tbdList: PS-TBD-001: The specific version of **Moodle** that will serve as the base for the **puget sound** enhancements needs to be formally determined and documented.
PS-TBD-002: Detailed, granular backup requirements for individual components of the **puget sound** system, beyond nightly assignment data, need to be fully defined.
PS-TBD-003: The final decision regarding the integration of external web search functionality, such as **Google**, on the dedicated search page requires further stakeholder discussion and approval.

introduction.purpose: The primary purpose of this specification is to define the functional requirements for the **elsfork** communication system, which facilitates data transfer between wind turbine control systems and remote monitoring computers. This document serves as a critical guide for establishing and operating such a system, particularly for procurement processes concerning communication solutions for wind power plants. It ensures clarity on data exchange mechanisms without prescribing specific SCADA system characteristics or HMI designs.
introduction.documentConventions: This document adheres to the guidelines set forth in IEEE 830-1998, 'Recommended Practice for Software Requirements Specifications,' ensuring a standardized and comprehensive approach to requirement definition. All requirements and identifiers are prefixed with 'E-' to maintain consistency and traceability throughout the **elsfork** project documentation. Technical terms, acronyms, and abbreviations are formally defined in the appended Glossary to ensure unambiguous interpretation across all stakeholders.
introduction.intendedAudience: This specification is primarily intended for procurement managers, system integrators, and technical developers involved in selecting, implementing, or evaluating communication solutions for wind power applications. It also serves as a foundational reference for wind farm operators and utility companies interested in the technical capabilities and interoperability of the **elsfork** communication system. The detailed requirements aim to facilitate clear communication between project stakeholders and development teams.
introduction.productScope: The **elsfork** product scope is strictly limited to the communication system supporting remote operation and supervision of wind power plants, encompassing both single wind turbines and large wind farms. This system is responsible for the transfer of data from the process/plant level to an application level in a standardized format and the distribution of commands and operational settings back to the process/plant level. It explicitly excludes the specification of SCADA system characteristics, human-machine interfaces, or control algorithms, focusing solely on the communication layer.
references: IEEE 830-1998 IEEE Recommended Practice for Software Requirements Specifications.
ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018 Systems and software engineering — Life cycle processes — Requirements engineering.

overallDescription.productPerspective: The **elsfork** communication system emerges from a critical need to address the prevailing issue of incompatible, proprietary communication solutions across various wind turbine manufacturers. Historically, each vendor has deployed unique systems, posing significant challenges for users who operate diverse fleets of wind turbines and seek unified management. This product is conceived as a foundational step towards establishing open standards, replacing fragmented approaches with a standardized communication concept that promotes vendor independence.
productFunctions: Facilitate standardized data transfer from wind turbine control units to remote monitoring systems.
Enable the secure transmission of control commands and operational settings from remote systems to wind turbines.
Support the coexistence of different data types, including verbal and visual communication, on a shared transmission network.
Define and manage data structures, types, and characteristics for coherent interpretation across the entire system.
Provide mechanisms for local data storage and handling within wind turbine control units to ensure data resilience.

userClassesAndCharacteristics.characteristics: These users are responsible for the remote supervision, operation, and maintenance of individual wind turbines and entire wind farms. They require reliable, real-time access to operational data and the ability to issue control commands securely. Their technical expertise is generally high, with a focus on operational efficiency and grid integration. They utilize the communication system for daily monitoring, fault diagnosis, and performance optimization, making them frequent and critical users.

This class comprises individuals involved in the acquisition of communication solutions for wind power plants. They utilize this specification as a comprehensive guide during the tendering and purchasing processes, ensuring that procured systems meet defined functional and non-functional requirements. Their primary need is a clear, unambiguous statement of requirements to evaluate vendor proposals effectively, focusing on compliance and cost-effectiveness rather than daily operational tasks.

These technical personnel are tasked with implementing, configuring, and integrating communication solutions based on the **elsfork** specification. They possess deep technical expertise in communication protocols, software development, and control systems. Their interactions involve translating requirements into practical system designs and code, ensuring interoperability between diverse hardware and software components. They are critical for the successful deployment and customization of the communication infrastructure.

overallDescription.operatingEnvironment: The **elsfork** communication system is designed to operate within diverse geographical settings, ranging from single, remotely located wind turbines to extensive wind farms with multiple turbines. It interfaces with various hardware platforms, including the internal control systems of wind turbines (**ControlUnit**), Wind Farm Main Controllers (**WFMC**), and remote **SCADA** (Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition) systems. The system must peacefully coexist with existing high-voltage feeder networks and medium-voltage grid connections, accommodating specific requirements for power quality and reactive power compensation. Its operational integrity must be maintained despite varying network conditions and potential communication latency.
designAndImplementationConstraints: The system shall adhere to established international standardization efforts within IEC TC88 Wind Turbine Systems to promote global interoperability and avoid proprietary vendor lock-in.

The communication solution must be compatible with a broad range of existing wind turbine control systems and **SCADA** platforms to facilitate integration without requiring complete overhauls of legacy infrastructure.

Implementation shall prioritize robust data integrity mechanisms to ensure that data transmitted from wind turbines to **SCADA** systems remains uncorrupted and accurately reflects the operational state.

The system must incorporate stringent security considerations, including mechanisms for data protection and authenticated command transmission, to prevent unauthorized access or control of wind turbine assets.

Design and implementation must account for the remote and often isolated nature of wind turbine installations, ensuring resilience against intermittent network connectivity and facilitating remote diagnostics and maintenance.
userDocumentation: A comprehensive **elsfork** System Integration Guide shall be provided, detailing the process for integrating the communication solution with various wind turbine control systems and **SCADA** platforms, including API specifications and configuration parameters.

An **elsfork** Troubleshooting and Maintenance Manual shall be available, offering guidance for diagnosing and resolving common communication issues, and outlining routine maintenance procedures for optimal system performance.
An **elsfork** Administrator’s Guide shall describe the setup, configuration, and security management of the communication system, including user access controls and network settings.
assumptionsAndDependencies: It is assumed that individual wind turbines possess autonomous control systems, capable of performing basic operations and safety functions independently without continuous external command or supervision.

The effective operation of the **elsfork** system is dependent on the availability of a functional underlying network infrastructure (e.g., cellular, satellite, fiber optic) connecting wind turbines to remote monitoring centers.

Future international communication standards for wind turbine control and monitoring will align with or build upon the principles outlined in this specification, influencing subsequent phases of development.

It is assumed that remote **SCADA** systems are in place or will be acquired, capable of processing, displaying, and acting upon the data and commands facilitated by the **elsfork** communication system.

The project relies on continued collaboration and input from industry stakeholders, including wind turbine manufacturers and utility companies, to refine and validate the standardized communication requirements.

externalInterfaceRequirements.userInterfaces: The **elsfork** communication system provides the backbone for data exchange that supports various user interfaces, primarily the Human Machine Interface (**HMI**) of **SCADA** systems. These interfaces are characterized by graphical displays of wind power plant data, control dashboards for issuing commands, and alarm/event notifications. The system requires capabilities to transfer data suitable for rendering trends, real-time values, and operational states on **HMI** screens, often following specific GUI standards defined by the **SCADA** platform. It must enable interactive control elements for operators to adjust set points and issue binary commands, with visual feedback for command execution status.
externalInterfaceRequirements.hardwareInterfaces: The **elsfork** communication system interfaces directly with the hardware components of wind turbines, including their internal control units (**CU**) and various sensors. It also interacts with Wind Farm Main Controllers (**WFMC**) in larger installations and potentially with local substations for grid integration data. This includes logical and physical connections for data acquisition from sensors, control signal transmission to actuators, and status feedback from the turbine's internal systems. Communication protocols for these hardware interactions must be specified, supporting data transmission over various physical media, such as Ethernet or serial connections, ensuring robust and reliable data exchange.
externalInterfaceRequirements.softwareInterfaces: This product will interface with multiple critical software components, including the internal control algorithms running within the **WindTurbine** **ControlUnit**, the overarching **SCADA System** for monitoring and control, and various databases for persistent data storage. It leverages communication stacks adhering to the **ISO Reference Model**, potentially utilizing protocols like **TCP/IP** and **MMS (Manufacturing Message Specification)** for application-level data exchange. Data items such as sensor readings, alarm states, event logs, and control commands must be precisely defined for bidirectional flow. The system must support the secure and reliable transfer of this shared data, specifying the nature of services and communication protocols used, while providing clear application programming interfaces (**API**) for seamless integration with other software components.
externalInterfaceRequirements.communicationsInterfaces: The **elsfork** communication system mandates robust communication capabilities for reliable data exchange across the wind power infrastructure. It requires support for standard network server communications protocols, such as **TCP/IP** over **Ethernet**, potentially utilizing **HTTP** for web-based services, and other specialized industrial protocols. The system must define pertinent message formatting for all data kinds, ensuring consistent interpretation across different system components. Specific requirements include defining communication security measures, such as **encryption** for sensitive data and authentication mechanisms to prevent unauthorized access or command injection. Furthermore, the system must specify acceptable data transfer rates, supporting both periodic and spontaneous data transmission, and effective synchronization mechanisms for time-tagged events to maintain chronological accuracy across distributed systems.

systemFeatures.name: Data Acquisition from Wind Turbines
systemFeatures.description: This feature facilitates the collection of critical operational data from individual wind turbines, providing **elsfork** operators with real-time and historical insights into turbine performance and health. By enabling comprehensive data analysis, it significantly improves operational efficiency, supports predictive maintenance strategies, and aids in informed decision-making regarding asset management. This capability is of High priority due to its fundamental role in monitoring the operational status and efficiency of wind power plants.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: SCADA system requests periodic operational data from a specific wind turbine. Response: The target wind turbine control unit acquires the requested data points (analogue, binary, counters) and transmits them to the SCADA system.

Stimulus: A wind turbine's internal sensor detects a significant change in an operational parameter. Response: The wind turbine control unit triggers a spontaneous data transmission, sending the updated parameter value and its timestamp to the SCADA system.
functionalRequirements: E-REQ-001: The system shall acquire analogue signals (e.g., wind speed, power output, temperature) from each connected wind turbine with configurable sampling rates.
E-REQ-002: The system shall acquire binary signals (e.g., operational status, breaker position) representing the discrete states of wind turbine components.
E-REQ-003: The system shall acquire counter values (e.g., energy production, operational hours) from each wind turbine.
E-REQ-004: The system shall support periodic data transmission at intervals configurable from 1 second to 60 minutes.
E-REQ-005: The system shall support spontaneous data transfer upon a predefined change-of-state or value threshold excursion for critical data points.

systemFeatures.name: Command and Control Transmission to Wind Turbines
systemFeatures.description: This feature enables remote operators to issue control commands and set points to individual wind turbines or wind farm main controllers, ensuring flexible and responsive management of wind turbine operations. It allows for rapid adjustments for optimization, safety protocols, and grid integration requirements. This capability is of High priority as it provides the essential means for operators to interact with and manage distributed wind energy assets effectively, ensuring safe and efficient power generation.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: An operator issues a 'Stop Turbine' command via the SCADA HMI for Turbine X. Response: The SCADA system sends the 'Stop Turbine' command to the target wind turbine control unit, which then initiates the turbine shutdown sequence and provides feedback on command execution.

Stimulus: An operator adjusts the reactive power compensation set point for a wind farm via the SCADA system. Response: The SCADA system transmits the new set point command to the Wind Farm Main Controller, which then distributes it to the relevant wind turbine control units.
functionalRequirements: E-REQ-006: The system shall transmit set point commands to adjust configurable wind turbine parameters (e.g., active power, reactive power, yaw angle).
E-REQ-007: The system shall transmit binary control commands (e.g., start/stop, reset, enable/disable) to change the operational states of wind turbine components.
E-REQ-008: The system shall ensure secure and authenticated transmission of all control commands to prevent unauthorized operation.
E-REQ-009: The system shall provide feedback on the successful execution or failure of transmitted commands within 2 seconds.
E-REQ-010: The system shall allow command prioritization for critical safety and operational commands.

systemFeatures.name: Alarm and Event Management
systemFeatures.description: This feature centralizes the detection, reporting, and acknowledgment of critical operational alarms and significant events originating from all connected wind turbines within the **elsfork** system. It facilitates timely intervention by operators, thereby minimizing potential downtime, preventing equipment damage, and enhancing overall plant safety. The system's ability to provide granular, time-tagged information for anomalies is of High priority, underpinning effective fault diagnosis and regulatory compliance for wind farm operations.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: A wind turbine's internal system detects a critical fault condition, such as a gearbox over-temperature. Response: The wind turbine control unit generates a time-tagged alarm event, transmits it to the SCADA system, which then displays the alarm prominently and logs it for historical analysis.

Stimulus: An operator acknowledges an active alarm via the SCADA HMI. Response: The SCADA system records the acknowledgment, clears the active alarm status for the operator, and updates the alarm log.
functionalRequirements: E-REQ-011: The system shall detect and report abnormal operational states as alarms from each wind turbine.
E-REQ-012: The system shall capture and report significant operational changes, such as mode transitions or component status changes, as events.
E-REQ-013: The system shall timestamp all alarms and events with a minimum resolution of 1 millisecond at the point of origin.
E-REQ-014: The system shall allow authenticated operators to acknowledge alarms remotely via the SCADA system.
E-REQ-015: The system shall provide capabilities for filtering, sorting, and querying historical alarm and event data based on turbine ID, alarm type, and time range.

systemFeatures.name: Local Data Storage and Retrieval
systemFeatures.description: This feature ensures the persistence of essential operational data directly at the wind turbine level, providing a robust backup mechanism and facilitating detailed local analysis even during periods of communication disruption. It enhances the overall data reliability of the **elsfork** system and supports forensic analysis by preserving a complete operational history. This capability is of Medium priority, as it mitigates data loss risks and provides autonomy to the wind turbine, ensuring data availability even when remote access is compromised.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: The communication link between a wind turbine control unit and the Wind Farm Main Controller is temporarily lost. Response: The wind turbine control unit continues to log all required operational data locally in non-volatile memory.

Stimulus: The communication link is restored, and the SCADA system requests historical data from a period when communication was lost. Response: The wind turbine control unit retrieves and transmits the locally stored historical data to the SCADA system.
functionalRequirements: E-REQ-016: The system shall store a minimum of 7 days of all acquired operational data locally within each wind turbine control unit.
E-REQ-017: The system shall utilize non-volatile memory for local data storage to ensure data persistence during power outages.
E-REQ-018: The system shall support configurable data retention policies for local storage, allowing for up to 30 days of historical data.
E-REQ-019: The system shall allow for secure retrieval of historical local data upon authenticated request from the SCADA system.
E-REQ-020: The system shall implement data integrity checks for locally stored data to detect and prevent corruption.

performanceRequirements: E-NFR-001: The overall transfer time for critical control commands from the **SCADA System** to a **Wind Turbine Control Unit** shall not exceed 500 milliseconds, with a rationale to ensure timely operational adjustments and emergency response capabilities. This includes all network and processing delays.

E-NFR-002: The overall transfer time for spontaneous alarm events from a **Wind Turbine Control Unit** to the **SCADA System** shall not exceed 1 second, with a rationale to facilitate rapid operator awareness and intervention for critical anomalies.

E-NFR-003: The system shall support a periodic data transmission rate from individual **Wind Turbines** to the **SCADA System** of at least once per second for designated real-time operational parameters, with a rationale to provide adequate granularity for performance monitoring and trend analysis.

E-NFR-004: The system response time for displaying newly received data points on the **SCADA HMI** shall not exceed 2 seconds from receipt of data, with a rationale to ensure near real-time visualization for operators.
safetyRequirements: E-NFR-005: In the event of a communication failure between a **Wind Turbine Control Unit** and the **SCADA System**, the **Wind Turbine** shall automatically revert to its local autonomous control and safety protocols, with a rationale to prevent unsafe operation or environmental hazards.

E-NFR-006: The communication system shall prevent the transmission or execution of conflicting or hazardous commands, such as simultaneously commanding 'Start' and 'Stop', with a rationale to maintain operational safety and prevent equipment damage.

E-NFR-007: All control commands transmitted through the system shall undergo a two-step validation process (e.g., confirmation prompt or double-check mechanism) before execution at the **Wind Turbine**, with a rationale to mitigate human error in critical operations.

E-NFR-008: The system shall log all safety-critical events and command transmissions in an immutable audit trail, with a rationale to provide forensic data for post-incident analysis and regulatory compliance.
securityRequirements: E-NFR-009: All communication channels for command and control signals shall be encrypted using industry-standard protocols (e.g., TLS v1.2 or higher) to prevent unauthorized interception or modification, with a rationale to protect the integrity and confidentiality of operational commands.

E-NFR-010: Remote access to **Wind Turbine Control Units** via the communication system shall require multi-factor authentication for all users, with a rationale to prevent unauthorized access and control by malicious actors.

E-NFR-011: The system shall implement role-based access control (**RBAC**) to restrict user permissions based on their operational responsibilities, ensuring that operators can only issue commands relevant to their authorized functions, with a rationale to enforce the principle of least privilege.

E-NFR-012: The system shall detect and alert on unusual communication patterns or unauthorized access attempts from external entities, with a rationale to provide early warning of potential cyber threats.
softwareQualityAttributes: E-NFR-013: Reliability: The communication system shall maintain a data transmission success rate of 99.9% for critical control commands and alarm events over a 24-hour period under normal operating conditions, with a rationale to ensure the dependable execution of essential functions.

E-NFR-014: Availability: The communication system shall be available for data exchange and command transmission 99.5% of the time, excluding scheduled maintenance, with a rationale to minimize operational downtime and ensure continuous monitoring capabilities.

E-NFR-015: Interoperability: The communication system shall be capable of exchanging data and commands with **Wind Turbine Control Units** from at least three different major manufacturers, as demonstrated during acceptance testing, with a rationale to achieve vendor independence and enable diverse wind farm configurations.

E-NFR-016: Maintainability: The average time to diagnose and resolve a critical communication fault shall not exceed 4 hours, with a rationale to minimize system downtime and simplify troubleshooting efforts through modular design and clear logging.

E-NFR-017: Scalability: The system shall support communication with up to 500 individual **Wind Turbines** within a single **Wind Farm Main Controller** deployment without degradation of critical performance requirements, with a rationale to accommodate future growth and expansion of wind energy projects.
businessRules: E-NFR-018: All communication protocols implemented by the **elsfork** system shall adhere to or be compliant with relevant sections of the IEC TC88 Wind Turbine Systems international standardization efforts.

E-NFR-019: The communication system design shall prioritize vendor independence, allowing for seamless integration with wind turbines from various manufacturers without requiring proprietary adaptations.

E-NFR-020: The system shall support a flexible architecture that accommodates the operational requirements of both single wind turbine installations and large-scale wind farms, ensuring adaptability to diverse deployment scenarios.
E-NFR-021: Data transfer and handling mechanisms shall be designed to be extensible, allowing for the addition of new data types and command structures as wind turbine technology evolves.

glossary.definition: Specified value of a parameter that represents the uncertainty in the measurement.
Field, or Fields, in a message identifying both the source and / or destination of the message.
Information for attracting attention to some abnormal state.
Signal in the form of a continuously variable value.
The top layer, Layer 7, in the ISO Reference Model comprising the interface between the ISO environment and the IED’s / user’s applications.
The availability of a unit or system characterises its ability to perform its required function at any given moment.

Ratio of the total number of hours during a certain period, excluding the number of hours that the wind turbine could not be operated due to maintenance or fault situations, to the total number of hours in the period, expressed as a percentage.
Monitored information of the status of operational equipment that is characterised by one of two states, for example on/off.
Part of a message used for error checking or error correcting purpose.
An IED, or user, that requests data or services from another IED, or user, which thus responds as a server.
Information used to cause a change of state of operational equipment.
A 7 Layer stack, ISO Reference Model, each layer performing a specific functional role in Open Systems Interconnection communication.

A step in system design e.g. selecting of functional units, assigning their locations and defining their interconnections. Configuration is the process setting up the required parameters for a specific application.

An association established between functional units for conveying information. A connection is established between two IEDs prior to any data exchange. A connection may be of short duration or long term.
Purposeful action on or in a system to meet specified objectives. Note – Control may include monitoring and safeguarding in addition to the control action itself.
A control centre is a location where a master station is located.
Sub-system that receives information about the condition of the wind turbine and/or its environment and adjusts the turbine in order to maintain it within its operating limits.

Cyclic Redundancy Check. A CRC is performed for each frame and the value is included in that frame when it is transmitted. The CRC check calculation may be simple or complex depending on the protocol being used. The CRC value is used by the recipient communication interface to check and if possible correct errors incurred during transmission of that frame.
Measure of conformity of information on variables at different locations in different instants.
The ability of a communication system to deliver data from its originator to its destination with an acceptable residual error rate.

Layer 2 of the ISO Reference Model, responsible for the transmission of data over a physical medium. After establishing the link, layer 2 performs data rate control, error detection, contention / collision detection and recovery.

A reference structure comprised of one or more data items. Used to represent the specific elements of functionality of the device. These data objects may be hierarchical and may be nested to any number of levels.
Procedures and actions designed to prevent the unauthorised disclosure, transfer, modification or destruction, whether accidental or intentional, of data.
A defined method of data presentation, for example data type INTEGER for whole numbers or data type OCTET STRING for an assembly of octets.
A mechanism or piece of equipment designed to serve a purpose or perform a function, e.g. Circuit Breaker, Relay or Substation Computer.
Digital representation of a measured value.
The cryptographic transformation of data to produce ciphertext.
Monitored information on the change of state of operational equipment.

Functions are tasks that are performed in the control centre or the wind power plant by the system. Generally, a function consists of subfunctions that exchange data which each other. Depending on the function definition functions itself exchange data with other functions. There is no unique allocation of functions or subfunctions to devices. One ore more functions may reside in a single device or be distributed among several devices at the same or at different control levels. In minimum, the most functions consist of three subfunctions, i.e. the subfunctions with the core functionality itself, the process interface function and the HMI (human-machine interface) function meaning human access to the function.
Control of the data transfer rate.

Human Machine Interface - A Display screen, either part of an IED or as a standalone device, presenting relevant data in a logical format with which the user interacts. An HMI will typically present windows, icons, menus, and pointers, and may include a keypad to enable user access and interaction.

Intelligent Electronic Device - e.g. numeric Protection relay, or Bay controller, or multi-function electronic Meter. An IED may have connections as a client, or as a server, or both, with other IEDs. An IED is, therefore, any device incorporating one or more processors, with the capability to receive, or send, data / control from, or to, an external source. An IED may provide multi-Function capability ...

A shared boundary between two functional units, defined by functional characteristics, e.g. common physical interconnection characteristics, signal characteristics or other characteristics as appropriate, and the provision of a declared collection of services.
The ability of two, or more, IEDs, from the same or different vendors, to communicate, exchange data and use that information for correct operation.

Internet Protocol - the TCP/IP standard protocol. IP defines the datagram that provides the basis of connectionless packet delivery. It includes control and error message protocol providing the equivalent functions to Network services, Layer 3, of the OSI Reference Model.
The combination of all technical and corresponding administrative actions intended to retain an item in or restore it to a state in which it can perform its required function.
Manufacturing Message Specification - standard ISO 9506.
A value representing the integrated total of a measurable variable (such as energy flow) taken of a specified point in time.
The time interval between the initiation of an event in a sending station and the output, in the same station, of the associated response coming from the receiving station.

The time duration by which information is delayed after the actual event in the sending station and until presentation at the receiving station. Note – The overall transfer time includes the delays due to the input peripheral device in the sending station and the corresponding peripheral output device at the receiving station.
Transmission of sets of data that is repeated in equal time intervals.
Piece of information for COMmunication. As defined in the approach of CIGRE Working Group 34.03 into data flows within Substations.
The rules for communication system operation that must be followed if communication is to be effected.
A set of characteristics of a connection described in terms of quality of service (QoS) parameters, normally negotiated between peer entities.
The ability of a functional unit to perform a required function under stated conditions for a stated period of time.
Control of an operation from a distance. This involves a link between the control device and the apparatus to be operated.
Remote Terminal Unit - ..., an RTU acts as an interface between the communication network and the substation equipment.
On a communication network, a server .. provides data, or allows access to its resources....
A command in which the value for the required state of operational equipment is transmitted to a controlled station where it is stored.
A visual, audible or other indication used to convey information.
Monitored information represented by only one bit characterising two determined states of operational equipment.
Data transfer initiated by an application process upon events or change of data.
A system that supervises and controls a geographically distributed process.
A command used to change the state of two-state operational equipment from one state to the other state.

Transmission Control Protocol/Internet Protocol. A suite of protocols which together provide the functionality up to layer 4, of the ISO OSI Reference Model, without exact layer for layer correspondence. ..
A specific application service element for telecontrol purpose.

glossary.term: Time resolution, limit of accuracy of chronology
glossary.definition: The minimum time by which two events must be separated in order that the corresponding time tags be different.
Method of transmission of change-of-state so that transmitted information is accompanied by data giving the registered time at which the change occurred, within the time resolution.
A transmission method in which messages are transmitted only as result of a request such as an interrogation command from a control centre or master station.

A term specifying some quality description of a communication network, e.g. bit error rate, availability of a dedicated channel, probability of bit error bursts, signal to noise ratio, amplitude and phase distortion, non-linearities, inter-channel interference.
Layer 4 of the ISO OSI Reference Model, acts as an intermediary between the Network and the User application.

A Wind farm is characterised by the size and the location of the wind power plant, i.e. the effects on the power network. Furthermore, the properties and functions of the wind farm are more advanced than of a smaller wind power plant. A wind farm generally has a main controller for co-ordinated control of the individual wind turbines. The wind farm will typically have its own high voltage feeder network with a number of small substations. The electrical properties and behaviour of the wind farm is of such a nature, that it typically will be met with specific requirements regarding power quality and reactive power compensation. In some locations, the wind farm must participate actively in the regulation of the network stability.
Wind power plant is the general name for one or more wind turbines that converts kinetic energy in the wind into electrical energy within a geographically confined area.
System that converts kinetic energy in the wind into electrical energy.

flowchartDiagram.syntaxExplanation: The flowchart diagram illustrates the typical data and command flow within the **elsfork** system. Nodes represent processes or states, with labels describing their function. Decision nodes (diamond shape) indicate a branching point based on a condition. Connections indicate the flow of control or data. All node IDs are alphanumeric, and labels with spaces are double-quoted. The diagram direction is Top Down (TD).
flowchartDiagram.code:
flowchart TD
    StartProcess(["Start System Monitoring"]) --> A1["Wind Turbine Control Unit (CU)"]
    A1 --> B1["Acquire Operational Data"]
    B1 --> C1{"Is Data Change Significant?"}
    C1 -- "Yes" --> D1["Package Spontaneous Data"]
    D1 --> E1["Transmit Data to WFMC"]
    C1 -- "No" --> F1["Package Periodic Data"]
    F1 --> E1
    E1 --> G1["Wind Farm Main Controller (WFMC)"]
    G1 --> H1["Forward Data to SCADA"]
    H1 --> I1["SCADA System"]
    I1 --> J1["Analyze & Display Data"]
    J1 --> K1{"Operator Issues Command?"}
    K1 -- "Yes" --> L1["Generate Control Command"]
    L1 --> M1["Transmit Command to WFMC"]
    M1 --> N1["WFMC Routes Command"]
    N1 --> A1
    K1 -- "No" --> EndProcess(["Continue Monitoring"])
    A1 -- "Receives Command" --> A1
flowchartDiagram.caption: elsfork Wind Power System Data and Command Flow

sequenceDiagram.syntaxExplanation: The sequence diagram illustrates the temporal interactions between key participants during a SCADA system's request for turbine data in the **elsfork** system. Participants are represented by vertical lifelines, and messages are depicted as horizontal arrows. Activation bars (implied by `->>+` and `-->>-`) indicate the period a participant is active. Participant aliases are simple alphanumeric IDs, and message labels avoid special characters.
sequenceDiagram.code:
sequenceDiagram
    participant SCADA as "SCADA System"
    participant WFMC as "Wind Farm Main Controller"
    participant CU as "Control Unit"
    participant DB as "Local Data Database"

    SCADA->>+WFMC: "Request Turbine Data (ID:X)"
    WFMC->>+CU: "Query Sensor_Y Value"
    CU->>DB: "Retrieve Sensor_Y from Local Storage"
    DB-->>-CU: "Sensor_Y Value (Timestamp)"
    CU-->>-WFMC: "Sensor_Y Value (Timestamp)"
    WFMC-->>-SCADA: "Turbine X, Sensor_Y Value (Timestamp)"
    SCADA->>SCADA: "Display Data to Operator"
sequenceDiagram.caption: elsfork SCADA Turbine Data Request Sequence

entityRelationshipDiagram.syntaxExplanation: The Entity Relationship Diagram (ERD) models the structural relationships between core entities within the **elsfork** communication and control system. Entities (tables) are defined with their attributes and primary (PK), foreign (FK), and unique (UK) keys. Relationships indicate how entities interact, with cardinality symbols representing the number of instances involved (e.g., ||--o{ for one-to-zero-or-many). All relationship labels are double-quoted, and attribute names are alphanumeric.
entityRelationshipDiagram.code:
erDiagram
    WindTurbine { 
        int id PK 
        string name 
        string location 
        int capacity_kw 
    }
    ControlUnit { 
        int id PK 
        int turbine_id FK 
        string firmware_version 
        datetime last_calibrated 
    }
    DataPoint { 
        int id PK 
        int cu_id FK 
        string name 
        float value 
        datetime timestamp 
    }
    Command { 
        int id PK 
        int cu_id FK 
        int scada_id FK 
        string type 
        string value 
        datetime issued_at 
    }
    SCADA_System { 
        int id PK 
        string name 
        string location 
    }

    ControlUnit }|--|| WindTurbine : "belongs to"
    ControlUnit ||--o{ DataPoint : "generates"
    ControlUnit }|--o{ Command : "receives"
    SCADA_System ||--o{ Command : "issues"
entityRelationshipDiagram.caption: elsfork Wind Power System Entities ERD
